Technical Design Document

Component-by-component technical detail — architecture, source dialog fields, Sling Model/HTL/CSS, target block implementation, and a full field-by-field AEM-to-target mapping for every component in the migration inventory.

Accordion Component — Technical Details

What it does

Renders a stack of collapsible "blades" (panels) with a title and expandable content region. Each blade can carry an optional icon or image, and the whole component can expose an "Expand All / Collapse All" control pair above and below the stack. In the source system this is a multifield-driven parbase component; each blade's body content is itself an authorable parsys (a nested drop-zone), so authors can put arbitrary components inside a blade.

Architecture

Author configures dialog fields (behavior, icons, justification) + N "Accordion Blades" (multifield)
         ↓
accordion.html (HTL) reads properties via MultiFieldHelper (com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.components.MultiFieldHelper)
         ↓
For each blade: data-sly-use.accordModel="AccordionModel" (per-item request-attribute injection:
    mvavalue, componentTitle, itemlabel, sectionTitleAnalytics, localizedSectionTitleAnalytics, mvaName, itemInteractionId)
         ↓
Renders <div class="abbv-accordion-blade"> with a nested parsys resourceType='wcm/foundation/components/parsys'
named "accordion{N}" for each blade's body content
         ↓
Client JS (accordion.js, jQuery-based IIFE) wires click/keyup handlers, toggles 'abbv-active',
swaps open/close icon classes, and manages ARIA state

Sling Model — AccordionModel.java

Extends AbstractComponentModel. It does not read the blade list directly for rendering (the HTL uses MultiFieldHelper for that) — its real job is analytics-ID bookkeeping and exposing per-item request-attribute values injected by the HTL loop:

@Model(adaptables = {SlingHttpServletRequest.class})
public class AccordionModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENT_ID_PREFIX = "abbv-accordion";
    private static final String INTERACTION_ID = "interactionID";

    @Inject @Optional @Named("mvavalue") @Source("request-attributes")
    private String mvavalue;
    @Inject @Optional @Named("componentTitle") @Source("request-attributes")
    private String componentTitle;
    @Inject @Optional @Named("itemlabel") @Source("request-attributes")
    private String itemlabel;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional
    private List<String> accordionItems;

    @PostConstruct
    public void init() {
        componentPosition = CommonUtility.getAnalyticsComponentPositionAndType(resource.getPath());
        interactionId = getDialogInteractionId();
        // In author mode only: writes a generated interaction ID back into the
        // component's own JCR properties, and a per-blade "-1", "-2"... suffix
        // into each accordionItems JSON blob, then commits via ResourceResolver.
        if (!isPublish() && (StringUtils.isBlank(interactionId) || !interactionId.equals(getId()))) {
            setDialogInteractionId();
        }
        if (accordionItems != null && !isPublish() && (StringUtils.isBlank(itemInteractionId) || !itemInteractionId.contains(getId()))) {
            setDialogInteractionIdForEachBladeItem();
        }
    }
}

Notable behavior: this model performs a JCR write-back during author-mode rendering (resource.getResourceResolver().commit()) purely to self-populate analytics interaction IDs — a side effect of a GET render, not something an EDS block would ever need since there is no author-mode JCR to write to.

Dialog

Two tabs: "OVERVIEW" (component-level settings) and "ACCORDION BLADES" (a multifield of per-blade fieldsets).

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Accordion IdentifieranchorIDtextid attribute on the outer container
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestextspace-separated
Accordion BehavioraccordionBehaviourselectabbv-accordion-single or abbv-accordion-multi
Accordion Content JustificationtextJustificationselectempty or abbv-text-center
Expand Accordion Blade IconopenIconselect (icon datasource)icon font class
Collapse Accordion Blade IconcloseIconselect (icon datasource)icon font class
Enable Expand All/Collapse AllenableExpandCollapsecheckboxoverrides per-blade open state defaults
Expand All Button TextexpandButtonNametextdefault "Expand All"
Collapse All Button TextcollapseButtonNametextdefault "Collapse All"
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIDtext (disabled)system-generated, read-only in dialog
Section Title for AnalyticssectionTitleAnalyticstext-
Localized Section Title for AnalyticslocalizedSectionTitleAnalyticstext-
Per-blade fields (multifield accordionItems):
Accordion Blade IdentifieritemAnchorIDtext-
Blade Custom CSS Class(es)itemCustomCssClassestext-
Blade IconlabelIconselect (icon datasource)rendered before/inline with label
Blade ImageimageBeforeLabelpathbrowser (/content/dam)-
Blade Image Alt TextimageAltTexttext-
Blade Image Title TextimageTitletext-
Blade Title TextitemLabeltext, required-
Open by DefaultopenAccordionItemcheckbox-
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIDtext (disabled)per-blade, auto-suffixed "-1", "-2"...
Blade Title for AnalyticsitemComponentTitletext-
MVA TitlemvaNametextMost Valued Action metadata
MVA Type and CategorymvaValueselect (analytics datasource)-
MVA Primary/SecondarymvaTierselect-

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<div id="${properties.anchorID}" class="abbv-accordion${...customCssClasses} ${properties.accordionBehaviour} ${properties.textJustification}"
     data-icon-open="${properties.openIcon}" data-icon-closed="${properties.closeIcon}">
  <div class="abbv-accordion-container">
    <div class="abbv-accordion-tools top">...expand/collapse buttons (if enableExpandCollapse)...</div>
    <div id="${itemList.itemAnchorID}" class="abbv-accordion-blade abbv-accordion-blade-${count} ${itemList.openAccordionItem ? 'abbv-active':''}">
      <div class="abbv-accordion-blade-content" tabindex="0" role="button" aria-expanded="false">
        <div class="abbv-accordion-blade-image"><img .../></div>
        <div class="abbv-accordion-blade-text ${itemList.labelIcon}">${itemList.itemLabel}</div>
      </div>
      <div class="abbv-accordion-blade-icon"><i class="${openIcon or closeIcon} i-a"></i></div>
      <div class="abbv-accordion-content" style="display:none|block">
        <!-- nested parsys: authors drop arbitrary components here -->
        <sly data-sly-resource="${@path='accordion'+count, resourceType='wcm/foundation/components/parsys'}"/>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="abbv-accordion-tools bottom">...duplicate expand/collapse buttons...</div>
  </div>
</div>

Every interactive element carries a data-digitaldata JSON attribute (page/component/interaction/MVA analytics payload) consumed by the site's tag-management layer — this is unique to the source system and has no EDS equivalent.

CSS

.abbv-accordion-blade { border-bottom: solid 1px #ccc; cursor: pointer; }
.abbv-accordion-blade-content { padding: 20px 40px 20px 15px; overflow: hidden }
.abbv-accordion-content { display: none; padding: 0 15px 15px 15px; }
.abbv-accordion-content-open { display: block; }
.abbv-accordion-tools .abbv-accordion-tools-expand,
.abbv-accordion-tools .abbv-accordion-tools-collapse { display: none; }
.abbv-accordion-tools .abbv-accordion-tools-expand.abbv-active,
.abbv-accordion-tools .abbv-accordion-tools-collapse.abbv-active { display: block; }

Client script (accordion.js) is a jQuery IIFE: accordion.init(), .open()/.close()/.openAll()/.closeAll(), toggling abbv-active and swapping icon classes via data-icon-open/data-icon-closed attributes read off the root element.

Important Constraints

  • Blade body content is a full nested parsys — any component can be dropped inside a blade in the source system; this is richer than a typical "rich text" accordion body.
  • The per-item interaction ID write-back happens on every author-mode render until it matches, coupling rendering to JCR writes.
  • "Enable Expand/Collapse All" overrides individual blade open-state defaults per the field description, an implicit precedence rule not enforced anywhere in code shown — it's a documented author expectation only.
  • jQuery dependency for all interactivity (no native <details> element used).

Accordion Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built
The block is fully implemented at blocks/accordion/. It uses native <details>/<summary> elements instead of ARIA-scripted divs, supports icon-font or image icon modes, an optional fragment-path body (fetched and sanitized client-side), and per-item accessibility labels.

File Structure

blocks/accordion/
├── accordion.js
├── accordion.css
├── _accordion.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/accordion.css, _accordion.css, block-config.js
├── botox/accordion.css, _accordion.css, block-config.js
├── linzess/accordion.css, _accordion.css, block-config.js
├── mavyret/accordion.css, _accordion.css, block-config.js
├── rinvoq/accordion.css, _accordion.css, block-config.js
├── rinvoq-hcp/accordion.css, _accordion.css, block-config.js
├── skyrizi-hcp/accordion.css, _accordion.css, block-config.js
└── venclexta/accordion.css, _accordion.css, block-config.js

Authoring Model (_accordion.json)

Two model ids: accordion (block-level: blockHeading, classes_allowMultipleOpen, classes_showExpandCollapseAll, expandAllLabel, collapseAllLabel, classes_iconType, expandAllIcon/collapseAllIcon/expandIcon/collapseIcon font names, matching *IconImage reference fields, ariaExpandAllLabel, ariaCollapseAllLabel, analyticsId, plus style selects for width/alignment/theme/margin) and accordion-item (summary, text richtext, fragmentPath, classes_defaultOpen, ariaExpandLabel/ariaCollapseLabel, anchorId, image, imageAlt).

How the JS works (accordion.js)

Row-index parsing is order-sensitive and documented in a code comment (0: blockHeading through 13: analyticsId), with an offset-detection heuristic (gteConfigIcons) to tolerate an extra "classes" row some authoring paths insert. For each item row it builds a <details class="accordion-item">, moves the label into a <summary>, and — uniquely — supports a fragmentPath column: if a row's third cell is an absolute path, the block fetches ${fragmentPath}.plain.html, strips scripts/iframes/dangerous URL schemes/inline styles, and injects the sanitized fragment as the item body, falling back to the originally authored content on error.

if (isFragmentPath) {
  body.dataset.fragmentPath = fragmentPath;
  const fallbackNodes = [...body.childNodes].map((n) => n.cloneNode(true));
  body.textContent = '';
  fetch(`${fragmentPath}.plain.html`)
    .then((resp) => (resp.ok ? resp.text() : ''))
    .then((html) => { /* sanitize + inject, or restore fallbackNodes */ })
    .catch(() => { /* restore fallbackNodes */ });
}

"Multiple open" behavior is implemented with a per-details toggle event listener that force-closes siblings unless allowmultipleopen/accordion-multi is present — the inverse default of the source system, which defaults to "One Blade at a Time" via a select rather than a boolean.

CSS

.accordion-block-heading {
    color: var(--bg-666b7a);
    font-family: var(--roboto-bold-font-family);
    text-transform: var(--font-uppercase);
}
.accordion .accordion-expand-all {
    display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--spacing-10);
    background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer;
}

Brand override pattern: each brand folder (abbvie/, botox/, linzess/, mavyret/, rinvoq/, rinvoq-hcp/, skyrizi-hcp/, venclexta/) ships its own accordion.css plus a minified _accordion.css and a per-brand block-config.js hook for brand-specific decoration overrides.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (accordion)EDS (accordion)
Interactive elementscripted div/ARIA (jQuery)native <details>/<summary>, no jQuery
Blade body contentfull nested parsys (any component)richtext field, or a fetched/sanitized fragment page
Default open behaviorselect, default single-openboolean allowMultipleOpen, default single-open enforced in JS
Analyticsper-element data-digitaldata JSON, auto-generated interaction IDs written back to JCRsingle optional flat analyticsId string attribute
Iconsicon-font datasource onlyicon-font or per-icon image reference, switchable via classes_iconType

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtextanchorId (on accordion-item, not block)RenamedMoved from component-level to item-level in EDS model
customCssClassestextclasses_* selectsNot mappedFree-text CSS classes replaced by a fixed set of style selects (desktopWidth/alignment/theme/margin) — no free-text class field
accordionBehaviourselectclasses_allowMultipleOpenRenamedSelect-with-two-values collapsed to a boolean, semantics inverted (true = multi-open)
textJustificationselectclasses_alignmentRenamedDifferent option set (left/center vs none/left/center/right)
openIconselect (icon datasource)expandIconRenamedDatasource-backed picker replaced by free-text icon-font name
closeIconselect (icon datasource)collapseIconRenamedSame as above
enableExpandCollapsecheckboxclasses_showExpandCollapseAllSame name (functionally)Boolean semantics preserved
expandButtonNametextexpandAllLabelRenamedSame purpose
collapseButtonNametextcollapseAllLabelRenamedSame purpose
interactionIDtext (system-generated)analyticsIdRenamedAuto-generated JCR write-back mechanism does not exist in EDS; field is now a plain author-entered optional string
sectionTitleAnalyticstext—Not mappedNo page-level digitaldata analytics object model in EDS; analytics is handled by a separate site-wide tracking layer, not per-field authoring
localizedSectionTitleAnalyticstext—Not mappedSame reason as above
itemAnchorIDtextanchorId (accordion-item)RenamedSame purpose
itemCustomCssClassestext—Not mappedNo free-text per-item CSS class field in EDS model
labelIconselect (icon datasource)—Not mappedEDS has block-level expand/collapse icons only, no per-item label icon
imageBeforeLabelpathbrowserimageRenamedDAM path browser replaced by Universal Editor reference component
imageAltTexttextimageAltRenamedSame purpose
imageTitletext—Not mappedNo image title-text field in EDS item model
itemLabeltext, requiredsummaryRenamedSame purpose
openAccordionItemcheckboxclasses_defaultOpenRenamedSame purpose
itemComponentTitletext—Not mappedAnalytics-only field, no EDS equivalent
mvaName, mvaValue, mvaTiertext/select—Not mapped"Most Valued Action" analytics taxonomy is specific to the source system's tag-management integration; no equivalent concept in the EDS model
——fragmentPathNot mapped (new in EDS)Net-new capability: item body can be a fetched fragment page, with no AEM-side analog (AEM instead used a nested parsys)
——ariaExpandLabel, ariaCollapseLabel, ariaExpandAllLabel, ariaCollapseAllLabelNot mapped (new in EDS)Explicit ARIA-label authoring fields added; the source system hardcoded aria-expanded state changes in JS without author-facing label fields

Anchor Nav Block — Technical Details

What it does

This is an EDS-native block with no direct AEM source component. The manifest lists an empty aemSources array for anchor-nav, and a targeted search of the source system confirms there is no matching in-page jump-navigation component: the source system does have components named anchor and anchortag, but both are hidden (componentGroup=".hidden") utility templates used internally by other components (e.g. to render a single styled link with analytics attributes) — not an authorable multi-link jump-nav bar. There is no source-system equivalent to migrate from; this section documents the EDS implementation only.

EDS Implementation

The block builds an in-page jump-navigation <nav> from a list of label/target rows, adds smooth-scroll click handling, a sticky-on-scroll behavior driven by an IntersectionObserver sentinel, and active-section highlighting via a second IntersectionObserver watching each target section.

File Structure

blocks/anchor-nav/
├── anchor-nav.js
├── anchor-nav.css
├── _anchor-nav.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/anchor-nav.css, _anchor-nav.css, block-config.js
├── botox/anchor-nav.css, _anchor-nav.css, block-config.js
└── rinvoq/anchor-nav.css, _anchor-nav.css, block-config.js

Authoring Model (_anchor-nav.json)

{
  "id": "anchor-nav",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "text", "name": "ariaLabel", "value": "Page sections", "label": "Nav Aria Label" }
  ]
},
{
  "id": "anchor-nav-item",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "text", "name": "label", "value": "", "label": "Link Label" },
    { "component": "text", "name": "targetId", "value": "", "label": "Target Section ID",
      "description": "The id attribute of the target section element (without the # prefix)." }
  ]
}

How the JS works (anchor-nav.js)

export default function decorate(block) {
  const nav = document.createElement('nav');
  nav.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Page sections');
  const links = [...block.children].map((row) => {
    const [labelCell, targetCell] = row.children;
    const a = document.createElement('a');
    a.href = `#${target}`;
    a.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
      e.preventDefault();
      document.getElementById(target)?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' });
    });
    return a;
  }).filter(Boolean);
  nav.append(...links);
  block.replaceChildren(nav);
  // Sticky sentinel
  const sentinel = document.createElement('div');
  block.before(sentinel);
  const stickyObserver = new IntersectionObserver(
    ([entry]) => block.classList.toggle('is-sticky', !entry.isIntersecting),
    { rootMargin: `-${navHeight}px 0px 0px 0px` },
  );
  stickyObserver.observe(sentinel);
  // Active section tracking via second IntersectionObserver on each target
}

Note the ariaLabel field authored in the JSON model is not actually read by decorate() — the aria-label is hardcoded as the string literal 'Page sections' in the JS, a discrepancy between the authoring model and the implementation.

CSS

.anchor-nav.is-sticky {
  position: sticky;
  top: var(--nav-height, 64px);
  z-index: var(--z-index-anchor-nav, 100);
}
.anchor-nav a.is-active {
  border-bottom-color: var(--anchor-nav-active-color, var(--link-color));
  color: var(--anchor-nav-link-hover, var(--link-color));
}

Horizontal scroll with hidden scrollbar (overflow-x: auto; scrollbar-width: none;) for mobile overflow of many links.

Constraints

  • Depends on target elements already having matching id attributes elsewhere on the page — the block does not create or validate these.
  • Sticky offset assumes a CSS custom property --nav-height is defined globally; falls back to a hardcoded 64px if not.
  • No AEM Sling Model, dialog, or HTL exists for this pattern — it is a genuinely net-new EDS block, not a migration target.

Banner Ad Component — Technical Details

What it does

Renders a paid-media ad placement. The source-system "Banner Ads Container" component lets an author paste raw ad markup (an ad-network tag) plus configure a banner-size preset, and separately attaches Important Safety Information (ISI) content by referencing another page's content parsys — either a plain reference page or a page carrying a project/reference number. A companion page template, ad-banner-page, exists purely to host this component standalone (for use inside iframes/rich-media ad units) and contributes no additional authoring fields of its own.

Architecture

Author selects Ad Banner Size (datasource-backed) + pastes Ad Markup (raw HTML/JS) + Download Button Label
         ↓
Author (ISI Content tab) points referencePath / referenceAndProjectNumberPath at other AEM pages
         ↓
BannerModel.java resolves those paths to Page objects via PageManager, then computes
  referencePath + "/jcr:content/contentpar" (falls back to the page path itself if that
  child resource does not exist)
         ↓
banner-ad.html renders the raw ad markup unsafely, then data-sly-resource-includes the
resolved ISI contentpar(s) inline into the page, plus a conditional "download" link
(hidden in edit mode and on publish)

Sling Model — BannerModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {SlingHttpServletRequest.class })
public class BannerModel {
    @ValueMapValue @Default(values="") @Optional
    private String referencePath;
    @ValueMapValue @Default(values="") @Optional
    private String referenceAndProjectNumberPath;

    @PostConstruct
    public void init() {
        isPublish = slingSettingsService.getRunModes().contains("publish");
        if (resourceResolver != null && StringUtils.isNotEmpty(referencePath)) {
            Page referencePage = getPageReferenceObject(referencePath);
            if (referencePage != null) {
                String pageContentPar = referencePath + "/jcr:content/contentpar";
                referencePathContentPar = resourceResolver.getResource(pageContentPar) != null
                    ? pageContentPar : referencePath;
            } else {
                referencePathContentPar = "";
            }
        } else {
            referencePathContentPar = "";
        }
        getReferenceWithProjectNumberPath(); // same pattern for referenceAndProjectNumberPath
    }
}

Dialog

Two tabs: "Overview" and "ISI Content".

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Choose Ad BannerbannerSizeselect (datasource ce-adbanner-size)Sets the outer abbv-banner-ad size class
Ad MarkupadMarkuptextarea (40 rows)Raw HTML/JS, rendered with @context="unsafe"
Download Button LabeldownloadButtonLabeltextOnly shown outside edit mode when not published
ISI Page PathreferencePathpathbrowser (/content/admpmediabanners)Resolved to a contentpar and included inline
Reference Page PathreferenceAndProjectNumberPathpathbrowser (/content/admpmediabanners)Separate reference/project-number ISI include

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<div class="abbv-banner-ad ${properties.bannerSize}">
    <div class="ad">
        ${properties.adMarkup @context="unsafe"}
    </div>
    <div class="isi" id="isiContainer">
        <div class="isi-content" id="isiContent">
            <sly data-sly-test="${bannerClass.referenceContentPath}" data-sly-resource="${bannerClass.referenceContentPath}"></sly>
            <sly data-sly-test="${bannerClass.referenceWithPrjNumContentPar}" data-sly-resource="${bannerClass.referenceWithPrjNumContentPar}"></sly>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>
<sly data-sly-test="${!wcmmode.edit && !bannerClass.isPublish}">
    <p class="download-btn-section" id="downloadbtn">
        <a href="#" id="download-btn" onclick="bannerDownload()">${properties.downloadButtonLabel}</a>
    </p>
</sly>

The download button is deliberately suppressed on the publish tier (!bannerClass.isPublish) — it is an author/QA-only affordance (backed by AdBannerDownloadServlet.java) for exporting the rendered banner as a static asset for traffic to ad networks, not something end users on the live site ever see.

Important Constraints

  • Ad markup is rendered completely unsanitized (@context="unsafe") — the component trusts the author entirely, matching third-party ad-tag reality (scripts must run as authored).
  • ISI resolution silently falls back to the raw page path if the expected jcr:content/contentpar child resource is absent, and silently degrades to an empty string if the referenced page doesn't resolve at all — no author-facing error surfaces from either failure mode.
  • Two independent ISI reference mechanisms exist side by side (plain reference vs. reference-with-project-number) with near-identical resolution logic duplicated in the model.

Banner Ad Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built
The block is fully implemented at blocks/banner-ad/, supporting two authoring modes: a Google Ad Manager (GPT) slot mode (ad-unit path + width/height), and a raw HTML embed mode (activated by a html variant class). Both modes lazy-load via IntersectionObserver with a 200px root margin.

File Structure

blocks/banner-ad/
├── banner-ad.js
├── banner-ad.css
├── _banner-ad.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/banner-ad.css, _banner-ad.css, block-config.js
├── botox/banner-ad.css, _banner-ad.css, block-config.js
└── rinvoq/banner-ad.css, _banner-ad.css, block-config.js

Authoring Model (_banner-ad.json)

Three tabs: "Google Ad Manager" (ad-unit, width, height text fields), "HTML Embed" (embedCode richtext), "Settings" (classes_mode select choosing between GPT and HTML-embed variant classes), plus the shared _common-properties.json block.

How the JS works (banner-ad.js)

const GPT_SCRIPT = 'https://securepubads.g.doubleclick.net/tag/js/gpt.js';

function decorateGPT(block, config) {
  const slot = document.createElement('div');
  slot.className = 'banner-ad-slot';
  block.replaceChildren(slot);
  const observer = new IntersectionObserver(async (entries) => {
    if (!entries[0].isIntersecting) return;
    observer.disconnect();
    await loadGPT();
    window.googletag.cmd.push(() => {
      const sizes = w && h ? [[w, h]] : ['fluid'];
      window.googletag.defineSlot(adUnit, sizes, slotId)?.addService(window.googletag.pubads());
      window.googletag.enableServices();
      window.googletag.display(slotId);
    });
  }, { rootMargin: '200px' });
  observer.observe(block);
}

export default function decorate(block) {
  const isEmbed = block.classList.contains('html');
  if (isEmbed) { decorateEmbed(block); return; }
  // parse ad-unit / width / height rows into config, then decorateGPT(block, config)
}

The embed mode re-executes any <script> tags found in the pasted markup (browsers do not execute scripts injected via innerHTML) by cloning each into a fresh <script> element with copied attributes — mirroring the "must run ad tag as authored" requirement from the source system, but scoped to a single block rather than rendered inline into the page.

CSS

.banner-ad { overflow: hidden; }
.banner-ad-slot:empty {
  background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--light-color, #f4f4f4) 25%, #e8e8e8 50%, var(--light-color, #f4f4f4) 75%);
  background-size: 200% 100%;
  animation: banner-ad-shimmer 1.5s infinite;
  min-height: 90px;
}

Unlike most blocks, banner-ad is "intentionally unstyled" beyond overflow containment and a loading shimmer — ad networks control their own creative dimensions.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (banner-ad + ad-banner-page)EDS (banner-ad)
Ad delivery mechanismAuthor pastes raw markup only (any ad tag, including GPT tags manually)Structured GPT mode (ad-unit/width/height authored as discrete fields) OR raw HTML embed mode as a fallback
Loading strategyRenders inline at parse time, no lazy-loadingIntersectionObserver-gated lazy load in both modes
ISI attachmentTwo page-reference fields resolved server-side to a contentpar and included inlineNot present — no equivalent ISI-inclusion mechanism in the EDS model
Download-for-export toolingAuthor/QA-only download button + backing servlet for exporting rendered banner as a static assetNot present — no equivalent export tooling

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
bannerSizeselect (datasource)width / heightRenamedPreset banner-size picker replaced by two free-text pixel dimension fields used only in GPT mode
adMarkuptextarea (unsafe HTML)embedCodeRenamedSame purpose (raw ad tag), now scoped to a variant mode rather than always-on
downloadButtonLabeltext—Not mappedNo author/QA export-to-static-asset workflow exists in EDS; this tooling has no equivalent
referencePathpathbrowser—Not mappedISI page-reference inclusion has no EDS equivalent; ISI content in EDS is composed directly on the page rather than transcluded from a separate reference page
referenceAndProjectNumberPathpathbrowser—Not mappedSame reason as above
——ad-unitNot mapped (new in EDS)Net-new structured GPT ad-unit path field; the source component never modeled GPT slot config as discrete fields
——classes_modeNot mapped (new in EDS)Net-new explicit mode switch between GPT and embed; the source component always ran in "raw markup" mode

Brand Explorer Component — Technical Details

What it does

A global, persistent utility bar (typically pinned to the page) that expands into a full-screen brand-navigation panel: a brand logo mark, a set of top-level utility links, and — on expand — a two-level browse experience of therapeutic areas (link lists) plus a curated grid of featured products/brands with imagery, description, and a CTA. In the source system this composite is assembled from five separate content components: brand-explorer-global (the outer bar + orchestration + Products section), brand-explorer-therapeutic and brand-explorer-therapeutic-link-list (the per-therapeutic-area link groupings, nested as child resources), and brand-explorer-card and brand-explorer-navigation (related presentational variants — a flip-card product summary and a navigation-context rendering of the same bar).

Architecture

brand-explorer-global (top-level component, authored once per site/page)
  Dialog tabs: Overview (customCssClasses, closeIcon, EPass number)
               Bar Content (barTitle, expandIcon/position, multifield linksDetails[])
               Therapeutic Area (customCss, linkListColumnItems, backIcon, homeIcon)
               Products Section (productSectionLabel, multifield productDetails[])
         ↓
BrandExplorerGlobalModel.java (@PostConstruct):
  - parses linksDetails[] JSON strings → List<BrandExplorerGlobalBean> (explorerLinksBeans)
  - parses productDetails[] JSON strings → List<BrandExplorerProductsBean>
  - builds a synthetic list "therapeuticitem0..N" (size = linkListColumnItems) used purely
    as a loop-count placeholder for including N child brand-explorer-therapeutic-link-list resources
         ↓
brand-explorer-global.html:
  - renders the fixed bar (logo via anchor.html template call, barTitle button, explorerLinksBeans as <li> links)
  - renders the expandable panel: nav buttons (close/home/back), then for each
    therapeuticItem includes a child resource of resourceType
    brand-explorer-therapeutic-link-list (selector "brand-explorer-therapeutic-link-panel"
    for the home slider, no selector for the browse-in panel)
  - renders the Products section from brandExplorerProductsBeans, each wrapped via
    anchor.html's "beProductLink" template

Sling Model — BrandExplorerGlobalModel.java

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class BrandExplorerGlobalModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    @Inject @Optional
    private List<String> linksDetails;
    @Inject @Optional
    private int linkListColumnItems;
    @Inject @Optional
    private List<String> productDetails;

    private List<BrandExplorerGlobalBean> explorerLinksBeans;
    private List<BrandExplorerProductsBean> brandExplorerProductsBeans;
    private List<String> therapeuticItem;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        if (linksDetails != null && !linksDetails.isEmpty()) linksDetails();
        if (productDetails != null && !productDetails.isEmpty()) productDetails();
        therapeuticItem = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < linkListColumnItems; i++) {
            therapeuticItem.add("therapeuticitem" + i);
        }
    }
}

Related models in the same family: BrandExplorerTherapeuticModel, BrandExplorerGlobalTherapeuticModel, and BrandExplorerCardItemModel (backing the flip-card variant), each with their own bean (BrandExplorerTherapeuticBean, BrandExplorerProductsBean, BrandExplorerLinkDetailsBean, BrandExplorerCardItemBean).

Dialog

brand-explorer-global — four tabs (abbreviated to authorable leaf fields; several MVA/analytics sub-fields per multifield row omitted for brevity but present in the real dialog):

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestext-
Close IconcloseIconselect (icon datasource)-
Brand Explorer EPass NumberexplorerEpassNumbertextLegal/reference number shown in the expanded panel footer
Expand Button TextbarTitletext-
Expand Icon / PositionexpandIcon / expandIconPositionselect / select-
Explorer Links Details (multifield)linksDetails → linkTitle, linkUrl, modalID, linkBehaviour, linkType, interactionId, componentTitle, sectionTitle, l10Titlemultifield of fieldsetsTop utility links row of the bar
Therapeutic Area Custom CSStherapeuticAreaCustomCssClassestext-
Number of Therapeutic AreaslinkListColumnItemsnumberfieldDrives the loop count for child therapeutic-link-list includes
Back Icon / Home IconbackIcon / homeIconselect / selectMobile+tablet navigation icons
Products Custom CSSproductsCustomCssClassestext-
TitleproductSectionLabeltext-
Products (multifield)productDetails → productCustomCssClasses, productImage, productImageTitle, productImageAltText, productDesc, ctaIcon, ctaURL, ctaModalID, ctaLinkType, ctaLinkBehaviour, plus analytics (interactionId, componentTitle, sectionTitle, l10Title)multifield of fieldsetsFeatured products grid

brand-explorer-card dialog — one tab, a multifield cardFieldSet per card: productPageURL, indicationTitle, indicationDesc, learnMoreTitle, learnMoreURL, learnMoreLinkType, learnMoreModalID, learnMoreLinkBehaviour, learnMoreLinkIcon, learnMoreLinkIconPosition, plus a full analytics block (componentTitle, componentPosition, contentJourney, patientJourney, messageBucket, learnMoreMVA, learnMoreMvaType, learnMoreMvaValue, learnMoreMvaTier).

brand-explorer-therapeutic-link-list dialog — indicationCustomCssClasses, indicatonHeading [sic, real field name], indicationDesc, expandIcon, plus a link (linkUrl, modalID, linkType...).

HTL — Key DOM Structure (brand-explorer-global.html)

<div class="abbv-brand-explorer-global ${properties.customCssClasses}" data-digitaldata='...'>
  <div class="abbv-brand-explorer-panel-container">
    <!-- beContainer template: close/home/back nav buttons + home slider (therapeutic areas + products) -->
  </div>
  <div class="abbv-brand-explorer-bar">
    <div class="brand-explorer-bar-content">
      <div class="abbv-brand-explorer-bar-logo">
        <!-- inline AbbVie logo SVG path via anchor.html "anchorLogoWithPath" template call -->
      </div>
      <button id="brand-explorer-expand" class="abbv-button-plain ${properties.expandIcon} ${properties.expandIconPosition}">${properties.barTitle}</button>
      <div class="abbv-brand-explorer-bar-links">
        <ul>
          <sly data-sly-list.links="${brandExplorerGlobalModel.explorerLinksBeans}">
            <li><!-- anchor.html "anchorBarLink" template call per link --></li>
          </sly>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
<template data-sly-template.beContainer="${@ brandExplorerGlobalModel}">
  <div class="brand-explorer-nav-buttons">
    <button id="brand-explorer-close" .../><button id="brand-explorer-home" disabled/><button id="brand-explorer-back" disabled/>
  </div>
  <div class="brand-explorer-home-slider">
    <!-- beSliderPanel template: per-therapeuticItem link groupings + product grid -->
    <div class="brand-explorer-panel"><!-- one child-resource include per therapeuticItem, no selector --></div>
  </div>
  <div class="abbv-legal-number"><span>${properties.explorerEpassNumber}</span></div>
</template>

CSS

Component-scoped CSS lives in brand-explorer-global/ui/brand-explorer-global.css plus a shared clientlib aggregation; class names follow the abbv-brand-explorer-* and brand-explorer-* prefixes seen throughout the HTL above (e.g. .abbv-brand-explorer-bar-logo, .abbv-link-exploration, .abbv-product-exploration).

Important Constraints

  • The "number of therapeutic areas" field (linkListColumnItems) is a raw integer that drives an index-based synthetic list (therapeuticitem0, therapeuticitem1, ...) used to include that many child brand-explorer-therapeutic-link-list resources by conventional node name — a fragile positional-authoring pattern rather than an explicit multifield of references.
  • The same set of therapeutic-area child resources is rendered twice from two different code paths (once inside beContainer's browse panel, once inside beSliderPanel's home slider) with different resource selectors, meaning authors must keep both region's content consistent by hand.
  • Five separate content components must be authored/maintained together for one coherent experience, with duplicated analytics-field patterns (componentTitle/sectionTitle/l10Title/interactionId) repeated across each.

Brand Explorer Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built
The block is fully implemented at blocks/brand-explorer/ as a single fixed bottom bar (not the source system's expand-to-fullscreen panel) with an inline brand logo SVG, a "Browse" trigger button, utility links, and — on click — a brand-picker panel built from parsed table rows describing each brand (image, name, safety text, URL, brand color, and per-brand indication links with severity tagging). Uniquely, the block's parser supports three distinct authored-content shapes for backward/author-tool compatibility.

File Structure

blocks/brand-explorer/
├── brand-explorer.js
├── brand-explorer.css
├── _brand-explorer.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/brand-explorer.css, _brand-explorer.css, block-config.js
├── botox/brand-explorer.css, _brand-explorer.css, block-config.js
├── rinvoq-hcp/brand-explorer.css, _brand-explorer.css, block-config.js
└── skyrizi-hcp/brand-explorer.css, _brand-explorer.css, block-config.js

Authoring Model (_brand-explorer.json)

Block-level fields include anchorId, barLabel, projectNumber, and up to three navLinkNLabel/navLinkNUrl pairs. The item model brand-explorer-item covers logo, logoAccessibleName, brandName, therapeuticArea, description, brandUrl, safetyContent, indications.

How the JS works (brand-explorer.js)

The block ships three parallel row-parsers and auto-detects which one applies to the authored markup:

function detectAndParseContent(rows, block) {
  const isTableFormat = firstRowCells > 1 || rows[0]?.querySelector('a') || rows[0]?.querySelector('img, picture');
  if (isTableFormat) {
    cfg = parseTableFormat(rows);          // multi-cell rows, first row = bar config
  } else {
    cfg = parseFlatXwalkFormat(rows);      // single-cell rows, positional heuristics
    if (!cfg.brands.length) {
      const ueCfg = parseUEEditorFormat(rows); // data-aue-component-tagged rows
      if (ueCfg.brands.length) cfg = ueCfg;
    }
  }
}

parseFlatXwalkFormat in particular uses regex heuristics (e.g. /^[A-Z]{2,}-[A-Z]+-\d/ to detect a project number token, and image-presence to delimit "bar config" rows from "brand entry" rows) rather than fixed field indices, because the flat authoring format does not delimit fields by column.

CSS

.brand-explorer-section {
  position: relative;
  width: 100vw;
  margin-left: calc(50% - 50vw);
  background: var(--be-color-bar-bg, #25282a);
}
.brand-explorer {
  --be-color-navy: var(--color-navy, #071d49);
  --be-color-rinvoq-gold: var(--color-rinvoq-gold, #ffd524);
  --be-color-skyrizi-blue: var(--color-skyrizi-blue, #00a8e1);
  --be-color-humira-magenta: var(--color-humira-magenta, #931947);
}

Block-scoped custom properties bake in specific brand accent colors (Brand A gold, Brand B blue, Brand C magenta) directly as CSS variable defaults, then brand folders (abbvie/, botox/, rinvoq-hcp/, skyrizi-hcp/) override further per site.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (5 components)EDS (brand-explorer)
Expand patternFull-screen overlay panel with home/back/close navigation and a multi-level browse (therapeutic areas → products)Single fixed bottom bar that reveals a flat brand-picker panel — no multi-level browse hierarchy
Content sourceFive separate authored component types (global bar, therapeutic, therapeutic-link-list, card, navigation) combined via child-resource includesSingle block, single authored table, with a per-brand item sub-model
Therapeutic area countPositional integer (linkListColumnItems) driving a synthetic child-resource loopNo therapeutic-area grouping concept; brands are a flat authored list
Content parsingServer-side, strongly typed via multifield beansClient-side, with three heuristic parsers to tolerate different authored table shapes

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
customCssClasses (global)text—Not mappedNo free-text CSS class field on the EDS block; styling is via the common-properties block and brand CSS folders
closeIconselect (icon datasource)—Not mappedEDS block's picker panel does not model a separate close-icon field; close affordance is hardcoded in CSS/markup
explorerEpassNumbertextprojectNumberRenamedSame purpose (legal/reference number)
barTitletextbarLabelRenamedSame purpose (bar trigger button text)
expandIcon / expandIconPositionselect / select—Not mappedEDS "Browse" button icon is a fixed CSS chevron (.brand-explorer-browse-icon), not an author-selectable icon
linksDetails[] (linkTitle/linkUrl/modalID/linkBehaviour/linkType)multifieldnavLink1Label/navLink1Url (...2, ...3)RenamedUnbounded multifield collapsed to a fixed maximum of 3 discrete label/URL pairs; no modal/link-type/behavior options carried over
linkType, linkBehaviour, modalID (utility links)select/select/text—Not mappedEDS utility links are plain hrefs; no modal or link-behavior authoring concept
therapeuticAreaCustomCssClasses, linkListColumnItems, backIcon, homeIcontext/numberfield/select/select—Not mappedNo therapeutic-area browse level exists in the EDS block
productSectionLabeltext—Not mappedNo separate "products section" heading; brands render as a single flat list
productDetails[] → productImagepathbrowserlogo (brand-explorer-item)RenamedSame purpose (brand/product image)
productDetails[] → productImageAltTexttextlogoAccessibleNameRenamedSame purpose
productDetails[] → productDesctextareadescriptionSame name (functionally)-
productDetails[] → ctaURLpathbrowserbrandUrlRenamedSame purpose
productDetails[] → ctaIcon, ctaModalID, ctaLinkType, ctaLinkBehaviourselect/text/select/select—Not mappedEDS brand entries use a plain link; no icon/modal/link-type authoring
productDetails[] analytics (interactionId, componentTitle, sectionTitle, l10Title, MVA fields)text/select—Not mappedNo per-item analytics/MVA taxonomy authoring in the EDS model
——therapeuticArea, safetyContent, indications (brand-explorer-item)Not mapped (new in EDS)Net-new per-brand fields; closest AEM analog (indication title/description) lived in the separate brand-explorer-therapeutic-link-list component, not on the product/brand entry itself

Breadcrumb Component — Technical Details

What it does

Renders an ordered breadcrumb trail from the site's page hierarchy, from a configured "home" page down to the current page, with each crumb carrying analytics attributes. It can be hidden entirely or authored to render collapsed on load (a client-side responsive behavior).

Architecture

Author configures homePagePath / homeTitle / hidebreadcrumb / collapsebreadcrumb / customCssClasses
         ↓
BreadcrumbModel.java (@PostConstruct):
  - resolves homePagePath to a Page via PageManager
  - walks currentPage.getAbsoluteParent(level) from the home page's depth up to
    (but not including) the current page's own depth, building one BreadcrumbBean per level
  - if the current page is NOT a descendant of the configured home page, falls back to
    a single-entry breadcrumb containing only the current page
  - computes and self-writes an analytics interactionId into the component's own JCR
    properties in author mode (same write-back pattern as AccordionModel)
  - builds a full digitalData analytics JSON string per crumb via DigitalDataObject
         ↓
breadcrumb.html renders an <ul> of <li>, each crumb rendered via the shared anchor.html
"breadcrumb" template call (passing analyticsString, url, linkTitle, patientJourney,
contentJourney, messageBucket)

Sling Model — BreadcrumbModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {SlingHttpServletRequest.class})
public class BreadcrumbModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    @ValueMapValue @Default(values="/content/common-elements/en-us")
    private String homePagePath;
    @Inject private Page currentPage;

    @PostConstruct
    public void init() {
        Page homePath = resourceResolver.adaptTo(PageManager.class).getPage(homePagePath);
        int startLevel = homePath.getDepth();
        int endLevel = currentPage.getDepth();
        if (currentPage.getAbsoluteParent(startLevel - 1).equals(homePath)) {
            pageList = new ArrayList<>();
            BreadcrumbBean homePageLink = new BreadcrumbBean();
            homePageLink.setTitle(getPageTitle(homePath));
            homePageLink.setLink(homePath.getPath());
            pageList.add(homePageLink);
            for (int level = startLevel; level < endLevel; ++level) {
                Page itemPage = currentPage.getAbsoluteParent(level);
                BreadcrumbBean link = new BreadcrumbBean();
                link.setTitle(getPageTitle(itemPage));
                link.setLink(itemPage.getPath());
                pageList.add(link);
            }
        } else {
            // current page is not under the configured home page — degrade to a single crumb
            pageList = new ArrayList<>();
            BreadcrumbBean link = new BreadcrumbBean();
            link.setTitle(getPageTitle(currentPage));
            link.setLink(currentPage.getPath());
            pageList.add(link);
        }
    }

    private String getPageTitle(Page pageVal) {
        // navigationTitle → pageTitle → title, in that priority order
    }
}

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Home Page PathhomePagePathpathbrowserRoot of the trail; defaults to /content/common-elements/en-us
Home TitlehomeTitletextOverrides the home page's own title in the first crumb
Hide BreadcrumbhidebreadcrumbcheckboxSuppresses the entire component render
Collapse BreadcrumbcollapsebreadcrumbcheckboxSets a data-bc-collapse attribute for client-side collapse behavior
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestext-
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtext (disabled)System-generated, read-only

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-test="${!properties.hidebreadcrumb}">
  <div class="abbv-breadcrumb-navigation ${properties.customCssClasses}" data-bc-collapse="${properties.collapsebreadcrumb}">
    <ul data-sly-list="${breadcrumb.pageList}">
      <sly data-sly-test.linkTitle="${properties.homeTitle || item.title}">
      <li data-sly-test="${itemList.first}">
        <!-- anchor.html "breadcrumb" template call: analyticsString, url, linkTitle, compType='navigation', compName='breadcrumb', compPosition='header' -->
      </li>
      <li data-sly-test="${!itemList.first}">
        <!-- same template call, using item.title instead of the homeTitle override -->
      </li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</sly>

Note only the first crumb honors the homeTitle override — subsequent crumbs always use the page's own resolved title (navigationTitle/pageTitle/title fallback chain).

Important Constraints

  • No client-side JS ships an interactive breadcrumb; "collapse" is a data-attribute hook only (rendering behavior driven by CSS/JS elsewhere in the clientlib, not shown in the component's own ui/breadcrumb.js beyond wiring).
  • The home-page-ancestry check uses exact object equality (.equals(homePath)) on the ancestor at startLevel - 1 — pages outside that exact subtree silently degrade to a single, non-hierarchical crumb rather than erroring.
  • No JSON-LD / structured data for search engines is emitted by this component — that is unique to the EDS reimplementation.

Breadcrumb Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built
The block is fully implemented at blocks/breadcrumb/, supporting both a manually authored crumb list and a fully automatic mode that derives the trail from the current URL path plus a site index, and injects BreadcrumbList JSON-LD into <head> for SEO.

File Structure

blocks/breadcrumb/
├── breadcrumb.js
├── breadcrumb.css
├── _breadcrumb.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/breadcrumb.css, _breadcrumb.css, block-config.js
├── botox/breadcrumb.css, _breadcrumb.css, block-config.js
├── rinvoq/breadcrumb.css, _breadcrumb.css, block-config.js
└── skyrizi-hcp/breadcrumb.css, _breadcrumb.css, block-config.js

Authoring Model (_breadcrumb.json)

{
  "id": "breadcrumb",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "boolean", "name": "auto", "value": false, "label": "Auto Generate",
      "description": "Auto-generate breadcrumb from URL path. Turn off to use manually authored crumb items." },
    { "component": "text", "name": "homeLabel", "value": "Home", "label": "Home Label" }
  ]
},
{
  "id": "breadcrumb-item",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "text", "name": "label", "value": "", "label": "Link Label" },
    { "component": "aem-content", "name": "href", "label": "Link URL" }
  ]
}

How the JS works (breadcrumb.js)

Two independent build paths, chosen by the auto config flag:

export default async function decorate(block) {
  const config = extractConfig(block);
  const items = extractItems(block);
  rows.forEach((row) => row.classList.add('breadcrumb-hidden')); // preserve UE instrumentation, hide raw rows

  let crumbs;
  if (config.auto) {
    crumbs = await buildAutoTrail(config.homeLabel); // derives trail from IndexUtils.getIndexData()
  } else {
    crumbs = items; // manually authored breadcrumb-item rows
  }
  if (!crumbs.length) return;
  const nav = buildNav(crumbs, config.title);
  if (config.anchorId) block.id = config.anchorId;
  block.append(nav);
  appendJsonLd(nav.querySelector('ol'));
}

buildAutoTrail splits the current pathname into segments, looks each partial path up against a site index (IndexUtils.getIndexData()) to resolve a human-readable navtitle, and falls back to a formatted version of the raw URL segment (hyphens → spaces) when no index match exists. The active crumb is detected by comparing normalized pathnames and gets aria-current="page" plus a flex-active class.

CSS

.breadcrumb .breadcrumb-hidden { display: none; }
.breadcrumb {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: flex-start;
  margin-bottom: var(--spacing-24);
}
.breadcrumb .breadcrumb-drop-title::after {
  content: "";
  font-family: var(--icon-font-family);
  transform: rotate(90deg);
  transition: transform 0.3s ease-out;
}

Includes a mobile "dropdown" affordance (.breadcrumb-drop-title, sub-744px) not present at all in the source-system component, which had no responsive collapse implementation shipped in its own files.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (breadcrumb)EDS (breadcrumb)
Trail generationAlways server-side, from JCR page hierarchy (Page.getAbsoluteParent)Either manually authored items, or client-side automatic generation from URL path + a fetched site index
Off-hierarchy pagesSilently degrades to a single non-hierarchical crumbAutomatic mode always derives full path segments; manual mode simply renders whatever was authored
SEO structured dataNone emittedInjects BreadcrumbList JSON-LD into <head>
Mobile behaviorData attribute hook only (data-bc-collapse), behavior implemented elsewhere/not shownDedicated dropdown-toggle CSS/markup pattern built into the block itself

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
homePagePathpathbrowser—Not mappedAuto mode derives the trail from the URL/site index rather than a configured root page reference; manual mode has no equivalent single "home path" concept, only per-item authored links
homeTitletexthomeLabelRenamedSame purpose, now scoped specifically to auto mode
hidebreadcrumbcheckbox—Not mappedNo equivalent field; the EDS way to hide a breadcrumb is to not place the block on the page at all
collapsebreadcrumbcheckbox—Not mappedSuperseded by an always-on, CSS-media-query-driven mobile dropdown pattern rather than an authorable toggle
customCssClassestext—Not mappedNo free-text class field; styling is via the shared common-properties block and brand CSS folders
interactionIdtext (system-generated)—Not mappedNo per-crumb analytics-ID authoring or write-back mechanism in the EDS model
——autoNot mapped (new in EDS)Net-new capability: automatic URL-driven breadcrumb generation did not exist in the source component, which was always server-hierarchy-driven
——label / href (breadcrumb-item)Not mapped (new in EDS)Net-new manual-authoring path; the source component never allowed hand-authored crumbs, only computed ones

Brightcove Podcast Player Component — Technical Details

What it does

This is an EDS-native block with no direct AEM source component. The manifest lists an empty aemSources array, and a search of the source system confirms there is no Brightcove-backed podcast/audio-only player component: the source system's only audio-related component is audio-player, a generic HTML5 <audio> element player (with bundled sample .mp3/.ogg/.wav fixtures) that has no relationship to Brightcove, no account/player-ID concept, and no video-SDK dependency — it is not a legitimate migration source for this block. There is no source-system equivalent to migrate from; this section documents the EDS implementation only.

EDS Implementation

The block renders a compact podcast-style player card (thumbnail + title + audio controls) backed by the Brightcove Player SDK running in "audio-only" mode (video surface hidden, only the control bar shown), lazy-loaded when the block scrolls into view.

File Structure

blocks/brightcove-podcast-player/
├── brightcove-podcast-player.js
├── brightcove-podcast-player.css
├── _brightcove-podcast-player.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/brightcove-podcast-player.css, _brightcove-podcast-player.css, block-config.js
├── botox/brightcove-podcast-player.css, _brightcove-podcast-player.css, block-config.js
└── rinvoq/brightcove-podcast-player.css, _brightcove-podcast-player.css, block-config.js

Authoring Model (_brightcove-podcast-player.json)

Two tabs: "Overview" (videoId, accountId select defaulting to corporate, playerId, podcastTitle, podcastThumbnail reference) and "Analytics" (analyticsInteractionId), plus the shared _common-properties.json fields.

How the JS works (brightcove-podcast-player.js)

const ACCOUNT_MAP = {
  corporate: '2157889325001',
  public: 'public',
  commercial: 'commercial',
};

function resolveAccountId(value) {
  const lower = value.toLowerCase();
  return ACCOUNT_MAP[lower] || value; // falls through to a raw authored ID if not a known keyword
}

function loadPlayer(container, cfg, imgContainer, titleEl) {
  const videoEl = document.createElement('video-js');
  videoEl.setAttribute('data-account', resolveAccountId(cfg.accountId));
  videoEl.setAttribute('data-player', cfg.playerId || 'default');
  videoEl.setAttribute('data-video-id', cfg.videoId);
  container.append(videoEl);
  loadBrightcoveScript(account, player).then(() => {
    window.bc(videoEl);
    bcPlayer.ready(function () {
      this.audioOnlyMode(true); // hides video surface, keeps audio controls
      this.on('loadedmetadata', () => {
        // fallback: if no authored title/thumbnail, pull them from Brightcove's mediainfo
      });
    });
  });
}

export default function decorate(block) {
  const cfg = readBlock(block);
  // ...builds thumbnail + title DOM...
  if (cfg.videoId) {
    const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
      if (entries[0].isIntersecting) { observer.disconnect(); loadPlayer(...); }
    }, { threshold: 0.1 });
    observer.observe(block);
  }
}

Account resolution deliberately maps three human-readable keywords (corporate/public/commercial) to real Brightcove account IDs, so authors never need to know or paste raw numeric account IDs for the common cases.

CSS

.brightcove-podcast-player {
  --brightcove-podcast-thumbnail-width: calc(95 * var(--px-to-rem));
  --brightcove-podcast-player-height: var(--spacing-41);
  --brightcove-podcast-progress-track-color: rgb(186 181 181 / 80%);
}

Extensive block-scoped custom-property namespace (--brightcove-podcast-*) drives a custom-styled control bar (play button, scrubber, volume track, time labels) layered on top of the Brightcove player's audio-only mode.

Constraints

  • Requires network access to players.brightcove.net to load the per-account/player SDK bundle; no offline/self-hosted fallback.
  • Title/thumbnail fallback-from-API only fires on the player's loadedmetadata event — if that event never fires (e.g. blocked script, invalid video ID), an authored title/thumbnail is the only way content appears.
  • No dialog, HTL, or Sling Model exists for this pattern in the source system — it is a genuinely net-new EDS block, not a migration target.

Brightcove Video Component — Technical Details

What it does

A full-featured Brightcove video embed supporting single video, playlist (carousel or card layout), and 360-degree video player types, with a configurable poster/overlay (image or color background, title, description, and a play button with font-icon or image-icon), autoplay, social sharing, video-chapter navigation, end-cards (time-triggered overlay content), recommended-video end screens, and transcript link authoring (new tab, same tab, modal, or hidden panel).

Architecture

Author configures Player Type (Video / Playlist / 360 degree video) → shows/hides
type-specific fields via cq-dialog-dropdown-showhide
         ↓
VideoModel.java (@PostConstruct):
  - resolves brightcoveAccountID from a shared OSGi config service (CommonConfigServiceImpl,
    key "video.brightcove.account.id") — NOT authored per-instance
  - sets playlistEnabled = true when playerType == "bC11jvCtd" (the Playlist item value)
  - parses endCardFieldset[] (JSON strings) into a single aggregated JSON object
    {"slots": [...]} consumed client-side to show/hide end-card containers by CSS class
    at specific playback-time intervals
  - builds a full digitalData analytics JSON string (component/link/interaction/MVA)
         ↓
video-player.html dispatches to one of single-video.html / playlist-carousel.html /
playlist-cards.html / video360-player.html based on playerType, each rendering a
<video-js> element with data-video-id / data-account / data-player / data-embed
attributes for the Brightcove Player SDK to hydrate
         ↓
transcript-template.html renders the configurable transcript link (new tab/same
tab/modal/hidden panel) alongside the player

Sling Model — VideoModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {SlingHttpServletRequest.class })
public class VideoModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    @ValueMapValue(injectionStrategy = InjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL) @Named("playerType")
    @Default(values="Mcp9TXMkPT")
    private String playerType;

    @Inject @Source("osgi-services")
    private CommonConfigServiceImpl commonConfigService;

    private String brightcoveAccountID = "";
    private boolean playlistEnabled = false;

    @PostConstruct
    public void init() {
        brightcoveAccountID = commonConfigService.getConfigPropertyVal("video.brightcove.account.id");
        if ("bC11jvCtd".equals(playerType)) {
            playlistEnabled = true;
        }
        if (!endCardFieldset.isEmpty()) {
            JSONArray jsonArray = new JSONArray();
            for (String endCard : endCardFieldset) {
                JSONObject jsonObject = new JSONObject(endCard);
                updateJSONObject(jsonObject, "on");
                updateJSONObject(jsonObject, "off");
                jsonArray.put(jsonObject);
            }
            JSONObject mainObject = new JSONObject();
            mainObject.put("slots", jsonArray);
            endCards = mainObject.toString();
        }
    }
}

The Brightcove account ID is deliberately not an authorable dialog field on this component — it comes from a shared OSGi service configured once per environment, unlike the podcast player block (EDS-native) which does expose an authorable account selector.

Dialog

Five tabs: Overview, End Cards, Transcripts, Analytics, and a further "recommended video" sub-configuration. Player-type-conditional fields are toggled via cq-dialog-dropdown-showhide.

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor IDanchorIDtext-
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestext-
Player TypeplayerTypeselectVideo / Playlist / 360 degree video (values are opaque IDs, e.g. Mcp9TXMkPT)
BrightCove Video IDvideoIDtext (conditional: Video)-
Brightcove 360 degree Video IDtdVideoIDtext (conditional: 360)-
Brightcove Playlist IDplaylistIDtext (conditional: Playlist)-
Default Playlist Video (Optional)defaultVideoIDtext (conditional: Playlist)Start the playlist on a non-first video
Playlist TypeplaylistTypeselect (conditional: Playlist)Carousel or Cards
Playlist LayoutplaylistLayoutselect (datasource, conditional: Playlist)Position relative to currently-playing video
Display Playlist MetadatadisplayPlaylistMetadatacheckbox (conditional: Playlist)Title/description/time per playlist item
Video Content LayoutcontentLayoutselect (datasource)Position of title/description/chapters/transcript relative to player
Autoplay the VideoautoplayVideocheckbox-
Enable Social SharingenableSocialSharecheckbox-
Social Share Button TextsocialShareButtonTexttextDefault "Share"
Display Video ChaptersdisplayVideoChapterscheckboxRequires chapters authored in Brightcove itself
Enable Recommended VideoenableRecommendedVideocheckboxSingle/360 only
Project NumberepassNumbertextPer-video, required on every instance
End Card details (multifield)endCardFieldset → class, on, offmultifield of fieldsetsCSS class + show/hide time interval in seconds
Video Transcript Link LabeltranscriptLabeltext-
Transcript Link Click BehaviortranscriptLinkBehaviourselectNew Tab/Window, Same Tab, Modal, Hidden Panel
Transcript Modal/Hidden Panel IDtranscriptModalIDtext-
Transcript Link Label Icon / PositiontranscriptIcon / transcriptIconPositionselect / select-

HTL — Key DOM Structure (single-video.html)

<div class="abbv-video-player ${properties.customCssClasses}"
     data-autoplay="${properties.autoplayVideo}"
     data-socialshare="${properties.enableSocialShare}"
     data-externalchaptersmenu="${properties.displayVideoChapters}"
     data-recommendedvideo="${properties.enableRecommendedVideo}"
     data-endcard='${videoModel.endCards}'
     data-digitaldata='${videoModel.analyticsString}'>
  <div class="abbv-video-container aspect-ratio-16-9">
    <video-js id="${videomodel.id}" class="video-js"
       data-video-id="${properties.videoID}"
       data-account="${videomodel.brightcoveAccountID}"
       data-player="${properties.playerType}"
       data-embed="default" controls playsinline>
    </video-js>
  </div>
  <!-- transcript-template.html "transcript" call -->
</div>

Important Constraints

  • Brightcove account ID is environment-configured, not authorable — migrating a page cannot change which Brightcove account a video plays from without an environment-level change.
  • Player-type values in the dialog are opaque hash-like IDs (Mcp9TXMkPT, bC11jvCtd, z6mAcMv1F) rather than readable strings, making the dialog XML hard to read without cross-referencing the text labels.
  • End-card timing is author-specified in raw seconds against a CSS class the author must also create/style themselves — there's no visual timeline editor.
  • playsinline is only set on the autoplay branch of the HTL (two near-duplicate <video-js> blocks exist for autoplay vs. non-autoplay), a maintenance duplication risk.

Brightcove Video Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built
The block is fully implemented at blocks/brightcove-video/ (716 lines), with a strict, comment-documented 44-row field order, a click-to-play overlay (image or color poster, title, description, button with font-icon or image-icon in a configurable position), autoplay-on-visibility via IntersectionObserver with an autoplay-blocked fallback, and full parity with the source system's playlist/360/transcript/social-share/chapters feature set.

File Structure

blocks/brightcove-video/
├── brightcove-video.js
├── brightcove-video.css
├── _brightcove-video.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/brightcove-video.css, _brightcove-video.css, block-config.js
├── botox/brightcove-video.css, _brightcove-video.css, block-config.js
└── rinvoq/brightcove-video.css, _brightcove-video.css, block-config.js

Authoring Model (_brightcove-video.json)

Three tabs — Overview (projectNumber, overlayTitle, overlayDescription richtext, posterType/posterImage/posterAlt/colorOverlay, overlayButtonText + icon type/font-icon/image-icon/position, playerType, accountId, playerId, videoId, playlistId, defaultPlaylistVideoId, playlistType, videoContentLayout, playlistLayout, enablePlaylistThumbnailMetadata), Video Features (enableAutoplay, enableLoop, enableCaptions, enableVideoChapters, enableRecommendedVideo, enablePlayerControls, enableSocialShare, enableTranscript, transcriptType, showTranscriptLabel, hideTranscriptLabel, transcriptClickBehavior, modalHiddenPanelId, transcriptLink (aem-content), transcript icon fields), and Accessibility (playButtonAriaLabel, videoCaption).

How the JS works (brightcove-video.js)

Row parsing is driven by a documented, fixed ROW index map (44 entries) matching the JSON model field order exactly, since tabs don't render as rows:

const ROW = {
  PROJECT_NUMBER: 0, OVERLAY_TITLE: 1, OVERLAY_DESCRIPTION: 2, POSTER_TYPE: 3,
  POSTER_IMAGE: 4, POSTER_ALT: 5, COLOR_OVERLAY: 6, OVERLAY_BUTTON_TEXT: 7,
  /* ... */ PLAY_BUTTON_ARIA_LABEL: 42, VIDEO_CAPTION: 43,
};

export default function decorate(block) {
  const cfg = readBlock(block);
  block.classList.add(`bcvideo-player-${cfg.playerType}`);
  block.classList.add(`bcvideo-content-${cfg.videoContentLayout}`);
  const videoContainer = document.createElement('div');
  const overlay = buildOverlay(cfg);
  overlay.addEventListener('click', () => {
    if (!playExistingPlayer(videoContainer)) loadPlayer(videoContainer, cfg);
    overlay.hidden = true;
  });
  if (cfg.enableAutoplay) {
    const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
      if (entries[0].isIntersecting) {
        observer.disconnect();
        overlay.hidden = true;
        loadPlayer(videoContainer, cfg, {
          attemptAutoplay: true,
          onAutoplayBlocked: () => { overlay.hidden = false; videoContainer.classList.remove('bcvideo-playing'); },
        });
      }
    }, { threshold: 0.5 });
    observer.observe(videoContainer);
  }
}

Unlike the source system, autoplay failures (browsers routinely block unmuted autoplay) are handled explicitly with an onAutoplayBlocked callback that restores the click-to-play overlay — the source system's dual autoplay/non-autoplay HTL branches had no such runtime fallback.

CSS

Layout/feature classes are toggled by the decorate function (bcvideo-player-*, bcvideo-content-*, bcvideo-playlist-layout-*, bcvideo-autoplay, bcvideo-loop, bcvideo-captions, bcvideo-chapters, bcvideo-social) and styled per-brand in the brand CSS folders.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (video-player)EDS (brightcove-video)
Account ID sourceEnvironment-level OSGi config, not authorable per instanceAuthorable accountId select per block instance
Poster/overlayNot modeled — player renders directly, no click-to-play poster state described in the source HTLExplicit poster (image or color) + overlay title/description/button authoring, with a distinct click-to-play interaction state
Autoplay-blocked handlingNone shown — HTL simply renders a duplicate autoplay-flagged player elementExplicit onAutoplayBlocked fallback restores the overlay
End cardsTime-interval-triggered overlay content via CSS-class + JSON, requires author-created CSSNot present in the row-index list read from the reviewed implementation — no end-card equivalent field

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtext—Not mappedNo dedicated anchor-ID row read by the EDS decorate function; anchoring is handled via the shared common-properties block fields instead
customCssClassestext—Not mappedNo free-text class field in the documented row list
playerTypeselect (opaque IDs)playerTypeSame nameEDS uses readable values (e.g. playlist) instead of opaque hash-like IDs
videoIDtextvideoIdSame name (case)-
tdVideoIDtextvideoIdRenamed360-degree video ID collapsed into the same field as the standard video ID, disambiguated by playerType instead of a separate field
playlistIDtextplaylistIdSame name (case)-
defaultVideoIDtextdefaultPlaylistVideoIdRenamedSame purpose
playlistTypeselectplaylistTypeSame name-
playlistLayoutselect (datasource)playlistLayoutSame nameDatasource-backed picker replaced by a fixed EDS select options list
displayPlaylistMetadatacheckboxenablePlaylistThumbnailMetadataRenamedSame purpose
contentLayoutselect (datasource)videoContentLayoutRenamedSame purpose
autoplayVideocheckboxenableAutoplayRenamedSame purpose
enableSocialSharecheckboxenableSocialShareSame name-
socialShareButtonTexttext—Not mappedNo custom share-button-text row in the documented EDS field list; social share is a boolean toggle only
displayVideoChapterscheckboxenableVideoChaptersRenamedSame purpose
enableRecommendedVideocheckboxenableRecommendedVideoSame name-
epassNumbertextprojectNumberRenamedSame purpose (per-video reference/legal number)
endCardFieldset[] (class/on/off)multifield—Not mappedNo end-card row group in the EDS field model; this feature was not carried over
transcriptLabeltextshowTranscriptLabel / hideTranscriptLabelRenamedSingle label split into explicit show/hide states in EDS
transcriptLinkBehaviourselecttranscriptClickBehaviorRenamedSame purpose
transcriptModalIDtextmodalHiddenPanelIdRenamedSame purpose
transcriptIcon / transcriptIconPositionselect / selecttranscriptButtonIconType/transcriptShowFontIcon/transcriptHideFontIcon/transcriptLinkIconPositionRenamedSingle icon field split into show/hide icon variants plus an explicit icon-type (font vs image) switch
——posterType, posterImage, posterAlt, colorOverlay, overlayTitle, overlayDescription, overlayButtonText + icon fieldsNot mapped (new in EDS)Net-new click-to-play poster/overlay authoring; not modeled at all in the source component's dialog
——enableLoop, enableCaptions, enablePlayerControls, transcriptLink (aem-content), playButtonAriaLabel, videoCaptionNot mapped (new in EDS)Net-new fields with no source-system equivalent

Cards Component — Technical Details

What it does

Manifest sources: dynamic-card and "Page Components: forms-product-grid-list". These are two structurally different source-system artifacts sharing only a conceptual resemblance (a filterable/browsable grid of card-like items) to the EDS cards block:

  • dynamic-card is a real, wired AEM component (has .content.xml, _cq_dialog, HTL) built on Adobe's Content Fragment List core component (sling:resourceSuperType="core/wcm/components/contentfragmentlist/v2/contentfragmentlist"). It generates flip-cards from Content Fragment items in a DAM folder, filterable by tags, with a custom DynamicCardTagFilterList Sling Model that builds the tag-filter UI's JSON configuration from a fixed CQ tag namespace.
  • forms-product-grid-list has no .content.xml, no _cq_dialog, no HTL, and no Sling Model anywhere in the source repository — only a `ui/forms-product-grid-list.php` reference prototype (a static design-tool mockup with a namespace/title comment and an empty `#product-grid-list` div awaiting client-side JS that is not present), plus companion `.css`/`.js`/`testResponse.json` files under the same `ui/` folder. This is a design-reference artifact only, not an authored, wired AEM component. No real AEM dialog/HTL/model exists for this source; it cannot be mapped field-for-field — it is documented here only as context for the "product grid" naming lineage of the EDS block.

Architecture (dynamic-card)

Author selects a Content Fragment Model + DAM parentPath + optional tag filters + button/label text
         ↓
dynamic-card.html:
  data-sly-use.list="com.adobe.cq.wcm.core.components.models.contentfragment.ContentFragmentList"
  data-sly-test="${list.listItems.size > 0}"
         ↓
data-sly-use.filterList="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.DynamicCardTagFilterList"
  (builds the client-side filter-config JSON from the "/content/cq:tags/admp/recipes" tag tree)
         ↓
Renders a filter-toggle UI, one flip-card per Content Fragment item (via templates.html),
a "no results" message, and a "load more" button — all client-behavior-driven (filtering,
pagination, flip) via a bundled clientlib, none of it server-rendered beyond the initial page.

Sling Model — DynamicCardTagFilterList.java

@Model(adaptables = {SlingHttpServletRequest.class, Resource.class},
       defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class DynamicCardTagFilterList extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private String recipeTagFilterPath = "/content/cq:tags/admp/recipes";
    private String filterArrayString;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        setInteractionIdInDialog();
        TagManager tagManager = resourceResolver.adaptTo(TagManager.class);
        Tag tags = tagManager.resolve(recipeTagFilterPath);
        if (tags != null) {
            Iterator<Tag> tagIterator = tags.listChildren();
            JsonArrayBuilder arrayBuilderFilter = Json.createArrayBuilder();
            while (tagIterator.hasNext()) {
                Tag tag = tagIterator.next();
                // builds {"category": tag.getTitle(), "children": [childTag.getTitle(), ...]}
                // for each top-level tag under the fixed recipe-tag namespace
            }
        }
    }
}

Note the hardcoded tag namespace path (/content/cq:tags/admp/recipes) — this filter model is a domain-specific ("recipes") reference implementation, not a general-purpose taxonomy filter, despite the component's generic "Dynamic Cards" title.

Dialog (dynamic-card)

FieldPropertyTypeNote
ModelmodelPathselect (Content Fragment Models datasource)Content Fragment Model to build the list from
Anchor IDanchorIDtext-
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestext-
Content to be displayedparentPathpathfield (DAM folder)-
Filter label textfilterlabeltexttext-
Clear Filters button textclearfilterbuttontexttext-
Show Results button textshowresultsbuttontexttext-
Load More button textloadmorebuttontexttext-
No Results Found textnoresultslabeltext-
Flip Card to Back/Front textflipcardtoback / flipcardtofronttext / textCard-flip interaction labels
Goto Landing Page textgotolandingpagetext-
TagstagNamestagfield (multiple)Filters the list
Order By / Sort OrderorderBy / sortOrderselect / selectCore Component datasources
Max ItemsmaxItemsnumberfield-
IDidtextHTML id attribute, validated unique
ElementselementNamesmultifield (Core Component datasource)Which Content Fragment elements to render
Analytics Interaction IDdynamicCardInteractionIdtext (disabled)-

HTL — Key DOM Structure (dynamic-card.html)

<div id="${properties.anchorID}" class="abbv-dynamic-cards-list ${properties.customCssClasses}"
     data-config-object="${filterList.scriptID}">
  <script data-sly-call="${scriptObjTemp.scriptObj @ filterList=filterList}"></script>
  <div class="cards-filter-toggle">${properties.filterlabeltext} <span class="cards-filter-count">(0)</span></div>
  <div class="cards-filters"></div>
  <button class="cards-filter-clear">${properties.clearfilterbuttontext}</button>
  <button class="cards-filter-show">${properties.showresultsbuttontext}</button>
  <sly data-sly-list.fragment="${list.listItems}">
    <sly data-sly-call="${fragmentTemplate.contentFragment @ fragment=fragment}"></sly>
  </sly>
  <div class="no-cards">${properties.noresultslabel}</div>
  <button class="cards-load-more">${properties.loadmorebuttontext}</button>
</div>

Important Constraints (dynamic-card)

  • Tightly coupled to Adobe's Content Fragment model and Core Component ContentFragmentList — every "card" is backed by a structured Content Fragment, not a freely authored parsys item.
  • The tag-filter taxonomy is hardcoded to a single namespace path (/content/cq:tags/admp/recipes), making the component effectively domain-specific despite its generic name.
  • Filtering, pagination ("load more"), and flip interactions are entirely client-driven by a bundled clientlib not included in the component's own files reviewed here.

Cards Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built
The block is fully implemented at blocks/cards/ as a generic, freely-authored card grid (no Content Fragment Model dependency) with automatic detection and inline playback of embedded video links (Brightcove or YouTube) inside a card, plus a distinct "story" variant that restructures flat authored content into semantic meta/CTA sub-elements.

File Structure

blocks/cards/
├── cards.js
├── cards.css
├── _cards.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/cards.css, _cards.css, block-config.js
├── botox/cards.css, _cards.css, block-config.js
├── rinvoq/cards.css, _cards.css, block-config.js
└── roy/cards.css, _cards.css, block-config.js, themes/bright/cards.css, themes/bright/_cards.css
└── themes/bright/cards.css, themes/bright/_cards.css

Authoring Model (_cards.json)

Block-level classes multiselect includes a "Story" style flag and a "Card Width Style" option group (4/5/6/7-column presets). Item model card is a generic image + body content pairing (no Content Fragment binding).

How the JS works (cards.js)

Two notable behaviors with no source-system analog: automatic video-link detection inside a card body, and a "story" content-restructuring pass.

function parseVideoUrl(href) {
  const url = new URL(href);
  if (url.hostname === 'players.brightcove.net') {
    const match = url.pathname.match(/^\/(\d+)\/([^/]+)_default/);
    if (match) return { type: 'brightcove', account: match[1], player: match[2], videoId: url.searchParams.get('videoId') };
  }
  if (url.hostname.includes('youtube')) {
    const embedMatch = url.pathname.match(/\/embed\/([^/?]+)/);
    const videoId = embedMatch ? embedMatch[1] : url.searchParams.get('v');
    if (videoId) return { type: 'youtube', videoId };
  }
  return null;
}

export default function decorate(block) {
  const isStory = block.classList.contains('story');
  [...block.children].forEach((row) => {
    // ...builds <li>, classifies child divs as .cards-card-image or .cards-card-body...
    const videoLink = [...bodyDiv.querySelectorAll('a')].find((a) => parseVideoUrl(a.href));
    if (videoLink) setupVideoCard(imageDiv, parseVideoUrl(videoLink.href), videoLink.textContent.trim(), block);
    if (isStory && bodyDiv) decorateStoryCard(bodyDiv); // splits "May 19, 2021 Profile" into date+tag, etc.
  });
}

setupVideoCard replaces the static card image with a play button overlay; on click it lazily creates an <iframe> pointed at the correct Brightcove or YouTube embed URL — no full player SDK is loaded for cards, unlike the dedicated brightcove-video block.

CSS

.cards {
    --cards-video-overlay-background: var(--overlay-navy-light);
    --cards-stats-image-card-overlay: linear-gradient(180deg, rgb(7 29 73 / 60%) 0%, rgb(7 29 73 / 80%) 100%);
}
.cards > ul {
    list-style: none;
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
    gap: var(--spacing-24);
}

A "stats" variant is detected structurally rather than by an authored class: if the .cards-wrapper is the first child of its enclosing .section, the section itself gets a cards-stats class for different visual treatment.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (dynamic-card)EDS (cards)
Content sourceAdobe Content Fragments in a DAM folder, via Core Component ContentFragmentListFreely authored block rows/items, no structured content-model dependency
FilteringTag-based filter UI backed by a hardcoded CQ tag namespace, with clear/show/load-more controlsNo filtering — all authored cards render statically
Card interactionFlip-to-back/front (two-sided card)No flip; instead supports inline video playback via detected embed links
Video handlingNot applicable — cards are Content Fragment text/image onlyAutomatic Brightcove/YouTube URL detection and lightweight iframe playback per card
