Pagination"Load more" button, client-drivenNot present — all authored cards render at once

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
modelPathselect (Content Fragment Models)—Not mappedEDS cards are not backed by any structured content model; there is no equivalent "select a model" concept
anchorIDtext—Not mappedNo dedicated anchor field on the cards block model as reviewed; anchoring, if present, comes from the shared common-properties block
customCssClassestextclasses (multiselect)RenamedFree-text class field replaced by a constrained multiselect of style/width presets
parentPathpathfield (DAM folder)—Not mappedNo folder-driven content source; each card is authored directly as a block item
filterlabeltext, clearfilterbuttontext, showresultsbuttontext, loadmorebuttontext, noresultslabeltext (x5)—Not mappedNo filtering or pagination UI exists on the EDS block; these label fields have no purpose to map to
flipcardtoback, flipcardtofronttext / text—Not mappedNo flip-card interaction in the EDS block
gotolandingpagetext—Not mappedNo equivalent single "go to landing page" affordance separate from the card's own link
tagNamestagfield—Not mappedNo tag-based filtering concept in the EDS block
orderBy, sortOrder, maxItemsselect/select/numberfield—Not mappedCard order and count are simply however many items an author places in the block; no server-side query/sort/limit
idtext (unique)—Not mappedNo equivalent unique-ID authoring field reviewed on the cards block model
elementNamesmultifield (Content Fragment elements datasource)—Not mappedNot applicable — EDS cards have no underlying structured element set to select from
dynamicCardInteractionIdtext (disabled)—Not mappedNo per-instance analytics-ID authoring/write-back mechanism in the EDS model
——classes_story / story variant behaviorNot mapped (new in EDS)Net-new: automatic restructuring of flat content into date/tag/CTA/timestamp sub-elements has no source-system equivalent
——Automatic Brightcove/YouTube link detection in card bodyNot mapped (new in EDS)Net-new capability; the source component's cards were Content-Fragment text/image only, with no video-link parsing
Note: forms-product-grid-list, the manifest's second listed source, has no real .content.xml, _cq_dialog, HTL, or Sling Model anywhere in the source repository (only a static .php design-reference prototype under its ui/ folder) — no fields from it could be verified or mapped.

Cards Grid Component — Technical Details

What it does

The EDS cards-grid block renders a set of link cards (image + up to four lines of rich text, plus an optional prescribing-information marker) in a responsive grid. It consolidates five distinct source-system components that all solved the same "cards from a folder / cards in a row" problem in different ways: a Content-Fragment-driven flip card list (dynamic-card), a service-driven auto-populated card grid (grid and its near-duplicate product-grid), a manually-authored grid/list toggle view (grid-list-view), and a UI-kit-only prototype that never shipped as a real component (forms-product-grid-list).

Architecture — source system (five separate components)

dynamic-card        → extends core Content Fragment List; renders content-fragment-backed
                       flip cards with client-side tag filtering (DynamicCardTagFilterList)
grid                → GridModel computes column breakpoints + analytics string;
                       HTL renders an empty container; a client JS module fetches
                       ".storylist...json" and injects cards
product-grid        → same GridModel, same fetch-and-inject pattern, adds a
                       "feature card" and safety-card-offset option
grid-list-view       → GridListViewModel (see below) renders cards directly from
                       authored multifield data (cardItems), no service fetch;
                       supports grid ⟷ list view toggle and two masonry variants
forms-product-grid-list → PHP-only UI-kit style-guide prototype (ui/forms-product-grid-list.php);
                       never promoted to a real AEM component (no .content.xml,
                       no _cq_dialog, no Sling Model, no HTL)

Sling Models

GridModel.java (shared by grid and product-grid) — builds the fetch URL, the responsive column-breakpoint string, and an analytics payload for the "load more" button:

@Model(adaptables = {SlingHttpServletRequest.class})
public class GridModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private String dataColumns;
    private String analyticsString;
    private String encodedServiceUrl;

    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional
    private List<String> storyPositionDetails;   // repeated {pixelBreakpoint, columnsInRow} pairs

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        String resourcePath = resource.getPath();
        String pagePath = StringUtils.substringBefore(resourcePath, JCR_CONTENT);
        String extractedPath = StringUtils.substringAfter(resourcePath, JCR_CONTENT).replace("/", ".");
        encodedServiceUrl = pagePath + ".storylist" + extractedPath + ".json";
        ...
        if (storyPositionDetails != null && !storyPositionDetails.isEmpty()) {
            // builds "789-3,600-1" style dataColumns string consumed by client JS
        }
    }
}

Notable side effect: on author instances, init() writes a generated gridInteractionId back onto the resource via ModifiableValueMap + resource.getResourceResolver().commit() if one isn't already set — an unusual write-on-read pattern used purely to auto-populate the read-only "Analytics Interaction ID" dialog field.

DynamicCardTagFilterList.java (used by dynamic-card) generates a client-config object (scriptID) consumed by the tag-filtering UI; the card markup itself comes from the Core Components Content Fragment List model, not a custom model.

GridListViewModel.java / GridListCardsViewModel.java (used by grid-list-view) resolve the typeOfGridList ("grid-list" vs "masonry"), compute an id, and expose the authored cardItems multifield entries to the HTL for direct rendering (no remote fetch — this is the one variant of the five that renders authored content synchronously in HTL rather than injecting fetched JSON).

product-grid / forms-product-grid-list have no dedicated model beyond the shared GridModel (product-grid) or none at all (forms-product-grid-list, which has no Sling Model, no dialog, and no HTL — see Constraints).

Dialog — grid / product-grid (representative)

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Grid Identifier (Anchor ID)anchorIDtextfieldDeep-link anchor
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestextfieldFree-text class injection
Layoutlayoutselectmasonry / abbv-grid-standard, show/hide toggles load-more masonry fields
Feature Page PathfeaturePagePathpathbrowserproduct-grid only — page featured at top of grid
Parent Folder PathparentFolderPathpathbrowserFolder whose child pages become cards
Safety Card OffsetsafetyCardOffsetnumberfieldInterval for inserting "safety" cards among generated cards
(repeatable) At this pixel breakpoint / Columns in rowstoryPositionDetails → pixelBreakpoint, columnsInRowmultifield of numberfieldsBuilds responsive column map
Add a Load More buttonaddloadmorebuttoncheckbox
Cards Per PagecardsPerPagenumberfield0 = load all
Load More Button TextloadMoreButtonTexttextfield
Load More Icon / PositionloadMoreIcon, loadMoreIconPositionselect
View Details / Add Item / Remove Item / Clear Button TextviewDetailsText, addItemText, removeItemText, clearButtonTexttextfieldproduct-grid only — labels for a per-card comparison/selection UI
Section Title for Analytics / Localized Section TitlesectionTitleForAnalytics, localizedSectionTitleForAnalyticstextfield
Analytics Interaction IDgridInteractionIdtextfield (disabled)Auto-generated by GridModel on save
Analytics TitleanalyticsTitletextfield
Most Valued Action (+ Secondary)analyticsEnableMVAPrimary, analyticsEnableMVASecondarycheckbox
MVA Title / Type and CategorymvaTitleForAnalytics, mvaTypeAndCategorytextfield / select

dynamic-card's dialog is materially different — it configures a Content Fragment Model (modelPath), tag filters (tagNames), an orderBy/sortOrder, filter UI copy (filterlabeltext, clearfilterbuttontext, showresultsbuttontext), flip-card interaction copy (flipcardtoback, flipcardtofront, gotolandingpage), and maxItems. grid-list-view's dialog adds a manually authored cardItems multifield (each with cardTitle, cardDesc, cardImage, cardImageTitle, cardImageAltText, a nested CTA sub-model, and per-item journey/message-bucket analytics fields) plus a grid/list view toggle.

HTL — grid.html (representative fetch-and-inject pattern)

<sly data-sly-use.gridModel = com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.GridModel>
    <div class="abbv-grid ${properties.layout} ${properties.customCssClasses}"
         data-request-url="${gridModel.encodedServiceUrl}"
         data-layout="${properties.layout}"
         data-columns="${gridModel.dataColumns}"
         data-per-load="${properties.cardsPerPage}"
         data-enable-load-more="${properties.addloadmorebutton}"
         data-safety-offset="${properties.safetyCardOffset}">
        <div class="abbv-grid-content"><!-- Dynamically built cards injected here --></div>
        <div class="abbv-grid-load-more-container">
            <button data-digitaldata='${gridModel.analyticsString @context="unsafe"}'
                    class="abbv-grid-load-more abbv-button-secondary ${properties.loadMoreIcon} ${properties.loadMoreIconPosition}">${properties.loadMoreButtonText}</button>
        </div>
        <div class="loading"><div class="abbv-animation-loading"></div></div>
    </div>
</sly>

The markup is an empty shell; a client-side JS module (loaded via the grid-component.v1 clientlib) reads the data-request-url attribute, fetches JSON (a Sling .storylist...json selector servlet response), and builds card DOM at runtime. dynamic-card instead server-renders each card immediately via data-sly-list over Content Fragment items and a shared templates.html card template, with client JS doing only tag-filter/flip interactions. grid-list-view server-renders authored cardItems directly through an included grid-list-template.html, with alternate includes (grid-view-masonry.html, grid-view-masonry-load-more.html) selected by typeOfGridList.

CSS

.abbv-grid { }
.abbv-grid-content { }
.abbv-grid-load-more-container { }
.loading .abbv-animation-loading { }

Layout classes (masonry / abbv-grid-standard) and responsive column counts are driven by the data-columns attribute rather than static CSS breakpoints, so most of the actual grid math lives in the accompanying clientlib JS rather than in CSS media queries.

Important Constraints

  • grid and product-grid are two near-duplicate components sharing the same Sling Model, differing only by a feature-card option and a comparison-tray text set (product-grid) — a maintenance-doubling pattern the target implementation collapses into one block.
  • grid/product-grid depend on a live selector servlet (.storylist...json) at render time; if that endpoint is slow or down, the grid renders empty except for the loading spinner and a generic "Failed to load requested pages" message.
  • forms-product-grid-list is not a real, deployable AEM component — it exists only as a PHP-templated entry in the internal UI style-guide kit (ui/forms-product-grid-list.php, ui/variations.php) with no .content.xml, no dialog, no HTL, and no Sling Model. It documents an intended pattern but was never authorable on a real page.
  • dynamic-card's filtering and flip-card interactions are entirely client-rendered against a Content-Fragment-backed list; it has no relationship to the fetch-servlet pattern used by grid/product-grid, despite serving a similar "cards from structured content" purpose.
  • grid-list-view's cardItems multifield is the only one of the five where card content is authored directly in the dialog rather than derived from pages, fragments, or a service call — closest in spirit to the EDS block's authoring model.

EDS Migration

The target implementation collapses all five source patterns into one authoring model: cards are always authored directly on the page as Universal Editor block items (closest to the grid-list-view pattern), never fetched from a servlet or a Content Fragment list at runtime. Per-instance visual variant (CTA card, image-text card, article card, recipe card, etc.) is chosen via a single classes multiselect rather than via separate components.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldSource componentTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDgrid, product-grid, dynamic-cardtextfield—Not mappedUniversal Editor sections handle deep-linking at the section level; no per-block anchor-ID field in _cards-grid.json
customCssClassesalltextfieldclassesRenamedFree-text class field replaced by a constrained multiselect of the eleven supported card layouts (cards-grid-cta-card, cards-grid-image-text, etc.) — same purpose (styling hook), tighter authoring contract
layout (masonry / abbv-grid-standard)grid, product-gridselect—Not mappedMasonry layout is not implemented in the EDS block; only the flat grid variants under classes exist
parentFolderPath / featurePagePathgrid, product-gridpathbrowser—Not mappedEDS has no server-side folder-listing servlet; each card is an authored grid-card item, not derived from a page tree
safetyCardOffsetproduct-gridnumberfield—Not mappedAuto-injection of "safety cards" at a computed interval has no analog once cards are directly authored
storyPositionDetails (pixelBreakpoint / columnsInRow)grid, product-gridmultifield—Not mappedResponsive column counts are fixed by the chosen classes layout's CSS, not author-configurable per breakpoint
addloadmorebutton / cardsPerPage / loadMoreButtonText / loadMoreIcon / loadMoreIconPositiongrid, product-gridcheckbox / numberfield / textfield / select—Not mappedPagination assumed a service-backed card feed; with all cards authored up front, "load more" has no runtime data source to page through
viewDetailsText / addItemText / removeItemText / clearButtonTextproduct-gridtextfield—Not mappedComparison-tray ("add to compare") interaction is not part of the EDS block's scope
modelPathdynamic-cardselect (Content Fragment Model)—Not mappedNo Content Fragment binding in the EDS card model; content is authored inline as richtext, not sourced from a CF Model
tagNames / orderBy / sortOrder / maxItemsdynamic-cardtagfield / select / select / numberfield—Not mappedTag-driven filtering/sorting of a generated list has no equivalent — EDS cards are a fixed, authored sequence
filterlabeltext / clearfilterbuttontext / showresultsbuttontext / noresultslabeldynamic-cardtextfield—Not mappedNo client-side filter UI in the target grid-card block
flipcardtoback / flipcardtofront / gotolandingpagedynamic-cardtextfield—Not mappedFlip-card front/back interaction is not implemented; grid-card is a single-face link card
link (implicit page link on dynamic-card CF items)dynamic-cardn/a (CF item path)linkSame nameBoth resolve to a destination URL for the whole card
cardImagegrid-list-viewpathfield (DAM asset)imageRenamedreference component now, same purpose
cardTitle / cardDescgrid-list-viewtextfield / textarealine1 / line2RenamedFixed title/description fields replaced by generic richtext "line" slots; card visual role (eyebrow vs. heading vs. body) is determined by the chosen layout class, not the field name
cardImageTitle / cardImageAltTextgrid-list-viewtextfield—Not mappedAlt text is now sourced from the DAM asset itself via the reference field, not a separate authored property
ctaTitle / ctaURL / ctaIcon / ctaIconPosition / ctaTargetType / ctaLinkType / ctaModalIDgrid-list-viewnested CTA sub-modellink (implicit, from anchor wrapping the card)RenamedDedicated CTA sub-fields collapse into the card's own wrapping link — no separate icon/modal/target-type controls in grid-card
contentJourney / patientJourney / messageBucketgrid-list-viewtextfield—Not mappedJourney/message-bucket taxonomy fields are analytics metadata not present in the current field model
analyticsEnableMVA / analyticsMVAType / analyticsMVATier / analyticsMVAValuegrid-list-view, grid, product-gridcheckbox / select / textfield—Not mappedMVA analytics tagging is handled outside the block's authoring model in the target implementation (site-wide analytics layer), not per-field
enableLoadMoreMasonrygrid-list-viewcheckbox—Not mappedMasonry load-more variant not implemented
(line 3 / line 4 text, e.g. fine-print or indication copy)n/a — new in EDS—line3, line4Not mapped (EDS-native addition)No AEM source component exposed a 3rd/4th text slot per card; added to support prescribing-information / indication text blocks seen in the card-grid-item-risa-pri decoration logic

Cards Grid Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/cards-grid/. It delegates most of its rendering to a shared cross-block helper, renderBlock (from scripts/multi-theme.js), and layers on card-specific DOM restructuring only for one authored variant (cards-grid-cta-card).

File Structure

blocks/cards-grid/
├── cards-grid.js
├── cards-grid.css
├── _cards-grid.json
├── block-config.js
├── linzess/
│   ├── _cards-grid.css
│   ├── block-config.js
│   └── cards-grid.css / cards-grid.js
├── mavyret/
├── rinvoq/
├── rinvoq-hcp/
├── skyrizi-hcp/
└── venclexta/

Authoring Model (_cards-grid.json)

Two Universal Editor definitions: a container block cards-grid (single classes multiselect choosing one of eleven layout presets) and a filtered child component grid-card (an aem-content link, a reference image, and four richtext "line" fields). A filters entry restricts cards-grid's children to grid-card only:

{
  "id": "cards-grid",
  "fields": [
    {
      "component": "multiselect",
      "name": "classes",
      "label": "Container layout",
      "options": [
        { "name": "CTA Cards 1", "value": "cards-grid-cta-card" },
        { "name": "Image Text Cards", "value": "cards-grid-image-text" },
        { "name": "Recipe Cards", "value": "cards-grid-recipe-cards" }
        /* ... 11 options total ... */
      ]
    }
  ]
}

How the JS works (cards-grid.js)

decorate() skips all restructuring while inside the Universal Editor (isInUniversalEditor() checks window.hlx?.uePreview) so authors see raw authored markup, and otherwise delegates to the shared renderBlock, which loads block-config.js (global then brand-specific) and calls its exported decorate:

export default function decorate(block) {
  if (isInUniversalEditor()) return;
  renderBlock(block);
}

block-config.js wires that back to this file's own decorateBlock:

import { decorateBlock } from './cards-grid.js';
export default async function getBlockConfigs() {
  return { decorations: { decorate: async (ctx) => decorateBlock(ctx) } };
}

decorateBlock only acts on the cards-grid-cta-card layout: for each authored row it wraps the row's contents in an anchor (createWrapperATag), preserving the first real link's href/target (validated against SAFE_HREF_RE to block javascript:-style hrefs), then runs each of decorateLine1 through decorateLine4 to convert the row's four generic <div> "line" slots into semantic <span class="card-grid-item-line-N"> elements. decorateLine1 additionally splits on a literal | character to separate a short code (wrapped as card-grid-item-risa-pri — the "RISA/primary" marker referenced in the manifest, used for product/indication abbreviations) from trailing text. splitIndicationItems (used by lines 2 and 4) walks child nodes to pair each link with its following description text into distinct <a>/<p> pairs, stripping a leading dash separator. A separate helper, decodePlatformCSupTags, repairs <sup> tags that arrive HTML-entity-encoded from the upstream authoring platform, rebuilding them as real <sup> elements via a `TreeWalker` over text nodes (explicitly avoiding `innerHTML` re-parsing of authored text to limit injection risk).

CSS

.cards-grid {
  --cards-grid-neutral-light: var(--background-color);
  --cards-grid-neutral-dark: var(--color-black);
}
.cards-grid .demo-wrap {
  max-width: var(--content-max-width-narrow);
  margin: 0 auto;
  padding: var(--spacing-24) var(--spacing-16) var(--spacing-80);
}
.cards-grid .i-a::after {
  margin-left: var(--spacing-10);
}

The base file is intentionally small (14 lines) — nearly all real layout/skin CSS for the eleven classes variants lives in per-brand overrides (e.g. linzess/cards-grid.css, linzess/_cards-grid.css), loaded by the same brand-config resolution mechanism used by block-config.js.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (grid / product-grid / dynamic-card / grid-list-view)EDS (cards-grid)
Card sourceService fetch (grid/product-grid), Content Fragment list (dynamic-card), or authored multifield (grid-list-view) — three different mechanismsAlways directly authored Universal Editor block items (grid-card)
Variant selectionFour separate components, each with its own dialogOne block, variant chosen via a single classes multiselect
Pagination"Load more" against a paged service responseNone — full card set renders at once
FilteringTag-based client filter UI (dynamic-card only)Not implemented
Responsive columnsAuthor-configured pixel-breakpoint/column pairsFixed per-layout CSS, not per-instance configurable

Carousel Component — Technical Details

What it does

Wraps a set of authored parsys slots ("carousel items") in a third-party carousel library (Owl Carousel 2) with author-configurable slide count, autoplay, looping, previous/next buttons, dot indicators, an optional "lightbox" active-slide highlight, and an escape hatch for arbitrary Owl Carousel config options the dialog doesn't otherwise expose.

Architecture

Author sets carouselItems (N) → HTL renders N empty parsys slots (carouselitem0..N-1)
         ↓
CarouselModel.java (adapts Resource) builds:
  - carouselItem: ["carouselitem0", "carouselitem1", ...] list of child resource names
  - slideToStart = slideNumberToStart - 1 (0-based)
  - carouselConfig: comma-joined "key:value" string built from the owlCarouselConfig
    multifield (raw escape-hatch options merged into the Owl Carousel init object)
  - interactionId (auto-populated analytics ID, same self-writing pattern as GridModel)
         ↓
carousel.html renders:
  <div class="abbv-carousel owl-carousel owl-theme ..."
       data-config='{"items":N,"itemsMobile":N,"loop":bool,"nav":bool,"dots":bool,...}'
       data-options='{"lightbox":bool,"center":bool,"autoplay":bool,...${carouselConfig}}'>
    <div class="item" id="slideN" data-index="N">
      <sly data-sly-resource="carouselitemN" resourceType="wcm/foundation/components/parsys">
         ↓
clientlib JS initializes Owl Carousel against data-config/data-options

Sling Model — CarouselModel.java

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class CarouselModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENT_ID_PREFIX = "abbv-carousel";

    @Inject @Optional private int carouselItems;
    @Inject @Optional private int slideNumberToStart;
    @Inject @Optional private List<String> owlCarouselConfig;

    private List<String> carouselItem;
    private int slideToStart;
    private String carouselConfig = "";

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        carouselItem = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < carouselItems; i++) {
            carouselItem.add("carouselitem" + i);
        }
        slideToStart = slideNumberToStart - 1;
        if (owlCarouselConfig != null) {
            for (String owlCarouselConfigOption : owlCarouselConfig) {
                JSONObject optionJson = new JSONObject(owlCarouselConfigOption);
                String key = optionJson.getString("optionName");
                String val = optionJson.getString("optionValue");
                carouselConfig = carouselConfig + "," + key + ":" + val;
            }
        }
        if (!isPublish() && (StringUtils.isBlank(interactionId) || !interactionId.equals(getId()))) {
            setInteractionIdInComponentDialog();
        }
    }
}

Same write-on-read analytics-ID auto-population pattern seen in GridModel. The model does not build actual carousel item content — it only produces the list of relative child-resource names (carouselitem0, carouselitem1, ...); the HTL resolves each as a generic parsys so any component type can be dropped into a slide.

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor IDanchorIDtextfieldAlso documented as the cookie name for carousel state — required/unique if multiple carousels appear on one page
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextfield
Number of Slides in CarouselcarouselItemsnumberfield (2–50)Determines number of parsys slots rendered
Number of Slides to ShownumberOfSlidesToShownumberfield (1–20)Owl Carousel "items"
Number of Slides to Show (Mobile)numberOfSlidesToShowMobilenumberfield (1–20)Owl Carousel "itemsMobile"
Bypass Carousel on MobilebyPassCarouselOnMobilecheckboxSuppresses carousel wrapping (not just CSS hiding) on mobile
Slide Number to Start WithslideNumberToStartnumberfield (1–50)1-based in dialog, converted to 0-based slideToStart
Center slide is ActiveenableCenterSlideActivecheckboxOverrides start position to carousel center
Enable Auto PlayenableAutoPlaycheckbox
Autoplay Slide Advance Interval (ms)autoAdvanceTimenumberfieldDefault 3000ms
Enable Carousel LoopingenableLoopcheckbox
Enable Previous Next NavigationenablePrevNextcheckboxDefault checked
Enable Dots NavigationenableDotscheckboxDefault checked
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtextfield (disabled)Auto-populated
Enable LightboxenableLightBoxcheckbox (Advanced tab)Adds a "lightbox" class to the active slide for custom highlight styling
Disable Lightbox Until Slide ChangedelayLightBoxcheckbox (Advanced tab)
Activate Lightbox on Slide ClickactivateLightboxOnClickcheckbox (Advanced tab)
Enable Lightbox to Tab Component IntegrationtabComponentIntegrationClasstextfield (Advanced tab)CSS class of a separate Tab component whose panel syncs to the active slide index
Add Option Name and Value (repeatable)owlCarouselConfig → optionName, optionValuemultifield (Advanced tab)Raw escape hatch into any undocumented Owl Carousel option; explicitly documented in the dialog as "experimental and not fully supported"

HTL — carousel.html

<sly data-sly-use.carouselModel="${'com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.CarouselModel'}">
    <sly data-sly-test="${carouselModel.carouselItems}">
        <div class="abbv-carousel owl-carousel owl-theme ${properties.customCssClasses}"
             data-config='{"items":${properties.numberOfSlidesToShow || 1}, "itemsMobile":${properties.numberOfSlidesToShowMobile || 1},
                            "loop":${properties.enableLoop}, "nav":${properties.enablePrevNext}, "dots":${properties.enableDots},
                            "hideMobile":${properties.byPassCarouselOnMobile || false},
                            "startPosition":${carouselModel.slideToStart >= 0 ? carouselModel.slideToStart : 0}}'
             data-options='{"lightbox":${properties.enableLightBox || false}, ... ${carouselModel.carouselConfig}}'
             data-interactionid="${carouselModel.interactionIdFromDialog}">
            <sly data-sly-list.carouselItem="${carouselModel.carouselItem}">
                <div class="item" id="slide${carouselItemList.count}" data-index="${carouselItemList.count}">
                    <sly data-sly-resource="${@path=carouselItem, resourceType='wcm/foundation/components/parsys'}"></sly>
                </div>
            </sly>
        </div>
    </sly>
</sly>

Note the raw string concatenation of the escape-hatch carouselConfig directly onto the end of the JSON literal in data-options — a fragile but functional way to merge author-supplied key/value pairs into an otherwise-fixed JSON object without a server-side JSON builder.

CSS

The component's own ui/carousel.css is the vendored, unmodified Owl Carousel 2.3.4 library stylesheet (.owl-carousel, .owl-stage, .owl-item, etc.) — no organization-specific carousel CSS lives here; brand skinning is applied on top via the shared clientlib/utility classes and the customCssClasses field.

Important Constraints

  • Hard runtime dependency on the third-party Owl Carousel 2 JS/CSS library — the dialog's own help text points authors at the external Owl Carousel documentation rather than internal docs, since most of the actual behavior is configured by pass-through JSON rather than server logic.
  • The owlCarouselConfig escape hatch is explicitly documented as unsupported/experimental and can silently override any of the structured dialog fields above it, since raw key/value pairs are concatenated after the structured JSON with no validation.
  • Anchor ID doubles as a cookie name for persisting carousel state, so it must be unique per page if multiple carousels are present, or state will collide.
  • Carousel item content is unconstrained — any component type can be dropped into a slide's parsys, so slide-to-slide layout consistency is entirely an authoring discipline concern, not an enforced structure.

Carousel Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/carousel/: a dependency-free, native-DOM slide carousel (no Owl Carousel or any third-party carousel library) using an IntersectionObserver to track the active slide and CSS scroll-snap for the actual sliding motion.

File Structure

blocks/carousel/
├── carousel.js
├── carousel.css
├── _carousel.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── _carousel.css
│   ├── block-config.js
│   └── carousel.css
├── botox/
├── rinvoq/
└── rinvoq-hcp/

Authoring Model (_carousel.json)

Two definitions: the carousel container (a single free-text classes field) and a carousel-item child component (background reference image + alt text + a richtext content field), filtered so a carousel may only contain carousel-item children:

{
  "id": "carousel-item",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "reference", "name": "media_image", "label": "Background Image" },
    { "component": "text", "name": "media_imageAlt", "label": "Background Alt" },
    { "component": "richtext", "name": "content_text", "label": "Text" }
  ]
}

How the JS works (carousel.js)

decorate() assigns each carousel instance a numeric id (carousel-${carouselId}) and treats each direct child row as one slide via createSlide, which relabels the first column carousel-slide-image and the rest carousel-slide-content, and derives an aria-labelledby from the slide's own heading:

function createSlide(row, slideIndex, carouselId) {
  const slide = document.createElement('li');
  slide.dataset.slideIndex = slideIndex;
  slide.setAttribute('id', `carousel-${carouselId}-slide-${slideIndex}`);
  slide.classList.add('carousel-slide');
  row.querySelectorAll(':scope > div').forEach((column, colIdx) => {
    column.classList.add(`carousel-slide-${colIdx === 0 ? 'image' : 'content'}`);
    slide.append(column);
  });
  const labeledBy = slide.querySelector('h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6');
  if (labeledBy) slide.setAttribute('aria-labelledby', labeledBy.getAttribute('id'));
  return slide;
}

Active-slide ARIA/tabindex management is driven by scroll position rather than by button clicks alone — an IntersectionObserver at a 0.5 threshold calls updateActiveSlide whenever a slide becomes the majority-visible one:

const slideObserver = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
  entries.forEach((entry) => {
    if (entry.isIntersecting) updateActiveSlide(entry.target);
  });
}, { threshold: 0.5 });

updateActiveSlide sets aria-hidden on every non-active .carousel-slide and tabindex="-1" on their links, disables the matching indicator button, and re-enables the others. Previous/next buttons and indicator clicks both funnel through the exported showSlide(block, slideIndex, behavior), which resolves negative/overflow indices (wrap-around) and calls native scrollTo({ left, behavior }) — the actual sliding motion is delegated entirely to the browser's scroll-snap behavior, not JS-driven animation. Single-slide carousels skip indicator/nav-button creation and event binding entirely (isSingleSlide). At the end, decorate() best-effort calls the shared renderBlock for brand-config decoration, swallowing failures so a broken brand config doesn't break base carousel function.

CSS

Notable inline comment: /* Todo: This carousel block has not been developed yet. We will revisit during block development or enhancement. */ — the base CSS is functional (scroll-snap slides, circular nav buttons, dot indicators, responsive slide-content width at the 600px breakpoint) but is explicitly flagged in-source as provisional pending further design work. Brand folders (abbvie, botox, rinvoq, rinvoq-hcp) layer visual overrides via the same block-config.js brand-resolution mechanism as other blocks; the shared block-config.js itself ships with all decoration hooks commented out, meaning no brand-specific JS behavior is currently wired for carousel.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (carousel)EDS (carousel)
Slide engineOwl Carousel 2 (third-party library)Native CSS scroll-snap + IntersectionObserver, no library dependency
Slide content modelUnconstrained parsys — any component type per slideFixed carousel-item shape: one background image + one richtext block
Configurability~20 dialog fields plus a raw key/value escape hatch for arbitrary Owl Carousel optionsSingle free-text classes field; behavior (loop, autoplay, lightbox, tab integration) is not currently authorable
Active-slide trackingOwl Carousel's internal stateCustom IntersectionObserver-driven aria-hidden/tabindex management
Autoplay / looping / lightboxSupported via dialog checkboxesNot implemented

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtextfield—Not mappedNo per-block anchor field in _carousel.json; Universal Editor sections handle deep links at a higher level
customCssClassestextfieldclassesSame name/purpose (renamed)Free-text styling hook retained, field renamed from customCssClasses to classes
carouselItemsnumberfield—Not mappedEDS slide count is implicit — one carousel-item child per authored row, no separate count field to keep in sync
numberOfSlidesToShow / numberOfSlidesToShowMobilenumberfield—Not mappedEDS carousel always shows one slide at a time (scroll-snap, full-width slide); multi-up display is not implemented
byPassCarouselOnMobilecheckbox—Not mappedNo mobile bypass mode; carousel always renders as a carousel
slideNumberToStartnumberfield—Not mappedEDS always starts at slide 0; not authorable
enableCenterSlideActivecheckbox—Not mappedNo "centered slide" start mode
enableAutoPlay / autoAdvanceTimecheckbox / numberfield—Not mappedAutoplay is not implemented in carousel.js — navigation is manual (buttons, indicators, scroll) only
enableLoopcheckbox—Not mappedshowSlide does wrap indices internally, but this is unconditional JS behavior, not an authorable on/off toggle
enablePrevNextcheckbox—Not mappedPrev/next buttons always render for multi-slide carousels (unless isSingleSlide); not independently toggleable
enableDotscheckbox—Not mappedDot indicators always render for multi-slide carousels; not independently toggleable
interactionIdtextfield (disabled)—Not mappedNo per-block analytics-ID field; analytics instrumentation, if present, lives outside the block's authoring model
enableLightBox / delayLightBox / activateLightboxOnClickcheckbox—Not mappedNo lightbox/highlight-active-slide feature in the EDS carousel
tabComponentIntegrationClasstextfield—Not mappedNo equivalent tab-component sync feature
owlCarouselConfig (optionName/optionValue)multifield—Not mappedNo third-party carousel library in the target implementation, so there is no library config surface to pass through
(slide background image, implicit via parsys content)n/a — free-form slide contentmedia_image, media_imageAltNot mapped (EDS-native structured field)AEM slides had no fixed image field — content was whatever component authors dropped into the parsys; EDS defines an explicit background image slot per slide
(slide text, implicit via parsys content)n/a — free-form slide contentcontent_textNot mapped (EDS-native structured field)Same rationale — a single richtext slot replaces the AEM version's unconstrained nested-component slide body

Carousel Video Playlist Component — Technical Details

What it does

The manifest maps this EDS block to the source system's video-player component, specifically its "Playlist" player type with the "Carousel" playlist-type variant (playerType=bC11jvCtd, playlistType=carousel) — one of several rendering modes that a single, much larger video-player component supports (single video, 360-degree video, playlist-as-carousel, playlist-as-cards). It embeds a Brightcove video-js player alongside a scrollable list of other videos in the same Brightcove playlist, letting a visitor switch videos without leaving the page.

Architecture — source system

Author sets playerType="Playlist" (bC11jvCtd), playlistID, playlistType="carousel" (default)
         ↓
VideoModel.java resolves brightcoveAccountID (from an OSGi config service), sets
playlistEnabled=true, builds an "endCards" JSON blob and an analytics payload
         ↓
video-player.html dispatches by playerType/playlistType/videoID combination to one
of four included templates; for playlist+carousel it includes playlist-carousel.html
         ↓
playlist-carousel.html renders:
  <div class="abbv-video-player abbv-video-playlist abbv-playlist-type-carousel ...">
    <div class="abbv-video-playlist-controls abbv-video-playlist-carousel" data-playlist-for="...">
       up/down scroll buttons + <div class="vjs-playlist"> (Brightcove plugin renders items here)
    <div class="abbv-video-content-container">
       <video-js data-playlist-id="..." data-playlist-video-id="..." data-account="..." data-player="...">
         ↓
video-player.js (clientlib): loads the Brightcove player script once per playerId
(not per videoId — a documented fix for a Brightcove behavior where loading a script
for one player initializes ALL players sharing that playerId), then lets the
Brightcove SDK's own playlist plugin populate .vjs-playlist and drive next/prev

Sling Model — VideoModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {SlingHttpServletRequest.class})
public class VideoModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENTIDPREFIX = "abbv-video";

    @ValueMapValue(injectionStrategy = InjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
    @Named("playerType") @Default(values = "Mcp9TXMkPT")
    private String playerType;

    private String brightcoveAccountID = "";
    private boolean playlistEnabled = false;

    @PostConstruct
    public void init() {
        brightcoveAccountID = commonConfigService.getConfigPropertyVal("video.brightcove.account.id");
        if ("bC11jvCtd".equals(playerType)) {
            playlistEnabled = true;
        }
        if (!endCardFieldset.isEmpty()) {
            // builds {"slots":[{...on/off int conversion...}]} JSON for Brightcove end-card plugin
        }
        if (!isPublish() && (StringUtils.isBlank(interactionId) || !interactionId.equals(getId()))) {
            setInteractionIdInComponentDialog();
        }
        setLoadMoreAnalyticsData();
    }
}

Notable design choice: the player-type dropdown values in the dialog are opaque generated IDs (Mcp9TXMkPT = single video, bC11jvCtd = playlist, z6mAcMv1F = 360-degree video) rather than readable strings — the Java model and every template branch on these exact opaque values. The Brightcove account ID is not authored per-instance; it comes from a shared OSGi CommonConfigServiceImpl config property (video.brightcove.account.id), meaning a single AEM instance is effectively locked to one Brightcove account for all videos.

Dialog (fields relevant to the playlist/carousel path)

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor IDanchorIDtextfield
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextfield
Player TypeplayerTypeselect (show/hide driver)Video / Playlist (bC11jvCtd) / 360 degree video — opaque values, not readable strings
Brightcove Playlist IDplaylistIDtextfieldShown only when Player Type = Playlist
Default Playlist Video (Optional)defaultVideoIDtextfieldMust be a member of the playlist
Playlist TypeplaylistTypeselectCarousel (default, this component) or Cards
Playlist LayoutplaylistLayoutselect (datasource-driven)Position of the playlist relative to the currently-playing video
Display Playlist MetadatadisplayPlaylistMetadatacheckboxShows title/description/time per playlist item
Video Content LayoutcontentLayoutselect (datasource-driven)Position of title/description/chapters/transcript relative to the player
Autoplay the VideoautoplayVideocheckbox
Enable Social Sharing for the VideoenableSocialSharecheckbox
Social Share Button TextsocialShareButtonTexttextfieldDefault "Share"
Display Video ChaptersdisplayVideoChapterscheckboxRequires chapters authored in Brightcove itself
Enable Recommended VideoenableRecommendedVideocheckboxDocumented as single/360 video only, not applicable to playlists

The dialog also carries transcript fields (transcriptLabel, transcriptIcon, transcriptIconPosition, transcriptLinkBehaviour, transcriptModalID), an endCardFieldset multifield (on/off second offsets for a Brightcove end-screen plugin), and analytics fields (selectTitle, localizedTitle, videoName, MVA fields) shared across all player-type variants of this one component.

HTL — playlist-carousel.html

<template data-sly-template.carouselVideo="${@ videomodel,displayMetaData}">
    <div class="abbv-video-player abbv-video-playlist abbv-playlist-type-carousel
                ${displayMetaData ? 'abbv-playlist-show-meta-data' : ''} ${properties.playlistLayout} ${properties.customCssClasses}"
         data-autoplay="${properties.autoplayVideo ? properties.autoplayVideo : 'false'}"
         data-endcard='${videoModel.endCards @context="unsafe"}'
         data-digitaldata='${videoModel.analyticsString @context="unsafe"}'>
        <div class="abbv-video-playlist-controls abbv-video-playlist-carousel" data-playlist-for="${videoModel.id}">
            <button data-scroll-direction="up" aria-label="Previous Video">...</button>
            <div class="abbv-playlist-container abbv-scroll-container">
                <div class="vjs-playlist"></div>
            </div>
            <button data-scroll-direction="down" aria-label="Next Video">...</button>
        </div>
        <div class="abbv-video-content-container ${properties.contentLayout}">
            <div class="abbv-video-container aspect-ratio-16-9">
                <video-js id="${videoModel.id}" data-playlist-id="${properties.playlistID}"
                          data-playlist-video-id="${properties.defaultVideoID}"
                          data-account="${videoModel.brightcoveAccountID}"
                          data-player="${properties.playerType}" data-embed="default" controls>
                </video-js>
            </div>
            <sly data-sly-call="${transcriptRenderer.transcript @ properties=properties}"/>
        </div>
    </div>
</template>

The .vjs-playlist container is left empty in HTL — its contents (thumbnails, titles) are generated entirely client-side by Brightcove's own playlist UI plugin once the player script loads and reads the playlist ID, not by AEM.

CSS

ui/video-player.css is scoped under organization-specific classes (.abbv-video-player, .abbv-video-playlist, .abbv-playlist-type-carousel, .abbv-video-playlist-controls) and layers presentation on top of Brightcove's own .vjs-playlist/video-js DOM, which it does not fully control since that markup is generated by the third-party SDK at runtime.

Important Constraints

  • Brightcove account ID is a single shared OSGi config value, not authored per-component — every video/playlist on the entire AEM instance uses the same account.
  • Player-script loading is explicitly documented as fragile in video-player.js: "loading a BC script only initializes the current player" was found to be wrong — a script load for one playerId actually initializes every player sharing that playerId on the page, and loadedmetadata does not fire reliably beyond the first player. The clientlib works around this by loading each distinct playerId's script only once per page.
  • The playlist thumbnail/name/metadata UI itself is a Brightcove SDK plugin, not AEM-rendered — the HTL only provides an empty .vjs-playlist mount point, so its structure/accessibility is outside this component's control.
  • Player-type dialog values are opaque generated strings (bC11jvCtd, etc.) rather than descriptive constants, making the HTL/Java branching logic hard to read without cross-referencing the dialog.

EDS Migration

The target implementation replaces the single monolithic four-mode video-player component with a dedicated carousel-video-playlist block, scoped specifically to the playlist use case (the single-video and 360-video modes are handled by separate blocks, out of this batch's scope). It supports two authoring paths rather than one: a Brightcove-playlist-ID-driven mode (closest analog to the source component) and a fully authored-item fallback mode where individual video entries (with patient-testimonial-oriented fields) are authored directly as child block items — a capability the source component's playlist mode did not have.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtextfield—Not mappedNo per-block anchor field in _carousel-video-playlist.json
customCssClassestextfieldclassesRenamedFree-text class field replaced by a constrained select of five named layouts (Cards Grid, Featured, Bottom, Left/Right Sidebar)
playerTypeselect (opaque values)—Not mappedEDS has a dedicated block per video mode; no in-block mode switch is needed since this block is playlist-only
playlistIDtextfieldplaylistIdSame purpose (renamed casing)Brightcove playlist ID, same concept
defaultVideoIDtextfield—Not mappedNo "start on a specific playlist video" override; playback always starts on the first playlist item or first authored item
playlistType (carousel vs. cards)selectclasses (Cards Grid option) / block identityRenamed / restructuredSource used one component with a type switch; EDS uses this dedicated block for the carousel/scrolling layouts and a card-grid layout option within the same classes field, rather than a separate "cards" mode
playlistLayoutselect (datasource)classes (Bottom / Left Sidebar / Right Sidebar options)RenamedPosition-relative-to-player concept preserved, values collapsed into the single layout selector
displayPlaylistMetadatacheckbox—Not mappedMetadata (title) display is unconditional in buildThumbnailCard/cvp-playlist-item-title, not a toggle
contentLayoutselect (datasource)—Not mappedPlayer-area content position is fixed by CSS per classes layout, not independently authorable
autoplayVideocheckbox—Not mappedPlayback in the EDS block always starts on user interaction (click a playlist card) or once the Brightcove SDK is ready; no autoplay-on-load toggle
enableSocialShare / socialShareButtonTextcheckbox / textfield—Not mappedNo social-share affordance in the EDS playlist UI
displayVideoChapterscheckbox—Not mappedNo chapters feature implemented
enableRecommendedVideocheckbox—Not mappedDocumented as single/360-video-only on the source side already; no equivalent for playlists in either system
(Brightcove account ID, from shared OSGi config)n/a — server config, not a dialog fieldaccountIdNot mapped (promoted from config to authored field)EDS authors the account ID directly per block instance rather than relying on one shared instance-wide config value — removes the "one account for the whole site" constraint
(playerId — implicit in HTL's data-player="${properties.playerType}", which is really the mode selector, not a Brightcove player ID)n/aplayerIdNot mapped (new explicit field)Source template conflated "player type" (single/playlist/360) with the data-player attribute; EDS separates the concept and lets authors set the actual Brightcove Player ID directly
——sectionHeading, sectionDescriptionNot mapped (EDS-native addition)No equivalent authored heading/description above the player in the source component's playlist-carousel template
——maxVisibleNot mapped (EDS-native addition)Caps the number of visible playlist cards; no equivalent limit in the source component (Brightcove SDK renders its full playlist)
——playMode (modal vs. inline)Not mapped (EDS-native addition)Source playlist-carousel always plays inline; no modal/overlay playback option existed
(playlist item title/thumbnail — generated by Brightcove SDK at runtime, not authored in AEM)n/a — Brightcove-managed metadatavideoId, thumbnail, title, transcriptNot mapped (moved from Brightcove-managed to AEM/EDS-authored, for the fallback authored-items mode only)The EDS block's Mode B (no Brightcove playlist ID) lets authors define each playlist item directly, something the source's carousel-playlist template had no path for — it only ever consumed Brightcove's own playlist plugin data
——patientName, condition, quoteNot mapped (EDS-native addition)Patient-testimonial-oriented fields with no analog in the source video component's generic playlist model

Carousel Video Playlist Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/carousel-video-playlist/, including lazy Brightcove SDK loading (deferred by both a fixed timeout and an IntersectionObserver), per-account/per-player script caching, five layout variants, two content-source modes (Brightcove playlist ID vs. authored items), and full keyboard-accessible tab semantics on the thumbnail rail.

File Structure

blocks/carousel-video-playlist/
├── carousel-video-playlist.js
├── carousel-video-playlist.css
├── _carousel-video-playlist.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
├── botox/
├── linzess/           (also carries its own _carousel-video-playlist.json override)
├── mavyret/
├── rinvoq/
├── rinvoq-hcp/
├── skyrizi-hcp/
└── venclexta/
    each with: _carousel-video-playlist.css, block-config.js, carousel-video-playlist.css

Authoring Model (_carousel-video-playlist.json)

Container fields include a classes layout select (Cards Grid default, Featured, Bottom, Left/Right Sidebar), a playMode select (modal overlay vs. inline), sectionHeading/sectionDescription, the three Brightcove identifiers (accountId, playlistId, playerId), and maxVisible. The filtered child, carousel-video-playlist-item, carries videoId, a reference thumbnail, title, transcript richtext, and three testimonial-specific fields (patientName, condition, quote).

How the JS works (carousel-video-playlist.js)

Brightcove player scripts are cached per account/player combination in a module-level object so repeated block instances on one page don't re-fetch the SDK:

const bcScripts = {};
function loadBrightcoveScript(account, player) {
  const key = `${account}/${player}_default`;
  if (!bcScripts[key]) {
    bcScripts[key] = new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
      const script = document.createElement('script');
      script.src = `https://players.brightcove.net/${key}/index.min.js`;
      script.onload = resolve;
      script.onerror = reject;
      document.head.append(script);
    });
  }
  return bcScripts[key];
}

readBlockConfig is written to tolerate two different historical authoring shapes: it first checks whether the first configuration cell looks like a long numeric Brightcove ID (a signal of "legacy content authored without the layout/heading fields") and, if so, infers field positions heuristically; otherwise it reads the current fixed field order (classes, sectionHeading, sectionDescription, accountId, playlistId, playerId, maxVisible). decorateBlock then branches into two content-source modes:

  • Mode A — Brightcove-driven: if both accountId and playlistId are present, the block clears its authored DOM, shows a loading placeholder, and defers SDK load behind both a 3-second setTimeout and an IntersectionObserver (so an off-screen block never loads Brightcove at all until scrolled near). Once the player is ready, it polls bcPlayer.playlist() (up to 20 attempts, 500ms apart) until Brightcove's own playlist array is populated, then builds the thumbnail rail from that data and wires clicks to bcPlayer.playlist.currentItem(idx).
  • Mode B — Authored items: if no playlist ID is configured, parsePlaylistItems reads each child row's videoId/thumbnail/title/transcript cells directly, and initSinglePlayer boots a single-video Brightcove player for the first item; clicking another thumbnail calls switchVideo, which uses bcPlayer.catalog.getVideo(videoId, …) + catalog.load(video) to swap the loaded video without re-initializing the player.

The thumbnail rail (renderPlaylist) is built as an ARIA role="tablist"/role="tab" widget with full arrow-key/Home/End keyboard navigation, and captions/subtitles are force-disabled per player unless enableCaptions is set, via this.textTracks() iteration inside the Brightcove ready() callback.

CSS

Defines an extensive local custom-property palette scoped to .carousel-video-playlist (colors, spacing, thumbnail/sidebar dimensions, font sizes) that falls back to the shared theme tokens (var(--background-color, #fff), var(--link-color, #0057b8), etc.), so brand folders can override the palette without touching structural CSS.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (video-player, playlist+carousel mode)EDS (carousel-video-playlist)
Brightcove account scopeOne shared account for the whole AEM instance (OSGi config)Authored per block instance (accountId field)
Content sourceAlways Brightcove's own playlist plugin dataBrightcove playlist ID (Mode A) or fully authored items (Mode B)
SDK load timingLoaded on page render, deduped only by playerIdDeferred via 3s timeout + IntersectionObserver, deduped by account/player key
Playlist UIRendered entirely by the Brightcove SDK plugin into an empty .vjs-playlist divRendered by the block itself as an accessible tablist, independent of any Brightcove UI plugin
Layout optionsDatasource-driven playlistLayout (values not visible without checking the datasource servlet)Five named layouts in classes: Cards Grid, Featured, Bottom, Left Sidebar, Right Sidebar

Chart Component — Technical Details

What it does

The manifest groups four distinct source components under this one EDS block, and they turn out to use two genuinely different rendering technologies for the same conceptual job (drawing a data chart): charts renders a Chart.js chart onto a <canvas> via a server-templated inline <script> block; chart-bar, chart-line, and chart-pie instead hand-draw the chart as raw SVG (<rect>/<text> elements positioned by CSS/JS from author-supplied numeric data), with no Chart.js dependency at all.

Architecture — source system (two families)

Family A — charts (Chart.js/canvas):
  Author picks chartType (bar/line/pie/doughnut), authors a `datasets` multifield
  (drugLabel, data, backgroundColor, borderColor, borderWidth per series)
         ↓
  ChartsModel.java parses each dataset JSON blob into a ChartsListBean,
  splits comma-separated color lists into quoted arrays
         ↓
  charts.html renders <canvas id="${chartsModel.id}">, then dataset.html
  emits inline <script> with an embedded `new Chart(...)` call whose config
  object is built entirely server-side by string substitution against
  template.html's per-chart-type HTL fragments (bar/line/circle/advanced)
         ↓
  Chart.js (loaded via the commom-elements-chartjs-plugin.v1 clientlib) executes
  the inlined config against the canvas at page load

Family B — chart-bar / chart-line / chart-pie (hand-drawn SVG):
  Author authors axisDetails, chartLabels, barGroupLabels, barDetails,
  legendsDetails multifields (each a small JSON blob with position/label/value)
         ↓
  BarChartModel.java (LineChartModel/PieChartModel are structural siblings)
  parses each multifield's JSON entries into typed Bean lists, builds
  comma-joined "axis config" strings consumed by client JS as data attributes
         ↓
  chart-bar.html renders two overlaid <svg> elements (a static "frame" axis/grid
  SVG and a "data" SVG holding one <g class="data-bar"> per bar/point) plus an
  optional <details> "Barchart Details" fallback data table
         ↓
  clientlib JS reads the data-* attributes (chart width/height, axis min/max/step,
  bar type/grouping/gap) and draws bar <rect>/<text> geometry directly into the SVG

Sling Models

ChartsModel.java (Family A) — parses the datasets multifield into ChartsListBeans and reformats comma-separated color strings into JS-array-literal-ready quoted strings:

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class})
public class ChartsModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    @Inject @Optional private List<String> datasets;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        if (datasets != null && !datasets.isEmpty()) {
            chartListBeans = new ArrayList<>();
            for (int i = 0; i < datasets.size(); i++) {
                chartsDetailsBean(datasets.get(i), i);
            }
        }
    }
    private String colorSpliter(JsonObject obj, String key) {
        String[] str = obj.get(key).getAsString().split(",");
        StringBuilder s = new StringBuilder();
        for (String o : str) s.append(String.format("'%s',", o));
        return s.substring(0, s.length() - 1);
    }
}

BarChartModel.java (Family B) — five separate multifields (hAxisLabels, axisDetails, chartLabels, barGroupLabels, barDetails, legendsDetails), each parsed independently from raw JSON strings into a shared BarChartBean type, plus the same write-on-read analytics-interaction-ID pattern seen in GridModel/CarouselModel/VideoModel:

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class})
public class BarChartModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    @Inject @Optional private List<String> hAxisLabels;
    @Inject @Optional private List<String> axisDetails;
    @Inject @Optional private List<String> barDetails;
    // ...

    private void getBarDetails() {
        barDetailsBeans = new ArrayList<>();
        for (String raw : barDetails) {
            BarChartBean bean = new BarChartBean();
            JsonObject obj = gson.fromJson(raw, JsonObject.class);
            // manually copies barCustomCssClasses / barLabel / barDataVal /
            // barValueLabel / positionLabelInsideBar into the bean
            barDetailsBeans.add(bean);
        }
    }
}

LineChartModel.java and PieChartModel.java are separate classes with the same structural shape as BarChartModel.java (each family member repeats the parse-JSON-multifield-into-Bean pattern independently rather than sharing a common base beyond AbstractComponentModel).

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeComponent family
Custom CSS Classes / Anchor IDcustomCssClasses, anchorIDtextfieldBoth
Chart TypechartTypeselect (bar/line/pie/doughnut)charts
Y-Axis LabelyaxisLabeltextfieldcharts
X-Axis LabelsxaxisLabelstextfield (comma list)charts
Circle Drug Label / Data / Background / Border Color / WidthcircleDrugLabel, circleData, circleBackgroundColor, circleBorderColor, circleBorderWidthtextfieldcharts (pie/doughnut path)
Datasets (repeatable: Drug Label, Data, Background/Border Color, Border Width)datasets → drugLabel, data, backgroundColor, borderColor, borderWidthmultifieldcharts (bar/line path)
Chart Object (raw override)chartObjecttextfieldcharts — bypasses all structured fields for a fully custom Chart.js config, per dataset.html's "advanced" branch
Chart Title / Legend TitlechartTitle, legendTitletextfieldchart-bar (and structurally chart-line/chart-pie)
Chart Width / HeightchartWidth, chartHeightnumberfieldchart-bar
Enable Animations / Animate Axis / Animate BarsenableAnimations, animateAxis, animateBarscheckbox / textfieldchart-bar
Show Overlay Contentshowoverlaycontentcheckboxchart-bar — enables a nested parsys overlay panel on the chart
Enable Summary / Summary Titleenablesummary, summarytitlecheckbox / textfieldchart-bar — controls the fallback <details> data table
Vertical/Horizontal Axis Min/Max/StepverticalAxisMin/Max/Step, horizontalAxisMin/Max/Stepnumberfieldchart-bar
Grid Lines OrientationgridLinesOrientationselectchart-bar
Axis Details (repeatable: orientation, position, position type, axis type, label type)axisDetails → axisOrientation, positionVal, positionValType, axisType, labelTypemultifieldchart-bar
H-Axis Labels Position / Labels (repeatable)hAxisLabelsPosition, hAxisLabels → hLabelTexttextfield / multifieldchart-bar
Chart Labels (repeatable: text, axis orientation, position)chartLabels → labelText, axisOrientationLabel, labelPositionmultifieldchart-bar
Top Label / Bottom Labeltoplabel, bottomlabeltextfield (rendered @context='unsafe')chart-bar
Bar Group Labels (repeatable)barGroupLabels → groupLabelmultifieldchart-bar
Bar Type / Data Grouping / Bar GapbarType, dataGrouping, barGapselect / textfield / numberfieldchart-bar
Bar Details (repeatable: custom CSS, label, data value, value label, inside-bar position)barDetails → barCustomCssClasses, barLabel, barDataVal, barValueLabel, positionLabelInsideBarmultifieldchart-bar
Legends Details (repeatable: custom CSS, text)legendsDetails → legendCustomCssClasses, legendTextmultifieldchart-bar
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtextfieldchart-bar

HTL — two representative fragments

<!-- charts.html (Family A — Chart.js) -->
<canvas data-sly-use.chartsModel="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.ChartsModel"
        id="${chartsModel.id}" width="1180" height="590" class="chartjs-render-monitor"></canvas>
<script data-sly-include="dataset.html"></script>

<!-- dataset.html excerpt: server-templates the Chart.js constructor call inline -->
new Chart(document.getElementById('${chartsModel.id}'), {
    type:'${properties.chartType}',
    <sly data-sly-test="${properties.chartType == 'bar'}">
        <sly data-sly-call="${bar.bar @ chartType=chartType}"/>
    </sly>
    , options: { responsive: !0, ... }
});

<!-- chart-bar.html (Family B — hand-drawn SVG) -->
<svg class="abbv-chart-base" viewBox="0 0 ${properties.chartWidth} ${properties.chartHeight}"></svg>
<svg class="abbv-chart-data abbv-bar-chart ${properties.barType}" data-grouping="${properties.dataGrouping}"
     data-bar-gap="${properties.barGap}" viewBox="0 0 ${properties.chartWidth} ${properties.chartHeight}">
    <sly data-sly-list.barDetails="${barChartModel.barDetailsBeans}">
        <g class="data-bar ${barDetails.barCustomCssClasses}">
            <rect class="bar-draw" data-value="${barDetails.barDataVal}"></rect>
            <text class="bar-value-label ${barDetails.positionLabelInsideBar}">${barDetails.barValueLabel}</text>
            <text class="bar-label">${barDetails.barLabel}</text>
        </g>
    </sly>
</svg>

Both families independently render an accessible fallback table for screen-reader/no-JS users: chart-bar.html emits a <details class="barchart-details"> block with a real <table> when enablesummary is checked.

CSS

Family A's charts.css is minimal (sizing/wrapper rules around the canvas); Family B's chart-bar.css carries the bulk of the actual chart's visual language, since bar geometry itself is computed and drawn by JS/SVG rather than styled declaratively — CSS there mostly governs label typography, legend markers, and the loading-spinner overlay.

Important Constraints

  • Two structurally unrelated implementations exist for "a chart" in the source system — Family A depends on Chart.js and server-templated JS construction; Family B hand-draws SVG bars/lines/slices with no external chart library. They share no code, no Sling Model base beyond AbstractComponentModel, and no dialog structure.
  • charts' chartObject raw-override field lets an author bypass the entire structured dialog and inject an arbitrary Chart.js config object server-side — a broad but powerful escape hatch with no validation.
  • Family B's toplabel/bottomlabel/barLabel/barValueLabel are rendered with @context='unsafe', i.e. raw HTML injection from authored text — acceptable only because dialog access is author-trusted, but notably more permissive than the escaped (@context='html') rendering used for chartTitle in the same template.
  • Family B splits chart configuration across six independent multifields (axisDetails, hAxisLabels, chartLabels, barGroupLabels, barDetails, legendsDetails) that must be authored in a mutually consistent order/count for the chart to render coherently — there is no cross-field validation tying bar count to label count.

EDS Migration

The target implementation unifies both source families into a single Chart.js-based block (closer in spirit to Family A, but with the item-authoring model of a normal Universal Editor block rather than raw JSON-blob multifields). Bar/line/pie/doughnut are one block with a type selector, chart geometry is entirely delegated to Chart.js (no hand-drawn SVG path is retained), and each data point is authored as a discrete chart-item child rather than a JSON string inside a multifield.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldSource componentTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
chartTypechartsselectclasses_typeRenamedSame four values (bar default/line/pie/doughnut), field renamed
customCssClassesboth familiestextfieldclassesRenamed / restructuredFree-text class field replaced by a constrained multiselect of three specific display options (animate, show-legend, horizontal)
anchorIDboth familiestextfieldanchorIdSame name (casing)
yaxisLabel / xaxisLabelschartstextfieldyAxisLabel / xAxisLabelSame purpose (renamed casing)xaxisLabels was a comma-separated label list for discrete categories; xAxisLabel in EDS is a single axis title — category labels now come from each chart-item's own label field instead
datasets (drugLabel/data/backgroundColor/borderColor/borderWidth JSON blobs)chartsmultifieldchart-item fields: dataset, value, colorRenamed/restructuredA single JSON-blob multifield becomes discrete authored child components; per-item border width/style is not carried over — EDS uses one fixed 2px border via buildChartConfig
chartObjectchartstextfield (raw override)—Not mappedNo raw Chart.js config escape hatch in the EDS field model; all config is derived from structured fields
circleDrugLabel / circleData / circleBackgroundColor / circleBorderColor / circleBorderWidthchartstextfieldchart-item fields (label, value, color)Renamed/restructuredPie/doughnut-specific field set collapses into the same generic chart-item shape used by bar/line
chartTitle / legendTitlechart-bar/line/pietextfieldchartTitle / legendTitleSame name
chartWidth / chartHeightchart-barnumberfield—Not mappedChart.js canvas is responsive/CSS-sized (.chart-canvas-wrap canvas { width: 100% !important }); no author-set pixel dimensions
enableAnimations / animateAxis / animateBarschart-barcheckbox / textfieldclasses ("Animate on enter" option)Renamed/simplifiedThree granular animation controls collapse into one on/off toggle passed straight to Chart.js's animation: { duration: 800 }
showoverlaycontentchart-barcheckbox—Not mappedNo nested-parsys overlay panel concept in the EDS chart block
enablesummary / summarytitlechart-barcheckbox / textfieldaccessibilitySummaryRenamed/simplifiedThe accessible data table is always rendered in EDS (buildA11yTable runs unconditionally once there is data); only its summary label text is authorable, not whether it appears
verticalAxisMin/Max/Stepchart-barnumberfieldyAxisMin / yAxisMax / yAxisStepSame purpose (renamed)
horizontalAxisMin/Max/Stepchart-barnumberfield—Not mappedX-axis in Chart.js is category-based (one tick per label), not a numeric min/max/step range
gridLinesOrientationchart-barselect—Not mappedGrid line rendering is delegated entirely to Chart.js defaults; not independently configurable
axisDetails, hAxisLabels/hAxisLabelsPosition, chartLabelschart-barmultifield—Not mappedAxis label placement/orientation is fixed by Chart.js's own axis rendering; the source system's manual axis-label positioning system has no equivalent
toplabel / bottomlabelchart-bartextfield (unsafe HTML)—Not mappedNo above/below free-HTML label slots in the EDS chart shell
barGroupLabelschart-barmultifield—Not mapped (superseded)Multi-series bar grouping in EDS is inferred automatically from each chart-item's dataset field via groupByDataset, not manually labeled per group
barType / dataGrouping / barGapchart-barselect / textfield / numberfieldclasses ("Horizontal bars" option)Renamed/simplifiedOnly the horizontal/vertical orientation is preserved as an authorable option; grouping and gap are Chart.js defaults
barDetails (barCustomCssClasses/barLabel/barDataVal/barValueLabel/positionLabelInsideBar)chart-barmultifieldchart-item fields: label, value, valueLabel, colorRenamed/restructuredPer-bar custom CSS class and inside-bar label positioning are not carried over — value-label placement is a fixed Chart.js tooltip callback rather than an in-bar text position choice
legendsDetails (legendCustomCssClasses/legendText)chart-barmultifield—Not mapped (superseded)Legend entries are generated automatically by Chart.js from dataset/series names (groups.map(([name]) ...)) rather than manually authored
interactionIdchart-bartextfieldanalyticsInteractionIdSame purpose (renamed)Pushed to window.dataLayer on canvas click in setupAnalytics, vs. a custom digitalData object in AEM

Chart Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/chart/: a single Chart.js-based implementation supporting bar, line, pie, and doughnut types, lazy-loading the Chart.js library from a CDN only once the block scrolls into view, with an always-present accessible data table fallback.

File Structure

blocks/chart/
├── chart.js
├── chart.css
├── _chart.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── _chart.css
│   ├── block-config.js
│   └── chart.css
├── botox/
└── rinvoq/

Authoring Model (_chart.json)

Container fields are grouped into three Universal Editor tabs — Overview (classes_type, classes, chartTitle, legendTitle), Axis (yAxisLabel, xAxisLabel, yAxisMin/Max/Step), and Accessibility & Analytics (accessibilitySummary, analyticsInteractionId, anchorId). The filtered child chart-item carries label, value, valueLabel, dataset, and color.

How the JS works (chart.js)

Chart.js is fetched from a CDN and cached in a module-level promise so multiple chart blocks on one page share a single load:

const CHARTJS_CDN = 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js@4/dist/chart.umd.min.js';
let chartJSPromise = null;
function loadChartJS() {
  if (window.Chart) return Promise.resolve(window.Chart);
  if (!chartJSPromise) {
    chartJSPromise = new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
      const s = document.createElement('script');
      s.src = CHARTJS_CDN;
      s.onload = () => resolve(window.Chart);
      s.onerror = reject;
      document.head.append(s);
    });
  }
  return chartJSPromise;
}

readConfig reads block-level fields positionally from the first ten rows (a documented, fixed field-order contract — see the in-source comment enumerating CFG.CHART_TITLE through CFG.ANCHOR_ID), then readItems reads each remaining row as one chart-item. groupByDataset buckets items by their dataset name (falling back to a single implicit group) to decide whether the chart is single- or multi-series. buildChartConfig branches on pie/doughnut vs. bar/line: pie/doughnut builds one dataset from all items with per-slice palette colors; bar/line builds one Chart.js dataset per named series, mapping each series' values onto a shared, de-duplicated label axis ([...new Set(items.map((d) => d.label))]). A small fixed PALETTE of eight CSS-variable-backed colors is cycled through by index unless an item supplies its own color override. The chart itself is only constructed once an IntersectionObserver reports the block visible:

const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
  if (!entries.some((e) => e.isIntersecting)) return;
  observer.disconnect();
  loadChartJS().then(() => {
    new window.Chart(canvas, buildChartConfig(type, items, cfg, block));
    setupAnalytics(canvas, cfg, type);
  });
});
observer.observe(block);

buildA11yTable always renders a <details>/<table> fallback (multi-column when the chart has named series, two-column otherwise) regardless of whether Chart.js has loaded yet, giving screen-reader and no-JS users equivalent data immediately. setupAnalytics only wires a click listener onto the canvas (pushing a chart_interaction event to window.dataLayer) when analyticsInteractionId is authored.

CSS

.chart-canvas-wrap canvas {
  display: block;
  width: 100% !important;
  height: auto !important;
  max-height: var(--chart-max-height, 480px);
}

Chart colors are theme-variable-backed with hard-coded fallbacks (e.g. var(--chart-color-0, #003087)), so brand folders (abbvie, botox, rinvoq) can override the palette purely via CSS custom properties without touching chart.js.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (charts / chart-bar / chart-line / chart-pie)EDS (chart)
Rendering technologyTwo unrelated implementations: Chart.js+canvas (charts) and hand-drawn SVG (chart-bar/line/pie)One implementation, always Chart.js+canvas
Chart.js load timingLoaded unconditionally via clientlib on every page with a charts instanceDeferred until the block is scrolled into view (IntersectionObserver)
Data authoringJSON blobs inside multifields (datasets, barDetails, etc.), parsed server-sideDiscrete chart-item child components with named fields
Multi-series groupingManually authored group/legend labels (chart-bar)Inferred automatically from each item's dataset field
Accessible fallback tableOptional, author-toggled (chart-bar's enablesummary)Always rendered once there is data
Raw config escape hatchchartObject field allows a fully custom Chart.js config (charts)Not available — all config derives from structured fields

Clinical Data Panel Component — Technical Details

What it does

Displays a clinical-trial data figure (a chart rendered as an image, not a live data visualization) with responsive desktop/tablet/mobile source images, an optional title, footnotes (p-values, study methodology, disclosures), and an optional "View Study Design" link/button that can either navigate to a URL or open an in-page modal.

Source-system mapping

The manifest lists an empty aemSources array for this block, and a repository-wide search for "clinical-data" under the source system's component tree turns up no matching component — only unrelated dispatcher URL-rewrite rules that happen to contain that substring (e.g. brand-specific rewrite rule files). This is a genuinely EDS-native block with no direct AEM source component; it does not correspond to a prior authoring pattern in the source system and should not be presented as a migration of any existing component. The closest conceptual relatives in the source system are the generic image-text/image-text-v2 components (an image plus text, unrelated authoring model) and the chart-bar/chart-line/chart-pie/charts components covered separately in this batch — but none of them combine a static chart image, responsive source-swapping, footnotes, and a study-design link/modal trigger the way this block does.

EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/clinical-data-panel/. Since there is no AEM source to migrate from, this section documents the block as delivered rather than a migration.

File Structure

blocks/clinical-data-panel/
├── clinical-data-panel.js
├── clinical-data-panel.css
├── _clinical-data-panel.json
├── block-config.js
├── mavyret/
│   ├── _clinical-data-panel.css
│   ├── block-config.js
│   └── clinical-data-panel.css
└── skyrizi-hcp/

Authoring Model (_clinical-data-panel.json)

A single model with no filtered children, organized into two Universal Editor tabs. The Chart tab carries title, three separate aem-content image fields for desktop/tablet/mobile chart renditions (chartDesktop required, chartTablet/chartMobile optional with documented fallback to desktop), and chartAlt. The Footnotes tab carries a richtext footnotes field and the study-design link pair (studyDesignLabel, studyDesignLink):

{
  "component": "text",
  "name": "studyDesignLink",
  "label": "Study Design URL or Modal ID",
  "description": "Type # followed by a name you create (e.g., #study-design-1). This opens a Modal
                  block on the same page. The name after # must match the Modal ID field on a
                  Modal block you add to this page. Leave empty if no link is needed."
}

How the JS works (clinical-data-panel.js)

decorate() explicitly supports two different authored-content shapes, detected by inspecting the first row's cell count:

const firstRowCells = rows[0]?.children?.length || 0;
const isTableFormat = firstRowCells > 1;

The table format path (isTableFormat === true) treats each row as a key/value pair — the first cell is a field-name label (matched case-insensitively against title, alt/chartalt, footnotes, studydesignlabel, studydesignlink) and the second is the value, while rows containing an image are consumed in order into images.desktop, then .tablet, then .mobile. The Universal-Editor-delivered format path assumes a fixed positional row order matching the model's field declaration order (title, chartDesktop, chartTablet, chartMobile, chartAlt, footnotes, studyDesignLabel, studyDesignLink) and reads each row by index via a switch. Supporting both formats in one decorate function means the block tolerates content authored either directly through the block model or through the flexible key/value table shape without requiring re-authoring.

Once fields are read, all original rows are hidden (row.style.display = 'none') rather than removed, and a semantic <figure> is built: a title paragraph (auto-wrapping trailing footnote-marker characters — digits followed by *†‡§‖¶# — in a <sup> tag via regex, unless the source markup already contains one), a <picture> with <source> elements for the 600px and 984px breakpoints falling back to the desktop <img>, a footnotes <figcaption>, and an optional CTA anchor. The CTA's href logic distinguishes three cases:

if (studyDesignLink && studyDesignLink.startsWith('#')) {
  cta.href = '#';
  cta.dataset.modalId = studyDesignLink.substring(1);   // opens a Modal block by ID
} else if (studyDesignLink) {
  cta.href = studyDesignLink;                            // plain URL
} else {
  cta.href = '#';
  cta.setAttribute('role', 'button');                    // no link configured — inert placeholder
}

CSS

All visual tokens are custom properties prefixed --cdp-*, imported from a shared styles/tokens.css, so brand folders (mavyret, skyrizi-hcp) can restyle the panel by overriding tokens rather than rules:

.clinical-data-panel-title {
  font-family: var(--cdp-font-heading);
  font-size: var(--cdp-font-size-title);
  font-weight: var(--cdp-font-weight-bold);
  letter-spacing: var(--cdp-letter-spacing-title);
}
.clinical-data-panel-cta::after {
  content: '>';
  font-size: 1.2em;
}

A single mobile breakpoint (width <= 600px) reduces title and footnote font sizes; responsive image switching itself is handled entirely by the <picture>/<source> markup built in JS, not by CSS media queries.

Key characteristics (no AEM comparison — net-new block)

AspectBehavior
Chart renderingStatic author-supplied image per breakpoint, not a live data visualization (contrast with the chart block covered elsewhere in this batch, which renders live Chart.js output from structured data)
Content authoringDual-format tolerant: accepts either Universal-Editor-native field order or a free-form key/value table
