Footnote markersAuto-superscripts trailing footnote symbols in the title via regex if not already marked up
Study design linkSame field doubles as a URL or a same-page Modal-block trigger, disambiguated by a leading #
No JS variation/decoration hooks configuredblock-config.js ships with empty flags/variations/decorations objects — all behavior lives directly in clinical-data-panel.js's decorate(), not in the shared renderBlock pipeline used by other blocks in this batch

Columns Component — Technical Details

What it does

Two source components combine into this one EDS block: columns is a generic multi-column layout container (standard equal-width columns or offset unequal columns, with a "flush"/no-gutter modifier), and background-container is a general-purpose wrapper that applies a background (color, gradient, static image, responsive-swapped image, or looping/non-looping video) behind whatever content is nested inside it — including, in practice, being wrapped around a columns instance to give a column row a background image or color.

Architecture — source system

columns:
  Author picks columnType (standard | offset) + a named column-count preset
  (e.g. "abbv-col-4-4-4" style class group), optional flush modifier, row margin
         ↓
  ColumnControlHelper (WCMUsePojo, NOT a Sling Model) splits the preset's
  pipe-delimited class string into a List<String> of per-column CSS classes,
  appending "-flush" into each class token if enabled
         ↓
  columns.html renders one <div class="abbv-col ${class}"> per resolved class,
  each wrapping an empty parsys slot for arbitrary nested content

background-container:
  Author picks backgroundType (color | gradient | image | video) and, for image,
  imageType (scaling vs. swapping) + optional parallaxEffect; for "swapping" mode,
  authors separate large/medium/small DAM image references
         ↓
  BackgroundContainer.java (real Sling Model) resolves each breakpoint image's
  full render path via CommonUtility.getImagePath(), and builds border/border-width
  CSS class strings from borderTop/borderBottom/borderWidth/borderColor
         ↓
  background-container.html branches into one of 7 templates (color, gradient,
  scale-image ± parallax, swap-image, video ± loop) via backgroundTypesTemplate.html;
  the "swap image" branch emits an inline <style> block with three @media rules
  binding each breakpoint's image as a CSS background-image on an ID-scoped selector

Sling Model / Use-API classes

Important distinction: columns' logic lives in ColumnControlHelper.java, a Sightly WCMUsePojo (the older per-request Use-API pattern), not a Sling Model — it has no @Model annotation and is invoked via data-sly-use with explicit parameter passing rather than field injection:

public class ColumnControlHelper extends WCMUsePojo {
    public List<String> getColumnClassesListStd() {
        String selectedClassGrpStd = get("selectedClassGrpStd", String.class);
        String flushVal = get("flushVal", String.class);
        if (StringUtils.isNotBlank(selectedClassGrpStd)) {
            columnClass = Arrays.asList(selectedClassGrpStd.trim().split("\\|"));
            if ("true".equalsIgnoreCase(flushVal)) {
                // rewrites e.g. "abbv-col-4" → "abbv-col-flush-4" by splicing
                // "-flush" in before the final "-N" segment of each class token
            }
        }
        return columnClass;
    }
    public List<String> getColumnClassesListOffset() {
        // parses a "abbv-col-offset-N-..." string, extracts N as a repeat count,
        // and returns N copies of the (optionally flush-modified) class name
    }
}

background-container, by contrast, is a real Sling Model (BackgroundContainer.java, @Model(adaptables=Resource.class)) that resolves three separate DAM asset references (large/medium/small) into rendered image paths via a shared CommonUtility.getImagePath() helper, and builds CSS border classes from boolean top/bottom flags plus a width/color pair.

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeSourceNote
Column TypecolumnTypeselect (standard/offset, show/hide driver)columns
Columns (standard)standardcolumnsselect (datasource-driven)columnsPipe-delimited class-group presets, e.g. equal 2/3/4-column splits
Columns (offset)offsetcolumnsselect (datasource-driven)columnsEncodes a repeat count + base class in the value string itself (parsed by splitting on -)
Enable FlushenableflushcheckboxcolumnsRemoves gutter by injecting -flush into each column's CSS class
Row Marginrowmarginselect (datasource-driven)columns
Custom CSS Classes / Anchor IDcustomCssClasses, anchorIDtextfieldcolumns, background-container
Border Top / Bottom, Width, ColorborderTop, borderBottom, borderWidth, borderColorcheckbox / textfieldbackground-container
Background TypebackgroundTypeselect (color/gradient/image/video)background-containerDrives which of 7 HTL template branches renders
Background Color / Gradient ColorbgColor, gradientColortextfield/selectbackground-container
Image TypeimageTypeselect (scaling/swapping)background-container"Scaling" = one image stretched/cropped responsively; "swapping" = distinct image per breakpoint
Parallax EffectparallaxEffectcheckboxbackground-containerOnly applies to "scaling" image type
Large/Medium/Small image + renditionslarge/file, medium/file, small/file (+ largeImageRendition etc.)pathfield / hiddenbackground-containerUsed only in "swapping" mode; resolved to per-breakpoint background-image URLs
Video URL / Loop / PostervideoUrl, videoLoop, videoPoster (+ rendition)textfield / checkbox / pathfieldbackground-container
Image Title / Alt TextimageTitle, imageAltTexttextfieldbackground-container
Content Position / Mobile Content Positioncontentposition, mobilecontentpositionsselectbackground-containerPositions the nested text panel over the background
Text Panel Color / Transparency / Takeover / Container WidthtextPanelColor, transparency, takeover, containerWidthselect/checkboxbackground-container
Link Title / Page Path / Modal ID / Icon / Target Type / Link TypelinkTitle, pagePath, modalID, linkIcon, linkIconPosition, targetType, linkTypevariousbackground-containerOptional CTA overlay on the background panel
Content/Patient Journey, Message Bucket, MVA fieldscontentJourney, patientJourney, messageBucket, analyticsEnableMVATier, mvaType, analyticsMVATier, analyticsMVATierValuevariousbackground-containerAnalytics metadata for the optional CTA

HTL

<!-- columns.html -->
<div data-sly-use.columnControlHelper="${'com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.components.ColumnControlHelper'
     @selectedClassGrpStd=properties.standardcolumns,selectedClassGrpOff=properties.offsetcolumns,flushVal=properties.enableflush}">
  <div class="abbv-row-container ${properties.customCssClasses}">
    <div class="abbv-row ${properties.rowmargin == 'abbv-row' ? '' : properties.rowmargin}">
      <sly data-sly-test="${properties.columnType == 'standard'}">
        <sly data-sly-list.stdColumnClass="${columnControlHelper.getColumnClassesListStd}">
          <div class="abbv-col ${stdColumnClass}">
            <sly data-sly-resource="${@path=stdColumnClassList.index.toString, resourceType='wcm/foundation/components/parsys'}"></sly>
          </div>
        </sly>
      </sly>
      <!-- offset branch mirrors this using getColumnClassesListOffset -->
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- background-container.html, "swap image" branch -->
<style type="text/css">
  @media (min-width: 985px) { #${bgModel.id} .abbv-background-container-display {
    background-image: url("${bgModel.largeImagePath}"); } }
  @media (min-width: 601px) and (max-width: 984px) { #${bgModel.id} .abbv-background-container-display {
    background-image: url("${bgModel.mediumImagePath}"); } }
  @media (max-width: 600px) { #${bgModel.id} .abbv-background-container-display {
    background-image: url("${bgModel.smallImagePath}"); } }
  ${properties.customInlineStyle @context="unsafe"}
</style>

Notably, each column's resource name is derived from its numeric index in the loop (stdColumnClassList.index.toString) — the parsys child node names are not author-visible identifiers, they're positional.

CSS

Column widths come entirely from the named preset classes resolved server-side (e.g. equal 3-column/4-column splits, or the offset component's repeated-class approach), not from a small, fixed set of CSS grid rules — meaning the visual column system is spread across the datasource-backed dropdown options rather than living in one readable stylesheet. background-container's CSS handles border classes, text-panel overlay positioning, and the "swapping" background's ID-scoped inline <style> block generated per-instance in HTL (a page that has many background-swap instances gets one inline stylesheet fragment per instance).

Important Constraints

  • columns' layout logic depends on a legacy Use-API (WCMUsePojo) class rather than a Sling Model, and depends on parsing pipe- and dash-delimited encoded strings from a generic datasource servlet (ce-standard-column/ce-offset-column categories) whose actual option values are not visible without inspecting that servlet's configuration.
  • background-container's "swap image" mode injects a fresh inline <style> block with three fixed breakpoints (985px/601-984px/600px) per component instance, rather than using a shared CSS custom-property or attribute-selector approach — this is the specific mechanism the manifest's purpose text (for the EDS block) references when it describes merging desktop/mobile picture pairs "at custom breakpoints."
  • Two structurally unrelated components (a plain grid layout container and a general-purpose background wrapper) are being merged into one EDS block; in the source system they are composed together on a page (a background-container wrapping a columns instance), not implemented as a single component.

EDS Migration

The target implementation folds background-image responsiveness directly into the column authoring model rather than keeping it a separate wrapping component: an author places two images (desktop and mobile) in the same column cell, and the block's own JS merges them into one <picture> with a <source> media-query swap — replicating background-container's "swap image" behavior without the per-instance inline <style> block or the separate wrapping component.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldSource componentTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
columnType (standard/offset)columnsselectclasses ("Offset" option)Renamed/simplifiedOffset is now one named option among four, not a separate mode with its own dedicated dialog field
standardcolumns / offsetcolumns (datasource presets)columnsselect—Not mappedColumn count is now implicit — one column per authored column child item — rather than chosen from a preset split; only the offset 1/3–2/3 ratio survives as a named variant
enableflushcolumnscheckboxclasses ("Flush" option)Same purpose (renamed)
rowmargincolumnsselect (datasource)—Not mappedRow spacing is a fixed CSS custom property (--columns-gap), not an authorable per-instance choice
customCssClassescolumns, background-containertextfield—Not mappedNo free-text class field in _columns.json; the fixed classes select replaces open-ended styling hooks
anchorIDcolumns, background-containertextfieldanchorIdSame purpose (renamed casing)
borderTop / borderBottom / borderWidth / borderColorbackground-containercheckbox/textfield—Not mappedNo border-configuration fields on the columns block; borders, if needed, would be a CSS-only per-brand override
backgroundType (color/gradient/image/video)background-containerselect—Not mappedNo solid-color, gradient, or video background mode in columns — only image backgrounds via the column's own image field are supported, and only at the column level, not the row level
bgColor / gradientColorbackground-containertextfield/select—Not mappedNo color/gradient background concept in the EDS columns model
imageType (scaling/swapping) / parallaxEffectbackground-containerselect/checkbox—Not mappedNo parallax or object-fit "scaling" mode; the merged responsive <picture> is the only image behavior
large/file / medium/file / small/file (+ renditions)background-containerpathfield/hiddenimage / mobileImageRenamed/simplifiedThree breakpoint-specific assets collapse to two (desktop, mobile) — no separate "medium/tablet" image slot; tablet falls back to desktop
imageTitle / imageAltTextbackground-containertextfieldimageAlt / mobileImageAltRenamed
videoUrl / videoLoop / videoPoster (+ rendition)background-containertextfield/checkbox/pathfield—Not mappedNo video-background capability in the columns block
contentposition / mobilecontentpositionsbackground-containerselect—Not mappedContent position within a column is standard document flow, not an overlay-position choice over a background panel
textPanelColor / transparency / takeover / containerWidthbackground-containerselect/checkbox—Not mappedNo overlay text-panel styling concept — column content and column image are siblings, not layered
linkTitle / pagePath / modalID / linkIcon / linkIconPosition / targetType / linkTypebackground-containervarious—Not mappedAny CTA needed in a column is authored as ordinary richtext link content inside column.content, not as dedicated CTA sub-fields
contentJourney / patientJourney / messageBucket / MVA fieldsbackground-containervarious—Not mappedAnalytics tagging for the (non-existent) CTA overlay has no equivalent
———classes ("Reverse" option)Not mapped (EDS-native addition)Mobile column-order swap has no analog in either source component

Columns Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/columns/, including the responsive desktop/mobile image-merge behavior and a brand-specific themes subfolder.

File Structure

blocks/columns/
├── columns.js
├── columns.css
├── _columns.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
├── botox/
├── linzess/
├── mavyret/
├── rinvoq/
├── rinvoq-dtc/
├── rinvoq-hcp/
├── roy/
├── skyrizi-hcp/
├── venclexta/
└── themes/
    └── bright/

Authoring Model (_columns.json)

The columns container has one classes select (Default, Offset, Flush, Reverse) and an anchorId text field. The filtered child column carries a richtext content, a reference image with imageAlt, and a separate optional mobileImage/mobileImageAlt pair explicitly documented as falling back to the desktop image if empty.

How the JS works (columns.js)

An in-source comment documents two different mobile-image swap breakpoints depending on brand-specific card type, applied by checking specific class names:

const MOBILE_IMAGE_MEDIA = '(max-width: 743px)';
const SAVINGS_MOBILE_IMAGE_MEDIA = '(max-width: 985px)';
const isSavings = block.classList.contains('columns-homepage-savings')
  || block.classList.contains('columns-resources-savings');
const imageMedia = isSavings ? SAVINGS_MOBILE_IMAGE_MEDIA : MOBILE_IMAGE_MEDIA;

applyMobileImageSource is the direct analog of background-container's inline-<style>-per-breakpoint mechanism, but implemented as a merged <picture> element instead: when a column's image cell contains two <picture> elements (desktop authored first, mobile second), it takes the mobile picture's optimized <source srcset> (falling back to the plain <img src>) and prepends it as a new <source media="..."></source> on the desktop picture, then removes the now-redundant mobile picture element and any wrapper it leaves empty:

function applyMobileImageSource(cell, mediaQuery = MOBILE_IMAGE_MEDIA) {
  const pictures = [...cell.querySelectorAll('picture')];
  if (pictures.length < 2) return;
  const [desktopPicture, mobilePicture] = pictures;
  const mobileImg = mobilePicture.querySelector('img');
  if (!mobileImg) return;
  const mobileSource = mobilePicture.querySelector('source[srcset]');
  const mobileSrcset = mobileSource?.getAttribute('srcset') || mobileImg.getAttribute('src');
  if (mobileSrcset) {
    const source = document.createElement('source');
    source.setAttribute('media', mediaQuery);
    source.setAttribute('srcset', mobileSrcset);
    desktopPicture.prepend(source);
  }
  // ...cleanup...
}

decorate() also has an undocumented-in-the-model convenience: if the first authored row is plain text with no HTML content, it's treated as an ID to assign to the block (block.id = firstRowText) and removed from rendering — a lightweight anchor mechanism distinct from the anchorId model field. Each remaining row becomes a .columns-item, and each of its cells is classified as .columns-item-image or .columns-item-content based on whether it contains a picture/img.

CSS

.columns {
  --columns-gap: var(--spacing-24);
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  gap: var(--columns-gap);
}
.columns.columns-offset { grid-template-columns: 1fr 2fr; }  /* desktop only */
.columns.columns-reverse > .columns-item:first-child { order: 2; }  /* mobile */

An in-source comment documents an important interaction with the page's outer grid system: .columns lives inside a container that sets display: grid with all descendant divs forced to grid-column: 1 / span 12, so .columns-item must explicitly override that back to grid-column: auto for the column layout to take effect.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (columns + background-container)EDS (columns)
Column count/ratio sourceDatasource-backed preset dropdowns with encoded class stringsImplicit from authored child count; only default/offset/flush/reverse as named variants
Column layout logicLegacy WCMUsePojo (ColumnControlHelper), string-parsing basedPlain CSS grid, no server-side class computation
Responsive background/column imageSeparate background-container component; per-instance inline <style> with 3 fixed breakpointsBuilt into the column's own image field pair; JS merges into one <picture>, brand-aware breakpoint choice
Non-image backgrounds (color/gradient/video)Supported via backgroundTypeNot supported — image only
CTA/analytics overlay on backgroundSupported via dedicated link/analytics fieldsNot supported — any CTA is plain content inside a column

CTA Component — Technical Details

What it does

A standalone call-to-action: renders as a styled link or button (four visual variants plus two toggle-switch variants), reads its label/destination/behavior from author fields, and supports several distinct click behaviors beyond a plain navigation — opening a modal, opening a hidden panel, toggling a body-level CSS class ("switch view"), or rewriting the link into a same-page indication-selection query parameter.

Architecture

Author sets type (button style), title, url, linkBehaviour, optional modalID/icon
         ↓
CtaModel.java serializes the ENTIRE resource ValueMap to JSON via Gson, then
deserializes it directly into a Cta bean (anchorbeans.Cta) — i.e. dialog property
names must exactly match Cta's field names for this round-trip to populate correctly
         ↓
CtaModel.init():
  - resolves the write-on-read "interactionID" analytics pattern (same as
    GridModel/CarouselModel/VideoModel)
  - if linkBehaviour == "indicationSelection": looks up the target page's name via
    PageManager, rewrites the CTA's own url to "?indicationName=<name>" against the
    CURRENT page, and forces linkBehaviour to "_self" — this is a real runtime page
    lookup, not just a URL string transform
  - populates ~15 analytics sub-fields (component type/position, content
    name/title/type via a URL-download-extension check, MVA analytics tier/type/
    category) via CommonUtility helpers, then serializes an analytics payload
         ↓
LinkChecker.java (adapts SlingHttpServletRequest, reads a "link" REQUEST ATTRIBUTE
rather than a resource property) formats the href, computes isExternal, and derives
a "jump-link" CSS class for same-page anchor links
         ↓
cta.html renders either:
  - anchortag.html's ctaanchor template (plain <a>) for all button/link types, or
  - button.html's buttonSwitch template (a toggle control) for round-switch/square-switch

Sling Model — CtaModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class})
public class CtaModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private Cta ctaBeanobj;

    @PostConstruct
    public void init() {
        interactionId = getInteractionIdFromDialog();
        if (!isPublish() && (StringUtils.isBlank(interactionId) || !interactionId.equals(getId()))) {
            setInteractionIdInDialog();
        }
        ctaBeanobj = setCTABean();
    }

    private Cta setCTABean() {
        ValueMap ctaRes = resource.getValueMap();
        Gson gson = new Gson();
        String ctaLogoJson = gson.toJson(ctaRes);         // whole resource → JSON
        Cta ctaObj = gson.fromJson(ctaLogoJson, Cta.class); // JSON → typed bean
        if (ctaObj != null) {
            setIndicationSelection(ctaObj);
            setAnalyticsValues(ctaObj);
            setAriaTitle(ctaObj.getTitle());
            ctaObj.setAnalyticsString(resourceResolver);
        }
        return ctaObj;
    }

    private void setIndicationSelection(Cta ctaObj) {
        if ("indicationSelection".equals(ctaObj.getLinkBehaviour())) {
            PageManager pageManager = resourceResolver.adaptTo(PageManager.class);
            String indicationPageName = pageManager.getContainingPage(ctaObj.getUrl()).getName();
            Page currentPage = pageManager.getContainingPage(resource);
            ctaObj.setUrl("?indicationName=" + indicationPageName);
            ctaObj.setLinkBehaviour("_self");
        }
    }
}

Notable design choice: rather than individually injecting each dialog property with @ValueMapValue, the model bulk-serializes the whole resource ValueMap to JSON and deserializes it straight into the Cta bean — a whole-object mapping shortcut that works only because Cta's field names are kept in lockstep with the dialog's name attributes (e.g. title, url, modalID, linkIcon).

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
CTA Identifier (Anchor ID)anchorIDtextfield
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestextfield
Call To Action Button StyletypeselectPrimary/Secondary/Tertiary/Link (each maps to an abbv-button-* class) plus Toggle-Round/Toggle-Square (round-switch/square-switch, rendered via an entirely different HTL template)
Call To Action Title Texttitletextfield
Call To Action URLurlpathbrowserRoot path "/" — supports internal or absolute external destinations
Open Call To Action inlinkBehaviourselect (show/hide driver, default "_blank")Same Tab / New Tab / Modal / Hidden Panel / Switch View / Indication Selection
Modal / Hidden Panel IDmodalIDtextfieldRequired when linkBehaviour is Modal or Hidden Panel
Add an Icon to the Call To ActionlinkIconselect (datasource-driven)
Icon location relative to the CTAlinkIconPositionselectNone / Left (i-b) / Right (i-a)
Prevent screen readers from announcing this CTAscreenReaderCheckcheckbox
Analytics Title / Section Title / Localized Section TitleanalyticsTitle, sectionTitle, l10sectionTitletextfieldAnalytics tab
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIDtextfield (auto-populated)
MVA / MVA Name / MVA Tier / MVA Valuemva, mvaName, mvaTier, mvaValuecheckbox/textfield/select
Third Party TagsthirdPartyTagstextfield

HTL — cta.html

<sly data-sly-use.ctaModel="${'com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.CtaModel'}">
    <sly data-sly-test="${ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.title}">
        <sly data-sly-use.linkhelper="${'com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.LinkChecker' @link = ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.url}">
            <sly data-sly-test.classGrp="${properties.linkBehaviour == 'indicationSelection' ? 'abbv-indication-selection' : ''}
                 ${ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.linkIcon} ${ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.type} ${ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.linkIconPosition}
                 ${ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.customCssClasses} ${linkhelper.jumpLinkClass}"></sly>
            <sly data-sly-test="${ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.type != 'round-switch' && ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.type != 'square-switch'}">
                <sly data-sly-use.anchorTemplate="/apps/common-elements/v1/components/content/anchortag/anchortag.html">
                    <sly data-sly-call="${anchorTemplate.ctaanchor @ ariaTitle=ctaModel.ariaTitle, linkBehaviour=ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.linkBehaviour,
                         analyticsString=ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.analyticsString, url=linkhelper.linkFormatted, modalID=ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.modalID,
                         linkTitle=ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.title, classGrp=classGrp, linkType=ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.linkType,
                         screenReaderCheck=ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.screenReaderCheck}"/>
                </sly>
            </sly>
            <sly data-sly-test="${ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.type == 'round-switch' || ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.type == 'square-switch'}">
                <sly data-sly-use.buttonTemplate="/apps/common-elements/v1/components/content/button/button.html">
                    <sly data-sly-call="${buttonTemplate.buttonSwitch @ title=ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.title, linkType=ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.type,
                         linkBehaviour=ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.linkBehaviour, analyticsString=ctaModel.ctaBeanobj.analyticsString}"/>
                </sly>
            </sly>
        </sly>
    </sly>
</sly>

The actual rendered markup is delegated to two other components' shared HTL templates (anchortag.html, button.html) rather than defined inline — cta's own template is purely orchestration: deciding which shared template to call and what class string to build.

Important Constraints

  • CtaModel's whole-ValueMap-to-JSON-to-bean mapping means adding, renaming, or retyping any dialog field requires the Cta bean's corresponding field to be kept in exact sync, with no compiler-enforced connection between the two (a mismatch silently drops the property rather than failing).
  • Indication Selection is not a pure client-side link rewrite — it performs a server-side page lookup against the *target* URL's containing page to get its name, then rewrites the *CTA's own* URL to a query string against the *current* page. This runtime page-tree dependency makes the behavior fragile if the target page is renamed/moved without republishing the CTA.
  • Two of the six "Button Style" options (round-switch/square-switch) render through a structurally different template (a toggle switch, not an anchor) with a different, smaller parameter set — icon, screen-reader-check, and most analytics detail are silently ignored for those two variants since buttonSwitch doesn't accept them.
  • LinkChecker reads its link input from a Sling *request attribute*, not a resource property — it must be explicitly passed a value via @link=... at the point of use rather than being auto-wired from the current resource.

EDS Migration

The target implementation keeps all six click-behavior options and both toggle variants, but flattens per-instance styling into two model areas: an "Icon" concept generalized to support either a font icon or an image icon (the source system had only a datasource-driven icon picker, implicitly font-based), and a classes multiselect for the button variant (allowing more than one class to be combined, vs. the single-select type field in AEM).

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtextfieldanchorIdSame name (casing)
customCssClassestextfield—Not mappedNo free-text class field; styling is constrained to the classes multiselect of named variants
typeselect (single)classesRenamed/restructuredSingle-select button style becomes a multiselect (author can combine variants); same six visual options (Primary/Secondary/Tertiary/Plain/Toggle-Round/Toggle-Square) map 1:1 by value
titletextfieldlabelRenamed
urlpathbrowserhrefRenamedComponent type changes from pathbrowser to aem-content
linkBehaviourselectctaTargetSame options (renamed field)All six values (_self/_blank/new-modal/new-hidden-panel/switch-view/indicationSelection) preserved verbatim
modalIDtextfieldmodalIdSame name (casing)In EDS, presence of modalId alone (regardless of ctaTarget) is what triggers buildButton() instead of buildLink() in cta.js — a simpler trigger condition than AEM's explicit Modal/Hidden-Panel linkBehaviour branch
linkIcon (datasource-driven)selecticonType + iconFontRenamed/restructuredSource had one flat icon picker; EDS splits into an explicit type selector (none/icon-font/image) plus a separate raw font-code field, and adds a new image-icon path (iconImage) with no source-system equivalent
linkIconPositionselecticonPositionSame values (renamed)i-b/i-a values preserved verbatim
screenReaderCheckcheckboxariaHiddenSame purpose (renamed)
analyticsTitletextfield—Not mappedanalyticsInteractionId alone is used for the GTM dataLayer push in cta.js; no separate analytics-specific title field
sectionTitle / l10sectionTitletextfield—Not mappedSection-level analytics context, if needed, is expected to come from the page/section structure rather than a per-CTA field
interactionIDtextfield (auto-populated)analyticsInteractionIdSame purpose (renamed)Author-set in EDS rather than auto-generated/written-back server-side
mva / mvaName / mvaTier / mvaValuecheckbox/textfield/select—Not mappedMost Valued Action tagging is not part of the CTA block's field model; the only interaction hook is the single analyticsInteractionId pushed with a fixed cta_interaction event shape in pushAnalytics
thirdPartyTagstextfield—Not mappedNo third-party tag pass-through field
(ARIA label — not directly authored in AEM; derived server-side from title via CommonUtility.html2text)n/a — computedariaLabelNot mapped (promoted from computed to authored)EDS lets authors set an explicit ARIA label distinct from the visible button text, rather than always deriving it from stripped-HTML title text

CTA Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/cta/, rendering either an anchor, a modal-trigger button, or a toggle switch, with a single GTM analytics event contract shared across all three output shapes.

File Structure

blocks/cta/
├── cta.js
├── cta.css
├── _cta.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
├── botox/
├── linzess/
├── mavyret/
├── rinvoq/
├── rinvoq-dtc/
├── rinvoq-hcp/
├── skyrizi-hcp/
└── venclexta/

Authoring Model (_cta.json)

Fields are split across four Universal Editor tabs (Overview, Styles, Common Properties, plus a shared _common-properties.json fragment spread in via "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop"). Icon fields use JSON-logic condition blocks to show/hide based on iconType:

{
  "component": "text",
  "name": "iconFont",
  "label": "Button Font Icon",
  "condition": { "===": [{ "var": "iconType" }, "icon-font"] }
}

How the JS works (cta.js)

readConfig reads the block's authored rows by fixed position (label, href, ariaLabel, target, modalId), then loop-scans any remaining rows to classify icon-related values heuristically — a row's text is checked against known enum values (icon-font/image/none, i-a/i-b) or, failing that, tested as a hex-looking font-icon code via regex:

} else if (text && /^[/\\]?(?:u\+?|0x|x)?[0-9a-f]{3,6}$/i.test(text) && !iconFont) {
  const match = text.match(/[0-9a-f]{3,6}/i);
  iconFont = match ? match[0] : '';
}

decorate() branches into exactly one of three output shapes: buildToggle (if the block carries either switch variant class), buildButton (if a modalId is present — renders a real <button> with data-modal-id rather than an anchor), or buildLink (the default anchor path, which sets target="_blank"/rel="noopener" only when target === '_blank'). All three attach an icon via the shared buildIcon/attachIcon helpers — font icons are rendered by converting a hex code point to a character via String.fromCharCode(parseInt(code, 16)), image icons via a plain <img>. Every interaction path funnels through pushAnalytics, which pushes a fixed-shape cta_interaction event (cta_action, cta_label, cta_variant) to window.dataLayer; the toggle variant fires this on its native change event with toggle_on/toggle_off as the action. Finally, decorate() dispatches a custom cta:ready event with the resolved config in its detail, letting other blocks on the page react to a CTA finishing its own decoration.

CSS

Explicitly documented as token-driven with no CSS fallback values — every brand must define its own design tokens (contrast with other blocks in this batch, like chart and clinical-data-panel, whose custom properties do carry hard-coded fallbacks):

/* All values reference tokens declared in brand token files.
   No fallback values — each brand must define its own tokens. */
.cta .abbv-cta {
  font-family: var(--heading-font-family-bold);
  font-size: var(--button-primary-font-size);
  border-radius: var(--border-radius-button-primary);
  padding: var(--button-primary-padding);
}

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (cta)EDS (cta)
Rendering delegationDelegates markup to two other components' shared HTL templates (anchortag.html, button.html)Self-contained — builds its own DOM in cta.js
Field-to-bean mappingWhole-resource JSON round-trip into a hand-maintained bean with matching field namesPositional row reading plus heuristic value-shape detection for icon rows
Icon supportFont-icon only (single datasource-driven picker)Font icon or image icon, explicit type selector
Indication Selection behaviorServer-side page-tree lookup rewrites the CTA's own URL at render timeNot present in cta.js — ctaTarget preserves the option name but no equivalent server/client rewrite logic exists in the reviewed block code
Analytics event shapeRich per-CTA analytics bean (component/content/interaction/MVA sub-objects) serialized server-sideSingle flat cta_interaction dataLayer event with three properties

Custom Image Component — Technical Details

What it does

Builds an Adobe Dynamic Media image URL by appending either an Image Preset (?$PresetName$) or a Smart Crop rendition (?smartcrop=RenditionName) query parameter, plus any raw query-string modifiers an author supplies, on top of a base image asset — with optional caption (overlaid or below), optional link-wrapping with several click behaviors, a "warn on leave" hook, and two shape variants (circle, logo).

Source-system mapping

The manifest lists an empty aemSources array, and a repository-wide search for a matching component (by name or by Dynamic Media/Scene7/Smart-Crop/Image-Preset keywords) under the source system's component tree finds no dedicated custom-image component. This is a genuinely EDS-native block with no direct AEM source component. Dynamic Media / Scene7 image-URL logic does exist in the source system, but only as generic helper methods inside a shared utility class (CommonUtility.getImageUrl, getDMImgUrl, getImagePathWithPreset) consumed internally by many different components' Sling Models — there is no standalone, dialog-driven "custom image" authoring component that exposes Dynamic Media preset/smart-crop selection directly to a page author the way this EDS block does. Notably, the source system's Dynamic Media integration is per-brand-config-driven (a dynamicMediaEnabled flag looked up from a brand admin-config page) and falls back to serving certain assets (SVG/WebP, oversized images, or images explicitly flagged doNotRenderFromDM) straight from DAM rather than through Dynamic Media at all — behavior with no analog in the EDS block's field model, which always builds a DM-style URL from whatever base image is authored.

EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/custom-image/ and ships with its own README documenting every field. Two authoring fields are explicitly marked as spikes with no real implementation.

File Structure

blocks/custom-image/
├── custom-image.js
├── custom-image.css
├── _custom-image.json
├── block-config.js
├── README.md
├── abbvie/
├── botox/
└── rinvoq/

Authoring Model (_custom-image.json)

Fields are organized across Overview, Accessibility, Analytics, and Styles tabs. Conditional visibility is expressed with JSON-logic condition blocks, e.g. the preset/rendition selects only show for their matching presetType value:

{
  "component": "select",
  "name": "imagePreset",
  "label": "Image Preset",
  "condition": { "===": [{ "var": "presetType" }, "image-preset"] },
  "options": [
    { "name": "None", "value": "" },
    { "name": "Feature", "value": "Feature" },
    { "name": "Hero", "value": "Hero" },
    { "name": "Responsive", "value": "Responsive" },
    { "name": "Square", "value": "Square" },
    { "name": "Tall", "value": "Tall" },
    { "name": "VideoThumbnail", "value": "VideoThumbnail" }
  ]
}

Per the README, two boolean fields — "Get alternative text from DAM" and "Get caption from DAM" — are explicitly documented as spikes with no implementation behind them in custom-image.js.

How the JS works (custom-image.js)

buildImageUrl is the core URL-construction function and mirrors the two Dynamic Media authoring paths from the model — Image Preset and Smart Crop — plus a plain-modifiers fallback:

export function buildImageUrl(src, presetType, imagePreset, rendition, modifiers) {
  const baseUrl = src.includes('?') ? src.split('?')[0] : src;
  if (presetType === 'image-preset' && imagePreset && imagePreset.toLowerCase() !== 'none') {
    const presetParam = `$${imagePreset}$`;
    return modifiers ? `${baseUrl}?${presetParam}&${modifiers}` : `${baseUrl}?${presetParam}`;
  }
  if (presetType === 'smart-crop' && rendition && rendition.toLowerCase() !== 'none') {
    const params = new URLSearchParams({ smartcrop: rendition });
    if (modifiers) {
      modifiers.split('&').forEach((p) => {
        const [k, v] = p.split('=');
        if (k?.trim()) params.set(k.trim(), v?.trim() ?? '');
      });
    }
    return `${baseUrl}?${params.toString()}`;
  }
  return modifiers ? `${baseUrl}?${modifiers}` : baseUrl;
}

decorate() reads authored rows by a fixed, explicitly documented position map (ROW.IMAGE through ROW.ANALYTICS_INTERACTION_ID), with several rows commented out as "handled by applyCommonProps" or "handled by framework" rather than read directly. It supports two distinct authored-image shapes: an already-rendered <picture>/<img> (the normal case), or a bare anchor tag whose href is treated as the image URL and whose text becomes the alt text (a fallback the in-source comments label "Case 1: image authored as URL"). Once resolved, buildImageUrl is applied not just to the main <img>'s src but to every <source>'s srcset already present in the picture, so a responsive picture authored with multiple sources still gets the preset/crop parameters consistently on all of them. Link wrapping (applyLink) sets data-modal or data-hidden-panel attributes for those two click behaviors but the README and inline // TODO comments both flag that the actual modal/hidden-panel trigger logic is not yet implemented — only the data attribute is set, "to be covered as part of modal story development." The same applies to data-warn-on-leave.

CSS

.custom-image figcaption {
  position: absolute;
  bottom: 0;
  left: 0;
  right: 0;
}
.custom-image figure.caption-below figcaption {
  position: static;
}
.custom-image.circle picture img {
  border-radius: 50%;
  aspect-ratio: 1 / 1;
  object-fit: cover;
}
.custom-image.logo picture img {
  height: 1.6rem;
  max-width: 8.8rem;
  object-fit: cover;
}

Caption position (overlay vs. below) is a pure CSS toggle driven by the caption-below class added in JS; the two shape variants (circle, logo) are independent class-driven modifiers with their own responsive breakpoints for the logo variant (744px, 1024px).

Key characteristics (no AEM comparison — net-new block)

AspectBehavior
Dynamic Media integration modelAlways builds a DM-style URL from whatever base image is authored, driven entirely by the presetType/imagePreset/rendition fields — no brand-level enable/disable flag or DAM-metadata-based bypass like the source system's dynamicMediaEnabled config and doNotRenderFromDM/oversized-image DAM fallback
Incomplete features (explicitly flagged)"Get alt text from DAM" and "Get caption from DAM" are spike fields with no JS behind them; modal, hidden-panel, and warn-on-leave click behaviors set data attributes only, with trigger logic explicitly deferred to future work
Dual authoring-shape toleranceAccepts either a rendered picture/img or a bare anchor-as-image-URL fallback
Responsive source handlingApplies preset/crop query parameters to every <source>'s srcset, not just the fallback <img>
Shape variantsCircle (1:1 cropped) and Logo (fixed height/max-width, three breakpoints) — no equivalent shape-variant system in the source system's generic image handling

Custom Title Component — Technical Details

What it does

Renders a single authorable heading (choice of h1–h6) with optional custom CSS classes and an anchor ID. It is a purely presentational, no-logic component on both sides of the migration.

Architecture

Author sets jcr:title, ./type (h1..h6), ./customCssClasses, ./anchorID, ./headCustomClasses
         ↓
titles.html (HTL) calls titles.js (a Sightly "use" helper, NOT a Java Sling Model)
         ↓
titles.js resolves the text via a fallback chain:
  granite.resource.properties['jcr:title']
    || resourcePage.getProperties().get('jcr:title')
    || resourcePage.getProperties().get('pageTitle')
    || wcm.currentPage.properties['jcr:title']
    || wcm.currentPage.properties['pageTitle']
    || wcm.currentPage.name
         ↓
<h{type}> rendered with the resolved text, wrapped in a .abbv-title div

Sling Model

There is no Java Sling Model backing this component. The source system uses a Sightly-native JavaScript "use" object (titles.js), a lighter-weight HTL scripting construct rather than a compiled Java class:

use(function () {
    var CONST = {
        PROP_TITLE: "jcr:title",
        PROP_PAGE_TITLE: "pageTitle",
        PROP_TYPE: "type",
        PROP_DEFAULT_TYPE: "defaultType"
    }
    var title = {};
    title.text = granite.resource.properties[CONST.PROP_TITLE]
            || resourcePage.getProperties().get(CONST.PROP_TITLE)
            || resourcePage.getProperties().get(CONST.PROP_PAGE_TITLE)
            || wcm.currentPage.properties[CONST.PROP_TITLE]
            || wcm.currentPage.properties[CONST.PROP_PAGE_TITLE]
            || wcm.currentPage.name;
    title.element = granite.resource.properties[CONST.PROP_TYPE]
            || currentStyle.get(CONST.PROP_DEFAULT_TYPE, "");
    title.CONST = CONST;
    return title;
});

Notably the actual heading text falls all the way back to the containing page's node name if nothing else is authored — a behavior with no equivalent on the EDS side, where the heading text is always an explicit field value.

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Titlejcr:titletextareaThe heading text itself; stored on the standard JCR title property, not a custom property name
Type / Sizetypeselecth1–h6, defaults to h2
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextfieldSpace-separated custom classes on the wrapper
Anchor IDanchorIDtextfieldRenders a hidden anchor <a> before the title
Header Custom CSS ClassesheadCustomClassestextfieldClasses applied to the heading element itself, distinct from the wrapper classes

A separate design dialog (_cq_design_dialog) only includes the standard style tab include and adds no component-specific fields.

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<a data-sly-test="${properties.anchorID}" id="${properties.anchorID}" aria-hidden="true"></a>
<div class="abbv-title ${properties.customCssClasses}">
    <h1 class="${properties.headCustomClasses}" data-sly-use.title="titles.js" data-sly-element="${title.element}">${title.text @context='html'}</h1>
</div>

The <h1> tag name is a static placeholder in markup — data-sly-element dynamically swaps the rendered tag to whatever title.element resolves to (h1–h6), a Sightly feature for changing an element's tag name at render time.

CSS

No component-specific CSS ships with the source component itself; heading typography in the source system is generally inherited from global type-scale styles rather than scoped per-component. Sizing/format/theme variants below are all EDS-side additions, not present in the source component.

Important Constraints

  • Text fallback chain means an unauthored title silently renders the page's node name — a behavior that can leak internal page-naming conventions into visible copy if authors forget to fill the field.
  • @context='html' on the title text means basic HTML markup (e.g. <sup>) authored into the title field is preserved and rendered, not escaped.
  • No analytics tab and no accessibility-specific fields (e.g. no way to decouple visual size from semantic level beyond the combined Type/Size selector).

EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/custom-title/ and considerably exceeds the source component's capability — it adds alignment, font-weight, decorative "format" treatments (outdented first letter, raised cap), and light/dark theme variants, none of which exist in the source dialog.

File Structure

blocks/custom-title/
├── custom-title.js
├── custom-title.css
├── _custom-title.json
├── block-config.js
├── rinvoq/
│   ├── custom-title.css
│   ├── _custom-title.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
│   ├── custom-title.css
│   ├── _custom-title.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── abbvie/
    ├── custom-title.css
    ├── _custom-title.css
    └── block-config.js

Authoring Model (_custom-title.json)

{
  "id": "custom-title",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "title", "value": "", "label": "Text" },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Styles", "name": "styles" },
    { "component": "select", "valueType": "string", "name": "titleType", "value": "h1", "label": "Semantic Heading",
      "options": [
        { "name": "H1", "value": "h1" }, { "name": "H2", "value": "h2" }, { "name": "H3", "value": "h3" },
        { "name": "H4", "value": "h4" }, { "name": "H5", "value": "h5" }, { "name": "H6", "value": "h6" }
      ] },
    { "component": "select", "valueType": "string", "name": "classes_alignment", "value": "align-left", "label": "Alignment",
      "options": [ { "name": "Left", "value": "align-left" }, { "name": "Center", "value": "align-center" }, { "name": "Right", "value": "align-right" } ] },
    { "component": "select", "valueType": "string", "name": "classes_fontWeight", "value": "", "label": "Font Weight",
      "options": [ { "name": "Default", "value": "" }, { "name": "Medium", "value": "weight-medium" }, { "name": "Book", "value": "weight-book" } ] },
    { "component": "select", "valueType": "string", "name": "classes_format", "value": "", "label": "Format",
      "options": [ { "name": "None", "value": "" }, { "name": "Outdented with Accent Color", "value": "format-outdented" }, { "name": "Raised Cap", "value": "format-raised" } ] },
    { "component": "select", "valueType": "string", "name": "classes_theme", "value": "theme-light", "label": "Theme",
      "options": [ { "name": "Light", "value": "theme-light" }, { "name": "Dark", "value": "theme-dark" } ] },
    { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
  ]
}

How the JS works (custom-title.js)

import { applyCommonProps } from '../../scripts/utils.js';

export default function decorate(block) {
  applyCommonProps(block);
  // Add BEM class to heading and unwrap any Universal Editor-injected spans

  const heading = block.querySelector('h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6');
  if (heading) {
    heading.classList.add('custom-title-heading');
  }
}

The Universal Editor already renders the correct semantic tag (from the titleType field) as markup in the authored block, so the decorate function does not need to build a heading element — it only locates whichever heading tag is present and stamps a single BEM class (custom-title-heading) onto it. All the alignment/weight/format/theme options are pure CSS driven off classes applied to the block via applyCommonProps (shared utility reading rows prefixed with known class keys, e.g. align-, theme-) rather than any block-specific logic.

CSS

.custom-title .custom-title-heading {
  font-family: var(--heading-font-family-bold);
  color: var(--custom-title-color);
  margin: 0;
  padding: 0;
  margin-bottom: var(--spacing-20);
  font-weight: var(--font-weight-800);
}

/* Format: Outdented — first letter changes to brand accent blue */
.format-outdented .custom-title-heading::first-letter {
  color: var(--custom-title-accent-color);
  display: inline;
}

/* Format: Raised Cap — first letter scales up, sits raised on the baseline */
.format-raised h1::first-letter {
  font-size: var(--font-size-80);
  line-height: var(--line-height-84);
}

Responsive heading sizes are defined per tag (h1–h6) at three breakpoints (mobile/base, ≥744px tablet, ≥1024px desktop) — a fixed type scale baked into the block's CSS rather than authored per-instance. Each brand folder (rinvoq/, botox/, abbvie/) overrides the CSS custom properties (--custom-title-color, --custom-title-accent-color, font families) to match brand tokens.

Key differences from AEM implementation

Source (titles)Target (custom-title)
Backing logicSightly JS "use" helper with page-title fallback chainPlain decorate() function, no fallback — title is always the explicit field value
Style optionsNone (relies on global CSS)Alignment, font-weight, decorative format (outdented/raised cap), light/dark theme — all block-scoped
Heading level controlCombined with sizing (Type/Size select)Separate titleType field only controls semantic tag; visual size is fixed per tag/breakpoint by CSS
Custom CSS classes fieldTwo separate fields (wrapper classes + heading classes)Not present — replaced by the fixed style select fields above

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
jcr:titletextareatitleRenamedSame purpose (heading text), different property name and simplified from textarea to single-line text
typeselect (h1–h6)titleTypeRenamedSame six-option semantic heading choice, renamed field
customCssClassestextfield—Not mappedFree-text CSS class injection has no EDS equivalent; replaced by the fixed enumerated style selects (classes_alignment, classes_fontWeight, classes_format, classes_theme), which are safer but less flexible
anchorIDtextfield—Not mappedNo anchor-ID field in the custom-title model; the common-properties include (_common-properties.json) may cover generic block IDs but not this specific per-field anchor pattern
headCustomClassestextfield—Not mappedFree-text class injection on the heading itself has no EDS equivalent for the same reason as customCssClasses

Dismiss Content Component — Technical Details

What it does

Displays a piece of authored content (typically referencing a shared "Short ISI"/important-safety-information fragment page, but usable for any Flex Content Page) with an optional close ("X") icon. Dismissal is tracked with a cookie so the content stays hidden across pages that share the same cookie name.

Architecture

Author sets shortIsiPath, cookieName, closeContentCheckbox, resetContentCheckbox, MVA/analytics fields
         ↓
DismissContentModel.java (Sling Model) resolves shortIsiPath → shortIsiPathContentPar
  (appends a fixed "short-isi" suffix path and verifies the resource exists)
         ↓
dismiss-content.html renders:
  <div class="abbv-dismiss-content ..." data-cookie="..." data-reset="..." data-can-close="...">
    [included Short-ISI/Flex Content Page content, via data-sly-resource]
    <span class="abbv-icon-close" role="button" ...>
         ↓
dismiss-content.js (client, jQuery):
  on load: if data-reset → delete cookie
  if cookie !== 'closed' → show widget, wire close click → hide + set cookie

Sling Model — DismissContentModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {SlingHttpServletRequest.class, Resource.class}, defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class DismissContentModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    ...
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String anchorID;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String customCssClasses;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String cookieName;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String shortIsiPath;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String closeContentCheckbox;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String resetContentCheckbox;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String interactionId;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String mvaCheck;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String mvaAnalyticsTitle;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String mvaValue;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String secondaryMva;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String thirdPartyTags;

    private String shortIsiPathContentPar = null;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        if (!isPublish() && (StringUtils.isBlank(interactionId) || !interactionId.equals(getId()))) {
            setInteractionIdInComponentDialog();
        }
        if (StringUtils.isNotEmpty(shortIsiPath)) { getShortIsiContentPar(); }
        setAnalyticsData();
    }

    private void getShortIsiContentPar() {
        PageManager pageMgr = resourceResolver.adaptTo(PageManager.class);
        Page referencePage = pageMgr.getPage(this.shortIsiPath);
        if (referencePage != null && !this.shortIsiPath.equals(currentPage.getPath())) {
            String pageContentPar = this.shortIsiPath + GlobalConstants.BACK_SLASH + GlobalConstants.SHORT_ISI_END_PATH;
            if (resourceResolver.getResource(pageContentPar) != null) {
                shortIsiPathContentPar = pageContentPar;
            } else {
                shortIsiPathContentPar = "";
            }
        } else {
            shortIsiPathContentPar = "";
        }
    }
}

The model builds an analytics "digital data" bean for the close-button interaction (component/link/interaction/MVA beans) and exposes it as analyticsString for the HTL's data-digitaldata attribute.

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor IDanchorIDtextfieldAdds an ID attribute to the component's parent container
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextfieldSpace-separated
Cookie NamecookieNametextfieldSession cookie key; must match across pages to share dismissed state
Content to be displayedshortIsiPathpathbrowserPath to a Short-ISI folder structure or any Flex Content Page; resolved by the model into a content-par path
Content can be dismissed on pagecloseContentCheckboxcheckbox (default checked)Toggles whether the close icon is shown
Reset the dismissed status on page loadresetContentCheckboxcheckboxDeletes the cookie on every load so content always re-shows
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtextfield (disabled/system-generated)Auto-populated, read-only in the dialog
Most Valued Action (MVA)mvaCheckcheckboxMarks the close interaction as a primary MVA for analytics
MVA Title for AnalyticsmvaAnalyticsTitletextfield
MVA Action Type and CategorymvaValueselect (datasource-backed)Populated from an admin-configured analytics taxonomy
MVA SecondarysecondaryMvacheckbox

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-test="${properties.shortIsiPath && !wcmmode.edit}">
    <sly data-sly-use.dismissModel="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.DismissContentModel">
        <div data-sly-attribute.id="${dismissModel.anchorID}"
             class="abbv-dismiss-content ${dismissModel.customCssClasses}"
             data-cookie="${dismissModel.cookieName}"
             data-reset="${dismissModel.resetContentCheckbox}"
             data-can-close="${dismissModel.closeContentCheckbox}">
            <sly data-sly-test="${dismissModel.shortIsiPathContentPar}"
                 data-sly-resource="${dismissModel.shortIsiPathContentPar}" data-sly-unwrap></sly>
            <span class="abbv-icon-close i-a" role="button" tabindex="0" aria-label="close"
                  data-digitaldata='${dismissModel.analyticsString @context="unsafe"}'></span>
        </div>
    </sly>
</sly>

CSS

.abbv-dismiss-content {
    background-color: lightblue;
    display: none;
    padding: 10px 30px 10px 10px;
    position: relative;
}
.abbv-dismiss-content .abbv-icon-close {
    cursor: pointer;
    display: none;
    font-size: 1rem;
    position: absolute;
    right: 10px;
    top: 10px;
}

The widget is hidden (display: none) by default and only shown by the client-side script once the cookie check passes — so JS is required for the content to ever become visible, and there is no CSS-only fallback for a no-JS scenario.

Important Constraints

  • Relies on jQuery ($(function(){...})) — a global dependency, not scoped to the component.
  • The cookie helper (cookieObj.createCookie) is called with no expiry argument in the widget's own init script, making it read as a session cookie in that code path, despite the dialog's help text describing "remainder of session" persistence — actual expiry depends on the shared cookieObj utility's default, which is not part of this component's own source.
  • The Short-ISI path resolution silently sets an empty content-par string (renders nothing) if the referenced page doesn't exist or the expected child resource is missing, rather than surfacing an authoring error.
  • A component instance that points at its own containing page (shortIsiPath equal to currentPage.getPath()) is explicitly rejected by the model to avoid infinite inclusion.

EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/dismiss/. It reimplements the same cookie-gated dismiss pattern but authors the banner's own rich text content directly (rather than including a separately-authored Short-ISI/Flex Content page), and adds an explicit sticky layout variant.

File Structure

blocks/dismiss/
├── dismiss.js
├── dismiss.css
├── _dismiss.json
├── block-config.js
├── rinvoq/
│   ├── dismiss.css
│   ├── _dismiss.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── ... (botox/, abbvie/ brand folders follow the same pattern)

Authoring Model (_dismiss.json)

{
  "id": "dismiss",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Content", "name": "tab-content" },
    { "component": "richtext", "name": "content", "value": "", "label": "Content",
      "description": "Content shown until the user dismisses it. A close button is added automatically.", "valueType": "string" },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Settings", "name": "tab-settings" },
    { "component": "select", "valueType": "string", "name": "classes", "label": "Layout",
      "options": [ { "name": "Default (inline)", "value": "" }, { "name": "Sticky (fixed top)", "value": "sticky" } ] },
    { "component": "text", "name": "cookieName", "label": "Cookie Name", "valueType": "string",
      "description": "Unique key for the dismissal cookie. Use the same value across pages to share dismissed state site-wide. Defaults to the page URL path if blank." },
    { "component": "text", "name": "closeLabel", "label": "Close Button Label", "valueType": "string",
      "description": "Accessible label for the close button (aria-label). Defaults to 'Close' if blank." },
    { "component": "boolean", "name": "resetOnLoad", "label": "Reset on Load", "valueType": "boolean",
      "description": "When enabled, the dismissal cookie is cleared on every page load so the banner always shows." },
    { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
  ]
}

How the JS works (dismiss.js)

function setCookie(name) {
  const expires = new Date(Date.now() + 365 * 864e5).toUTCString();
  document.cookie = `${encodeURIComponent(name)}=1;expires=${expires};path=/;SameSite=Lax;Secure`;
}
function hasCookie(name) {
  return document.cookie.split(';').some((c) => c.trim().startsWith(`${encodeURIComponent(name)}=`));
}
function deleteCookie(name) {
  document.cookie = `${encodeURIComponent(name)}=;expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 GMT;path=/;SameSite=Lax;Secure`;
}

export default function decorate(block) {
  const { cookieName, closeLabel, resetOnLoad } = readFields(block);
  const key = cookieName || `dismiss-${window.location.pathname}`;
  const wrapper = block.closest('.dismiss-wrapper') ?? block;

  if (block.classList.contains('sticky') && !block.dataset.aueResource) {
    hoistBeforeMain(wrapper);
  }

  if (resetOnLoad) {
    deleteCookie(key);
  } else if (hasCookie(key)) {
    wrapper.remove();
    return;
  }

  const closeBtn = document.createElement('button');
  closeBtn.className = 'dismiss-close';
  ...
  closeBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
    setCookie(key);
    block.classList.add('dismiss-is-closing');
    const duration = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(block).getPropertyValue('--transition-duration-medium') || '0.3') * 1000;
    setTimeout(() => wrapper.remove(), duration);
  });
}

Unlike the source component, cookie expiry is explicit and real: a plain document.cookie write with a 365-day expiry, SameSite=Lax, and Secure. A dedicated readFields() helper strips the raw authoring rows (cookie name / close label / reset flag) out of the block's DOM before the rich text content is rendered, and a sticky variant hoists the block's wrapper to just before <main> so position: sticky works against full-page scroll — skipped while the Universal Editor is actively instrumenting the block (data-aue-resource present) to avoid breaking editor overlays.

CSS

.dismiss.dismiss-is-closing {
  opacity: 0;
}
body > .dismiss-wrapper:has(.dismiss.sticky) {
  position: sticky;
  top: var(--nav-height);
  z-index: var(--z-index-anchor-nav);
}
.dismiss-close::before,
.dismiss-close::after {
  background: currentcolor;
  content: '';
  ...
}

The close button is a pure-CSS "X" built from two rotated pseudo-elements rather than an icon font glyph (contrast with the source component's abbv-icon-close icon-font class).

Key differences from AEM implementation

Source (dismiss-content)Target (dismiss)
Content sourceIncluded Short-ISI/Flex Content Page fragment via path browserRich text authored directly on the block
CookieSession-scoped via a shared cookieObj utility, no explicit expiry in this component's own codeExplicit 365-day cookie with SameSite=Lax; Secure
LayoutSingle inline layout onlyInline or Sticky (fixed-to-top) variant
Close iconIcon-font glyphCSS-drawn X (no icon font dependency)
Analytics/MVAFull digital-data bean (component/link/interaction/MVA) on the close clickNot present — handled by whatever generic EDS analytics layer sits outside the block
Cross-page dismissShared cookie name links dismissal across pagesSame mechanism preserved; defaults to page path if left blank (source has no such default, an empty cookie name is a broken authoring state)

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
cookieNametextfieldcookieNameSame nameIdentical purpose and identical field name on both sides
shortIsiPathpathbrowsercontentRenamedSource references a separately-authored fragment page by path; target authors the content directly as richtext inline on the block — different authoring model, same rendered outcome
closeContentCheckboxcheckbox—Not mappedTarget's close button is always present (always dismissible); there is no field to disable it
resetContentCheckboxcheckboxresetOnLoadRenamedSame behavior (delete cookie on every page load)
anchorIDtextfield—Not mappedNo dedicated anchor field; likely covered generically by the shared _common-properties.json block ID convention rather than this field specifically
customCssClassestextfieldclassesRenamedSource allows arbitrary free-text classes; target constrains to an enumerated select (currently just Default/Sticky)
interactionIdtextfield (system)—Not mappedNo analytics/interaction-ID concept implemented in the target block
mvaCheckcheckbox—Not mappedNo MVA/analytics tagging fields in the target model
mvaAnalyticsTitletextfield—Not mappedSame reason as mvaCheck
mvaValueselect (datasource)—Not mappedSame reason as mvaCheck; the datasource-backed MVA taxonomy has no EDS-side equivalent
secondaryMvacheckbox—Not mappedSame reason as mvaCheck
(none in source)—closeLabelNot mapped (net-new)New accessible-label field for the close button with no source-side equivalent (source hardcodes aria-label="close")

Dismissible Block — Technical Details

What it does

A minimal close button that, once clicked, removes its entire containing .section from the DOM for the remainder of the browser session (via sessionStorage, not a cookie).

Architecture

This is an EDS-native block with no direct AEM source component. The manifest lists an empty aemSources array for it, and no component in the source system tree implements this specific session-storage-based, whole-section-removal pattern (the closest relative is dismiss-content, covered separately above, which uses a cookie and only hides its own widget div rather than the parent section, and requires a Short-ISI/Flex Content path to render at all). dismissible should be read as a lighter-weight, general-purpose sibling to dismiss/dismiss-content rather than a migration of any specific source component.

EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/dismissible/.

File Structure

blocks/dismissible/
├── dismissible.js
├── dismissible.css
├── _dismissible.json
├── block-config.js
├── rinvoq/
│   ├── dismissible.css
│   ├── _dismissible.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── ... (botox/, abbvie/ brand folders follow the same pattern)

Authoring Model (_dismissible.json)

{
  "id": "dismissible",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Content", "name": "content" },
    { "component": "richtext", "name": "text", "value": "", "label": "Content",
      "description": "Rich text content for the dismissible banner. A close button is added automatically.", "valueType": "string" },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Settings", "name": "settings" },
    { "component": "select", "valueType": "string", "name": "classes_variant", "label": "Variant",
      "options": [ { "name": "Default (inline)", "value": "" }, { "name": "Sticky (fixed top)", "value": "sticky" } ] },
    { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
  ]
}

How the JS works (dismissible.js)

export default function decorate(block) {
  const storageKey = `dismissible-${window.location.pathname}`;
  if (sessionStorage.getItem(storageKey) === 'closed') {
    block.closest('.section')?.remove();
    return;
  }

  const closeBtn = document.createElement('button');
  closeBtn.className = 'dismissible-close';
  closeBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Close');

  block.append(closeBtn);

  closeBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
    sessionStorage.setItem(storageKey, 'closed');
    block.classList.add('is-closing');
    block.addEventListener('transitionend', () => block.closest('.section')?.remove(), { once: true });
  });
}

The storage key is derived automatically from window.location.pathname — there is no authorable "cookie name" field, so dismissal state cannot be intentionally shared across different page paths the way dismiss's cookieName field allows. It is also sessionStorage, not a persistent cookie, so state resets when the browser tab/session ends, unlike dismiss's 365-day cookie.

CSS

.dismissible {
  background-color: var(--dismissible-bg, var(--light-color, #f4f4f4));
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--dismissible-border, #e0e0e0);
  position: relative;
  padding: var(--spacing-12, 12px) var(--spacing-48, 48px) var(--spacing-12, 12px) var(--spacing-24, 24px);
  opacity: 1;
  transition: opacity 0.3s ease;
}
.dismissible.sticky {
  position: sticky;
  top: var(--nav-height, 64px);
  z-index: var(--z-index-dismissible, 90);
}

Every custom property here has an inline fallback (e.g. var(--dismissible-bg, var(--light-color, #f4f4f4))), a defensive pattern distinct from dismiss.css, which assumes the design tokens are always defined.

Important Constraints

  • Dismissal is per-browser-session and per-exact-path only — no cross-page or persistent-across-visits dismissal is possible, unlike the cookie-backed dismiss block.
  • Removing the whole .section (not just the block) means any other blocks placed in the same section are also removed when the banner is dismissed — an important authoring constraint (this block should generally be the only content in its section).

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

Not applicable in the standard sense — there is no real AEM source component to map from. For completeness, its fields are simply: text (richtext content), classes_variant (layout select), plus the shared common-properties include. None of these have an AEM-side origin to classify against.

Doctor Locator Components — Technical Details

What it does

The source system ships two separate, largely parallel "find a doctor" components under this manifest entry:

  • doctor-locator ("Doctor Locator (Brand API)") — a ZIP-code search form with specialty/condition filters, radius filtering, paginated map+list results, and per-brand marker styling, driven by DrLocatorModel.java.
  • doctor-locator-app ("Doctor Locator (AbbVie)") — a second, separately-implemented variant with its own model (DrLocatorAppModel.java) and its own inline script-object HTL file, oriented around an organization-wide brand/indication admin-config structure (referencing /content/admin/BRANDNAME/... paths) rather than the simpler Brand-API-keyed configuration of the first component.

Both are Google Maps-backed doctor-search widgets; they are not the same component reused, but two independently maintained implementations of the same concept.

Architecture (doctor-locator)

Author sets indication, specialty[], defaultSpecialtyVal, conditionsTreated[], radiusOptions[],
             plus ~30 text/label/error-message fields and API version/endpoint-type selects
         ↓
DrLocatorModel.java (Sling Model, adaptable from Resource) resolves:
  - googleMapsKey from CommonConfigServiceImpl ("drlocator.maps.api.key" config property)
  - drLocatorBeans (radius options, parsed from JSON multifield rows)
  - drLocatorSpecialtyBeans (specialty checkboxes/radios, default-flagged)
  - drLocatorCondtnBeans (condition filter options, default-flagged)
         ↓
doctor-locator.html renders:
  <script src="https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/js?key=${drLocatorModel.googleMapsKey}...">
  <div class="abbv-dr-locator" data-search-radius data-results-perPage data-speciality
       data-api-endpoint="${properties.apiEndPointType}" data-api-version="${properties.apiVersion}">
    <form> ZIP input, "use my location" button, specialty checkboxes/radios,
           condition radios, terms checkbox, submit/reset, reCAPTCHA </form>
    <div class="DLResults"> radius filter, pagination, map div, list div </div>
    <div class="loading"> spinner + error message span </div>
  </div>

Sling Model — DrLocatorModel.java

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class DrLocatorModel {
    @Inject @Optional private List<String> radiusOptions;
    @Inject @Optional private List<String> conditionsTreated;
    @Inject @Named("indication") @Optional private String indication;
    @Inject @Named("specialty") @Optional private String[] specialty;
    @Inject @Named("defaultCondition") @Optional private String defaultCondition;
    @Inject @Named("defaultSpecialtyVal") @Optional private String defaultSpecialtyVal;
    @Inject @Source("osgi-services") private CommonConfigServiceImpl commonConfigService;

    private List<DrLocatorBean> drLocatorBeans;          // radius options
    private List<DrLocatorBean> drLocatorCondtnBeans;    // condition filter options
    private List<DrLocatorBean> drLocatorSpecialtyBeans; // specialty options
    private String googleMapsKey = "";

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        googleMapsKey = commonConfigService.getConfigPropertyVal("drlocator.maps.api.key");
        if (radiusOptions != null && !radiusOptions.isEmpty()) { radiusOptions(); }
        if (defaultCondition != null && !defaultCondition.isEmpty()) {
            defaultConditionVal = defaultCondition.split("\\|")[1];
        }
        if (conditionsTreated != null && !conditionsTreated.isEmpty()) { conditionsTreated(); }
        if (specialty != null) { specialtyOptions(specialty); }
        // In authoring mode only: resolve the inherited admin-config path and
        // write it back into the dialog via ModifiableValueMap + resourceResolver.commit()
    }
}

Two behaviors are notable: (1) the Google Maps API key is pulled from an OSGi-backed configuration service by a fixed config key (drlocator.maps.api.key) rather than being an authorable dialog field, and (2) in author mode the model performs a write-back — it resolves an inherited page property and persists it onto the component's own node (adminConfigPath) via ModifiableValueMap and an explicit resourceResolver.commit(), a side-effecting read-modify-write inside a Sling Model's @PostConstruct, not just a read.

Dialog

An eight-tab dialog (~45 fields total across tabs); the largest of the components in this batch. Representative fields per tab:

TabFieldPropertyTypeNote
OverviewTitletitletextfieldSection heading
OverviewSearch Input PlaceholdersearchInputPlaceholdertextfield
OverviewDisable Use My LocationdisableUseMyLocationcheckboxHides the geolocation button
OverviewEnable Virtual VisitsenableVirtualVisitscheckboxGates an entire virtual-visit CTA subsystem
OverviewVirtual Visits IconvirtualVisitIconselect (datasource: ce-link-icon)
Specialty OptionsIndicationindicationselect (datasource: ce-doctor-locator-indication-list)Drives data-indication-id on the widget
Specialty OptionsSpecialty Selection TypespecialtySelectionTypeselect (checkbox / radiobtn)Switches the specialty filter's UI control type
Specialty OptionsDefault SpecialtydefaultSpecialtyValselect (datasource: ce-doctor-locator-specialties-list)
Conditions TreatedEnable Condition FilterenableConditionsFiltercheckbox
Conditions TreatedConditions Treated filtersconditionsTreatedmultifield of conditionOption selectsEach option stored as a JSON string, parsed by the model
Conditions TreatedDefault ConditiondefaultConditionselect (datasource: ce-dr-locator-condition-filter)Required for certain indications per the field's own help text
Terms & ConditionsTerms & Condition TexttermsCondTextnested Text component (includewidget)Embeds the standalone Text component's editor inline
Search ResultsResults Per PageresultsPerPagenumberfield (5–26, default 10)
Search ResultsMap LocationmapLocationselect (datasource: ce-map-location)
Search ResultsDefault Search RadiusdefaultRadiusnumberfield (default 25, step 5)
Search ResultsRadius Filter OptionsradiusOptionsmultifield of radiusOption selects
MarkersMarker ColormarkerFilltextfield (hex)Defaults to #071d49 if unset in HTL
MarkersMarker Outline ColormarkerStroketextfield (hex)Defaults to #ffffff
MarkersSVG D PathmarkerPathtextareaRaw SVG path data embedded directly into a <path> element
Error MessagesZIP Code Submission ErrorzipcodeErrortextfieldReused as fallback if length/character-specific errors are blank
Error MessagesNo results error messagenoresultsErrortextfield
Error MessagesNo Services ErrorapiServicesErrortextfield
API Metadata ConfigDoctor Locator API VersionapiVersionselect (V1–V5, default V3)
API Metadata ConfigAPI End Point TypeapiEndPointTypeselect (prod/int/stage)

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<script src="https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/js?key=${drLocatorModel.googleMapsKey}&loading=async"></script>
<div class="abbv-dr-locator ${properties.customCssClasses}" data-search-radius="${properties.defaultRadius}"
     data-results-perPage="${properties.resultsPerPage}" data-speciality="${properties.defaultSpecialtyVal}"
     data-condition="${properties.enableConditionsFilter ? drLocatorModel.defaultConditionVal : ''}"
     data-indication-id="${drLocatorModel.indication}"
     data-marker-fill="${properties.markerFill ? properties.markerFill : '#071d49'}"
     data-marker-stroke="${properties.markerStroke ? properties.markerStroke : '#ffffff'}"
     data-api-endpoint="${properties.apiEndPointType}" data-api-version="${properties.apiVersion}">
    <form action="javascript:void(0);">
        <input type="text" pattern="[0-9]{5}" id="zip_code"/>
        <button id="use_geo">...</button>
        <div class="g-recaptcha js-v2" data-sitekey="${fapModel.captchaSiteKey}" data-badge="inline" data-size="invisible"></div>
    </form>
    <div class="DLResults">
        <div class="drMap"><div id="docMap"></div></div>
        <div class="drList"><div id="docList"><ul></ul></div></div>
    </div>
</div>

The form's action="javascript:void(0);" confirms all submission handling is client-side JS (not a real HTTP form post); the visible markup only lays out the DOM hooks (#zip_code, #docMap, #docList, .DLResults) that an external clientlib script (loaded via the doctor-locator-component.v1 category, deferred) attaches behavior to. That client script itself is not part of the files reviewed here — the HTL only proves the DOM contract it depends on.

Important Constraints

  • Requires a live Google Maps JavaScript API key sourced from a shared OSGi config service — not something an individual page author can override per-instance.
  • reCAPTCHA site key comes from a different, unrelated model (fapModel.captchaSiteKey) referenced directly in this component's HTL, an implicit cross-component dependency.
  • Marker SVG path is raw author-entered path data injected directly into SVG markup with only a textarea field — no validation against malformed path syntax.
  • The second, parallel doctor-locator-app component duplicates most of this surface with its own model and admin-config-path convention, meaning any migration must decide whether both are in scope or only one representative variant is carried forward.

EDS Migration — Ground Truth vs. Design Narrative

The live EDS field model for doctor-locator is deliberately minimal — six fields — and must not be confused with a richer design narrative that has circulated for this block. Per the block requirements ground truth (models-normalized.json), the shipped model is exactly:

FieldComponentLabelDefault
headingtextHeading"Find a Doctor"
apitextAPI Endpoint"" — receives ?zip=&limit= query params
placeholdertextZIP Input Placeholder"Enter ZIP code"
no-resultstextNo Results Message"No results found."
errortextError Message"An error occurred. Please try again."
results-per-pagetextResults Per Page"5"

Any narrative describing additional fields such as a search "mode" toggle, a gender-search filter, or a selectable "API endpoint type" (production/integration/staging, mirroring the source's apiEndPointType) is proposed-only and not implemented in the current block. The real _doctor-locator.json confirms this six-field set exactly, and doctor-locator.js confirms the block reads no other configuration keys from its authored rows except the fixed list ['api', 'maps-key', 'placeholder', 'no-results', 'error', 'results-per-page'] — of which maps-key itself is unused by the model/JSON and dead code in the JS (a leftover key from an earlier design iteration, not a live field).

EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/doctor-locator/, but it is a ground-up rebuild rather than a port: a single flat text-based fetch to an arbitrary JSON API (?zip=&limit=), no map rendering, no specialty/condition filters, no reCAPTCHA, and no Google Maps dependency at all.

File Structure

blocks/doctor-locator/
├── doctor-locator.js
├── doctor-locator.css
├── _doctor-locator.json
├── block-config.js
├── rinvoq/
│   ├── doctor-locator.css
│   ├── _doctor-locator.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── ... (botox/, abbvie/ brand folders follow the same pattern)

Authoring Model (_doctor-locator.json)

{
  "id": "doctor-locator",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Content", "name": "content" },
