    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "heading", "value": "Find a Doctor", "label": "Heading" },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Configuration", "name": "config" },
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "api", "value": "", "label": "API Endpoint",
      "description": "URL of the doctor search API. Receives ?zip=&limit= query parameters." },
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "placeholder", "value": "Enter ZIP code", "label": "ZIP Input Placeholder" },
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "no-results", "value": "No results found.", "label": "No Results Message" },
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "error", "value": "An error occurred. Please try again.", "label": "Error Message" },
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "results-per-page", "value": "5", "label": "Results Per Page" },
    { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
  ]
}

How the JS works (doctor-locator.js)

export default async function decorate(block) {
  const rows = [...block.children];
  const config = {};
  const textRows = [];

  rows.forEach((row) => {
    const cells = [...row.children];
    const key = cells[0]?.textContent.trim().toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, '-');
    const knownKeys = ['api', 'maps-key', 'placeholder', 'no-results', 'error', 'results-per-page'];
    if (knownKeys.includes(key)) {
      config[key] = cells[1]?.textContent.trim();
    } else {
      textRows.push(row);
    }
  });

  const heading = textRows[0]?.textContent.trim() || 'Find a Doctor';
  ...
  async function search(zip) {
    if (!/^\d{5}$/.test(zip)) { status.textContent = 'Please enter a valid 5-digit ZIP code.'; return; }
    const resp = await fetch(`${config.api}?zip=${zip}&limit=${config['results-per-page'] || 5}`);
    const data = await resp.json();
    const doctors = data.results || data.doctors || data || [];
    ...
  }

  geoBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
    navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition(async (pos) => {
      const resp = await fetch(`https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/reverse?lat=${pos.coords.latitude}&lon=${pos.coords.longitude}&format=json`);
      const zip = (await resp.json()).address?.postcode?.slice(0, 5);
      if (zip) { input.value = zip; search(zip); }
    }, () => { status.textContent = 'Location access denied. Please enter ZIP code manually.'; });
  });
}

The "Use my location" flow reverse-geocodes via the free OpenStreetMap Nominatim API to derive a ZIP code from coordinates, rather than any Google/Maps-based geocoding — a deliberate dependency swap consistent with the block dropping the Google Maps API key requirement entirely. Results render as a plain <ul> list (name/address/distance), with no map view.

CSS

.doctor-locator-form {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: var(--spacing-8, 8px);
  margin-bottom: var(--spacing-16, 16px);
}
.doctor-locator-status {
  color: var(--text-color, #111);
  font-size: var(--body-font-size-s, 0.875rem);
  min-height: 1.5em;
}

Key differences from AEM implementation

Source (doctor-locator / doctor-locator-app)Target (doctor-locator)
MapGoogle Maps JS API-rendered interactive map with custom SVG markersNone — list results only
FiltersSpecialty (checkbox/radio), condition-treated (radio), radius select, indication-scoped defaultsNone — ZIP + results-per-page only
Terms & Conditions / reCAPTCHARequired checkbox + invisible reCAPTCHA v2 badge before submitNone
GeolocationBrowser geolocation feeding an internal API (implementation not in the reviewed HTL)Browser geolocation reverse-geocoded via OpenStreetMap Nominatim
API configurationFixed OSGi-config Maps key + versioned/environment-selected backend endpoint (prod/int/stage, V1–V5)Single free-text API URL field, any backend
PaginationFull paged results with page-range header textNone — single fetch, "Results Per Page" only limits requested count

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
titletextfieldheadingRenamedSame purpose (section heading text)
searchInputPlaceholdertextfieldplaceholderRenamedSame purpose
noresultsErrortextfieldno-resultsRenamedSame purpose
apiServicesErrortextfielderrorRenamedSource distinguishes API-down errors from other error states; target has a single generic error field covering both fetch failure and bad input
resultsPerPagenumberfield (5–26)results-per-pageRenamedSource constrains to a numeric range with a UI stepper; target is a free-text field defaulting to "5"
(implicit: OSGi drlocator.maps.api.key config)—apiRenamedSource had no authorable API URL at all (fixed Maps key + versioned backend selection); target exposes one plain, author-editable endpoint URL — a genuine architecture change, not a like-for-like rename, but the closest conceptual counterpart
indicationselect (datasource)—Not mappedNo indication-scoping concept in the target model
specialty (multi)checkbox group—Not mappedNo specialty filter implemented
defaultSpecialtyValselect—Not mappedSame reason as specialty
specialtySelectionTypeselect—Not mappedSame reason as specialty
enableConditionsFiltercheckbox—Not mappedNo condition-treated filter implemented
conditionsTreated (multifield)fieldset/multifield—Not mappedSame reason as enableConditionsFilter
defaultConditionselect—Not mappedSame reason as enableConditionsFilter
disableUseMyLocationcheckbox—Not mappedTarget's geolocation button is always present; there is no field to hide it
useMyLocationtextfield—Not mappedTarget hardcodes the button label ("Use my location") in JS rather than exposing it as a field
enableVirtualVisits, availableTitle, virtualVisitIcon, appointmentText, virtualVisitText, exitmodalIDvarious—Not mappedEntire virtual-visit CTA subsystem has no equivalent in the target block
termsCondnLabel, termsCondText, tncErrorvarious—Not mappedNo terms-and-conditions gate before search in the target block
mapLocation, defaultRadius, radiusFilterLabel, radiusOptionsvarious—Not mappedNo map or radius filtering implemented
markerFill, markerStroke, markerLableColor, markerPathvarious—Not mappedNo map/marker rendering implemented
zipcodeError, zipcodeLengthError, zipcodeCharError, locationErrortextfields—Not mappedTarget uses one hardcoded validation string ("Please enter a valid 5-digit ZIP code.") rather than four distinct authorable messages
apiVersion, apiEndPointTypeselects—Not mappedTarget's single api URL field replaces the version/environment-selection pattern entirely — an author now points directly at a concrete endpoint rather than choosing a version+environment that resolves to one server-side

Dr.com Widget Component — Technical Details

What it does

Embeds a third-party physician-directory widget (a <directory-widget> custom element supplied by an external script from Doctor.com) into the page, passing a partner site ID and optionally pre-filling a ZIP code from the page's own query string.

Architecture

Author sets partnerSiteid, apiEndPointType, anchorID, customClass
         ↓
drcom-widget.html (pure HTL, no Java Sling Model) renders:
  <div class="abbv-drcom-widget" data-env="${properties.apiEndPointType}" id="widget-container"></div>
         ↓
deferred clientlib (drcomwidget.v1) loads ui/drcom-widget.js:
  reads data-env → sets a <script id="directory-widget"> src to that env URL
  (falls back to 'https://widget.doctor.com/directory/app.js' if data-env is empty)
         ↓
inline <script> (in the HTL itself) then always runs, regardless of clientlib load order:
  creates <directory-widget partner="...">, sets zipcode from ?zip= query param,
  appends it into #widget-container

Sling Model

There is no Java Sling Model for this component — confirmed both by the absence of any Drcom*.java file under the source models package and by the HTL, which reads every field directly off ${properties.*} (properties.customClass, properties.apiEndPointType, properties.anchorID, properties.partnerSiteid) with no data-sly-use of any backing class.

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor IDanchorIDtextfieldFor deep-linking to the component
Custom CSS ClassescustomClasstextfield
Partner Site IDpartnerSiteidtextfield"Passed to Dr.com so it knows how to configure the data you receive"
Choose the API endpoint to retrieve dataapiEndPointTypeselect (datasource: ce-dr-com-widget)Determines which environment URL the widget script loads from

A duplicate, simplified ui/dialog.json also exists describing the same four fields for an alternate (non-Coral) authoring surface, with the endpoint select hardcoded to ["Select", "Production", "Test"] rather than the live datasource — evidence of the dialog having been ported/duplicated across two authoring UI generations without being kept perfectly in sync.

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<div class="${properties.customClass} abbv-drcom-widget" data-env="${properties.apiEndPointType}" id="widget-container"></div>
<sly data-sly-use.clientLib="${'/apps/clientlib-async/sightly/templates/clientlib.html'}" data-sly-unwrap>
    <sly data-sly-call="${clientLib.js @ categories='drcomwidget.v1',loading='defer'}" data-sly-unwrap></sly>
</sly>
<script type="text/javascript">
   (function () {
   const queryParams = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
   const container = document.getElementById("widget-container");
   const widget = document.createElement('directory-widget');
   widget.partner = ${properties.partnerSiteid @ context='scriptString'};
   widget.zipcode = queryParams.get('zip') || '';
   container.appendChild(widget);
   })()
</script>

CSS

No component-specific CSS file exists for drcom-widget in the source system (only a clientlibs/css.txt manifest with no entries found) — visual presentation is entirely delegated to the external Doctor.com widget script itself.

Important Constraints

  • The inline HTL script that appends the <directory-widget> element runs unconditionally on page load and does not wait for the deferred clientlib script to actually finish loading and define the custom element — a race condition inherent to the source markup (the browser's custom-elements upgrade mechanism papers over this in practice, since undefined custom elements silently accept properties until their definition registers, but there's no explicit ordering guarantee in this component's own code).
  • widget.partner and widget.zipcode are set as JS properties on the element, not HTML attributes — meaning the widget's implementation must read them via property getters, not getAttribute().
  • No loading state, spinner, or error handling of any kind if the external script fails to load.

EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/drcom-widget/. It preserves the same third-party <directory-widget> integration but adds a proper loading state, viewport-based lazy loading, and error handling — all absent from the source component.

File Structure

blocks/drcom-widget/
├── drcom-widget.js
├── drcom-widget.css
├── _drcom-widget.json
├── block-config.js
├── rinvoq/
│   ├── drcom-widget.css
│   ├── _drcom-widget.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── ... (botox/, abbvie/ brand folders follow the same pattern)

Authoring Model (_drcom-widget.json)

{
  "id": "drcom-widget",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Content", "name": "content" },
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "heading", "value": "", "label": "Heading",
      "description": "Optional heading shown above the Dr.com widget." },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Configuration", "name": "config" },
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "partner-id", "value": "", "label": "Partner ID",
      "description": "Required. The Dr.com partner site ID provided by Doctor.com." },
    { "component": "select", "valueType": "string", "name": "api-env", "label": "API Environment",
      "options": [ { "name": "Production", "value": "prod" }, { "name": "Staging / Test", "value": "test" } ] },
    { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
  ]
}

How the JS works (drcom-widget.js)

const DRCOM_ENDPOINTS = {
  prod: 'https://widget.doctor.com/directory/app.js',
  test: 'https://widget-staging.doctor.com/directory/app.js',
};

function loadDrcomScript(env) {
  const src = DRCOM_ENDPOINTS[env] || DRCOM_ENDPOINTS.prod;
  if (document.getElementById('drcom-script')) return Promise.resolve();
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    const script = document.createElement('script');
    script.id = 'drcom-script';
    script.src = src;
    script.defer = true;
    script.onload = resolve;
    script.onerror = reject;
    document.head.append(script);
  });
}

export default function decorate(block) {
  ...
  const { 'partner-id': partnerId, heading, 'api-env': apiEnv = 'prod' } = config;
  if (!partnerId) {
    block.textContent = 'drcom-widget: partner-id is required.';
    return;
  }
  ...
  const observer = new IntersectionObserver(async (entries) => {
    if (!entries[0].isIntersecting) return;
    observer.disconnect();
    try {
      await loadDrcomScript(apiEnv);
      const widget = document.createElement('directory-widget');
      widget.setAttribute('widget.partner', partnerId);
      const zipParam = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search).get('zip');
      if (zipParam) widget.setAttribute('widget.zipcode', zipParam);
      placeholder.replaceWith(widget);
    } catch {
      placeholder.textContent = 'Doctor search is currently unavailable.';
      placeholder.removeAttribute('aria-busy');
    }
  }, { rootMargin: '200px' });
  observer.observe(block);
}

Two concrete behavioral upgrades over the source: (1) the widget script and the <directory-widget> element are only created once the block scrolls near the viewport (IntersectionObserver, 200px margin) rather than unconditionally on page load, and (2) the widget's partner ID and ZIP are now set as real HTML attributes (widget.partner, widget.zipcode — note these are literal attribute names containing a dot, not nested properties) via setAttribute rather than as JS object properties, removing the source's custom-element-upgrade race condition.

CSS

.drcom-widget-placeholder {
  background: var(--light-color, #f4f4f4);
  border-radius: 4px;
  min-height: 200px;
  position: relative;
}
.drcom-widget-placeholder[aria-busy='true']::after {
  animation: drcom-spin 0.8s linear infinite;
  border: 3px solid var(--light-color, #e0e0e0);
  border-top-color: var(--link-color, #0057cc);
  border-radius: 50%;
  ...
}

Key differences from AEM implementation

Source (drcom-widget)Target (drcom-widget)
Script loadingDeferred clientlib, unconditional on page loadLazy — only when the block nears the viewport
Widget data passingJS object properties (widget.partner, widget.zipcode as properties)HTML attributes (widget.partner, widget.zipcode as literal attribute names)
Loading stateNoneAnimated placeholder with aria-busy
Error handlingNonePlaceholder replaced with a user-facing unavailability message
Environment selectionDatasource-backed select, value passed through as a raw data-env URLFixed two-option enum (prod/test) mapped internally to concrete URLs
Optional headingNot supportedOptional heading field rendered as an <h2> above the widget

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
partnerSiteidtextfieldpartner-idRenamedSame purpose (Dr.com partner identifier)
apiEndPointTypeselect (datasource: ce-dr-com-widget)api-envRenamedSource's datasource-backed list of environment URLs is replaced by a fixed two-value enum (prod/test) resolved to hardcoded URLs in the JS, rather than an admin-configurable datasource
anchorIDtextfield—Not mappedNo anchor-ID field in the target model; likely covered generically by the shared common-properties block ID rather than this specific field
customClasstextfield—Not mappedNo free-text custom-class field in the target model
(none in source)—headingNot mapped (net-new)New optional heading field with no source-side equivalent — the source component has no title/heading of its own

EDS Form Block — Technical Details

What it does

A generic, spreadsheet/JSON-schema-driven form builder. Rather than authoring individual fields in a dialog, the author points the block at a spreadsheet (or JSON) source that defines the form's fields, and at a submission endpoint; the block fetches that schema at runtime and renders typed inputs, radios, checkboxes, selects, and buttons dynamically, plus a "Find an Injector" specialization with map/geolocation search.

Architecture

Author places two links in the block: a "source" link (spreadsheet/JSON schema URL) and a "submit" link (endpoint)
         ↓
eds-form.js decorate(block):
  IntersectionObserver — when block enters viewport:
    new FormTypeHandler(block) → initialize()   (from find-an-injector.js — recognizes special form types e.g. "find-an-injector")
    loadSpreadsheet(source) → fetch JSON schema rows
    buildForm(data, submit, formTypeHandler)
      → buildField() per row, dispatched by row.type/category
          (text/textarea/radio/checkbox/select/submit/reset/terms/recaptcha/...
           or a formTypeHandler.createSpecialField() override for form-type-specific fields)
      → enableConditionals(form)   — show/hide fields based on `conditional` expressions
      → enableSubmission(form, submit, fields)
    block.replaceChildren(form)
    formTypeHandler.finalizeForm(form)   — e.g. wires up map/geolocation for find-an-injector

Sling Model — the AEM source is a design-pattern showcase, not an authorable component

forms-text-inputs has no .content.xml, no _cq_dialog, no HTL template, and no Java Sling Model anywhere in the source tree. It exists only as ui/forms-text-inputs.php and ui/variations.php — PHP-rendered pages in the pattern-library/style-guide section of the source system (a CQ5-era "admp catalog" showcase used to document markup conventions and default styling for text inputs, text areas, and number counters, exercised by jQuery-driven disabled/error/valid state toggles in variations.php). It is not something a page author can drag onto a page — it is documentation of how forms should be hand-built using the underlying form CSS/markup conventions, not a reusable component in its own right. Any "Sling Model" or "Dialog" section for this entry would be fabricated; there genuinely is none.

Dialog

None exists (see above). The showcase page instead hardcodes example markup directly, e.g.:

<div class="abbv-form-wrapper label-inline">
    <div class="label-wrapper">
        <label class="abbv-label-text" for="Text">Text Input Label</label>
        <button class="abbv-button-plain more-info abbv-icon-info i-a"></button>
    </div>
    <input type="text" pattern="[0-9]{5}" placeholder="Text Field (Zipcode Pattern)" name="Text">
    <p class="abbv-additional-info">This is a long description...</p>
    <span class="abbv-error-msg" data-err-msg="Invalid Zip Code"></span>
</div>

Important Constraints (source)

  • Because there is no real component behind this, there is no field inventory to port — the "migration" is conceptual: the target block (eds-form) reimplements the general idea of "organization-styled form fields" as a generic, data-driven renderer rather than porting any specific authorable component.

EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/eds-form/ and is substantially larger and more capable than anything in the showcase page it nominally replaces — it is closer in scope to a lightweight forms framework than a single block.

File Structure

blocks/eds-form/
├── eds-form.js               (826 lines — field builders, form assembly, conditionals, submission)
├── eds-form.css
├── find-an-injector.js       (870 lines — FormTypeHandler, map/geolocation specialization)
├── maps.js                   (map rendering helpers used by find-an-injector)
├── recaptcha.js              (reCAPTCHA token helpers)
├── _eds-form.json
├── block-config.js
├── rinvoq/
│   ├── eds-form.css
│   ├── _eds-form.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── ... (botox/, abbvie/ brand folders follow the same pattern)

Authoring Model (_eds-form.json)

{
  "id": "eds-form",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "select", "name": "classes", "label": "Select Form", "valueType": "string",
      "options": [ { "name": "find-an-injector", "value": "find-an-injector" } ] },
    { "component": "aem-content", "name": "text", "label": "Form Link", "multi": false, "required": true },
    { "component": "aem-content", "name": "optionsLink", "label": "Options Link", "multi": false, "required": true,
      "condition": { "===": [ { "var": "classes" }, "find-an-injector" ] } },
    { "component": "boolean", "name": "showMap", "label": "Show Map", "valueType": "boolean", "value": true,
      "condition": { "===": [ { "var": "classes" }, "find-an-injector" ] } }
  ]
}

Only four fields are authorable — the actual field-by-field form structure is not authored in the Universal Editor at all, it lives in the external spreadsheet/JSON the text (Form Link) field points to. optionsLink and showMap only apply when the "find-an-injector" variant is selected (conditional visibility via a JSON-logic condition block).

How the JS works (eds-form.js)

export default function decorate(block) {
  block.style.visibility = 'hidden';
  const [source, submit] = [...block.querySelectorAll('a[href]')].map((a) => a.href);

  if (source) {
    const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
      entries.forEach(async (entry) => {
        if (entry.isIntersecting) {
          try {
            const formTypeHandler = new FormTypeHandler(block);
            await formTypeHandler.initialize();

            const resp = await loadSpreadsheet(source);
            if (!resp.ok) { throw new Error(`${resp.status}: ${resp.statusText}`); }
            const { data } = await resp.json();
            if (!data) throw new Error(`No form fields at ${source}`);

            const form = await buildForm(data, submit, formTypeHandler);
            block.replaceChildren(form);

            await formTypeHandler.finalizeForm(form);
            block.removeAttribute('style');
          } catch (error) {
            console.error('Could not build form from', source, error);
            block.parentElement.remove();
          }
          observer.disconnect();
        }
      });
    }, { threshold: 0 });
    observer.observe(block);
  } else {
    console.error('Unable to create form without source');
    block.parentElement.remove();
  }
}

Field construction (buildField) dispatches on each row's type/category: submit/reset buttons, radio/checkbox groups (buildOptions), category-specific special-cases like Terms and Condition (renders a checkbox + injected error paragraph) and recaptcha_text (renders the reCAPTCHA legal text), and defers first to formTypeHandler.createSpecialField() so a form-type specialization (like "find-an-injector") can override how any given row renders. Field grouping in buildForm sorts fields by category into inputs-container, filters-container, terms-conditions, and a submit wrapper.

CSS

.eds-form-container .eds-form-wrapper {
  max-width: var(--spacing-1200);
  margin: 0 auto;
}
.eds-form.find-an-injector {
  font-family: var(--form-font-family);
  margin: 0 auto;
  background: var(--form-background-color);
}

The stylesheet carries an explicit stylelint-disable and a code comment flagging it for revisit ("TODO Revisit Stylelint and Code Refactor during Block Development") — an acknowledged, not-yet-cleaned-up piece of the implementation.

Key differences from AEM implementation

Source (forms-text-inputs showcase)Target (eds-form)
NatureStatic style-guide page demonstrating markup conventionsFully functional, schema-driven form renderer
Field authoringN/A — hand-written HTML per exampleExternalized to a spreadsheet/JSON source fetched at runtime; only 4 fields (variant, form link, options link, show-map) are authored in the block itself
SpecializationN/AFormTypeHandler pattern supports named form variants (e.g. "find-an-injector") with map/geolocation/reCAPTCHA behavior
Validation/error UIDemonstrated via toggled CSS classes (abbv-error/abbv-valid) in the showcase's JS, not real validationReal conditional field logic (enableConditionals) and submission handling (enableSubmission)

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

Because forms-text-inputs is a documentation page rather than an authorable component, it has no dialog fields to classify. The table below instead documents the eds-form block's own four authorable fields for completeness, all of which are net-new (no AEM source counterpart):

EDS fieldTypeMapping typeNotes
classes (Select Form)selectNot mapped (net-new)No source-side concept of a named form variant/type — the entire schema-driven approach has no analog in the showcase page
text (Form Link)aem-contentNot mapped (net-new)The idea of an externally-fetched form schema has no source-side counterpart
optionsLink (Options Link)aem-content, conditionalNot mapped (net-new)Specific to the "find-an-injector" variant; no source equivalent
showMap (Show Map)boolean, conditionalNot mapped (net-new)Specific to the "find-an-injector" variant; no source equivalent

Efficacy Tabs Block — Technical Details

What it does

Renders nested tabs for presenting head-to-head/switch efficacy data: an outer tab per comparison ("H2H"), each with introductory copy and one or more inner tabs (e.g. per study week), and each inner tab showing a 60/40 chart-image-plus-descriptive-text layout.

Architecture

This is an EDS-native block with no direct AEM source component — the manifest lists an empty aemSources array, and no component under the source system's component tree implements this directive-keyword-driven nested-tabs pattern. It should be understood as a purpose-built block for a specific efficacy-data presentation need in the target implementation, not a migration of prior authored content.

Authoring is a FLAT table of directive rows, each: | rowKind | image | imageAlt | content |
  outer  → starts a new outer tab (label = content)
  intro  → header copy + objective, shown above the inner tabs for the current outer
  inner  → starts a new inner tab within the current outer (label = content)
  chart  → header copy + footnotes for the current inner tab's left/chart column; its own
           image cell carries the actual chart <picture>
  text   → rich text + callouts/CTAs for the current inner tab's right/text column
         ↓
efficacy-tabs.js decorate(block):
  parse flat rows into a tree: outers[] → { intro, introImage, inners[] { chart, chartImage, text } }
  render outer tablist + panels; render inner tablist (only if >1 inner tab) + panels per outer

Authoring Model

FieldPropertyTypeNote
efficacy-tabs (block-level model)
VariantclassestextFree-text class hook (e.g. for a brand-specific variant like the rinvoq-hcp CSS override folder)
efficacy-tabs-row (per-row item model, filtered to children of efficacy-tabs)
Row TyperowKindselect (outer / intro / inner / chart / text, default "text")The directive keyword driving the flat-table parser
ImageimagereferenceChart/intro picture, kept in its own cell so it doesn't get swallowed into the richtext content field during document-to-content conversion
Image Alt TextimageAlttextNote: authored as a separate field but the JS's pictureOf() reads the picture directly out of the same image cell — the alt text field's value is not separately consumed by efficacy-tabs.js itself; alt text is expected to already be baked into the <picture>/<img> markup from the reference component
ContentcontentrichtextLabel text (for outer/inner rows) or body copy (for intro/chart/text rows)

The block-level model's filters entry restricts efficacy-tabs to only accept efficacy-tabs-row children, enforcing the flat-table authoring shape at the Universal Editor level.

How the JS works (efficacy-tabs.js)

rows.forEach((row) => {
  const cells = [...row.children];
  const key = (cells[0]?.textContent || '').trim().toLowerCase();
  const picture = pictureOf(cells[1]);
  const contentCell = cells[2];
  const label = (contentCell?.textContent || '').trim();

  if (key === 'outer') {
    currentOuter = { label, intro: null, introImage: null, inners: [] };
    outers.push(currentOuter);
  } else if (key === 'intro' && currentOuter) {
    currentOuter.intro = contentCell;
    currentOuter.introImage = picture;
  } else if (key === 'inner' && currentOuter) {
    currentInner = { label, chart: null, chartImage: null, text: null };
    currentOuter.inners.push(currentInner);
  } else if (key === 'chart' && currentInner) {
    currentInner.chart = contentCell;
    currentInner.chartImage = picture;
  } else if (key === 'text' && currentInner) {
    currentInner.text = contentCell;
  }
});

A notable secondary behavior inside the chart-column rendering: the chart cell's content is split at the first child element carrying a .footnote class — everything before that split point becomes a full-width "chart header," everything from the footnote onward is appended below the chart image, letting one authored rich-text field serve two visually distinct zones without a second field. A second targeted DOM transform tags any standalone <p><strong>DATA LIMITATIONS...</strong></p> paragraph with an underline-brush class, deliberately excluding inline-bold text and callouts so only that specific label gets the decorative underline treatment. The inner tablist itself is only rendered when an outer tab has more than one inner tab — a single-inner-tab outer renders its chart/text content directly with no redundant single-item tab control.

CSS

.efficacy-tabs-panel[hidden] {
  display: none;
}
.efficacy-tabs-flex {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: 24px;
}
@media (width >= 985px) {
  .efficacy-tabs-flex {
    flex-direction: row;
    gap: 29px;
    align-items: flex-start;
  }
  .efficacy-tabs-image {
    flex: 0 0 740px;
    max-width: 740px;
  }
  .efficacy-tabs-text {
    flex: 1;
  }
}

Base efficacy-tabs.css holds only structural rules (flex layout, tab list, hidden-panel toggling); all brand-specific visual styling lives in per-brand override folders (e.g. rinvoq-hcp/efficacy-tabs.css and rinvoq-hcp/_efficacy-tabs.css) — notably this block currently ships only a rinvoq-hcp brand folder, not the fuller rinvoq/botox/abbvie set seen on the other blocks in this batch, consistent with it being a purpose-built block for one specific brand/audience context rather than a general cross-brand component.

Important Constraints

  • The directive-keyword flat-table format is fragile to authoring order — rows are strictly stateful (an inner/chart/text row with no preceding outer/inner row is silently dropped, since the parser guards every branch on currentOuter/currentInner being already set).
  • No live ARIA relationship (aria-controls/id pairing) is wired between tab buttons and their panels beyond role="tab"/role="tabpanel" and aria-selected — keyboard arrow-key navigation between tabs (the typical native tablist pattern) is not implemented; only click-to-select is wired.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

Not applicable — there is no real AEM source component to map from (confirmed empty aemSources in the manifest, and no matching component directory anywhere under the source system's component tree). The fields listed above (classes, rowKind, image, imageAlt, content) are all net-new to the target implementation with no source-side counterpart to classify against.

Embed Component — Technical Details

What it does

The manifest maps this target block to two source components: embed (a generic organization-approved iframe embed, for bringing arbitrary approved third-party experiences onto a page) and youtube (a dedicated YouTube player embed). Both ultimately render an iframe, but with materially different authoring surfaces and constraints.

Architecture (embed)

Author sets embedUrl, embedExperience, desktop/tablet/mobileHeight, anchorId, customCssClasses
         ↓
EmbedModel.java (near-empty Sling Model — only generates a unique component ID) via embed.html
         ↓
embed.html renders:
  <style> per-breakpoint iframe height rules, keyed by ebdModel.id </style>
  <div class="abbv-embed" data-unique-id="${ebdModel.id}">
      <iframe src="${properties.embedUrl}" title="${properties.embedExperience}"></iframe>
  </div>

Sling Model — EmbedModel.java

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class EmbedModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENT_ID_PREFIX = "abbv-embed";

    @Override
    protected String getIDPrefix() {
        return COMPONENT_ID_PREFIX;
    }
}

This is the thinnest model in the batch — it exists purely to generate a unique, prefixed component ID (inherited from AbstractComponentModel) so the inline per-breakpoint height <style> block can scope its selectors to one specific instance on the page ([data-unique-id="..."]). It reads none of the actual authored fields — those are all read directly from properties.* in the HTL.

Dialog (embed)

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Container Identifier (Anchor ID)anchorIdtextfieldNote the lowercase "d" — distinct from the anchorID convention used by most other components in this batch
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestextfield
Name this Embedded ExperienceembedExperiencetextfieldUsed as the iframe's title attribute and shown in the edit-mode component label
URL to EmbedembedUrlpathbrowserDirect iframe src
Height of Experience on DesktopdesktopHeightnumberfieldApplied via an inline <style> block, not an inline style attribute
Height of Experience on TablettabletHeightnumberfieldApplied at a max-width: 985px breakpoint
Height of Experience on MobilemobileHeightnumberfieldApplied at a max-width: 600px breakpoint

The dialog's own help text is explicit about governance: "This component can only be published by APPROVAL only. Please visit [an internal approvals portal] for approvals" — this is a deliberately gated, compliance-reviewed component in the source system, not a self-service one.

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure (embed)

<style>
    .abbv-embed[data-unique-id="${ebdModel.id @ context='text'}"] iframe { height: ${properties.desktopHeight @ context="styleToken"}px; }
    @media (max-width: 985px) { .abbv-embed[data-unique-id="${ebdModel.id @ context='text'}"] iframe { height:${properties.tabletHeight @ context="styleToken"}px } }
    @media (max-width: 600px) { .abbv-embed[data-unique-id="${ebdModel.id @ context='text'}"] iframe { height: ${properties.mobileHeight @ context="styleToken"}px; } }
</style>
<div class="abbv-embed ${properties.customCssClasses}">
    <div class="abbv-embed" data-unique-id="${ebdModel.id}">
        <iframe src="${properties.embedUrl}" title="${properties.embedExperience}"></iframe>
    </div>
</div>

Note the outer and inner <div> both carry the class abbv-embed — a duplicate-class structure in the source markup itself, not a transcription artifact.

Architecture (youtube, second source component)

Author sets youtubeVideoId, playerWidth, autoPlay, allowFullScreen, plus a full analytics tab
         ↓
YoutubeModel.java builds an analytics "digital data" bean for the video (link/component/interaction/MVA)
         ↓
youtube.html renders:
  <div class="abbv-youtube-player" id="${properties.anchorID}" data-digitaldata='...'>
      <iframe src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/${properties.youtubeVideoId}?autoplay=...&enablejsapi=1"
              style="width:${properties.playerWidth || '100%'}"
              allowfullscreen="${properties.allowFullScreen=='true'}">
      </iframe>
  </div>

Sling Model — YoutubeModel.java

@Model(adaptables = { SlingHttpServletRequest.class })
public class YoutubeModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENTIDPREFIX = "abbv-youtube";
    ...
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String youtubeVideoId;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String analyticsTitle;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String mvaPrimary;
    ...
    private void setAnalyticsData() {
        Link link = DigitalDataObject.getAnalyticsLinkBean(checkNullProperty(analyticsTitle), "", "external",
                "https://www.youtube.com/embed/" + youtubeVideoId);
        Component component = DigitalDataObject.getAnalyticsComponentBean("Video", checkNullProperty(l10Title),
                checkNullProperty(sectionTitle), "Youtube", DigitalDataConstants.CONTENT);
        ...
        analyticsString = DigitalDataObject.getDigitalDataObject(link, new Menu(), component, new Content(), interaction, mva);
    }
}

Unlike EmbedModel, YoutubeModel is a full analytics-bearing model — it builds a complete "digital data" bean (link/component/interaction/MVA) describing the video as an analytics-trackable asset, exposed to the HTL as data-digitaldata on the player wrapper.

Dialog (youtube)

TabFieldPropertyTypeNote
OverviewYouTube Identifier (Anchor ID)anchorIDtextfield
OverviewVideo IDyoutubeVideoIdtextfield (required)Bare YouTube video ID, not a full URL
OverviewWidthplayerWidthtextfieldRaw CSS width value (e.g. "100px"); defaults to 100% if blank
OverviewAuto play the VideoautoPlaycheckboxWhen true, appends ?autoplay=1&mute=1 (browsers generally block non-muted autoplay, hence the forced mute)
OverviewAllow Full ScreenallowFullScreencheckbox
OverviewProject NumberepassNumbertextfieldPer-video project/approval tracking number; required per video per the field description
AnalyticsAnalytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtextfield (disabled/system)
AnalyticsAnalytics TitleanalyticsTitletextfieldAlso used in sitemap generation for embedded projects, per its field description
AnalyticsSection Title for AnalyticssectionTitletextfield
AnalyticsLocalized Section Title for Analyticsl10TitletextfieldNon-English sites only, per its field description
AnalyticsMost Valued Action (MVA)mvaPrimarycheckbox
AnalyticsMVA Title for AnalyticsmvaTitleForAnalyticstextfield
AnalyticsMost Valued Action Type and CategorymvaTypeAndCategoryselect (datasource)
AnalyticsMVA SecondaryanalyticsEnableMVASecondarycheckbox

CSS

Neither embed nor youtube ships a component-specific CSS file in the source tree (only empty/unused clientlib*/css.txt manifests) — both rely on their inline <style> block (embed's per-breakpoint heights) or global styles for any presentation beyond the bare iframe.

Important Constraints

  • embed is explicitly approval-gated in the source system and gives the author raw control of an arbitrary iframe src — a materially higher-risk authoring surface than youtube, which is constrained to a bare video ID resolved into a known YouTube embed URL pattern.
  • youtube's autoplay always forces mute (autoplay=1&mute=1) — there is no way to autoplay with sound, consistent with browser autoplay policies.
  • youtube's width field accepts a raw CSS string with no unit validation.
  • Neither source component performs any lazy-loading — both render the iframe (or, for embed, the entire experience) eagerly regardless of scroll position.

EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/embed/, sourced from the standard block-collection "Embed" pattern (documented upstream at the block-collection's own reference docs) rather than authored from scratch for this migration. It generalizes past both source components: it recognizes and specially handles YouTube, Vimeo, and Twitter/X links with provider-specific embed markup, and falls back to a generic responsive iframe for anything else (which subsumes the source embed component's "any URL" capability) — but it has no per-breakpoint custom height authoring and no approval-gate concept; those are purely editorial/governance controls that live outside the block itself in the target implementation.

File Structure

blocks/embed/
├── embed.js
├── embed.css
├── _embed.json
├── block-config.js
├── rinvoq/
│   ├── embed.css
│   ├── _embed.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── ... (botox/, abbvie/ brand folders follow the same pattern)

Authoring Model (_embed.json)

{
  "id": "embed",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "reference", "valueType": "string", "name": "embed_placeholder", "label": "Placeholder Image", "multi": false },
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "embed_placeholderAlt", "value": "", "label": "Placeholder Image Alt Text" },
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "embed_title", "value": "", "label": "Overlay Title",
      "description": "Title displayed over the placeholder image" },
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "embed_description", "value": "", "label": "Overlay Description",
      "description": "Description displayed over the placeholder image" },
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "embed_buttonLabel", "value": "", "label": "Button Label",
      "description": "Label for the play button (e.g. Watch 2:33)" },
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "embed_uri", "label": "URI" }
  ]
}

How the JS works (embed.js)

const EMBEDS_CONFIG = [
  { match: ['youtube', 'youtu.be'], embed: embedYoutube },
  { match: ['vimeo'], embed: embedVimeo },
  { match: ['twitter', 'x.com'], embed: embedTwitter },
];

const embedYoutube = (url, autoplay) => {
  const usp = new URLSearchParams(url.search);
  const suffix = autoplay ? '&muted=1&autoplay=1' : '';
  let vid = usp.get('v') ? encodeURIComponent(usp.get('v')) : '';
  if (url.origin.includes('youtu.be')) { [, vid] = url.pathname.split('/'); }
  return `<div style="...padding-bottom: 56.25%;">
      <iframe src="https://www.youtube.com${vid ? `/embed/${vid}?rel=0&v=${vid}${suffix}` : url.pathname}"
      allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share"
      allowfullscreen loading="lazy" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin"></iframe>
    </div>`;
};

export default function decorate(block) {
  const placeholder = block.querySelector('picture') || block.querySelector('img');
  const anchor = block.querySelector('a');
  let link = anchor?.href || '';
  ...
  if (!link) {
    if (placeholder) { /* show placeholder image only, no embed */ }
    return;
  }

  if (placeholder) {
    // Build a click-to-load placeholder with optional title/description/watch-button overlay,
    // and only calls loadEmbed() (which injects the real iframe) on click.
    wrapper.addEventListener('click', () => { loadEmbed(block, link, false); });
  } else {
    // No placeholder image — lazy-load the embed itself via IntersectionObserver.
    const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
      if (entries.some((e) => e.isIntersecting)) { observer.disconnect(); loadEmbed(block, link); }
    });
    observer.observe(block);
  }
}

The block reads overlay text (title/description/button label) from two different possible DOM shapes to stay compatible with both an imported-page format (title in an <h2>, remaining fields as ordered <p> tags) and a Universal-Editor-authored format (all fields rendered as ordered <p> tags with no heading) — a dual-parsing compatibility shim visible directly in the code comments ("Import format" vs. "xwalk format"). Video-provider detection is a simple hostname substring match against a small fixed provider table; anything unmatched falls through to getDefaultEmbed(), a plain responsive iframe.

CSS

.embed .embed-placeholder {
  width: 100%;
  aspect-ratio: 16 / 9;
  position: relative;
  overflow: hidden;
  cursor: pointer;
}
.embed .embed-placeholder:has(.embed-placeholder-overlay)::after {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  inset: var(--spacing-00);
  background: var(--overlay-navy, rgb(7 29 73 / 70%));
  pointer-events: none;
}
.embed iframe {
  width: 100%;
  aspect-ratio: 16 / 9;
  border: none;
}

Height is always derived from a fixed 16:9 aspect-ratio, not an authored pixel value — a structural difference from the source embed component's three explicit per-breakpoint height fields.

Key differences from AEM implementation

Source (embed / youtube)Target (embed)
Provider awarenessembed: none (raw iframe to any URL). youtube: YouTube-only, purpose-builtYouTube, Vimeo, and Twitter/X specifically recognized; generic iframe fallback for anything else
Height controlThree explicit authored pixel heights (desktop/tablet/mobile)Fixed 16:9 aspect-ratio, not authorable per instance
Loading behaviorEager — iframe (or, for embed, the whole experience) renders immediatelyClick-to-load if a placeholder image is authored; otherwise lazy via IntersectionObserver
Overlay contentNot supported by either source componentOptional title/description/watch-button overlay on the placeholder image
Approval gatingembed explicitly requires organizational approval before publish per its own dialog help textNo equivalent gate in the block itself (a governance/workflow concern outside block scope)
Analyticsyoutube builds a full digital-data bean (link/component/interaction/MVA) for the videoNot present in the block itself

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldSource componentTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
embedUrlembedpathbrowserembed_uriRenamedSame purpose (the iframe/embed source URL)
youtubeVideoIdyoutubetextfieldembed_uriRenamedSource stores a bare video ID and constructs the embed URL server-side; target expects a full URL and derives the video ID client-side from query/path parsing — a representational change, not a pure rename, but the closest counterpart
(none in source)——embed_placeholderNot mapped (net-new)Neither source component supports a click-to-load placeholder image; both render the iframe/experience immediately
(none in source)——embed_placeholderAltNot mapped (net-new)Same reason as embed_placeholder
(none in source)——embed_titleNot mapped (net-new)No overlay-title concept in either source component (embed's embedExperience is an internal label / iframe title attribute, not a visible on-page overlay heading)
(none in source)——embed_descriptionNot mapped (net-new)No overlay-description concept in either source component
(none in source)——embed_buttonLabelNot mapped (net-new)No "watch" CTA concept in either source component
embedExperienceembedtextfield—Not mappedUsed in the source as the iframe's accessible title attribute and an edit-mode label; the target block sets iframe title programmatically (e.g. "Content from Youtube") rather than from an authored field
desktopHeight / tabletHeight / mobileHeightembednumberfield—Not mappedTarget uses a fixed 16:9 aspect-ratio for all breakpoints; no per-breakpoint pixel-height authoring exists
anchorIdembedtextfield—Not mappedNo anchor-ID field in the target model; likely covered generically by shared common-properties conventions rather than this specific field
customCssClassesembedtextfield—Not mappedNo free-text custom-class field in the target model
anchorIDyoutubetextfield—Not mappedSame reason as embed's anchorId
customCssClassesyoutubetextfield—Not mappedSame reason as embed's customCssClasses
playerWidthyoutubetextfield—Not mappedTarget's iframe is always full-width (100%) with height derived from aspect-ratio; no authored width override
autoPlayyoutubecheckbox—Not mappedTarget only autoplays as a side effect of the (non-authorable) placeholder-click interaction, not a persistent authored autoplay-on-load setting
allowFullScreenyoutubecheckbox—Not mappedTarget's generated iframes always include allowfullscreen unconditionally; not an authored toggle
epassNumberyoutubetextfield—Not mappedInternal project/approval-tracking number has no equivalent concept in the target block
interactionId, analyticsTitle, sectionTitle, l10Title, mvaPrimary, mvaTitleForAnalytics, mvaTypeAndCategory, analyticsEnableMVASecondaryyoutubevarious—Not mappedThe entire analytics/MVA tagging tab has no equivalent in the target block, which carries no analytics fields of its own

Embed Form Component — Technical Details

What it does

Embeds an externally-hosted AEM Adaptive Form into a page. The source-system component (forms-embed) fetches the form's rendered HTML client-side and injects it into a placeholder div; the EDS block (embed-form) does the same thing but adds explicit cross-origin handling, rewriting same-origin resource URLs so scripts and stylesheets in the fetched markup resolve against the AEM Publish host rather than the page's own origin.

Architecture

Author sets formsUrl (e.g. /content/forms/af/brand-a/sign-up), prefixed "abbviecloud"
         ↓
FormsEmbed.java reads formsUrl from JCR (@ValueMapValue, OPTIONAL) → exposes getFormsUrl()
         ↓
forms-embed.html renders:
  <div class="cmp-formsembed-widget" data-forms-url="...">
    <div class="cmp-adaptiveform-container-form-loading abbv-animation-loading">  (publish mode only)
    <div class="afsection">                                                     (edit mode: "Form Will Appear Here")
         ↓
forms-embed.js (abbvFormsEmbed IIFE), deferred clientlib:
  showLoader() → shows the FIRST widget's loader, hides the rest
  init() → for each .cmp-formsembed-widget on the page:
    fetch(data-forms-url) → response.text()
    → updateElementContent(targetElement, html, index)
      → innerHTML = html
      → re-creates every <script> tag (clone attrs + text) so it executes
      → on each re-created script's onload, increments loadedCount;
        once loadedCount === total scripts, hideLoader()

Sling Model — FormsEmbed.java

Minimal model — single field, no business logic:

@Model(adaptables = SlingHttpServletRequest.class, resourceType = FormsEmbed.RESOURCE_TYPE)
@Exporter(name = ExporterConstants.SLING_MODEL_EXPORTER_NAME, extensions = ExporterConstants.SLING_MODEL_EXTENSION)
public class FormsEmbed {
    public static final String RESOURCE_TYPE = "common-elements/v1/components/content/forms-embed";

    @ValueMapValue(injectionStrategy = InjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
    @Nullable
    protected String formsUrl;

    public String getFormsUrl() { return formsUrl; }
}

Dialog

Single-tab, single field:

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Forms URLformsUrltextField label reads "Forms URL - should be prefixed with the external forms platform identifier" — the dialog itself documents a hostname convention rather than validating it

No analytics tab, no CSS class field, no anchor ID — this is a bare-minimum component.

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<div class="cmp-formsembed__item" data-sly-use.model="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.FormsEmbed">
    <div class="cmp-formsembed-widget" data-forms-url=${model.formsUrl}>
        <div class="cmp-adaptiveform-container-form-loading abbv-animation-loading" data-sly-test="${!wcmmode.edit}"></div>
        <div class="afsection">
            <div data-sly-test="${wcmmode.edit}">Form Will Appear Here</div>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>
<sly data-sly-call="${clientLib.js @ categories='forms-embed-component.v1', loading='defer'}"></sly>

CSS

.cmp-adaptiveform-container {
    display: none;
}

Hides the AEM Forms default container wrapper to avoid double-rendering.

Important Constraints (source system)

  • Multi-instance aware but indexed positionally — fetchData writes into document.querySelectorAll('.afsection')[index], so instance order on the page must match iteration order of .cmp-formsembed-widget elements; there is no ID-based matching.
  • Loader visibility is coupled across instances — showLoader() shows only the first widget's spinner and hides the others' at page-load time, and hideLoader() only ever hides that first widget's spinner once every re-executed script across every instance has fired its onload. A slow or non-firing script on one instance can leave the shared loader visible indefinitely.
  • Same-origin assumption — no URL rewriting is performed on the fetched HTML; it depends on the AEM Forms host being reachable and CORS-permitting relative to the fetch call itself.
  • No error UI — catch(error) only console.error()s.

Embed Form Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/embed-form/. It is a "Reuse+Enhance" of the source forms-embed behavior: same fetch-and-inject pattern, but adds explicit same-origin URL rewriting against a configured AEM Publish host, sequential (not parallel) script re-execution with de-duplication of already-loaded external scripts, a jQuery bootstrap (the fetched AEM Forms markup expects window.jQuery to exist), and custom adaptiveform:loaded / adaptiveform:error events.

File Structure

blocks/embed-form/
├── embed-form.js
├── embed-form.css
├── _embed-form.json
├── block-config.js
├── README.md
├── abbvie/
│   ├── embed-form.css
│   ├── _embed-form.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
│   ├── embed-form.css
│   ├── _embed-form.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── rinvoq/
    ├── embed-form.css
    ├── _embed-form.css
    └── block-config.js

Authoring Model (_embed-form.json)

{
  "definitions": [
    {
      "title": "Embed Form",
      "id": "embed-form",
      "plugins": {
        "xwalk": {
          "page": {
            "resourceType": "core/franklin/components/block/v1/block",
            "template": { "name": "Embed Form", "model": "embed-form" }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  ],
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "embed-form",
      "fields": [
        {
          "component": "aem-content",
          "name": "formLink",
          "label": "AEM Adaptive Form Link",
          "valueType": "string"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "filters": []
}

Single field, matching the source component's single-field dialog — but authored as a link reference (aem-content) rather than free text.

How the JS works (embed-form.js)

The block reads the anchor's href from the first row and classifies it into a final form path (real code, decorate()):

const DEFAULT_HOST = 'https://publish-pXXXXX-eXXXXXXX.adobeaemcloud.com'; // literal fallback constant in the real file, host segment genericized here

const aemHost = (await getConfigValue('aemPublishUrl')) || DEFAULT_HOST;

if (isUniversalEditor()) {
  finalFormPath = href;
} else if (formLink.host !== window.location.host) {
  finalFormPath = href;
} else {
  href = formLink.pathname + formLink.search + formLink.hash;
  if (/\/content\/forms\//.test(href)) {
    const formsPath = href.slice(href.indexOf('/content/forms'));
    const normalised = formsPath.endsWith('.html') ? formsPath : `${formsPath}.html`;
    finalFormPath = `${aemHost}${normalised}`;
  } else {
    finalFormPath = `${window.location.origin}${href}`;
  }
}

It then loads jQuery from a CDN if not already present (the fetched Adaptive Form markup is jQuery-dependent), fetches the form with window.jQuery.ajax, and on success rewrites same-origin script/link URLs in the injected fragment to point at the AEM Publish host:

function rewriteResourceUrls(container, formUrlObj) {
  container.querySelectorAll('script[src], link[href]').forEach((el) => {
    const attr = el.tagName.toLowerCase() === 'script' ? 'src' : 'href';
    try {
      const parsed = new URL(el[attr]);
      if (parsed.host === window.location.host) {
        el[attr] = `${formUrlObj.protocol}//${formUrlObj.hostname}${parsed.pathname}`;
      }
    } catch {
      // relative or invalid — leave as-is
    }
  });
}

Scripts are then re-executed sequentially — external scripts move to <head> with a duplicate-source check, so the AEM Forms SDK bootstrap does not register its listeners twice if the block runs more than once on a page:

function runScripts(container) {
  const runOne = (oldScript) => {
    const newScript = document.createElement('script');
    [...oldScript.attributes].forEach((attr) => newScript.setAttribute(attr.name, attr.value));
    newScript.textContent = oldScript.textContent;
    if (newScript.src) {
      if (document.querySelector(`head script[src="${newScript.src}"]`)) {
        oldScript.remove();
        return Promise.resolve();
      }
      return new Promise((resolve) => {
        newScript.onload = resolve;
        newScript.onerror = resolve;
        document.head.appendChild(newScript);
        oldScript.remove();
      });
    }
    oldScript.replaceWith(newScript);
    return Promise.resolve();
  };
  return [...container.querySelectorAll('script')].reduce(
    (chain, script) => chain.then(() => runOne(script)),
    Promise.resolve(),
  );
}

Dispatches adaptiveform:loaded on success and adaptiveform:error on failure, both documented in the block's README.

CSS

.embed-form {
  width: 100%;
  margin: 0 auto;
}

.embed-form-container {
  min-height: 100px;
}

.embed-form-loading {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: center;
  align-items: center;
  min-height: 200px;
  font-style: italic;
  color: var(--text-color-light, var(--text-color));
}

.embed-form-error {
  color: var(--color-error, var(--text-color));
  text-align: center;
  padding: var(--spacing-16, 1.6rem);
}

The abbvie brand override hard-codes the loading-text color (#666) instead of the token, then @imports the base file — a brand CSS pattern seen elsewhere in this batch as well.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (forms-embed)EDS (embed-form)
Field authoringFree-text URL fieldaem-content link picker
Cross-origin handlingNone — relies on same-origin fetchExplicit host rewriting against a configured/default AEM Publish host, plus Universal Editor and external-link branches
Script executionParallel re-creation of all scripts; loader hidden only once every script's onload firesSequential re-execution with de-duplication against existing <head> scripts
jQuery dependencyAssumed already on the AEM page (clientlib pulls jQuery in in most page templates)Explicitly bootstrapped from CDN if absent, since EDS pages are jQuery-free by default
Multi-instance supportPositional index into querySelectorAll('.afsection') — fragile if page order and DOM order divergeEach block instance owns its own container element reference; no global index
Error handlingconsole.error only, loader never hidden on failureRenders an .embed-form-error message and fires adaptiveform:error

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
formsUrltextformLinkRenamedSame purpose (form path), different field name and component type (text → aem-content link reference)

Fact Card Component — Technical Details

What it does

Renders a single stat/fact card (eyebrow label, large data point, optional suffix, description, optional image) sourced from an AEM Content Fragment. In the source system this behavior is split across three separate components: the generic contentfragment component (renders any Content Fragment's paragraphs/elements via the Core Components CFM API), dynamic-card (an older, largely PHP/pattern-library demo component using local sample recipe data — not a real content-driven implementation), and dynamic-card-cfm (the one that actually matches the "fact card" purpose: a thin custom Sling Model layered on the Core Components Content Fragment component to add analytics and title lookups). The EDS fact-card block reproduces the dynamic-card-cfm/contentfragment combination as a single block that fetches structured CF data via GraphQL (falling back to .model.json) and renders fixed fields: eyebrow, dataPoint, dataPointSuffix, description, image.

Architecture

Source system (dynamic-card-cfm + contentfragment):
Author drags a Content Fragment onto the dynamic-card-cfm component, sets fragmentPath
         ↓
dynamic-card-cfm.html:
  data-sly-use.fragment="com.adobe.cq.wcm.core.components.models.contentfragment.ContentFragment"
  data-sly-use.model="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.DynamicCardCFModel"
  → calls cfTemplates.contentFragment(fragment, fragmentPath, wcmmode, model.digitalData)
         ↓
templates.html renders fragment.elements via element.html, keyed by element.name
  (title, featuredImage, recipePdfUrl, recipePdfCtaTitle, recipePageUrl, recipeTags, ...)
         ↓
DynamicCardCFModel.java (@PostConstruct):
  fetchMasterTitle() reads the CF's master variation node directly via ResourceResolver
  to pull title / recipePdfCtaTitle / recipePdfUrl for the analytics digitalData string

EDS fact-card:
Author selects a Content Fragment reference (contentFragment field)
         ↓
fact-card.js decorate():
  normalizePath() strips rootPath / .html
  fetchDashboardCardData(path, 'cfFactsBaseUrl') → GraphQL query result
         ↓
renderCfFactCard() reads item.eyebrow / dataPoint / dataPointSuffix / description / image
  falls back to renderCfElementsFallback() (generic CF "elements" shape) if the
  GraphQL shape isn't present, then falls back again to a raw .model.json fetch

Sling Model — DynamicCardCFModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class, SlingHttpServletRequest.class},
       resourceType = DynamicCardCFModel.RESOURCE_TYPE,
       defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class DynamicCardCFModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    protected static final String RESOURCE_TYPE = "common-elements/v1/components/content/dynamic-card-cfm";

    @ValueMapValue(name = "fragmentPath")
    private String fragmentPath;

    @ValueMapValue(name = "variationName")
    private String variationName;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        if (fragmentPath != null && !fragmentPath.isEmpty()) {
            fetchMasterTitle();
        }
        // ...interaction-id bookkeeping shared with other AbstractComponentModel subclasses
    }

    protected void fetchMasterTitle() {
        String masterNodePath = fragmentPath + "/jcr:content/data/" + variationName;
        Resource contentFragmentResource = resourceResolver.getResource(masterNodePath);
        if (contentFragmentResource != null) {
            ValueMap properties = contentFragmentResource.adaptTo(ValueMap.class);
            if (properties != null) {
                masterTitle = properties.get("title", String.class);
                recipePdfCtaTitle = properties.get("recipePdfCtaTitle", String.class);
                recipePdfUrl = properties.get("recipePdfUrl", String.class);
            }
        }
    }

    public String getDigitalData() {
        // builds an analytics Link/Component/Interaction/Mva bean bundle
        // keyed off recipePdfCtaTitle / recipePdfUrl / masterTitle
    }
}

Note the field names (recipePdfCtaTitle, recipePdfUrl, masterTitle) — this model was written for a specific recipe-card use case and never generalized; it reads the CF's raw JCR properties by hard-coded name rather than through the generic Core Components element API.

Dialog

dynamic-card-cfm has no custom _cq_dialog of its own in the tree searched — it inherits its dialog from its sling:resourceSuperType, core/wcm/core/components/contentfragment/v1/contentfragment (standard AEM Content Fragment component dialog: fragment path picker, variation selector). The sibling contentfragment component (used generically elsewhere) exposes its own dialog:

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextSpace-separated utility classes
Id for AnchoringanchorIdtextAnchor/deep-link target
Fragment ThemefragmentThemeselect (datasource)ce-content-fragment-themes category
Variationvariationselect (datasource)Core Components variations datasource

dynamic-card-cfm itself only injects two @ValueMapValue fields directly (fragmentPath, variationName); the rest of its rendering surface comes from the inherited Content Fragment fields above and from the fixed CF element names it happens to read (title, featuredImage, recipePdfUrl, recipePdfCtaTitle, recipePageUrl, recipeTags).

HTL — Key Structure

<!-- dynamic-card-cfm.html -->
<sly data-sly-use.fragment="com.adobe.cq.wcm.core.components.models.contentfragment.ContentFragment"
     data-sly-test="${properties.fragmentPath}" data-sly-use.cfTemplates="templates.html">
    <sly data-sly-use.model="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.DynamicCardCFModel"></sly>
    <sly data-sly-call="${cfTemplates.contentFragment @ fragment=fragment,
         fragmentPath=properties.fragmentPath, wcmmode=wcmmode, digitaldata=model.digitalData}"></sly>
</sly>

<!-- element.html (excerpt) -->
<sly data-sly-test="${element.name == 'featuredImage'}">
    <sly data-sly-test.featuredImage="${element.value}">
        <dd class="cmp-contentfragment_element-value-${element.name}">
            <img src="${featuredImage}" alt="${title}" title="${title}" height="100" width="100" />
        </dd>
    </sly>
</sly>

CSS

The source components carry no dedicated fact-card-specific stylesheet in the paths searched — contentfragment and dynamic-card-cfm rely on the shared cmp-contentfragment* classes styled globally elsewhere in the clientlib tree, not a component-scoped CSS file.

Important Constraints (source system)

  • Field names are use-case-specific, not generic — recipePdfCtaTitle/recipePdfUrl hard-code a recipe-card mental model into a component that is otherwise meant to render any CF.
  • fetchMasterTitle() reads JCR properties directly off the master variation node by path string concatenation rather than through the Content Fragment API, so it breaks silently (logs an error, leaves fields null) if the variation name or node structure changes.
  • No image-preset/rendition support — the source renders element.value as a raw src with fixed height="100" width="100" attributes, no responsive sizing.

Fact Card Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

Fully implemented at blocks/fact-card/, marked "Reuse As-Is" in the delivery ledger. It generalizes the source system's ad hoc recipe fields into a stable four-field CF contract (eyebrow, dataPoint, dataPointSuffix, description, image), fetched via a shared GraphQL helper rather than direct JCR/property access.

File Structure

blocks/fact-card/
├── fact-card.js
├── fact-card.css
├── _fact-card.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── fact-card.css
│   ├── _fact-card.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
│   ├── fact-card.css
│   ├── _fact-card.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── rinvoq/
    ├── fact-card.css
    ├── _fact-card.css
    └── block-config.js

Authoring Model (_fact-card.json, excerpt)

{
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "fact-card",
      "fields": [
        { "component": "tab", "label": "Overview", "name": "overview" },
        {
          "component": "aem-content", "valueType": "string", "name": "contentFragment",
          "label": "Content Fragment",
          "description": "Select a content fragment to source data for the card (eyebrow, data point, suffix, description, image)."
        },
        { "component": "boolean", "name": "hideImage", "label": "Hide Image", "value": false },
        {
          "component": "select", "name": "classes_cardColor", "label": "Card Color",
          "options": [
            { "name": "Default", "value": "" },
            { "name": "Light (Light With Stroke)", "value": "light-theme-stroke" },
            { "name": "Medium (Light Blue)", "value": "medium-theme" },
            { "name": "Dark (Navy)", "value": "dark-theme" }
          ]
        },
        {
          "component": "select", "name": "imagePreset", "label": "Image Preset",
          "options": [
            { "name": "None", "value": "" },
            { "name": "testImagePreset1", "value": "testImagePreset1" },
            { "name": "testImagePreset2", "value": "testImagePreset2" }
          ],
          "condition": { "===": [{ "var": "hideImage" }, false] }
        },
        {
          "component": "text", "name": "imageModifiers", "label": "Image Modifiers",
          "condition": { "===": [{ "var": "hideImage" }, false] }
        },
        { "component": "tab", "label": "Analytics", "name": "analytics" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "analyticsInteractionId", "label": "Analytics Interaction ID" },
        { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

How the JS works (fact-card.js)

Row order is hard-coded to mirror the model field order (comment in the real file):

const ROW = {
  CONTENT_FRAGMENT: 0,
  HIDE_IMAGE: 1,
  IMAGE_PRESET: 2,
  IMAGE_MODIFIERS: 3,
  ANALYTICS_ID: 4,
};

It extracts the CF path from the first row's anchor (or plain text starting with /), normalizes it (strips a configured rootPath prefix and any .html suffix), then fetches via a shared helper:

const path = await normalizePath(rawPath);
const cfData = await fetchDashboardCardData(`${path}?8`, 'cfFactsBaseUrl');
renderCfFactCard(card, cfData, options);

renderCfFactCard reads a GraphQL item shape (dashboardCardFactFragmentByPath / dashboardCardByPath / raw item) and falls back to a generic CF "elements" shape (renderCfElementsFallback) if the expected shape is absent — same fallback-ladder pattern used by the block's sibling quote block. On GraphQL failure it retries against the CF's own .model.json endpoint before giving up silently.

CSS

Real, token-driven, with an explicit grid variation (fact-card-feature-grid) laying out up to 6 cards across mobile/tablet/desktop breakpoints:

.fact-card .fact-card-eyebrow {
  font-family: var(--roboto-bold-font-family);
  font-size: var(--font-size-16);
  font-weight: var(--font-weight-700);
  letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-wide-96);
  text-transform: uppercase;
  overflow: hidden;
  display: -webkit-box;
  -webkit-line-clamp: 1;
  -webkit-box-orient: vertical;
}
.fact-card .fact-card-data-point {
  font-family: var(--heading-font-family);
  font-size: var(--font-size-56);
  color: var(--link-color);
}
.fact-card.dark-theme .fact-card-inner { background-color: var(--color-navy-dark); }
.fact-card.light-theme-stroke .fact-card-inner { border: var(--border-01) solid var(--container-border-color); }

Theme variants (medium-theme, light-theme-no-stroke, dark-theme, light-theme-stroke) correspond 1:1 to the classes_cardColor select options.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (dynamic-card-cfm + contentfragment)EDS (fact-card)
Data accessDirect JCR property reads by hard-coded path (fragmentPath + "/jcr:content/data/" + variationName)GraphQL query with a documented shared fetch helper, plus .model.json fallback
Field vocabularyRecipe-specific (recipePdfCtaTitle, recipePdfUrl, recipeTags) mixed with generic CF element renderingGeneric four-field contract (eyebrow, dataPoint, dataPointSuffix, description) plus image
Image handlingFixed 100×100 <img>, no rendition controlimagePreset (Smart Crop rendition) and free-form imageModifiers query params
ThemingAuthor-selected "Fragment Theme" from a datasource, applied as a wrapper class with no defined CSS in the searched treeclasses_cardColor with four concretely styled variants
AnalyticsCustom digitalData bean built in Java from hard-coded CF fieldsSingle free-text analyticsInteractionId, applied as a data-analytics-id attribute

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
fragmentPath (dynamic-card-cfm)text (ValueMapValue)contentFragmentRenamedSame purpose (CF reference); EDS field is an aem-content picker rather than a raw path string
variationNametext (ValueMapValue)Not mappedNot mappedEDS fetch always resolves the default/master GraphQL data shape for the referenced fragment; there is no per-block variation selector in _fact-card.json
customCssClasses (contentfragment dialog)textCommon properties customClass (via _common-properties.json include)RenamedFolded into the shared common-properties block rather than a component-specific field
anchorIdtextCommon properties blockIdRenamedSame folding as above
fragmentThemeselect (datasource ce-content-fragment-themes)classes_cardColorRenamedConcept survives (a themed color variant) but the AEM datasource-driven open list becomes a fixed 4-option EDS select
variation (Core Components variation selector)select (datasource)Not mappedNot mappedNo variation concept in the EDS GraphQL contract — the block always renders the single fixed shape it queries for
Recipe-specific CF element names (recipePdfCtaTitle, recipePdfUrl, recipePageUrl, recipeTags) — read implicitly by element.html, not dialog fieldsCF content, not component propertiesNot mappedNot mappedThese were content-fragment-model fields, not component dialog fields; the EDS block's CF contract (eyebrow/dataPoint/dataPointSuffix/description/image) is a different, generalized content fragment model and does not carry these recipe-specific names forward
hideImage—hideImageSame nameNet-new toggle in EDS; no equivalent existed in the source dialog (image display was implicit/always-on there)
imagePreset / imageModifiers—imagePreset / imageModifiersSame nameNet-new EDS fields; no source-system equivalent (fixed-size <img> only)
analyticsInteractionId—analyticsInteractionIdSame nameNet-new simplified analytics hook; replaces the Java-built digitalData bean with a single free-text ID

Find A Provider Component — Technical Details

What it does

A physician/support-group/infusion-site locator: an author-configured search form (ZIP/name + radius + optional filters + Terms & Conditions + reCAPTCHA) that calls a configured backend API and renders a paginated, mappable results list. The source component find-a-provider is one of the largest and most heavily-configured components in the source system, with seven dialog tabs. The EDS find-provider block reproduces the same search/results flow with a smaller, flatter field set and hands API-shape branching (the source system's XML-derived "PhysicianLocatorResponse" shape vs. a generic results/providers array) to the client JS instead of author-selected API type/version/environment/action dialog fields.

Architecture

Author configures Overview / Terms / Filters / Results / Errors / API Config / Analytics tabs
         ↓
find-a-provider.html: data-sly-use.fapModel="FindAProviderModel"
  loads Google Maps JS API script unconditionally
  branches by fapModel.apiAction ('SupportGroup' | 'PhysicianLocator' | 'InfusionSiteLocator')
  to include one of three "-script-obj.html" fragments, each of which emits an inline
  config object (radius options, filters, labels, API endpoint pieces) for the clientlib JS
         ↓
<form name="providerSearch"> renders: optional name-search input (InfusionSiteLocator only),
  zip/location input, radius <select>, dynamic filters container, terms checkbox,
  submit/reset buttons, invisible reCAPTCHA v2 badge, disclaimer text
         ↓
find-a-provider-component.v1 clientlib JS (deferred) wires up submit handling,
  builds the request from the emitted config + form values, renders map/list results
  into .find-a-provider-results, handles pagination and show-more/less

EDS find-provider:
Author selects a Variant (default/location/map/support) + fills text/richtext labels + API config
         ↓
find-provider.js decorate(): reads rows positionally (FIELD_ORDER) in Universal Editor mode,
  or key|value rows in document authoring; builds the form DOM directly (buildForm)
         ↓
On submit: POSTs/GETs config['api-endpoint'] with { PublisherName: config.indication, ... },
  extractParties() branches on response shape:
    - data.PhysicianLocatorResponse.PhysicianLocatorResponseBody (source-API-compatible shape)
    - otherwise data.results / data.providers / raw array (generic shape)
  getBestAddress() ranks a provider's PartyAddress[] by BestAddressIndicator==='Yes',
  falling back to nearest DistanceInMiles

Sling Model — FindAProviderModel.java

A very large flat model — one @Inject field per dialog field, no nested beans (unlike the footer's outer/inner multifield beans):

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class}, defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class FindAProviderModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENTIDPREFIX = "abbv-find-a-provider";

    @Inject private String anchorID;
    @Inject private String customCssClasses;
    @Inject private String title;
    @Inject private String searchInputTitle;
    @Inject private String searchInputPlaceholder;
    @Inject private String nameSearchInputTitle;
    @Inject private String nameSearchInputPlaceholder;
    @Inject private String searchBtnText;
    @Inject private String resetBtnText;
    @Inject private String radiusFieldLabel;
    @Inject private String defaultRadius;
    @Inject private String[] radiusOptions;
    @Inject private String enableVirtualVisits;
    @Inject private String virtualVisitsTitle;
    @Inject private String appointmentCtaBtnText;
    @Inject private String virtualVisitCtaBtnText;
    @Inject private String exitmodalID;
    @Inject private String scheduleUrl;
    @Inject private String termsAndConditionsLabel;
    @Inject private String termsAndConditionsText;
    @Inject private String[] filter;
    @Inject private String resultsTitle;
    @Inject private String mapLocation;
    @Inject private String distanceLabel;
    @Inject private String drivingDirectionsLinkText;
    @Inject private String paginationNextPageLabel;
    @Inject private String resultsPerPage;
    @Inject private String markerLabel;
    @Inject private String markerSvgData;
    @Inject private String invalidZipcodeError;
    @Inject private String noresultsError;
    @Inject private String api;
    @Inject private String apiVersion;
    @Inject private String apiEnv;
    @Inject private String apiAction;
    @Inject private String interactionId;
    @Inject private String searchBranded;
    // (40+ fields total; abbreviated here — see dialog table below for the full set)

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        if (!isPublish() && (StringUtils.isBlank(interactionId) || !interactionId.equals(getId()))) {
            setInteractionIdInComponentDialog();
        }
    }
}

Dialog

Seven tabs. Representative fields (the real dialog has ~50 leaf fields across nested multifields for radius options and filters):

TabFieldPropertyTypeNote
OverviewAnchor IDanchorIDtext
OverviewMain Titletitletext
OverviewLocation Search Field Label/PlaceholdersearchInputTitle / searchInputPlaceholdertext
OverviewName Search Field Label/PlaceholdernameSearchInputTitle / nameSearchInputPlaceholdertext"Only used for APIs that allow name search"
OverviewAdd a Search Radius value (repeatable)radiusOptions/radiusOptionmultifield > select (datasource ce-dr-locator-radius-filter)
OverviewDefault Search RadiusdefaultRadiusnumberfield (default 25, min 5, step 5)
OverviewEnable Virtual Visits optionenableVirtualVisitscheckbox
OverviewSchedule URLscheduleUrltextBase URL for telemedicine scheduling site
TermsTerms & Conditions Label/Text/Help TexttermsAndConditionsLabel / termsAndConditionsText / termsAndConditionsHelpTexttext / includewidget / textarea
TermsDisclaimer TextdisclaimerTextincludewidgetBelow submit button
FiltersFilters HeadingfilterHeadingTextincludewidget
FiltersAdd a Filter (repeatable): Name / Placeholder / Help / Required / Hidden / Type / Querystring Param / Itemsfilter/name, ddPlaceHolder, help, required, hideFilter, type, qsParm, options/name+value+defaultValueCheckedmultifield > fieldset (nested multifield for options)Type is checkbox/radiobtn/dropDown
ResultsResults Title / Search Again / Show Map labelsresultsTitle / searchAgainBtnLabel / showMapBtnLabeltextresultsTitle supports {{rangeLower}}/{{rangeUpper}}/{{totalResults}} tokens
ResultsMap PositionmapLocationselect (datasource ce-map-location)
ResultsDistance Label / Driving Directions LabeldistanceLabel / drivingDirectionsLinkTexttext
ResultsResults Per PageresultsPerPagenumberfield (default 10, max 26)
ResultsMarker Label / Custom Marker SVG DatamarkerLabel / markerSvgDatatext / textarea
ResultsEnable Email Results / Email Title / CTA TextemailFeature / authorTitle / emailCtaBtnTextcheckbox / text / text
