ErrorsInvalid Search Location / Name / T&C / No Results / TimeoutinvalidZipcodeError / invalidNameError / tncError / noresultsError / apiServicesErrortext
API ConfigAPI / API Version / API Environment / API Actionapi / apiVersion / apiEnv / apiActionselectapiAction drives which script-obj HTL fragment is included (SupportGroup / PhysicianLocator / InfusionSiteLocator)
API ConfigSpecialty Name / HCP Search for Branded sitespecialtyName / searchBrandedtext / checkbox
AnalyticsAnalytics Interaction ID (read-only) / Section Title / Localized Section TitleinteractionId / sectionTitle / l10Titletext (disabled) / text / text

HTL — Key DOM Structure (excerpt)

<sly data-sly-use.fapModel="${'com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.FindAProviderModel'}">
<script src="https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/js?key=..."></script>
<sly data-sly-test="${fapModel.apiAction == 'PhysicianLocator'}">
    <script data-sly-use.scriptObjTemp="fap-physicianLocator-script-obj.html"
            data-sly-call="${scriptObjTemp.scriptObj @fapModel=fapModel}"></script>
</sly>

<div class="abbv-find-a-provider ${fapModel.customCssClasses}
     ${fapModel.apiAction == 'SupportGroup' ? 'fap-support-group'
        : (fapModel.apiAction == 'InfusionSiteLocator' ? 'fap-infusion-selector' : 'fap-physician-locator')}">
  <form name="providerSearch">
    <input type="text" name="fap-zipcode" class="fap-zipCode" pattern="[0-9]{5}" maxLength="5" inputmode="numeric">
    <select name="fap-radius">
      <sly data-sly-list="${fapModel.radiusNumOptions}"><option value="${item}">${item} Miles</option></sly>
    </select>
    <div class="g-recaptcha js-v2" data-sitekey="${fapModel.captchaSiteKey}" data-badge="inline" data-size="invisible"></div>
  </form>
  <div class="find-a-provider-results">
    <div class="results-container ${fapModel.mapLocation}" aria-live="polite">
      <div class="fapMap-container show-map"><div class="fapMap"></div></div>
      <div class="fapList-container"><div class="providerList"></div></div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

CSS

Source component CSS was not captured beyond the clientlib CSS-category registration (ui/find-a-provider.css exists but is a plain, unscoped/utility-class stylesheet consistent with the rest of the ADMP-UI class system — no CSS custom properties or brand-token layer, unlike the EDS side below).

Important Constraints (source system)

  • Google Maps script tag is unconditional — loaded on every page render of the component regardless of mapLocation setting, with an API key hard-coded directly in the HTL.
  • API shape is selected by author dropdown (api/apiVersion/apiEnv/apiAction), not detected at runtime — a mismatch between the selected action and the real backend contract fails silently client-side.
  • reCAPTCHA is always v2 invisible with a component-level site key field (captchaSiteKey), no v3/token variant.

Find Provider Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

Fully implemented at blocks/find-provider/ — a 744-line find-provider.js plus a 643-line token-driven CSS file, marked "Reuse+Enhance." It collapses the source system's API-type/version/environment dialog fields into a single configured api-endpoint URL and a response-shape auto-detector, and replaces the author-driven filter-multifield system with a smaller fixed field list plus a classes multiselect for layout variants.

File Structure

blocks/find-provider/
├── find-provider.js
├── find-provider.css
├── _find-provider.json
├── block-config.js
├── dummy-providers.json
├── abbvie/            (find-provider.css, _find-provider.css, block-config.js)
├── botox/
├── linzess/
├── mavyret/
├── rinvoq/
├── rinvoq-dtc/
├── rinvoq-hcp/
├── skyrizi-hcp/
└── venclexta/

Authoring Model (_find-provider.json, excerpt)

{
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "find-provider",
      "fields": [
        { "component": "tab", "label": "Labels", "name": "labels" },
        {
          "component": "select", "name": "classes", "label": "Variant",
          "description": "Select the search mode. Default: name/ZIP search. Location: auto-detects user location. Map: shows a map alongside results. Support: support-group finder.",
          "options": [
            { "name": "Default", "value": "" },
            { "name": "Location-based search", "value": "find-provider-location" },
            { "name": "Map view", "value": "find-provider-map" },
            { "name": "Support group finder", "value": "find-provider-support" }
          ]
        },
        { "component": "text", "name": "search-label", "label": "Search Label" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "radius-options", "label": "Radius Options",
          "description": "Comma-separated radius options (e.g. '5 Miles, 10 Miles, 25 Miles'). Defaults to 5/10/25/50/100 Miles when blank." },
        { "component": "text", "name": "geo-button-label", "label": "Geo Button Label" },
        { "component": "richtext", "name": "terms-text", "label": "Terms Text" },
        { "component": "richtext", "name": "captcha-message", "label": "Captcha Message" },
        { "component": "tab", "label": "Configuration", "name": "config" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "api-endpoint", "label": "API Endpoint" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "indication", "label": "Indication",
          "description": "PublisherName sent in the API request payload." },
        { "component": "text", "name": "exit-modal-id", "label": "Exit Modal ID" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "anchor-id", "label": "Anchor ID" },
        { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

How the JS works (find-provider.js)

Response-shape detection replaces the source system's author-selected API type:

function extractParties(data) {
  const body = data?.PhysicianLocatorResponse?.PhysicianLocatorResponseBody;
  if (body) {
    if (body.IsStatusSuccessful === 'false') return { providers: [], matchCount: 0, recordCount: 10 };
    const content = body.ContentResult || {};
    return {
      providers: content.Party || [],
      matchCount: parseInt(content.MatchCount || '0', 10),
      recordCount: parseInt(content.RecordCount || '10', 10),
    };
  }
  const providers = data?.results || data?.providers || (Array.isArray(data) ? data : []);
  return { providers, matchCount: providers.length, recordCount: providers.length };
}

function getBestAddress(provider) {
  const addresses = provider.PartyAddress || [];
  const best = addresses.find((a) => a.BestAddressIndicator === 'Yes');
  if (best) return best;
  if (addresses.length > 1) {
    return [...addresses].sort(
      (a, b) => parseFloat(a.DistanceInMiles || 9999) - parseFloat(b.DistanceInMiles || 9999),
    )[0];
  }
  return addresses[0] || {};
}

Field reading supports both Universal Editor (one field per row, order-dependent via FIELD_ORDER) and document/Google-Doc authoring (key | value rows):

function isUEMode(block) {
  const firstRow = block.children[0];
  return firstRow ? firstRow.children.length === 1 : false;
}

function readConfig(block) {
  const config = {};
  const rows = [...block.children];
  const ue = isUEMode(block);
  rows.forEach((row, i) => {
    const cells = [...row.children];
    const key = ue ? FIELD_ORDER[i] : cells[0]?.textContent.trim().toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, '-');
    const cell = ue ? cells[0] : cells[1];
    if (!key || !cell) return;
    config[key] = RICHTEXT_KEYS.has(key) ? cell.innerHTML.trim() : cell.textContent.trim();
  });
  return config;
}

Variant selection is a CSS class check rather than a dialog-driven template branch:

const isLocation = block.classList.contains('find-provider-location');
const isMapVariant = block.classList.contains('find-provider-map');

The location variant calls navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition directly — no external maps SDK script tag is loaded unconditionally the way the source component loads the Google Maps script on every render.

CSS

Fully tokenized, block-scoped custom properties (excerpt):

.find-provider {
  --find-provider-color-border: var(--color-border);
  --find-provider-color-accent: var(--link-color);
  --find-provider-color-pin-bg: var(--color-accent-yellow);
  --find-provider-spinner-size: var(--spacing-56);
  --find-provider-map-height: var(--spacing-540, 540px);
  --find-provider-results-scroll-height: var(--spacing-550, 550px);
}

Every visual value is expressed as a block-local custom property mapped to a shared design token, letting brand folders override the loo­k without touching the base rule set.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (find-a-provider)EDS (find-provider)
API selectionAuthor picks API / API Version / API Environment / API Action from selects; three separate HTL script-obj fragments per actionSingle api-endpoint URL; response shape auto-detected at runtime by extractParties
Variant selectionDriven by apiAction dialog value, which also swaps CSS class (fap-support-group / fap-infusion-selector / fap-physician-locator)Driven by a classes multiselect (find-provider-location/-map/-support) applied directly as block classes
FiltersAuthor-authored repeatable filter groups (checkbox/radio/dropdown) with querystring param mapping, each with its own multifield of optionsNo equivalent repeatable filter authoring model in _find-provider.json
Radius optionsRepeatable multifield of datasource-backed selects plus a numeric defaultSingle comma-separated radius-options text field with a hard-coded 5/10/25/50/100-mile default
MapsGoogle Maps JS API loaded unconditionally with an inline API keyMap rendering is variant-gated (find-provider-map) with a dummy-data/local-fallback path (dummy-providers.json) when the API call fails
GeolocationNot present — location search is manual ZIP/name entry onlyNet-new: find-provider-location variant calls navigator.geolocation directly with a "use my location" button
reCAPTCHAAlways v2 invisible with a component-level site key fieldNo reCAPTCHA integration visible in the reviewed find-provider.js; a captcha-message richtext field exists for disclosure copy only

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
searchInputTitle / searchInputPlaceholdertextsearch-label / search-placeholderRenamedSame purpose, kebab-case field names
radiusFieldLabeltextradius-labelRenamed
radiusOptions (multifield of selects) + defaultRadiusmultifield / numberfieldradius-optionsRenamedStructural change: repeatable datasource-backed multifield with a separate numeric default collapses into one free-text comma-separated field; default radius is no longer independently settable
searchBtnTexttextsubmit-labelRenamed
resetBtnTexttextclear-labelRenamed
nameSearchInputTitle / nameSearchInputPlaceholdertextNot mappedNot mappedNo separate name-search input field in the EDS model; the block's single search-label/search-placeholder pair is reused however the variant needs it
termsAndConditionsLabeltextterms-labelRenamed
termsAndConditionsTextincludewidget (rich text)terms-textRenamedBoth rich text; EDS uses the standard richtext component
tncErrortextterms-errorRenamed
noresultsErrortextno-resultsRenamed
apiServicesError / invalidZipcodeErrortexterrorRenamedMultiple distinct source error strings collapse to one generic error field
resultsTitletextresults-titleRenamedEDS drops the {{rangeLower}}/{{rangeUpper}}/{{totalResults}} templating support seen in the source field description
drivingDirectionsLinkTexttextdirections-labelRenamed
showMoreLinkText/showLessLinkTexttextdetails-labelRenamedTwo source toggle labels collapse to one EDS "Show all practice details" label
api / apiVersion / apiEnv / apiActionselect ×4api-endpointRenamedFour dropdown-driven routing fields collapse into a single configured URL; routing logic moves from author-time dialog selection to runtime response-shape detection in JS
specialtyNametextindicationRenamedBoth feed a request payload field (source: specialty for Physician Locator API; EDS: PublisherName)
searchBrandedcheckboxNot mappedNot mappedNo branded/DSA-site toggle in the EDS model; that distinction is presumed to be handled by which brand folder's block-config is active, not an authorable field
exitmodalID / drivingDirectionsExitModalIdtextexit-modal-idRenamedTwo separate source modal IDs (virtual-visit exit and driving-directions exit) collapse to one EDS field
anchorIDtextanchor-idSame purpose, renamed casingcamelCase → kebab-case
enableVirtualVisits / virtualVisitsTitle / appointmentCtaBtnText / virtualVisitCtaBtnText / scheduleUrlcheckbox / text ×4Not mappedNot mappedNo virtual-visits/telemedicine CTA concept in the reviewed EDS field model or JS
filter multifield (name, ddPlaceHolder, help, required, hideFilter, type, qsParm, options)multifieldNot mappedNot mappedNo repeatable filter authoring surface in _find-provider.json; would need to be reintroduced as a structured field if filter parity is required
mapLocationselect (datasource)classes (find-provider-map option)RenamedAuthor-positioned map placement becomes a fixed map-variant toggle
markerLabel / markerSvgDatatext / textareaNot mappedNot mappedNo custom marker authoring in the EDS model; marker rendering (if any) is presumed hard-coded in JS/CSS
resultsPerPage / pagination labelsnumberfield / textNot mappedNot mappedNo pagination-related fields found in _find-provider.json; result-set size appears to be handled entirely by the API response
emailFeature / authorTitle / emailCtaBtnTextcheckbox / text / textNot mappedNot mappedNo "email these results" feature in the EDS model
interactionId / sectionTitle / l10Titletext (disabled) / text / textNot mappedNot mappedNo dedicated analytics tab; the EDS model relies on the shared common-properties block for id/language, with no interaction-ID or MVA bean equivalent
New EDS-only fields: geo-button-label, filter-label/filter-options, indication-label/indications, captcha-message, recaptcha-adjacent copy—as namedSame name (net-new)No source-system equivalent; introduced for the location-geo and filter/indication variants that did not exist as generalized concepts before

Flexbox Component — Technical Details

What it does

A generic CSS-flexbox layout container into which authors drop an arbitrary number of child paragraphs ("flex items"), each independently configurable for grow/basis/order/align-self. The source system actually ships two parallel implementations side by side: flexbox/flexboxitem (the original, dialog-heavy version with per-container and per-item flex property selects) and flexbox-v2/flexboxitem-v2 (a stripped-down version with no dialog at all — layout is presumably controlled entirely by custom CSS classes). The EDS flexbox block consolidates both into a single block with an image/content/width item model, and separately reproduces a source-side table-authoring workaround as a documented client-side rebuild step.

Architecture

flexbox / flexboxitem (v1):
Author sets flexItemsNumber on flexbox, AEM synthesizes N child paths "flexitem0..N-1"
         ↓
FlexboxModel.java (@PostConstruct): builds List<String> flexItem = ["flexitem0", "flexitem1", ...]
         ↓
flexbox.html: data-sly-list over flexboxModel.flexItem, each rendered via
  data-sly-resource(@path=flexItem, resourceType='.../flexboxitem')
         ↓
flexboxitem.html: wraps a nested parsys (@path='flexItem') in
  <div class="abbv-flex-item ${flexGrow} ${flexBasis} ${alignSelf} ${flexOrder}">

flexbox-v2 / flexboxitem-v2:
No Sling Model, no per-item dialog — flexboxitem-v2.html is just a parsys wrapper
  <div class="abbv-flex-item-v2 ${customCssClasses}">
Layout entirely via custom CSS classes on the container/items, authored free-text

EDS flexbox:
Author fills flexbox-item rows (image, imageAlt, content richtext, itemClasses width)
         ↓
flexbox.js decorate(): detects Universal Editor vs document authoring,
  builds one .flexbox-item div per row, moves width hint to a data-width attribute,
  and calls rebuildTables() on each item's rich-text content cell
         ↓
rebuildTables(): scans consecutive <p> rows matching a pipe-delimited pattern
  (TABLE_ROW_RE) and replaces the run with a real <table>, honoring per-cell
  {th|rN|cN|vl} hint prefixes and a {{head}} row marker for <thead> placement

Sling Model — FlexboxModel.java

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class FlexboxModel {
    private static final String FLEX_ITEM = "flexitem";

    @Inject
    @Optional
    private int flexItemsNumber;

    private List<String> flexItem;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        flexItem = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < flexItemsNumber; i++) {
            flexItem.add(FLEX_ITEM + i);
        }
    }

    public List<String> getFlexItem() { return flexItem; }
}

flexbox-v2 has no Sling Model at all — its HTL renders a bare parsys directly.

Dialog

ComponentFieldPropertyTypeNote
flexboxCustom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestext
flexboxAnchor IDanchorIDtext
flexboxNumber of flex itemsflexItemsNumbernumberfield (min 0)Drives synthetic child path generation in the Sling Model
flexboxFlex Direction / Flex Wrap / Justify Content / Align Items / Align ContentflexDirection / flexWrap / justifyContent / alignItems / alignContentselect (datasources ce-flexbox-*)Concatenated directly into the container's class attribute
flexboxitemCustom CSS Classes / Anchor IDcustomCssClasses / anchorIDtext
flexboxitemFlex Grow / Flex Basis / Align Self / Flex OrderflexGrow / flexBasis / alignSelf / flexOrderselect (datasources ce-flexbox-*)Per-item overrides
flexbox-v2 / flexboxitem-v2———No _cq_dialog found for either component; authoring is by custom CSS class only via the page/design dialog, not visible in the searched tree

HTL — Key Structure

<!-- flexbox.html -->
<sly data-sly-use.flexboxModel="${'com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.FlexboxModel'}">
    <div class="abbv-flex-container ${properties.customCssClasses} ${wcmmode.edit ? 'flex-direction-column' : contClasses}">
        <sly data-sly-list.flexItem="${flexboxModel.flexItem}">
            <div data-sly-resource="${@path=flexItem, resourceType='/apps/common-elements/v1/components/content/flexboxitem'}" data-sly-unwrap></div>
        </sly>
    </div>
</sly>

<!-- flexboxitem.html -->
<div class="abbv-flex-item ${properties.customCssClasses} ${properties.flexGrow} ${properties.flexBasis} ${properties.alignSelf} ${properties.flexOrder}">
    <sly data-sly-resource="${@path='flexItem', resourceType='wcm/foundation/components/parsys'}"></sly>
</div>

CSS

Not present as a scoped stylesheet in the searched source tree beyond the clientlib category registration; layout classes (abbv-flex-container, abbv-flex-item, plus whatever the ce-flexbox-* datasource option values resolve to as class names) are presumed defined in a shared/global stylesheet rather than a component-local one.

Important Constraints (source system)

  • Two parallel, functionally-overlapping components (flexbox vs. flexbox-v2) exist with no documented deprecation of either — a real migration source-selection ambiguity, not an EDS artifact.
  • flexItemsNumber creates a fixed, ordered set of synthetic child paths; reducing the number after authoring content into higher-numbered items orphans that content in the JCR rather than deleting it.
  • No table markup support at all in the source dialogs — the pipe-delimited-row workaround the EDS side documents is purely an EDS/content-round-trip concern (rich text authored in a Word/Google-Doc pipeline cannot preserve a real <table>), not a constraint inherited from the source system.

Flexbox Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

Fully implemented at blocks/flexbox/, marked "Reuse As-Is." Consolidates the source system's flexbox/flexbox-v2 split into one block with a fixed item shape (image, alt text, rich-text content, width keyword) rather than open-ended per-item flex-property selects.

File Structure

blocks/flexbox/
├── flexbox.js
├── flexbox.css
├── _flexbox.json
├── block-config.js
├── linzess/       (flexbox.css, _flexbox.css)
├── rinvoq-hcp/    (flexbox.css, _flexbox.css)
├── skyrizi-hcp/   (flexbox.css, _flexbox.css)
└── venclexta/     (block-config.js only)

Note: unlike every other block in this batch, flexbox has no abbvie/ brand folder in the reviewed tree — Brand A-equivalent overrides for this block are absent.

Authoring Model (_flexbox.json)

{
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "flexbox",
      "fields": [
        {
          "component": "multiselect", "valueType": "string", "name": "classes", "label": "Layout Variant",
          "options": [
            { "name": "Column direction", "value": "column" },
            { "name": "Row reverse", "value": "row-reverse" },
            { "name": "Column reverse", "value": "column-reverse" },
            { "name": "Center (justify + align)", "value": "center" },
            { "name": "Space between", "value": "space-between" },
            { "name": "No wrap", "value": "no-wrap" },
            { "name": "Align stretch", "value": "align-stretch" },
            { "name": "Align top", "value": "align-top" },
            { "name": "Align bottom", "value": "align-bottom" }
          ]
        },
        { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "anchorId", "value": "", "label": "Anchor ID" }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "flexbox-item",
      "fields": [
        { "component": "reference", "valueType": "string", "name": "image", "label": "Item Image", "multi": false },
        { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "imageAlt", "value": "", "label": "Image Alt Text" },
        { "component": "richtext", "name": "content", "value": "", "label": "Item Content", "valueType": "string" },
        {
          "component": "select", "valueType": "string", "name": "itemClasses", "value": "", "label": "Item Width",
          "options": [
            { "name": "Auto (equal flex)", "value": "" },
            { "name": "Full (100%)", "value": "full" },
            { "name": "Sixty (60%)", "value": "sixty" },
            { "name": "Half (50%)", "value": "half" },
            { "name": "Third (33%)", "value": "third" },
            { "name": "Thirty (30%)", "value": "thirty" },
            { "name": "Quarter (25%)", "value": "quarter" }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "filters": [ { "id": "flexbox", "components": ["flexbox-item"] } ]
}

How the JS works (flexbox.js)

The table-rebuild workaround, documented in the real file's header comment:

// A table authored inside a flexbox item's rich text cannot survive the
// publish round-trip as a real <table> (md2jcr flattens block structure in
// rich-text fields to plain <p> rows). Authors instead write one paragraph
// per row with cells separated by "|". flexbox.js rebuilds the <table> here.
//
// Per-cell prefix hints in leading {…}: th (header cell), rN (rowspan N),
// cN (colspan N), vl (vertical-label wrapper div). Example:
//   <p>{th,r3,vl}CBC Differential|{th}Neutrophils|&lt;1,000 cells/mm³</p>

export function rebuildTables(contentEl) {
  const children = [...contentEl.children];
  let rowEls = [];
  const flush = () => {
    if (rowEls.length < 2) { rowEls = []; return; }
    const table = document.createElement('table');
    const thead = document.createElement('thead');
    const tbody = document.createElement('tbody');
    const anyExplicitHead = rowEls.some((p) => /^\s*\{\{head\}\}/.test(p.innerHTML));
    rowEls.forEach((p, i) => {
      const html = p.innerHTML.trim();
      const isHead = anyExplicitHead ? /^\{\{head\}\}/.test(html) : i === 0;
      const rowHtml = html.replace(/^\{\{head\}\}/, '');
      const tr = document.createElement('tr');
      rowHtml.split('|').forEach((rawCell) => tr.append(buildCell(rawCell)));
      (isHead ? thead : tbody).append(tr);
    });
    table.append(thead, tbody);
    rowEls[0].replaceWith(table);
    rowEls.slice(1).forEach((p) => p.remove());
    rowEls = [];
  };
  children.forEach((el) => {
    if (el.tagName === 'P' && TABLE_ROW_RE.test(el.textContent)) rowEls.push(el);
    else flush();
  });
  flush();
}

decorate() branches on whether rows carry data-aue-type instrumentation (Universal Editor context, decorate in place to preserve authoring bindings) versus document authoring (give a brand block-config.js the first chance to build items via renderBlock, then fall back to a base positional build using a fixed FIELD_ORDER-equivalent: image cell 0, alt 1, content 2, width 3).

CSS

Base flexbox.css was not included among the files read in depth for this section beyond the JS-documented class contract (.flexbox-item, .flexbox-item-image, .flexbox-item-content, data-width attribute selectors); brand folders (linzess, rinvoq-hcp, skyrizi-hcp) each carry their own flexbox.css/_flexbox.css pair following the same override pattern seen in every other block in this batch.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (flexbox/flexboxitem, flexbox-v2/flexboxitem-v2)EDS (flexbox)
Item countFixed by an author-set flexItemsNumber, generating synthetic flexitemN pathsOpen-ended — one block row per item, no count field
Item content modelFree nested parsys — any component can go in a flex itemFixed shape: image + alt + richtext + width select
Per-item flex controlOpen datasource-backed selects for grow/basis/align-self/orderSingle "Item Width" keyword select mapped to fixed percentages
Container-level flex controlflexbox: 5 independent selects (direction/wrap/justify/align-items/align-content, v1 only); flexbox-v2: none, free CSS classes onlySingle classes multiselect of 9 named variants
Table authoringNot applicable — nested parsys can contain a real table component directlyPipe-delimited paragraph convention rebuilt into a real <table> client-side, to work around rich-text round-trip limitations

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorID (flexbox)textanchorIdSame purpose, renamed casingcamelCase → lowerCamel field name change only
customCssClasses (flexbox)textclassesRenamedOpen free-text class list becomes a closed multiselect of 9 named layout variants — narrower but structured
flexDirection / flexWrap / justifyContent / alignItems / alignContentselect ×5 (open datasource)classes (subset of multiselect options: column/row-reverse/column-reverse/center/space-between/no-wrap/align-stretch/align-top/align-bottom)RenamedFive independent open-ended selects collapse into named combinations within one multiselect; not a 1:1 value mapping — some source datasource values (e.g. arbitrary CSS justify-content keywords not in the 9-item list) have no EDS equivalent
flexItemsNumbernumberfieldNot mappedNot mappedEDS items are authored as repeated block rows directly; there is no synthetic count field because the DOM row list itself is the item collection
customCssClasses (flexboxitem)textitemClassesRenamedOpen free-text becomes a closed 7-option width select — narrower scope, purpose-specific (width only, not arbitrary classes)
anchorID (flexboxitem)textNot mappedNot mappedNo per-item anchor field in flexbox-item model; only the container-level anchorId survives
flexGrow / flexBasis / alignSelf / flexOrderselect ×4 (open datasource)itemClassesRenamedFour independent per-item flex properties collapse into one "Item Width" keyword; fine-grained order/align-self control is not reproducible via the current EDS field
Nested parsys content (flexboxitem's free child components)parsys (any component)image + content (richtext)Renamed / narrowedOpen component slot becomes a fixed image + rich text pair; item content types beyond image/text (e.g. an embedded CTA button component) have no direct field and would need to be authored as rich-text markup or dropped
flexbox-v2 / flexboxitem-v2 fieldsnone (no dialog)—Not applicableNo fields to map — v2 components carried no authorable properties beyond free CSS classes on a parsys wrapper

Footer Component — Technical Details

What it does

Renders the page-global footer: a horizontal- or vertical-layout set of author-defined link lists (nested multifields — outer list groups, each containing inner link items), a legal text block, an "epass" legal/regulatory number pulled from page properties, and a logo (image or inline SVG fallback) with an optional "indication" swap state. The manifest's third listed source, "Page Components: full-width-content-page," is a per-brand page template/component (found under e.g. apps/upa/v1/components/page/full-width-content-page, outside the shared common-elements tree) that hosts the inherited footer parsys on every page — it is a page-structure concern, not a content component with its own dialog fields, and is noted here for completeness rather than mapped as a field source.

Architecture

Author configures Overview (anchor/CSS), footer layout (horizontal/vertical),
  repeatable Link List(s) (outerFieldset → innerFieldset multifields), logo fields
         ↓
footer.html: data-sly-use.footerModel="FooterMultiModel"
  branches on properties.footerlayout:
    horizontal → <ul class="abbv-footer-horizontal"> per outer list, flat <li> per link
    vertical   → <div class="abbv-col"> per outer list with an <h5> title, <ul class="abbv-footer-vertical">
  each link resolved through LinkChecker (jump-link detection) then rendered via
  anchortag.html's anchorFooter template, or button.html's buttonSwitch template
  for round-switch/square-switch link styles
         ↓
  legal text (${properties.legaltext} unsafe HTML), epass number (${pageProperties.epassNumber}),
  logo block: <img> if footerModel.logoImagePath present, else an inline AbbVie SVG wordmark

Sling Model — FooterMultiModel.java

Extends AbstractComponentModel; reads the repeatable outerFieldset/innerFieldset multifield names directly and (per the surrounding beans package) assembles them into FooterOuterListDetailsBean/FooterNestedListDetailsBean lists exposed as footerOuterListDetailsBeans:

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class FooterMultiModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENT_ID_PREFIX = "abbv-footer";

    @Inject @Optional private List<String> outerFieldset;
    @Inject @Optional private List<String> innerFieldset;
    @Inject @Optional private String logotitle;
    @Inject @Optional private String logolinkurl;
    @Inject @Optional private String logolinkbehaviour;
    @Inject @Optional private String interactionID;
    // ... additional analytics-bean-building fields (analyticsTitleLogo, analyticsMVAType, etc.)

    // getFooterOuterListDetailsBeans(), getLogoImagePath(), getLogoTitle(),
    // getLogoAnalyticsString(), getIndicationOn(), getFooterLinksClass() etc.
}

Dialog (fields with a direct authoring surface)

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Footer Identifier (Anchor ID)anchorIDtext
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestext
Footer Layoutfooterlayoutselecthorizontal / vertical
Add Link List(s) — outer group: Custom CSS Class(es), Link List Title, Section/Localized Title for AnalyticsouterFieldset/customCssClasses, listtitle, linkListSectionTitle, linkListL10Titlemultifield > fieldsetRepeatable outer list
Add Link List Item(s) — inner group (nested inside outer)innerFieldset/... (link URL, title, icon, icon position, style, behavior, modal ID, custom CSS classes, analytics fields — inferred from HTL's innerMultiList.* references)multifield > fieldset (nested, acs-commons-nested="4")Repeatable inner links per outer list

Legal text, epass number, and logo fields were referenced in the HTL (properties.legaltext, pageProperties.epassNumber, properties.logotitle/logolinkurl/logolinkbehaviour/logomodalID) but their originating dialog markup was not present in the single _cq_dialog/.content.xml section reviewed in depth here — they are dialog/page-property fields consumed by the HTL and model regardless of which tab defines them.

HTL — Key Structure (vertical layout branch)

<sly data-sly-test="${properties.footerlayout=='vertical'}">
    <div class="abbv-row abbv-row-flush">
        <sly data-sly-list.outerMultiList="${footerModel.footerOuterListDetailsBeans}">
            <div class="abbv-col ${footerModel.footerLinksClass} ${outerMultiList.customCssClasses}">
                <h5 role="heading" aria-level="4">${outerMultiList.listTitle @context="html"}</h5>
                <ul class="abbv-footer-vertical">
                    <sly data-sly-list.innerMultiList="${outerMultiList.listDetails}">
                        <li>
                            <sly data-sly-use.linkhelper="${'com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.LinkChecker' @link = innerMultiList.linkURL}">
                                <sly data-sly-use.anchorTemplate="/apps/common-elements/v1/components/content/anchortag/anchortag.html">
                                    <sly data-sly-call="${anchorTemplate.anchorFooter @ analyticsString=innerMultiList.digitalData,
                                         url=linkhelper.linkFormatted, modalID=innerMultiList.modalID,
                                         linkTitle=innerMultiList.linkTitle, ...}"/>
                                </sly>
                            </sly>
                        </li>
                    </sly>
                </ul>
            </div>
        </sly>
    </div>
</sly>
<div class="abbv-legal-text abbv-rich-text-common">${properties.legaltext @ context='unsafe'}</div>
<p class="abbv-legal-number">${pageProperties.epassNumber}</p>

CSS

No footer-scoped stylesheet was found beyond the clientlib category registration (ui/footer.css exists but was not reviewed in depth for this section); the class names in the HTL (abbv-footer, abbv-footer-horizontal, abbv-footer-vertical, abbv-legal-text) indicate a shared, non-token-based styling approach consistent with the rest of the source system.

Important Constraints (source system)

  • Legal text is rendered with context='unsafe' — raw HTML injection with no sanitization at the HTL layer; XSS risk is pushed entirely onto author trust.
  • Logo fallback is an inline SVG path string hard-coded directly in the HTL template (not a referenced asset) — any brand wanting a different fallback mark must patch this component's HTL, not author content.
  • The double-nested multifield (outer list groups containing inner link items, acs-commons-nested) is an ACS AEM Commons–specific authoring pattern with known Classic-UI/Touch-UI quirks around ordering and default values.

Footer Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

Fully implemented at blocks/footer/, marked "Reuse+Enhance." Unlike every other component in this batch, the footer block has no field model at all — there is no _footer.json in the block folder. The footer is fragment-driven: content authored once as a reusable page (a "fragment," in Universal Editor/EDS terms) at a configurable path (page footer metadata, defaulting to /footer), fetched and its DOM restructured into columns by position or heading, with no authorable dialog fields on the footer block itself. This section documents behavior instead of a field table, per the block's actual architecture.

File Structure

blocks/footer/
├── footer.js
├── footer.css
├── block-config.js          (no _footer.json — fragment-driven, no field model)
├── abbvie/         (footer.css, _footer.css, block-config.js)
├── botox/
├── linzess/
├── mavyret/
├── rinvoq/
├── rinvoq-hcp/
├── roy/
│   └── themes/bright/  (footer.css, _footer.css)
├── skyrizi-hcp/
├── themes/bright/      (footer.css, _footer.css — base-level theme variant)
└── venclexta/

How the JS works (footer.js)

Fragment loading, with a page-metadata override:

export async function loadFooterFragment() {
  const footerMeta = getMetadata('footer');
  const footerPath = footerMeta ? new URL(footerMeta, window.location).pathname : '/footer';
  return loadFragment(footerPath);
}

Default column assembly is positional, not field-driven — it walks the fetched fragment's first .section's children (collected from .default-content-wrapper and .social-media-wrapper) and buckets them by shape:

export function buildColumnsByPosition(children) {
  const container = document.createElement('div');
  container.className = 'footer-columns';
  let cursor = 0;

  const logoChildren = [];
  if (children[cursor] && children[cursor].querySelector('picture')) {
    logoChildren.push(children[cursor].cloneNode(true));
    cursor += 1;
  }
  container.appendChild(createColumn('footer-column footer-logo', logoChildren));

  const primaryLinkChildren = [];
  while (cursor < children.length
      && (children[cursor].tagName === 'UL' || children[cursor].classList.contains('social-media'))) {
    primaryLinkChildren.push(children[cursor].cloneNode(true));
    cursor += 1;
  }
  container.appendChild(createColumn('footer-column footer-links-primary', primaryLinkChildren));
  // ... secondary (P + UL pair), tertiary (remainder)
  return container;
}

An alternative heading-based strategy is also exported (buildColumnsByHeading) — each H1–H6 starts a new column — for brand block-configs that want a different fragment-authoring convention. The second fragment .section, if present, becomes .footer-bottom (legal text / epass-number equivalents, copyright line, etc.) via buildBottom(). The default export simply delegates to the shared brand-config runner:

export default async function decorate(block) {
  await renderBlock(block);
}

CSS

Not reviewed in full-file depth for this section; the block ships a base footer.css plus per-brand overrides (abbvie, botox, linzess, mavyret, rinvoq, rinvoq-hcp, roy, skyrizi-hcp, venclexta) and a base-level and roy-level themes/bright variant — the deepest brand/theme override structure of any block in this batch.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (footer + headerlogo)EDS (footer)
Content sourceNested multifields authored directly on the footer component instance, per page (or per template if placed in an inherited parsys)A single reusable fragment page (default /footer), loaded via fetch + .plain.html, overridable per page via the footer page-metadata key
Column assemblyExplicit outer/inner multifield structure — authors see and control list grouping directly in the dialogInferred from DOM shape/position (or headings) at render time — authors structure the fragment page's content, not a dialog
Layout switchAuthor-selected horizontal/vertical dialog field driving two different HTL branchesNo layout field — layout is entirely a function of the fragment's authored content shape plus CSS
LogoSeparate headerlogo component/model (HeaderLogoModel) supplying logoImage/dimensions/alt text, with an inline hard-coded SVG fallback in the footer's own HTLLogo is just the first fragment child if it contains a <picture> — no separate logo component/model, no hard-coded SVG fallback found in the reviewed JS
Legal/epass textproperties.legaltext (unsafe HTML) + pageProperties.epassNumber, both page/dialog-property drivenWhatever content the fragment's second .section contains, wrapped in .footer-bottom — no dedicated "epass number" concept
AnalyticsPer-link digitalData strings built by LinkChecker/analytics beans in JavaNot present in the reviewed footer.js; presumed handled by shared page-level analytics instrumentation rather than per-link server-built strings

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

Not applicable in table form — the footer block has no _footer.json field model to map against. Per the assignment instructions, this is stated explicitly rather than forcing a mapping: the source system's dialog-driven, per-instance link-list authoring model (outer/inner multifields, layout select, logo fields, legal/epass text) has no field-for-field EDS counterpart, because the EDS implementation moved that entire authoring surface into ordinary page content on a fragment page, structurally interpreted by footer.js at render time rather than declared through a component dialog.

Form Component — Technical Details

What it does

The manifest's listed source, forms-text-inputs, is not a real, authorable AEM component. Its component folder contains only a .php file and an icon — no .content.xml, no _cq_dialog, no HTL template, no Sling Model. Reading the PHP file confirms it is a static style-guide/pattern-library showcase page (part of the ADMP-UI component catalog demo shell, namespaced forms/"Forms") that renders example text-input, textarea, and number-counter markup for design reference, plus a companion variations.php with jQuery demo wiring (disabled-state toggles, error/valid state toggles) for that showcase page — it was never meant to be dragged onto a real content page. There is therefore no real dialog, Sling Model, or field set to document or map for this source. The EDS form block is a distinct, independently-sourced implementation (the standard block-collection JSON-driven form renderer), not a redesign of forms-text-inputs.

Architecture (source: showcase page, not a component)

forms-text-inputs/ui/forms-text-inputs.php — static demo markup only:
  <div class="abbv-form-wrapper">
    <label class="abbv-label-text" for="Text">Text Input Label</label>
    <input type="text" pattern="[0-9]{5}" placeholder="Text Field (Zipcode Pattern)" name="Text">
    <p class="abbv-additional-info">...</p>
    <span class="abbv-error-msg" data-err-msg="Invalid Zip Code"></span>
  </div>
  (repeated for textarea and number-counter variants)

forms-text-inputs/ui/variations.php — demo-only jQuery:
  toggles .abbv-error / .abbv-valid classes and disabled state across the showcase
  block on checkbox interaction, purely for documentation purposes

Sling Model

None exists. No Java class implements a resource type for forms-text-inputs; it is not registered as a cq:Component at all (no .content.xml in its folder).

Dialog

None exists. No _cq_dialog folder is present under forms-text-inputs/. There are no authorable fields to list or map.

HTL

Not applicable — the source is plain PHP demo markup, not an HTL (Sightly) template, and is not part of the Sling resource-resolution/component pipeline at all.

CSS

Uses the same global utility classes as the rest of the ADMP-UI pattern library (abbv-form-wrapper, abbv-label-text, abbv-additional-info, abbv-error-msg, abbv-switch/abbv-slider) — no component-scoped stylesheet, because there is no component.

Important Constraints

  • This manifest entry is best read as "the design-system reference for form field styling," not a migratable component — there is nothing to functionally port; the visual language (label-above vs. label-inline, help text, error text) is what a real forms component would need to reproduce, but the file itself contributes no logic.

Form Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

Fully implemented at blocks/form/, marked "Reuse+Enhance." This is the standard block-collection form pattern: the block's two authored links (a form-definition JSON endpoint and a submit endpoint) drive a client-side field-builder that fetches a JSON row-list of field definitions and renders typed inputs, grouping fields into <fieldset>s by a data-fieldset attribute.

File Structure

blocks/form/
├── form.js
├── form-fields.js
├── form.css
├── _form.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/   (form.css, _form.css, block-config.js)
├── botox/
└── rinvoq/

Authoring Model (_form.json)

{
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "form",
      "fields": [
        { "component": "aem-content", "name": "reference", "label": "Form" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "action", "label": "Action URL", "valueType": "string" }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "filters": []
}

Just two fields: the form's JSON definition reference and the submission action URL. All per-field configuration (label, type, options, required, fieldset grouping) lives in the fetched JSON, not in the block's own authoring model.

How the JS works (form.js / form-fields.js)

async function createForm(formHref, submitHref) {
  const { pathname } = new URL(formHref);
  const resp = await fetch(pathname);
  const json = await resp.json();

  const form = document.createElement('form');
  form.dataset.action = submitHref;

  const fields = await Promise.all(json.data.map((fd) => createField(fd, form)));
  fields.forEach((field) => { if (field) form.append(field); });

  const fieldsets = form.querySelectorAll('fieldset');
  fieldsets.forEach((fieldset) => {
    form.querySelectorAll(`[data-fieldset="${fieldset.name}"`).forEach((field) => {
      fieldset.append(field);
    });
  });
  return form;
}

form-fields.js maps each JSON row's Type to a builder (createHeading, createPlaintext, createSelect, and others not shown here), each reading a common shape (fd.Name, fd.Label, fd.Mandatory, fd.Placeholder, fd.Value, fd.Style, fd.Fieldset, fd.Options). Select fields can source their options from an inline comma-separated string or a remote JSON endpoint:

if (fd.Options.startsWith('https://')) {
  const optionsUrl = new URL(fd.Options);
  const resp = await fetch(`${optionsUrl.pathname}${optionsUrl.search}`);
  const json = await resp.json();
  json.data.forEach((opt) => { options.push({ text: opt.Option, value: opt.Value || opt.Option }); });
} else {
  options = fd.Options.split(',').map((opt) => ({ text: opt.trim(), value: opt.trim() }));
}

Submission builds a flat payload object (comma-joining multi-checked checkbox values) and POSTs JSON to the block's configured action URL, redirecting to a confirmation URL on success if one is set.

CSS

Not reviewed in full-file depth for this section; the block follows the same base + abbvie/botox/rinvoq brand-override structure as the rest of this batch.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (forms-text-inputs)EDS (form)
NatureStatic style-guide showcase page — not a real, authorable componentA real, data-driven form-rendering block
Field definitionHard-coded demo markupFetched JSON row list (external field authoring, e.g. a spreadsheet/sheet-backed source), interpreted client-side
SubmissionNone — demo onlyReal JSON POST to a configured action URL with client-side validation

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
Not applicable. forms-text-inputs has no dialog, no Sling Model, and no authorable properties — there is nothing to classify as Same name / Renamed / Not mapped, because there was never a mapped source field set. The EDS form block's two fields (reference, action) and its JSON-driven per-field schema (Name, Label, Type, Mandatory, Placeholder, Value, Style, Fieldset, Options) are net-new, sourced from the standard block-collection form pattern rather than any component documented in this batch's source tree.

Formulary Lookup Component — Technical Details

What it does

An insurance/formulary coverage-lookup widget: an author-configured first screen (state/county selection, or a static-JSON-backed single-state lookup) that submits to either a live API or a DAM-hosted static JSON file, then swaps to a results screen showing coverage data. The source system ships two variants: formulary-lookup (state + optional county, either "Dynamic API" or "Static Data" data-source mode) and formulary-lookup-zipcode (a ZIP-code-driven variant). The EDS formulary-lookup block consolidates both into one block with a classes multiselect (dynamic vs. zip variant, plus card/centered layout modifiers) and per-brand config-flag overrides for icon/disclaimer/reCAPTCHA behavior.

Architecture

Author picks Option 1 (Dynamic API, with API Endpoint Type int/stage/prod) or
  Option 2 (Static Data — pathbrowser to a DAM JSON file), configures Lookup screen
  (heading, description, state/county labels+placeholders, button text+icon),
  Results screen (heading, note), Errors (data-load / no-results messages), Analytics
         ↓
formulary-lookup.html: data-sly-use.model="FormularyLookupModel"
  model.getConfigType() resolves to "api" or "json" based on enableAPI
  model.getScriptID() derives a per-instance script identifier (id with dashes stripped)
  FormularyLookupModel.init() also resolves an inherited adminConfigPath up the page tree,
    resolves a brand config folder via CommonUtility.getBrandConfig(),
    and looks up a "productIdentifier" title from an admin API-config page —
    i.e. the API target/credentials are resolved from a per-brand admin config page,
    not typed directly into this component's own dialog
         ↓
formulary-lookup-script-obj.html emits an inline config object (state/county data URLs,
  labels, error messages) consumed by the deferred formulary-lookup-component.v1 clientlib JS,
  which drives the two-screen (lookup → results) UI swap

EDS formulary-lookup:
Author picks Variant & Layout (dynamic/zip + card/centered), fills labels/heading/description,
  Configuration tab: api endpoint, recaptcha-key, results-per-page, no-results/error messages,
  recaptcha-notice, disclaimer, analyticsId
         ↓
formulary-lookup.js: reads config via a fixed FIELD_ORDER list (richtext-aware for
  terms-like fields), branches UI by classes.contains('dynamic'/'zip'/'card'/'centered'),
  calls getRecaptchaToken() before the API request if recaptcha-key is set,
  shows a disclaimer overlay/modal gated by a brand block-config flag (disclaimerModal)

Sling Model — FormularyLookupModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class}, defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class FormularyLookupModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENTIDPREFIX = "abbv-formulary-lookup";

    @Inject private String interactionId;
    @Inject private String enableAPI;
    @Inject @Source("osgi-services") private SlingSettingsService slingSettingsService;

    private String productIdentifier;
    private String configType;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        if (!isPublish() && (StringUtils.isBlank(interactionId) || !interactionId.equals(getId()))) {
            setInteractionIdInComponentDialog();
        }
        configType = "true".equalsIgnoreCase(enableAPI) ? "api" : "json";

        PageManager pageManager = resource.getResourceResolver().adaptTo(PageManager.class);
        if (pageManager != null) {
            Page currentPage = pageManager.getContainingPage(resource);
            InheritanceValueMap inheritedProperties = new HierarchyNodeInheritanceValueMap(currentPage.getContentResource());
            String adminConfigPath = inheritedProperties.getInherited("adminConfigPath", String.class);
            String brandConfig = CommonUtility.getBrandConfig(adminConfigPath, resourceResolver);
            String defaultPath = MessageFormat.format(AbbvieFormConstants.ADMIN_APICONFIG_PATH, brandConfig);
            resource = FormsConfigUtil.getAdminPageResourceCE(
                AbbvieFormConstants.ADMIN_APICONFIG_PATH_RUNMODE, defaultPath,
                new Object[]{FormsConfigUtil.getEnvRunMode(), FormsConfigUtil.getEnvType(), brandConfig}, resource);
            if (resource != null) {
                final Page page = resource.adaptTo(Page.class);
                productIdentifier = FormsConfigUtil.getTitleByValue(page, AbbvieFormConstants.PRODUCT_IDENTIFIER);
            }
        }
    }

    public String getScriptID() { return getId().replace("-", ""); }
    public String getConfigType() { return configType; }
}

Notably, the API target itself is not a field on this component at all — it is resolved indirectly through an inherited page property (adminConfigPath) pointing at a brand-specific admin config page, read via a shared FormsConfigUtil/CommonUtility layer shared with other API-backed components.

Dialog

TabFieldPropertyTypeNote
OverviewFormulary Lookup Identifier (Anchor ID)anchorIDtext
OverviewCustom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestext
OverviewUse the Dynamic APIenableAPIcheckboxDrives configType ("api" vs "json")
OverviewAPI Endpoint TypeapiEndPointTypeselectIntegration/Staging/Production
OverviewStatic File Option — Formulary Lookup Data FilelookupPathpathbrowser (rootPath /content/dam)Used when enableAPI is off
LookupLookup TitlelookupHeadingtext
LookupLookup DescriptionlookupDescriptionincludewidget (rich text)
LookupState Menu Label/PlaceholderlookUpStateLabel / lookUpPlaceholderTexttext
LookupCounty Menu Label/PlaceholderlookUpCountyLabel / lookUpCountyPlaceholderTexttext
LookupLookup Button TextlookUpButtonTexttext
LookupLookup Button Icon / PositionsearchIcon / searchIconPositionselect (datasource ce-link-icon) / select
Results SectionResults TitleresultsHeadingtext
Results SectionResults DescriptionresultsNoteincludewidget (rich text)
ErrorsData Load Error MessagedataLoadErrorMsgtextDefault: "The data was not able to be loaded. Please try again."
ErrorsNo Results Found Error MessagenoResultsErrMsgtextDefault: "No results were found, please try another selection"
AnalyticsAnalytics Interaction ID (read-only)interactionIdtext (disabled)
AnalyticsSection Title / Localized Section Title for AnalyticssectionTitle / l10Titletext
AnalyticsMost Valued Action (MVA) / MVA Title / MVA SecondarymvaPrimary / mvaTitle / mvaSecondarycheckbox / text / checkbox

HTL — Key Structure

<sly data-sly-use.model="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.FormularyLookupModel">
    <div class="abbv-formulary-lookup" data-config-type="${model.configType}"
         data-script-id="${model.scriptID}">
        <!-- lookup screen: state/county selects or static-data driven markup,
             conditional on data-config-type -->
        <!-- results screen: populated client-side after successful lookup -->
    </div>
</sly>
<sly data-sly-call="${clientLib.js @ categories='formulary-lookup-component.v1', loading='defer'}"></sly>

(Full literal HTL body for formulary-lookup.html was not re-quoted verbatim beyond this structural summary; the script-obj include pattern mirrors the one documented in detail for find-a-provider above — an inline config-object <script> feeding the deferred clientlib JS.)

CSS

Registered via clientlib category (ui/formulary-lookup.css); not reviewed in full-file depth here, consistent with the unscoped/utility-class styling approach seen across the rest of the source system.

Important Constraints (source system)

  • The live-API target is not directly authorable on the component — it is resolved through an inherited adminConfigPath page property plus a brand-config lookup, meaning the actual endpoint used depends on page placement in the content tree, not anything visible in this component's own dialog.
  • The "Static Data Method" only supports a single state (no county drill-down), a real functional gap relative to the Dynamic API mode.
  • No reCAPTCHA field exists on formulary-lookup itself in the reviewed dialog (unlike find-a-provider, which has an explicit captchaSiteKey) — bot protection, if any, would come from the shared API layer rather than this component.

Formulary Lookup Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

Fully implemented at blocks/formulary-lookup/, marked "Reuse+Enhance." Consolidates the source system's state/county and ZIP variants into one block, adds a real reCAPTCHA v3 token flow, and moves several previously-fixed behaviors (icon visibility, disclaimer-as-modal-vs-inline, error display style) into brand-specific config flags rather than either a fixed template choice or an author-facing field.

File Structure

blocks/formulary-lookup/
├── formulary-lookup.js
├── formulary-lookup.css
├── _formulary-lookup.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/     (formulary-lookup.css, _formulary-lookup.css, block-config.js — flags empty)
├── botox/      (flags: submitIconEnabled: false, autoSubmitOnStateChange: false)
├── mavyret/    (flags: submitIconEnabled: false, autoSubmitOnStateChange: false, disclaimerModal: true, errorDisplayMode: 'modal')
├── rinvoq/     (block-config.js — flags empty)
├── rinvoq-hcp/ (flags: submitIconEnabled: true, errorDisplayMode: 'tooltip')
└── skyrizi-hcp/(flags: submitIconEnabled: true, errorDisplayMode: 'tooltip')

Every brand carries its own flag set — this is the concrete "brand-specific config overrides (icon, disclaimer, reCAPTCHA)" behavior named in the manifest, expressed as data (flags returned from each brand's block-config.js) rather than as authorable dialog fields.

Authoring Model (_formulary-lookup.json, excerpt)

{
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "formulary-lookup",
      "fields": [
        { "component": "tab", "label": "Content", "name": "content" },
        {
          "component": "multiselect", "name": "classes", "label": "Variant & Layout",
          "description": "Select variant and optional layout modifiers. Default: state dropdown. Dynamic: state + county. ZIP: ZIP code input. Card: white card with shadow. Centered: centered layout.",
          "options": [
            { "name": "Dynamic (State + County)", "value": "dynamic" },
            { "name": "ZIP Code", "value": "zip" },
            { "name": "Card Container", "value": "card" },
            { "name": "Centered Layout", "value": "centered" }
          ]
        },
        { "component": "reference", "name": "icon", "label": "Icon" },
        { "component": "richtext", "name": "heading", "label": "Heading" },
        { "component": "richtext", "name": "description", "label": "Description" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "state-label", "value": "Select your state", "label": "State Dropdown Label" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "county-label", "value": "Select your county", "label": "County Dropdown Label" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "zip-label", "value": "Enter your ZIP code", "label": "ZIP Code Input Label" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "zip-placeholder", "label": "ZIP Code Placeholder" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "submit-label", "value": "Search", "label": "Submit Button Label" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "filter-label", "label": "Filter Dropdown Label" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "filter-options", "label": "Filter Options",
          "description": "Comma-separated filter options (e.g., 'Commercial,Medicare,State Medicaid')." },
        { "component": "text", "name": "indication-label", "label": "Indication Label" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "indications", "label": "Indications",
          "description": "Comma-separated indication values (e.g., 'AD,PsA,Ps')." },
        { "component": "tab", "label": "Configuration", "name": "config" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "api", "label": "API Endpoint",
          "description": "Formulary lookup API URL. Receives ?state=, ?state=&county=, or ?zip= query parameters depending on variant." },
        { "component": "text", "name": "recaptcha-key", "label": "reCAPTCHA Site Key" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "results-per-page", "value": "10", "label": "Results Per Page" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "no-results", "value": "No coverage information found.", "label": "No Results Message" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "error", "value": "An error occurred. Please try again.", "label": "Error Message" },
        { "component": "richtext", "name": "recaptcha-notice", "label": "reCAPTCHA Notice Text" },
        { "component": "richtext", "name": "disclaimer", "label": "Disclaimer Text" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "analyticsId", "label": "Analytics ID" },
        { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

How the JS works (formulary-lookup.js)

import { getRecaptchaToken } from '../eds-form/recaptcha.js';

const RICHTEXT_KEYS = new Set(['recaptcha-notice', 'disclaimer']);

// ... reads config['recaptcha-key'] and requests a token before the API call:
if (config['recaptcha-key']) {
  const token = await getRecaptchaToken(config['recaptcha-key'], 'formulary_lookup');
  headers['x-recaptcha-token'] = token;
}

// disclaimer is either an inline block or a modal overlay, gated by a brand flag:
if (!disclaimerShown && flags.disclaimerModal && config.disclaimer) {
  disclaimerShown = true;
  const disclaimerEl = section.closest('.formulary-lookup')?.querySelector('.formulary-lookup-disclaimer-overlay');
  if (disclaimerEl && typeof disclaimerEl.open === 'function') disclaimerEl.open();
}

// submit-button icon visibility is a brand flag, not an authorable field:
if (flags.submitIconEnabled) btn.classList.add('has-icon');

Icon, reCAPTCHA notice, and disclaimer each render conditionally purely on whether the corresponding config value is present:

function buildIcon(config) {
  if (!config.icon) return null;
  // ... img.src = config.icon
}
function buildDisclaimer(config) {
  if (!config.disclaimer) return null;
  // ... div.innerHTML = config.disclaimer
}

CSS

Not reviewed in full-file depth for this section; follows the same base + per-brand override structure as the rest of the batch.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (formulary-lookup + formulary-lookup-zipcode)EDS (formulary-lookup)
Variant selectionTwo separate components (state/county vs. ZIP), chosen by which one an author drags onto the pageOne block; variant chosen via the classes multiselect (dynamic vs. zip), plus independent card/centered layout modifiers
Data source modeAuthor toggle between "Dynamic API" and "Static Data" (DAM JSON file) per instanceSingle api endpoint field; no static-file/DAM-JSON authoring mode found in the EDS model
API target resolutionIndirect — inherited adminConfigPath page property plus brand-config lookup resolves the actual endpointDirect — api is a literal field value on the block instance
Bot protectionNot present on this component (no captcha field found)Real reCAPTCHA v3 token flow via recaptcha-key + getRecaptchaToken()
IconDatasource-backed icon/position selects (ce-link-icon), applied to the submit buttonreference-type icon field rendered above the heading; visibility/behavior of the submit-button icon is a brand block-config flag (submitIconEnabled), not an authored per-instance choice
Disclaimer presentationNot modeled as inline-vs-modal in the reviewed dialog (results screen has a free-text "note" field only)Explicit inline-vs-modal-overlay behavior gated by the brand flag disclaimerModal
Error displayTwo fixed text fields (data-load / no-results), presentation not configurablePresentation mode itself (tooltip vs modal) is a brand flag (errorDisplayMode), independent of the message text fields

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
lookupHeadingtextheadingRenamedType also changes text → richtext, allowing partial emphasis per the EDS field description
lookupDescriptionincludewidget (rich text)descriptionRenamedBoth rich text
lookUpStateLabel / lookUpPlaceholderTexttextstate-labelRenamedSource has separate label and placeholder fields; EDS retains only a label field with the same default text pattern — no distinct state-placeholder field found
lookUpCountyLabel / lookUpCountyPlaceholderTexttextcounty-labelRenamedSame placeholder-field narrowing as state above
lookUpButtonTexttextsubmit-labelRenamed
searchIcon / searchIconPositionselect (datasource) / selecticonRenamedStructural change: a datasource-driven icon-glyph + position pair becomes a single image reference field rendered in a fixed position (above the heading), with icon visibility on the submit button itself now controlled by the brand flag submitIconEnabled rather than an authored position choice
resultsHeading / resultsNotetext / includewidgetNot mappedNot mappedNo dedicated results-screen heading/note fields found in _formulary-lookup.json; results presentation appears to be handled by fixed JS/CSS rather than authored copy
dataLoadErrorMsgtexterrorRenamed
noResultsErrMsgtextno-resultsRenamed
enableAPI / apiEndPointTypecheckbox / selectapiRenamedSource's boolean API-mode toggle plus environment select collapses into a single literal endpoint URL field; environment routing becomes an authoring-time (not template-time) concern
lookupPath (Static Data DAM JSON file)pathbrowserNot mappedNot mappedNo static-file/DAM-JSON data-source mode exists in the EDS model — the EDS block always calls a live api endpoint
anchorID / customCssClassestextCommon properties blockId / customClassRenamedFolded into the shared common-properties include
interactionId / sectionTitle / l10Title / mvaPrimary / mvaTitle / mvaSecondarytext (disabled) / text / text / checkbox / text / checkboxanalyticsIdRenamedSix source analytics/MVA-bean-building fields collapse into a single free-text analytics ID attribute; no MVA-tier concept survives in the EDS model
(no source equivalent)—classes (variant/layout multiselect)Same name (net-new)Replaces the source system's "which component did you drag onto the page" variant selection with an explicit authored field
(no source equivalent)—zip-label / zip-placeholderSame name (net-new)Corresponds conceptually to formulary-lookup-zipcode's (not individually reviewed) ZIP input, but as fields on the unified block rather than a separate component's dialog
(no source equivalent)—filter-label / filter-options / indication-label / indicationsSame name (net-new)No filter or indication concept found in either source dialog reviewed
(no source equivalent)—recaptcha-key / recaptcha-noticeSame name (net-new)No captcha field on formulary-lookup in the source system
(no source equivalent)—results-per-page / disclaimerSame name (net-new)No pagination or disclaimer-text field found on the source components' reviewed dialogs

Fragment Component — Technical Details

What it does

Includes the content of another page/paragraph inline on the current page, by resource path. In the source system this maps most directly to admp-reference — a component (hidden from the component browser, allowed only inside a parsys) that resolves an author-chosen path via data-sly-resource, i.e. a same-request, server-side JCR include. The manifest's second listed source, contentfragment, is a distinct component (documented in the fact-card section above) that renders AEM Content Fragment data, not an arbitrary page/paragraph reference — it is included here as a source mapping but does not share admp-reference's "include another page's content" purpose; the two are grouped in the manifest likely because both are content-inclusion mechanisms, not because they overlap in implementation. The EDS fragment block matches admp-reference's purpose closely but replaces the server-side JCR include with a client-side fetch of the target page's .plain.html.

Architecture

Source system (admp-reference):
Author browses to a path via a pathbrowser (predicate="nosystem", rootPath="/content", no page paths allowed)
         ↓
admp-reference.html: data-sly-use.model="ReferenceModel", data-sly-call="${renderReference @ model=model}"
         ↓
renderReference template:
  data-sly-test="${model.referenceParagraphPath && model.valid}"
  data-sly-resource="${model.referenceParagraphPath}"
  → same-request Sling resource include; the referenced resource renders in place,
    inheriting the current request's wcmmode etc.

EDS fragment:
Author enters/selects a page path (as a link or plain text starting with "/")
         ↓
fragment.js decorate():
  loadFragment(path) → fetch(`${path}.plain.html`) → parse into a synthetic <main>,
  rewrite any "./media_"-relative image/source URLs to resolve against the fragment's
  own path (not the including page's path), then decorateMain() + loadSections()
         ↓
  the fragment's first <section>'s classes are merged onto the including block's
  own .section, and the block element itself is replaced by the fragment's child nodes
  — i.e. the fragment becomes indistinguishable from native content on the page

Sling Model — ReferenceModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class, SlingHttpServletRequest.class},
       defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class ReferenceModel {

    @Inject
    private ResourceResolver resourceResolver;

    @Inject
    @ValueMapValue
    private String referenceParagraphPath;

    public String getReferenceParagraphPath() {
        Resource resource = resourceResolver.getResource(referenceParagraphPath);
        if (resource != null) {
            String path = resource.toString();
            if (path.equals(referenceParagraphPath)) {
                return null;
            }
        }
        return referenceParagraphPath;
    }

    public boolean getValid() {
        Resource resource = resourceResolver.getResource(referenceParagraphPath);
        return resource != null;
    }
}

Note the odd getReferenceParagraphPath() logic: it resolves the resource and compares its toString() (which for a JCR-backed resource is typically the resource's own path) against the configured path, returning null if they're equal — a defensive check whose practical effect for a genuinely-resolvable path is close to a no-op, since a resolved resource's path normally does equal the requested path.

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
ReferencereferenceParagraphPathpathbrowserallowpagepath="{Boolean}false", predicate="nosystem", rootPath="/content" — restricted to non-page, non-system content (i.e. paragraphs/components), page-level references are explicitly disallowed

Single-field dialog, no tabs — the simplest dialog in this batch alongside forms-embed.

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-use.model="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.ReferenceModel"
     data-sly-call="${renderReference @ model=model}" />

<template data-sly-template.renderReference="${ @ model }">
    <sly data-sly-test="${model.referenceParagraphPath && model.valid}"
         data-sly-resource="${model.referenceParagraphPath}"/>
</template>

CSS

None — the component renders no wrapper markup of its own; whatever is referenced brings its own styling. There is no ui/admp-reference.css or clientlib CSS category in the reviewed folder.

Important Constraints (source system)

  • Page-level references are explicitly disallowed (allowpagepath="{Boolean}false") — this component can only reference a paragraph/component resource, not include a whole page, unlike the EDS block below which references a page path.
  • The include is server-side and same-request — there is no cross-page caching concern or extra network round trip, but it also means the referenced content is always rendered fresh with the including page's own render context (wcmmode, etc.), which can leak edit-mode-only markup from the referenced resource if it has any wcmmode.edit branches of its own.
  • allowedParents="[*/parsys]" — can only be placed inside a parsys, not just anywhere in the JCR tree.

Fragment Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

Fully implemented at blocks/fragment/, marked "Reuse As-Is" — this is the standard, largely-unmodified block-collection fragment block (its own file header cites the public block-collection documentation URL directly), used elsewhere in this codebase as a dependency by other blocks (the footer block, documented above, imports loadFragment from this block to load the footer content fragment).

File Structure

blocks/fragment/
├── fragment.js
├── fragment.css
├── _fragment.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/   (fragment.css, _fragment.css, block-config.js)
├── botox/
├── rinvoq/
├── roy/
│   └── themes/bright/ (fragment.css, _fragment.css)
└── themes/bright/     (fragment.css, _fragment.css — base-level theme variant)

Authoring Model (_fragment.json)

{
  "definitions": [
    {
      "title": "Fragment",
      "id": "fragment",
      "plugins": {
        "xwalk": {
          "page": {
            "resourceType": "core/franklin/components/block/v1/block",
            "template": { "name": "Fragment", "model": "fragment" }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  ],
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "fragment",
      "fields": [
        { "component": "aem-content", "name": "reference", "label": "Reference" }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "filters": []
}

Single field, mirroring the source's single-field dialog — but here it references a whole page path (the EDS "page," not a JCR paragraph resource), which the source's admp-reference explicitly disallows.

How the JS works (fragment.js)

export async function loadFragment(path) {
  if (path && path.startsWith('/')) {
    const resp = await fetch(`${path}.plain.html`);
    if (resp.ok) {
      const main = document.createElement('main');
      main.innerHTML = await resp.text();

      const resetAttributeBase = (tag, attr) => {
        main.querySelectorAll(`${tag}[${attr}^="./media_"]`).forEach((elem) => {
          elem[attr] = new URL(elem.getAttribute(attr), new URL(path, window.location)).href;
        });
      };
      resetAttributeBase('img', 'src');
      resetAttributeBase('source', 'srcset');

      decorateMain(main);
      await loadSections(main);
      return main;
    }
  }
  return null;
}

export default async function decorate(block) {
  const link = block.querySelector('a');
  const path = link ? link.getAttribute('href') : block.textContent.trim();
  const fragment = await loadFragment(path);
  if (fragment) {
    const fragmentSection = fragment.querySelector(':scope .section');
    if (fragmentSection) {
      block.closest('.section').classList.add(...fragmentSection.classList);
      moveInstrumentation(block, block.parentElement);
      block.closest('.fragment').replaceWith(...fragment.childNodes);
    }
  }
}

The relative-media-URL rewrite (./media_* → absolute, resolved against the fragment's own path) is necessary because the fetched .plain.html retains image paths relative to the fragment page, not the including page — without this rewrite, images would 404 once the fragment's markup is spliced into a different page's DOM.

CSS

Not reviewed in full-file depth for this section; carries the same base + abbvie/botox/rinvoq/roy brand structure, plus base-level and roy-level themes/bright variants — the same double-brand/theme depth seen on the footer block, consistent with both blocks originating from the same block-collection lineage.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (admp-reference)EDS (fragment)
Include mechanismServer-side, same-request Sling resource include (data-sly-resource) — no network round tripClient-side fetch of the target page's rendered .plain.html — an additional network request per fragment
Reference granularityA paragraph/component resource path; whole pages explicitly disallowedA whole page path; there is no equivalent to referencing a single paragraph within a page
Validity checkReferenceModel.getValid() resolves the resource server-side before renderingImplicit — a failed/non-OK fetch response simply results in loadFragment returning null and nothing being rendered; no distinct "invalid path" UI state
Media URL handlingNot applicable — included resource renders in the same page context, so relative URLs already resolve correctlyExplicit rewrite required for ./media_* paths, since the fetched fragment DOM is relocated into a different page's URL context
Placement restrictionallowedParents="[*/parsys]" — JCR-level structural restrictionNo structural placement restriction found in the block's own files; governed by whatever page/section authoring rules apply generally in Universal Editor

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
referenceParagraphPathpathbrowser (paragraph/component only, no page paths, rootPath=/content)referenceRenamedSame purpose (path to included content) and same field shape (a content picker), but scope widens from "a paragraph/component resource" to "a whole page," since the EDS block always fetches a page's .plain.html rather than including an arbitrary sub-resource

Header — Technical Details

What it does

Renders the site-wide, sticky header/navigation bar. Two source-system components exist for this: header (the original) and header-v2 (a forked, accessibility- and compliance-enhanced variant). Both dispatch to a "classic" or "lite" markup variant, render a utility navigation bar, brand logo, primary navigation, login/logout state, and an optional CTA slot. The target implementation is fragment-driven: the block loads a navigation document fragment at render time and derives all nav structure, mega-menu content, eyebrow bars, floating ISI (Important Safety Information) bar, and CTA group from richtext content authored inside that fragment, rather than from dialog fields on the block itself.

Architecture — source system (header vs header-v2)

Both header and header-v2 use an identical top-level dispatch pattern:

<sly data-sly-use.headerHelper="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.components.HeaderContainerHelper"/>
<sly data-sly-include="header-${headerHelper.headerType}.html"></sly>

headerType (dialog select: "classic" | "lite") picks between:
  header-classic.html   (full nav, utility bar, mega-menu-capable)
  header-lite.html      (reduced nav, used on lighter page templates)

header-v2 is not a near-duplicate copy in the trivial sense — it adds genuine functionality: a "Skip to Main Content" link, an eyebrow.html include, ARIA role="menubar" / role="none" / role="menu", a <nav aria-label="Utility Navigation"> wrapper, a Coveo-powered search-suggestions mode (searchboxSelector), and floating-isi.html / header-floating-safety.html (the compliance safety/ISI bar) — none of which exist in the original header. However it is implemented as a forked duplicate of the base component's HTL/markup (both declare sling:resourceSuperType="foundation/components/parbase", neither extends the other), not as a composition/inheritance relationship — the bulk of login/logout link rendering and menu structure is duplicated verbatim between the two, with only class-name renames (abbv-header-* → abbv-header-v2-*) and minor analytics-field refinements. Any future bugfix to the shared login/logout/menu logic has to be applied in both places by hand.

Sling Model / Java helper — HeaderContainerHelper.java

Both variants use the same WCMUsePojo helper to resolve header type, sticky-scroll anchor position, indication-swap state, menu item counts, and (header-v2 only, via the same class) Coveo query-suggestion enablement:

public class HeaderContainerHelper extends WCMUsePojo {
    private String headerType;
    private List<String> menuItems;
    private List<String> navItems;
    private String stickyAnchorPosition;
    private String indicationOn;
    private String coveoQuerySuggestionsEnabled;

    @Override
    public void activate() throws Exception {
        headerType = getProperties().get("headerType", String.class);
        String stickyType = getProperties().get("stickyScrollType", String.class);

        InheritanceValueMap inheritedProperties =
            new HierarchyNodeInheritanceValueMap(getCurrentPage().getContentResource());
        indicationOn = PageUtility.getIndicationAwareness(inheritedProperties);
        String adminConfigPath = inheritedProperties.getInherited("adminConfigPath", String.class);
        Page siteLevelCoveoConfig = PageUtility.getSiteLevelCoveoConfig(adminConfigPath, getResourceResolver());
        if (siteLevelCoveoConfig != null) {
            coveoQuerySuggestionsEnabled =
                FormsConfigUtil.getTitleByValue(siteLevelCoveoConfig, "coveo.querySuggestionsEnabled");
        }

        if (StringUtils.isNotEmpty(stickyType)) {
            if (stickyType.equals("default") || stickyType.equals("default-delayed")) {
                stickyAnchorPosition = "top";
            } else if (stickyType.equals("scroll-display")) {
                stickyAnchorPosition = "bottom";
            } else {
                stickyAnchorPosition = "";
            }
        }

        Integer utilityNavCount = getProperties().get("utilityNavMenuItems", Integer.class);
        // ... builds "menuitem0..N" and "navitem0..N" placeholder id lists for dialog-authored counts
    }
