    public String getHeaderType() { return headerType != null ? headerType : "classic"; }
    // getMenuItems(), getNavItems(), getStickyAnchorPosition(), getIndicationOn(),
    // getCoveoQuerySuggestionsEnabled() ...
}

Login/logout link rendering in both HTL templates additionally uses com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.LoginModel (guest/logged-in link beans) and com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.LinkChecker (URL formatting) — these are shared, not header-specific, models.

Dialog — header (v1)

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Select Header TypeheaderTypeselect"classic" / "lite"
Utility Navigation ItemsutilityNavMenuItemsnumberfieldcount-driven placeholder list, min 0
Primary Navigation ItemsprimaryNavMenuItemsnumberfieldcount-driven placeholder list, min 0
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextfield
Anchor IDanchorIDtextfield
Enable Login LinkenableLoginLinkcheckbox
Enable Call to ActionenableCtacheckboxshows a nested cta component resource
Guest Links: Custom CSS ClassesguestCustomCssClassestextfieldGuest Links tab
Login Link TextloginLinkTexttextfield
Login Open/Close Link IconloginOpenLinkIcon / loginCloseLinkIconselect (datasource ce-link-icon)
Add More Links (guest)addGuestMoreLinks multifieldfieldset multifieldper-link: addAbbvLoginClass, linkTitle, pagePath, modalID, linkIcon/linkIconPosition, targetType, linkType, plus full analytics field set (componentTitle, componentPosition, contentJourney, patientJourney, messageBucket, analyticsEnableMVATier, mvaType, analyticsMVATier, analyticsMVATierValue)
Logged In Links: Custom CSS ClassesloggedInCustomCssClassestextfieldLogged In Links tab
Welcome TextwelcomeTexttextfieldsupports {{firstName}}/{{lastName}} tokens
Logged In Open/Close Link IconlogoutOpenLinkIcon / logoutCloseLinkIconselect
Add More Links (logged in)addLoggedInMoreLinks multifieldfieldset multifieldsame shape as guest, plus enableLogoutLink checkbox
Logo Centred in MobilecenteredLogoMobilecheckboxCustomization tab
Sticky Scroll TypestickyScrollTypeselectnone / default / default-delayed / scroll-display, required

Nested/allowed components (both header and header-v2): menu/menu.html (its own dialog), headerlogo, link-list (v1 classic only), cta, and a utility-navigation resource type per menu item.

Dialog — header-v2 (additions/changes over v1)

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Skip to Main Content TextskipToMainContenttextfieldnet-new — overrides the default "Skip to Main content" accessibility link text
Section Title for AnalyticssectionTitletextfieldnet-new, per Guest Links / Logged In Links sections
Localized Section Title for Analyticsl10sectionTitletextfieldnet-new, required for non-English sites only
MVA Primary / MVA Title / MVA Type and Category / MVA SecondarymvaPrimary, mvaTitle, mvaTypeAndCategory, mvaSecondaryselect/textreplaces v1's looser analyticsEnableMVATier/analyticsMVATier naming with a refined MVA field set
Enable Floating ISIenableFloatingIsicheckboxnet-new — turns on the compliance safety bar (floating-isi.html), gated additionally on stickyScrollType != 'none'

HTL — key differences (header-classic.html)

// header/header-classic.html (v1) — no skip link, no ARIA menubar roles
<header class="abbv-header ${properties.customCssClasses} ${stickyClass} ...">
  <div class="abbv-header-utility-navigation abbv-navigation ">
      <ul data-sly-list.menuItem="${headerHelper.menuItems}">
          <li data-sly-resource="${menuItem @ resourceType='.../utility-navigation'}"></li>
      </ul>
  </div>
  ...

// header-v2/header-classic.html (v2) — skip link + eyebrow + ARIA roles + ISI include
<sly data-sly-include="floating-isi.html"></sly>
<sly data-sly-test.searchboxSelector =
    "${headerHelper.coveoQuerySuggestionsEnabled == 'true' ? 'search-box-coveo': 'search-box-classic'}"></sly>
<header data-sly-attribute.id="${properties.anchorID}"
    class="abbv-header-v2 ${properties.customCssClasses} ${stickyClass} ... ${searchboxSelector}" ...>
  <a href="#main-content" class="abbv-skip-to-main-content sr-only">${properties.skipToMainContent ? properties.skipToMainContent : 'Skip to Main content'}</a>
  <sly data-sly-include="eyebrow.html"></sly>
  <div class="abbv-header-v2-utility-navigation abbv-navigation">
    <nav aria-label="Utility Navigation">
      <ul role="menubar" data-sly-list.menuItem="${headerHelper.menuItems}">
        <li role="none" data-sly-resource="${menuItem @ resourceType='.../utility-navigation'}"></li>
      </ul>
    </nav>
  </div>
  ...
// header-v2/floating-isi.html — the compliance safety bar
<sly data-sly-test="${properties.enableFloatingIsi && (properties.stickyScrollType != 'none')}">
    <div class="abbv-floating-isi abbv-floating-isi-v1">
        <div class="abbv-content-top">
            <div class="abbv-content-container">${properties.floatingisitext @context='html'}</div>
            <span class="abbv-icon-add i-b icon" tabindex="0" aria-label="Expand Safety Information"></span>
        </div>
    </div>
</sly>

header-v2 also calls a different anchor-template macro for login/logout (headerAnchorWithOpenAndCloseIcon vs v1's anchorWithOpenAndCloseIcon), and drops the singleton per-link MVA-tier sly tests v1 used inline, in favor of the refined MVA field set.

CSS / JS (source system)

header ships header.v1/common-elements.v1 clientlibs (header.js, header.css). header-v2 ships its own headerv2.v1/common-elements.v1 clientlib (header-v2.js, header-v2.css) plus an extra author-mode-only clientlib, header-authoring (dropdownshowhide.js), not present in v1. header.js is explicitly marked deprecated in its own header comment (// AbbVie HeaderV1 Component (deprecated)) and handles sticky-scroll class toggling, indication-swap AJAX content replacement, login-state cookie reads (admp_login_token, admp_ufln), and mobile primary-nav anchor-scroll handling.

Important Constraints (source system)

  • header-v2 is a copy-paste fork of header, not an extension via sling:resourceSuperType — shared bugfixes must be applied twice.
  • Coveo query-suggestion mode and the floating ISI bar exist only in header-v2; sites still on header cannot get compliance-required safety-bar behavior without migrating components.
  • header.js is self-documented as deprecated but still shipped/loaded on sites using the v1 component.

Header Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/header/. Unlike the source components, it has no field-authored dialog and no _header.json Universal Editor model at all — it is fragment-driven. The block reads a navigation path from page metadata, fetches that fragment's HTML, and derives every piece of UI (utility nav, brand/logo, primary nav with mega-menu, eyebrow bars, floating ISI, CTA group, search) from fixed positional parsing of that fragment's authored richtext content. This is a deliberate architecture choice in the target implementation, not a gap — there is no direct AEM dialog-field equivalent to map.

File Structure

blocks/header/
├── header.js
├── header.css
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── header.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
│   ├── header.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── linzess/
├── mavyret/
├── rinvoq/
├── rinvoq-hcp/
├── skyrizi-hcp/
├── venclexta/
└── themes/bright/
    └── header.css

No _header.json exists in the repository — confirmed also against the parsed field-model catalog (models-normalized.json), which has no entry keyed to header.

How the JS works (header.js)

The decorate() function loads a navigation document fragment (path from getMetadata('nav'), default /nav) via loadFragment(), then walks its .navigation-content-container looking for .navigation-content[data-type="..."] blocks — each a differently-typed authored row (utility-nav, logo, navigation-content, language-links, search, eyebrow, floating-isi, cta-group). Content is positionally parsed out of packed <p>/<ul> children because EDS flattens authored richtext fields into ordered sibling elements rather than named JSON properties:

function buildFloatingIsi(headerEl) {
  const isiBlock = headerEl.querySelector('.navigation-content[data-type="floating-isi"]');
  if (!isiBlock) return null;
  // Fixed positions in row 0's inner cell (model field order — same in UE and production):
  //   p[0]      = searchIcon (template default)
  //   p[1]      = eyebrowPosition ("top" — template default)
  //   p[2..n-3] = floatingIsiText (authored ISI text)
  //   p[n-2]    = floatingIsiExpandLabel (authored or default "See Full ISI")
  //   p[n-1]    = floatingIsiCollapseLabel (authored or default "Collapse ISI")
  const cell = isiBlock.querySelector(':scope > div > div');
  const paras = [...(cell?.querySelectorAll(':scope > p') || [])];
  ...
}

Other notable behaviors: buildMegaMenu() assembles a two-card mega-menu panel (primary card + a secondary card fetched live via fetchDashboardCardData() against a content-fragment GraphQL-style endpoint); buildLevelTwoNavigations() resolves second-level nav either from a parsed <ul> (language-links case) or from a cached flat site index lookup (IndexUtils.findIndexItem()) keyed by the clicked link's path, with results memoized in a module-level navigationCache Map; a scroll handler (handleScroll, throttled via requestAnimationFrame) hides/shows the header based on scroll direction and a threshold derived from the page's hero block height; wireHcpModalLinks() intercepts any a.hcp-modal/[data-hcp-modal] link to show a native <dialog> "leave site" warning before opening in a new tab.

CSS

:root {
  --nav-logo-size-desk: 11rem;
  --nav-logo-size-mobile: 8.8rem;
  --nav-height: 88px;
  --nav-height-mobile: 72px;
  --nav-sections-bottom-gap: 17.6rem;

  /* Utility nav — overridable per brand */
  --nav-utility-bg: var(--color-gray-light);
  --nav-utility-text: var(--color-charcoal);
  ...
  /* Active/hover indicator — overridable per brand (e.g., yellow for RINVOQ HCP) */
  --nav-active-indicator: var(--text-color);
  --nav-active-bg: transparent;
  ...
}

Base header.css defines the full custom-property contract (logo sizing, utility bar colors, dropdown shadows, mega-menu card heights, hamburger geometry). Each brand folder (abbvie/, botox/, linzess/, mavyret/, rinvoq/, rinvoq-hcp/, skyrizi-hcp/, venclexta/) supplies its own header.css that redeclares the same :root block (often with identical base values, e.g. Brand A's override repeats --nav-height: 88px) plus brand-specific layout rules such as the CSS grid template (grid-template: 'brand tools hamburger' var(--nav-height) 'sections sections sections' / 1fr auto auto;). This is the same override pattern as other multi-brand blocks in the target implementation: base file supplies structure/behavior, brand file supplies token values and visual variants.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (header / header-v2)EDS (header)
Authoring modelDialog fields + nested resource components (headerlogo, link-list, cta, utility-navigation) per instanceNo dialog/UE field model — single navigation document fragment, positionally parsed
Variant handlingTwo forked components (header vs header-v2) with duplicated HTLSingle JS/CSS file, brand differences isolated to CSS folders + block-config.js
Menu item countsAuthor-specified counts (utilityNavMenuItems/primaryNavMenuItems) drive placeholder loopsMenu items are however many are authored in the fragment — no count field
Compliance safety barOnly in header-v2, gated by enableFloatingIsi checkbox + sticky typePresent in single implementation, gated by presence of a data-type="floating-isi" fragment row
SearchCoveo query-suggestions mode toggled by a site-level admin config lookup (header-v2 only)Plain GET-form search with client-side non-empty validation; no Coveo suggestions integration observed
Login/logout stateCookie-driven (admp_login_token, admp_ufln) via deprecated header.jsNo login/logout state handling observed in header.js — guest/user CTA split exists in buildCtaGroup() but is structural, not cookie-driven

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
headerTypeselect—Not mappedNo per-instance dialog exists in EDS; classic/lite distinction is now a CSS class (lite) on the block, detected from block classList, not authored per instance the same way
utilityNavMenuItems / primaryNavMenuItemsnumberfield—Not mappedEDS derives item count directly from however many rows exist in the fragment; no count field needed
customCssClassestextfield—Not mappedNo equivalent per-instance custom-class field; styling variation handled via brand CSS folders and block-level modifier classes (e.g. lite, sticky)
anchorIDtextfield—Not mappedNo equivalent authored anchor id on the EDS header block
enableLoginLinkcheckbox—Not mappedNo cookie-driven login/logout state machine found in header.js; closest structural analog is the guest/user split in buildCtaGroup(), driven by content presence, not a toggle
enableCtacheckboxcontent_ctaPrimaryLink presence (fragment row)RenamedSame purpose (show a CTA), now expressed as content presence in the nav fragment rather than a dialog checkbox
guestCustomCssClasses / loggedInCustomCssClassestextfield—Not mappedNo equivalent; guest/user CTA wrappers get fixed classes nav-cta-guest/nav-cta-user
loginLinkText / welcomeTexttextfield—Not mappedNo cookie-based welcome-text templating ({{firstName}}/{{lastName}}) found in the EDS implementation
loginOpenLinkIcon / loginCloseLinkIcon / logoutOpenLinkIcon / logoutCloseLinkIconselect (datasource)—Not mappedIcon picker datasource pattern (/bin/abbvie/admpGenericDataSource) has no EDS equivalent; icons in EDS come from inline SVG spans embedded in authored content
addGuestMoreLinks / addLoggedInMoreLinks multifieldsfieldset multifieldcontent_ctaGuestLinks / content_ctaUserLinks (richtext, positional)RenamedSame purpose (list of extra links per auth state) but authored as inline richtext paragraphs in the nav fragment rather than a structured multifield; analytics sub-fields (MVA, journey, message bucket) have no EDS equivalent — see below
componentTitle / componentPosition / contentJourney / patientJourney / messageBucket / analyticsEnableMVATier / mvaType / analyticsMVATier / analyticsMVATierValuetext/select (analytics)—Not mappedNo analytics-tagging metadata fields found anywhere in the EDS header block or its JS — this entire analytics taxonomy is absent from the target implementation
enableLogoutLinkcheckbox—Not mappedNo logout-specific toggle; see enableLoginLink note above
centeredLogoMobilecheckbox—Not mappedNo equivalent found; logo layout is fixed by CSS grid, not an authoring toggle
stickyScrollTypeselect (required)sticky block modifier class / stickyheadercheckbox fragment rowRenamedSame purpose (control sticky/scroll-hide behavior), reimplemented as a block CSS class plus a "has-sticky-scrollback" feature flag read from either a fragment row or page metadata, rather than a 4-way select
skipToMainContent (header-v2 only)textfield—Not mappedNo "Skip to Main Content" link found in the EDS header.js/CSS — an accessibility feature present in header-v2 that has not been carried into the target implementation
sectionTitle / l10sectionTitle / mvaPrimary / mvaTitle / mvaTypeAndCategory / mvaSecondary (header-v2 only)text/select (analytics)—Not mappedheader-v2's refined MVA/analytics fields have no EDS equivalent, consistent with the broader analytics-field gap noted above
enableFloatingIsi (header-v2 only)checkboxdata-type="floating-isi" fragment row presenceRenamedSame purpose (toggle the compliance safety bar) but now expressed as presence of an authored fragment row (buildFloatingIsi()) instead of a dialog checkbox + sticky-type gate

Background Container / Hero — Technical Details

What it does

The source-system component, background-container, is a generic wrapper that lets an author drop any children into it while configuring a background (solid color, gradient, single scaling image with optional parallax, a swap set of small/medium/large images at breakpoints, or a looping/non-looping video), plus an optional text panel and CTA overlaid on top. Page hero banners in the source system were built by composing this generic container with a text panel and CTA rather than by a purpose-built "hero" component. The target implementation's hero block is a purpose-built, single-block equivalent: authors fill in a fixed content model (background image + optional distinct mobile image, eyebrow, heading/body richtext, optional video, optional badge/layers image, optional caption/indication banner) and the JS assembles the same visual outcome — including the breakpoint-based mobile/desktop image swap the manifest calls out, and a further per-variant override (the "editorial hero" pattern) that moves that swap to the desktop breakpoint instead of the default tablet one.

Architecture — source system (background-container)

Author sets backgroundType = color | gradient | image | video (dialog select)
         ↓
background-container.html branches on backgroundType and (for image) imageType:
  color              → backgroundTypesTemplate.html # bgColor.color
  gradient           → backgroundTypesTemplate.html # bgGradient.gradient
  image + scaling, no parallax  → # bgImageWithoutParallax.scaleImageWithoutParallax
  image + scaling, parallax     → # bgImageWithParallax.scaleImageWithParallax
  image + swapping   → inline <style> media queries picking small/medium/large
                        image path by viewport width, then # bgSwapImage.swapImage
  video (loop/no-loop)          → # bgVideoWithoutLoop / (loop variant)
         ↓
Text panel + CTA (if authored) render inside the same container, positioned/colored
per textpanel dialog fields (contentposition, textPanelColor, transparency, takeover,
containerWidth)

The breakpoint-based swap is implemented as literal inline <style> media queries emitted per-instance in the HTL, keyed off a per-instance id token:

@media (min-width: 985px)
{ #${bgModel.id @ context='styleToken'} .abbv-background-container-display {
    background-image : url("${bgModel.largeImagePath @context='uri'}");
} }
@media (min-width: 601px) and (max-width: 984px)
{ #${bgModel.id @ context='styleToken'} .abbv-background-container-display{
    background-image : url("${bgModel.mediumImagePath @context='uri'}");
} }
@media (max-width: 600px)
{  #${bgModel.id @ context='styleToken'} .abbv-background-container-display {
    background-image : url("${bgModel.smallImagePath @context='uri'}");
} }

Sling Model — BackgroundContainer.java

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class BackgroundContainer extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENT_ID_PREFIX = "background-container";

    @Inject @Named("borderTop") @Optional private boolean borderTop;
    @Inject @Named("borderBottom") @Optional private boolean borderBottom;
    @Inject @Named("borderWidth") @Optional private String borderWidth;
    @Inject @Named("borderColor") @Optional private String borderColor;
    @Inject @Named("customCssClasses") @Optional private String customCssClasses;
    @Inject @Named("customInlineStyle") @Optional private String customInlineStyle;
    @Inject @Named("anchorid") @Optional private String anchorid;
    @Inject @Named("backgroundType") @Optional private BackgroundType backgroundType;
    @Inject @Named("bgColor") @Optional private String bgColor;
    @Inject @Named("gradientColor") @Optional private String gradientColor;
    @Inject @Named("imageType") @Optional private String imageType;
    @Inject @Named("parallaxEffect") @Optional private boolean parallaxEffect;
    @Inject @Named("videoUrl") @Optional private String videoUrl;
    @Inject @Named("videoLoop") @Optional private boolean videoLoop;
    @Inject @Named("posterFileReference") @Optional private String posterFileReference;
    @Inject @Named("imageAltText") @Optional private String imageAltText;
    @Inject @Named("imageTitle") @Optional private String imageTitle;
    // ... additional fields for large/medium/small image renditions and paths
}

Extends a shared AbstractComponentModel base (component-id generation), and works together with a separate ImageComponent use-class (com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.components.ImageComponent) for scaling-image rendition resolution.

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Top Border / Bottom BorderborderTop / borderBottomcheckboxOverview tab
Border WidthborderWidthselect (datasource ce-border-width)
Border ColorborderColorselect (datasource ce-border-colors)
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextfield
Anchor IDanchorIDtextfield
Background TypebackgroundTypeselect (show/hide)color / gradient / image / video, required, default "color"
Background ColorbgColorselect (datasource ce-background-colors)shown when type=color
Gradient ColorgradientColorselect (datasource ce-gradient-colors)shown when type=gradient
Image TypeimageTypeselectscaling / swapping, shown when type=image
Parallax EffectparallaxEffectcheckboxfieldset, shown when type=image
Video Reference UrlvideoUrlpathbrowser (DAM)shown when type=video
LoopvideoLoopcheckboxVideo Loop Container, shown when type=video
Large Imagelarge/file, large/fileReferencefileuploadLarge Image tab
Large Image RenditionlargeImageRenditionselect (datasource ce-edam-renditions-list)
Medium Image / Renditionmedium/file, mediumImageRenditionfileupload / selectMedium Image tab, shown when imageType=swapping
Small Image / Renditionsmall/file, smallImageRenditionfileupload / selectSmall Image tab, shown when imageType=swapping
Video PostervideoPoster, posterFileReference, posterImageRenditionfileupload / select
Image Title / Image Alt TextimageTitle / imageAltTexttextfieldVideo Poster fieldset
Descriptiondescriptionrich text (includewidget)Text Panel tab
Mobile Content Positionsmobilecontentpositionsselect"stays in image" vs "falls below image"
Content Positioncontentpositionselect (datasource ce-content-positions)
Background Color (text panel)textPanelColorselect (datasource ce-background-colors-rgb-form)
Transparencytransparencynumberfield (0-10)
Select for Takeovertakeovercheckbox
Text Container WidthcontainerWidthselect (datasource ce-bg-container-text-panel-width)
Link Title / Link / Modal ID / Link Icon / Link Icon Position / Target Type / Link TypelinkTitle, pagePath, modalID, linkIcon, linkIconPosition, targetType, linkTypevariousCTA tab
Title (Analytics) / Component Position / Content Journey / Patient Journey / Message Bucket / MVA Tier fieldscomponentTitle, componentPosition, contentJourney, patientJourney, messageBucket, analyticsEnableMVATier, mvaType, analyticsMVATier, analyticsMVATierValuevarious (analytics)CTA tab

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-use.bgModel="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.BackgroundContainer">
<sly data-sly-use.imgHelper="${'com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.components.ImageComponent' @ currentNodePath=currentNode.path}"></sly>
<sly data-sly-test.backgroundType = "${properties.backgroundType}"></sly>
<a data-sly-test="${properties.anchorID}" id="${properties.anchorID}" aria-hidden="true"></a>

<sly data-sly-test="${backgroundType == 'image' && bgModel.imageType == 'swapping'}">
  <style type='text/css'>
    @media (min-width: 985px) { ... url("${bgModel.largeImagePath}") ... }
    @media (min-width: 601px) and (max-width: 984px) { ... url("${bgModel.mediumImagePath}") ... }
    @media (max-width: 600px) { ... url("${bgModel.smallImagePath}") ... }
    ${properties.customInlineStyle @context="unsafe"}
  </style>
  <div data-sly-use.bgSwapImage="backgroundTypesTemplate.html">
    <sly data-sly-call="${bgSwapImage.swapImage @ bgModel=bgModel}"/>
  </div>
</sly>

CSS / Clientlib

background-container.v1/common-elements.v1 clientlib ships background-container.css. Border classes are generated in Java as abbv-border-{0}-width-{1} (border color + width combined into a single class token), applied conditionally per borderTop/borderBottom.

Important Constraints (source system)

  • properties.customInlineStyle @context="unsafe" is injected raw into a <style> block — author-controlled CSS injection with no sanitization beyond the "unsafe" context marker itself being a deliberate escape hatch; a real XSS/CSS-injection risk if the field is exposed to untrusted authors.
  • The swap-image breakpoints (600px / 601–984px / 985px+) are hardcoded in the HTL, not authorable — any brand needing different breakpoints must fork the component.
  • Parallax and swap-image are mutually exclusive image sub-modes (imageType: scaling vs swapping) — parallax only applies to the scaling path.

Hero Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/hero/. It replaces the generic background-container + text-panel + CTA composition with a single fixed content model and a JS decorator that classifies block rows by content shape (which row has a heading, which has only plain text, which has two <picture> elements, etc.) rather than by named dialog fields.

File Structure

blocks/hero/
├── hero.js
├── hero.css
├── _hero.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── hero.css
│   ├── _hero.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
├── linzess/
├── mavyret/
├── rinvoq/
├── rinvoq-hcp/
├── skyrizi-hcp/
├── venclexta/
├── roy/
│   └── themes/bright/
└── themes/bright/

Authoring Model (_hero.json)

{
  "component": "multiselect",
  "name": "classes",
  "label": "Style",
  "options": [
    { "name": "Profile", "value": "profile" },
    { "name": "Landing", "value": "landing" },
    { "name": "Full Height", "value": "full" },
    { "name": "Compact", "value": "compact" },
    { "name": "Split Layout", "value": "split" },
    { "name": "Centered", "value": "centered" },
    { "name": "Video Background", "value": "video" },
    { "name": "Solid Background", "value": "solid" },
    { "name": "Stat Overlay", "value": "stat-overlay" },
    { "name": "Multilayer", "value": "multilayer" },
    { "name": "Dual CTA", "value": "dual-cta" },
    { "name": "Height Tall", "value": "height-tall" },
    { "name": "Light Font", "value": "light-font" },
    { "name": "No Padding", "value": "no-padding" }
  ]
},
{ "component": "reference", "name": "image", "label": "Background Image" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "imageAlt", "label": "Image Alt" },
{ "component": "reference", "name": "mobileImage", "label": "Mobile Image (optional — different crop for < 744px)" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "mobileImageAlt", "label": "Mobile Image Alt" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "eyebrow", "label": "Eyebrow Text" },
{ "component": "richtext", "name": "indication", "label": "Indication Text (optional — renders as dark box at top of hero, e.g. ISI header)" },
{ "component": "richtext", "name": "text", "label": "Heading & Body Text" },
{ "component": "reference", "name": "layers", "label": "Badge / Layers Image" },
{ "component": "reference", "name": "video", "label": "Background Video (Video variant — select a DAM video asset, e.g. .mp4)" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "imageCaption", "label": "Image Caption (e.g. Actor portrayal.) — appears bottom-right over the image" },
{ "component": "select", "name": "classes_textAlign", "label": "Text Alignment", "options": [...] },
{ "component": "select", "name": "classes_textColor", "label": "Text Color Override", "options": [...] },
{ "component": "text", "name": "classes_customClass", "label": "Custom Class" }

How the JS works (hero.js)

extractRows() classifies the block's authored rows purely by DOM shape (no named fields exist at runtime — EDS flattens the model into ordered block rows): the video row is whichever row's link resolves to a video-like href; the image row is always row 0; the text row is the first row (excluding video) containing a heading or a link; the mobile image row is any row before the text row containing a picture/img; the indication row is richtext with no heading/link/image; the caption row is plain text with no markup at all.

const HERO_IMAGE_SWAP_BREAKPOINT = 985;
const HERO_IMAGE_SWAP_BREAKPOINT_DESKTOP = 1024;
const LINZESS_EDITORIAL_HERO_CLASS = 'linzess-behind-nav-linzess-editorial-hero';

function mergeMobileImage(imageCell, mobileImageRow, swapMinWidth = HERO_IMAGE_SWAP_BREAKPOINT) {
  ...
  const combined = document.createElement('picture');
  const source = document.createElement('source');
  source.media = `(min-width: ${swapMinWidth}px)`;
  source.srcset = desktopImg.src;
  combined.appendChild(source);
  combined.appendChild(mobileImg.cloneNode(true));
  desktopPicture.replaceWith(combined);
  ...
}

This is the direct target-implementation analog of the source system's hardcoded three-tier media-query swap: instead of small/medium/large images and 600/985px breakpoints baked into inline <style>, it produces a native <picture>/<source> pair with a single configurable swap width, defaulting to 985px but overridden to 1024px for any block carrying an editorial-hero-suffixed variant class (the Brand A "editorial hero" case named in the manifest). The video variant (initVideo()) resolves an absolute publish URL from project config outside Universal Editor mode, and bails out (removing the row) rather than guessing a URL if no config value is present. The multilayer variant (initMultilayer()) listens for a custom abbv:buddy:stateChange DOM event to toggle which layered image is active, using a WeakMap-tracked AbortController per image cell to avoid listener accumulation across multiple hero instances.

CSS

.section.abbvie-container.hero-container {
  max-width: none;
  width: 100%;
  background: transparent;
  margin: var(--spacing-00);
}

/* Navy-overlap hero: pull wrapper into section padding so bg image shows */
.hero-navy {
  padding-bottom: var(--spacing-00);
  margin-bottom: var(--spacing-32);
  background-color: var(--color-white);
}

.hero-navy .hero-wrapper {
  margin: var(--spacing-n40) -3.5rem var(--spacing-00);
  max-width: none;
  overflow: hidden;
}

Base hero.css plus per-brand hero.css/_hero.css overrides in each brand folder (abbvie/, botox/, linzess/, mavyret/, rinvoq/, rinvoq-hcp/, skyrizi-hcp/, venclexta/, roy/), plus a shared themes/bright/ theme override folder used by more than one brand (also present under roy/themes/bright/) — indicating the theme layer sits alongside, not strictly nested inside, the brand layer.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (background-container)EDS (hero)
ScopeGeneric reusable background wrapper for any content (not hero-specific)Purpose-built hero block; not reused for other backgrounds
Background type selectionExplicit dialog select: color / gradient / image / videoInferred from which optional fields are populated (image, video, layers) — classes multiselect adds layout/style variants instead
Responsive image swapHardcoded 3-tier (small/medium/large) inline media queries, fixed 600px/985px breakpoints2-tier <picture>/<source> swap, single configurable breakpoint (985px default, 1024px for editorial-hero variant)
Text panelSeparate dialog tab with position/color/transparency/takeover/width fieldsSingle text richtext field; layout controlled by classes style keywords (profile/landing/split/centered, etc.)
Custom inline CSScustomInlineStyle field injected raw (@context="unsafe")No raw-CSS-injection field found; only a classes_customClass free-text class name
Analytics taggingFull MVA/journey/message-bucket field set on the CTANo analytics fields in the model — analytics is presumably handled by a separate, page-level mechanism outside the block

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
backgroundTypeselect—Not mappedEDS hero infers background mode from which fields are populated (image, video) rather than an explicit type selector; color/gradient backgrounds have no dedicated fields — expressed via CSS class only if at all
bgColor / gradientColorselect (datasource)—Not mappedNo color/gradient picker in the EDS hero model; solid/gradient backgrounds would need to be expressed through the classes multiselect (e.g. "Solid Background") plus CSS, not a color datasource
Large/Medium/Small Image + Renditionsfileupload/select (3 fields x2)image / mobileImageRenamedCollapsed from a 3-tier swap set to a 2-tier desktop/mobile pair; rendition picker (ce-edam-renditions-list) has no equivalent — EDS relies on the DAM/asset reference component's own responsive delivery
imageType (scaling/swapping)select—Not mappedEDS hero always behaves like a picture/source swap when a distinct mobileImage is present; there's no separate "scaling" single-image sub-mode field
parallaxEffectcheckbox—Not mappedNo parallax effect implemented anywhere in hero.js/CSS
videoUrlpathbrowser (DAM)videoRenamedSame purpose (background video asset), now a reference field resolved through initVideo() instead of a DAM pathbrowser
videoLoopcheckbox—Not mappedhero.js hardcodes video.setAttribute('loop', '') unconditionally for the video variant — not authorable
videoPoster / posterFileReference / posterImageRenditionfileupload/select—Not mappedNo poster-image field or behavior found in hero.js; the video element has no poster attribute set
imageTitle / imageAltTexttextfieldimageAlt / mobileImageAltRenamedSame purpose (accessible alt text), split into desktop/mobile alt pairs instead of one shared title/alt pair
description (rich text)rich text (includewidget)textRenamedSame purpose (hero heading/body copy), single richtext field instead of an includewidget reference to a separate text component
mobilecontentpositionsselect—Not mappedNo equivalent — EDS layout is controlled entirely by the classes style multiselect (e.g. profile/landing/split), not a mobile-specific content-position toggle
contentpositionselect (datasource)classes_textAlignRenamedLoosely equivalent (text placement), reduced from a full datasource-driven position matrix to a 3-way left/center/right text alignment select
textPanelColorselect (datasource)classes_textColorRenamedLoosely equivalent (visual color treatment of the text area), reduced to a 2-option light/dark override instead of a full background-color datasource
transparencynumberfield—Not mappedNo transparency control on the EDS text panel/stack
takeovercheckbox—Not mappedNo "takeover width" concept found; closest analog is the full/height-tall style keywords in classes, which are not a direct equivalent
containerWidthselect (datasource)—Not mappedNo text-container-width field; width is implied by the chosen style keyword and brand CSS
linkTitle / pagePath / modalID / linkIcon / linkIconPosition / targetType / linkType (CTA)various—Not mappedThe EDS hero model itself has no dedicated CTA link field — CTA/button markup is expected to arrive as part of the text richtext field's authored links, not a structured CTA field set
componentTitle / componentPosition / contentJourney / patientJourney / messageBucket / analyticsEnableMVATier / mvaType / analyticsMVATier / analyticsMVATierValuevarious (analytics)—Not mappedNo analytics-tagging fields anywhere in the EDS hero model, consistent with the broader analytics-field gap also seen in the header block
borderTop / borderBottom / borderWidth / borderColorcheckbox/select—Not mappedBorder styling is a generic-container concern the purpose-built hero block does not carry forward; not observed in _hero.json or CSS as an authorable option
customCssClassestextfieldclasses_customClassSame name (renamed field, identical purpose)Free-text custom class field, same purpose, new field name matching EDS's classes_* naming convention
customInlineStyletextarea (unsafe HTML/CSS injection)—Not mappedNo raw CSS-injection field in the EDS model — a deliberate security-posture improvement, not a gap
anchorIDtextfield—Not mappedNo per-instance anchor id field on the EDS hero block
— (no source field)—eyebrowNot mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new dedicated eyebrow text field; the source system produced an eyebrow visually only by CSS-styling the first line of the includewidget-referenced text component, not a distinct field
— (no source field)—indicationNot mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new dedicated ISI/indication banner field; no equivalent in background-container's dialog
— (no source field)—layersNot mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new badge/multilayer image field backing the multilayer style variant; no equivalent concept in the source component
— (no source field)—imageCaptionNot mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new dedicated image-caption field (e.g. "Actor portrayal."); source system had no equivalent structured caption field
— (no source field)—classes (Style multiselect)Not mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new layout/style variant switch (profile/landing/full/compact/split/centered/video/solid/stat-overlay/multilayer/dual-cta/height-tall/light-font/no-padding) replacing what were previously separate dialog fields and/or hand-authored component compositions

Hot Spot / Interactive Image — Technical Details

What it does

Two related but distinct source-system components map to the target implementation's single hotspot block. hot-spot renders a base image with a multifield-authored list of click/hover-triggered points positioned by left/top percentage, each opening a small info panel with title, description, and optional CTA — this is the direct ancestor of the EDS block. interactive-image is a different, simpler concept: a single image with a configurable "behaviour" (hover zoom, drag, external link, or open-in-modal) and an optional icon overlay — closer to an enhanced image-link component than a multi-point hotspot map. The target implementation's hotspot block only carries forward the hot-spot concept (image + N click-to-open points); no drag/hover-zoom/modal-image behaviors from interactive-image appear to have been carried forward.

Architecture — hot-spot

Author uploads a base image + authors a "Hot Spots" multifield
(each entry: title, description, left/top position %, icon, optional CTA)
         ↓
HotSpotModel.java (Sling Model) builds a List<HotSpotBean> from the multifield
         ↓
hot-spot.html iterates hotSpotModel.hotSpotBeans:
  <div class="abbv-hot-spot-point" style="top: X%; left: Y%;">
    <div class="abbv-hot-spot-click" data-hover="${properties.enableHover}"><i class="{icon}"></i></div>
    <div class="abbv-hot-spot-panel {contentPosition}">
      <h5>{title}</h5><p>{description}</p>
      <a class="abbv-button-primary">{ctaTitle}</a> (optional)
      <span class="abbv-hot-spot-panel-close"></span>
    </div>
  </div>

Architecture — interactive-image

Author uploads image + sets imageBehaviour (dialog field, not in ui/dialog.json —
resolved via a separate authoring path/design dialog, since ui/dialog.json only
lists the Images tab's 5 basic image fields)
         ↓
InteractiveImageModel.java resolves imagePath (incl. eDAM rendition lookup),
imageheight/imagewidth (auto-derived from the asset if not authored)
         ↓
interactive-image.html branches on imageBehaviour:
  image-hover | image-drag  → plain <img> + optional icon span, behavior driven by CSS/JS class
  external-link | modal-image → wraps in an anchor via anchorTemplate.interactiveImage macro

Sling Model — HotSpotModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class})
public class HotSpotModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENT_ID_PREFIX = "abbv-hotspot";
    private static final String INTERACTION_ID = "interactionId";

    @Inject @Optional
    private List<String> hotSpots;   // raw multifield node names

    private List<HotSpotBean> hotSpotBeans;  // resolved per-point beans

    @Inject @Source("osgi-services")
    private SlingSettingsService slingSettingsService;
    // ... resolves imagePath from imageref/fileReference, builds hotSpotBeans
    //     from the ./hotSpots multifield, each bean exposing analytics
    //     digitalData JSON (open/close variants) via the shared DigitalDataObject helper
}

Sling Model — InteractiveImageModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class}, defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class InteractiveImageModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String ID_PREFIX = "abbv-interactiveimage";
    @Inject private String imageBehaviour;   // image-hover | image-drag | external-link | modal-image
    @Inject private String icon;
    @Inject private String iconPosition;
    @Inject private String imageAltText;
    @Inject private String imageTitle;
    @Inject private String imageheight;
    @Inject private String imagewidth;
    @Inject private String pagePath;
    @Inject private String modalID;
    @Inject @Optional @Named("image/fileReference") private String image;
    private String imagePath;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        // Resolves imagePath — special-cases /content/dam/remotedam paths via
        // CommonUtility.getImagePathBasedonEDAMRendition("large", ...)
        // Auto-derives imageheight/imagewidth from the asset metadata when a
        // site-level "enable image height and width" flag is on and the author
        // left the fields blank — explicit CLS (layout shift) mitigation.
    }
}

Dialog — hot-spot

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextfieldOverview tab
Anchor IDanchorIDtextfield
Imageimageref/fileReferencefileuploadbase hotspot image
Image Title / Image Alt TextimgTitle / imgAltTexttextfield
Enable hotspot on hover instead of mouse clickenableHovercheckboxnote: mobile devices always fall back to click since hover is unsupported
Hot Spot TitlehotSpotTitletextfieldContent tab, multifield hotSpots
Hot Spot DescriptionhotSpotDesctextfield
Analytics Title for AnalyticsanalyticsTitletextfield
Hot Spot Content PositionhotSpotContentPositionselect (datasource ce-hot-spot-content-position)
Hot Spot IconhotSpotIconselect (datasource ce-link-icon)
Icon Title (Analytics)iconComponentTitletextfield
Left Position (0-100)leftPositionnumberfield% from left of image
Top Position (0-100)topPositionnumberfield% from top of image
CTA Title / CTA URL / CTA Modal IDctaTitle, ctaurl, ctaModalIDtextfield/pathbrowser
CTA Link Icon / Icon Position / Click Behavior / Link TypectaLinkIcon, ctaLinkIconPosition, ctaLinkBehaviour, ctaLinkTypeselect
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtextfield (disabled)auto-populated, read-only display
CTA Title (Analytics) / Component Position / Section Title / Localized Section TitlectaComponentTitle, ctaComponentPosition, sectionTitle, l10Titlevarious
Most Valued Action (MVA) / MVA Title / MVA Type and Category / MVA Secondarymva, mvaTitle, mvaValue, mvaSecondarycheckbox/text/select

Dialog — interactive-image (ui/dialog.json, custom schema)

FieldProperty (inferred id)TypeNote
Drag an image hereimage field, id 200imageImages tab
Image Alt Textid 210text
Image Titleid 220text
Image Heightid 230textnumeric px, CLS mitigation note in-file
Image Widthid 240textnumeric px, CLS mitigation note in-file

The ui/dialog.json "Overview" tab is empty ("fields": []) in this file, yet the HTL and Java model reference many more properties (imageBehaviour, icon, iconPosition, pagePath, modalID, targetType, linkType, full analytics/MVA field set). These are real, injected Sling Model fields with no visible dialog source in this file — they are either authored via a design dialog not captured here or via the custom UI-kit dialog framework's other tabs not present in this JSON snapshot; treat this component's authoring surface as only partially discoverable from ui/dialog.json alone.

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure (hot-spot)

<div class="abbv-hot-spot ${properties.customCssClasses}">
  <div class="abbv-hot-spot-image-container">
    <img src="${hotSpotModel.imagePath}" alt="${properties.imgAltText || true}" />
    <div class="abbv-hot-spot-point" style="top: ${imgHotSpots.topPosition}%; left: ${imgHotSpots.leftPosition}%;">
      <div class="abbv-hot-spot-click" data-hover="${properties.enableHover}">
        <i class="${imgHotSpots.hotSpotIcon} i-b"></i>
      </div>
      <div class="abbv-hot-spot-panel ${imgHotSpots.hotSpotContentPosition}">
        <h5>${imgHotSpots.hotSpotTitle}</h5>
        <p>${imgHotSpots.hotSpotDesc}</p>
        <a class="abbv-button-primary">${imgHotSpots.ctaTitle}</a>
        <span class="abbv-hot-spot-panel-close"></span>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

Note the repeated alt="${... || true}" pattern (also seen in grid-list-view and interactive-image) — if the alt-text field is empty, HTL renders the literal string "true" as the alt attribute, a real accessibility bug baked into this template pattern.

CSS / Clientlib

hot-spot ships hot-spot-component.v1 clientlib (deferred JS via the async clientlib template) plus a base clientlibs category for CSS. interactive-image ships two deferred JS clientlibs — commom-elements-zoom-plugin.v1 (note the real, uncorrected typo "commom" in the category name) and interactive-image-component.v1 — plus an author-only clientlib (dropdownshowhide.js, validator.js) for its show/hide dialog behavior.

Important Constraints

  • alt="${properties.imgAltText || true}" / alt="${model.imageAltText || true}" — renders the literal string "true" as alt text when the field is empty, a real accessibility defect present in both components.
  • Hover-vs-click hotspot activation is a single global toggle (enableHover) for the whole image, not per-point.
  • hot-spot positions are raw 0–100 numeric percentages with no responsive adjustment — a hotspot placed relative to a wide desktop crop may land in the wrong spot on a differently-cropped mobile image (no separate mobile position fields).
  • interactive-image's per-instance behavior fields (imageBehaviour, analytics/MVA set) are used by the Sling Model and HTL but have no visible authoring path in the sampled ui/dialog.json — an authoring/documentation gap in the source, not just a migration gap.

Hotspot Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/hotspot/. It corresponds only to the hot-spot half of the source-system pairing — a base image plus N labeled points, each opening a content panel on click. The interactive-image behaviors (hover-zoom, drag, external-link-wrap, modal-image) have no counterpart in this block.

File Structure

blocks/hotspot/
├── hotspot.js
├── hotspot.css
├── _hotspot.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── hotspot.css
│   ├── _hotspot.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
└── rinvoq/

Authoring Model (_hotspot.json)

{
  "definitions": [
    {
      "title": "Hotspot", "id": "hotspot",
      "plugins": { "xwalk": { "page": {
        "resourceType": "core/franklin/components/block/v1/block",
        "template": { "name": "Hotspot", "model": "hotspot", "filter": "hotspot", "blockId": "id:" }
      } } }
    },
    {
      "title": "Hotspot Point", "id": "hotspot-item",
      "plugins": { "xwalk": { "page": {
        "resourceType": "core/franklin/components/block/v1/block/item",
        "template": {
          "name": "Hotspot Point", "model": "hotspot-item",
          "title": "Point Label", "content": "", "left": "50%", "top": "50%",
          "position": "top-right"
        }
      } } }
    }
  ],
  "models": [
    { "id": "hotspot", "fields": [
        { "component": "tab", "label": "Image", "name": "image" },
        { "component": "reference", "name": "image", "label": "Background Image" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "imageAlt", "label": "Image Alt Text" },
        { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
    ]},
    { "id": "hotspot-item", "fields": [
        { "component": "tab", "label": "Overview", "name": "overview" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "title", "label": "Point Title",
          "description": "Accessible label for the hotspot button." },
        { "component": "richtext", "name": "content", "label": "Panel Content",
          "description": "Content shown in the tooltip panel when the hotspot is activated." },
        { "component": "tab", "label": "Position", "name": "position" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "left", "value": "50%", "label": "Left Position (%)" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "top", "value": "50%", "label": "Top Position (%)" },
        { "component": "select", "name": "position", "label": "Panel Direction",
          "options": [
            { "name": "Top Right", "value": "top-right" },
            { "name": "Top Left", "value": "top-left" },
            { "name": "Bottom Right", "value": "bottom-right" },
            { "name": "Bottom Left", "value": "bottom-left" }
          ] }
    ]}
  ],
  "filters": [{ "id": "hotspot", "components": ["hotspot-item"] }]
}

The block uses two model definitions — a container (hotspot) and a repeatable child block/item type (hotspot-item) constrained by the filters entry — the Universal Editor equivalent of the source system's hotSpots multifield, but expressed as nested authorable child blocks rather than a fieldset multifield on one dialog.

How the JS works (hotspot.js)

export default function decorate(block) {
  const rows = [...block.children];
  const imageRow = rows[0];
  const [imgCell, altCell] = imageRow.children;
  const img = imgCell?.querySelector('img');
  if (img && altCell) img.alt = altCell.textContent.trim();

  const wrapper = document.createElement('div');
  wrapper.className = 'hotspot-wrapper';
  wrapper.append(img || imgCell);

  rows.slice(1).forEach((row, i) => {
    const cells = [...row.children];
    const title = cells[0]?.textContent.trim();
    const content = cells[1]?.innerHTML || '';
    const left = cells[2]?.textContent.trim() || '50%';
    const top = cells[3]?.textContent.trim() || '50%';
    const position = cells[4]?.textContent.trim() || 'top-right';

    const point = document.createElement('button');
    point.className = 'hotspot-point';
    point.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');
    point.setAttribute('aria-controls', `hotspot-panel-${i}`);
    point.setAttribute('aria-label', title);
    point.style.setProperty('--hotspot-x', left.includes('%') ? left : `${left}%`);
    point.style.setProperty('--hotspot-y', top.includes('%') ? top : `${top}%`);
    ...
  });
  block.replaceChildren(wrapper);
}

Each row after the image row is one hotspot point, positionally parsed (title / content / left / top / position) rather than named — the direct EDS-flattened equivalent of the hotSpots multifield's per-entry fields. Position is set via CSS custom properties (--hotspot-x/--hotspot-y) rather than inline top/left style declarations. The point is a real <button> with aria-expanded/aria-controls/aria-label, closed by Escape key or an explicit close button, and only one panel is open at a time (opening one force-closes all others) — same single-active-panel behavior as the source component's click/hover toggle.

CSS

Base hotspot.css defines panel positioning (.hotspot-panel-top-right, etc.) and the active/expanded visual state; brand folders (abbvie/, botox/, rinvoq/) override colors and point marker styling per brand, following the same base+brand pattern as other blocks in the target implementation.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (hot-spot)EDS (hotspot)
Point authoringhotSpots fieldset multifield on one dialogRepeatable hotspot-item child blocks, filtered under the hotspot container
Click vs hoverGlobal enableHover checkbox toggles all points between click and hover activationClick/tap-only (button with click + Escape handlers); no hover-activation mode observed
IconAuthor-selected icon per point (hotSpotIcon datasource)No icon field in the model; the point marker itself is a plain styled button, not an author-chosen icon
CTA in panelStructured CTA field set (title/url/modalID/icon/behavior/type) plus analytics fieldsCTA, if any, is expected to be authored inline inside the content richtext field, not a dedicated field set
AnalyticsFull digitalData JSON built server-side per point, with MVA fieldsNo analytics fields or digitalData construction found in hotspot.js

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
imageref/fileReference (hot-spot)fileuploadimageRenamedSame purpose (base hotspot image), reference field instead of fileupload+fileReference pair
imgTitletextfield—Not mappedNo separate image title field in the EDS model
imgAltTexttextfieldimageAltRenamedSame purpose (accessible alt text), new field name; also fixes the || true literal-"true"-alt bug since EDS renders alt only from this field directly in JS (img.alt = altCell.textContent.trim())
customCssClassestextfieldvia _common-properties.json#/common-prop includeRenamedReferenced by include rather than declared inline; exact field name not visible in this file (shared fragment)
anchorIDtextfieldvia _common-properties.json#/common-prop includeRenamedSame as above — likely present in the shared common-properties fragment, not confirmed by name in this file
enableHovercheckbox—Not mappedhotspot.js only wires click + Escape-key handlers; no hover-activation code path found
hotSpotTitletextfield (multifield)title (hotspot-item)Same name (different level — per-item model field vs multifield sub-field)Same purpose and same field name, now the label of a repeatable child block instead of a multifield row
hotSpotDesctextfield (multifield)contentRenamedSame purpose (panel body), upgraded from plain textfield to a richtext field, allowing formatted content and links (covers the CTA gap below)
analyticsTitletextfield—Not mappedNo analytics-title field in hotspot-item
hotSpotContentPositionselect (datasource)positionRenamedSame purpose (panel direction relative to point), reduced from a datasource-driven list to a fixed 4-option select (top-right/top-left/bottom-right/bottom-left)
hotSpotIcon / iconComponentTitleselect/textfield—Not mappedNo icon field on hotspot-item; the point marker has a fixed visual style, not an author-chosen icon
leftPosition / topPositionnumberfield (0-100)left / topRenamedSame purpose (point position as % of image), changed from a bare 0–100 number to a text field carrying the literal % suffix (e.g. "50%"), consumed via CSS custom properties instead of inline style attributes
ctaTitle / ctaurl / ctaModalID / ctaLinkIcon / ctaLinkIconPosition / ctaLinkBehaviour / ctaLinkTypevarious—Not mappedNo structured CTA field set on hotspot-item; a CTA link is expected to be authored as a link inside the content richtext field instead
interactionId / ctaComponentTitle / ctaComponentPosition / sectionTitle / l10Title / mva / mvaTitle / mvaValue / mvaSecondaryvarious (analytics)—Not mappedNo analytics/MVA fields anywhere in the EDS hotspot model, consistent with the analytics-field gap already noted for header and hero
— (interactive-image: imageBehaviour)text (injected, no visible dialog)—Not mappedThe hover/drag/external-link/modal-image behavior spectrum from interactive-image has no counterpart at all in the EDS hotspot block, which only implements the click-to-open-panel behavior
— (interactive-image: imageheight / imagewidth)text (auto-derived)—Not mappedNo explicit height/width authoring or CLS-mitigation logic found in hotspot.js; EDS relies on the underlying <picture>/asset delivery pipeline for sizing instead

Image Comparison Slider / Multiple Image Slider — Technical Details

What it does

The manifest maps this block to three source components. image-compare-slider is the direct ancestor: a single before/after image pair with a draggable divider handle (or, in "progressive" mode, an explicit range-slider control), optional text overlays on each image, and per-image content positioning. multiple-image-slider extends the same idea to a tabbed set of image pairs authored via an imagesList multifield. interactive-image (already documented under the hotspot section) contributes only its general "interactive image behaviors" concept to this mapping and is not otherwise specific to comparison sliders. The target implementation's image-compare block unifies both the single-pair and multi-tab/gallery use cases into one block with a large, explicitly-indexed content model.

Architecture — image-compare-slider

Author uploads Image 1 + Image 2 (same dimensions, non-transparent), picks sliderType
(handle-drag vs "progressive" range-slider), sliderStart (%), sliderIcon
         ↓
ImageSliderModel.java resolves image1Path/image2Path (+ optional height/width),
computes per-image transparency ints, builds analyticsString (digitalData JSON)
         ↓
image-compare-slider.html renders:
  .abbv-img-slider[data-slide-direction][data-start-point]
    <img> (image1) + optional text overlay
    <img> (image2) + optional text overlay
    .slider-handle  (drag mode)         — OR —
    .abbv-range-slider (progressive mode, explicit range input + fill + handle)

Architecture — multiple-image-slider

Author configures sliderOrientation, accessibility toggle/label/instructions,
lazyLoading, fetchPriority, imageHeight/imageWidth, imageTitlePosition, firstImageSize
         ↓
authors an "imagesList" multifield: each entry = imageFile, imageTitle,
imageAltText, contentText
         ↓
MultipleImageSliderModel.java resolves each entry's image path, builds a list
of slide beans, computes shared analytics/MVA data
         ↓
multiple-image-slider.html renders a tabbed/paged set of before/after pairs

Sling Model — ImageSliderModel.java

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class ImageSliderModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENT_ID_PREFIX = "abbv-image-slider";
    private static final String COMPONENT_NAME = "Comparison Slider";

    @Inject @Named("image2Transparency") @Optional private int image2Transparency;
    @Inject @Named("image1Transparency") @Optional private int image1Transparency;
    @Self private Resource resource;
    // ... resolves image1Path/image2Path from image1/fileReference, image2/fileReference;
    //     exposes imageoneheight/imageonewidth, imagetwoheight/imagetwowidth;
    //     builds analyticsString via the shared DigitalDataObject/Mva/Interaction beans
}

MultipleImageSliderModel.java follows the same base-class pattern (extends AbstractComponentModel), additionally resolving a list of slide beans from the imagesList multifield rather than two fixed image slots.

Dialog — image-compare-slider

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextfieldOverview tab
Anchor IDanchorIDtextfield
Slider TypesliderTypeselect (datasource ce-image-slider-type)e.g. drag-handle vs "progressive" range mode
Slider Start (%)sliderStartselect (datasource ce-image-slider-start)default "50%"
Slider IconsliderIconselect (datasource ce-link-icon)
Enable lazy loading for this imagelazyLoadingcheckboxchecked by default; in-dialog guidance text warns against lazy-loading above-the-fold images
Image 1image1/fileReferencefileuploadImage 1 tab — dialog note: "should not have transparent background and must be of same dimensions as Image 2"
Image 1 Title / Alt Textimage1Title / image1AltTexttextfield
Image 1 Height / Widthimageoneheight / imageonewidthtextfield (validated)CLS mitigation
Image 1 Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassesImage1textfield
Image 1 Content Textimage1Textrich text (includewidget)labels the left value in progressive mode
Image 1 Content Positionimage1ContentPositionselect (datasource ce-content-positions)
Image 1 Content Background Colorimage1BgColorselect (datasource ce-background-colors-rgb-form)
Image 1 Content Background Transparency (%)image1Transparencynumberfield (0-10)
Image 1 Content Container Width (%)image1ContainerWidthselect (datasource ce-image-slider-container-width)
Hide Content in Mobile (Image 1)image1HideInMobilecheckbox
Image 2 [...same field set as Image 1]image2/fileReference, image2Title, image2AltText, imagetwoheight, imagetwowidth, customCssClassesImage2, image2Text, image2ContentPosition, image2BgColor, image2Transparency, image2ContainerWidth, image2HideInMobilevariousImage 2 tab — dialog note: "must be of same dimensions as Image 1"
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtextfield (disabled)Analytics tab
Analytics TitleanalyticsTitletextfield
Section Title for Analytics / Localized Section TitlesectionTitle / l10sectionTitletextfield

Dialog — multiple-image-slider (key fields)

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor ID / Custom CSS ClassesanchorID / customCssClassestextfield
Slider OrientationsliderOrientationselect
Accessibility View Toggle / Label / InstructionsaccessibilityViewToggle, accessibilityViewToggleLabel, additionalInstructionAccessibilitycheckbox/textexplicit accessible-fallback-view pattern, same as forms-symptom-selector
Lazy Loading / Fetch PrioritylazyLoading / fetchPrioritycheckbox/select
Image Height / WidthimageHeight / imageWidthtextfield
Image Title PositionimageTitlePositionselect
First Image SizefirstImageSizetextfield

Analytics Interaction ID / Title / Section Title / Localized Section TitleinteractionId, analyticsTitle, sectionTitle, l10sectionTitlevarious

HTL — key DOM structure (image-compare-slider)

<div class="abbv-img-slider ${properties.customCssClasses}"
     data-slide-direction="${properties.sliderType}"
     data-start-point="${properties.sliderStart}">
  <img src="${imageSliderModel.image1Path}" alt="${properties.image1AltText || true}" />
  <div class="image-text-overlay-content ${properties.image1ContentPosition} ..."
       style="background: rgba(${properties.image1BgColor}, ${imageSliderModel.image1Transparency});">
    ${properties.image1Text @ context='html'}
  </div>
  <div class="img-alt-container">
    <img src="${imageSliderModel.image2Path}" alt="${properties.image2AltText || true}" />
  </div>

  <!-- drag-handle mode -->
  <span class="slider-handle ${properties.sliderIcon} i-a"
        data-digitaldata='${imageSliderModel.analyticsString}'></span>

  <!-- OR progressive/range mode -->
  <div class="abbv-range-slider" data-direction="horizontal" data-range-type="number"
       data-range="0,100" data-initial-value="0"
       data-onSliderMove="abbvImageCompare.onSliderMove">
    <input type="text" class="rangeInput"/>
    <span class="abbv-range-slider-label abbv-left">${properties.image1Text}</span>
    <div class="abbv-slider-container">
      <div class="abbv-slider-fill"></div>
      <div class="abbv-slider-handle"><span>0</span></div>
    </div>
    <span class="abbv-range-slider-label abbv-right">${properties.image2Text}</span>
  </div>
</div>

CSS / Clientlib

image-compare-slider ships a base clientlibs CSS category, a deferred clientlibs-component JS category, and an author-only image-compare-slider-authoring clientlib (dialog field validation). multiple-image-slider ships a single clientlib category bundling both its CSS and JS.

Important Constraints

  • alt="${properties.image1AltText || true}" / image2AltText || true — the same literal-"true"-alt-text bug seen elsewhere in this component family.
  • Both images must be pre-cropped to identical dimensions by the author; there is no cropping/alignment tooling in the dialog, only a text warning.
  • Background color and transparency values are interpolated directly into an inline style attribute with @context='unsafe' on the color value — another raw-injection point, though scoped to a color-picker-sourced value rather than free text.
  • multiple-image-slider's accessibility fallback (a plain toggle/label/instructions) is optional and author-controlled — if omitted, the tabbed slider has no confirmed accessible alternative path (consistent with the pattern already flagged for forms-symptom-selector).

Image Compare Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/image-compare/. It merges the single-pair and multi-set use cases from the two source components into one model: a base heading/description/prompt/label set, a "Simple Mode" before/after image pair, and two explicit tab configurations (Tab 1 / Tab 2), each holding two image pairs with thumbnails and labels — a fixed two-tab, two-image-per-tab gallery structure rather than an open-ended multifield.

File Structure

blocks/image-compare/
├── image-compare.js
├── image-compare.css
├── _image-compare.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── image-compare.css
│   ├── _image-compare.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
├── rinvoq/
└── skyrizi-hcp/

Authoring Model (_image-compare.json)

{
  "component": "multiselect", "name": "classes", "label": "Variant",
  "description": "Block mode and visual options.",
  "options": [
    { "name": "Simple Slider (default)", "value": "" },
    { "name": "Gallery (tabs + thumbnails)", "value": "gallery" },
    { "name": "Toggle Buttons", "value": "toggle" },
    { "name": "Show Drag Prompt", "value": "prompt" },
    { "name": "Reversed (slider left, content right)", "value": "reversed" },
    { "name": "Vertical", "value": "vertical" },
    { "name": "Hidden on Mobile", "value": "mobile-hidden" }
  ]
},
// Content tab: heading, description, sliderPrompt, beforeLabelPrefix, afterLabelPrefix
// Simple Mode tab: beforeImage, beforeAlt, afterImage, afterAlt
// Tab 1 Images: tab1Label, tab1Img1Before/After/Thumb/Label/SubLabel,
//               tab1Img2Before/After/Thumb/Label/SubLabel
// Tab 2 Images: tab2Label, tab2Img1Before/After/Thumb/Label/SubLabel,
//               tab2Img2Before/After/Thumb/Label/SubLabel
// Configuration tab: initialPosition (25/50/75%), anchorId
// Common Properties tab: blockId, classes_commonCustomClass, language

How the JS works (image-compare.js)

Rather than reading named fields directly, the JS defines an explicit column index map matching the model's field order, then reconstructs which authored row corresponds to which named field by content shape (image-bearing row vs text row) — necessary because EDS flattens the tabbed/nested model into a flat sequence of block rows:

const COL = {
  heading: 0, description: 1, sliderPrompt: 2, beforeLabelPrefix: 3, afterLabelPrefix: 4,
  beforeImage: 5, beforeAlt: 6, afterImage: 7, afterAlt: 8,
  tab1Label: 9, tab1Img1Before: 10, tab1Img1After: 11, tab1Img1Thumb: 12,
  tab1Img1Label: 13, tab1Img1SubLabel: 14, tab1Img2Before: 15, tab1Img2After: 16,
  tab1Img2Thumb: 17, tab1Img2Label: 18, tab1Img2SubLabel: 19,
  tab2Label: 20, tab2Img1Before: 21, tab2Img1After: 22, tab2Img1Thumb: 23,
  tab2Img1Label: 24, tab2Img1SubLabel: 25, tab2Img2Before: 26, tab2Img2After: 27,
  tab2Img2Thumb: 28, tab2Img2Label: 29, tab2Img2SubLabel: 30,
  initialPosition: 31, anchorId: 32,
};

function buildModelRowCells(block) {
  const rows = [...block.children].map((row) => row.children[0]);
  const cells = new Array(COL_NAMES.length).fill(null);
  for (let colIdx = 0, rowIdx = 0; colIdx < COL_NAMES.length && rowIdx < rows.length; colIdx += 1) {
    const expectsImage = IMAGE_FIELDS.has(COL_NAMES[colIdx]);
    const row = rows[rowIdx];
    const hasImage = !!row?.querySelector('img');
    if (expectsImage === hasImage) { cells[colIdx] = row; }
    rowIdx += 1;
  }
  return cells;
}

A separate code path, buildXwalkCells(), reads the same data directly from data-aue-prop attributes when running inside Universal Editor (where named property annotations are present on the DOM), with a PROP_ALIASES map normalizing a few renamed properties (beforeLabel → beforeLabelPrefix, etc.) — detectFormat() picks between this, the legacy positional format (decorateLegacy), and a key/value row format (decodeKeyValue/parseKeyValueRows), indicating the block has been through at least one content-model revision and still supports older authored content shapes. The slider itself (setupSlider) is a real drag/keyboard-operable widget: pointer-down/move on the container repositions a CSS-driven clip percentage, fixBeforeWidth()/scheduleFixBeforeWidth() keep the "before" image's clipped width in sync on resize, and the handle is a genuine role="slider" element with aria-valuemin/max/now and left/right arrow-key support.

CSS

Base image-compare.css defines the slider container/clip-path mechanics and label/prompt styling; per-brand overrides live in abbvie/, botox/, rinvoq/, and skyrizi-hcp/, each with a matching _image-compare.css/image-compare.css pair (the underscore-prefixed file appears to be a Universal-Editor-only or pre-processed variant, consistent with the naming convention seen on other blocks' brand folders).

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (image-compare-slider / multiple-image-slider)EDS (image-compare)
Multi-set authoringOpen-ended imagesList multifield (multiple-image-slider) — any number of slidesFixed two-tab, two-image-pair-per-tab structure (Tab 1/Tab 2 x Image 1/Image 2) — a bounded model, not an open multifield
Slider interaction modeAuthor-selected sliderType (drag-handle vs "progressive" range input)Single unified drag/keyboard slider implementation; mode variation expressed via classes (toggle, prompt, vertical, reversed) rather than a distinct progressive-range widget
Accessibility fallbackExplicit optional accessible dropdown/text view (multiple-image-slider)No separate accessible fallback view; accessibility is built into the one slider (ARIA slider role, keyboard support) rather than an alternate UI
Content model format evolutionSingle fixed Granite dialog shapeJS explicitly supports three distinct authored formats (Universal-Editor-annotated/model/legacy/key-value) via detectFormat(), implying at least one prior content-model migration within the target implementation itself
Per-image stylingPer-image background color, transparency %, container width %, position, hide-on-mobile (full dialog tab each)No equivalent per-image background/transparency/width fields; visual variation is via classes keywords only

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
customCssClassestextfieldclasses_commonCustomClassRenamedSame purpose, moved under the Common Properties tab naming convention
anchorIDtextfieldanchorIdSame name (case variant)Same purpose, camelCase capitalization changed
sliderTypeselect (datasource)classes (toggle/prompt/reversed/vertical values)RenamedSame general purpose (control slider presentation mode), collapsed from a dedicated datasource-driven type select into style keywords in the multiselect
sliderStartselect (datasource, default 50%)initialPositionRenamedSame purpose, reduced from an open datasource list to a fixed 25/50/75% select
sliderIconselect (datasource ce-link-icon)—Not mappedNo icon-selection field on the EDS handle; handle styling is fixed/CSS-driven
lazyLoading (image-compare-slider)checkbox—Not mappedNo explicit lazy-loading toggle in _image-compare.json; presumably handled by the underlying reference/picture rendering pipeline instead of a per-block field
image1/fileReference / image2/fileReferencefileuploadbeforeImage / afterImage (Simple Mode)RenamedSame purpose (the compared image pair), renamed to explicit before/after semantics instead of numbered image1/image2
image1Title / image2Titletextfield—Not mappedNo separate image title field in the EDS model, only alt text
image1AltText / image2AltTexttextfieldbeforeAlt / afterAltRenamedSame purpose, before/after naming; also avoids the literal-"true"-alt-text bug since EDS JS clones the real <img alt> rather than falling back with || true
imageoneheight/imageonewidth, imagetwoheight/imagetwowidthtextfield (validated)—Not mappedNo explicit height/width fields; sizing is delegated to the reference/picture component's own responsive rendering
customCssClassesImage1 / customCssClassesImage2textfield—Not mappedNo per-image custom class field in the EDS model
image1Text / image2Textrich text (includewidget)beforeLabelPrefix / afterLabelPrefixRenamedLoosely equivalent (labels the two sides of the comparison), reduced from a full richtext overlay to a short plain-text label prefix (default "BEFORE"/"AFTER")
image1ContentPosition / image2ContentPositionselect (datasource)—Not mappedNo per-image content-position field; label placement is fixed by CSS (.image-compare-label-before/-after)
image1BgColor / image2BgColorselect (datasource)—Not mappedNo per-image background color field in the EDS model
image1Transparency / image2Transparencynumberfield (0-10)—Not mappedNo transparency control in the EDS model
image1ContainerWidth / image2ContainerWidthselect (datasource)—Not mappedNo content-container-width field
image1HideInMobile / image2HideInMobilecheckboxmobile-hidden (classes value)RenamedLoosely equivalent purpose (hide something on mobile), but the EDS value hides the entire block on mobile via a style keyword, not a per-image toggle
interactionId / analyticsTitle / sectionTitle / l10sectionTitle / mvaEnable / mvaName / mvaValue / mvaTiervarious (analytics)—Not mappedNo analytics/MVA fields anywhere in the EDS image-compare model, consistent with the analytics-field gap already seen across this batch
multiple-image-slider: sliderOrientationselectvertical (classes value)RenamedSame purpose (orientation control), reduced to a single boolean-like style keyword instead of a full orientation select
multiple-image-slider: accessibilityViewToggle / accessibilityViewToggleLabel / additionalInstructionAccessibilitycheckbox/text—Not mappedNo dedicated accessible-fallback-view fields; the EDS block instead builds accessibility directly into the one slider widget (ARIA slider role + keyboard support)
multiple-image-slider: fetchPriority / imageHeight / imageWidth / imageTitlePosition / firstImageSizeselect/text—Not mappedNo equivalents in the EDS model; these performance/layout controls are presumably handled by the shared picture/reference rendering pipeline
multiple-image-slider: imagesList multifield (imageFile, imageTitle, imageAltText, contentText)multifieldtab1Img1* / tab1Img2* / tab2Img1* / tab2Img2* field groupsRenamedSame general purpose (multiple labeled image slides), but converted from an open-ended multifield to a fixed two-tab, two-image-per-tab structure — a real capability reduction if more than 2 tabs x 2 images were ever authored on the source component
— (no source field)—heading / descriptionNot mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new block-level heading/description fields; neither source component had a dedicated heading/description pair at the top level
— (no source field)—sliderPromptNot mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new "click and drag" prompt text field, paired with the new prompt style keyword; no equivalent authored prompt text found in either source component
— (no source field)—tab1Img*Thumb / tab2Img*ThumbNot mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new dedicated thumbnail image fields for the gallery variant; the source components render tab labels as text only, with no separate thumbnail image concept
— (no source field)—blockId / languageNot mapped (EDS-native addition)Common-Properties-tab fields shared across many EDS blocks (unique block id regex-validated as id:..., and a language tag) with no equivalent in either source component's dialog

Image with Rich Text (image-text / image-text-v2) — Technical Details

What it does

A standard media+copy component: an image (single "scaling" image, or a small/medium/large swap set shown at different breakpoints) paired with rich text and an optional CTA. Three layout variants exist based on where the text sits relative to the image (in-in / in-out / out-out — separate HTL includes). image-text-v2 is a visible-in-picker, actively maintained successor to the original image-text (which is hidden from the component browser, componentGroup=".hidden", implying template/policy-only usage) — it adds performance fields (fetch priority, DAM rendition pickers, explicit per-size height/width), a full-screen toggle, and a refined/consolidated analytics field set, while dropping the separate image-level analytics fields v1 carried alongside the CTA's own analytics set.

Architecture

Author sets containerPosition (in-in | in-out | out-out) → dispatches to:
  image-text-in-in.html | image-text-in-out.html | image-text-out-out.html
         ↓
TextAndImageModel.java resolves imageType (scaling vs swapping) → largeImagePath
(+ mediumImagePath/smallImagePath for swapping), image link fields, digitalData JSON
         ↓
Per-breakpoint <style> block toggles which of the 3 <img> swap-set elements
is display:inline-block, keyed to a per-instance styleToken id:
  @media (min-width: 985px)             → -large
  @media (min-width: 601-985px)         → -medium
  @media (max-width: 600px)             → -small
         ↓
Renders image (optionally link-wrapped) + rich text description + optional CTA,
laid out according to containerPosition/contentposition/containerWidth/takeover

Sling Model — TextAndImageModel.java

Resolves image paths (large/medium/small), image link fields (URL/type/modal/behaviour), and builds a digitalData JSON string for the image link via the shared analytics bean helpers — mirroring the same pattern seen in BackgroundContainer/InteractiveImageModel/HotSpotModel (all extend a common base class and reuse the DigitalDataObject/Link/Mva analytics bean helpers). Exposes getters consumed directly in the HTL, e.g. imageModel.largeImagePath, imageModel.imageLinkURL, imageModel.imageDigitalData, imageModel.id (used as the CSS style-token for the swap-image media queries).

Dialog — image-text (v1)

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor IDanchorIDtextfieldOverview tab
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextfield
TitleimgTitletextfield
Alt TxtimgAltTxttextfield
Image TypeimageTypeselect"Swap Images – Only Load Needed Image" (responsive-swapping, default), "Scaling", "Swap Images - Deprecated" (swapping, explicitly labeled deprecated in the picklist itself)
Enable lazy loading for this imagelazyLoadingcheckbox
Add Icon on imageaddIconcheckbox
Image IconchoosenIconselect (datasource ce-link-icon)note real field-name typo "choosen"
Image Link URLimageLinkURLpathbrowser
Image Link TypeimageLinkTypeselect (datasource ce-link-type)
Image Link Modal IDimageLinkModalIDtextfield
Image Link Click BehaviorimageLinkBehaviourselect (datasource ce-link-click-behavior, default _self)
Large/Medium/Small Imagelarge/file, medium/file, small/file (+fileReference)fileuploadImages tab; only Large required if imageType=scaling
Descriptiondescriptionrich textImage Text tab
Container PositioncontainerPositionselectin-in / in-out / out-out — picks HTL include
Content Positioncontentpositionselect (datasource)
Background ColorbgColorselect (datasource ce-background-colors-rgb-form)
Transparencytransparencynumberfield
Select for Takeovertakeovercheckbox
Container WidthcontainerWidthselect (datasource)
Image Interaction ID / Analytics Title / Link Component Title / Localized Section TitleimageInteractionId, imageAnalyticsTitle, imageLinkComponentTitle, imageL10sectionTitlevariousAnalytics tab — image-level analytics, separate from the CTA's own set below
Image Link MVA / MVA Title / MVA Value / MVA SecondaryimageLinkMva, imageMvaTitle, imageLinkMvaValue, imageMvaSecondarycheckbox/text/select
Link Title / Page Path / Modal IDlinktitle, pagepath, modalIDtextfield/pathbrowserCTA tab
Link Icon / Icon Position / Target Type / Link TypelinkIcon, linkIconPosition, targettype, linktypeselect
CTA Interaction ID / Analytics Title / Component Title / Localized Section TitlectaInteractionId, ctaAnalyticsTitle, componentTitle, ctaL10sectionTitlevarious
Enable MVA Tier / MVA Title / MVA Tier Value / MVA SecondaryanalyticsEnableMVATier, ctaMvaTitle, analyticsMVATierValue, ctaMvaSecondarycheckbox/text/select

Dialog — image-text-v2 (key differences)

FieldPropertyTypeNote
(no equivalent — imgTitle removed)——v1's separate image Title field is dropped in v2's Overview tab
Fetch PriorityfetchPriorityselectnet-new performance hint field
Full ScreenfullScreencheckboxnet-new layout toggle
Large/Medium/Small Image RenditionlargeImageRendition, mediumImageRendition, smallImageRenditionselect (datasource ce-edam-renditions-list)net-new — explicit DAM rendition picker per size, absent in v1
