Large/Medium/Small Image Height/WidthlargeImageHeight/largeImageWidth, mediumImageHeight/mediumImageWidth, smallImageHeight/smallImageWidthtextfieldnet-new — explicit CLS-mitigation sizing fields per breakpoint, absent in v1
CTA Link Titlectalinktitletextfieldrenamed from v1's linktitle (lowercase, "cta" prefix)
Interaction ID / Analytics Title / Section Title / Localized TitleinteractionId, analyticsTitle, sectionTitle, l10Titlevariousconsolidated single analytics field set — v1's separate image-level (image*) and CTA-level (cta*) analytics fields are merged into one set
MVA Primary / MVA Title / MVA Type and Category / MVA SecondarymvaPrimary, mvaTitle, mvaTypeAndCategory, mvaSecondaryselect/text/checkboxrefined MVA naming, same pattern seen in header-v2
Third Party TagsthirdPartyTagstextfieldnet-new

containerWidth/takeover/bgColor/transparency (v1's Image Text tab styling controls) are not present by name in the v2 field list sampled above — v2 appears to simplify or relocate the text-panel styling controls relative to v1, though a design-dialog-level equivalent cannot be ruled out without further inspection.

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure (image-text-in-in.html)

<sly data-sly-test="${properties.imageType == 'swapping'}">
  <style>
    @media (min-width: 985px) { .${imageModel.id}-large { display: inline-block; } }
    @media (min-width: 601px) and (max-width: 985px) { .${imageModel.id}-medium { display: inline-block; } }
    @media (max-width: 600px) { .${imageModel.id}-small { display: inline-block; } }
  </style>
</sly>
<div class="abbv-image-text ${properties.customCssClasses}">
  <div class="abbv-image-content-container" data-sly-test="${properties.imageType == 'scaling'}">
    <img src="${imageModel.largeImagePath}" alt="${imageAltText || true}" />
  </div>
  <div class="abbv-image-content-container abbv-swap-images" data-sly-test="${properties.imageType == 'swapping'}">
    <img src="${imageModel.largeImagePath}" class="${imageModel.id}-large" alt="${imageAltText || true}" />
    <img src="${imageModel.mediumImagePath}" class="${imageModel.id}-medium" alt="${imageAltText || true}" />
    <img src="${imageModel.smallImagePath}" class="${imageModel.id}-small" alt="${imageAltText || true}" />
  </div>
  <!-- rich text + CTA follow, container-position dependent -->
</div>

image-text.html itself is a one-line dispatcher: <sly data-sly-include="image-text-${properties.containerPosition ? properties.containerPosition : 'in-in'}.html"></sly>.

CSS / Clientlib

image-text ships a single clientlib category (CSS + JS together). image-text-v2 ships the same plus an author-only clientlib-authoring clientlib (validator.js) for dialog field validation.

Important Constraints

  • alt="${imageAltText || true}" — the same literal-"true"-alt-text bug present across this component family, repeated three times per instance in swap mode (once per size).
  • The "Swap Images - Deprecated" option remains selectable in v1's imageType picklist alongside its labeled replacement ("Swap Images – Only Load Needed Image") — a deprecated option left live rather than removed.
  • image-text (v1) is hidden from the component browser (componentGroup=".hidden") yet still fully functional and presumably still rendering on existing pages/templates — a live component with no discoverable authoring entry point for new usage.
  • Breakpoints for the swap set (600px/601–985px/985px+) are hardcoded in the HTL, identical to the pattern in background-container — not authorable, and duplicated logic across at least two components.

Image Text Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/image-text/. It consolidates the three container-position HTL variants and the scaling/swapping image-type split into one block whose visual variant is controlled entirely by a classes multiselect, and reduces the desktop/tablet/mobile three-tier swap set to a two-tier desktop/mobile pair, matching the same simplification pattern seen in the hero block.

File Structure

blocks/image-text/
├── image-text.js
├── image-text.css
├── _image-text.json
├── block-config.js
└── venclexta/
    ├── image-text.css
    ├── _image-text.css
    └── block-config.js

Only one brand override folder (venclexta/) exists for this block, unlike header/hero/hotspot/image-compare which have several — most brands presumably rely on the base styling as-is.

Authoring Model (_image-text.json)

{
  "component": "multiselect", "name": "classes", "label": "Style",
  "options": [
    { "name": "Reverse (text left, image right)", "value": "image-text-reverse" },
    { "name": "Swap (responsive image at mobile)", "value": "image-text-swap" },
    { "name": "Scale (image scales on hover)", "value": "image-text-scale" },
    { "name": "Stretched Link (entire card clickable)", "value": "image-text-stretched" },
    { "name": "Large image (60%)", "value": "image-text-image-large" },
    { "name": "Medium image (40%)", "value": "image-text-image-medium" },
    { "name": "Small image (33%)", "value": "image-text-image-small" }
  ]
},
{ "component": "reference", "name": "image", "label": "Image" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "imageAlt", "label": "Image Alt Text", "required": true },
{ "component": "reference", "name": "mobileImage", "label": "Mobile Image (swap variant only)" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "mobileImageAlt", "label": "Mobile Image Alt Text" },
{ "component": "richtext", "name": "content", "label": "Content" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "ctaLabel", "label": "CTA Label" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "ctaHref", "label": "CTA URL" },
{ "component": "select", "name": "ctaTarget", "value": "_self", "label": "CTA Target",
  "options": [{ "name": "Same Tab", "value": "_self" }, { "name": "New Tab", "value": "_blank" }] },
{ "component": "text", "name": "modalId", "label": "Modal ID (overrides CTA URL with modal trigger)" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "anchorId", "label": "Anchor ID" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "analyticsId", "label": "Analytics ID" }

How the JS works (image-text.js)

const BASE_ROW = {
  IMAGE: 0, MOBILE_IMAGE: 1, CONTENT: 2, CTA_LABEL: 3, CTA_HREF: 4,
  CTA_TARGET: 5, MODAL_ID: 6, ANCHOR_ID: 7, ANALYTICS_ID: 8,
};

function extractPicture(row) {
  // Handles three authoring cases: EDS-standard <picture>, a bare <img>,
  // or an AEM-authoring-time reference rendered as an <a> to the asset URL.
  ...
}

export default function decorate(block) {
  ...
  if (anchorId) block.id = anchorId;   // page-anchor targeting, matches AEM's anchorID
  ...
  const isStretched = block.classList.contains('image-text-stretched');
  const hasDestination = ctaHref || modalId;
  if (ctaLabel || (isStretched && hasDestination)) {
    const link = document.createElement('a');
    link.classList.add('button');
    if (modalId) {
      link.href = '#';
      link.dataset.modal = modalId;
      link.setAttribute('aria-haspopup', 'dialog');
    } else if (ctaHref) {
      link.href = ctaHref;
      link.target = ctaTarget;
      if (ctaTarget === '_blank') link.rel = 'noopener noreferrer';
    }
    if (analyticsId) link.dataset.analytics = analyticsId;
    ...
  }
  ...
}

A getRowOffset() pre-scan detects whether row 0 is an image or a leading placeholder/empty row and shifts the entire row-index map accordingly — a defensive measure against template-default rows that can appear before the real content, similar in spirit to the source system's positional-parsing patterns in the header block. The CTA either becomes a normal link (ctaHref/ctaTarget) or a modal trigger (data-modal attribute + aria-haspopup="dialog") when modalId is set — directly analogous to the source system's linkType/modalID pattern for triggering the `modal` component. The swap variant (image-text-swap) swaps the rendered image's src/alt at a single 599px breakpoint via matchMedia, rather than the source system's three-tier CSS-only display toggle.

CSS

Base image-text.css implements the grid layout and all classes-driven variants (reverse, swap, scale-on-hover, stretched-link, image-size ratios); the venclexta/ folder supplies brand-specific overrides following the same base+brand pattern as other blocks.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (image-text / image-text-v2)EDS (image-text)
Layout variant selectionThree separate HTL includes dispatched by containerPosition (in-in/in-out/out-out)Single JS/CSS implementation; layout variation via classes keywords (reverse, image-large/medium/small, stretched)
Image swap tiers3-tier small/medium/large, fixed 600px/985px breakpoints2-tier desktop/mobile, single 599px breakpoint via matchMedia
CTA-triggers-modalmodalID field on the CTA fieldset, alongside full link-type/behavior select setmodalId field, simpler binary branch (modal vs href) with no separate link-type select
Icon on imageaddIcon/choosenIcon — author-selected icon overlay on the imageNo icon field in the model; not carried forward
Performance/CLS fields (v2 only)Per-size rendition picker + explicit height/widthNo equivalent explicit sizing fields; relies on the reference field's underlying responsive image pipeline and a computed sizes attribute based on the image-size class

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtextfieldanchorIdSame name (case variant)Same purpose, applied directly as the block's DOM id in JS instead of a separate anchor <a> tag
customCssClassestextfield—Not mappedNo free-text custom class field in _image-text.json; styling variation is limited to the fixed classes option set
imgTitle (v1) / dropped in v2textfield—Not mappedNo image title field in the EDS model, consistent with v2 already having dropped it
imgAltTxttextfieldimageAltRenamedSame purpose, now a required field, and consumed directly (no || true fallback) — fixes the literal-"true"-alt-text bug
imageType (scaling/swapping/responsive-swapping)selectimage-text-swap (classes value)RenamedSame general purpose (control whether a distinct mobile image is used), collapsed from a 3-option select with a deprecated option into a single style keyword
lazyLoadingcheckbox—Not mappedNo lazy-loading toggle in the EDS model; handled by the underlying image pipeline
addIcon / choosenIconcheckbox/select—Not mappedNo icon-overlay field or behavior in image-text.js
imageLinkURL / imageLinkType / imageLinkModalID / imageLinkBehaviourpathbrowser/select/textfield—Not mappedNo separate "the whole image is a link" field set; the closest EDS equivalent is the image-text-stretched style keyword combined with the one shared CTA link, not an independent image-level link
large/fileReference / medium/fileReference / small/fileReferencefileuploadimage / mobileImageRenamedCollapsed from a 3-tier set to a 2-tier desktop/mobile pair, same simplification as the hero block
largeImageRendition / mediumImageRendition / smallImageRendition (v2)select (datasource)—Not mappedNo rendition-picker field; EDS relies on the reference field's own delivery pipeline
largeImageHeight/Width, mediumImageHeight/Width, smallImageHeight/Width (v2)textfield—Not mappedNo explicit per-size height/width fields; CLS mitigation instead comes from a computed sizes attribute in JS based on the chosen image-size class
fetchPriority (v2)select—Not mappedNo fetch-priority field or attribute set in image-text.js
fullScreen (v2)checkbox—Not mappedNo full-screen layout mode in the EDS model or CSS
description (rich text)rich textcontentRenamedSame purpose, new field name
containerPosition (in-in/in-out/out-out)select—Not mappedNo direct equivalent; EDS uses a single fixed grid layout, varied only by the reverse/image-size classes keywords, not a 3-way nesting-position select
contentpositionselect (datasource)—Not mappedNo content-position field; text column position is implied by image-text-reverse only
bgColor / transparencyselect/numberfield—Not mappedNo background color/transparency fields on the content column in the EDS model
takeover / containerWidthcheckbox/selectimage-text-image-large / -medium / -small (classes values)RenamedLoosely equivalent purpose (control image/content column width ratio), reimplemented as three fixed percentage-ratio style keywords instead of a free-form takeover flag + width select
linktitle (v1) / ctalinktitle (v2)textfieldctaLabelRenamedSame purpose (CTA button text), new field name matching EDS's cta* convention
pagepathpathbrowserctaHrefRenamedSame purpose (CTA destination), changed from a content-tree pathbrowser to a free-text URL field
modalIDtextfieldmodalIdSame name (case variant)Same purpose and behavior (CTA opens a modal instead of navigating)
linkIcon / linkIconPositionselect—Not mappedNo icon field on the EDS CTA; the button is plain text
targettype / imageLinkBehaviourselect (datasource)ctaTargetRenamedSame purpose (link target/behavior), reduced from a datasource-driven behavior list to a 2-option same-tab/new-tab select
linktypeselect (datasource)—Not mappedNo link-type select; EDS branches only on presence of modalId vs ctaHref, not an explicit type enum
imageInteractionId/ctaInteractionId (v1), interactionId (v2)textfieldanalyticsIdRenamedLoosely equivalent purpose (an analytics-tracking identifier), consolidated from separate image/CTA interaction IDs into a single field, surfaced in the DOM as data-analytics rather than a digitalData JSON blob
imageAnalyticsTitle/ctaAnalyticsTitle (v1), analyticsTitle (v2)textfield—Not mappedNo separate analytics-title field in the EDS model beyond the single analyticsId
imageLinkComponentTitle/componentTitle (v1), consolidated in v2textfield—Not mappedNo component-title analytics field in EDS
imageL10sectionTitle/ctaL10sectionTitle (v1), sectionTitle/l10Title (v2)textfield—Not mappedNo section-title/localized-title fields in EDS
imageLinkMva/ctaMva-family (v1), mvaPrimary/mvaTitle/mvaTypeAndCategory/mvaSecondary (v2)checkbox/text/select—Not mappedNo MVA/analytics-tagging fields anywhere in the EDS image-text model, consistent with the analytics-field gap already documented across this batch
thirdPartyTags (v2)textfield—Not mappedNo third-party tag field in EDS
— (no source field)—image-text-scale (classes value)Not mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new hover-scale image effect; no equivalent visual behavior found in either source component

Info Tree — Technical Details

What it does

A single-question decision widget ("Quick Poll" pattern): an author-authored question with a set of answer buttons; clicking an answer reveals a pre-authored content panel tied to that answer and writes the user's choice to a browser cookie so the same panel is shown automatically on return visits (persisted for one year). An optional reset button lets the user clear the cookie and answer again. The manifest's initial characterization of this component purely as a "cookie helper utility" undersells it — reading the real dialog, HTL, and JS confirms it is a complete authored question/answer decision-tree UI, with the cookie as an implementation detail of persisting the user's selection, not the component's primary purpose.

Architecture

Author sets infoTreeName (also used as the cookie name), optional image/heading/question,
authors an "Answers" multifield (each: choiceText + auto-populated interactionId),
then authors one sibling `text` component per answer choice (named `{choiceText}{index}`)
holding the revealed content for that answer
         ↓
InfoTreeModel.java builds infoTreeBeans from the answerOptions multifield,
each bean carrying digitalData JSON for click tracking
         ↓
info-tree.html renders:
  .abbv-info-tree[data-qPollID="{infoTreeName}"]
    image, heading, question
    button.multistep-qPoll-option (one per answer, data-question-id={choiceText})
    .multistep-qPoll-results[data-answer-id={choiceText}] (one per answer, hidden by default)
    optional reset button
         ↓
info-tree.js (client):
  on load: read cookie named {infoTreeName} → if present, show matching results panel
  on answer click: cookieObj.createCookie({infoTreeName}, selectedAnswer, 365) → show panel
  on reset click: cookieObj.deleteCookie({infoTreeName}) → hide all panels, show buttons again

Sling Model — InfoTreeModel.java

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class InfoTreeModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String INTERACTION_ID = "interactionId";
    private static final String INFOTREE_INTERACTION_ID = "infoTreeInteractionId";
    private static final String ANSWER = "choiceText";
    private static final String CONTENT = "content";
    private static final String INFO_TREE = "Info Tree";
    List<String> answersList = new ArrayList<>();
    // ... builds InfoTreeBean list from the ./answerOptions multifield;
    //     each bean exposes choiceText + a digitalData JSON string (click analytics);
    //     also exposes resetButtonDigitalData for the reset button's own analytics
}

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Info Tree Identifier (Anchor ID)anchorIDtextfieldOverview tab
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestextfield
Info Tree Name and Cookie NameinfoTreeNametextfield (required)doubles as both the component's identifier and the literal cookie name; in-dialog guidance warns against spaces/special characters
Interaction IDinfoTreeInteractionIdtextfield (disabled)auto-populated display of the parent interaction id
Allow user to reset their answerenableResetButtonCheckboxcheckbox
Reset Button textresetButtonTexttextfield
Reset Button Interaction IDinteractionIdtextfield (disabled)note: reuses the generic interactionId name distinct from infoTreeInteractionId above
Info Tree ImageinfoTreeImagefile/infoTreeImagefileReferencefileuploadQuestion & Answers tab
Image Title / Image Alt TextimageTitle / imageAltTexttextfield
HeadingheadingTexttextfield
Question TextquestionTexttextfield
Answers (multifield)answerOptions: choiceText, interactionId (disabled)multifieldeach entry is one answer button; the actual revealed content is authored separately as a sibling text component named {choiceText}{index}, not inline in this multifield
Section Title for Analytics / Localized Section TitlesectionTitle / l10TitletextfieldAnalytics tab
Analytics TitleanalyticsTitletextfield
Most Valued Action (MVA) / MVA Title / MVA Type and Category / MVA SecondarymvaPrimary, mvaTitle, mvaTypeAndCategory, mvaSecondarycheckbox/text/selectuses the refined MVA naming convention (same as header-v2 / image-text-v2), not the older analyticsEnableMVATier style

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<div class="abbv-info-tree ${properties.customCssClasses}" data-qPollID="${properties.infoTreeName}">
  <img class="multistep-qPoll-image" alt="${properties.imageAltText || true}" src="${properties.infoTreeImagefileReference}"/>
  <h2>${properties.headingText}</h2>
  <div class="multistep-qPoll-question">
    <p>${properties.questionText}</p>
    <div class="multistep-qPoll-btn">
      <sly data-sly-list.answerOptions="${infoTreeModel.infoTreeBeans}">
        <button data-digitaldata='${answerOptions.digitalData}' class="multistep-qPoll-option abbv-button-tertiary"
                data-question-id="${answerOptions.choiceText}">${answerOptions.choiceText}</button>
      </sly>
    </div>
  </div>
  <sly data-sly-list.answerOptions="${infoTreeModel.infoTreeBeans}">
    <div class="multistep-qPoll-results" data-answer-id="${answerOptions.choiceText}">
      <sly data-sly-test.parName="${answerOptions.choiceText}${answerOptionsList.count}">
        <div data-sly-resource="${@path=parName, resourceType='.../text'}" data-sly-unwrap></div>
      </sly>
    </div>
  </sly>
  <button class="abbv-button-tertiary abbv-icon-replay i-b">${properties.resetButtonText}</button>
</div>

The parName="${answerOptions.choiceText}${answerOptionsList.count}" pattern is notable: the revealed-content component for each answer is located by constructing a resource name from the answer's own label text plus its list index, not by an explicit reference field — a fragile naming-convention coupling between the multifield and sibling content nodes.

Client JS — abbvInfoTree (info-tree.js)

var abbvInfoTree = (function () {
    return {
        init: function () {
            $('.abbv-info-tree:not([data-initialized="true"])').each(function() {
                let cookieValue = cookieObj.getCookie($(this).data('qpollid'));
                if (cookieValue) { me.showSummary($(this), cookieValue); }
                else { me.hideSummary($(this)); }
            });
            $('.multistep-qPoll-option').on('click', function() {
                let cookieName = $(this).closest('.abbv-info-tree').data('qpollid');
                let selectedAns = $(this).data('question-id');
                cookieObj.createCookie(cookieName, selectedAns, 365);
                me.showSummary(...);
            });
            $('.abbv-multistep-qPoll-reset button').on('click', function() {
                me.resetInfoTree($(this).closest('.abbv-info-tree'));
            });
        },
        // showSummary / hideSummary toggle .abbv-answered class + show/hide result panel
        // resetInfoTree deletes the cookie via the shared cookieObj utility
    };
})();

Relies on a shared global cookieObj utility (also used by the header's indication-swap logic) for getCookie/createCookie/deleteCookie — this is the "cookie helper utility" pattern the manifest's initial (file-scan-only) pass likely picked up on, one layer below the actual question/answer UI logic in the same file.

CSS / Clientlib

Two clientlib categories: a base clientlibs for CSS, and a deferred clientlibs-component (info-tree-component.v1) for JS, loaded via the async clientlib template — consistent with the pattern used by other interactive components in this family (hot-spot, interactive-image).

Important Constraints

  • alt="${properties.imageAltText || true}" — the same literal-"true"-alt-text bug present throughout this component family.
  • The cookie name is directly the author-typed infoTreeName with no server-side sanitization visible in the HTL/JS shown — the dialog relies entirely on an in-field instruction ("Don't use spaces or special characters") rather than validation.
  • The link between an answer's choiceText and its revealed content is a generated resource-name convention ({choiceText}{index}), not an explicit reference — renaming an answer's text after content has been authored under the old name would silently break the connection.
  • No accessible live-region or focus-management is visible in the HTL/JS for the revealed answer panel (no aria-live on .multistep-qPoll-results).

Info Tree Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/info-tree/. It keeps the same core mechanic (question, N answer buttons, per-answer revealed content, cookie persistence, optional reset) but removes the answer→content naming-convention coupling by making each answer a proper repeatable child block (info-tree-item) that carries its own content directly, and adds a structured optional CTA per answer.

File Structure

blocks/info-tree/
├── info-tree.js
├── info-tree.css
├── _info-tree.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── info-tree.css
│   ├── _info-tree.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
└── rinvoq/

Authoring Model (_info-tree.json)

// info-tree (container):
{ "component": "tab", "label": "Overview", "name": "overview" },
{ "component": "reference", "name": "image", "label": "Side Image",
  "description": "Optional image displayed beside the question (e.g. Quick Poll badge)." },
{ "component": "text", "name": "imageAlt", "label": "Image Alt Text" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "treeName", "label": "Tree Name",
  "description": "Internal name to identify this info tree (e.g. ra-tnf-poll, ad-satisfaction-poll)." },
{ "component": "richtext", "name": "question", "label": "Question" },
{ "component": "richtext", "name": "disclaimer", "label": "Disclaimer",
  "description": "Optional footnote or trademark text shown below the answer buttons." },
{ "component": "tab", "label": "Settings", "name": "settings" },
{ "component": "select", "name": "classes", "label": "Layout",
  "options": [{ "name": "Stacked (default)", "value": "" }, { "name": "Inline (side by side)", "value": "inline" }] },
{ "component": "text", "name": "cookieName", "label": "Cookie Name",
  "description": "Unique name for the browser cookie that persists the user's answer. No spaces or special characters." },
{ "component": "boolean", "name": "showReset", "value": false, "label": "Show Reset Button" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "resetLabel", "value": "Start over", "label": "Reset Button Label",
  "condition": { "===": [{ "var": "showReset" }, true] } }

// info-tree-item (repeatable answer child block):
{ "component": "tab", "label": "Answer", "name": "answer" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "answerLabel", "label": "Answer Button Label" },
{ "component": "richtext", "name": "answerContent", "label": "Answer Content",
  "description": "Rich text content revealed when this answer option is selected." },
{ "component": "select", "name": "itemVariant", "label": "Variation",
  "options": [{ "name": "Without CTA", "value": "without-cta" }, { "name": "With CTA", "value": "with-cta" }] },
{ "component": "text", "name": "ctaLabel", "label": "Button Label",
  "condition": { "===": [{ "var": "itemVariant" }, "with-cta"] } },
{ "component": "aem-content", "name": "ctaHref", "label": "Button Link",
  "condition": { "===": [{ "var": "itemVariant" }, "with-cta"] } }

Separates the treeName (an internal/authoring label, e.g. "ra-tnf-poll") from cookieName (the literal persisted-cookie key) — a real improvement over the source system's single infoTreeName field that served both purposes at once.

How the JS works (info-tree.js)

function sanitizeCookieName(name) {
  return name.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/g, '');
}
function setCookie(name, value, days) {
  const safeName = sanitizeCookieName(name);
  if (!safeName) return;
  document.cookie = `${safeName}=${encodeURIComponent(value)};max-age=${days * 86400};path=/;SameSite=Strict;Secure`;
}

Unlike the source system (which passes the author-typed name straight into cookieObj.createCookie with no sanitization), the EDS block strips any character outside [a-zA-Z0-9_-] from the cookie name before every read/write/delete, and sets the cookie with an explicit SameSite=Strict;Secure policy plus a numeric max-age instead of an expires-date string — both are real security/robustness improvements over the source component. extractConfig()/extractItems() support three different authored-content shapes (Universal-Editor data-aue-prop annotated rows, a plain two-column key/value row format, and a bare heuristic fallback keyed on text shape — e.g. treating any short unspaced line as the cookie name) — the same multi-format defensiveness already seen in image-compare.js. Each answer button toggles visibility via a hidden results panel keyed by data-answer-id, and the results container carries aria-live="polite" — closing the accessibility gap flagged in the source component's constraints above.

CSS

Base info-tree.css defines the stacked/inline layout toggle (classes: "inline") and the hidden/answered state classes (.info-tree-hidden, .is-answered); brand overrides live in abbvie/, botox/, and rinvoq/, each with a paired _info-tree.css/info-tree.css file following the same convention as other blocks' brand folders.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (info-tree)EDS (info-tree)
Answer→content linkageNaming-convention coupling: content is a sibling text resource named {choiceText}{index}Each answer is a proper repeatable info-tree-item child block carrying its content directly — no naming coupling
Per-answer CTANot supported — only the block-level reset button has a distinct actionEach answer item can independently be "With CTA" or "Without CTA", with its own label/link
Cookie name vs identifierSingle infoTreeName field serves as both label and literal cookie nameSeparate treeName (internal label) and cookieName (persisted key) fields
Cookie safetyAuthor-typed name passed directly to the cookie utility, no sanitization; no explicit SameSite/Secure attributes visibleName sanitized to [a-zA-Z0-9_-] before every cookie operation; cookie set with SameSite=Strict;Secure
Accessibility of revealed answerNo aria-live observed on the results containeraria-live="polite" on .info-tree-results; buttons grouped under role="group" with aria-labelledby pointing at the question
Disclaimer/footnote textNo dedicated disclaimer fieldNet-new disclaimer richtext field, rendered below the answer buttons

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtextfieldvia _common-properties.json#/common-prop includeRenamedReferenced by shared include, not declared inline; exact field name not directly visible in this file
customCssClassestextfieldvia _common-properties.json#/common-prop includeRenamedSame as above
infoTreeNametextfield (required, dual-purpose)treeName + cookieNameRenamed (split into two fields)Same combined purpose split apart: treeName is now a purely internal/authoring label, cookieName is the actual persisted cookie key — a real capability improvement, not just a rename
infoTreeInteractionId / reset-button interactionIdtextfield (disabled, auto-populated)—Not mappedNo analytics-interaction-id display fields in the EDS model
enableResetButtonCheckboxcheckboxshowResetRenamedSame purpose, boolean component type instead of a checkbox with a boolean value string
resetButtonTexttextfieldresetLabelRenamedSame purpose; EDS gives it a sensible default ("Start over") and a condition that only shows it when showReset is true
infoTreeImagefile/infoTreeImagefileReferencefileuploadimageRenamedSame purpose, reference field instead of fileupload+fileReference pair
imageTitletextfield—Not mappedNo separate image title field in the EDS model, only alt text
imageAltTexttextfieldimageAltRenamedSame purpose, new field name; also fixes the literal-"true"-alt-text bug since EDS JS sets img.alt directly from this field only when populated
headingTexttextfield—Not mappedNo separate heading field in _info-tree.json; the question richtext field can itself contain a heading element instead
questionTexttextfieldquestionRenamedSame purpose, upgraded from plain text to richtext (allows formatting/embedded elements)
answerOptions multifield (choiceText, interactionId)multifieldanswerLabel (info-tree-item)Renamed (different level)Same purpose (the answer button's label), now the field of a repeatable child block instead of a multifield row; the disabled analytics interactionId sub-field has no equivalent
sibling text resource named {choiceText}{index} (revealed content)resource (naming convention)answerContent (info-tree-item)RenamedSame purpose (content shown when this answer is picked), now a direct richtext field on the answer item instead of a same-named sibling resource — removes the fragile naming coupling
— (no equivalent CTA on answers)—itemVariant / ctaLabel / ctaHref (info-tree-item)Not mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new per-answer CTA capability; the source component's answers can only reveal static content, with no structured CTA field
sectionTitle / l10Title / analyticsTitle / mvaPrimary / mvaTitle / mvaTypeAndCategory / mvaSecondaryvarious (analytics)—Not mappedNo analytics/MVA fields anywhere in the EDS info-tree model, consistent with the analytics-field gap already documented across this batch
— (no source field)—disclaimerNot mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new footnote/trademark text field shown below the answer buttons; no equivalent in the source dialog
— (no source field)—classes (Layout: Stacked/Inline)Not mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new layout toggle; the source component has a single fixed vertical layout with no inline/side-by-side option

Link List / Brand Explorer Therapeutic Link List — Technical Details

What it does

link-list is a generic list-of-links component with two layouts — horizontal (single line, no title-link support) and vertical (multiple titled sub-lists, each with its own set of links) — each link individually configurable with icon, click-behavior (same tab/new tab/modal/hidden panel), and full analytics/MVA metadata. brand-explorer-therapeutic-link-list is a distinct, hidden (template-only, componentGroup=".hidden") variant used specifically inside a larger "Brand Explorer" navigation system — it renders one expandable therapeutic-area panel (heading + description + expand icon) containing a list of per-brand links, each optionally carrying its own sub-title and click-behavior, and is driven by its own dedicated Sling Model rather than the shared link-list model. The target implementation's linklist block generalizes both use cases into a single block with a "carousel" variant and a "child pages" auto-list mode.

Architecture — link-list

Author picks linkListLayout (horizontal | vertical, default horizontal)
         ↓
link-list.html dispatches:
  horizontal → link-list-horizontal.html   (single flat list, no title-link)
  vertical   → link-list-vertical.html     (LinkedListMultiModel — outer multifield
                                             of titled sub-lists, each with an inner
                                             multifield of individual links)
         ↓
Vertical: for each outer group → optional <h3> title-link, then <ul> of <li> links
Horizontal: flat <ul> of <li> links, no group titles

Architecture — brand-explorer-therapeutic-link-list

Author configures one therapeutic-area "panel": heading, description, expand icon
(mobile), and either a direct link/modal OR an expandable panel of links
         ↓
BrandExplorerGlobalTherapeuticModel.java resolves brandExplorerTherapeuticBeans
(per-brand link entries) + indicationAnalytics digitalData
         ↓
brand-explorer-therapeutic-link-list.html renders:
  .link-grouping-header-container (heading + expand button)
  <ul>
    <li> (heading itself is a link OR opens an expandable panel, mutually exclusive)
    <li> (data-sly-list over brandExplorerTherapeuticBeans — one per brand link)

Sling Model — LinkedListMultiModel.java (link-list) / BrandExplorerGlobalTherapeuticModel.java

LinkedListMultiModel resolves the nested outerFieldset/innerFieldset multifield structure into a list of outer groups, each carrying a list of inner link beans, plus a per-outer-group analytics string and a computed highlightCurrentLink flag consumed client-side. BrandExplorerGlobalTherapeuticModel is a distinct model (not shared with link-list) that resolves the panel's own heading/description/link fields plus a list of brandExplorerTherapeuticBeans (one per authored brand link), each with its own linkAnalyticsBean for click tracking.

Dialog — link-list

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Link List Identifier (Anchor ID)anchorIDtextfieldOverview tab
Link List LayoutlinkListLayoutselecthorizontal / vertical, show/hide driven
Highlight the current link on the pagehighlightCurrentLinkcheckbox (checked by default)matches on location.pathname only, no querystring/hash
Custom CSS Class(es) (outer group)customCssClassestextfieldLink List & Analytics tab, outer multifield
Display on Mobile BreakpointshowInMobilecheckboxoverrides a default mobile-hidden behavior
Link List TitlelistTitletextfieldvertical layout only
Link List URLlistUrlpathbrowsermakes the group title itself a link
Open List Link inlistLinkTypeselectSame Tab / New Tab/Window / Modal / Hidden Panel, default _blank
Modal / Hidden Panel IDmodalIDtextfield
Add an Icon to the Link List / Icon locationlinkIcon / linkIconPositionselect
Analytics Interaction ID / Analytics Title / Section Title / Localized Section TitleinteractionID, listComponentTitle, sectionTitle, l10sectionTitlevariousouter group-level analytics
MVA / MVA Title / MVA Type and Category / MVA Secondarymva, mvaName, mvaValue, mvaTiercheckbox/text/selectouter group-level
Link List Item (inner multifield)linkdetails → innerFieldset: customCssClasses, linkTitle, linkUrl, linkBehaviour, modalID, linkIcon, linkIconPosition, interactionID, componentTitle, sectionTitle, l10sectionTitle, mva, mvaName, mvaValue, mvaTiermultifieldsame field shape as the outer group, repeated per individual link

Dialog — brand-explorer-therapeutic-link-list (key fields)

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Indication Custom CSS ClassesindicationCustomCssClassestextfield
Indication Heading / DescriptionindicatonHeading (note real typo, missing "i") / indicationDesctextfield / textarea
Expand Icon (Mobile)expandIconselect (datasource ce-link-icon)
Link URL / Modal ID / Link TypelinkUrl, modalID, linkTypepathbrowser/textfield/selectif set, the heading itself becomes a direct link instead of an expandable panel

(Full field surface also includes a per-brand-link sub-structure analogous to link-list's inner multifield, not exhaustively enumerated above.)

HTL — key DOM structure (link-list-vertical.html)

<sly data-sly-use.outerField="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.LinkedListMultiModel">
  <sly data-sly-list.outerMultiList="${outerField.linkedListOuterListDetails}">
    <div class="abbv-link-list ${outerMultiList.customCssClasses}"
         data-highlight-current="${outerField.highlightCurrentLink}">
      <h3>
        <sly data-sly-call="${anchorTemplate.anchor @ linkBehaviour=outerMultiList.listLinkType,
             url=linkhelper.linkFormatted, linkTitle=outerMultiList.listTitle, ...}"/>
      </h3>
      <ul>
        <sly data-sly-list.innerMultiList="${outerMultiList.listDetails}">
          <li> ... individual link, icon, analytics ... </li>
        </sly>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </sly>
</sly>

CSS / Clientlib

link-list ships one clientlib category bundling both CSS and JS. brand-explorer-therapeutic-link-list has no dedicated clientlib folder of its own — it is consumed as a child resource inside a larger brand-explorer navigation surface and relies on that parent's clientlibs.

Important Constraints

  • Horizontal layout explicitly does not support a title-link (dialog help text states this outright) — an intentional, documented feature gap between the two layouts, not a bug.
  • highlightCurrentLink matches only on location.pathname, ignoring querystring/hash — a link differing only by querystring will not be highlighted even if functionally "the current page."
  • brand-explorer-therapeutic-link-list has a real field-name typo (indicatonHeading) baked into the persisted property name — any migration/mapping script must account for the misspelling verbatim, not the corrected spelling.
  • Default click-behavior for both outer-group and inner links is _blank (opens in a new tab/window) — an unusual default for what is likely often in-site navigation.

Linklist Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/linklist/. It unifies the horizontal/vertical layout split, the icon/link/detailed variants, and the brand-explorer panel concept into one block with a variant select (including a net-new "Carousel" mode) and a linkSource switch that can either read author-curated linklist-item child blocks or auto-generate the list from the site index under a given parent page — a capability with no equivalent at all in either source component.

File Structure

blocks/linklist/
├── linklist.js
├── linklist.css
├── _linklist.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── linklist.css
│   ├── _linklist.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
└── rinvoq/

Authoring Model (_linklist.json)

// linklist (container):
{ "component": "select", "name": "variant", "value": "standard", "label": "Variant",
  "options": [
    { "name": "Standard", "value": "standard" },
    { "name": "Rows with Arrows", "value": "rows-with-arrows" },
    { "name": "Icons", "value": "icons" },
    { "name": "Footer - primary", "value": "footer-primary" },
    { "name": "Footer - legal", "value": "footer-legal" },
    { "name": "Detailed list", "value": "detailed-list" },
    { "name": "Carousel", "value": "carousel" }
  ] },
{ "component": "select", "name": "linkSource", "value": "custom", "label": "Link Source",
  "description": "Child Pages: auto-list from site index. Icons / Custom: add Link List Item blocks.",
  "options": [
    { "name": "Child Pages", "value": "child-pages" },
    { "name": "Icons", "value": "icons" },
    { "name": "Custom", "value": "custom" }
  ] },
// linkSource === "child-pages" reveals: parentPage, childDepth, excludeCurrentPage,
// enableDescription, enableTags, enableSubtitle, enableDate, orderBy, sortOrder, maxItems, layout
{ "component": "text", "name": "fontIcon", "label": "Icon",
  "description": "Icon applied to every link when Variant is Icons. Works for all link sources — the only icon option when using Child Pages." },
{ "component": "tab", "label": "Accessibility", "name": "tab-accessibility-linklist" },
{ "...": "../../models/_language.json#/language-field" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "ariaLabel", "label": "ARIA Label",
  "description": "Accessible name for the link list landmark (nav)." }

// linklist-item (repeatable child block):
{ "component": "boolean", "name": "cookieConsentLink", "label": "Cookie Consent Link",
  "description": "When enabled, opens cookie consent instead of navigating. Link and tab fields below are ignored." },
{ "component": "aem-content", "name": "link", "label": "Link" },
{ "component": "boolean", "name": "openInNewTab", "label": "Open in a new tab" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "linkText", "label": "Link Text" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "subtitle", "label": "Subtitle" },
{ "component": "richtext", "name": "description", "label": "Description" },
{ "component": "aem-tag", "name": "categoryTags", "label": "Category Tags" },
{ "component": "select", "name": "iconType", "value": "none", "label": "Icon Type",
  "options": [{ "name": "None", "value": "none" }, { "name": "Icon Font", "value": "font" }, { "name": "Image", "value": "image" }] },
{ "component": "boolean", "name": "enableConfirmationModal", "label": "Enable Confirmation Modal",
  "description": "Prompts users with a modal before navigation (useful for warnings or external links)." },
{ "component": "select", "name": "confirmationModalType", "value": "standard", "label": "Type of confirmation modal",
  "options": [{ "name": "Standard", "value": "standard" }, { "name": "Exit modal", "value": "exit-modal" }] }

How the JS works (linklist.js)

import indexUtils from '../../scripts/index-utils.js';
const INDEX_CACHE_KEY = 'abbvie-index-data';
const carouselROMap = new WeakMap();

// linkSource === "child-pages": resolves links from the cached flat site index
await indexUtils.getIndexData();
// falls back to a direct fetch('/query-index-en.json') if the cache is unavailable,
// and reports a "no-index" diagnostic reason if the index truly can't be loaded

// variant === "carousel": builds prev/next controls, observes container size via
// ResizeObserver (tracked per-block in carouselROMap for cleanup on re-decoration)
if (variant === 'carousel' && list.children.length > 0) {
  const prev = document.createElement('button');
  prev.className = 'linklist-carousel-prev';
  ...
  const ro = new ResizeObserver(...);
  carouselROMap.set(block, ro);
}

This confirms the manifest's characterization precisely: the block reads a cached flat site index keyed abbvie-index-data to auto-generate a list of links (the "child pages" mode), and can optionally render that list — or an author-curated one — as a carousel. Diagnostic reason codes (e.g. 'no-index') surface a helpful in-block message when the index JSON hasn't loaded, rather than failing silently.

CSS

Base linklist.css implements all seven variant layouts plus carousel controls; brand folders (abbvie/, botox/, rinvoq/) supply paired _linklist.css/linklist.css overrides following the established base+brand convention.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (link-list / brand-explorer-therapeutic-link-list)EDS (linklist)
Link sourceAlways author-curated (multifield entries or per-brand link beans)Can auto-generate from the site index under a parent page (linkSource: child-pages), with depth/sort/count controls — no equivalent in either source component
Layout variantsTwo fixed layouts (horizontal/vertical) plus a separate hidden brand-explorer panel componentSeven variants (standard, rows-with-arrows, icons, footer-primary, footer-legal, detailed-list, carousel) unified in one block
Confirmation before navigationNot supported — link click behavior is same-tab/new-tab/modal/hidden-panel onlyNet-new enableConfirmationModal per item, with a distinct "exit modal" style for leaving-site warnings
Cookie-consent triggerFooter's linkbehaviour field has a "cookie-consent-options" value (per the footer component's own dialog, not link-list's)Dedicated cookieConsentLink boolean directly on linklist-item
Highlight current pageGlobal per-group checkbox, pathname-only matchingNo equivalent highlight-current-link field found in _linklist.json

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtextfieldidRenamedSame purpose (deep-link anchor), shorter field name
linkListLayout (horizontal/vertical)selectvariantRenamedLoosely equivalent (controls overall presentation), expanded from a 2-option layout select into a 7-option variant select covering far more visual patterns
highlightCurrentLinkcheckbox—Not mappedNo current-page-highlight field or behavior found in _linklist.json or referenced in the JS excerpt reviewed
outer group customCssClassestextfieldcustomClassRenamedSame purpose, block-level instead of per-outer-group (EDS has no separate outer-group concept outside the child-pages parent path)
showInMobilecheckbox—Not mappedNo mobile-visibility toggle in the EDS model
listTitle / listUrl / listLinkTypetextfield/pathbrowser/select—Not mappedNo group-title-as-link concept in the flat linklist/linklist-item model; a heading would need to be authored separately (e.g. in a preceding richtext block) rather than as a field on the list itself
outer/inner modalIDtextfieldmodalId (on linklist-item, confirmation-modal context)RenamedLoosely equivalent purpose (modal-triggering), but repurposed specifically for the new confirmation-modal flow rather than a general modal/hidden-panel link type
linkIcon / linkIconPositionselect (datasource)fontIcon (linklist) / iconType+fontIcon+imageIcon+iconPosition (linklist-item)RenamedSame general purpose (icon on the link), expanded to support both font-icon and image-icon sources per item, plus a single shared icon for the whole list in Icons variant
outer/inner interactionID / listComponentTitle/componentTitle / sectionTitle / l10sectionTitlevarious (analytics)—Not mappedNo analytics/tagging fields anywhere in the EDS linklist model, consistent with the analytics-field gap already documented across this batch
outer/inner mva / mvaName / mvaValue / mvaTiercheckbox/text/select—Not mappedSame analytics-field gap as above
inner linkTitletextfieldlinkText (linklist-item)RenamedSame purpose, new field name; also gains a "may default to target page title when blank" behavior not present in the source field
inner linkUrlpathbrowserlink (linklist-item)RenamedSame purpose, shorter field name
inner linkBehaviourselectopenInNewTab (linklist-item)RenamedSame general purpose (control target/tab behavior), reduced from a 4-option select (same tab/new tab/modal/hidden panel) to a boolean new-tab toggle — modal/hidden-panel behavior is now handled separately via enableConfirmationModal
inner customCssClassestextfieldcustomClass (linklist-item)RenamedSame purpose, new field name
brand-explorer: indicationCustomCssClasses / indicatonHeading / indicationDesc / expandIconvarious—Not mappedNo dedicated expandable-panel-with-heading concept survives in the flat EDS linklist model; an equivalent panel would need to be composed from other blocks (e.g. an accordion) rather than authored as linklist fields
— (no source field)—linkSource (Child Pages / Icons / Custom)Not mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new auto-list-from-site-index capability with no equivalent in either source component
— (no source field)—parentPage / childDepth / excludeCurrentPage / enableDescription / enableTags / enableSubtitle / enableDate / orderBy / sortOrder / maxItems / layoutNot mapped (EDS-native addition)Entire child-pages auto-list configuration surface is net-new
— (no source field)—ariaLabel (linklist and linklist-item)Not mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new accessible-name fields; no equivalent ARIA-label authoring in either source component's dialog
— (no source field)—categoryTags (linklist-item)Not mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new taxonomy tagging field per item
— (no source field)—cookieConsentLink (linklist-item)Not mapped (EDS-native addition, loosely inspired by footer's own field)Not present on link-list itself — the source system's closest analog lives on the separate footer component's linkbehaviour field, not on link-list

Modal / Hidden Panel — Technical Details

What it does

A single, richly-configurable component that renders authored content (referenced from another page/fragment) inside one of several overlay presentations: a standard centered modal, an in-place "hidden panel" (no dimming/repositioning by default), and four presentation variants kept only "for backward compatibility" per an explicit code comment (exit-modal, small-exit-modal, information-modal, image-modal). It supports force-open behavior (always, once via cookie, once via session, or specifically for an indication-selection flow), a cookie expiration override, a required accessible description, and an "interact to close" mode that disables the default click-mask-to-close behavior.

Architecture

Author sets modalId (required, regex-validated), referencePath (content to display),
modalType (modal | hidden-panel | exit-modal | small-exit-modal | information-modal |
image-modal), labelariadescription (required, for screen readers)
         ↓
ModalModel.java resolves referenceContentPath from referencePath, referenceEpassNum
(legal/compliance number from the referenced page), MRP (marketing-reference-page)
debug attributes gated on a `mrp` request param + non-prod check, digitalData for
close-button analytics, isPublish flag
         ↓
modal.html branches on properties.modalType:
  modal            → .abbv-modal[data-id][data-load-open][data-cookie-expire]
  hidden-panel     → .abbv-hidden-panel[data-disable-scroll] (no data-load-open — panels
                      are trigger-only, never force-opened)
  exit-modal / small-exit-modal / information-modal / image-modal →
                      legacy markup variants, explicitly marked
                      "for backward compatibility only" in an HTL comment
         ↓
Trigger elsewhere on the page (e.g. a CTA/link-list item with linkType=modal,
matching modalID) opens/closes this component via shared client JS

Sling Model — ModalModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {SlingHttpServletRequest.class})
public class ModalModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    public static final String COMMONELEMENTS = "common-elements";

    @Self @Via("resource") private Resource resource;
    @ValueMapValue @Default(values = "") @Optional private String referencePath;
    @Inject private Page currentPage;
    @Inject @Source("sling-object") private ResourceResolver resourceResolver;
    @Inject @Source("osgi-services") private SlingSettingsService slingSettingsService;
    @ValueMapValue @Default(values = "") @Optional private String modalType;
    // ... resolves referenceContentPath (the actual resource rendered inside the
    //     overlay), referenceEpassNum (legal number pulled from the referenced page's
    //     properties), referenceMrpPageTileNum/referenceMrpPageName/referenceMrpSiteDomain
    //     (debug attributes gated on request param "mrp" + isNonProd), isPublish/isNonProd
    //     flags, digitalData (close-button click analytics), interactToCloseModal
}

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Modal / Hidden Panel IDmodalIdtextfield (required, regex-validated eaem.regex.val)the linkage key other components' trigger fields reference
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestextfield
Modal / Hidden Panel PathreferencePathpathbrowser (validated referencePath.match.val)the content to display inside the overlay
TypemodalTypeselect (show/hide)Modal / Hidden Panel / Exit Modal / Small Exit Modal / Information Modal / Image Modal — in-dialog note: "Indication Modal will rely on the global cookie - indication_name"
Modal Descriptionlabelariadescriptiontextfield (required)explicitly required for screen-reader support
Disable Reposition on Hidden Panel ClickdisableScrollcheckboxHidden Panel type only
Forced Modal TypeforcedModalselectModal type only — blank / "Force the Modal Open Always" / "once" (cookie) / "onceSession" (session) / "indicationSelector"
(interact-to-close description text)—info text"User must interact with a modal to close it"
(unlabeled checkbox)interactToCloseModalcheckboxreal field has an empty text="" attribute — the label text lives in the separate info-text field above it instead of on the checkbox itself
Cookie Expiration DayscookieExpirationnumberfield (min 0, default 365 per field description)Modal type only
Modal Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtextfield (disabled)auto-populated display

Single-tab dialog with three in-dialog guidance text blocks, including an explicit naming convention instruction ("Please name every ACTIVE modal as follows: modal type + title description") and an operational instruction to deactivate unused modals — governance concerns addressed via author instructions rather than dialog validation.

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-test="${properties.modalType == 'modal'}">
  <div class="abbv-modal ${properties.modalId} ${properties.customCssClasses} ${modalClass.interactToCloseModal ? 'js-must-interact' : ''}"
       data-id="${properties.modalId}"
       data-load-open="${modalClass.isPublish ? properties.forcedModal : ''}"
       data-cookie-expire="${properties.cookieExpiration}"
       aria-modal="true" role="dialog" tabindex="-1" aria-hidden="false"
       aria-label="${properties.labelariadescription}">
    <div class="abbv-modal-content-container">
      <span class="abbv-modal-close abbv-icon-close i-a" role="button" aria-label="close" tabindex="0"
            data-digitaldata='${modalClass.digitalData}'></span>
      <div class="abbv-modal-content">
        <div class="abbv-animation-loading"></div>
        <div data-sly-resource="${modalClass.referenceContentPath}" data-sly-unwrap></div>
        <p class="abbv-legal-number">${modalClass.referenceEpassNum}</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</sly>

<!-- hidden-panel: no data-load-open (panels are trigger-only) -->
<sly data-sly-test="${properties.modalType == 'hidden-panel'}">
  <div class="abbv-hidden-panel ${properties.modalId} ${properties.customCssClasses}"
       data-id="${properties.modalId}" data-disable-scroll="${properties.disableScroll ? properties.disableScroll : 'false'}"
       aria-modal="true" role="dialog" tabindex="-1" aria-hidden="false"
       aria-label="${properties.labelariadescription}">
    ...
  </div>
</sly>

The four "backward compatibility only" variants (exit-modal, small-exit-modal, information-modal, image-modal) are explicitly bracketed in the file with <!--/* START/END: These are the modals for backward compatibility only */--> HTL comments — a rare case of the source repository stating its own deprecation intent directly in code rather than only in a picklist label (contrast with image-text's "Swap Images - Deprecated" option, which stays selectable with no such comment).

CSS / Clientlib

A base clientlibs category (CSS+JS) plus an author-only clientlibsauth clientlib containing regex-validator.js — the client-side implementation of the dialog's eaem.regex.val/referencePath.match.val field validators.

Important Constraints

  • Five of six modalType values are described in code comments as legacy/backward-compatibility-only, yet remain fully selectable in the dialog with no deprecation warning shown to authors at author time.
  • The MRP (marketing reference page) debug attributes are only emitted when a mrp=true request parameter is present AND the environment is detected as non-production — a debug feature gated by URL parameter, not a proper admin toggle.
  • interactToCloseModal's checkbox has an empty text="" attribute, relying on an adjacent, separately-defined info-text field for its actual label — a real dialog-authoring inconsistency, not a documentation choice.
  • The "Indication Modal" behavior mentioned in the modalType field description depends on a global indication_name cookie set elsewhere (by the header's indication-swap logic), an implicit cross-component dependency not otherwise declared in this dialog.

Modal Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/modal/. It consolidates all six source modalType values plus the forcedModal force-open sub-options into a single classes multiselect, and replaces the source system's referencePath (an AEM content-tree page reference) with a fragmentPath pointing at a target-implementation content fragment.

File Structure

blocks/modal/
├── modal.js
├── modal.css
├── _modal.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── modal.css
│   ├── _modal.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
├── linzess/
├── mavyret/
├── rinvoq/
├── rinvoq-hcp/
├── skyrizi-hcp/
└── venclexta/

Authoring Model (_modal.json)

{
  "component": "multiselect", "name": "classes", "label": "Variant",
  "options": [
    { "name": "Standard (default)", "value": "" },
    { "name": "Panel — slide-in from right", "value": "panel" },
    { "name": "Exit — triggered on mouse leave", "value": "exit" },
    { "name": "Exit Small", "value": "exit-small" },
    { "name": "Small — narrow dialog", "value": "small" },
    { "name": "Media — wide, no padding", "value": "media" },
    { "name": "Image — lightbox", "value": "image" },
    { "name": "Information — accent border", "value": "information" },
    { "name": "Show Once (cookie)", "value": "once" },
    { "name": "Show Once (session)", "value": "once-session" },
    { "name": "Force Open — no dismiss", "value": "force" }
  ]
},
{ "component": "text", "name": "modalId", "label": "Modal ID", "required": true,
  "description": "Unique slug — triggers reference this via data-modal-id attribute" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "fragmentPath", "label": "Fragment Path", "required": true,
  "description": "Path to the fragment that contains modal content" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "openLabel", "value": "Open modal", "label": "Trigger Button Label",
  "description": "Label for the trigger button (not used for exit/once/force variants)" }

How the JS works (modal.js)

/*
 *   exit         — triggered by mouseleave on document (desktop)
 *   once         — show only once per user (persistent cookie)
 *   once-session — show once per session (sessionStorage)
 */

function setCookie(name, value, days) {
  const n = ...; const v = ...;
  const d = new Date(); d.setTime(d.getTime() + days * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
  document.cookie = `${n}=${v};expires=${d.toUTCString()};path=/;SameSite=Lax`;
}

function hasSeenModal(modalId, variants) {
  if (variants.includes('once')) return getCookie(`modal-seen-${modalId}`) === '1';
  if (variants.includes('once-session')) {
    return sessionStorage.getItem(`modal-seen-${modalId}`) === '1';
  }
  return false;
}

function registerExitIntent(trigger, variants) {
  document.addEventListener('mouseleave', (e) => { ... });
}

This is a direct, cleaner re-implementation of the source system's dual persistence mechanism: forcedModal: "once" (cookie) and forcedModal: "onceSession" (session) become the once and once-session classes values respectively, each now backed by an explicit, purpose-named helper function rather than a single overloaded data-load-open/data-cookie-expire attribute pair interpreted by shared jQuery modal-init code. The mouse-leave "exit" trigger (a real DOM mouseleave listener on document) is the direct analog of the source system's exit-modal/small-exit-modal types. Focus management (getFocusable()) and a real role="dialog"/aria-modal="true" overlay (getOverlay()) are implemented directly in the shared openModal()/closeModal() functions rather than per-variant markup blocks.

CSS

Base modal.css implements all eleven classes variants (panel slide-in, exit, exit-small, small, media, image-lightbox, information accent border, plus the three force/persistence variants which are behavioral, not visual). Brand folders (abbvie/, botox/, linzess/, mavyret/, rinvoq/, rinvoq-hcp/, skyrizi-hcp/, venclexta/) each carry a paired _modal.css/modal.css override, the widest brand-folder coverage of any block in this batch alongside header/hero.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (modal)EDS (modal)
Variant selectionTwo separate fields (modalType for presentation, forcedModal for force-open behavior), plus 4 types explicitly marked legacy in code commentsSingle classes multiselect combining presentation and force-open/persistence behavior into one field, with no distinct "legacy" options carried forward
Content sourcereferencePath — a pathbrowser into the AEM content treefragmentPath — a path to a content fragment in the target implementation, not a full page reference
Persistence mechanismforcedModal=once uses a cookie read/written by shared jQuery init code, keyed by modalId and a configurable cookieExpirationonce uses a fixed-format cookie (modal-seen-{modalId}) via a small dedicated setCookie/getCookie pair; no separate cookie-expiration-days field observed in the model
Session-only persistenceNot clearly modeled as a distinct type in the reviewed dialog — closest is forcedModal=onceSessiononce-session classes value backed explicitly by sessionStorage
Legal/compliance number displayreferenceEpassNum auto-pulled from the referenced page and rendered as .abbv-legal-numberNo epass/legal-number field or rendering found in _modal.json; presumably expected to live inside the fragment content itself
MRP debug attributesdata-mrp-page-tile-num/-page-name/-site-domain, gated by request param + non-prod checkNo equivalent debug-attribute mechanism found in modal.js

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
modalIdtextfield (required, regex-validated)modalIdSame nameIdentical purpose and field name; validation pattern not directly comparable without the referenced regex definitions
customCssClassestextfield—Not mappedNo free-text custom class field; the fixed classes multiselect is the only class-driving mechanism
referencePathpathbrowser (validated)fragmentPathRenamedSame general purpose (content to display), narrowed from "any content-tree page" to specifically a content fragment path
modalType (modal / hidden-panel / exit-modal / small-exit-modal / information-modal / image-modal)selectclasses (panel / exit / exit-small / small / media / image / information values)RenamedSame purpose, consolidated into one multiselect; "hidden-panel" maps loosely to the "panel" (slide-in) value, and "modal" (standard) maps to the default empty-string variant
labelariadescriptiontextfield (required)—Not mappedNo dedicated accessible-description field in _modal.json; presumably the fragment's own content supplies the accessible name, or it is generated from openLabel/trigger context instead
disableScrollcheckbox—Not mappedNo scroll-lock-disable field in the EDS model
forcedModal (blank / true / once / onceSession / indicationSelector)selectclasses (once / once-session / force values)RenamedSame general purpose (force-open behavior), folded into the same classes field as the presentation type rather than a separate select; the source's specific "indicationSelector" sub-type has no distinct EDS equivalent — see below
interactToCloseModalcheckbox—Not mappedNo must-interact-to-close toggle found in _modal.json; if present, it would need to be inferred from one of the classes values (e.g. force) rather than an independent field
cookieExpirationnumberfield (default 365)—Not mappedNo configurable expiration-days field; modal.js's setCookie() takes a days parameter in code but there is no corresponding authorable field in the model to set it per-instance
interactionIdtextfield (disabled, auto-populated)—Not mappedNo analytics-interaction-id display field, consistent with the analytics-field gap already documented across this batch
— (no source field)—openLabelNot mapped (EDS-native addition)Net-new trigger-button label field; the source system's triggers are authored entirely on the separate calling component (e.g. link-list/image-text's own CTA fields), with no label field on the modal component itself
"Indication Modal" behavior (via modalType field description, backed by global indication_name cookie)select value + external cookie dependency—Not mappedNo indication-selector-specific variant or cookie dependency found in the EDS modal model or JS; this cross-component behavior does not appear to have been carried forward

Navigation Content — Technical Details

What it does

navigation-content is a single, multi-purpose EDS block that stands in for three separate source-system components: headerlogo (the site logo plus optional "site indication" badge link), primary-navigation (top-level nav items with an optional hover mega-menu of section headings and links), and utility-navigation (the small link/menu strip that sits above the main header, e.g. locale switcher, phone number, "Find a location"). In the target implementation these three authoring surfaces are collapsed into one block whose rendered behavior is selected at author time via a single classes field acting as a type-class allowlist (logo, language-links, search, utility-nav, eyebrow, floating-isi, cta-group, or empty string for a plain nav item/mega-menu). The decorate function reads whichever of those classes is present on the block and stamps it into data-type so CSS and any later JS can branch on it.

Architecture

Source system (three distinct components, one dialog each):
  headerlogo            -> HeaderLogoModel.java      -> headerlogo.html
  primary-navigation     -> PrimaryNavigationItemModel.java -> primary-navigation.html
  utility-navigation     -> UtilityNav.java          -> utility-navigation.html
         |
         | (all three collapse into one EDS block + one field model)
         v
Target implementation:
  Universal Editor author picks "Navigation Content Type" (classes select)
         |
         v
  block gets CSS class matching the type (logo / language-links / search /
  utility-nav / eyebrow / floating-isi / cta-group / "" for nav item)
         |
         v
  navigation-content.js decorate(block):
    - BLOCK_TYPES.find() matches the block's class list against the
      known type-class allowlist, sets block.dataset.type
    - promotes preview heading/link paragraphs to marker classes for
      the (dev-only) preview styling
    - applyCommonProps(block) applies common id/class/lang properties

Sling Models — three separate classes

HeaderLogoModel.java (extends AbstractComponentModel) — resolves the logo image path/rendition, computes image height/width from DAM metadata, builds two separate analytics "digital data" JSON blobs (one for the logo link, one for the optional site-indication badge), and self-writes interaction IDs back into the JCR on first author-mode render:

@Model(adaptables = { SlingHttpServletRequest.class, Resource.class },
       defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class HeaderLogoModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    @Inject @Via("resource") private String primaryimgalttext;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Named("primarylogo/fileReference") private String logoImage;
    @Inject @Via("resource") private String linkurl;
    @Inject @Via("resource") private String indicationRequired; // drives the optional badge
    ...
    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        logoImage = CommonUtility.getImagePath(logoImage, resourceResolver, resource, primarylogoRendition);
        setImageHeightAndWidth();
        setInteractionID(interactionId, INTERACTION_ID, 1);
        setInteractionID(indicationInteractionId, INDICATION_INTERACTION_ID, 2);
        logoDigitalData = getLogoAnalyticsDigitalData();
        indicationDigitalData = getIndicationAnalyticsDigitalData();
    }
}

PrimaryNavigationItemModel.java (extends AbstractComponentModel) — the most structurally complex of the three. A single nav item has a top-level link (mainMenuTitle/mainMenuURL), and optionally a nested multifield of menu sections (subMenuSectionset, each entry a JSON blob parsed with Gson), each of which optionally has its own nested multifield of links (subMenuLinksFieldset). That is a real three-level nesting: nav item → section → link. Fallback navigation-title logic walks the linked page's navigationTitle / pageTitle / title properties and honors isHideInNav. "Current page" highlighting is computed by comparing currentPage.getPath() against each level's URL:

@Model(adaptables = {SlingHttpServletRequest.class, Resource.class})
public class PrimaryNavigationItemModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String mainMenuTitle;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String mainMenuURL;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private List<String> subMenuSectionset; // JSON strings, one per section
    private List<OuterListLinkDetailsBean> subMenuSectionDetailBeans;          // parsed sections
    // OuterListLinkDetailsBean.listDetails -> List<NestedListLinkDetailsBean>   // parsed links per section

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        if (StringUtils.isNotBlank(mainMenuURL)) {
            this.mainMenuTitle = getNavigationItemProperties(mainMenuURL, mainMenuTitle); // fallback chain
        }
        if (subMenuSectionset != null) {
            for (int i = 0; i < subMenuSectionset.size(); i++) {
                getSubMenuSectionDetailsBean(subMenuSectionset.get(i), i); // parses nested JSON per section
            }
        }
        setMainMenuAnalyticsData();
    }
}

UtilityNav.java (extends AbstractComponentModel) — a flat multifield of link items (utilitynavlinks, JSON strings parsed with Gson into UtilityNavBean). Renders either a bare link (single item, no title) or a dropdown menu (multiple items under one navItemTitle). Also derives an accessible ariaLabelLinkTitle by stripping HTML from the rich-text link title via Jsoup:

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class})
public class UtilityNav extends AbstractComponentModel {
    @Inject @Optional private List<String> utilitynavlinks;
    @Inject @Optional private String navItemTitle;
    private List<UtilityNavBean> utilityNavBeans;
    private int utilityNavItemsSize;

    public void setAriaLabelLinkTitle(UtilityNavBean bean) {
        Document doc = Jsoup.parse(bean.getLinkTitle());
        bean.setAriaLabelLinkTitle(doc.text());
    }
}

Dialogs

Nested multifield sectionlinkdetails (./subMenuLinksFieldset) — one level deeper, per link inside a section:
Custom CSS Class(es)subMenuLinkCustomCsstextfieldper link

Open Link insubMenuLinkBehaviourselectper link

Analytics TitlesubMenuAnalyticsTitletextfieldper link

Utility Navigation dialog — single tab, top-level fields plus one flat multifield:

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestextfield
Utility Navigation Menu TitlenavItemTitletextfieldif set, links render as a dropdown under this title
Show this item on Mobile and Tablet devicesshowinmobilecheckbox
Multifield navigationlinks (./utilitynavlinks), per link:
Utility Navigation Item Titlelinktitletextfieldper link
Utility Navigation Item URLlinkurlpathbrowserper link
Open Utility Navigation Link inlinkclickbehaviourselectper link
Modal IDmodalIDtextfieldper link
Add an Icon / Icon Positionlinkicon, linkIconPositionselectper link
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIDtextfield (disabled)per link
Analytics TitleanalyticsTitletextfieldper link
Section Title for AnalyticscomponentTitletextfieldper link
Localized Section Title for AnalyticslocalizedAnalyticsTitletextfieldper link
MVA / MVA Title / MVA Value / MVA SecondaryanalyticsEnableMVATier, mvaTitle, analyticsMVAVal, analyticsMVATiervariousper link

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<!-- headerlogo.html: single logo anchor + optional indication anchor -->
<sly data-sly-use.headerLogoClass="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.HeaderLogoModel"></sly>
<sly data-sly-call="${anchorTemplate.anchorForLogo @ ... imgFile=headerLogoClass.logoImage,
     imgAltTxt=headerLogoClass.PrimaryImgAltText, imgHeight=headerLogoClass.logoHeight,
     imgWidth=headerLogoClass.logoWidth}"/>
<sly data-sly-test="${properties.indicationRequired}">
  <!-- second, independent anchor for the site-indication badge -->
</sly>

<!-- primary-navigation.html: single link, or link + submenu -->
<sly data-sly-test="${navModel.subMenuSectionDetailBeans && navModel.isMainMenuPageEnabled}">
  <!-- top link rendered with 'abbv-has-submenu' class + caret span -->
</sly>
<div class="abbv-header-primary-navigation-submenu abbv-submenu">
  <sly data-sly-list.subMenuSectionBean="${navModel.subMenuSectionDetailBeans}">
    <ul role="menu">
      <li class="abbv-header-primary-navigation-submenu-title ..."><!-- section heading link --></li>
      <sly data-sly-list.subMenuBean="${subMenuSectionBean.listDetails}">
        <li role="none"><!-- one <a> per link inside the section --></li>
      </sly>
    </ul>
  </sly>
</div>

<!-- utility-navigation.html: bare link (1 item) or menu (>1 item) -->
<div data-sly-test="${utilityNavItems.utilityNavItemsSize == 1}"><!-- single <a> --></div>
<div data-sly-test="${utilityNavItems.utilityNavItemsSize > 1}">
  <a role="menuitem" aria-expanded="false" class="... abbv-has-submenu ...">${properties.navItemTitle}</a>
  <div class="abbv-header-utility-navigation-submenu abbv-submenu"><ul role="menu">...</ul></div>
</div>

CSS

CSS for all three source components lives in shared header clientlibs, not per-component stylesheets local to these three folders (only headerlogo ships its own clientlib/ reference; the nav components rely on the parent header markup structure classes such as abbv-has-submenu, abbv-submenu, nav-tier1, sm-display seen above in the HTL).

Important Constraints (source system)

  • Interaction IDs for analytics are generated and written back into the JCR from the Sling Model on first non-publish render (setInteractionID/setInteractionIdInProperty) — a side-effecting read that mutates content, only suppressed in publish run mode.
  • Primary navigation's per-item digital-data analytics blob is recomputed on every request from the multifield JSON, including deep MVA (Most Valued Action) tier logic duplicated at all three nesting levels (item, section, link).
  • Navigation title has a fallback chain (Navigation Title → Page Title → Title) resolved live against the linked page's properties at render time, meaning a change to a target page's title can silently change nav item text even if the nav item itself is never re-saved.
  • The "current page" active-state check in primary navigation only compares full-depth pages at depth 3 for the root path special-case (mainMenuURL.equals("/") && currentPageDepth == 3), a hard-coded assumption about site content depth.

EDS Implementation — What's already built

The target block is implemented as a single reusable block, navigation-content, driven entirely by one field model with a classes select acting as a variant switch, plus common-properties fields (blockId, classes_commonCustomClass, language) shared across all EDS blocks. It is authored once per "slot" (logo, one nav item, one utility link group, etc.) and repeated as needed in the header's block tree, rather than being three separate component types.

File Structure

blocks/navigation-content/
├── navigation-content.js
├── navigation-content.css
├── _navigation-content.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── navigation-content.css
│   ├── _navigation-content.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
│   ├── navigation-content.css
│   ├── _navigation-content.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── linzess/
    ├── navigation-content.css
    ├── _navigation-content.css
    └── block-config.js

Authoring Model (_navigation-content.json)

Single classes select field with options logo, "" (Navigation Item / mega-menu), language-links, search, utility-nav, eyebrow, floating-isi, cta-group. Every other field is conditionally shown based on that value, for example:

{
  "component": "select",
  "name": "classes",
  "value": "",
  "label": "Navigation Content Type",
  "options": [
    { "name": "Logo", "value": "logo" },
    { "name": "Navigation Item", "value": "" },
    { "name": "Language/Links", "value": "language-links" },
    { "name": "Search", "value": "search" },
    { "name": "Utility Nav", "value": "utility-nav" },
    { "name": "Eyebrow Bar", "value": "eyebrow" },
    { "name": "Floating ISI (HCP compliance)", "value": "floating-isi" },
    { "name": "CTA Group (Register / Sign In)", "value": "cta-group" }
  ]
}

The "Navigation Item" (empty-string) variant carries a flat mega-menu shape — megamenutitle, megamenudescription, megamenu_link/megamenu_linkText, megamenudashboardlinks, megamenucardtitle/megamenucardcontent/ megamenucard_link/megamenucard_linkText, and a single megamenudashboardcard reference with a dashboardCardType (dark/light) toggle. It has no multifield and no nesting at all. The utility-nav variant collapses the source's per-link multifield into one content_utilityItems richtext field, documented as "one row per item: [type | label | href | options]" — a free-text convention rather than structured sub-fields.

How the JS works (navigation-content.js)

The real, already-shipped decorate() is intentionally thin:

const BLOCK_TYPES = [
  'logo', 'language-links', 'search', 'utility-nav',
  'eyebrow', 'floating-isi', 'cta-group',
];

export default function decorate(block) {
  const typeClass = BLOCK_TYPES.find((t) => block.classList.contains(t));
  block.dataset.type = typeClass || 'navigation-content';

  const secondCol = block.children[1];
  if (secondCol) {
    const toolsLink = secondCol.querySelector(':scope > div > div > p:nth-of-type(2) > a');
    if (toolsLink) toolsLink.classList.add('navigation-content-preview-tools-link');
  }

  const firstCol = block.children[0];
  if (!firstCol) return;
  const wrapper = firstCol.firstElementChild;
  if (!wrapper) return;

  wrapper.querySelector(':scope > p:first-child')
    ?.classList.add('navigation-content-preview-heading');
  wrapper.querySelector(':scope > p:nth-of-type(2) > a')
    ?.classList.add('navigation-content-preview-link');
  applyCommonProps(block);
}

It does not render menus, submenus, or nested links at all — all of the analytics-string building, fallback navigation-title resolution, active-page detection, and section/link nesting logic that lived in PrimaryNavigationItemModel.java and UtilityNav.java has no equivalent in this file. The comment explicitly names the block a "Multi-purpose nav content slot whose behavior is picked by an explicit type-class allowlist," and the shipped code only tags the block with its type and adds a couple of preview-only marker classes — the surrounding header assembly/rendering logic (mega-menu open/close, submenu injection) lives elsewhere (outside this block's own JS), matching the manifest's "Reuse As-Is" status for the shell itself.

CSS

main .header-content {
  position: relative;
  background-color: var(--gray-4);
  padding: var(--spacing-20);
