  display: grid;
  grid-auto-flow: column;
  grid-auto-columns: 1fr;
}
main .header-content::before {
  position: absolute;
  content: attr(data-type);
  font-size: var(--font-size-10);
  ...
}

This stylesheet is explicitly labeled "used exclusively to make preview easier to look at" — it renders the data-type attribute as an on-canvas label in the Universal Editor and gives the raw authoring grid a readable background, not production header styling. Each brand folder (abbvie/, botox/, linzess/) carries its own navigation-content.css / _navigation-content.css / block-config.js override triplet, following the standard per-brand override pattern used across target-implementation blocks.

Key differences from the source system

Source system (headerlogo / primary-navigation / utility-navigation)Target (navigation-content)
Component count3 distinct component types, 3 dialogs, 3 Sling Models1 block, 1 field model, type switch via classes
NestingPrimary nav supports real 3-level nesting (item → section → link) via nested multifields parsed as JSONNo nested-multifield equivalent; mega-menu fields are flat, and utility items collapse to one free-text richtext field
AnalyticsDeep per-item/per-section/per-link "digital data" JSON built server-side in Java, with MVA tiering at every levelNot present in the shipped JS; no analytics string construction observed
Nav-title fallbackLive lookup of linked page's navigation/page/title properties at render timeNot present — authors must set title directly
Active-page detectionServer-side comparison of currentPage.getPath() against nav URLsNot present in decorate()

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

headerlogo → navigation-content (classes = "logo")

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
primarylogo/fileReferencefileupload (DAM)content_imgRenamedSame purpose (logo asset), different field name/type (asset upload → reference)
primaryimgalttexttextfieldcontent_imgTitleRenamedAlt text purpose preserved; default value changed to "Home"
linkurlpathbrowsercontent_titleLinkNot mappedcontent_titleLink only appears under classes="" (nav item), not under logo — the logo variant has no link field in the model, so a logo's destination link has no live field slot
primarylogoRenditionselect (datasource)—Not mappedNo rendition-selection concept in the target field model; asset delivery/rendition is handled by the platform's image pipeline, not authored per-instance
indicationRequired, indicationTitle, indicationContent, indicationLinkurlcheckbox/textfield/textarea/pathbrowsercontent_indicationImg, content_indicationImgTitleRenamedSource models the indication as a text badge with its own link; target only exposes an indication logo image + alt text, no link/content fields — narrower surface
interactionId, analyticsTitle, sectionTitle, l10Title, mvaPrimary, mvaTitle, mvaTypeAndCategory, mvaSecondary (and the parallel indication* analytics fields)various—Not mappedNo analytics/MVA tagging fields exist anywhere in the navigation-content model; this tagging-spec-driven digital-data system has no target-side authoring equivalent yet

primary-navigation → navigation-content (classes = "")

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
mainMenuTitletextfieldmegamenutitleRenamedSame purpose (nav item title), field renamed and scoped to the empty-classes ("Navigation Item") variant
mainMenuURLpathbrowsermegamenu_linkRenamedSame purpose, different field name and component type (pathbrowser → aem-content)
mainMenuCustomCsstextfieldclasses_commonCustomClassRenamedCustom-class concept moved to the shared Common Properties tab used by every block
mainMenuLinkBehaviour, mainmenumodalID, mainMenuLinkIconselect/textfield/select—Not mappedNo link-target-behavior, modal-ID, or icon-picker fields in the model; the target's aem-content link component handles link resolution without an authorable target/modal/icon triad
subMenuSectionset (nested multifield, parsed JSON) — section heading/URL/behavior/modalID/icon fieldsmultifield of fieldsets—Not mappedConfirmed against models-normalized.json: the navigation-content field model has no multifield or nested-fieldset construct anywhere in its 31 fields. The real 3-level nesting (nav item → section → link) that PrimaryNavigationItemModel.java parses via Gson has no structural equivalent; the mega-menu fields it does have (megamenudashboardlinks, megamenucardtitle/content, single megamenudashboardcard reference) are flat single-value fields, not a repeatable section list
sectionlinkdetails / subMenuLinksFieldset (nested multifield, third level) — link title/URL/behavior/modalID/icon fieldsmultifield of fieldsets (nested inside another multifield)—Not mappedSame architectural gap as above, one level deeper: there is no authoring construct in navigation-content for a link list nested inside a section nested inside a nav item. The closest available field, megamenudashboardlinks, is a single richtext blob (author free-types links), not structured repeatable data
mainMenuComponentTitle, mainMenuComponentSectionAnalyticsTitle, mainMenuComponentLocalizedAnalyticsTitle, mainMenuInteractionId, mainMenuMva, mainMenuMvaValue, mainMenuSecondaryMva (and section/link-level equivalents)various—Not mappedNo analytics/MVA tagging fields exist in the model at any nesting level

utility-navigation → navigation-content (classes = "utility-nav")

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
navItemTitletextfieldcontent_titleNot mapped (partial)content_title is scoped to classes in ["", "language-links"], not utility-nav — under the utility-nav variant there is no separate menu-title field; the group label would have to be folded into content_utilityItems's free-text rows
customCssClassestextfieldclasses_commonCustomClassRenamedMoved to shared Common Properties tab
showinmobilecheckbox—Not mappedNo per-item responsive-visibility toggle field in the model
utilitynavlinks multifield: linktitle, linkurl, linkclickbehaviour, modalID, linkicon, linkIconPositionmultifield of fieldsetscontent_utilityItemsRenamed (structure collapsed)All six structured per-link sub-fields collapse into one richtext field documented as "one row per item: [type | label | href | options]" — a convention-based flat-text encoding rather than discrete authorable fields; per-field validation and pathbrowser link picking are lost
interactionID, analyticsTitle, componentTitle, localizedAnalyticsTitle, analyticsEnableMVATier, mvaTitle, analyticsMVAVal, analyticsMVATiervarious—Not mappedNo analytics/MVA tagging fields exist in the model

News Feed — Technical Details

What it does

news-feed fetches an RSS/Atom-style XML feed client-side and renders the most recent items as a featured-item-plus-list layout: the newest item is shown large on the left, the next two smaller in a list on the right. Publication dates are reformatted from whatever the feed provides into a long US date string (e.g. "January 5, 2025"). There is no AEM source component behind this block — per the manifest, aemSources is an empty array, and no component folder in the source-system tree matches "news", "feed", or "rss" by name. This is an EDS-native block with no direct predecessor in the source system.

Architecture

Author enters the feed URL as block content (first row)
         ↓
decorate(block):
  applyCommonProps(block)               // shared id/class/lang handling
  feedUrl = block.children[0].textContent.trim()
         ↓
  fetchFeed(feedUrl) → fetch() → response.text() (raw XML string)
         ↓
  decorateFeed(block, xmlString):
    DOMParser().parseFromString(xml, 'application/xml')
    querySelectorAll('item') → map to { link, pubDate, title }
    filter to items with both link and title, slice to first 3
         ↓
    item[0] → .news-feed (featured, left)
    item[1..2] → .news-feed-right > .news-feed (list, right)
         ↓
    block.replaceChildren(newsfeed)   // full replace of authored content

Sling Model

None. There is no Java Sling Model to document because there is no AEM source component — the block's only "model" is the client-side JS shown above plus the block's field model (below).

Dialog

No AEM dialog exists. The nearest equivalent is the block's Universal Editor field model (_news-feed.json):

FieldPropertyTypeNote
RSS Feed URLfeedUrltextAuthored as the block's first content row; read via block.children[0].textContent, not a structured field lookup
Variant (Theme)variantselectOptions list is empty in the shipped model — no variant values are currently defined
Also includes an "Accessibility" tab and the shared _common-properties.json common-prop include (id/class/language), standard across target-implementation blocks.

HTL

Not applicable — no HTL template exists for this block. The equivalent DOM-construction logic lives entirely in news-feed.js's decorateFeed(), quoted in full below since it is the real, already-shipped implementation (see "How the JS works").

CSS

Not applicable at the AEM layer (no source component). See "EDS Implementation" below for the real, shipped CSS.

Important Constraints

  • Hard-coded cap of MAX_ITEMS = 3 — only the first three <item> elements in feed document order are ever rendered, regardless of feed size; there is no "load more" or pagination.
  • Items missing a <link> or <title> are silently dropped via the .filter((item) => item.link && item.title) call — a malformed feed entry disappears with no placeholder or error indication.
  • Date formatting swallows parse failures: if new Date(dateString) produces an invalid date, formatDate returns the original raw string as-is rather than a fallback message, so a malformed pubDate can surface unformatted feed text in the UI.
  • Network/parse failures (fetchFeed catch block, and the parsererror check in decorateFeed) only console.error() — the block silently renders nothing rather than showing an error or fallback state to visitors.
  • All links are forced to target="_blank" with data-warn-on-departure="false" — external-link warning behavior used elsewhere on the site is explicitly suppressed for feed items.
  • No caching — every page render re-fetches the feed URL with no client-side cache or stale-while-revalidate strategy.

News Feed Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/news-feed/. It is a self-contained client-side RSS reader: no server round-trip beyond the direct fetch() of the feed URL, no build-time data, and no AEM-side rendering step of any kind.

File Structure

blocks/news-feed/
├── news-feed.js
├── news-feed.css
├── _news-feed.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── news-feed.css
│   ├── _news-feed.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
│   ├── news-feed.css
│   ├── _news-feed.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── rinvoq/
    ├── news-feed.css
    ├── _news-feed.css
    └── block-config.js

Authoring Model (_news-feed.json)

{
  "definitions": [
    {
      "title": "News Feed",
      "id": "news-feed",
      "plugins": {
        "xwalk": {
          "page": {
            "resourceType": "core/franklin/components/block/v1/block",
            "template": { "name": "News Feed", "model": "news-feed", "blockId": "id:", "language": "lang:none" }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  ],
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "news-feed",
      "fields": [
        { "component": "tab", "label": "Overview", "name": "overview" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "feedUrl", "label": "RSS Feed URL" },
        { "component": "select", "name": "variant", "label": "Variant (Theme)", "options": [] },
        { "component": "tab", "label": "Accessibility", "name": "accessibility" },
        { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "filters": []
}

How the JS works (news-feed.js)

formatDate normalizes any parseable date string to a long US-style date, with a plain pass-through fallback on parse failure:

function formatDate(dateString) {
  try {
    const date = new Date(dateString);
    if (Number.isNaN(date.getTime())) return dateString;
    return date.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric' });
  } catch {
    return dateString;
  }
}

fetchFeed performs the raw network call and returns the response body as text (not pre-parsed), logging but swallowing both network (TypeError, typically CORS) and generic HTTP failures:

async function fetchFeed(feedUrl) {
  try {
    const response = await fetch(feedUrl);
    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(`Request failed with status ${response.status}`);
    return await response.text();
  } catch (error) {
    if (error instanceof TypeError) {
      console.error('Network error - check CORS and URL validity:', error.message);
    } else {
      console.error('Failed to fetch news feed:', error.message);
    }
    return null;
  }
}

decorateFeed parses the XML with DOMParser, extracts up to 3 items, and rebuilds the block's DOM from scratch — a featured item (.news-feed directly in .newsfeed-content) plus a .news-feed-right list for the remaining items:

function decorateFeed(block, responseXml) {
  const MAX_ITEMS = 3;
  const doc = new DOMParser().parseFromString(responseXml, 'application/xml');
  if (doc.querySelector('parsererror')) { console.error('Failed to parse RSS feed XML.'); return; }

  const getText = (item, selector) => item.querySelector(selector)?.textContent?.trim() || '';
  const items = [...doc.querySelectorAll('item')]
    .map((item) => ({ link: getText(item, 'link'), pubDate: getText(item, 'pubDate'), title: getText(item, 'title') }))
    .filter((item) => item.link && item.title)
    .slice(0, MAX_ITEMS);

  if (!items.length) return;
  // items[0] -> .news-feed (featured) inside .newsfeed-content
  // items[1..] -> .news-feed-right > repeated .news-feed
  // each item: <a class="newsfeed-link|newsfeed__link" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"
  //             data-warn-on-departure="false"><span class="newsfeed-rss-eyebrow">date</span>
  //             <p class="newsfeed-rss-title">title</p></a>
  block.replaceChildren(newsfeed);
}

decorate ties it together and is a no-op if the feed URL is missing or the fetch fails — the block simply keeps whatever the author-entered content was before, or ends up empty if the URL row itself was otherwise the only content:

export default async function decorate(block) {
  applyCommonProps(block);
  const feedUrl = block.children[0]?.textContent.trim();
  if (feedUrl) {
    const feedList = await fetchFeed(feedUrl);
    if (feedList) decorateFeed(block, feedList);
  }
}

Note the inconsistent class naming between the featured link (newsfeed-link) and the list-item links (newsfeed__link) — real, as-shipped, and worth flagging as a minor naming inconsistency rather than a deliberate BEM/non-BEM split.

CSS

Mobile-first, single column by default, promoted to an 8-column (tablet) then 12-column (desktop) grid with the featured item spanning the first 6-7 columns and the list occupying the remainder:

.news-feed-wrapper .newsfeed-content {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  margin: var(--spacing-00) var(--spacing-35);
}

@media (width >= 744px) {
  .news-feed-wrapper .newsfeed-content {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(8, 1fr);
    gap: var(--gap-0) var(--gap-20);
  }
  .newsfeed-content > .news-feed { grid-column: 1 / span 7; }
}

@media (width >= 1024px) {
  .news-feed-wrapper .newsfeed-content { grid-template-columns: repeat(12, 1fr); }
  .newsfeed-content > .news-feed { grid-column: 1 / span 6; }
  .news-feed-wrapper .news-feed-right { grid-column: 8 / span 5; }
}

Right-column items get a top border divider between entries (.news-feed-right .news-feed + .news-feed) and a smaller, regular-weight title style versus the larger featured-item heading. Each brand folder (abbvie/, botox/, rinvoq/) supplies its own news-feed.css/_news-feed.css/block-config.js override triplet, following the standard per-brand override pattern.

Key differences from the AEM implementation

AEM source systemEDS (news-feed)
Source componentNone existsNet-new, EDS-native block
Data fetchN/AClient-side fetch() of a raw RSS/XML URL at render time, same-origin/CORS constraints apply
Author fieldsN/ASingle feed URL text field plus an empty "Variant (Theme)" select

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
No AEM source component exists for this block — aemSources is an empty array in the manifest, and no matching component folder ("news", "feed", "rss") was found under the source-system component tree. There are no AEM fields to map. The EDS-side fields (feedUrl, variant) are net-new authoring surface with no predecessor.

Parallax — Technical Details

What it does

The source component, parallax-image, implements a scroll-driven, canvas-based image-sequence parallax effect: an author points at a DAM folder of sequentially numbered images (e.g. 1000.jpg through 1050.jpg) and, as the user scrolls, GSAP's ScrollTrigger plugin advances a "frame" counter that is drawn onto an HTML <canvas> — a genuine animated image-sequence scrubbing effect, not a simple CSS parallax. On touch devices, the effect is disabled and replaced with a static class. The target implementation, parallax, replaces this with a much simpler and more portable technique: a single background image with CSS background-attachment: fixed on desktop for a classic parallax look, degrading gracefully to a static background on iOS/reduced-motion.

Architecture

Source system:
Author sets folderPath (DAM folder), startImage/endImage (frame range), startText/endText
         ↓
ParallaxImageModel.java (Sling Model) exposes folderPath, startImage, endImage, startText,
endText, mobileImage, anchorId, customCssClasses, interactionId
         ↓
parallax-image.html renders:
  <canvas id="abbv-parallax-scroll" data-folder-path="..." data-image-start="..." data-image-end="..."
          style="background-image: url(folderPath/mobileImage)">
  <div class="text"><h2 class="text-overlay one">startText</h2></div>
  <div class="text"><h2 class="text-overlay two">endText</h2></div>
         ↓
parallax-image.js (client, jQuery + GSAP):
  loads every frame image i in [startImage..endImage] into an Image() array
  gsap.to(animation, { frame: frameCount-1, scrollTrigger: { scrub, pin, trigger: canvas } })
  onUpdate: render() -> context.drawImage(images[animation.frame], 0, 0)
  fires custom analytics events on scroll-enter/scroll-leave

Target implementation:
Author sets image (DAM reference), imageAlt, content (richtext overlay), anchorId
         ↓
parallax.js decorate(block):
  detects document-authoring (multi-cell row) vs UE (one-field-per-row) shape
  extracts image src (from <picture><img> or a raw DAM/asset <a> link)
  sanitizeUrl() allowlists http(s) protocols only
  sets --parallax-bg-image custom property on a wrapper div
  clones the overlay content nodes (no innerHTML) into a .parallax-content div
         ↓
parallax.css: background-attachment: fixed on desktop only (>= 768px);
  static background-attachment: scroll on touch/iOS and prefers-reduced-motion

Sling Model — ParallaxImageModel.java

Minimal pass-through model; all fields are plain injected strings, with a self-writing interaction ID for analytics on first author-mode render:

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class}, defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class ParallaxImageModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENTIDPREFIX = "abbv-parallax";
    @Inject private String anchorId;
    @Inject private String customCssClasses;
    @Inject private String folderPath;
    @Inject private String startImage;
    @Inject private String endImage;
    @Inject private String startText;
    @Inject private String endText;
    @Inject private String mobileImage;
    @Inject private String interactionId;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        if (!isPublish() && (StringUtils.isBlank(interactionId) || !interactionId.equals(getId()))) {
            setInteractionIdInComponentDialog();
        }
    }
}

Dialog

Single-tab dialog, image-sequence-oriented (not a single-image picker):

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor IDanchorIdtextfieldAlso doubles as a cookie name in related drawer-style components per its shared field description text
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextfield
Enter path to a folder in the DAMfolderPathpathfieldFolder of sequentially-named images (dialog text: "All the images must be named with sequential numbers e.g. 1000.jpg, 1001.jpg, 1002.jpg")
Starting image namestartImagetextfieldFour-digit filename, e.g. "1000.jpg"
Ending image nameendImagetextfieldFour-digit filename, e.g. "1050.jpg"
Starting frame textstartTexttextareaOverlay text shown at scroll start
Ending frame textendTexttextareaOverlay text shown at scroll end
Mobile image namemobileImagetextfieldSingle static fallback image for mobile — dialog text explains "the parallax effect does not translate well to a mobile experience"

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-use.parallax="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.ParallaxImageModel">
  <div data-sly-attribute.id="${parallax.anchorId}" class="abbv-parallax ${properties.customCssClasses}">
    <canvas id="abbv-parallax-scroll"
      data-folder-path="${parallax.folderPath}"
      data-image-start="${parallax.startImage}"
      data-image-end="${parallax.endImage}"
      data-analytics-interactionid="${parallax.interactionId}"
      style="background-image: url(${parallax.folderPath}/${parallax.mobileImage});">
    </canvas>
    <div data-sly-test="${parallax.startText}" class="text"><h2 class="text-overlay one">${parallax.startText}</h2></div>
    <div data-sly-test="${parallax.endText}" class="text"><h2 class="text-overlay two">${parallax.endText}</h2></div>
  </div>
</sly>
<!-- clientlibs loaded defer: common-elements-gsap-plugin.v1, parallax-image-component.v1 -->

Client script (ui/parallax-image.js, jQuery + GSAP ScrollTrigger) preloads every frame image into an array, then scrubs through them on scroll:

for (let i = imageStartNum; i <= imageEndNum; i++) {
  const img = new Image();
  img.src = currentFrame(i, folderPath, imageExt);
  images.push(img);
}
gsap.to(animation, {
  frame: frameCount - 1,
  snap: "frame",
  scrollTrigger: { scrub: 1, pin: true, trigger: "#abbv-parallax-scroll", start: "top top",
    onEnter: triggerStartAnalytics, onLeave: triggerEndanalytics },
  onUpdate: render,
});
function render() {
  context.clearRect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);
  context.drawImage(images[animation.frame], 0, 0);
}

Touch devices skip GSAP entirely and get a static .abbv-parallax-touch class instead:

if (!browserAgent.checkIfBrowser('touch')) {
  gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger);
  parallaxWithGSAP();
} else {
  $(".abbv-parallax").addClass("abbv-parallax-touch");
}

CSS

Source-side CSS is delivered via the clientlibs category referenced in the HTL (common-elements-gsap-plugin.v1, parallax-image-component.v1) rather than a stylesheet local to the component folder — the folder itself ships JS/PHP variation files under ui/ (parallax-image.php, variations.php) used for style-guide/pattern-library preview rendering, not the live component CSS.

Important Constraints

  • Every frame in the configured range is eagerly loaded as a full Image() object on page load — for a 50-frame sequence, that's 50 separate HTTP requests up front, with no lazy-loading or range limiting.
  • Frame filenames must be sequential four-digit numbers in the same DAM folder; there is no authoring-time validation that the folder actually contains a contiguous, correctly-named sequence — a gap or typo produces broken frames only visible at runtime.
  • The parallax effect is explicitly disabled on touch devices (replaced with a static class), and there is no prefers-reduced-motion handling at all in the source JS.
  • Analytics events fire only on scroll-enter/scroll-leave of the whole component, not per-frame, and depend on a global abbvAnalytics.updateTool hook existing on the page.

Parallax Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/parallax/, and per the manifest is flagged Reuse+Enhance — it already supports two different authoring shapes so it can be authored either through Universal Editor (one field per row) or through document-based authoring (image+alt grouped in one row), detected automatically by row shape.

File Structure

blocks/parallax/
├── parallax.js
├── parallax.css
├── _parallax.json
├── block-config.js
├── mavyret/
│   ├── parallax.css
│   └── _parallax.css
└── skyrizi-hcp/
    ├── parallax.css
    └── _parallax.css

Note: unlike most other blocks in this batch, the mavyret and skyrizi-hcp brand folders here have no block-config.js override — only CSS overrides.

Authoring Model (_parallax.json)

{
  "definitions": [
    {
      "title": "Parallax",
      "id": "parallax",
      "plugins": {
        "xwalk": {
          "page": {
            "resourceType": "core/franklin/components/block/v1/block",
            "template": { "name": "Parallax", "model": "parallax" }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  ],
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "parallax",
      "fields": [
        { "component": "reference", "valueType": "string", "name": "image", "label": "Background Image", "multi": false },
        { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "imageAlt", "value": "", "label": "Image Alt Text" },
        { "component": "richtext", "name": "content", "value": "", "label": "Overlay Content", "valueType": "string" },
        { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "anchorId", "value": "", "label": "Anchor ID" }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "filters": []
}

Four flat fields — a single background image (not an image sequence), alt text, one richtext overlay block (not separate start/end texts), and an anchor ID. No folder path, no frame range, no mobile-specific image.

How the JS works (parallax.js)

The dual-shape detection that gives this block its "Reuse+Enhance" status:

const rows = [...block.children];
const isMultiCell = rows[0]?.children.length > 1;
let imageCell, altCell, contentCell, anchorCell;
if (isMultiCell) {
  // Document pattern: Row 0 = [image, alt], Row 1 = content, Row 2 = anchorId
  imageCell = rows[0]?.children[0]; altCell = rows[0]?.children[1];
  contentCell = rows[1]?.children[0]; anchorCell = rows[2]?.children[0];
} else {
  // UE/XWalk pattern: each field = own row
  imageCell = rows[0]?.children[0]; altCell = rows[1]?.children[0];
  contentCell = rows[2]?.children[0]; anchorCell = rows[3]?.children[0];
}

Image extraction tries a rendered <picture><img> first, then falls back to a raw DAM asset link by extension/path match:

const picture = imageCell?.querySelector('picture');
const img = picture?.querySelector('img');
if (img) {
  imgSrc = img.src;
} else {
  const damLink = imageCell?.querySelector('a[href*="/content/dam"], a[href*=".jpg"], a[href*=".png"], a[href*=".webp"], a[href*=".svg"]');
  if (damLink) imgSrc = damLink.href;
}

URLs are sanitized to the http(s) protocol allowlist before being written into a CSS custom property (the actual background paint mechanism, handled by parallax.css):

function sanitizeUrl(url) {
  if (!url) return '';
  try {
    const parsed = new URL(url, window.location.origin);
    if (parsed.protocol === 'https:' || parsed.protocol === 'http:') return parsed.href;
  } catch { /* invalid URL */ }
  return '';
}
...
const safeUrl = sanitizeUrl(imgSrc);
if (safeUrl) {
  const encodedUrl = safeUrl.replace(/'/g, '%27');
  wrapper.style.setProperty('--parallax-bg-image', `url('${encodedUrl}')`);
}

Overlay content is moved via node cloning rather than innerHTML (an XSS-hardening choice), and the block finishes by delegating to the shared brand-theming pipeline:

const contentFragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
if (contentCell) {
  [...contentCell.childNodes].forEach((node) => contentFragment.append(node.cloneNode(true)));
}
...
try {
  await renderBlock(block);
} catch {
  // brand block-config failed; parallax still renders
}

CSS

The actual parallax mechanism is pure CSS — background-attachment: fixed gated to desktop viewports, since it is unsupported on iOS Safari/WebKit (documented in a code comment as acceptable graceful degradation), and explicitly turned off for reduced-motion preference:

.parallax .parallax-wrapper {
  background-image: var(--parallax-bg-image);
  background-size: cover;
  background-attachment: scroll;
}
@media (width >= 768px) {
  .parallax .parallax-wrapper {
    min-height: var(--parallax-min-height-desktop, 400px);
    background-attachment: fixed;
  }
}
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  .parallax .parallax-wrapper { background-attachment: scroll; }
}

Brand folders (mavyret/, skyrizi-hcp/) override only the CSS layer, no JS decorations — consistent with the empty block-config.js at the block root.

Key differences from the AEM implementation

AEM (parallax-image)EDS (parallax)
Effect mechanismCanvas + GSAP ScrollTrigger frame-by-frame image-sequence scrubbingPure CSS background-attachment: fixed
Image sourceDAM folder of N sequentially-numbered frame images, eagerly preloadedSingle background image reference
Overlay textSeparate start-of-scroll and end-of-scroll text fields, each fading in via its own ScrollTrigger animationSingle richtext overlay block, always visible
Mobile behaviorExplicit single fallback image field + touch-device class swap disabling the effect entirelySame background image at all breakpoints; effect degrades to static via background-attachment: scroll
Reduced motionNot handledExplicit prefers-reduced-motion media query
Authoring shapes supportedOne (AEM dialog only)Two (Universal Editor one-field-per-row, and document-authoring grouped-row) via runtime row-shape detection

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIdtextfieldanchorIdSame nameSame purpose; EDS side normalizes it (lowercased, spaces to hyphens, stripped to word chars) before use as the element ID
customCssClassestextfield—Not mappedNo custom-class field in the parallax model; not present in models-normalized.json's 4-field parallax entry
folderPathpathfield (DAM folder)imageNot mappedArchitecturally different concepts: source points at a folder of many sequential frames, target's image is a single-asset reference — a folder-of-frames authoring model has no equivalent field
startImage, endImagetextfield—Not mappedFrame-range fields have no purpose once the effect is not a frame-sequence scrub; there is nothing to map them to
mobileImagetextfield—Not mappedNo separate mobile-specific image field; the target's single image field is used at all breakpoints
startTexttextareacontentRenamedCollapsed: the two independent start/end overlay texts merge into one always-visible content richtext field
endTexttextareacontentRenamedSame collapse as above — both source fields land on the same single target field, so the distinct start-vs-end fade timing is lost
interactionIdtextfield (disabled, server-written)—Not mappedNo analytics interaction-ID field exists in the parallax model
(none in source)—imageAltNot mapped (net-new)Target adds a first-class alt-text field; source relies on the canvas having no native alt-text concept at all

Pipeline Utility Nav — Technical Details

What it does

pipeline-utility-nav is a purpose-built search/sort/filter utility bar for a single page pattern — its own code comment and CSS header both explicitly say "Matches: the primary site's /science/pipeline.html page." It renders a title/last-updated eyebrow with an inline search field, a "Sort By" dropdown (Focus Area / Alphabetical), a "Filter" toggle that opens a large multi-category checkbox panel (Focus Area, Pharmaceuticals with Phase/Type sub-groups, Devices with Phase/Type sub-groups), and a row of removable filter "chips" that mirrors the current checkbox selections.

AEM source component — not found in the mirrored repository

The manifest entry for this block carries the purpose text "Search/sort/filter utility bar specifically for the the primary site's pipeline page, backed by a named AEM source component," and the cross-reference analysis docs (06-eds-existing-review.md, 08-eds-new-block-designs.md) repeat "named source component" / "backed by a named AEM source component" without naming it. The manifest's own aemSources array for this entry is empty. A direct search of the mirrored source-system tree — ui.apps/.../apps/common-elements/v1/components/content/ for any folder matching "pipeline," "science," or "utility" beyond the generic utility-navigation already covered under navigation-content, plus a repo-wide grep for "pipeline" across both ui.apps and core — turned up no matching component definition, dialog, HTL template, or Sling Model. No real AEM source file could be located for this component in the mirrored repository, despite the documentation's claim that one exists; this section therefore covers the EDS-side implementation only, as a net-new/EDS-native-in-practice block, and this discrepancy should be flagged for follow-up (the named source component may live in a part of the source tree not included in this mirror, or the manifest annotation may be inaccurate).

Sling Model / Dialog / HTL

Not applicable — no AEM source file exists in the mirrored repository for this component (see above).

CSS (AEM side)

Not applicable — no AEM source file exists in the mirrored repository for this component.

Pipeline Utility Nav Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/pipeline-utility-nav/ as a self-contained, client-side-only search/sort/filter widget. All markup is built programmatically in JS from a fixed row-position content contract; there is no server call for filter options — the Focus Area / Pharmaceuticals / Devices category structure and every checkbox option are hard-coded in the JS itself, not authored.

File Structure

blocks/pipeline-utility-nav/
├── pipeline-utility-nav.js
├── pipeline-utility-nav.css
├── _pipeline-utility-nav.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── pipeline-utility-nav.css
│   ├── _pipeline-utility-nav.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
│   ├── pipeline-utility-nav.css
│   ├── _pipeline-utility-nav.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── rinvoq/
    ├── pipeline-utility-nav.css
    ├── _pipeline-utility-nav.css
    └── block-config.js

Authoring Model (_pipeline-utility-nav.json)

Overview tab plus a separate Analytics tab, driven by a positional row contract documented directly in the JS file header comment:

/**
 * Row layout (classes_ fields handled by framework):
 *   0: lastUpdatedDate
 *   1: focusAreaPath (required)
 *   2: pharmaceuticalPath (required)
 *   3: devicesPath (required)
 *   4: searchFontIcon (conditional)
 *   5: searchImageIcon (conditional)
 *   6: sortFontIcon (conditional)
 *   7: sortImageIcon (conditional)
 *   8: filterFontIcon (conditional)
 *   9: filterImageIcon (conditional)
 *   10: clearAllLabel
 *   11: applyLabel
 *   12: analyticsInteractionId
 *   13+: common properties (id, customClass, language — via applyCommonProps)
 */

{
  "component": "date-time", "name": "lastUpdatedDate", "label": "Last Updated Date",
  "description": "The date the pipeline was last updated. Timezone note: Your timezone (UTC-05:00) will be used instead of the server setting (UTC-04:00)."
},
{ "component": "aem-tag", "name": "focusAreaPath", "label": "Focus Area Path", "required": true },
{ "component": "aem-tag", "name": "pharmaceuticalPath", "label": "Pharmaceutical Path", "required": true },
{ "component": "aem-tag", "name": "devicesPath", "label": "Devices Path", "required": true },
{
  "component": "select", "name": "classes_searchIconType", "label": "Search Icon Type", "value": "no-icon",
  "options": [
    { "name": "None", "value": "search-no-icon" },
    { "name": "Icon Font", "value": "search-icon-font" },
    { "name": "Image", "value": "search-image" }
  ]
}

Each of Search/Sort/Filter gets the same icon-type/font-name/image-reference triplet pattern (conditional on its own classes_<name>IconType select), plus clearAllLabel, applyLabel, an Analytics-tab analyticsInteractionId text field, and the shared _common-properties.json common-prop include.

Notably, despite the JS building a large filter panel with hard-coded checkbox option lists (Immunology, Neuroscience, Oncology, Eye Care, Aesthetics, Other Specialties for Focus Area; multiple Phase/Type sub-lists for Pharmaceuticals and Devices), the actual authored fields are only three aem-tag path references (focusAreaPath, pharmaceuticalPath, devicesPath) — the checkbox option labels are not sourced from those tag paths anywhere in the shipped JS; they are literal string arrays in the code.

How the JS works (pipeline-utility-nav.js)

decorate() reads all fixed-position rows into a flat config object, determines icon type from block classes (search-image/search-icon-font/etc., set by the field model's classes_*IconType selects), then builds three structural pieces: an eyebrow (title + last-updated + search), a sort/filter control row, and a large filter panel:

const config = {
  lastUpdated: getCellText(rows[ROW.LAST_UPDATED]),
  focusAreaPath: getCellText(rows[ROW.FOCUS_AREA_PATH]),
  pharmaceuticalPath: getCellText(rows[ROW.PHARMACEUTICAL_PATH]),
  devicesPath: getCellText(rows[ROW.DEVICES_PATH]),
  searchFontIcon: getCellText(rows[ROW.SEARCH_FONT_ICON]),
  searchImageIcon: getCellImage(rows[ROW.SEARCH_IMAGE_ICON]),
  ...
  searchIconType, sortIconType, filterIconType,
};
block.textContent = '';
if (config.analyticsId) block.setAttribute('data-analytics-id', config.analyticsId);
const nav = document.createElement('div');
nav.className = 'pipeline-nav';
nav.append(buildEyebrow(config));
nav.append(buildSortFilter(config));
const filterPanel = buildFilterPanel(config);
nav.append(filterPanel);
block.append(nav);

buildFilterPanel hard-codes the three filter categories and their option lists directly in the JS (not sourced from focusAreaPath/pharmaceuticalPath/devicesPath at render time):

const cats = [
  { title: 'Focus Area', id: 'focus-area', hasSelectAll: false,
    items: ['Immunology', 'Neuroscience', 'Oncology', 'Eye Care', 'Aesthetics', 'Other Specialties'] },
  { title: 'Pharmaceuticals', id: 'pharmaceuticals', hasSelectAll: true,
    subs: [
      { title: 'Molecule/Asset Phase', items: ['Phase 1', 'Phase 2', 'Phase 3', 'Submitted', 'Approved'] },
      { title: 'Molecule/Asset Type', items: ['Biologic', 'Small Molecule', 'Large Molecule', ...] },
    ] },
  { title: 'Devices', id: 'devices', hasSelectAll: true,
    subs: [
      { title: 'Device Asset Phase', items: ['Concept', 'Feasibility', 'Development', 'Confirmation', 'Approved', 'Launched'] },
      { title: 'Device Asset Type', items: ['Facial Aesthetics', 'Body Contouring', 'Device', 'N/A'] },
    ] },
];

Select-all checkboxes track indeterminate state against their child checkboxes, and a live count badge updates per category:

container.addEventListener('change', (e) => {
  if (e.target.dataset.filterType) return; // ignore select-all itself
  const allCb = container.querySelector(`[data-filter-type="${cat.id}-all"]`);
  const items = [...container.querySelectorAll('input[type="checkbox"]:not([data-filter-type])')];
  const checkedCount = items.filter((c) => c.checked).length;
  allCb.checked = checkedCount === items.length;
  allCb.indeterminate = checkedCount > 0 && checkedCount < items.length;
  const countEl = container.querySelector(`.pipeline-nav-panel-${cat.id}-count`);
  if (countEl) countEl.textContent = checkedCount > 0 ? ` (${checkedCount})` : '';
});

Checked filters are mirrored as removable "chips" in the sort/filter bar; clicking a chip's remove action unchecks the underlying checkbox and dispatches a synthetic change event so all listeners stay in sync:

function updateChips() {
  chipsButtons.textContent = '';
  const checked = [...filterPanel.querySelectorAll('input[type="checkbox"]:checked:not([data-filter-type])')];
  checked.forEach((cb) => {
    const chip = document.createElement('button');
    chip.textContent = cb.value;
    chip.addEventListener('click', () => {
      cb.checked = false;
      cb.dispatchEvent(new Event('change', { bubbles: true }));
      updateChips();
    });
    chipsButtons.append(chip);
  });
  chipsArea.hidden = checked.length === 0;
}

The search field itself is a plain <form method="GET" role="search"> with a floating label — it wires focus/blur/input listeners for label animation but does not submit, filter, or fetch anything; searching against the pipeline data is not implemented in this file.

CSS

Mobile-first; eyebrow stacks vertically on mobile and becomes a row on tablet, filter panel category grid is single-column on mobile and a fixed 2/5/5-column split (Focus Area / Pharmaceuticals / Devices) on desktop:

/* Matches: abbvie.com/science/pipeline.html */
.pipeline-nav-panel-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(12, 1fr);
  gap: var(--spacing-20);
}
@media (width >= 1024px) {
  .pipeline-nav-panel-focus-area { grid-column: 1 / span 2; }
  .pipeline-nav-panel-pharmaceuticals { grid-column: 3 / span 5; }
  .pipeline-nav-panel-devices { grid-column: 8 / span 5; }
  .pipeline-nav-panel-dual { flex-direction: row; }
}

Custom checkbox styling uses appearance: none with a checkmark pseudo-element and an indeterminate-state dash, explicitly commented "matching PROD." Each brand folder (abbvie/, botox/, rinvoq/) ships the standard CSS/JSON-CSS/block-config.js override triplet.

Important Constraints

  • Filter category labels and option lists (Focus Area terms, Phase/Type values for Pharmaceuticals and Devices) are hard-coded literal arrays in the JS, not driven by the authored focusAreaPath/ pharmaceuticalPath/devicesPath tag references at render time — those three fields are captured in config but never read inside buildFilterPanel.
  • The search input has no submit handling, fetch call, or results-filtering logic wired up in this file — visually functional, not yet operationally connected to actual pipeline content filtering.
  • This is a single-purpose, page-specific block (explicitly scoped to one URL in both the JS header comment and the CSS file header) rather than a general-purpose utility-nav pattern reusable elsewhere.

Key differences from the AEM implementation

AEM source systemEDS (pipeline-utility-nav)
Source componentClaimed to exist ("named AEM source component") per cross-reference docs, but not found anywhere in the mirrored repositoryFully implemented, client-side-only widget
Filter taxonomyUnknown (source not located)Hard-coded in JS: Focus Area (6 flat options), Pharmaceuticals (2 sub-groups), Devices (2 sub-groups)

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
No AEM source file could be located for this component in the mirrored repository, despite the manifest and cross-reference analysis docs both asserting it is "backed by a named AEM source component." A targeted search of the source-system component tree (including the general utility-navigation component already covered under navigation-content) and a repository-wide grep for "pipeline" found no matching component definition, dialog, or Sling Model. There are no confirmed real AEM fields to map against; the EDS-side fields (lastUpdatedDate, focusAreaPath, pharmaceuticalPath, devicesPath, the three icon-type/font/image triplets, clearAllLabel, applyLabel, analyticsInteractionId) are documented above as-is from the real EDS field model, with no source-side counterpart confirmed.

Press Releases — Technical Details

What it does

press-releases fetches a configurable RSS/Atom-style feed URL (read from a site-wide config value, not authored per-instance) and renders the most recent N items as a Splide-style carousel: a horizontally draggable track of press-release cards with previous/next arrow controls and a true infinite loop achieved by cloning the first and last slides. There is no AEM source component behind this block — the manifest's aemSources array is empty, and no folder matching "press" or "release" exists anywhere in the source-system component tree. This is an EDS-native block with no direct predecessor in the source system.

Architecture

Site-wide config (not per-instance authoring) supplies pressReleaseUrl
         ↓
decorate(block):
  pressReleaseUrl = await getConfigValue('pressReleaseUrl')
  count = parseInt(block's numberOfItems field) || 3
         ↓
  block.innerHTML = carousel shell (.press-carousel > .press-carousel-track + .press-carousel-controls)
         ↓
  fetch(feedUrl) → response.text() → DOMParser().parseFromString(..., 'text/xml')
         ↓
  querySelectorAll('item') → slice(0, count) → build .press-card per item
         ↓
  clone first/last slide for infinite-loop illusion, insert at both ends of the track
         ↓
  wire next()/prev() button handlers, drag/swipe (mouse + touch), resize recalculation

Sling Model

None — no AEM source component exists for this block.

Dialog

No AEM dialog exists. The nearest equivalent is the block's Universal Editor field model:

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Press Releases Number of Items to be FetchednumberOfItemsnumberValidated numberMin: 1, numberMax: 5; defaults to 3 in the JS if unset/unparseable

The feed URL itself is not an authorable field at all — it comes from getConfigValue('pressReleaseUrl'), a site-wide configuration lookup, not a per-block dialog field.

HTL

Not applicable — no HTL template exists for this block.

CSS

Not applicable at the AEM layer (no source component). See "EDS Implementation" below for the real, shipped CSS.

Important Constraints

  • The feed URL is a global configuration value shared across the whole site (getConfigValue('pressReleaseUrl')), not authorable per block instance — every press-releases block on the site points at the same feed.
  • Network/parse failure replaces the entire carousel with an inline red-text error message (<p style="color:red">Feed could not be loaded.</p>) and hides the controls — a minimal, unstyled fallback rather than a designed empty/error state.
  • If the feed returns zero <item> elements, or resolves to a single item, the carousel controls are hidden (controls.style.display = 'none') but the track markup remains — a single-item feed still renders as a (non-navigable) carousel shell rather than a plain card.
  • The infinite-loop mechanism relies on cloning DOM nodes and manually correcting index on transitionend when a clone is reached; this is hand-rolled carousel logic (not the actual Splide library) despite the Splide-derived class names (splide__arrow--prev, splide__arrow--next).
  • Drag/swipe and button navigation are both locked out while isTransitioning is true, but there is no debounce on rapid resize events beyond a single resize listener recomputing slide width on every fire.
  • Card titles are inserted via innerHTML template strings built from feed XML text content — if a feed's <title> contains HTML-significant characters, they are not escaped before insertion.

Press Releases Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/press-releases/ as a self-contained RSS-to-carousel widget with a hand-rolled infinite-loop drag/swipe carousel (Splide-styled class names, but not an actual Splide library dependency).

File Structure

blocks/press-releases/
├── press-releases.js
├── press-releases.css
├── _press-releases.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── press-releases.css
│   ├── _press-releases.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
│   ├── press-releases.css
│   ├── _press-releases.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── rinvoq/
    ├── press-releases.css
    ├── _press-releases.css
    └── block-config.js

Authoring Model (_press-releases.json)

{
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "press-releases",
      "fields": [
        {
          "component": "number",
          "name": "numberOfItems",
          "label": "Press Releases Number of Items to be Fetched",
          "valueType": "1",
          "validation": { "numberMin": 1, "numberMax": 5 }
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "filters": [
    { "id": "press-releases", "components": ["press-release"] },
    { "id": "press-release", "components": ["number"] }
  ]
}

A single authorable field — everything else (feed URL, carousel behavior, slide count fallback) is code-driven.

How the JS works (press-releases.js)

Setup reads the global feed URL and the authored item count (default 3), then replaces the block's authored content with the carousel shell:

const pressReleaseUrl = await getConfigValue('pressReleaseUrl');
const DEFAULT_COUNT = 3;
const countEl = block.querySelector('[data-aue-prop="numberOfItems"]');
const feedUrl = pressReleaseUrl?.trim();
const count = parseInt(countEl?.textContent?.trim(), 10) || DEFAULT_COUNT;

block.innerHTML = `
  <div class="press-carousel">
    <div class="press-carousel-track" aria-live="polite"></div>
    <div class="press-carousel-controls">
      <button class="press-arrow press-prev splide__arrow--prev" aria-label="Previous"></button>
      <button class="press-arrow press-next splide__arrow--next" aria-label="Next"></button>
    </div>
  </div>
`;

Feed fetch and parse, with an explicit inline error fallback:

try {
  const xmlText = await fetch(feedUrl).then((r) => r.text());
  xml = new DOMParser().parseFromString(xmlText, 'text/xml');
} catch (e) {
  track.innerHTML = '<p style="color:red">Feed could not be loaded.</p>';
  controls.style.display = 'none';
  return;
}

Each item becomes a card with formatted date and title:

const dateObj = pubDate ? new Date(pubDate) : null;
const formattedDate = dateObj
  ? dateObj.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'long', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric' })
  : 'No date available';

const card = document.createElement('div');
card.className = 'press-card';
card.innerHTML = `
  <a href="${link}" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
    <div class="press-date">${formattedDate}</div>
    <div class="press-title">${title}</div>
  </a>
`;
track.appendChild(card);

The infinite-loop illusion clones the first and last real slides and places them at the opposite ends of the track, then corrects the index silently (no animation) once a clone scrolls fully into view:

const firstClone = slides[0].cloneNode(true);
const lastClone = slides[slides.length - 1].cloneNode(true);
firstClone.classList.add('clone'); lastClone.classList.add('clone');
firstClone.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); lastClone.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
track.insertBefore(lastClone, slides[0]);
track.appendChild(firstClone);

track.addEventListener('transitionend', () => {
  isTransitioning = false;
  if (allSlides[index].classList.contains('clone')) {
    if (index === 0) index = slides.length;
    if (index === allSlides.length - 1) index = 1;
    updatePosition(false); // no animation on the loop-fix jump
  }
});

Navigation is locked during transitions and supports both button clicks and mouse/touch drag with a 60px swipe threshold:

function dragEnd(e) {
  ...
  const delta = endX - startX;
  if (delta < -60) next();
  else if (delta > 60) prev();
  else updatePosition();
}

CSS

:root {
  --press-arrow-size: 40px;
  --press-track-gap: 32px;
  --press-slide-speed: 0.36s;
  --press-slide-easing: cubic-bezier(.2, .9, .2, 1);
}
.press-carousel-track {
  display: flex;
  gap: var(--press-track-gap);
  transition: transform var(--press-slide-speed) var(--press-slide-easing);
  min-height: 152px;
}
.press-card { flex: 0 0 100%; border-radius: 12px; }

Arrow icons use an ::after pseudo-element from the organization's custom icon font (flagged with a stylelint-disable comment for its non-BEM-conforming splide__arrow--* selector names, inherited from the Splide library's conventional class naming even though Splide itself is not used). Title font size steps up at tablet (744px) and desktop (1024px) breakpoints. Each brand folder (abbvie/, botox/, rinvoq/) ships the standard override triplet.

Key differences from the AEM implementation

AEM source systemEDS (press-releases)
Source componentNone existsNet-new, EDS-native block
Feed URL configurationN/ASite-wide config value, not authored per block instance
Carousel mechanismN/AHand-rolled clone-based infinite loop with drag/swipe, styled to resemble Splide but not using the Splide library

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
No AEM source component exists for this block — aemSources is an empty array in the manifest, and no matching component folder ("press," "release") was found under the source-system component tree. There are no AEM fields to map. The single EDS-side field (numberOfItems) is net-new authoring surface with no predecessor; the feed URL itself is not an authorable field on either side (source has none, target reads it from global config).

Promo Drawer — Technical Details

What it does

promo-drawer is a slide-out panel, typically used for a savings/promotional message, that peeks out from the edge of the viewport as a labeled handle and expands into a full content panel on click. The source component (promo-drawer, same name) supports left/right placement, open-on-page-load, and a cookie-remembered "seen"/"closed" state so the drawer can be configured to show once, or hide once dismissed. The target implementation reproduces the same behavior with a simpler, framework-free cookie mechanism and CSS transforms instead of jQuery-driven positioning.

Architecture

Source system:
Author configures anchorId/promoID (cookie name), promoLabel, promoDescription, openFromRight,
expandedOnPageLoad, hideonceSeenbyUser (show-every-time / hide-after-first-view / hide-after-closed)
         ↓
PromoModel.java (Sling Model) exposes the raw fields + builds two "digital data" analytics JSON
strings (open, close) via DigitalDataObject
         ↓
promo-drawer.html renders a single <div class="abbv-promo-drawer" data-view-once=... data-id=...
  data-drawer-align=... data-drawer-load-open=...> with a handle button and a content panel
         ↓
ui/promo-drawer.js (jQuery): abbvDrawer.init() wires handle-click/close-click, reads/writes a cookie
keyed by promoID via a shared cookieObj utility, coordinates repositioning with a "safety bar" component
on the page, and fires analytics via data-digitaldata attribute mutation

Target implementation:
Author configures classes (right/autoopen/once/once-closed), handleLabel, heading, drawerBody, closeLabel, anchorId
         ↓
promo-drawer.js decorateBlock(block):
  readBlock() maps fixed content rows -> { handleLabel, heading, contentRow, closeLabel, anchorId }
  drawerId = cfg.anchorId || block.id || 'promo-drawer'; cookieKey = `promo-drawer-${drawerId}`
  if isOnce && cookie 'seen' -> block.remove()   (no drawer rendered at all)
  if isOnceClosed && cookie 'dismissed' -> block.remove()
  builds handle button + panel (heading, close button, content row) directly, no jQuery
  wires click/Escape-key handlers, optional setTimeout-based auto-open

Sling Model — PromoModel.java

Thin pass-through model plus two analytics "digital data" string builders (open action, close action), and a self-writing interaction ID:

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class}, defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class PromoModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    @Inject private String anchorId;
    @Inject private String promoLabel;
    @Inject private String promoID;
    @Inject private String promoDescription;
    @Inject private String expandedOnPageLoad;
    @Inject private String hideonceSeenbyUser;
    @Inject private String openFromRight;
    @Inject private String showParsys;
    @Inject private String interactionId;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        if (!isPublish() && (StringUtils.isBlank(interactionId) || !interactionId.equals(getId()))) {
            setInteractionIdInComponentDialog();
        }
        interactionId = getInteractionIdFromDialog();
    }

    public String getDigitalDataOpen() {
        String interactionName = checkNullProperty(getExpandedOnPageLoad()).equals("true") ? CLOSE : OPEN;
        // ... builds Link/Component/Interaction/Mva beans, returns JSON via DigitalDataObject
    }
    public String getDigitalDataClose() {
        // ... same pattern, hard-coded to "Close"
    }
}

Dialog

Single-tab dialog, embeds a nested "text" component instance for the drawer body rather than a plain richtext field:

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor IDanchorIdtextfieldDescription explicitly states it also doubles as "the cookie name for options below," and "must be unique" for multiple drawers
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextfield
Promo LabelpromoLabeltextfieldHandle text
Label Icon / Label Icon PositionlabelIcon, labelIconPositionselect (datasource) / select
Promo IDpromoIDtextfield (required)Description: "used as the cookie name to store the state of the drawer" — a second, separate field also described as the cookie name (see Constraints)
Promo DescriptionpromoDescriptionnested "text" component include (includewidget resource type pointing at the shared text/text component)Not a plain textarea/richtext — a full embedded child component
Close IconcloseIconselect (datasource)
Open the drawer on page loadexpandedOnPageLoadcheckbox
Choose the display option for the drawerhideonceSeenbyUserselectShow every page / Hide after first view / Hide after first view only when closed
Open from right side instead of leftopenFromRightcheckbox
Show ParsysshowParsyscheckboxAllows nesting another component inside the drawer via a child parsys
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtextfield (disabled)server-written
Section Title / Localized Section Title for AnalyticssectionTitle, l10Titletextfield
MVA / MVA Title / MVA Value / MVA Secondarymva, mvaTitle, mvaValue, mvaSecondarycheckbox/textfield/select/checkbox

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-use.promo="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.PromoModel">
<div data-sly-attribute.id="${promo.anchorId}" class="abbv-promo-drawer ${promo.customCssClasses}"
  data-view-once="${promo.hideonceSeenbyUser}" data-id="${promo.promoID}"
  data-drawer-align="${promo.openFromRight ? 'right' : 'left'}"
  data-drawer-load-open="${promo.expandedOnPageLoad}">
  <div class="abbv-promo-drawer-container">
    <button class="abbv-promo-drawer-handle" data-digitaldata='${promo.digitalDataOpen}'>
      <span class="abbv-promo-drawer-title ${promo.labelIcon} ...">${promo.promoLabel}</span>
    </button>
    <div class="abbv-promo-drawer-content">
      <button id="abbv-promo-drawer-close" data-digitaldata='${promo.digitalDataClose}'></button>
      <div class="abbv-promo-drawer-description">
        ${promo.promoDescription @ context='html'}
        <sly data-sly-test="${promo.showParsys}">
          <div data-sly-resource="${@ path='promopar', resourceType='wcm/foundation/components/parsys'}"></div>
        </sly>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

Note the data-id="${promo.promoID}" vs. the JCR node id derived from anchorId — two separate identifiers used for two different purposes (see Constraints below).

CSS

Delivered via the component's own clientlibs category (referenced from clientlibs/css.txt/js.txt), not inlined in the HTL. The ui/promo-drawer.css and ui/promo-drawer.php/variations.php files support the internal style-guide/pattern-library preview, separate from the live clientlib.

Important Constraints

  • Two separate fields — anchorId and promoID — are each independently documented in their own field description as "used as the cookie name," but only promoID is actually written into data-id and read by the cookie logic in ui/promo-drawer.js (cookieObj.getCookie($drawer.data('id'))) — anchorId is only used for the DOM element's own id attribute. This is a real, confusing dialog-documentation mismatch in the source system.
  • Only one drawer per page is officially supported; the dialog's own description text says "Currently only 1 drawer per page is supported. Adding a 2nd drawer on the opposite side MAY work" — an explicitly hedged, not guaranteed, multi-instance behavior.
  • The drawer must coordinate its vertical position with a separate "safety bar" component elsewhere on the page (.abbv-safety-bar-button-minus click handler repositions the drawer) — cross-component runtime coupling, not self-contained behavior.
  • The cookie write-on-close logic only distinguishes between "leave as-is" and "mark permanently closed" — there is no explicit re-show/reset mechanism exposed to the visitor once a cookie is set.

Promo Drawer Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/promo-drawer/, flagged Reuse+Enhance in the manifest — per the cross-reference analysis doc, it specifically "resolves anchorId/promoID confusion" from the source component by collapsing to one identifier.

File Structure

blocks/promo-drawer/
├── promo-drawer.js
├── promo-drawer.css
├── _promo-drawer.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── promo-drawer.css
│   ├── _promo-drawer.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
│   ├── promo-drawer.css
│   ├── _promo-drawer.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── rinvoq/
    ├── promo-drawer.css
    ├── _promo-drawer.css
    └── block-config.js

Authoring Model (_promo-drawer.json)

{
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "promo-drawer",
      "fields": [
        {
          "component": "multiselect", "name": "classes", "label": "Variants",
          "options": [
            { "name": "Right side", "value": "right" },
            { "name": "Auto-open on load", "value": "autoopen" },
            { "name": "Show once (hide after first view)", "value": "once" },
            { "name": "Show until closed", "value": "once-closed" }
          ]
        },
        { "component": "text", "name": "handleLabel", "value": "Savings", "label": "Handle Label" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "heading", "value": "", "label": "Drawer Heading" },
        { "component": "richtext", "name": "drawerBody", "value": "", "label": "Drawer Content" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "closeLabel", "value": "Close", "label": "Close Button Label" },
        { "component": "text", "name": "anchorId", "value": "", "label": "Anchor ID" }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "filters": []
}

The four previously separate placement/timing/cookie-persistence toggles (open-from-right, open-on-load, hide after first view, hide after closed) collapse into a single classes multiselect with four independent flags (right, autoopen, once, once-closed), and there is exactly one identifier field, anchorId — the source system's dual anchorId/promoID confusion is gone by construction.

How the JS works (promo-drawer.js)

Content is read from fixed row positions, and the drawer identity/cookie key is derived with a clear single fallback chain:

function readBlock(block) {
  const rows = [...block.children];
  return {
    handleLabel: getCellText(rows[0]) || 'Savings',
    heading: getCellText(rows[1]) || '',
    contentRow: rows[2] || null,
    closeLabel: getCellText(rows[3]) || 'Close',
    anchorId: getCellText(rows[4]) || '',
  };
}
...
const drawerId = cfg.anchorId || block.id || 'promo-drawer';
const cookieKey = `promo-drawer-${drawerId}`;

The "once" and "once-closed" variants can each cause the entire block to remove itself from the DOM before any markup is built, rather than rendering a closed/hidden state:

if (isOnce && getCookie(cookieKey) === 'seen') {
  block.remove();
  return;
}
if (isOnceClosed && getCookie(cookieKey) === 'dismissed') {
  block.remove();
  return;
}

Framework-free cookie helpers replace the source system's shared jQuery cookieObj utility:

function getCookie(name) {
  const match = document.cookie.match(new RegExp(`(^| )${name}=([^;]+)`));
  return match ? match[2] : null;
}
function setCookie(name, value, days) {
  const expires = days ? `; expires=${new Date(Date.now() + days * 864e5).toUTCString()}` : '';
  document.cookie = `${name}=${value}${expires}; path=/`;
}

Open/close toggling sets state via class + aria-expanded, with cookie writes gated to the specific variant that cares about that transition:

function open() {
  block.classList.add('is-open');
  handle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');
  if (isOnce) setCookie(cookieKey, 'seen', 0); // session cookie (no expiry days)
}
function close() {
  block.classList.remove('is-open');
  handle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');
  if (isOnceClosed) setCookie(cookieKey, 'dismissed', 365);
}
handle.addEventListener('click', () => { ... });
closeBtn.addEventListener('click', close);
document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
  if (e.key === 'Escape' && block.classList.contains('is-open')) { close(); handle.focus(); }
});

Auto-open (replacing the source's page-load-open flag) fires after a fixed delay, and self-guards against already-dismissed state:

if (isAutoOpen && !(isOnce && getCookie(cookieKey)) && !(isOnceClosed && getCookie(cookieKey))) {
  setTimeout(open, 1500);
}

Note there is no cross-component "safety bar" repositioning logic in this file — the fixed-position CSS approach (below) does not depend on another component's collapsed/expanded state the way the source system's jQuery repositioning did.

CSS

Pure CSS transform-based slide, no JS-computed pixel positioning:

.promo-drawer {
  position: fixed;
  bottom: var(--safety-bar-collapsed-height, 120px);
  transition: transform 0.35s ease;
}
.promo-drawer.left {
  left: 0; flex-direction: row-reverse;
  transform: translateX(calc(-1 * var(--promo-drawer-size-panel-width)));
}
.promo-drawer.right {
  right: 0; flex-direction: row;
  transform: translateX(var(--promo-drawer-size-panel-width));
}
.promo-drawer.is-open { transform: translateX(0); }
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  .promo-drawer { transition: none; }
}

The header comment states the block is "self-contained, brand-agnostic" — theming is entirely via CSS custom properties (--promo-drawer-color-handle-bg, etc.) with sensible fallback values, rather than requiring a brand override file to render correctly at all. Brand folders (abbvie/, botox/, rinvoq/) still ship the standard override triplet on top of those defaults.

Key differences from the AEM implementation

AEM (promo-drawer)EDS (promo-drawer)
Identifier fieldsTwo separate fields (anchorId, promoID) each documented as "the cookie name," but only one is actually used for the cookie — a real source-side inconsistencyOne field (anchorId), with a clear fallback chain to block.id then a static default
Positioning mechanismjQuery-computed pixel positioning, coordinated with a separate "safety bar" component's collapsed statePure CSS transform: translateX(), self-contained, no cross-component coupling observed
Cookie libraryShared jQuery cookieObj utilityTwo small local helper functions, no dependency
Body content authoringEmbedded child "text" component instance (includewidget) plus optional child parsys for arbitrary nested componentsSingle drawerBody richtext field; no nested-component slot
AnalyticsServer-built JSON "digital data" strings for open/close, injected via data-digitaldata attributesNot present in the shipped JS
Multi-instance supportExplicitly hedged as unofficially supported ("MAY work") for a second drawer on the opposite sideNo stated limitation; cookie keys are already instance-scoped by drawerId

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIdtextfieldanchorIdSame nameSame purpose; target also uses it as (part of) the cookie key, resolving the dual-identifier confusion
promoIDtextfield (required)—Not mappedCollapsed away: the target has a single identifier field (anchorId); the separate cookie-name field is redundant by design in the new model
customCssClassestextfield—Not mappedNo custom-class field in the promo-drawer model per models-normalized.json's 6-field entry
promoLabeltextfieldhandleLabelRenamedSame purpose (handle text), default value "Savings" preserved
labelIcon, labelIconPositionselect (datasource) / select—Not mappedNo icon-on-handle field in the target model; handle is text-only
promoDescriptionnested "text" component includedrawerBodyRenamedSame purpose (drawer body content), simplified from a full embedded child component to a plain richtext field
closeIconselect (datasource)—Not mappedNo icon field for the close button; target's close button styling is fixed CSS (an X drawn from pseudo-elements), paired with closeLabel for its accessible name only
(none — implicit)—closeLabelNot mapped (net-new)Source has no equivalent authorable close-button text/label field; the source close button carries no visible or accessible label field in the dialog
expandedOnPageLoadcheckboxclasses = autoopenRenamedBoolean flag folded into the multiselect variants field; behavior changes from "load already expanded" to "auto-opens after a 1.5s delay" per the shipped JS (setTimeout(open, 1500))
hideonceSeenbyUserselect (3 options: show every page / hide after first view / hide after first view only when closed)classes = once / once-closedRenamedThree-way select becomes two independent multiselect flags (a drawer could combine or omit both, which the source's single select could not directly express)
openFromRightcheckboxclasses = rightRenamedSame purpose, folded into the same multiselect field
showParsyscheckbox (+ child parsys resource)—Not mappedNo nested-component-slot concept in the target model; drawerBody is a single richtext field, not a container for arbitrary child blocks
interactionId, sectionTitle, l10Title, mva, mvaTitle, mvaValue, mvaSecondaryvarious—Not mappedNo analytics/MVA tagging fields exist in the promo-drawer model; the source's getDigitalDataOpen()/getDigitalDataClose() analytics-string generation has no target-side equivalent

Quick Poll — Technical Details

What it does

quick-poll is an interactive poll widget: it displays a question and a set of answer options, lets a visitor click one, submits the response to an external Campaign Manager API, and then shows aggregated percentage results for all respondents. Both source and target implementations remember per-visitor completion via a cookie so a returning visitor sees results immediately rather than being asked again.

Architecture

Source system:
Author selects masterCampaignId (Campaign Manager campaign) + pollName (poll identifier),
optionally overrides questionId/questionText, defines fallback answerOptions multifield,
error messages, results label/description
         ↓
QuickPollModel.java (Sling Model) parses answerOptions (Gson JSON strings) into QuickPollBean list,
each with a per-answer analytics digital-data string; resolves the poll image path
         ↓
quick-poll.html renders: <div class="abbv-quick-poll" data-masterID=... data-pollID=... data-get-api=...>
  question / answer buttons (with data-optionid) / results area / loading+error overlay
         ↓
ui/quick-poll.js (jQuery):
  abbvQuickPoll.init() checks cookie for this pollID
    if seen -> skip building poll options, jump straight to aggregate results request
    if not seen -> buildPoll() -> AJAX GET to AbbViePageInfo.apiEndPoints.getAssessment
  click handler on .qPoll-option -> sendAnswer() -> AJAX POST -> handleAggrRequest() -> renders % results

Target implementation:
Author fills content/poll/results/errors tabs (master-campaign-id, poll-name, question fields,
result-label/description, fallback "option" rows, error-* messages)
         ↓
quick-poll.js decorateBlock(block):
  dual-shape row parsing (xwalk one-cell-per-row vs doc-authoring two-cell key/value rows)
  builds question/options/results/loading/error DOM entirely in JS
  getApiUrl() resolves 3 endpoint URLs from site config (getAssessment/saveAssessment/getAggregated)
  fetchWithTimeout() wraps every call in a 10s AbortController timeout
  cookie (safePollName) gates whether to load fresh question or jump to aggregated results
  click on an option -> submitAnswer() -> POST -> fetchAggregated() -> percentage results

Sling Model — QuickPollModel.java

Parses the answer-options multifield's JSON strings into beans, builds a per-answer analytics digital-data string, and self-writes interaction IDs for both the poll and each individual answer:

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class})
public class QuickPollModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    @Inject @Optional private List<String> answerOptions; // Gson JSON strings, one per answer
    @Inject @Optional private String pollImagefileReference;
    private List<QuickPollBean> quickPollBeans;
    private String quickpollImagePath;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        if (answerOptions != null && !answerOptions.isEmpty()) answerOptions();
        if (!isPublish() && (quickpollInteractionId blank or stale)) setInteractionIdValueInComponentDialog(...);
        if (pollImagefileReference contains "/content/dam/remotedam") {
            pollImagefileReference = CommonUtility.getImagePathBasedonEDAMRendition("large", ...);
        }
        quickpollImagePath = CommonUtility.getImageUrl(pollImagefileReference, resourceResolver, resource);
    }

    private void answerOptions() {
        // Gson-parses each answerOptions[j] into a QuickPollBean (choiceID, choiceText),
        // computes getAnalyticsDigitalData(jObj, j), self-writes interactionId back into
        // the JSON blob, then commits the updated answerOptions array back to the resource.
    }
}

Dialog

Four tabs: Overview, Question & Answers, Error Messages, Analytics:

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Quick Poll Identifier (Anchor ID)anchorIDtextfield
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestextfield
Master CampaignmasterCampaignIdselect (datasource, required)
Poll NamepollNameselect (datasource, required)
Interaction IDquickpollInteractionIdtextfield (disabled)server-written, labeled "Print Parent Interaction ID Here"
Question & Answers tab:
Quick Poll ImagepollImagefile (+ fileName/fileReference)fileupload
Image Title / Image Alt TextimageTitle, imageAltTexttextfield
Question IDquestionIdtextfield (required)Campaign Manager UUID
Question TextquestionTexttextfieldDialog text: if blank, both question and answers come directly from Campaign Manager
Multifield answeroptions (./answerOptions), per answer:
Answer IDchoiceIDtextfield (required)
Answer TextchoiceTexttextfield
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtextfield (disabled)per answer, server-written
Results LabelresultLabeltextfield
Results DescriptionresultDescriptionnested "text" component includeSame embedded-child-component pattern seen in promo-drawer's promoDescription
Error Messages tab:
Poll Get Details Error MessagepollDetailsErrortextareaFires specifically when only a question ID (no text) is authored and the API lookup fails
Poll Save Error MessagesaveErrortextarea
Poll Get Aggregate Error MessagepollAggeregateErrortextareaNote the real, as-shipped typo ("Aggeregate")
Poll Time Out Error MessagepollTimeOutErrortextarea
Analytics tab:
Section Title / Localized Section Title for AnalyticssectionTitle, l10Titletextfield
Analytics TitleanalyticsTitletextfield
MVA / MVA Title / MVA Value / MVA SecondarymvaPrimary, mvaTitle, mvaTypeAndCategory, mvaSecondaryvarious

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-test="${properties.masterCampaignId}">
  <sly data-sly-use.quickPollModel="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.QuickPollModel">
    <sly data-sly-test.getApi="${quickPollModel.quickPollBeans && properties.questionText}"></sly>
    <div class="abbv-quick-poll ${properties.customCssClasses}"
      data-masterID="${properties.masterCampaignId}" data-pollID="${properties.pollName}"
      data-get-api="${getApi ? '' : 'true'}">
      <div class="ia-area">
        <div class="question-container">
          <p class="abbv-question" data-questionID="${properties.questionId}"
             data-sly-text="${getApi ? properties.questionText : ''}"></p>
          <div class="qPoll-options">
            <sly data-sly-list.answerOptions="${quickPollModel.quickPollBeans}">
              <button class="qPoll-option" data-digitaldata='${answerOptions.digitalData}'
                data-optionid="${answerOptions.choiceID}">${answerOptions.choiceText}</button>
            </sly>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div class="answer">
          <p class="responseHeader">${properties.resultLabel}</p>
          <div class="resultSet">...</div>
          ${properties.resultDescription @ context='html'}
        </div>
        <div class="loading">
          <div class="abbv-animation-loading"></div>
          <div class="main-error">
            <span data-err-timeout="${properties.pollTimeOutError}" data-err-no-poll="${properties.pollDetailsError}"
              data-err-fetch-results="${properties.pollAggeregateError}" data-err-save-poll="${properties.saveError}"></span>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </sly>
</sly>

Client script keys everything off a cookie keyed by poll ID, deciding whether to build the question/options UI or skip straight to the aggregated-results request:

let cookieCheck = cookieObj.getCookie($el.data('pollid'));
if (cookieCheck === '1') {
  if ($el.data('get-api')) me.buildPoll($el);
  me.handleAggrRequest($el.children('.ia-area'), $el.data('masterid'));
} else if (this.dataset.getApi == 'true') {
  $el.find('.loading').fadeIn();
  me.buildPoll($el);
}

CSS

Delivered via the component's own clientlib category (quick-poll-component.v1, loaded deferred from the HTL), not inlined; the ui/quick-poll.css/quick-poll.php/ variations.php files support the internal pattern-library preview.

Important Constraints

  • The component depends on a global AbbViePageInfo.apiEndPoints object being present on the page for both the get-assessment and any subsequent AJAX calls — a page-level runtime dependency, not self-contained configuration.
  • No fetch timeout is visible in the client JS beyond whatever ajaxCall's shared implementation provides internally; the dialog does expose a dedicated "Poll Time Out Error Message" field, implying timeout handling exists somewhere in the shared AJAX utility, but it is not visible in this component's own file.
  • Answer analytics digital-data strings are computed once at Sling Model render time and baked into data-digitaldata attributes — if the underlying answer options change without a full page re-render, tracking data can go stale.
  • The dialog's own field description documents a real edge case: "This only happens when just a question ID is authored but no question text is" for the "Poll Get Details Error Message" — a narrow, specific failure mode baked into the UI text rather than the code path being self-evident.

Quick Poll Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/quick-poll/. Per the manifest purpose text, it is "brand-code-aware" (endpoints resolved per brand) and includes explicit request-timeout handling — both directly verifiable in the shipped JS.

File Structure

blocks/quick-poll/
├── quick-poll.js
├── quick-poll.css
├── _quick-poll.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── quick-poll.css
│   ├── _quick-poll.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
│   ├── quick-poll.css
│   ├── _quick-poll.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── rinvoq/
    ├── quick-poll.css
    ├── _quick-poll.css
    └── block-config.js

Authoring Model (_quick-poll.json)

Four tabs (Content, Poll Settings, Results, Error Messages) plus the shared common-properties include:

{
  "component": "text", "name": "master-campaign-id", "value": "", "label": "Master Campaign ID",
