  "description": "UUID from Campaign Manager. Required — block will not render without this."
},
{
  "component": "text", "name": "poll-name", "value": "", "label": "Poll Name (cookie key)",
  "description": "Unique key used as the cookie name to track whether the visitor has already voted. Use kebab-case (e.g. rinvoq-as-poll-1). Required."
},
{
  "component": "richtext", "name": "option", "value": "", "label": "Answer Option (fallback)",
  "description": "Format: 'Option Label | OptionId-UUID'. Used when Campaign Manager API is unavailable. Add one row per option. Must have at least 2 to activate fallback."
}

Unlike the source system's datasource-backed selects for Master Campaign and Poll Name, both are now plain free-text fields — no validated picklist. Answer options are a single richtext field with a documented "Label | OptionId" convention (mirroring the free-text-row convention seen in navigation-content's utility items), rather than a structured multifield of ID/text pairs.

How the JS works (quick-poll.js)

Handles two authoring shapes (Universal Editor one-cell-per-row vs. document-authoring two-cell rows), with an explicit self-correcting fallback if the positional map produces no campaign ID:

const XWALK_FIELDS = [
  'image', 'image-alt', 'master-campaign-id', 'poll-name', 'question-id', 'question-text',
  'result-label', 'result-description', 'option', 'error-timeout', 'error-no-poll',
  'error-fetch-results', 'error-save',
];
const isXwalk = rows.length > 0 && rows.every((r) => r.children.length === 1);
...
// Doc-authoring path, or xwalk fallback when positional map yields no campaign ID
// (indicates XWALK_FIELDS indices shifted — e.g. classes/tab fields gained a row).
if (!isXwalk || !fields['master-campaign-id']) { ... re-parse as key/value rows ... }

Every outbound API call is wrapped in a hard 10-second abort timeout:

const FETCH_TIMEOUT = 10000;
async function fetchWithTimeout(url, options = {}) {
  const controller = new AbortController();
  const id = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), FETCH_TIMEOUT);
  try {
    return await fetch(url, { ...options, signal: controller.signal });
  } finally {
    clearTimeout(id);
  }
}

API endpoint URLs are resolved per-request from site config, and the outbound requests are tagged with the brand code:

async function getApiUrl(key) {
  const value = (await getConfigValue(key)) || '';
  return (value.startsWith('http') || value.startsWith('/')) ? value : '';
}
...
const brandCode = getBrandCode();
[getAssessmentUrl, saveAssessmentUrl, getAggregatedUrl] = await Promise.all([
  getApiUrl('getAssessment'), getApiUrl('saveAssessment'), getApiUrl('getAggregated'),
]);
...
url.searchParams.set('brand', brandCode);
url.searchParams.set('CampaignMasterId', masterCampaignId);

The cookie mechanism gates whether the visitor sees the question again or jumps straight to results, exactly mirroring the source system's cookie-branching structure but reimplemented without jQuery:

if (getCookie(safePollName) === '1') {
  const ok = await loadQuestion(true);
  if (ok) await fetchAggregated();
} else {
  await loadQuestion(false);
}

A local fallback poll (from the authored "option" richtext rows) activates automatically whenever the live API is unreachable, provided at least two options were authored — a resilience feature with no equivalent in the source system's client JS:

} catch (err) {
  if (err?.name === 'AbortError') { showError(errors.timeout); return false; }
  if (authoredOptions.length >= 2) {
    buildOptionButtons(authoredOptions);
    if (!keepLoading) hideLoading();
    return true;
  }
  showError(errors.noPoll);
  return false;
}

Cookies are written with modern security attributes not present in any cookie helper seen elsewhere in this batch:

function setCookie(name, value, days) {
  const expires = new Date(Date.now() + days * 864e5).toUTCString();
  const secure = window.location.protocol === 'https:' ? '; Secure' : '';
  document.cookie = `${encodeURIComponent(name)}=${encodeURIComponent(value)}; expires=${expires}; path=/; SameSite=Lax${secure}`;
}

CSS

Fully custom-property-driven theming, letting brand overrides restyle the whole widget by redefining a small set of tokens:

.quick-poll {
  --qpoll-bg: var(--color-white, #fff);
  --qpoll-border-color: var(--color-border, #e0e0e0);
  --qpoll-option-color: var(--link-color, #035fe6);
  --qpoll-option-hover-bg: var(--link-color, #035fe6);
  --qpoll-option-hover-color: var(--color-white, #fff);
  ...
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: var(--qpoll-gap);
  padding: var(--qpoll-padding);
}

Brand folders (abbvie/, botox/, rinvoq/) ship the standard override triplet on top of these token defaults.

Key differences from the AEM implementation

AEM (quick-poll)EDS (quick-poll)
Campaign/poll selectionValidated datasource-backed selectsFree-text fields, no validated picklist
Answer options authoringStructured multifield (Answer ID, Answer Text, per-answer interaction ID)Single richtext field, "Label \| OptionId" convention per row
API endpoint resolutionGlobal AbbViePageInfo.apiEndPoints object expected on the pagegetConfigValue() per-key async config lookup, resolved per block instance
Brand awarenessNot evident in this component's own filesExplicit getBrandCode() passed as a brand query param on every API call
Timeout handlingImplied by a dedicated dialog error-message field, not directly visible in this component's client JSExplicit 10s AbortController timeout wrapping every fetch
Offline/API-down fallbackNot present in the client JS reviewedAuthored fallback options render automatically when live API calls fail (≥2 authored options required)
Cookie security attributesUses shared jQuery cookieObj utility (attributes not visible in this file)Explicit SameSite=Lax and conditional Secure flag

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtextfield—Not mappedNo anchor-ID field in the quick-poll model; block identity/cookie keying is driven entirely by poll-name
customCssClassestextfieldclassesRenamedSource's free-text custom-class field becomes a constrained variant select (Default / No Image) rather than an arbitrary class list
masterCampaignIdselect (datasource, required)master-campaign-idRenamedSame purpose (Campaign Manager UUID), demoted from validated select to free-text field
pollNameselect (datasource, required)poll-nameRenamedSame purpose and same role as cookie key (explicit in both source dialog help text and target field description); demoted from validated select to free-text field
quickpollInteractionIdtextfield (disabled, server-written)—Not mappedNo analytics interaction-ID field in the target model
pollImagefile / fileReferencefileupload (DAM)imageRenamedSame purpose (poll image), different field type (asset upload → reference)
imageTitletextfield—Not mappedNo separate image-title field in the target model; only alt text is carried forward
imageAltTexttextfieldimage-altRenamedSame purpose
questionIdtextfield (required)question-idRenamedSame purpose (Campaign Manager question UUID); no longer marked required at the field-model level
questionTexttextfieldquestion-textRenamedSame purpose and same optional-override semantics (blank = fetch from API)
answerOptions multifield: choiceID, choiceText, interactionIdmultifield of fieldsetsoptionRenamed (structure collapsed)Three structured sub-fields per answer collapse into one richtext field using a "Label \| OptionId" text convention; per-answer analytics interaction ID is dropped entirely
resultLabeltextfieldresult-labelRenamedSame purpose; target adds a literal default value ("See how others responded") where source has none
resultDescriptionnested "text" component includeresult-descriptionRenamedSame purpose, simplified from an embedded child component to a plain richtext field
pollDetailsErrortextareaerror-no-pollRenamedSame purpose (poll/question lookup failure message)
saveErrortextareaerror-saveRenamedSame purpose (answer submission failure message)
pollAggeregateErrortextareaerror-fetch-resultsRenamedSame purpose; target field name additionally corrects the source's "Aggeregate" typo
pollTimeOutErrortextareaerror-timeoutRenamedSame purpose; target's explicit AbortController timeout gives this field a concrete, directly-observable trigger condition
sectionTitle, l10Title, analyticsTitle, mvaPrimary, mvaTitle, mvaTypeAndCategory, mvaSecondaryvarious—Not mappedNo analytics/MVA tagging fields exist in the quick-poll model; the source's per-answer getAnalyticsDigitalData generation has no target-side equivalent

Quote — Technical Details

What it does

quote is a testimonial/pull-quote block. It renders quotation text plus an attribution (name, title, optional avatar image), with an optional background image and theme/alignment/width styling classes. It supports two content sources: inline-authored "Basic" content, or a reference to an existing Content Fragment (an external structured-content record). This block is unusual in this batch: the manifest lists aemSources: ["quote"], but no component folder named "quote" (or a testimonial/blockquote equivalent) exists anywhere in the mirrored source-system component tree, and the project's own cross-reference gap-analysis document (07-eds-mapping-matrix.md) explicitly annotates this entry as "content/quote (no direct AEM source entry — referenced via rte-lite/testimonial patterns)" with mapping notes of "N/A (existing-side only observation)."

AEM source component — not found in the mirrored repository

A direct search of ui.apps/.../apps/common-elements/v1/components/content/ for "quote," "testimonial," and "blockquote" found no matching component folder, dialog, HTL template, or Sling Model. The project's own analysis explicitly documents this as an existing-EDS-side-only observation rather than a like-for-like migration target — quote-style content in the source system was apparently expressed through the generic rich-text-editor widget (rte-lite, itself "folded into rich-text/other blocks' rich-text fields" per the same analysis doc) rather than a dedicated quote/testimonial component. No real AEM source file could be located for this component, so despite the manifest's aemSources listing, this section covers the EDS-side implementation only.

Sling Model / Dialog / HTL

Not applicable — no AEM source file exists in the mirrored repository for this component (see above).

CSS (AEM side)

Not applicable — no AEM source file exists in the mirrored repository for this component.

Quote Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built

The block is fully implemented at blocks/quote/, and — unusually for this batch — supports three distinct authoring/rendering paths in one decorate(): a "simple imported" 2-row shape (from bulk content migration), a structured "Basic" Universal Editor shape, and a "Content Fragment" shape that fetches external structured content with a two-tier fallback (page-fragment loading, then raw CF JSON).

File Structure

blocks/quote/
├── quote.js
├── quote.css
├── _quote.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── quote.css
│   ├── _quote.css
│   └── block-config.js
├── botox/
│   ├── quote.css
│   ├── _quote.css
│   └── block-config.js
└── rinvoq/
    ├── quote.css
    ├── _quote.css
    └── block-config.js

Authoring Model (_quote.json)

Three tabs — Overview, Styles, Analytics — with a quoteVariant select gating whether the block is in Basic or Content Fragment mode:

{
  "component": "select", "name": "quoteVariant", "value": "basic", "label": "Quote Type", "required": true,
  "options": [
    { "name": "Basic", "value": "basic" },
    { "name": "Content Fragment", "value": "content-fragment" }
  ]
},
{
  "component": "richtext", "name": "quotation", "label": "Quote Text", "description": "Provide the quote",
  "condition": { "in": [{ "var": "quoteVariant" }, ["basic"]] }
},
{
  "component": "aem-content", "name": "quoteFragment", "label": "Quote Fragment",
  "description": "Select a quote from the Content Fragments",
  "condition": { "in": [{ "var": "quoteVariant" }, ["content-fragment"]] }
}

Background image, theme, content alignment, and content width are all top-level (not conditional on quote type) — they apply uniformly whether the block is in Basic or Content Fragment mode:

{ "component": "reference", "name": "backgroundImage", "label": "Background Image" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "backgroundImageAlt", "value": "", "label": "Background Image Alt Text" },
{
  "component": "select", "name": "classes_theme", "label": "Theme",
  "options": [
    { "name": "None", "value": "quote-none" },
    { "name": "Light Theme", "value": "quote-light" },
    { "name": "Dark Theme", "value": "quote-dark" }
  ]
},
{
  "component": "select", "name": "classes_contentWidth", "label": "Content Width", "value": "quote-content-col-none",
  "options": [
    { "name": "Default (No Columns)", "value": "quote-content-col-none" },
    { "name": "Narrow", "value": "quote-content-col-1" },
    { "name": "Small", "value": "quote-content-col-3" },
    { "name": "Medium", "value": "quote-content-col-6" },
    { "name": "Wide", "value": "quote-content-col-9" },
    { "name": "Full Width", "value": "quote-content-col-12" }
  ]
}

An Analytics tab supplies a single analyticsInteractionId text field — the only analytics authoring surface in this model, notably not integrated into an MVA/digital-data system the way the source-system components in this batch consistently are.

How the JS works (quote.js)

The block first detects a "simple imported" 2-row shape (raw migrated content) before falling back to the structured field-model row positions — this dual-path detection appears consistently across several blocks in this repository (parallax, pipeline-utility-nav, quick-poll):

const isSimpleFormat = rows.length <= 2;
const quoteType = isSimpleFormat ? 'basic' : getCellText(rows[ROW.QUOTE_TYPE]) || 'basic';
...
if (isSimpleFormat) {
  blockquote = decorateSimpleQuote(rows);
} else if (quoteType === 'content-fragment') {
  blockquote = await decorateFragmentQuote(rows);
} else {
  blockquote = decorateBasicQuote(rows);
}

decorateSimpleQuote parses attribution out of loosely-structured imported markup — name from an <em>, title from a second <p>:

const em = attrCell.querySelector('em');
const nameText = em?.textContent?.trim() || '';
const paragraphs = attrCell.querySelectorAll('p');
const titleText = paragraphs.length > 1 ? paragraphs[1].textContent.trim() : '';

decorateFragmentQuote normalizes the authored path (stripping a configured root-path prefix and any .html suffix) and then tries, in order: a dashboard-card GraphQL fetch, a full page-fragment load, and finally a raw Content Fragment .model.json endpoint — three fallback tiers for a single authored reference:

try {
  const path = await normalizePath(rawPath);
  const quoteData = await fetchDashboardCardData(path, 'cfBaseUrl');
  renderCfQuote(blockquote, quoteData);
  if (blockquote.childNodes.length > 0) return blockquote;
  const fragment = await loadFragment(path);
  if (fragment) { ... return blockquote; }
} catch (e) { /* page fragment loading failed, try CF fallback */ }

try {
  const cfPath = rawPath.replace(/\.html$/, '');
  const resp = await fetch(`${cfPath}.model.json`);
  if (resp.ok) {
    const cfData = await resp.json();
    renderCFContent(blockquote, cfData);
    if (blockquote.childNodes.length > 0) return blockquote;
  }
} catch (e) { /* CF JSON fetch failed */ }

return null;

A background image can come from the authored backgroundImage field, or — for simple/imported content — be recovered from a standalone image paragraph immediately preceding the block in the DOM, which is then removed from its original position:

if (!bgPicture) {
  const wrapper = block.closest('.quote-wrapper');
  const prev = wrapper?.previousElementSibling;
  if (prev?.classList.contains('default-content-wrapper')) {
    const img = prev.querySelector('img');
    if (img && prev.children.length === 1) {
      bgPicture = prev.querySelector('picture') || img;
      prev.remove();
    }
  }
}

All three rendering paths converge on the same output structure — a semantic <blockquote> with a .quote-quotation div and a .quote-attribution div containing an optional avatar and a name (<p>) / title (<cite>) pair — regardless of whether the content came from inline authoring, a page fragment, dashboard-card GraphQL data, or raw CF JSON.

CSS

Desktop-only grid placement is driven by CSS custom properties set per alignment/width class, ignored below 1024px (mobile/tablet render full-width, single column):

.quote {
  --quote-content-span-desktop: 6;
  --quote-col-start-desktop: 1;
}
.quote.quote-content-col-3  { --quote-content-span-desktop: 3; }
.quote.quote-content-col-12 { --quote-content-span-desktop: 12; }
.quote.quote-align-left   { --quote-col-start-desktop: 2; }
.quote.quote-align-center { --quote-col-start-desktop: 4; }

.quote blockquote::before {
  content: '\e909';
  font-family: var(--icon-font-family);
  color: var(--highlight-dark-background-color);
}

The quotation mark glyph is rendered via an icon-font ::before pseudo-element rather than an authored image or literal character. Brand folders (abbvie/, botox/, rinvoq/) ship the standard override triplet.

Key differences / architectural notes

AEM source systemEDS (quote)
Source componentNone found; quote-style content was expressed via the generic rte-lite rich-text widget per the project's own gap analysis, not a dedicated componentDedicated block with two structured content modes (Basic, Content Fragment) plus a third resilience path for bulk-imported/unstructured content
Content Fragment integrationN/AThree-tier fallback: dashboard-card GraphQL → page-fragment load → raw CF .model.json fetch

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
No AEM source component exists for this block in the mirrored repository, despite the manifest listing aemSources: ["quote"]. The project's own cross-reference gap-analysis document explicitly flags this same conclusion — "no direct AEM source entry — referenced via rte-lite/testimonial patterns" — and marks the mapping columns "N/A (existing-side only observation)." There are no confirmed real AEM fields to map. The EDS-side fields documented above (quoteVariant, quotation, attributionName, attributionTitle, attributionImage, quoteFragment, backgroundImage, backgroundImageAlt, classes_theme, classes_contentAlignment, classes_contentWidth, analyticsInteractionId) are net-new authoring surface with no confirmed source-side counterpart.

Rich Text — Technical Details

What it does

Renders freeform authored copy (headings, paragraphs, lists, tables, links) as sanitized HTML. In the source system this is split across two overlapping components — a lightweight "RTE Lite" used inside other components (e.g. as an included widget) and a full-featured "Rich Text Editor" used directly on parsys-based pages. In the target implementation both collapse into a single reusable rich-text block. The target implementation block additionally does one thing neither source component did: it repairs a publish-pipeline artifact where a footnote sentence ending in an inline link gets split into separate paragraph fragments and auto-decorated as a pill-style button.

Architecture

Author edits RTE field (rte-lite: ./rteLiteText | text: ./text)
         ↓
[rte-lite]  properties.rteLiteText read directly in HTL (no Java model)
[text]      TextComponentModel.java (Sling Model) reads ./text, rewrites
            embedded <img> src paths (dynamic media), optionally tags
            lazy-loading, records an RTE "interaction id" for analytics
         ↓
HTL renders:
  <div class="abbv-rich-text ${customCssClasses}">
    ${text @ context='html'}
  </div>
         ↓ (target implementation)
Universal Editor authors a `richtext` field ("text") directly on the block
         ↓
md2jcr publish flattens the row into a positional <div> — rich-text.js
unwraps the single cell back to top level, then (if `.footnote`) re-flows
any <p> fragments that were split mid-sentence and strips false `.button`
styling that EDS's auto-block-decoration applied to a lone trailing link.

Sling Model — TextComponentModel.java

Backs the "Rich Text Editor" (text) component only — "RTE Lite" has no Java model and reads properties.rteLiteText straight from the resource in HTL.

@Model(adaptables = { Resource.class })
public class TextComponentModel extends AbstractComponentModel {

    private static final String COMPONENT_ID_PREFIX = "rte";

    @Inject @Optional private String anchorID;
    @Inject @Optional private String customCssClasses;
    @Inject @Optional private String text;
    @Inject @Optional private String enableLazyLoading;

    @PostConstruct
    public void init() {
        if (StringUtils.isNotEmpty(text)) {
            if (!isPublish()) {
                CommonUtility.updateRTEInteractionId("text", text, getId(), resource);
            }
            updatedText = updateImageFilesPath(text);
        }
    }

    // Rewrites <img src> via CommonUtility.getImageUrl() (dynamic-media aware)
    // and, when enableLazyLoading is "true", adds loading="lazy" to each <img>.
    private String updateImageFilesPath(String rteText) { /* Jsoup DOM walk */ }

    public String getUpdatedText() { return updatedText; }
    public String getAnchorID() { return anchorID; }
    public String getCustomCssClasses() { return customCssClasses; }
}

The lazy-loading and dynamic-media rewriting are real, non-trivial runtime behaviors — they run on every render, not just at save time.

Dialog

RTE Lite — single-section dialog:

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextSpace-separated
Anchor IDanchorIDtext
Topic ContentrteLiteTextrichtextRestricted RTE toolbar (bold/italic/underline, paragraph format, justify, sub/superscript, special chars) — no image/link-picker plugins configured in this dialog instance

Rich Text Editor ("text") — tabbed, "Overview" tab only:

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextSpace-separated
Anchor IDanchorIDtext
Topic Contenttextrichtext (via an includewidget indirection pointing at a shared full RTE definition)Full RTE toolbar
Enable lazy loading for the imagesenableLazyLoadingcheckboxDefaults checked; only affects <img> tags inside the RTE HTML

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<!-- rte-lite.html -->
<a data-sly-test="${properties.anchorID}" id="${properties.anchorID}"></a>
<div class="abbv-rich-text ${properties.customCssClasses}" data-sly-test="${properties.rteLiteText}">
  ${properties.rteLiteText @context='html'}
</div>

<!-- text.html -->
<sly data-sly-use.textModel="${'com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.TextComponentModel'}">
  <a data-sly-test="${textModel.anchorID}" id="${textModel.anchorID}" aria-hidden="true"></a>
  <div class="abbv-rich-text ${textModel.customCssClasses} abbv-rich-text-common" data-sly-test="${textModel.updatedText}">
    ${textModel.updatedText @ context='html'}
  </div>
</sly>

Note the two components emit visually similar markup (abbv-rich-text) but one reads straight from properties, the other from a Sling Model — meaning image-path rewriting and lazy-loading only apply to content authored through the "text" component, not "RTE Lite".

CSS (source system)

The AEM-side stylesheet (ui/text.css) targets the same .abbv-rich-text class family; the target implementation's CSS is a from-scratch, more exhaustively documented rewrite covering the same semantic surface (headings, lists, tables, blockquote, code, links) plus new style variants not present in the source dialogs (small/large/legal/footnote/boxed-warning/glossary — these map to the target-only classes_textVariant field, discussed below).

Important Constraints

  • Two source components with overlapping purpose but different rendering paths (raw properties vs. Sling Model) — only the "text" component gets image-path rewriting and lazy-loading; "RTE Lite" authors get neither.
  • "RTE Lite" has a deliberately reduced RTE toolbar (no image insertion, limited link options) — it is meant for short inline text fragments embedded in other dialogs, not full page copy.
  • The target implementation is a single unified block, so authors lose the "RTE Lite" toolbar restriction — richer formatting becomes available in contexts where the source system intentionally limited it.
  • The footnote-reflow fix in the target's decorate() is a workaround for a publish-pipeline side effect (fragmenting a sentence that ends in a link into separate paragraphs, then auto-styling the lone-link fragment as a button) rather than an AEM-side behavior being ported — it does not correspond to anything in the source system.

Rich Text Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built
The block is fully implemented at blocks/rich-text/, with per-brand CSS overrides for eight brand folders (only one, venclexta, materially diverges — its rich-text.css/_rich-text.css are 839/572 lines vs. ~236/1 for the others, and linzess similarly diverges at 1111/822 lines).

File Structure

blocks/rich-text/
├── rich-text.js
├── rich-text.css
├── _rich-text.json
├── abbvie/rich-text.css, _rich-text.css
├── botox/rich-text.css, _rich-text.css
├── linzess/rich-text.css, _rich-text.css      (heavily overridden)
├── mavyret/rich-text.css, _rich-text.css
├── rinvoq/, rinvoq-dtc/, rinvoq-hcp/rich-text.css, _rich-text.css
├── skyrizi-hcp/rich-text.css, _rich-text.css
└── venclexta/rich-text.css, _rich-text.css    (heavily overridden)

Authoring Model (_rich-text.json)

{
  "id": "rich-text",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Content", "name": "content" },
    { "component": "richtext", "name": "text", "value": "", "label": "Text", "valueType": "string" },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Styles", "name": "styles" },
    { "component": "select", "name": "classes_textVariant", "label": "Text Variant",
      "options": [ "Default","Small","Large","Legal","Disclaimer","Footnote","Boxed Warning","Glossary" ] },
    { "component": "select", "name": "classes_textAlignment", "label": "Text Alignment",
      "options": [ "Left","Center","Right" ] },
    { "component": "select", "name": "classes_theme", "label": "Theme",
      "options": [ "Light","Dark" ] },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Common Properties", "name": "commonProperties" },
    { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
  ]
}

How the JS works (rich-text.js)

decorate() unwraps the single authored richtext cell from its positional row/cell wrapper, discards leftover placeholder rows ("-", empty, "none"), then, only if the block carries the footnote class, runs reflowFootnoteLinks():

function reflowFootnoteLinks(block) {
  block.querySelectorAll('a.button').forEach((a) => {
    a.classList.remove('button');
    const wrapper = a.closest('.button-container');
    if (wrapper) wrapper.classList.remove('button-container');
  });

  const endsSentence = (el) => /[.!?:]\s*$/.test(el.textContent);
  let current = block.querySelector(':scope > p');
  while (current) {
    const next = current.nextElementSibling;
    if (next && next.tagName === 'P' && !endsSentence(current)) {
      const startsWithPunct = /^\s*[.,!?:;)]/.test(next.textContent);
      if (current.lastChild && !/\s$/.test(current.textContent) && !startsWithPunct) {
        current.append(document.createTextNode(' '));
      }
      while (next.firstChild) current.append(next.firstChild);
      next.remove();
    } else {
      current = next;
    }
  }
}

CSS

Base rich-text.css defines the style variants selected via classes_textVariant (small/large/legal/disclaimer/footnote/boxed-warning/glossary), alignment and theme modifiers, and full typographic rules for headings, lists, links (including a tel: bold-and-nowrap rule and a "(PDF)" suffix on .pdf links), tables, blockquote, code/pre, and hr. Brand folders override the same selector set with brand-specific tokens; abbvie/rich-text.css and botox/rich-text.css are near-identical restatements of the base file, while linzess and venclexta carry substantially larger, brand-specific rule sets.

Key differences from source system

Source (rte-lite / text)Target (rich-text)
Component countTwo separate components with different toolbars and rendering pathsOne block
Image handlingDynamic-media path rewriting + optional lazy-loading (text component only)Not present — target implementation relies on standard EDS/Universal Editor image handling, which already normalizes asset URLs at publish time
Style variantsNone built into either component — styling was via free-text customCssClassesStructured classes_textVariant/classes_textAlignment/classes_theme selects
Footnote handlingN/APublish-pipeline-artifact workaround (re-flow + de-button)

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldComponentTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
rteLiteTextrte-literichtexttextRenamedSame purpose; toolbar restriction from the source dialog is not carried over — target implementation exposes the full richtext toolbar for all instances
texttextrichtext (via includewidget)textRenamedSame purpose, single unified field name in the target model
anchorIDbothtext(none)Not mappedNo equivalent field on the rich-text model; anchor/id targeting in the target implementation is handled by common properties (id) rather than a component-specific field
customCssClassesbothtext(none, superseded)Not mappedFree-text CSS classes are replaced by the structured classes_textVariant/classes_textAlignment/classes_theme selects plus the shared common-properties classes field — authors no longer hand-type class names
enableLazyLoadingtextcheckbox(none)Not mappedNo target field — lazy-loading of images inside rich text is not reproduced as an explicit authoring toggle in the target implementation

Safety Bar — Technical Details

Highest compliance risk item in this analysis. This component renders regulatory Important Safety Information (ISI) and Use statements. Any behavioral drift between the source system and the target implementation risks silently dropping or duplicating safety copy.

What it does

Renders a bottom-docked, collapsible bar with a minimized ("docked") state and a maximized ("expanded") state, toggled by separate plus/minus controls. The minimized state has independently-authored desktop and mobile copy, each with a "first impression" (more) and "after interaction" (less) variant. The maximized state renders the full Use statement, full ISI, and (optionally) Miscellaneous ISI.

Architecture — and the real content-sourcing precedence

The critical, easy-to-miss fact about this component: the source system's maximized-view content does not come from a single authored field. SafetyBarModel.java assembles it from up to three different places, with real, code-enforced precedence:

1. Page Properties "ADMP tab" (inherited page metadata: globalSafetyFolderPath +
   indication-page lookup) → PageUtility.getSafetyContentPar("use"/"isi"/"miscisi")
   — WINS when a matching content-par path is found.
        ↓ (only reached if step 1 found nothing for BOTH Use and ISI)
2. Component-level ./col1MaximizedContentPar / ./col2MaximizedContentPar
   — populated from ./col1FlexPage / ./col2FlexPage (a pathbrowser field
   pointing at a "flexible page")
        ↓
3. ./col1FlexPage itself is labeled in the dialog "Safety Bar Maximized
   Content - Deprecated" and is disabled in the authoring UI — it is dead
   authoring surface. Any value already stored there from historical content
   still flows through step 2's fallback path, but new authoring is blocked.

The real precedence, taken directly from SafetyBarModel.init() and the HTL template, is: page-level Use/ISI wins outright; component-level col1MaximizedContentPar/col2MaximizedContentPar is used only as a fallback when both page-level Use and ISI are empty; the deprecated col1FlexPage/col2FlexPage pathbrowser fields are the (inert, author-disabled) source that fallback content par is derived from. The HTL enforces this explicitly:

<sly data-sly-test="${!safetyBarModel.safetyISI && !safetyBarModel.safetyUse}">
    <div data-sly-test="${safetyBarModel.col1MaximizedContentPar}" ...>
        <div data-sly-resource="${safetyBarModel.col1MaximizedContentPar}" data-sly-unwrap></div>
    </div>
    ...
</sly>

i.e. the component-level fallback block is only rendered at all when both safetyISI and safetyUse are falsy. The page-level lookup itself (PageUtility.getSafetyContentPar) has its own internal precedence: an indication-specific safety page (safetyFolderPath/indicationPageName/isi) wins over the brand-default safety page (safetyFolderPath/isi) when both exist.

Sling Model — SafetyBarModel.java

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class SafetyBarModel extends AbstractComponentModel {

    @Inject @Named("col1FlexPage") @Optional private String col1FlexPage;   // deprecated, disabled in dialog
    @Inject @Named("col2FlexPage") @Optional private String col2FlexPage;   // deprecated, disabled in dialog
    @Inject @Named("miscISI") @Optional private String miscISI;
    @Inject @Named("enableMobileScroll") @Optional private String enableMobileScroll;
    // ... col1/col2 TextDocked{More,Less}{Desktop,Mobile} — 8 minimized-state fields

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        // 1. Derive component-level fallback content-par paths from the
        //    deprecated flex-page fields (still evaluated even though the
        //    dialog field is disabled — old content keeps working).
        if (StringUtils.isNotEmpty(col1FlexPage)) {
            col1MaximizedContentPar = getMaximizedContentPar(col1FlexPage);
        }
        // 2. Resolve page-level Use/ISI/MiscISI via inherited page properties.
        Page currentPage = resourceResolver.adaptTo(PageManager.class).getContainingPage(resource);
        InheritanceValueMap inheritedPageProperties =
            new HierarchyNodeInheritanceValueMap(currentPage.getContentResource());
        String safetyFolderPath = inheritedPageProperties.getInherited(GlobalConstants.SAFETY_FOLDER_PATH, String.class);
        String indicationPageName = PageUtility.getIndicationPageName(currentPage, resourceResolver, inheritedPageProperties);

        safetyISI = PageUtility.getSafetyContentPar(safetyFolderPath, indicationPageName, "isi", resourceResolver);
        safetyUse = PageUtility.getSafetyContentPar(safetyFolderPath, indicationPageName, "use", resourceResolver);
        if (isMiscIsiDisplayed()) {
            safetyMiscISI = PageUtility.getSafetyContentPar(safetyFolderPath, indicationPageName, "miscisi", resourceResolver);
        }
    }
}

PageUtility.getSafetyContentPar (the page-level lookup): indication-specific page wins over brand-default page, both resolved under the inherited globalSafetyFolderPath:

public static String getSafetyContentPar(String safetyFolderPath, String indicationPageName,
        String safetyTypeNodeName, ResourceResolver resourceResolver) {
    Page defaultSafetyPage = pageManager.getPage(safetyFolderPath + '/' + safetyTypeNodeName);
    Page safetyPage = null;
    if (StringUtils.isNotEmpty(indicationPageName)) {
        safetyPage = pageManager.getPage(safetyFolderPath + '/' + indicationPageName + '/' + safetyTypeNodeName);
    }
    if (null != safetyPage) {
        safetyNodeParPath = getContentParPath(safetyPage);        // indication-specific wins
    } else if (defaultSafetyPage != null) {
        safetyNodeParPath = getContentParPath(defaultSafetyPage); // falls back to brand default
    }
    return safetyNodeParPath;
}

Dialog

TabFieldPropertyTypeNote
OverviewAnchor IDanchorIDtext
OverviewCustom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestext
OverviewSafety Bar Minimized LayoutlayoutDockedselect"Half & Half" (abbv-col-6|abbv-col-6) or "Full Width" (abbv-col-12)
OverviewEnable mobile scroll in collapsed viewenableMobileScrollcheckboxOpt-in; default false; lets minimized content scroll on mobile instead of being clipped
OverviewInclude MiscISI in expanded safety barmiscISIcheckbox
OverviewSafety Bar Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtext (disabled, auto-set)
First ColumnDesktop — More Information Visiblecol1TextDockedMoreDesktoprichtext (via includewidget)"First impression" copy
First ColumnMobile — More Information Visiblecol1TextDockedMoreMobilerichtext
First ColumnDesktop — Less Information Visiblecol1TextDockedLessDesktoprichtextShown after first interaction
First ColumnMobile — Less Information Visiblecol1TextDockedLessMobilerichtext
First ColumnSafety Bar Maximized Content - Deprecatedcol1FlexPagepathbrowser (disabled)Dead authoring field; description literally says "Please author this in the Page Properties - ADMP tab"
Second Column(same four Desktop/Mobile More/Less fields for column 2)col2TextDockedMoreDesktop, col2TextDockedMoreMobile, col2TextDockedLessDesktop, col2TextDockedLessMobilerichtextNo visible dialog field for col2FlexPage in this dialog instance, though the model injects it

Note what is not in this dialog: there is no field to author the maximized Use/ISI/MiscISI text directly. That content is explicitly documented as living in "Page Properties ADMP tab" — a separate, page-level authoring surface outside this component's dialog entirely.

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<div class="abbv-safety-bar ${indicationOn?'abbv-indication-swap':''} ${customCssClasses} ${enableMobileScroll?'abbv-safety-bar-mobile-scroll':''}">
  <div class="abbv-safety-bar-button-plus ..." role="button" aria-label="Expand Safety Information" .../>
  <div class="abbv-safety-bar-button-minus ..." aria-label="Minimize Safety Information" .../>  <!-- no role="button" — asymmetric a11y gap -->
  <div class="abbv-safety-bar-content abbv-safety-bar-less abbv-safety-bar-content-minimized-desktop">...col1/2TextDockedLessDesktop...</div>
  <div class="abbv-safety-bar-content abbv-safety-bar-less abbv-safety-bar-content-minimized-mobile">...col1/2TextDockedLessMobile...</div>
  <div class="abbv-safety-bar-content abbv-safety-bar-more abbv-safety-bar-content-minimized-desktop-more">...col1/2TextDockedMoreDesktop...</div>
  <div class="abbv-safety-bar-content abbv-safety-bar-more abbv-safety-bar-content-minimized-mobile-more">...col1/2TextDockedMoreMobile...</div>
  <div class="abbv-safety-bar-content abbv-safety-bar-content-maximized">
    <div data-sly-test="${safetyUse}"><div data-sly-resource="${safetyUse}" data-sly-unwrap/></div>
    <div data-sly-test="${safetyISI}"><div data-sly-resource="${safetyISI}" data-sly-unwrap/></div>
    <div data-sly-test="${safetyMiscISI}"><div data-sly-resource="${safetyMiscISI}" data-sly-unwrap/></div>
    <sly data-sly-test="${!safetyISI && !safetyUse}">
      <div data-sly-test="${col1MaximizedContentPar}"><div data-sly-resource="${col1MaximizedContentPar}" data-sly-unwrap/></div>
      <div data-sly-test="${col2MaximizedContentPar}"><div data-sly-resource="${col2MaximizedContentPar}" data-sly-unwrap/></div>
    </sly>
  </div>
</div>

Important Constraints (source system)

  • The expand button has role="button"; the collapse (minus) button does not — a real, asymmetric accessibility gap in the source markup.
  • Desktop and mobile minimized copy are independently authored fields, not CSS-driven truncations of one another — mobile copy can legitimately differ in content, not just layout.
  • The maximized-view precedence (page-level wins, component-level fallback, dead flex-page field) is implicit in code, not visible to authors from the dialog alone — an author editing this component's dialog cannot tell where the maximized content is actually coming from.

Safety Bar Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. blocks/safety-bar/ implements a sticky, floating bar with expand/collapse and an is-expanded/aria-expanded toggle. The real, live field model has only four fields — confirmed against the authoritative parsed model (models-normalized.json), not the illustrative field list found in prior gap-analysis notes:

FieldComponentNotes
collapsedContentrichtextCollapsed-state content; for the split variant this is the left column
collapsedContentCol2richtextRight column, split variant only
expandedContentrichtextFull ISI content shown when expanded
classes_variantselect"" (default), center, split

This is a materially smaller surface than the source system's 8-field minimized-state model (desktop/mobile × more/less × 2 columns) plus its 3-tier maximized-content sourcing. A prior internal proposal sketched an expanded field set for this block — including a two-value layout select, a mobile-scroll toggle, a per-instance includeMiscISI checkbox, and separately-authored desktop/mobile minimized copy — intended to close that gap. None of that proposed field set is implemented. It should be treated strictly as a design proposal, not a shipped capability, until it appears in the live model.

File Structure

blocks/safety-bar/
├── safety-bar.js
├── safety-bar.css
├── _safety-bar.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/, botox/, linzess/, mavyret/, rinvoq/, rinvoq-hcp/, skyrizi-hcp/, venclexta/
│   ├── safety-bar.css / _safety-bar.css
│   └── block-config.js

Authoring Model (_safety-bar.json)

{
  "id": "safety-bar",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Content", "name": "content" },
    { "component": "richtext", "name": "collapsedContent", "value": "", "label": "Collapsed Content",
      "description": "Text shown in the bar when collapsed. For the 'split' variant, this is the left column." },
    { "component": "richtext", "name": "collapsedContentCol2", "value": "",
      "label": "Collapsed Content – Column 2 (Split only)",
      "description": "Right column content in collapsed state. Only used with the 'split' variant." },
    { "component": "richtext", "name": "expandedContent", "value": "", "label": "Expanded Full ISI Content",
      "description": "Complete Important Safety Information displayed when the bar is expanded." },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Settings", "name": "settings" },
    { "component": "select", "name": "classes_variant", "label": "Variant",
      "options": [
        { "name": "Default – single column, right aligned", "value": "" },
        { "name": "Center – single column, collapsed center aligned", "value": "center" },
        { "name": "Split – two-column collapsed, single-column expanded", "value": "split" }
      ] },
    { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
  ]
}

How the JS works (safety-bar.js)

The authored block is never rendered in place — decorate() hides it (or its whole section, if it's the section's only block) and instead builds a detached, document.body-appended sticky bar from the extracted content:

function extractContentFields(block, isSplit) {
  const hasAueProps = block.querySelector('[data-aue-prop]');
  if (hasAueProps) {
    return {
      collapsed: getNonNestedProp(block, 'collapsedContent'),
      collapsedCol2: isSplit ? getNonNestedProp(block, 'collapsedContentCol2') : null,
      expanded: getNonNestedProp(block, 'expandedContent'),
    };
  }
  // positional fallback for published (non-UE-instrumented) markup — rows[0]/[1]/[2]
  ...
}

export default function decorate(block) {
  const isSplit = block.classList.contains('split');
  const { collapsed, collapsedCol2, expanded } = extractContentFields(block, isSplit);

  const section = block.closest('.section');
  const blockWrappers = section.querySelectorAll(':scope > div[class*="-wrapper"]');
  if (blockWrappers.length <= 1) { section.hidden = true; }
  else { block.closest('.safety-bar-wrapper')?.remove(); section.classList.remove('safety-bar-container'); }

  // ... builds stickyBlock (.safety-bar), abbrevEl (.safety-bar-abbreviated),
  // a toggle button, and lazily creates .safety-bar-full only when first expanded.
}

The toggle is a real <button> with aria-expanded and a dynamic aria-label ("Expand Important Safety Information" / "Collapse Important Safety Information") — the source system's asymmetric role/no-role gap between its plus and minus controls is not reproduced, since the target implementation uses one button that toggles state rather than two separate elements.

CSS

Base safety-bar.css defines block-scoped custom properties (--safety-bar-bg, --safety-bar-toggle-bg, etc.) that brand folders override; fixed-position sticky section, a scrollable .safety-bar-full panel capped at 60vh (50vh at desktop width), a circular +/− toggle, a dark full-screen .safety-bar-overlay shown while expanded, and dedicated rules for the center and split variants (the latter using CSS grid for the two-column collapsed layout, collapsing to a single scrollable block under 744px).

Key differences from source system

Source (safety-bar, AEM)Target (safety-bar block)
Minimized-state fields8 (desktop/mobile × more/less × col1/col2)2–3 (collapsedContent, collapsedContentCol2 for split only)
"First impression" vs. post-interaction copyExplicitly separate authored fields (More/Less)Not modeled — one collapsed-content field regardless of interaction history
Maximized content source3-tier precedence: page-level Use/ISI > component fallback > deprecated flex-pageSingle expandedContent richtext field, authored directly on the block instance
MiscISIExplicit checkbox + separate page-level MiscISI content-par lookupNot present as a distinct field in the live model (a separate MiscISI toggle has been proposed but is not implemented)
Mobile scroll behaviorExplicit enableMobileScroll opt-in checkboxNot present as an authoring field in the live model
Toggle accessibilityTwo elements, asymmetric role usage (plus has role="button", minus does not)One real <button> with aria-expanded toggling state

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
col1TextDockedLessDesktoprichtextcollapsedContentRenamed (narrowed)Source has a separate "Less" (post-interaction) desktop variant; target collapses more/less and desktop/mobile into one field
col1TextDockedMoreDesktoprichtext(absorbed into collapsedContent)Not mappedNo distinct "first impression" field exists on the live target model — the more/less distinction is dropped
col1TextDockedLessMobilerichtext(none)Not mappedNo separate mobile-specific collapsed content field on the live model; mobile presentation is CSS-responsive only
col1TextDockedMoreMobilerichtext(none)Not mappedSame reason as above
col2TextDockedLessDesktoprichtextcollapsedContentCol2Renamed (narrowed)Only used when classes_variant=split; more/less and desktop/mobile distinctions dropped as above
col2TextDockedMoreDesktop, col2TextDockedLessMobile, col2TextDockedMoreMobilerichtext(none)Not mappedSame reason as col1 more/mobile variants
col1FlexPage / col2FlexPagepathbrowser(none)Not mappedGenuinely deprecated in the source system itself (disabled field, "Deprecated" label) — correctly not carried forward
Page-level safetyUse / safetyISI (ADMP tab, inherited page property lookup)n/a (page metadata, not a component field)expandedContentRenamed / re-scopedSource resolves this from page-level metadata with indication-aware fallback; target authors it directly per block instance — the page-metadata provenance model and its indication-specific override are not reproduced
miscISI (checkbox) + page-level MiscISI content-par lookupcheckbox + page metadata(none)Not mappedNo equivalent in the live 4-field model; a MiscISI toggle (includeMiscISI) has been proposed for a future iteration but is not implemented
layoutDockedselect (Half/Half, Full Width)classes_variantRenamedRe-modeled as a 3-way variant (default/center/split) rather than the source's 2-way column-width select — not a 1:1 value mapping
enableMobileScrollcheckbox(none)Not mappedNo equivalent field in the live model; not confirmed whether the target block's fixed-height collapsed state reproduces the source's opt-in scroll behavior at all
anchorIDtext(none)Not mappedNo component-specific anchor field; common-properties id, if used, would take this role generically
customCssClassestext(none, superseded)Not mappedReplaced by structured classes_variant plus shared common-properties classes field
interactionIdtext (disabled, auto-set)(none)Not mappedSource-system analytics interaction tracking has no counterpart field in the live model

Search — Technical Details

What it does

Renders a full search-results experience: an input box plus a results list, either backed by a live query index (target implementation) or a hosted third-party search widget (source system's Coveo-based results component and companion page templates).

Architecture

[source system]
Author places Search Results Coveo component + selects a
"Search Page Type" (basic vs. faceted) → resolved against a
global admin config page to an externally-hosted search page id
         ↓
SearchResultsCoveoModel.java resolves:
  - coveoHostedSearchPageId  (via FormsConfigUtil.getTitleByValue
    against /content/admin/admp-global/coveo-search-config)
  - coveoFacetListString     (JSON built from page-property tags,
    used to render optional category facets)
         ↓
HTL emits a <hosted-search-page id="hsp"> custom element and a small
inline <script> that sets window.coveosearch = { coveoHostedSearchPageId }
— the actual search UI and results are rendered by an externally loaded
Coveo widget script, not by AEM-rendered markup.

[target implementation]
decorate() reads a query-index source (an authored link, else the site's
query-index.json) and placeholders, builds a search box + <ul> results
list entirely client-side; filters/highlights results in-browser against
the fetched index — no external search vendor involved.

Sling Model — SearchResultsCoveoModel.java

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class SearchResultsCoveoModel {
    @Inject @Named("anchorID") @Optional private String anchorID;
    @Inject @Named("customCssClasses") @Optional private String customCssClasses;
    @Inject @Named("searchpagetype") @Default(values = "basic") private String searchpagetype;
    @Inject @Named("brandName") @Optional private String brandName;

    private String coveoHostedSearchPageId;
    private String coveoFacetListString;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        getGlobalCoveoConfig();   // resolves coveoHostedSearchPageId from a global admin config page
        initCoveoFacetList();     // builds coveoFacetListString JSON from page-property tags
    }
}

This model has no counterpart for actually rendering results — it only resolves configuration that is handed off to an externally-hosted widget via window.coveosearch.

Dialog (Search Results Coveo)

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor IDanchorIDtext
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestext
Search Page Typesearchpagetypeselect (datasource-driven, default "basic")"Basic" = single search input; "Faceted" adds category facets

There are also two dedicated page-component templates in the source system (search-results-coveo-page and search-results-page) — full page templates pre-wired for search-results layout, rather than droppable content components.

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-use.coveoSearchModel="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.SearchResultsCoveoModel">
  <div class="coveoFacetDiv" data-coveoFacetListString="${coveoSearchModel.coveoFacetListString}"></div>
  <script type="text/javascript" data-sly-use.scriptObjTemp="search-results-coveo-script-object.html"
          data-sly-call="${scriptObjTemp.scriptObj @coveoSearchModel=coveoSearchModel}"></script>
  <hosted-search-page id="hsp"></hosted-search-page>
</sly>

<!-- search-results-coveo-script-object.html -->
<template data-sly-template.scriptObj="${@coveoSearchModel}">
    window.coveosearch={
        coveoHostedSearchPageId:'${coveoSearchModel.coveoHostedSearchPageId @context="html"}'
    }
</template>

Important Constraints (source system)

  • Actual search execution, ranking, and rendering happen entirely inside a third-party hosted widget identified only by an opaque coveoHostedSearchPageId — the AEM component is a thin configuration/bootstrap layer, not a search implementation.
  • Facet configuration is sourced from page-level tags, not authored directly on the component.
  • Search page type ("basic" vs. "faceted") is resolved against a global admin config page at render time, meaning behavior can change without touching the component instance.

Search Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. blocks/search/ is a complete, self-contained client-side search implementation with no external search vendor dependency.

File Structure

blocks/search/
├── search.js
├── search.css
├── _search.json
├── block-config.js
└── abbvie/, botox/, rinvoq/ (search.css, _search.css, block-config.js)

Authoring Model (_search.json)

{
  "id": "search",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "index", "label": "Index" },
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "mobilePlaceholder", "label": "Mobile Placeholder" },
    { "component": "multiselect", "name": "classes", "value": "", "label": "Options", "valueType": "string",
      "options": [ { "name": "Search Type", "children": [ { "name": "Minimal", "value": "minimal" } ] } ] }
  ]
}

How the JS works (search.js)

decorate() reads a source (authored link, else query-index.json) and a mobile placeholder from the authored rows, builds a search box and results list, and on input fetches the index, filters client-side, and highlights matched terms:

export default async function decorate(block) {
  const placeholders = await fetchPlaceholders();
  const source = block.querySelector('a[href]')?.href || `${window.hlx.codeBasePath}/query-index.json`;
  const mobilePlaceholder = [...block.querySelectorAll(':scope > div > div')]
    .find((el) => !el.querySelector('a'))?.textContent?.trim();
  block.innerHTML = '';
  block.append(
    searchBox(block, { source, placeholders: { ...placeholders, mobilePlaceholder } }),
    searchResultsContainer(block),
  );
  if (searchParams.get('q')) { /* pre-fill + trigger search from ?q= */ }
  decorateIcons(block);
}

Result heading level is inferred contextually rather than hardcoded — findNextHeading() walks backwards through preceding siblings to find the last heading on the page and picks the next level down (capped at H6):

function findNextHeading(el) {
  let preceedingEl = el.parentElement.previousElement || el.parentElement.parentElement;
  let h = 'H2';
  while (preceedingEl) {
    const lastHeading = [...preceedingEl.querySelectorAll('h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6')].pop();
    if (lastHeading) {
      const level = parseInt(lastHeading.nodeName[1], 10);
      h = level < 6 ? `H${level + 1}` : 'H6';
      preceedingEl = false;
    } else {
      preceedingEl = preceedingEl.previousElement || preceedingEl.parentElement;
    }
  }
  return h;
}

Matched-term highlighting wraps substrings in <mark> via highlightTextElements(), applied to both result titles and descriptions. Search terms below 3 characters clear results rather than querying. Query state round-trips to the URL (?q=) via history.replaceState, so a search is shareable/bookmarkable and re-runs on load.

CSS

Grid-based result cards (repeat(auto-fill, minmax(278px, 1fr))) with a bordered card per result by default; a minimal variant switches to a single-column list with a small circular thumbnail floated left of the title/description and link-colored (not full-card) styling — selected via the classes multiselect's "Minimal" option.

Key differences from source system

Source (search-results-coveo)Target (search block)
Search executionDelegated to externally-hosted third-party widgetEntirely client-side, against a local query-index.json
FacetingPage-tag-driven category facets (faceted page type)Not present — no facet/category filtering in the target block
Result highlightingDelegated to the hosted widget (behavior unknown/opaque)Explicit in-block <mark>-based term highlighting
Configuration sourceGlobal admin config page, resolved at render timeAuthored per-block (source link + placeholder text)

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
searchpagetypeselect (basic/faceted)(none directly; partially classes)Not mappedNo faceted mode exists in the target block; the target's classes: minimal option controls visual density, not faceting, so it is not a real substitute
anchorIDtext(none)Not mappedNo component-specific anchor field on the target model
customCssClassestextclassesRenamedFree-text classes replaced by a structured multiselect, currently offering only the "Minimal" option
brandNametext(none)Not mappedBrand-specific search behavior in the target implementation is handled by per-brand CSS/JS folders, not an authored field
(implicit: hosted search page id, resolved from global config)n/aindexRenamed / re-scopedSource resolves a search backend id from global admin config; target authors a concrete index/source URL directly on the block instance — fundamentally different sourcing model, not a like-for-like rename

Search Input — Technical Details

What it does

A standalone search input box — no results rendering of its own. On submit it validates and sanitizes the typed query, then navigates the browser to a configured results page with the query as a URL parameter. In the source system this is the shared search-box component, which has three HTL rendering variants (classic, lite, coveo) selected by which template is included, plus optional in-page suggestion dropdown wiring.

Architecture

[source system — search-box, "classic" variant shown]
properties.resultsUrl configured (pathbrowser)
         ↓
LinkChecker (Sling Model) validates/normalizes the results URL
         ↓
HTL renders a toggle span + hidden input[type=text] with
data-suggest-toggle / data-max-suggestions / data-char-trigger attributes
         ↓
ui/search-box-basic.js (client-side, not shown in dialog-driven data)
reads those data-* attributes to decide whether/how to show a
suggestions dropdown and where to submit the typed query

[target implementation — search-input block]
decorate() reads 3 authored rows (results page link/path, desktop
placeholder, mobile placeholder) → builds a floating-label <form
role="search"> with client-side validation + sanitization, submits by
navigating to `${searchResultsPath}?q=${sanitized}&_charset_=UTF-8`

Dialog (search-box)

No dedicated Sling Model — fields are read directly as properties.* in HTL, with one inline model use for link validation (LinkChecker).

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Search Placeholder TextsearchInputPlaceHoldertext
Search Results Page URLresultsUrlpathbrowser (rootPath /content/)Passed through LinkChecker for href normalization
Enable Search SuggestionautoSuggestcheckboxDescription explicitly notes suggestions are disabled for "basic" search regardless of this flag
Number of Maximum SuggestionsmaxSuggestionsnumberfield
Length of Character after which Search is triggeredcharTriggernumberfield
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestext

HTL — Key DOM Structure (classic variant)

<span class="abbv-search-toggle" role="button" tabindex="0" aria-expanded="false"
      aria-label="${properties.searchInputPlaceHolder}"><i class="abbv-icon-search i-a"></i></span>
<div class="abbv-search-navigation abbv-hide-mobile abbv-hide-tablet">
  <sly data-sly-use.link="${'com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.LinkChecker' @link = properties.resultsUrl}" />
  <a aria-hidden="true" style="display:none" href="${link.linkFormatted}"></a>
  <input id="header-search" type="text" maxlength="100" role="searchbox" aria-label="Search"
         class="abbv-search ${properties.customCssClasses}"
         placeholder="${properties.searchInputPlaceHolder}"
         data-suggest-toggle="${properties.autoSuggest ? properties.autoSuggest : 'no'}"
         data-results-page="" data-max-suggestions="${properties.maxSuggestions}"
         data-char-trigger="${properties.charTrigger}">
  <em role="button" class="abbv-icon-search i-a"></em>
  <div class="abbv-search-suggestions"><ul></ul></div>
</div>

The "basic" variant (search-box.html) is a simpler single .abbv-search-v2-input-container input without the toggle/suggestions chrome; both variants share the same dialog fields.

Important Constraints (source system)

  • Suggestion dropdown behavior is entirely client-JS-driven via data-* attributes on the input — the dialog fields configure it but don't guarantee it activates ("Enable Search Suggestion" is explicitly documented as a no-op for basic search).
  • Results page URL is validated through LinkChecker, but the actual submit/navigation wiring lives in an external clientlib JS file not included in the dialog-adjacent files, so the exact submit mechanics (form vs. JS location assignment) are not visible at the component-definition level.

Search Input Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. blocks/search-input/ is fully implemented with client-side validation, XSS-safe sanitization, and a responsive floating-label input. Unlike most other blocks in this batch, it has no per-brand CSS/config subfolders — styling is brand-agnostic, relying entirely on shared CSS custom properties (e.g. --color-navy-dark).

File Structure

blocks/search-input/
├── search-input.js
├── search-input.css
└── _search-input.json

Authoring Model (_search-input.json)

{
  "id": "search-input",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Overview", "name": "tabOverview" },
    { "component": "aem-content", "valueType": "string", "name": "searchResultsPagePath",
      "label": "Search Results Page Path", "required": true,
      "description": "Enter the path for the search results page." },
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "searchButtonPlaceholder",
      "label": "Search Button Placeholder Text",
      "description": "Placeholder text displayed on the search action/button in desktop and larger viewports. Optional; shown when no custom label is provided." },
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "searchButtonPlaceholderMobile",
      "label": "Search Button Placeholder Text (Mobile View)",
      "description": "Placeholder text for the search action/button optimized for mobile view. Optional; used when mobile-specific label differs from desktop." },
    { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
  ]
}

How the JS works (search-input.js)

Reads three authored rows positionally (results path/link, desktop placeholder, mobile placeholder), builds a <form role="search"> with a floating label, a hidden _charset_ field, and an ARIA live-region alert box for validation errors:

function sanitize(str) {
  const div = document.createElement('div');
  div.textContent = str;
  return div.innerHTML;
}

form.addEventListener('submit', (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  const trimmedValue = input.value.trim();
  if (!trimmedValue) {
    alertBox.classList.add('visible');
    input.classList.add('search-input-error');
    input.focus();
    return;
  }
  const safeValue = sanitize(trimmedValue);
  const url = new URL(searchResultsPath, window.location.origin);
  url.searchParams.set('q', safeValue);
  url.searchParams.set('_charset_', charsetInput.value);
  window.location.href = url.toString();
});

Placeholder text swaps responsively via a matchMedia('(max-width: 743px)') listener, and the query pre-fills from the URL's ?q= on load (mirroring the results page's own querystring, so navigating back to the input shows the last search).

CSS

A floating-label pattern: label sits above the input at rest, animates down into a large placeholder-like position when the input is empty and unfocused (.search-input-label-down), with distinct font sizes at mobile/tablet/desktop/wide breakpoints (20px → 30px → 40px). An inline "no results" notice block (.search-input-no-results) is present in markup at decoration time but is never populated or shown by this block's own JS — this block only navigates to a results page, it does not render results itself, so any live "no results" state would have to come from the destination page.

Key differences from source system

Source (search-box)Target (search-input)
Suggestions dropdownPresent in classic HTL markup (.abbv-search-suggestions <ul>), driven by data-* config + external JSNot present — target block only submits to a results page, no inline suggestion list
SanitizationNot visible at the dialog/HTL layer (delegated to external clientlib JS)Explicit, in-block sanitize() via a detached <div>.textContent/innerHTML round-trip
Brand themingPer-brand clientlib CSS overrides (typical source pattern)No brand subfolders — single shared CSS using brand-neutral custom properties
Validation feedbackNot specified in dialog-level filesExplicit ARIA live-region alert box with configurable error text from EDS placeholders

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
resultsUrlpathbrowsersearchResultsPagePathRenamedSame purpose; target field type is aem-content (Universal Editor content picker) vs. source's Coral pathbrowser, and is marked required in the target model
searchInputPlaceHoldertextsearchButtonPlaceholderRenamedSame purpose (desktop placeholder); source had one placeholder for all viewports
(none — source has no mobile-specific placeholder field)n/asearchButtonPlaceholderMobileNet-new in targetTarget adds a dedicated mobile placeholder; source only ever had one placeholder value shared across breakpoints
autoSuggestcheckbox(none)Not mappedNo suggestions dropdown feature in the target block at all
maxSuggestionsnumberfield(none)Not mappedSame reason — suggestions feature not carried over
charTriggernumberfield(none)Not mappedSame reason — suggestions feature not carried over
customCssClassestext(none, superseded)Not mappedReplaced by the shared common-properties classes field; no dedicated free-text class field on this block's own model

Section Nav — Technical Details

What it does

Renders in-page anchor navigation to jump between sections of a long page, with a mobile-specific prev/next/menu control set and desktop link list. In both systems this is authored as a list of label + target pairs, with a sticky (pinned) presentation as the default.

Architecture

[source system]
Author fills a multifield of ./sectionDetails (label, anchor id, plus
several analytics-only fields per item) and picks 4 optional mobile icons
         ↓
SectionNavigationModel.java parses each sectionDetails JSON string into a
SectionNavigationBean (Gson), also back-writing a generated interactionId
into each item's stored JSON on first author-mode render
         ↓
HTL renders a mobile prev/next/menu control cluster + a <ul> of anchor
links built via a shared anchor.html template, each carrying an
analytics data-digitaldata attribute

[target implementation]
decorate() parses authored rows positionally: 1-cell rows = anchorId
config, 2-cell rows = {label, href} nav items. Builds a <nav> — either a
flat link list, or (mobile-menu variant) a toggleable hamburger menu with
focus-trapping. Adds IntersectionObserver-based sticky-pin detection and
active-section highlighting; smooth-scrolls to targets with a computed
offset (header height + nav height + gap).

Sling Model — SectionNavigationModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class})
public class SectionNavigationModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    @Inject @Optional private List<String> sectionDetails;   // JSON-per-item multifield
    @Inject @Optional private String stickyNotReq;
    @Inject @Optional private String previousIcon, nextIcon, menuIcon, closeIcon;

    private List<SectionNavigationBean> sectionNavBeans;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        if (sectionDetails != null && !sectionDetails.isEmpty()) {
            sectionNavInterator();   // parses each JSON string into a bean
        }
        if (!isPublish()) {
            setInteractionIdforSectionNavItems();  // writes back a generated id per item, commits resource
        }
    }
}

Note the model performs a repository write (ModifiableValueMap + resourceResolver.commit()) as a side effect of rendering in author mode — it back-fills a deterministic interactionId (getId() + "-" + index) into each nav item's stored JSON if missing or stale.

Dialog

TabFieldPropertyTypeNote
OverviewCustom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestext
OverviewAnchor IDanchorIDtext
OverviewSticky Not RequiredstickyNotReqcheckboxInverse-sense flag — sticky is the default, this opts out
OverviewSection Navigation Details (multifield)sectionDetails → per item: sectionLabel, sectionAnchorID (required), interactionId (disabled/auto), componentTitle, componentPosition, sectionTitle, l10Title, mva, mvaTitle, mvaValue, mvaSecondary, customCssClassesmultifield of fieldsetsOnly sectionLabel/sectionAnchorID affect rendering; the rest are pure analytics tagging metadata
IconsMobile Previous/Next/Menu/Close IconpreviousIcon, nextIcon, menuIcon, closeIconselect (datasource-driven icon picker)Default to abbv-icon-keyboard_arrow_down / abbv-icon-dehaze / abbv-icon-close when unset

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<div id="${anchorID}" class="abbv-section-navigation ${!stickyNotReq?'abbv-sticky':''} ${customCssClasses}"
     data-stick-behavior="${!stickyNotReq?'default':''}" data-stick-offset="0" data-stick-anchor-pos="top">
  <div class="mobile-section-navigation">
    <button id="section-nav-prev" class="... ${previousIcon || 'abbv-icon-keyboard_arrow_down'} i-b"></button>
    <span class="current-pos">${firstItem.sectionLabel}</span>
    <button id="section-nav-next" .../>
    <button id="section-nav-menu" data-icon-open="..." data-icon-close="${closeIcon||'abbv-icon-close'}"></button>
  </div>
  <ul class="section-navigation-list">
    <sly data-sly-list.itemList="${sectionNavBeans}">
      <li>
        <!-- classGrp includes "subnav-active" on the first item only -->
        <sly data-sly-call="${anchorTemplate.anchorSection @ url='#'+itemList.sectionAnchorID,
                              digitalData=itemList.analyticsDigitalData, classGrp=classGrp, title=linkTitle}"/>
      </li>
    </sly>
  </ul>
</div>

Important Constraints (source system)

  • The first nav item is marked active (subnav-active) unconditionally at render time — there is no scroll-based active-section tracking in the markup itself; any such behavior lives in the separate clientlib JS, not visible in the dialog-adjacent files.
  • The model performs a JCR write and commit during a GET render in author mode, which is an unusual and somewhat risky pattern (write-on-read) — a failed commit is only logged, not surfaced.
  • Sticky behavior is expressed as data-stick-* attributes for an external sticky-behavior library, not implemented inline.

Section Nav Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. blocks/section-nav/ is fully implemented, including sticky pinning, a mobile hamburger-menu variant, IntersectionObserver-driven active-section tracking, and smooth-scroll with computed offset.

File Structure

blocks/section-nav/
├── section-nav.js
├── section-nav.css
├── _section-nav.json
└── linzess/, rinvoq-hcp/, skyrizi-hcp/ (section-nav.css, _section-nav.css)

Authoring Model (_section-nav.json)

{
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "section-nav",
      "fields": [
        { "component": "tab", "label": "Overview", "name": "overview" },
        { "component": "multiselect", "valueType": "string", "name": "classes", "label": "Variants",
          "options": [
            { "name": "Sticky (pins below header on scroll)", "value": "sticky" },
            { "name": "Mobile Menu (hamburger on small screens)", "value": "mobile-menu" }
          ] },
        { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "anchorId", "value": "", "label": "Anchor ID",
          "description": "Optional ID for this nav block (e.g. section-menu). Used for deep-linking to the nav itself." }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "section-nav-item",
      "fields": [
        { "component": "tab", "label": "Overview", "name": "overview" },
        { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "label", "value": "", "label": "Link Label" },
        { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "href", "value": "", "label": "Target Section ID",
          "description": "The id of the target section element (e.g. #dosing or dosing)." }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "filters": [ { "id": "section-nav", "components": ["section-nav-item"] } ]
}

How the JS works (section-nav.js)

Parses rows positionally (single-cell = anchorId config, two-cell = nav item), builds either a flat link list or a hamburger menu (mobile-menu class), then wires up smooth-scroll, sticky-pin detection, and active-section tracking:

const SCROLL_GAP = 24;
const getOffset = () => getCssPx('--header-height') + block.offsetHeight + SCROLL_GAP;

links.forEach((a) => {
  a.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
    const target = document.getElementById(a.getAttribute('href').slice(1));
    if (!target) return;
    e.preventDefault();
    links.forEach((l) => l.classList.remove('is-active'));
    a.classList.add('is-active');
    pendingActiveHref = a.getAttribute('href');
    window.scrollTo({ top: target.getBoundingClientRect().top + window.scrollY - getOffset(), behavior: 'smooth' });
  });
});

A documented, deliberate piece of resilience logic: since migrated content applies section style classes but not anchor IDs, assignTargets() positionally maps each nav link to the Nth following .section sibling only when no element already carries that id anywhere in the document — preserving any authored ID as the source of truth and removing any stray positional ID it previously stamped if an authored target later appears (re-checked for 30 animation frames to survive decoration races).

Sticky pinning uses a zero-height sentinel + IntersectionObserver rather than position: sticky alone, because the CSS also needs to switch to position: fixed once stuck (to persist across section boundaries) and reserve the vacated layout height. Active-section highlighting uses a second IntersectionObserver with a rootMargin tuned to the nav's own offset, highlighting only the topmost currently-visible section and temporarily deferring to a just-clicked link until its target is confirmed in view (pendingActiveHref).

CSS

Horizontally scrollable nav bar with hidden scrollbar; sticky variant pins at top: var(--header-height) and switches to position: fixed plus a shadow once .is-stuck; active link gets a bottom border and color change via .is-active.

Key differences from source system

Source (section-navigation)Target (section-nav)
Active-section trackingFirst item hardcoded active at render; no visible scroll tracking in dialog-adjacent filesReal-time IntersectionObserver-based tracking of the topmost visible section
Mobile presentationDedicated prev/next/menu button cluster with datasource-driven icon pickersOptional hamburger-menu variant with focus-trapping; no prev/next stepper
Analytics metadataRich per-item tagging (MVA type/category/tier, section/localized titles, component position) feeding a digitalData beanNot present — no equivalent analytics fields on the target model
Sticky mechanismdata-stick-* attributes for an external libraryNative IntersectionObserver + CSS position: sticky/fixed toggle
Anchor resolution robustnessN/APositional fallback targeting with authored-ID precedence, to tolerate migrated content missing explicit anchor IDs

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
sectionDetails[].sectionLabeltext (multifield item)labelSame name (shortened)Same purpose — display text for the nav link
sectionDetails[].sectionAnchorIDtext (multifield item, required)hrefRenamedSource stores a bare anchor id and the HTL prepends #; target stores/accepts either form directly
anchorIDtextanchorIdSame name (case)Same purpose — id for the nav block itself, for deep-linking to the nav
stickyNotReqcheckbox (inverse sense)classes: stickyRenamed / invertedSource defaults to sticky and requires an explicit opt-out; target defaults to non-sticky and requires an explicit opt-in via the sticky variant class — the default behavior is inverted, not just the field name
customCssClassestext(none, superseded)Not mappedReplaced by structured classes multiselect plus shared common properties
previousIcon / nextIconselect (icon picker)(none)Not mappedNo prev/next stepper control exists in the target block at all
menuIcon / closeIconselect (icon picker)(none)Not mappedTarget's hamburger toggle uses a fixed CSS-drawn icon (.section-nav-hamburger), not an author-selectable icon
sectionDetails[].interactionId, componentTitle, componentPosition, sectionTitle, l10Title, mva, mvaTitle, mvaValue, mvaSecondary, customCssClassesvarious (multifield item)(none)Not mappedSource-specific analytics/tagging metadata with no equivalent field on the target's section-nav-item model, which only carries label and href

Send Mail — Technical Details

What it does

An "email a friend" / share-by-email form: sender fills in their name/email and a recipient's name/email, optionally with a page-share link and reCAPTCHA, and submits to trigger an outbound email. The source system actually ships two near-duplicate components for this — send-mail and send-email — with different field names and slightly different scopes but the same fundamental shape; both map to the target implementation's single send-mail block.

Architecture

Author configures form copy/labels/buttons + from/to field config
         ↓
SendMailModel.java (or SendEmailModel.java for the sibling component)
resolves all display strings, a reCAPTCHA site key (via a shared
AbbvieConfigurationUtil/CommonUtil lookup), and an analytics interaction id
         ↓
HTL renders a <form data-share-api="${resource.path}"> with from/to
name+email fields, a submit + cancel button, and an invisible-badge
reCAPTCHA widget
         ↓
A deferred clientlib script (send-mail-component.v1 / send-email-component.v1)
handles submit — POSTs to the component's own resource path
(data-share-api), which server-side renders the configured HTML email
template with token substitution (${fullUseText}, ${spl.SenderName}, etc.)
and dispatches the email; success/error swaps in a `.loading` overlay

[target implementation]
decorate() reads authored key/value rows into a `config` object, builds a
plain HTML form client-side, validates + calls fetch(config.api, {POST})
directly — no server-side templating, no reCAPTCHA, no page-share link.

Sling Model — SendMailModel.java

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class SendMailModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENTIDPREFIX = "abbv-send-mail";

    @Inject @Named("formTitle") @Optional private String formTitle;
    @Inject @Named("fromNameLabel") @Optional private String fromNameLabel;
    @Inject @Named("fromEmailLabel") @Optional private String fromEmailLabel;
    @Inject @Named("toNameLabel") @Optional private String toNameLabel;
    @Inject @Named("toEmailLabel") @Optional private String toEmailLabel;
    @Inject @Named("sharePageLink") @Optional private String sharePageLink;
    @Inject @Named("submitButtonLabel") @Optional private String submitButtonLabel;
    @Inject @Named("cancelButtonLabel") @Optional private String cancelButtonLabel;
    // ... plus icon/icon-position fields for both buttons, and analytics fields

    @OSGiService public AbbvieConfigurationUtil abbvieConfig;

    public String getCaptchaSiteKey() {
        HashMap<String,String> hashMap = new HashMap<>(CommonUtil.getCaptchaKeys(abbvieConfig, resourceResolver));
        return hashMap.get(AbbvieFormConstants.CAPTCHA_SITE_KEY);
    }

    public String getDigitalData() { /* builds an analytics bean from sectionTitle/l10Title/thirdPartyTags */ }
}
