The sibling SendEmailModel.java (backing send-email) follows the same shape with renamed fields (senderNameLabel/senderEmailLabel/recipientNameLabel/recipientEmailLabel instead of fromNameLabel/fromEmailLabel/toNameLabel/toEmailLabel), and drops the required-field toggles that send-mail exposes.

Dialog (send-mail — largest of the two, 5 tabs)

TabFieldPropertyTypeNote
OverviewSend Mail Identifier (Anchor ID)anchorIDtext
OverviewCustom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestext
OverviewTitle of this Send Mail ComponentformTitletext
OverviewAnalytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtext (disabled, auto-set)
OverviewSection Title / Localized Section Title for AnalyticssectionTitle, l10Titletext
OverviewFrom Display Name / From Email Address / Subject LinefromDisplayName, fromEmailAddress, emailsubjecttextThe actual sender identity/subject used for the outbound email — distinct from the user-facing "From" form fields below
OverviewScreenshot DAM path URLscreenshotLinkURLpathbrowser
FromFrom Name Label / Placeholder / Help Text / Validation Message / Is RequiredfromNameLabel, fromNamePlaceHolderText, fromNameFormText, fromNameValMsg, fromNameIsRequiredtext/checkboxOptional field group — hidden entirely in HTL if fromNameLabel is empty
FromFrom Email Label / Placeholder / Help Text / Validation Message / Is RequiredfromEmailLabel, fromEmailPlaceholderTxt, fromEmailInfoHelpTxt, fromEmailValMsg, fromEmailIsRequiredtext/checkbox
ToTo Form Fields Title TexttoFormTitleTxttext
ToTo Name Label / Placeholder / Help Text / Validation Message / Is RequiredtoNameLabel, toNamePlaceholderTxt, toNameInfoHelpTxt, toNameValMsg, toNameIsRequiredtext/checkbox
ToTo Email Label (required) / Placeholder / Help Text / Validation MessagetoEmailLabel, toEmailPlaceholderTxt, toEmailInfoHelpTxt, toEmailValMsgtextTo-email is always required — no toEmailIsRequired toggle exists (unlike the other three fields), and the HTL hardcodes data-required="true"
SubmissionSuccess Message / Error MessagesuccessMsg, errorMsgtext
SubmissionBack To Email Form Button TextbacktoFormButtonTexttextButton only renders if this is set
SubmissionSubmit/Cancel Button Text + Icon + Icon PositionsubmitButtonLabel, submitBtnIcon, submitBtnIconPosition, cancelButtonLabel, cancelBtnIcon, cancelBtnIconPositiontext/select
Email BodyPage to SharesharePageLinkpathbrowserDefaults to the current page if unset
Email BodyPre-Existing HTML TemplatereuseEmailTemplatepathbrowser
Email BodyUse Statement Path (hidden field)usestatementrefpathbrowser (granite:hide)Present in the dialog XML but hidden from authors
Email BodyEmail Body HTMLhtmlbodytextarea (40 rows)Supports tokens: ${fullUseText}, ${condensedUseText}, ${fullISI}, ${condensedISI}, ${spl.SenderName}, ${spl.SenderEmail}, ${spl.RecipientName}, ${spl.RecipientEmail}, ${spl.sharePageLink}, ${screenshotURL}
Email BodyImportant Safety Information (ISI) Path (hidden field)safetyinforefpathbrowser (granite:hide)

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<div class="abbv-send-mail ${customCssClasses}" data-digitaldata='${digitalData}'>
  <form action="#" class="abbv-send-email-form" data-share-api="${resource.path}"
        data-pagescreenshotpath="${screenshotLinkURL}">
    <a href="${link.linkFormatted}" id="abbv-email-share-url" style="display:none"></a>
    <div data-sly-test="${fromNameLabel}" class="abbv-form-wrapper abbv-from-name">
      <input class="abbv-fromName" data-required="${fromNameIsRequired}" name="fromName">
      <span class="abbv-error-msg" data-err-msg="${fromNameValMsg}"></span>
    </div>
    <!-- fromEmail, toName analogous -->
    <div data-sly-test="${toEmailLabel}" class="abbv-form-wrapper abbv-to-email">
      <input class="abbv-toEmail" data-required="true" name="toEmail">  <!-- always required, hardcoded -->
    </div>
    <div class="g-recaptcha js-v2" data-sitekey="${captchaSiteKey}" data-badge="inline" data-size="invisible"></div>
  </form>
  <div class="loading">
    <div class="main-success"><span data-success-msg="${successMsg}"></span></div>
    <div class="main-error"><span data-err-no-api="${errorMsg}"></span></div>
  </div>
</div>

Important Constraints (source system)

  • Two near-duplicate components (send-mail, send-email) with different field names for the same concepts (from/to vs. sender/recipient) — a real, unreconciled source-side duplication, not an artifact of this analysis.
  • Invisible reCAPTCHA (data-size="invisible") with a badge disclosure notice, tied to a site-key resolved from shared OSGi configuration — the target implementation has no reCAPTCHA equivalent at all.
  • The actual email send is server-side, driven by an HTML template with token substitution — including regulated-content tokens (${fullUseText}, ${fullISI}) that can inject Use/ISI copy directly into the outbound email body.
  • Only "To Email" is unconditionally required; all other fields (including "From Name"/"From Email"/"To Name") are individually toggleable per-instance via *IsRequired checkboxes.

Send Mail Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. blocks/send-mail/ implements a materially simpler client-side form: fixed four fields (from name/email, to name/email), a single submit action, and no page-share or reCAPTCHA.

File Structure

blocks/send-mail/
├── send-mail.js
├── send-mail.css
├── _send-mail.json
├── block-config.js
└── abbvie/, botox/, rinvoq/ (send-mail.css, _send-mail.css, block-config.js)

Authoring Model (_send-mail.json)

{
  "id": "send-mail",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Content", "name": "content" },
    { "component": "text", "name": "heading", "value": "", "label": "Form Heading" },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Field Labels", "name": "labels" },
    { "component": "text", "name": "from-name-label", "value": "Your name", "label": "Sender Name Label" },
    { "component": "text", "name": "from-email-label", "value": "Your email", "label": "Sender Email Label" },
    { "component": "text", "name": "to-name-label", "value": "Recipient name", "label": "Recipient Name Label" },
    { "component": "text", "name": "to-email-label", "value": "Recipient email", "label": "Recipient Email Label" },
    { "component": "text", "name": "submit-label", "value": "Send", "label": "Submit Button Label" },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Configuration", "name": "config" },
    { "component": "text", "name": "api", "value": "", "label": "API Endpoint",
      "description": "URL that receives the POST request with form data." },
    { "component": "text", "name": "success", "value": "Your message has been sent!", "label": "Success Message" },
    { "component": "text", "name": "error", "value": "Unable to send. Please try again.", "label": "Error Message" },
    { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
  ]
}

How the JS works (send-mail.js)

decorate() reads authored rows into a flat config key/value map (key = lowercased, hyphenated first-cell text), builds four labeled inputs plus a submit button and status line, then wires validation and submission:

function validateEmail(email) {
  return /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(email);
}

form.addEventListener('submit', async (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  let valid = true;
  [fromName, fromEmail, toName, toEmail].forEach(({ input, err }) => {
    err.textContent = '';
    if (!input.value.trim()) { err.textContent = 'This field is required.'; valid = false; }
    else if (input.type === 'email' && !validateEmail(input.value)) { err.textContent = 'Please enter a valid email.'; valid = false; }
  });
  if (!valid) return;
  if (!config.api) { status.textContent = config.success || 'Form submitted.'; return; }
  // else: fetch(config.api, { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ fromName, fromEmail, toName, toEmail, pageUrl }) })
});

If no API endpoint is configured, submission is a no-op that just shows the success message — a stub mode useful for prototyping but silent about not actually sending anything.

CSS

A simple vertical flex form capped at 480px wide, with per-field error text (min-height reserved to avoid layout shift when an error appears) and a bold status line for the final success/error message.

Key differences from source system

Source (send-mail / send-email)Target (send-mail block)
Field count~30+ authorable fields across 5 tabs (labels, placeholders, help text, validation messages, icons, per-field required toggles)~10 fields across 3 tabs (heading, 4 field labels, submit label, API endpoint, success/error message)
Required-field controlPer-field opt-in checkboxes (except To Email, always required)Hardcoded — all four fields always required, client-side
Email deliveryServer-side HTML template rendering with token substitution, dispatched via the component's own resource pathGeneric client-side fetch() POST to an authored API endpoint; no templating
reCAPTCHAInvisible reCAPTCHA tied to shared site-key configurationNot present
Page-share linkExplicit sharePageLink field, defaults to current page, injected into the email body via a tokenOnly pageUrl: window.location.href included in the POST body — no configurable share target, no template token system
Regulated-content tokensUse/ISI text can be injected directly into the email body (${fullUseText}, ${fullISI})Not present — no equivalent capability

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
formTitletextheadingRenamedSame purpose
fromNameLabeltextfrom-name-labelRenamedSame purpose; target has a default value ("Your name"), source defaults to hiding the field entirely if empty
fromEmailLabeltextfrom-email-labelRenamedSame purpose
toNameLabeltextto-name-labelRenamedSame purpose
toEmailLabeltext (required)to-email-labelRenamedSame purpose; both systems treat this field as always-required
submitButtonLabeltextsubmit-labelRenamedSame purpose
(implicit: the component's own resource path, data-share-api)n/aapiRenamed / re-scopedSource posts back to its own JCR resource path for server-side processing; target posts to an arbitrary authored external endpoint — a fundamentally different integration model, not a like-for-like rename
successMsgtextsuccessRenamedSame purpose
errorMsgtexterrorRenamedSame purpose
fromNamePlaceHolderText, fromEmailPlaceholderTxt, toNamePlaceholderTxt, toEmailPlaceholderTxttext(none)Not mappedTarget's field labels double as visible labels only; there is no separate placeholder-text field distinct from the label
fromNameFormText, fromEmailInfoHelpTxt, toNameInfoHelpTxt, toEmailInfoHelpTxttext(none)Not mappedNo info-icon/help-text affordance exists on the target form at all
fromNameValMsg, fromEmailValMsg, toNameValMsg, toEmailValMsgtext(none — hardcoded strings)Not mappedTarget uses fixed English strings ("This field is required.", "Please enter a valid email.") baked into the JS, not per-instance authorable text
fromNameIsRequired, fromEmailIsRequired, toNameIsRequiredcheckbox(none — always required)Not mappedTarget hardcodes all four fields as required; per-field opt-out is not authorable
cancelButtonLabel, cancelBtnIcon, cancelBtnIconPositiontext/select(none)Not mappedNo cancel/reset button in the target form
submitBtnIcon, submitBtnIconPositionselect(none)Not mappedTarget submit button has no icon slot
sharePageLinkpathbrowser(none — hardcoded to window.location.href)Not mappedTarget always shares the current page URL in the POST body; there is no authored override
reuseEmailTemplate, htmlbody, usestatementref, safetyinforefpathbrowser/textarea(none)Not mappedTarget has no server-side templating layer at all — email body composition (including any regulated-content token substitution) is entirely outside the target block's scope
fromDisplayName, fromEmailAddress, emailsubjecttext(none)Not mappedNo equivalent "actual sender identity for the outbound email" configuration on the target block — that concern is presumed to live entirely in whatever service api points to
screenshotLinkURLpathbrowser(none)Not mappedNo screenshot-attachment capability in the target implementation
anchorID, customCssClasses, sectionTitle, l10Title, interactionIdtext(none, superseded by common properties where applicable)Not mappedNo dedicated fields on this block's model; generic id/classes handled by shared common-properties, analytics fields dropped entirely
backtoFormButtonTexttext(none)Not mappedTarget's status line is a simple inline message with no "try again" affordance
reCAPTCHA site key (resolved via getCaptchaKeys)n/a (resolved server-side)(none)Not mappedNo bot-mitigation mechanism in the target implementation

Separator — Technical Details

What it does

Renders a plain horizontal rule (<hr>) wrapped in a div, with an optional authored id for anchor targeting. This is confirmed as an EDS-native block with no direct AEM source component — the manifest lists an empty aemSources array for separator, and no visual-divider component of this shape was found under the source system's component tree. Visual separation in the source system was typically achieved through spacing/margin utility classes or component-level layout options rather than a standalone rule component, so there is nothing to port from — this is a genuinely new, simple authoring primitive introduced in the target implementation.

EDS Implementation

What's already built. blocks/separator/ is fully implemented and deliberately minimal.

File Structure

blocks/separator/
├── separator.js
├── separator.css
├── _separator.json
├── block-config.js
└── abbvie/, botox/, rinvoq/ (separator.css, _separator.css, block-config.js)

Authoring Model (_separator.json)

{
  "id": "separator",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "text", "valueType": "string", "name": "id", "value": "", "label": "Id" }
  ]
}

A single field — no style variant, no color/thickness options, no common-properties inclusion.

How the JS works (separator.js)

export default function decorate(block) {
  const idCell = block.querySelector(':scope > div > div');
  const idValue = idCell ? idCell.textContent.trim() : '';

  const wrapper = document.createElement('div');
  wrapper.className = 'abbvie-separator';
  if (idValue) wrapper.id = idValue;

  const hr = document.createElement('hr');
  hr.className = 'separator-hr';
  wrapper.appendChild(hr);

  block.innerHTML = '';
  block.appendChild(wrapper);
}

CSS

.abbvie-separator {
    height: var(--separator-height-8);
}

.margin-24 .abbvie-separator {
    margin-bottom: var(--gap-24);
}

Height and bottom-margin are the only styled properties, both token-driven; the margin-24 class is applied externally (presumably via the shared common-properties spacing utility), not authored directly on this block's own model.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
No AEM source component exists for this block. The manifest's aemSources array is empty, and no matching divider/rule component was found under the source component tree. This is a net-new, EDS-native authoring primitive with no field mapping to state — it is not a migration of any existing source-system field surface.

Social Media — Technical Details

What it does

Renders a row of icon links (e.g. platform icons pointing at social profile URLs), forcing every link to open in a new tab with rel="noopener noreferrer". Like Separator, this is confirmed as an EDS-native block with no direct AEM source component — the manifest lists an empty aemSources array, and no dedicated "social media icon bar" component was found under the source system's component tree. Social links in the source system were most likely authored as generic link-list content inside the footer or header components rather than as a standalone, purpose-built block, so there is no source dialog, HTL, or Sling Model to document here.

EDS Implementation

What's already built. blocks/social-media/ is fully implemented as a container block with an authored, filtered child-item type (social-link).

File Structure

blocks/social-media/
├── social-media.js
├── social-media.css
├── _social-media.json
├── block-config.js
└── abbvie/, botox/, rinvoq/ (social-media.css, _social-media.css, block-config.js)

Authoring Model (_social-media.json)

{
  "definitions": [
    { "title": "Social Media", "id": "social-media",
      "plugins": { "xwalk": { "page": { "resourceType": "core/franklin/components/block/v1/block",
        "template": { "name": "Social Media", "model": "social-media", "filter": "social-media",
                       "language": "lang:none", "blockId": "id:" } } } } },
    { "title": "Social Link", "id": "social-link",
      "plugins": { "xwalk": { "page": { "resourceType": "core/franklin/components/block/v1/block/item",
        "template": { "name": "Social Link", "model": "social-link" } } } } }
  ],
  "models": [
    { "id": "social-media", "fields": [ { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" } ] },
    { "id": "social-link", "fields": [
      { "component": "reference", "name": "icon", "label": "Social Media Icon", "valueType": "string" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "link", "label": "Platform Link", "valueType": "string" }
    ] }
  ],
  "filters": [ { "id": "social-media", "components": ["social-link"] } ]
}

The parent social-media model carries no fields of its own beyond shared common properties — all real content lives in repeated social-link child items (icon + link), enforced by the filters entry restricting this block's children to that one item type.

How the JS works (social-media.js)

export default function decorate(block) {
  applyCommonProps(block);
  [...block.children].forEach((row) => {
    const cells = Array.from(row.children);
    if (cells.length >= 2) {
      const iconCell = cells[0];
      const linkCell = cells[1];
      const iconElement = iconCell.querySelector('img, picture');
      const linkText = linkCell.textContent.trim();
      if (iconElement && linkText) {
        const link = document.createElement('a');
        link.href = linkText;
        link.target = '_blank';
        link.rel = 'noopener noreferrer';
        // ... clone the icon <img> into the link ...
        row.innerHTML = '';
        row.appendChild(link);
        row.classList.add('social-link-item');
      }
    }
  });
}

Rows missing either an icon image or link text are left untransformed and rendered with a visibly distinct "incomplete entry" style (grey background box) rather than silently dropped — a deliberate authoring-feedback affordance rather than an accident.

CSS

Flex row layout with wrapping (gap: var(--spacing-32), widening to --spacing-40 at 744px+). Transformed .social-link-item rows render as plain inline-block icon links; untransformed rows (.social-media > div:not(.social-link-item)) get a flex layout with a light grey translucent background and border-radius, visually flagging incomplete authoring.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
No AEM source component exists for this block. The manifest's aemSources array is empty, and no dedicated social-icon-bar component was found under the source component tree. This is a net-new, EDS-native authoring primitive (parent block + filtered social-link child item type) with no field mapping to state — it is not a migration of any existing source-system field surface.

Social Share — Technical Details

What it does

Renders a row of share-to-social buttons (Facebook, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Tumblr, Pinterest, Instagram) plus copy-to-clipboard and share-by-email actions. Which networks are shown is driven by an inherited, page-level admin configuration list rather than per-instance authoring, and each button carries a pre-computed analytics payload.

Architecture (source system)

Author places Social Share component on a page, sets layout + optional override link
         &#8595;
social-share.html invokes SocialShareModel (Sling Model) via data-sly-use
         &#8595;
SocialShareModel.init():
  - reads an inherited "adminConfigPath" page property
  - resolves a Generic List at <adminConfigPath>/ce-social-share-urls
  - reads inherited boolean/array properties (enableCopyLink, enableEmailShare,
    socialshareTags) up the page tree via HierarchyNodeInheritanceValueMap
  - builds one JSON blob (getSocialShareJsonStr) describing which network URLs
    are enabled, plus a pre-serialized analytics payload per network
         &#8595;
HTL renders one <li> per network, gated by data-sly-test on the model's
isEnableXxx() booleans (or inheritedPageProperties.enableCopyLink/enableEmailShare)
         &#8595;
Each <button> carries data-digitaldata='<model.xxxDigitalData>' — the
already-built analytics JSON string, injected unescaped (@context="unsafe")

Sling Model — SocialShareModel.java

Extends a shared AbstractComponentModel. No fields are authored directly on the network toggles — those come from inherited page-level configuration, not from the component's own dialog:

@Model(adaptables={SlingHttpServletRequest.class, Resource.class})
public class SocialShareModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String interactionId;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String link;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String sectionTitle;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String mvaPrimary;
    @Inject @Via("resource") @Optional private String mvaTypeAndCategory;
    ...
    private boolean enableFacebook, enableTwitter, enableTumblr,
                     enableLinkedIn, enablePinterest, enableInstagram;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        String socialShareAdminConfig = getInheritedPropValue("adminConfigPath")
                                         + "/ce-social-share-urls";
        socialShareJson(socialShareAdminConfig); // builds enable flags + URLs
        // also self-heals a missing/stale interactionId back onto the resource
    }
}

A second model, LinkChecker, is used only for the optional override "Link" field — it classifies the authored link as internal/external and formats it (com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.LinkChecker, invoked via data-sly-use.linkhelper with a request-attribute-bound link parameter):

@Model(adaptables = SlingHttpServletRequest.class)
public class LinkChecker {
    @Inject @Optional @Named("link") @Source("request-attributes")
    private String link;
    private String linkFormatted;
    private boolean isExternal;
    // getLinkFormatted(), isExternal(), getJumpLinkClass()
}

Dialog — Overview + Analytics tabs

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor IDanchorIDtextfieldAdds an anchor tag above the component
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestextfieldFree-text space-separated classes
Layoutlayoutselectabbv-social-horizontal / abbv-social-vertical
LinklinkpathbrowserOverrides the shared page URL for all networks; rooted at /content
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtextfield (disabled)System-generated, read-only
Analytics TitleanalyticsTitletextfieldTagging-spec navigation title
Section Title for AnalyticssectionTitletextfieldComponent/section title for tagging
Localized Section Title for Analyticsl10TitletextfieldNon-English sites only
Most Valued Action (MVA)mvaPrimarycheckboxMarks interaction as MVA Primary
Most Valued Action (MVA) Title for AnalyticsmvaTitletextfieldMVA title per tagging spec
Most Valued Action Type and CategorymvaTypeAndCategoryselect (datasource)Populated from an admin config datasource
Most Valued Action (MVA) SecondarymvaSecondarycheckboxMarks interaction as MVA Secondary tier

Notably, which networks actually appear (Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, Tumblr, Pinterest, Instagram) is not a dialog field on this component at all — it's controlled by a separate, page-level "admp social share" configuration under Page Properties, inherited down the page tree.

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<div class="abbv-social-share abbv-social-share-horizontal ${properties.customCssClasses} ${properties.layout}">
  <a aria-hidden="true" href="${linkhelper.linkFormatted}" class="abbv-share-url"></a>
  <ul>
    <li data-sly-test="${inheritedPageProperties.enableCopyLink}">
      <button data-digitaldata='${socialshareModel.copyClipBoardDigitalData @context="unsafe"}'
              class="abbv-button-plain abbv-share-btn" data-network="copy"><svg>...</svg></button>
    </li>
    <li data-sly-test="${socialshareModel.enableFacebook}">
      <button data-digitaldata='${socialshareModel.faceBookDigitalData @context="unsafe"}'
              class="abbv-button-plain abbv-share-btn ${inheritedPageProperties.exitModalID ? 'abbv-modal-open' : ''}"
              data-network="facebook" data-id="${inheritedPageProperties.exitModalID}"><svg>...</svg></button>
    </li>
    <!-- twitter, linkedin, tumblr, pinterest, instagram follow the same pattern -->
  </ul>
</div>

Every network icon is an inline SVG path embedded directly in the HTL template (no icon font, no sprite sheet). An optional "exit modal" gate (inheritedPageProperties.exitModalID) can intercept the click before navigating off-site.

Important Constraints

  • Which networks render is controlled by inherited page-tree configuration, not by the component instance — two Social Share components on the same page cannot show different network sets.
  • The admin config Generic List lookup fails closed to a hardcoded default JSON string (all networks disabled except structurally present) if the config page or list parsing fails.
  • interactionId self-heals by writing back to the JCR on first author-mode render, which is a side effect of a read path.
  • Analytics payload is fully server-rendered JSON embedded as an unescaped attribute (@context="unsafe"), coupling markup directly to the internal digital-data schema.

Social Share Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/social-share/ and is a client-rendered rewrite: instead of reading a page-level admin config list, the set of networks is authored per-block-instance as a multiselect field, and analytics/exit-intent concerns are handled through DOM events instead of server-rendered JSON blobs.

File Structure

blocks/social-share/
├── social-share.js
├── social-share.css
├── _social-share.json
└── block-config.js

Authoring Model (_social-share.json)

{
  "component": "select", "name": "classes", "label": "Layout",
  "options": [
    { "name": "Horizontal", "value": "social-share-horizontal" },
    { "name": "Vertical", "value": "social-share-vertical" }
  ]
},
{
  "component": "multiselect", "name": "networks", "label": "Social Networks",
  "options": [
    { "name": "Facebook", "value": "facebook" },
    { "name": "Twitter/X", "value": "twitter" },
    { "name": "LinkedIn", "value": "linkedin" },
    { "name": "Pinterest", "value": "pinterest" },
    { "name": "Copy Link", "value": "copy" },
    { "name": "Email", "value": "email" }
  ]
},
{
  "component": "aem-content", "name": "link", "label": "Share URL Override",
  "description": "URL to share. Defaults to current page URL if blank."
},
{ "component": "text", "name": "emailModalId", "label": "Email Modal ID" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "exitModalId", "label": "Exit Modal ID" }

How the JS works (social-share.js)

Networks are rebuilt entirely in JS from a fixed PLATFORMS map (Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, Pinterest — Tumblr and Instagram from the source system were dropped) holding a display label, share-URL builder, and inline SVG path data. readBlockFields parses the authored rows heuristically:

function readBlockFields(block) {
  // aem-content fields render as <a> elements in the block DOM
  const anchor = cell.querySelector('a');
  if (anchor && !result.shareUrl) { result.shareUrl = anchor.href || cell.textContent.trim(); return; }
  // Comma-separated list containing at least one known network key → networks row
  if (parts.some((k) => NETWORK_KEYS.has(k))) { result.networks = text; return; }
  // Remaining plain-text rows are modal IDs
  modalIds.push(text);
}

Click handling replaces server-rendered analytics JSON with a DOM event contract: clicking a network button either opens a popup directly or dispatches a modal:open custom event (for the exit-disclaimer flow), and copy-to-clipboard dispatches social-share:copied to drive a toast:

function openSocialPopup(url) {
  document.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('exit-intent:suppress', { detail: { ms: 2000 } }));
  window.open(url, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer,width=600,height=600');
}

CSS

Uses CSS custom properties for all sizing/color (--social-share-icon-size, --social-share-icon-bg-hover, etc.), a flex list that switches to column layout under .social-share-vertical, and a fixed-position copy-toast (.social-share-copy-toast) that slides in/out via a translateY transform toggle.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (social-share)EDS (social-share)
Network selectionInherited page-level admin config (Generic List)Per-instance multiselect authored field
AnalyticsServer-rendered JSON in data-digitaldataDispatched DOM CustomEvents (modal:open, social-share:copied)
IconsInline SVG in HTL markupInline SVG built at runtime from a JS path map
Networks supportedFacebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, Tumblr, Pinterest, Instagram, copy, emailFacebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, Pinterest, copy, email (Tumblr/Instagram not carried over)
Exit-intent gateabbv-modal-open class + data-idmodal:open CustomEvent with onConfirm callback

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtextfieldcommon block ID propertyRenamedHandled by the shared common-properties block ID field, not a social-share-specific field
customCssClassestextfieldcommon block classes propertyRenamedHandled by shared common-properties, not component-specific
layoutselectclassesRenamedSame two values (horizontal/vertical), field renamed to generic classes
linkpathbrowserlinkSame nameSame purpose; component type changed from pathbrowser to aem-content
(page-level) enabled networks listGeneric List (admin config)networksRenamedConverted from inherited page-tree config to a per-instance authored multiselect
(page-level) enableEmailShare / emailShareModalinherited boolean/textfieldemailModalIdRenamedEmail now toggled by presence in networks, modal ID authored per-instance
(page-level) exitModalIDinherited textfieldexitModalIdRenamedSame purpose, moved from inherited page config to per-instance field
interactionIdtextfield (disabled)—Not mappedServer-side self-healing JCR write-back has no equivalent; EDS is static-content, client-rendered
analyticsTitletextfield—Not mappedNo structured server-rendered analytics payload exists in the EDS block; would need a new analytics layer
sectionTitle / l10Titletextfield—Not mappedSame reason — no digital-data JSON generation on the EDS side
mvaPrimary / mvaTitle / mvaTypeAndCategory / mvaSecondarycheckbox/select/textfield—Not mappedMVA analytics tagging concept has no equivalent in the current EDS model

Sticky Sidebar — Technical Details

What it does

A two-column layout container: a main content column and a sidebar column, where the sidebar sticks in place as the user scrolls past it. In the source system this is purely a layout/authoring container (two nested parsys drop zones) with no dedicated Sling Model — all "stickiness" behavior is delegated to a shared client-side scroll behavior utility rather than server logic.

Architecture (source system)

Author places Sticky Sidebar on a page, picks Sticky Side (left/right)
         &#8595;
sticky-sidebar.html (no Sling Model — sling:resourceSuperType="foundation/components/parbase")
         &#8595;
<div class="abbv-sticky-sidebar ${properties.customCssClasses} ${properties.stickySide}">
  <div class="content">
    <div data-sly-resource="nonstickycontentpar" resourceType="wcm/foundation/components/parsys"></div>
  </div>
  <div class="sidebar">
    <div class="sticky-container abbv-sticky" data-stick-behavior="default-capped"
         data-stick-offset="0" data-stick-anchor-pos="top">
      <div data-sly-resource="linklist" resourceType=".../link-list" selectors="link-list-vertical"></div>
      <div data-sly-resource="stickycontentpar" resourceType="wcm/foundation/components/parsys"></div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

Two separate parsys drop zones (nonstickycontentpar for main content, stickycontentpar for sidebar content) let an author drag in any other component. A vertical link list is always rendered first inside the sidebar's sticky container. The actual "stick" behavior (data-stick-behavior="default-capped") is read and executed by a shared client-side script (outside this component), not by anything the component itself renders beyond data attributes.

Sling Model

None exists for this component. There is no StickySidebar.java (or similarly named class) under core/src/main/java/com/abbvie/admp/commonelements/core/models/, and the HTL contains no data-sly-use reference to any model. The component's only "logic" is HTL expression conditionals on dialog-authored properties.

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextfieldSpace-separated custom classes
Anchor IDanchorIDtextfieldNot actually rendered — see constraints below
Sticky SidestickySideselect (required)Left → sidebar-left; Right → a single space character " "

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<a data-sly-test="${properties.anchorID}" id="${properties.anchorID}"></a>
<div class="abbv-sticky-sidebar ${properties.customCssClasses ? properties.customCssClasses : ''} ${properties.stickySide}">
  <div class="content">
    <div data-sly-resource="${@path='nonstickycontentpar',resourceType='wcm/foundation/components/parsys'}"></div>
  </div>
  <div class="sidebar">
    <div class="sticky-container abbv-sticky" data-stick-behavior="default-capped" data-stick-offset="0" data-stick-anchor-pos="top">
      <div data-sly-resource="${'linklist' @ resourceType='/apps/common-elements/v1/components/content/link-list',selectors='link-list-vertical'}"></div>
      <div data-sly-resource="${@path='stickycontentpar',resourceType='wcm/foundation/components/parsys'}"></div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

CSS

.abbv-sticky-sidebar{ display: flex; align-items: stretch; flex-direction: row; }
.abbv-sticky-sidebar.sidebar-left{ flex-direction: row-reverse; }
.abbv-sticky-sidebar .content,
.abbv-sticky-sidebar .sidebar{ width: 50%; }
.abbv-sticky-sidebar .sticky-container{
	background: rgb(234, 234, 234);
    padding: 15px;
    border-radius: 3px;
    transition: margin 500ms;
}
.abbv-sticky-sidebar .sticky-container.abbv-fixed{ margin-top: 15px; }
@media (max-width: 600px) {
	.abbv-sticky-sidebar,
	.abbv-sticky-sidebar.sidebar-left{ flex-direction: column-reverse;}
    .abbv-sticky-sidebar .content,.abbv-sticky-sidebar .sidebar{ width: 100%; }
}

Fixed 50/50 split via flex, reversed on mobile to stack sidebar above/below content. Sticky visual state is toggled by JS-applied classes (abbv-fixed, abbv-scroll-capped), not CSS position: sticky.

Important Constraints

  • Fixed 50/50 column split — no way to author an asymmetric ratio (e.g. 70/30) without a Custom CSS Class override.
  • The "Right" option for Sticky Side authors a single literal space character as the CSS class value rather than an explicit class — a design/data-quality smell, though it works because the default flex direction is already row (sidebar on the right).
  • Sticky behavior relies on a shared external JS behavior keyed by data-stick-behavior attributes; the component contributes no JS of its own.
  • A vertical link list component is always injected into the sidebar ahead of author content, whether or not the author wants one.

Sticky Sidebar Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/sticky-sidebar/. It replaces the two-parsys authoring model with two rows (main, sidebar), each of which may either hold direct content or a link to a fragment that is loaded and injected client-side. True CSS Grid position: sticky replaces the source system's JS-driven sticky behavior.

File Structure

blocks/sticky-sidebar/
├── sticky-sidebar.js
├── sticky-sidebar.css
├── _sticky-sidebar.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   └── sticky-sidebar.css
├── botox/
│   └── sticky-sidebar.css
└── rinvoq/
    └── sticky-sidebar.css

Authoring Model (_sticky-sidebar.json)

{ "component": "tab", "label": "Main Content", "name": "main" },
{ "component": "richtext", "name": "mainContent", "label": "Main Content",
  "description": "Primary content area. Enter a fragment URL to load an authored fragment, or add content directly." },
{ "component": "tab", "label": "Sidebar", "name": "sidebar" },
{ "component": "richtext", "name": "sidebarContent", "label": "Sidebar Content",
  "description": "Sticky sidebar content. Enter a fragment URL to load an authored fragment, or add content directly." },
{ "component": "tab", "label": "Settings", "name": "settings" },
{ "component": "select", "name": "classes_variant", "label": "Sidebar Position",
  "options": [ { "name": "Right (default)", "value": "" }, { "name": "Left", "value": "left" } ] }

How the JS works (sticky-sidebar.js)

export default async function decorate(block) {
  const [mainRow, sidebarRow] = [...block.children];
  const mainLink = mainRow?.children[0]?.querySelector('a');
  const sidebarLink = sidebarRow?.children[0]?.querySelector('a');

  if (mainLink?.href) {
    const frag = await loadFragment(mainLink.pathname);
    if (frag) mainEl.append(frag);
  } else {
    mainEl.append(mainCell || '');
  }
  // same pattern for sidebarLink / sideEl
  layout.append(mainEl, sideEl);
  block.replaceChildren(layout);
}

If a row's cell contains a link, its target is loaded as a fragment (loadFragment); otherwise the cell's own content is used directly — this single block now covers both "sidebar as fragment reference" and "sidebar as inline authored content", where the source system needed a whole separate parsys drop-zone mechanism for the same flexibility.

CSS

.sticky-sidebar-layout {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  gap: var(--spacing-32, 32px);
}
@media (width >= 768px) {
  .sticky-sidebar-layout { grid-template-columns: 1fr 300px; align-items: start; }
  .sticky-sidebar-side { position: sticky; top: calc(var(--nav-height, 64px) + var(--spacing-24, 24px)); }
  .sticky-sidebar.left .sticky-sidebar-layout { grid-template-columns: 300px 1fr; }
  .sticky-sidebar.left .sticky-sidebar-side { order: -1; }
}

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (sticky-sidebar)EDS (sticky-sidebar)
Column ratioFixed 50/50 flex splitFixed sidebar width (300px) + fluid main column via CSS Grid
Sticky mechanismJS-driven, via shared data-stick-behavior utility and abbv-fixed class togglingNative CSS position: sticky
Content sourceTwo parsys drop zones (author drags any component in directly)Two richtext fields, each optionally resolving to a loaded fragment
Always-present link listVertical link list always injected into sidebarNo forced content — sidebar is purely author-controlled
Side selection fieldstickySide select (Left → sidebar-left, Right → literal space)classes_variant select (empty string default = right, left explicit)

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
customCssClassestextfieldcommon block classes propertyRenamedHandled by shared common-properties block, not component-specific
anchorIDtextfieldcommon block ID propertyRenamedHandled by shared common-properties block
stickySideselectclasses_variantRenamedSame two positions, field renamed; "Right" is now an explicit empty-string default instead of a literal space character
nonstickycontentpar (parsys)parsys drop zonemainContentRenamedFreeform component drop zone replaced by a single richtext field that can also resolve to a fragment link
stickycontentpar (parsys)parsys drop zonesidebarContentRenamedSame conversion as main content
Sidebar vertical link list (fixed inclusion)forced data-sly-resource include—Not mappedNo forced link-list injection exists in the EDS block; equivalent content must be authored explicitly inside sidebarContent if needed
data-stick-behavior / data-stick-offset / data-stick-anchor-posdata attributes consumed by shared JS—Not mappedReplaced entirely by native CSS position: sticky; no equivalent authorable offset/anchor-position fields exist

Stock Ticker — Technical Details

What it does

Fetches a stock quote from a configured endpoint on an interval and renders the price (split into large integer + smaller fraction, e.g. "$225" + ".09"), a formatted timestamp, and a colored up/down change indicator. This is a purely EDS-native, net-new block — no corresponding AEM source component exists. The manifest lists an empty aemSources array for this block, and a direct search of the mirrored source tree confirms no component, dialog, HTL template, or Sling Model with a matching name exists anywhere in the source system.

Stock Ticker Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/stock-ticker/. It has no authorable fields at all — the data source URL comes from site-wide runtime configuration, not from block content.

File Structure

blocks/stock-ticker/
├── stock-ticker.js
├── stock-ticker.css
├── _stock-ticker.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   └── stock-ticker.css
├── botox/
│   └── stock-ticker.css
└── rinvoq/
    └── stock-ticker.css

Authoring Model (_stock-ticker.json)

{
  "definitions": [{
    "title": "Stock Ticker", "id": "stock-ticker",
    "plugins": { "xwalk": { "page": {
      "resourceType": "core/franklin/components/block/v1/block",
      "template": { "name": "Stock Ticker", "model": "stock-ticker" }
    } } }
  }],
  "models": [ { "id": "stock-ticker", "fields": [] } ],
  "filters": []
}

An empty fields array — the block is a drop-in widget with zero author-configurable content.

How the JS works (stock-ticker.js)

function renderSplitPrice(price, priceEl) {
  const [integer, fraction] = price.toFixed(2).split('.');
  priceEl.innerHTML = `<span class="price">$${integer}</span><span class="fraction">.${fraction}</span>`;
}

export default async function decorate(block) {
  const stockTickerUrl = await getConfigValue('stockTickerUrl');
  block.innerHTML = `... .stock-wrapper skeleton with .symbol, .timestamp, .trade-container, .change, .source ...`;

  async function fetchStock() {
    const res = await fetch(stockTickerUrl, { cache: 'no-store' });
    const data = await res.json();
    const price = Number(data?.price);
    const prev = Number(data?.previousClose);
    const diff = price - prev;
    const percent = prev ? (diff / prev) * 100 : 0;
    symbolEl.textContent = data?.symbol ?? '--';
    timestampEl.textContent = `${formatTimestamp(data?.timestamp)} EST`;
    renderSplitPrice(price, priceEl);
    if (diff < 0) { changeEl.classList.add('red'); iconEl.classList.add('down-icon'); }
    else { changeEl.classList.add('green'); iconEl.classList.add('up-icon'); }
  }

  await fetchStock();
  setInterval(fetchStock, 5 * 60 * 1000); // poll every 5 minutes
}

The endpoint URL is read from site config (getConfigValue('stockTickerUrl')) rather than authored per-block, so a single config change updates the ticker sitewide. Failure handling replaces the entire block with a plain error message (block.innerHTML = '<p>Error fetching stock data</p>') rather than preserving the last known price.

CSS

All colors are pulled through design-token aliases (no literal hex values in the block CSS) — --stock-up: var(--color-accent-green), --stock-down: var(--error-color). Up/down indicators are pure-CSS triangles built with border tricks on ::before pseudo-elements rather than icon assets:

.stock-ticker .up-icon::before {
  width: 0; height: 0;
  border-left: var(--spacing-08) solid transparent;
  border-right: var(--spacing-08) solid transparent;
  border-bottom: var(--spacing-12) solid var(--stock-up);
}

Responsive breakpoints at 744px and 1024px scale up the price font size progressively (24px base heading size up through 80px on desktop). The abbvie brand override file is present but numerically identical to the base file — it re-declares the same token aliases with no divergent values, i.e. a no-op override kept for structural consistency across brand folders.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

Not applicable — there is no AEM source component for this block, so there are no AEM properties to classify. The block's only "field" concept, the data endpoint URL, is sourced from site-wide runtime configuration (stockTickerUrl) rather than any per-instance authored property on either side.

Story Card — Technical Details

What it does

Renders a single patient/customer/leadership "story" card: an image, eyebrow, title, description, publication date/read-time metadata, and a CTA — all pulled dynamically from a referenced page's metadata and content, not authored inline on the card itself. This is a net-new EDS block: the manifest lists an empty aemSources array, and no matching component exists under the mirrored AEM source tree. It supports five distinct visual variants (Card Info, Story Card Info, Leader Info, Side Panel, Related Content) driven by a single storyCardVariant field.

Story Card Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/story-card/, and is one of the most complex blocks in the target implementation — it resolves a referenced page's metadata via multiple fallback paths (site index, fetched HTML <meta> tags, and legacy inline richtext) and can additionally resolve AEM DAM asset paths into Adobe Asset-delivery ("OpenAPI") URLs at render time.

File Structure

blocks/story-card/
├── story-card.js
├── story-card.css
├── _story-card.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   └── story-card.css
├── botox/
│   └── story-card.css
└── rinvoq/
    └── story-card.css

Authoring Model (_story-card.json)

{ "component": "select", "name": "storyCardVariant", "label": "Story Card Type", "value": "cardInfo",
  "options": [
    { "name": "Card Info", "value": "cardInfo" },
    { "name": "Story Card Info", "value": "storyCardInfo" },
    { "name": "Leader Info", "value": "leaderInfo" },
    { "name": "Side Panel", "value": "sidePanel" },
    { "name": "Related Content", "value": "relatedContent" }
  ] },
{ "component": "boolean", "name": "hidePublicationDate", "label": "Hide Publication Date" },
{ "component": "boolean", "name": "hideReadTime", "label": "Hide Read/Watch Time" },
{ "component": "boolean", "name": "hideRole", "label": "Hide Title" },
{ "component": "boolean", "name": "hideDescription", "label": "Hide Description" },
{ "component": "boolean", "name": "hideImage", "label": "Hide Image" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "id", "label": "Id" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "customClass", "label": "Custom Class" },
{ "component": "aem-content", "name": "page", "label": "Page", "required": true },
{ "component": "boolean", "name": "openInNewTab", "label": "Open in New Tab" },
{ "component": "select", "name": "imagePreset", "label": "Image Preset",
  "options": ["Default","Landscape","Wide","Square","Portrait"] },
{ "component": "text", "name": "imageModifiers", "label": "Image Modifiers" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "ctaLabel", "label": "CTA Text" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "analyticsInteractionId", "label": "Analytics Interaction ID", "readOnly": true }

How the JS works (story-card.js)

The card's content comes almost entirely from the referenced page, not from fields authored on the card block itself (only storyCardVariant, the hide-toggles, and layout/CTA overrides are authored directly). getStoryCardData resolves that page's story data through two parallel sources and merges them:

const pending = (async () => {
  const pageProps = await indexUtils.getPageProperties(pageReference.path);   // site index lookup
  indexData = buildStoryData(pageProps);
  const html = await fetch(htmlCandidate).then((r) => r.text());             // fetched page HTML
  documentData = buildStoryData(parsePageDocument(new DOMParser().parseFromString(html, 'text/html')));
  const mergedData = mergeStoryData(documentData, indexData);
  const resolvedImage = await resolveDamAssetToOpenApi(mergedData.image, aemPublishUrl);
  return { valid, data: { ...mergedData, image: resolvedImage } };
})();

resolveDamAssetToOpenApi is a fallback path unique to this block: if the referenced page's image is still a raw AEM DAM path (/content/dam/...), it fetches that asset's metadata JSON and reconstructs an Adobe Asset-delivery URL from the returned repo:repositoryId/repo:assetId (or a UUID-based delivery-origin fallback) — a direct accommodation for content still mid-migration off the legacy DAM.

Author-mode UX is a first-class concern: if the referenced page can't be resolved yet, the block renders an explicit "Configure Story Card" placeholder (visible only in the Universal Editor) instead of silently failing; in production it simply removes itself from the DOM (removeBlock).

CSS

Mobile-first, with explicit per-variant selectors (.story-card.is-leader-card, .story-card.is-side-panel, .story-card.is-related-content, .story-card.large-title) each redefining layout direction, image aspect ratio, and typography scale independently at 744px and 1024px breakpoints. Visibility toggles for the hide-booleans are pure CSS class gates (.story-card.hide-role .story-card-title { display: none; }) rather than conditional rendering, so the DOM always contains the full markup.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

Not applicable in the traditional sense — there is no AEM source component. All fields in the table below are EDS-native (no AEM equivalent to classify against); listed here for completeness per the required field inventory.

EDS fieldTypeAEM equivalentNotes
storyCardVariantselectNot mappedNo AEM source component exists; this is a net-new authoring concept
pageaem-contentNot mappedNet-new — story content is pulled live from the referenced page rather than authored on the card
hidePublicationDate / hideReadTime / hideRole / hideDescription / hideImagebooleanNot mappedNet-new display toggles with no AEM precedent
imagePreset / imageModifiersselect / textNot mappedNet-new responsive-image controls, tied to the Dynamic Media / OpenAPI delivery logic in the JS
ctaLabeltextNot mappedNet-new override; default CTA text otherwise comes from the referenced page's own metadata
analyticsInteractionIdtext (read-only)Not mappedDeterministically generated client-side from a hash of the block's resource path, replacing any server-generated interaction ID scheme

Story Cards — Technical Details

What it does

A grid/list container of story-card items. It is a thin orchestration layer over the story-card block: it reuses story-card.js's decorateStoryCard, applyStoryCardVariation, and buildStoryCardItem helpers directly rather than duplicating card-rendering logic, and additionally supports a legacy authoring shape where a card item is a flat row of image + link content rather than a full nested story-card block. Like story-card, this is a net-new EDS block — the manifest lists an empty aemSources array, and no matching AEM source component exists.

Story Cards Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/story-cards/.

File Structure

blocks/story-cards/
├── story-cards.js
├── story-cards.css
├── _story-cards.json
├── metadata.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   └── story-cards.css
└── botox/
    └── story-cards.css

Authoring Model (_story-cards.json)

{
  "definitions": [
    { "title": "Story Cards", "id": "story-cards",
      "plugins": { "xwalk": { "page": { "template": { "model": "story-cards", "filter": "story-cards" } } } } },
    { "title": "Story Card", "id": "story-card-item",
      "plugins": { "xwalk": { "page": { "resourceType": ".../block/v1/block/item",
        "template": { "model": "story-card", "storyCardVariant": "cardInfo", ... } } } } }
  ],
  "models": [
    { "id": "story-cards", "fields": [
      { "component": "select", "name": "classes", "value": "leader-cards-grid", "label": "Variation",
        "options": [
          { "name": "Leader Cards Grid", "value": "leader-cards-grid" },
          { "name": "Story Card Wide", "value": "story-card-wide" }
        ] }
    ] }
  ],
  "filters": [ { "id": "story-cards", "components": ["story-card-item"] } ]
}

Each item added inside a Story Cards block is itself a full story-card model instance (same fields as the standalone Story Card block), not a cut-down variant.

How the JS works (story-cards.js)

decorate distinguishes between two authoring shapes per row — a modern nested story-card item (detected via data-aue-prop markers for page/hideImage/imagePreset) versus a legacy flat image+link row:

function isStoryCardItemRow(row) {
  return !!(
    row.querySelector('[data-aue-prop="page"]')
    || row.querySelector('[data-aue-prop="hideImage"]')
    || row.querySelector('[data-aue-prop="imagePreset"]')
    || row.children.length > 2
  );
}

[...block.children].forEach((row) => {
  if (isStoryCardItemRow(row)) {
    const { li, storyCard } = buildStoryCardItem(row, block);   // delegates to story-card.js
    nestedStoryCards.push(storyCard);
    return;
  }
  const { ul: legacyList } = buildLegacyCardItem(row);          // flat image+link fallback
  ul.append(...legacyList.children);
});

if (nestedStoryCards.length) {
  await Promise.all(nestedStoryCards.map((storyCard) => decorateStoryCard(storyCard)));
}

For legacy rows, buildLegacyCardItem also does URL-based image-type sniffing: if a converted picture element was lost and only a link with an image-like extension remains, it reconstructs a <picture> from that URL (isImageUrl checks for scene7.com URLs or common image extensions). The block also strips authoring-only id:/lang: marker rows before rendering (applyContainerProps).

CSS

Two layout variants selected via the classes field: leader-cards-grid (CSS grid of cards) and story-card-wide (full-width stacked list). Both share hover-scale transforms on card images (transform: scale(1.05) on link hover) and a large bottom margin on the last grid item (margin-bottom: var(--spacing-190)) to create breathing room before the next page section.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

Not applicable — no AEM source component exists for this block.

EDS fieldTypeAEM equivalentNotes
classes (Variation: Leader Cards Grid / Story Card Wide)selectNot mappedNet-new layout variant control with no AEM precedent
nested story-card-item fields(full story-card model)Not mappedSame field set as the standalone Story Card block — see the Story Card section's field table for the same "Not mapped, net-new" classification

Table — Technical Details

What it does

Recreates a standard HTML <table> (with <thead>/<tbody>) from block rows, supporting a striped/bordered/no-header style multiselect and a 1–5 column authoring filter. This is the standard block-library "Table" pattern, not an AEM-derived component — the manifest lists an empty aemSources array, and no matching component exists anywhere under the mirrored AEM source tree.

Table Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/table/. Its one notable piece of non-generic logic is stripping authoring-only marker rows/cells that the document-to-JCR conversion pipeline (md2jcr) injects to drive the Universal Editor's per-column-count filter mechanism.

File Structure

blocks/table/
├── table.js
├── table.css
├── _table.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   └── table.css
├── botox/
│   └── table.css
├── rinvoq/
│   └── table.css
└── linzess/
    └── table.css

Authoring Model (_table.json)

{ "component": "multiselect", "name": "classes", "label": "Options",
  "options": [
    { "name": "Border", "children": [ { "name": "Striped", "value": "striped" }, { "name": "Bordered", "value": "bordered" } ] },
    { "name": "Header", "children": [ { "name": "No Header", "value": "no-header" } ] }
  ] },
{ "component": "select", "name": "filter", "label": "Columns", "value": "table",
  "options": [
    { "name": "1 Column", "value": "table" }, { "name": "2 Columns", "value": "table-2-columns" },
    { "name": "3 Columns", "value": "table-3-columns" }, { "name": "4 Columns", "value": "table-4-columns" },
    { "name": "5 Columns", "value": "table-5-columns" }
  ] }

Row-item models scale with column count — table-row (1 col) through table-col-4 (4 cols), each a flat set of column<N>text richtext fields, gated by a matching filters entry (table-2-columns → table-col-2, etc.).

How the JS works (table.js)

const FILTER_ROW_RE = /^table-\d+-columns$/i;
const COL_MARKER_RE = /^table-col-\d+$/i;

export default async function decorate(block) {
  const header = !block.classList.contains('no-header');
  // Drop any single-cell filter-marker rows (e.g. "table-4-columns").
  const rows = [...block.children].filter((row) => {
    const cells = [...row.children];
    return !(cells.length === 1 && FILTER_ROW_RE.test(cells[0].textContent.trim()));
  });

  rows.forEach((row, i) => {
    let cells = [...row.children];
    // Strip a leading component-id marker cell (e.g. "table-col-4").
    if (cells.length && COL_MARKER_RE.test(cells[0].textContent.trim())) {
      cells = cells.slice(1);
    }
    cells.forEach((cell) => {
      const td = document.createElement(i === 0 && header ? 'th' : 'td');
      if (i === 0) td.setAttribute('scope', 'column');
      td.innerHTML = cell.innerHTML;
      tr.append(td);
    });
  });
}

The first surviving row becomes <thead> unless .no-header is set on the block; all others become <tbody> rows.

CSS

.table.block table thead tr { border-top: 2px solid; border-bottom: 2px solid; }
.table.block table tbody tr { border-bottom: 1px solid; }
.table.block.no-header table tbody tr { border-top: 1px solid; }
.table.block.striped tbody tr:nth-child(odd) { background-color: var(--overlay-background-color); }
.table.block.bordered table th, .table.block.bordered table td { border: 1px solid; }

Font size scales up at 600px and 900px breakpoints via --body-font-size-xs/s/m tokens; the table itself scrolls horizontally on overflow (overflow-x: auto) rather than reflowing.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

Not applicable — no AEM source component exists for this block.

EDS fieldTypeAEM equivalentNotes
classes (Striped / Bordered / No Header)multiselectNot mappedNet-new style toggles; no comparable AEM data-table component in the source tree
filter (Columns: 1–5)selectNot mappedNet-new authoring-time column-count selector, tied to the md2jcr filter-marker-row mechanism the JS strips out
column1text…column4textrichtextNot mappedNet-new per-column cell content fields

Tabs — Technical Details

What it does

Renders a set of clickable tab buttons that show/hide associated content panels. In the source system, tab panel content is bound to its tab by list position — a real, confirmed bug that causes content to become mismatched whenever tabs are reordered, inserted, or deleted. The current target implementation avoids this entirely by matching tab buttons to page sections by name instead of position, but the live authoring model is otherwise extremely minimal — most of the source system's per-tab authoring surface (placement, tab type/icon/image, per-tab analytics, MVA fields) currently has no equivalent field in the live block.

Architecture (source system)

Author adds a "Tabs" component, then adds N tab entries via a multifield
("Tab Details" → ./tabDetails), each becoming one JSON-serialized TabsBean
         &#8595;
TabsModel.tabDetails():
  for (int j = 0; j < tabDetails.size(); j++) {
      TabsBean tabItemBean = gson.fromJson(tabDetails.get(j), TabsBean.class);
      if (isAuthor() && (tabID is blank or stale)) {
          tabItemBean.setTabID(getId() + "-" + j);      // ID derived from LIST INDEX j
      }
  }
         &#8595;
tabs-container.html renders one <a> button per tabsBeans entry (in list order)
tabs-content-container.html renders one panel per tabsBeans entry:
      <sly data-sly-test.parPath="abbvtab${tabsDetailsList.count}">
        <sly data-sly-resource="${@path=parPath, resourceType='wcm/foundation/components/parsys'}"></sly>
      </sly>

The parPath — the JCR node name holding a given panel's actual authored content — is computed as abbvtab${tabsDetailsList.count}, i.e. the 1-based position of the tab in the current list, not any stable identifier tied to the tab's title or content. If an author reorders the tabs, inserts a new tab in the middle, or deletes one, every panel's abbvtabN parsys path shifts — but the actual JCR nodes named abbvtab1, abbvtab2, etc. do not move with them. The result is tab N's button showing tab M's previously-authored content.

Sling Model — TabsModel.java

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class TabsModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    @Inject @Optional private List<String> tabDetails;   // JSON strings, one per multifield row

    private void tabDetails() {
        tabsBeans = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int j = 0; j < tabDetails.size(); j++) {
            TabsBean tabItemBean = gson.fromJson(tabDetails.get(j), TabsBean.class);
            if (isAuthor() && (StringUtils.isBlank(tabItemBean.getTabID())
                    || !tabItemBean.getTabID().equals(getId() + "-" + j))) {
                tabItemBean.setTabID(getId() + "-" + j);   // position-derived ID, re-stamped on every author save
                saveToCrx = true;
            }
            setAnalyticsData(j, tabItemBean);
            tabsBeans.add(tabItemBean);
        }
        boolean anyOpen = tabsBeans.stream().anyMatch(e -> e.getOpenTabOnPageLoad().equals("true"));
        if (!anyOpen) tabsBeans.get(0).setOpenTabOnPageLoad("true"); // first tab defaults active
    }
}

TabsBean (the multifield row shape) carries tabTitle, tabTitleMobile, tabImage, tabImageAltText, tabImageTitle, tabIcon, hideInMobile, componentTitle, titleCustomCssClasses, contentCustomCssClasses, tabID, openTabOnPageLoad, plus a full analytics/MVA field set (tabsInteractionId, mvaTier, mvaValue, mvaTypeAndCategory, mvaSecondaryTier).

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor IDanchorIDtextfieldDeep-link anchor for the whole component
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestextfieldApplied to full Tabs component
Tabs PlacementtabsContainerLayoutselectTop / Left / Bottom / Right
Tabs Text AlignmenttabTextAlignmentselect (datasource)Admin-configured alignment options
Tab TypetabsTextTypeselectOnly Text / Text Icon / Text Image
Section Title (Analytics)sectionTitletextfield
Localized Section Title (Analytics)localizedSecTitletextfield
Tab Details (multifield, per tab)tabDetails → per-row fields belowmultifieldOne entry per tab
  Tab IDtabIDtextfield (disabled)System-generated from list position
  Custom CSS Classes (Tab)titleCustomCssClassestextfield
  Custom CSS Classes (Tab Panel)contentCustomCssClassestextfield
  Tab Title on DesktoptabTitletextfield
  Tab Title on MobiletabTitleMobiletextfield
  Tab ImagetabImagepathbrowserMutually exclusive with Tab Icon
  Tab Image Alt TexttabImageAltTexttextfield
  Tab Image Title TexttabImageTitletextfield
  Tab IcontabIconselect (datasource)
  Open this Tab by DefaultopenTabOnPageLoadcheckboxShould be set on exactly one tab
  Hide In MobilehideInMobilecheckbox
  Tab Title (Analytics)componentTitletextfield
  Analytics Interaction IDtabsInteractionIdtextfield (disabled)System-generated
  Enable MVA TiermvaTiercheckbox
  MVA Title (Analytics)mvaValuetextfield
  MVA Action Type and CategorymvaTypeAndCategoryselect (datasource)
  Enable MVA Tier SecondarymvaSecondaryTiercheckbox

HTL — the position-bound panel binding

<!-- tabs-content-container.html -->
<sly data-sly-list.tabsDetails="${tabClass.tabBeans}">
    <div class="abbv-tab ${tabsDetails.tabID} ${tabsDetails.openTabOnPageLoad == 'true' ? 'abbv-active' : ''} ..." id="${tabsDetails.tabID}">
        <sly data-sly-test.parPath="abbvtab${tabsDetailsList.count}">
            <sly data-sly-resource="${@path=parPath, resourceType='wcm/foundation/components/parsys'}"></sly>
        </sly>
    </div>
</sly>

tabsDetailsList.count is the HTL list's 1-based loop index — a live re-evaluation of position on every render, with no relationship to a stable per-tab key. This is the literal mechanism behind the reorder/insert/delete content-mismatch bug.

CSS

ui/tabs.css in the source system drives layout via placement classes (abbv-tabs-controls-left, abbv-tabs-controls-right, etc.) and active/hidden state toggling via abbv-active, applied and removed by a shared client script (abbvTabs.init()), not by any framework-level state binding.

Important Constraints

  • The core bug: panel content is bound to tab list position (abbvtabN), not to a stable identifier. Reordering, inserting, or deleting a tab causes every subsequent panel's content to shift and mismatch its tab button.
  • Tab Image and Tab Icon are mutually exclusive by authoring convention only — the dialog does not enforce this, and the HTL simply prefers image over icon based on tabsTextType.
  • "Open this Tab by Default" should be set on exactly one tab; if none is set, the model force-defaults the first tab (by position) to active — again reinforcing positional dependence.
  • Deep-linking is via a generated tabID (#abbv-tab-123456), not a human-readable or content-stable slug.

Tabs Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/tabs/. Per the live authoring model (confirmed against the ground-truth field inventory), it is deliberately minimal: the container-level tabs model has exactly one field (classes, a Default/Pill style select), and the per-tab tab-item model has exactly one field (tabName, required). There is currently no live equivalent for tab placement, tab type/icon/image, per-tab analytics, or MVA fields from the source system — those simply have no field in the live model today, not a renamed or repurposed one.

File Structure

blocks/tabs/
├── tabs.js
├── tabs.css
├── _tabs.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/, botox/, rinvoq/, rinvoq-hcp/, linzess/, mavyret/, skyrizi-hcp/, venclexta/, roy/
│   └── tabs.css  (+ roy/themes/bright/tabs.css, themes/bright/tabs.css)

Authoring Model (_tabs.json)

{
  "models": [
    { "id": "tabs", "fields": [
      { "component": "select", "name": "classes", "label": "Style",
        "options": [ { "name": "Default", "value": "" }, { "name": "Pill", "value": "pill" } ] }
    ] },
    { "id": "tab-item", "fields": [
      { "component": "text", "name": "tabName", "value": "", "label": "Tab Name", "required": true,
        "description": "Name shown on the tab button. Must match a Tab Panel's Tab Name (case-insensitive)." }
    ] }
  ],
  "filters": [ { "id": "tabs", "components": ["tab-item"] } ]
}

This matches the live, ground-truth field model exactly: one field per model, both confirmed against the current EDS block-requirements inventory.

How the JS works (tabs.js) — name-matching instead of position

function normalize(value) {
  return value?.trim().toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-').replace(/^-|-$/g, '') || '';
}

function getSectionIdentifier(section) {
  if (section.id) return section.id;
  if (section.dataset.aueLabel) return section.dataset.aueLabel;
  // ...falls back to a section-metadata "name"/"tabname" row if present
}

const tabNames = tabItems.map((item) => item.firstElementChild?.textContent.trim() || '');

const tabPanelMap = new Map();
tabNames.forEach((name) => {
  const normalizedName = normalize(name);
  const matched = panels.filter(
    (section) => normalize(getSectionIdentifier(section)) === normalizedName,
  );
  if (matched.length > 0) tabPanelMap.set(name, matched);
});

Each tab button's name is matched case-insensitively against a following page section's identifier (its id attribute, sourced from a sectionId section-metadata row). Because this is a name match rather than an index match, reordering the tab-item rows or the page sections independently does not desynchronize content — a tab still finds its panel by name regardless of either list's order. This is the direct fix for the source system's positional-binding bug. Multiple sections can share one tab name and are grouped into a single panel; sections with no matching tab name are left outside the tab structure entirely.

CSS

Tab buttons render as an overflow-scrollable row (overflow-x: auto) with body-size tokens scaling at 600px/900px breakpoints, consistent with the table block's responsive type pattern. Nine brand/campaign folders (abbvie, botox, rinvoq, rinvoq-hcp, linzess, mavyret, skyrizi-hcp, venclexta, roy, including a nested roy/themes/bright/ theme variant) override active/inactive tab colors — this block has one of the widest brand-override footprints of any block in the target implementation.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtextfieldcommon block ID propertyRenamedHandled by shared common-properties block, not tabs-specific
customCssClassestextfieldclasses (partially)RenamedLive classes field is a fixed Default/Pill style select, not a freeform class list — only partial equivalence
tabTitle (Tab Title on Desktop)textfieldtabNameRenamedNow also functions as the join key to its panel (by name), not just display text
tabsContainerLayout (Tabs Placement)select—Not mappedNo live field controls tab placement (top/left/bottom/right); current block only supports a horizontal top row
tabTextAlignmentselect (datasource)—Not mappedNo live equivalent
tabsTextType (Tab Type: text/icon/image)select—Not mappedLive tabs are text-only; no icon or image tab-type support exists yet
tabTitleMobiletextfield—Not mappedNo separate mobile-label field in the live model
tabImage / tabImageAltText / tabImageTitlepathbrowser/textfield—Not mappedNo per-tab image support in the live model
tabIconselect (datasource)—Not mappedNo per-tab icon support in the live model
openTabOnPageLoadcheckbox—Not mappedLive block always activates the first tab whose name matches a panel; no per-tab override authored
hideInMobilecheckbox—Not mappedNo live equivalent
titleCustomCssClasses / contentCustomCssClassestextfield—Not mappedNo per-tab or per-panel custom class fields in the live model
tabIDtextfield (disabled, position-derived)—Not mappedSuperseded entirely by name-based matching; the live model has no generated-ID concept and does not reproduce the position-binding bug
sectionTitle / localizedSecTitle / componentTitle / tabsInteractionIdtextfield—Not mappedNo analytics field set exists in the live model
mvaTier / mvaValue / mvaTypeAndCategory / mvaSecondaryTiercheckbox/textfield/select—Not mappedMVA analytics tagging concept has no live equivalent

Tag Utility Nav — Technical Details

What it does

An in-page search box combined with an optional category-filter dropdown, used to filter a list of items (e.g. a news/resource listing) that sits adjacent to the block on the page. The manifest lists an empty aemSources array for this block. Its own JS file header and CSS file both self-document a claimed AEM source — TagsUtilityNav component backed by com.abbvie.abbviecom2.core.models.TagsUtilityModel — but this source could not be located: no file, class, or path fragment matching TagsUtility or the referenced abbviecom2 package exists anywhere in the mirrored source tree (searched exhaustively by filename and by content). The mirrored source only contains the commercal-legacy/common-elements-aem-parent module (package com.abbvie.admp.commonelements); the referenced abbviecom2 module is a different, unavailable codebase. This section therefore documents the EDS implementation only, and notes explicitly that the claimed AEM source is undocumented/unavailable rather than inferring its structure.

Tag Utility Nav Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built. The block is fully implemented at blocks/tag-utility-nav/. It authors as a fixed sequence of configuration rows followed by a variable number of category child rows, and at runtime attaches a combined text-search + category filter over whatever list it finds adjacent to it in the page.

File Structure

blocks/tag-utility-nav/
├── tag-utility-nav.js
├── tag-utility-nav.css
├── _tag-utility-nav.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   └── tag-utility-nav.css
├── botox/
│   └── tag-utility-nav.css
└── rinvoq/
    └── tag-utility-nav.css

Authoring Model (_tag-utility-nav.json)

{ "component": "text", "name": "searchPlaceholder", "label": "Search Placeholder Text" },
{ "component": "select", "name": "classes_searchIconType", "label": "Search Icon Type", "value": "no-icon",
  "options": [ { "name": "None", "value": "no-icon" }, { "name": "Icon Font", "value": "icon-font" }, { "name": "Image", "value": "image" } ] },
{ "component": "text", "name": "searchFontIcon", "label": "Search Font Icon",
  "condition": { "===": [ { "var": "classes_searchIconType" }, "icon-font" ] } },
{ "component": "reference", "name": "searchIcon", "label": "Search Image Icon", "multi": false,
  "condition": { "===": [ { "var": "classes_searchIconType" }, "image" ] } },
{ "component": "text", "name": "searchInID", "label": "Search in ID",
  "description": "Enter the ID of the container to search in. If left empty, searches the next sibling list." },
{ "component": "text", "name": "browseCategories", "label": "Browse Categories Placeholder Text",
  "description": "Default label for the category dropdown. Leave empty to disable." },
{ "component": "text", "name": "clearCategories", "label": "Reset Categories Placeholder Text",
  "description": "Enter text to enable the clear/reset button. Leave empty to disable." }

// child model "tag-utility-nav-category":
{ "component": "text", "name": "categoryTag", "label": "Category Tag" },
{ "component": "aem-content", "name": "categoryLink", "label": "Category Link" },
{ "component": "text", "name": "categoryTitle", "label": "Category Title" }

How the JS works (tag-utility-nav.js)

Configuration is read positionally from the first six rows, then everything after that is treated as category child rows:

const CONFIG_ROW_COUNT = 6; // rows 0-6 are block-level config fields

const searchPlaceholder = getCellText(rows[0]) || '';
const searchFontIcon = getCellText(rows[1]) || '';
const iconSrc = getCellImage(rows[2]) || '';
const searchInID = getCellText(rows[3]) || '';
const browseCategories = getCellText(rows[4]) || '';
const clearCategories = getCellText(rows[5]) || '';
const categories = parseCategories(rows); // rows[6..] → { tag, link, title }

The block does not assume the filterable list is a specific sibling — findTargetContainer checks, in order, an explicit searchInID element by ID, the block's direct next sibling, or (if the block was wrapped by the section renderer) the wrapper's next sibling, then narrows to a <ul> if the match isn't already one:

function findTargetContainer(block, targetId) {
  if (targetId) { const el = document.getElementById(targetId); if (el) return el; }
  let next = block.nextElementSibling;
  if (!next) {
    const wrapper = block.closest('.tag-utility-nav-wrapper');
    if (wrapper) next = wrapper.nextElementSibling;
  }
  if (!next) return null;
  if (next.tagName === 'UL') return next;
  return next.querySelector('ul') || null;
}

Filtering combines free-text search and an active category selection with simple substring matching against each item's full text content (applyFilters), toggling hidden per item and showing/hiding an empty-results message and a "clear" button as appropriate. The category dropdown renders its options as either all <a> (if any category has a link, for cross-page category navigation) or all <button> (for in-page filtering only) — never a mix.

CSS

A floating-label search input (label overlaps the input and fades/moves on focus or value), a custom dropdown built from a <ul> menu rather than a native <select>, and a responsive eyebrow toolbar that stacks vertically on mobile and goes row-aligned at 744px:

.tag-utility-nav-search-input:focus ~ .tag-utility-nav-search-label,
.tag-utility-nav-search-field.has-value .tag-utility-nav-search-label {
 opacity: 0;
 top: 0;
}
@media (width >= 744px) {
  .tag-utility-nav-eyebrow { flex-direction: row; justify-content: space-between; }
}

The stylesheet also carries a large block of surrounding-content styling (.tag-utility-nav-container .default-content-wrapper h2/p/h4/ul/hr) intended for the section that wraps this block on real pages, matching spacing/typography observed on the live site.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

The claimed AEM source (TagsUtilityModel) could not be located in the mirrored repository, so no real AEM field names are available to classify. All fields below are documented as EDS-native pending discovery of the actual source dialog, if one exists in an unmirrored codebase.

EDS fieldTypeClaimed AEM equivalentNotes
searchPlaceholdertextUnverifiableSource component/dialog not present in the mirrored repository
classes_searchIconTypeselectUnverifiableSame — cannot confirm against a real dialog field
searchFontIcon / searchIcontext / referenceUnverifiableConditional on icon type; no source dialog available to compare
searchInIDtextUnverifiableNo source to compare against
browseCategories / clearCategoriestextUnverifiableNo source to compare against
categoryTag / categoryLink / categoryTitletext / aem-content / textUnverifiableChild item model; no source to compare against

Teaser Component — Technical Details

What it does

Renders a two-column promotional teaser: an eyebrow label above the content, a title/heading in the left column, and a description with an optional call-to-action button in the right column. It is a pure content-presentation block with no data-fetching or third-party integration.

Architecture

No direct AEM source component exists for this block. The manifest cross-reference and the source-system component tree under ui.apps/.../components/content/ were both checked directly, and there is no teaser (or equivalently named) component in the source system. The closest source-side building blocks that could have supplied similar authoring fields individually — a titles component (heading text), a text component (rich text), and a button/cta component (link + button styling) — exist as separate, independently-authored components in the source system; there was no single "teaser" component that combined eyebrow + title + description + CTA into one authoring unit. The EDS teaser block is therefore best understood as a **net-new composition pattern** built directly against the target implementation's block-authoring model, not a straight migration of an existing source component. It is included in this report because it is a real, already-built EDS block, but the "AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping" section below reflects that there is no source-side field to map from.

Author fills 8 rows in Universal Editor (eyebrow, title, description, buttonText,
buttonURL, buttonType, clickType, ariaLabel)
         ↓
md2jcr publishes rows positionally as .teaser > div (row 0..7)
         ↓
teaser.js decorate(block):
  row 0  → .teaser-eyebrow (inserted above .teaser-content, role="heading")
  row 1  → .teaser-left   (title, gets .teaser-title + role="heading")
  row 2  → .teaser-right  (description; button appended here if present)
  rows 3-7 (buttonText/buttonURL/buttonType/clickType/ariaLabel) → read then removed
         ↓
.teaser
  .teaser-eyebrow
  .teaser-content (flex/grid row)
    .teaser-left  (title)
    .teaser-right (description + optional .button-container > a.button)

Sling Model

Not applicable — there is no source-system Sling Model backing this block, because there is no corresponding source component.

Dialog
