Not applicable for the same reason. The table below instead shows the real target-implementation authoring model (from _teaser.json), which functions as the block's dialog equivalent under Universal Editor.

FieldComponent typeNotes
eyebrowrichtextSmall label rendered above the two-column content
titlerichtextRendered as whatever heading/paragraph tag is authored; JS tags it .teaser-title, forces role="heading" aria-level="3"
descriptionrichtextBody copy in the right column
buttonTexttextCTA label
buttonURLaem-contentCTA destination
buttonTypeselect (Internal=link-internal / External=link-external)Adds a CSS class to the link; drives arrow-icon glyph in CSS (::after content)
clickTypeselect (_self / _blank)Sets the link's target; combined with link-external forces rel="noopener noreferrer"
ariaLabeltextApplied to the CTA <a> and (bugged) also used as aria-labelledby value on the block itself, pointing at a non-existent id (see Constraints)

HTL

Not applicable — no HTL template exists for this block.

CSS

.teaser {
  max-width: var(--content-max-width);
  margin: var(--spacing-00) auto var(--spacing-80);
  padding: var(--spacing-24) var(--spacing-16);
}

.teaser .teaser-content {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(6, 1fr);
  margin-left: var(--spacing-35);
  margin-right: var(--spacing-35);
}

@media (min-width: 1024px) {
  .teaser .teaser-content {
    grid-template-columns: repeat(12, 1fr);
    gap: var(--spacing-24);
  }
  .teaser .teaser-left  { grid-column: 1 / span 6; }
  .teaser .teaser-right { grid-column: 8 / span 5; }
}

.teaser .button.link-internal::after {
  content: '\e901';
  font-family: var(--icon-font-family);
}
.teaser .button.link-external::after {
  content: '\e900';
  font-family: var(--icon-font-family);
}

Mobile-first: single 6-column grid stacking left/right at narrow widths, expanding to a 12-column grid with the title spanning 6 columns and the description/CTA spanning 5 (offset) at desktop. Brand overrides for abbvie/, botox/, and rinvoq/ each simply @import '../teaser.css'; with no per-brand rule changes at the moment — placeholder brand scaffolding, not yet differentiated.

Important Constraints

  • The eyebrow-extraction logic supports two different authored shapes (<p><strong>text</strong></p> or plain <p>text</p>) to bridge local testing content against real author-produced markup — a sign the block was hardened against real authoring drift after initial build.
  • aria-labelledby is set to the literal eyebrow text string, not an element id — aria-labelledby requires an id reference, so this attribute does not function as intended and should be treated as a known accessibility defect, not a working feature.
  • Button rows are removed in reverse index order specifically to avoid re-indexing bugs while iterating — an implementation detail, not a design decision, but worth preserving during any refactor.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
Not applicable — no source-system component maps to this block. All 8 fields in _teaser.json (eyebrow, title, description, buttonText, buttonURL, buttonType, clickType, ariaLabel) were defined directly against the target authoring model with no prior source-side field to migrate from. This is recorded explicitly rather than fabricating a source mapping.

Teaser Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built
The block is fully implemented at blocks/teaser/, including brand-specific CSS override folders.

File Structure

blocks/teaser/
├── teaser.js
├── teaser.css
├── _teaser.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   └── teaser.css   (@import '../teaser.css'; no brand deltas yet)
├── botox/
│   └── teaser.css   (@import '../teaser.css'; no brand deltas yet)
└── rinvoq/
    └── teaser.css   (@import '../teaser.css'; no brand deltas yet)

How the JS works — see Architecture above; decorate() reads eight positional rows, builds an eyebrow div, wraps title/description in a flex/grid container, and conditionally appends a CTA button when both buttonText and buttonURL are present.

block-config.js is an unused scaffold — all three lifecycle hooks (beforeDecorate, decorate, afterDecorate) and the variations array are commented out, meaning the block currently relies entirely on its default decorate() export with no configured variations.

Text Container Component — Technical Details

What it does

Renders one or more rows of rich text (and optionally images) in either a single column or a two-column layout, with a large catalog of CSS-only presentation variants (footnote, disclaimer, references, legal, boxed warning, glossary) and spacing/width/alignment/direction/theme options, all driven by author-selected CSS classes rather than component logic.

Architecture

No direct AEM source component exists for this block — confirmed by checking both the manifest (empty aemSources) and the source component tree directly (no text-container directory). The closest source-system analog for "a block of rich text" is the generic text component (used, for example, as the nested widget backing the Tooltip component's Text tab), but there is no source component that models the multi-row, multi-column, class-directive authoring pattern this block uses. This is therefore a target-implementation-native pattern, not a migrated component.

Author authors N rows in Universal Editor; optionally authors a row whose sole
content is the literal text "class:somecustomclass" or "id:..."/"lang:..." to
inject a class/id/lang onto the block and have that row self-remove
         ↓
text-container.js decorate(block):
  applyCommonProps(block)          — scans first 4 rows for lang:/id: directive
                                      text and strips matching rows
  isPlaceholder(row) check         — also strips '', '-', 'none' placeholder rows
                                      (but never removes a genuine paragraph-only
                                      content row — a fixed regression, see code
                                      comment)
  unwrap each remaining row's inner div, tag children
    <picture>          → .text-container-picture
    everything else    → .text-container-text
         ↓
.text-container[.text-two-columns][.align-*][.theme-*][.text-variant classes...]
  > .text-container-text / .text-container-picture (flattened, one per row)

Sling Model

Not applicable — no source-system Sling Model exists for this block.

Dialog

Not applicable. The real target-implementation authoring model (_text-container.json) is summarized below in place of a dialog table, because it plays the equivalent role.

FieldComponent typeNotes
classes_customDynamicClasstextFree-text comma-separated custom class list
classes_textWidthselectfull-width / large-width / content-width / narrow-width — desktop-only 12-column grid span presets
classes_textTopSpacing / classes_textBottomSpacingselecttight/compact/comfortable/spacious margin utility classes
classes_textAlignmentselectleft/center/right/justify
classes_textDirectionselectltr/rtl — flips list bullet/padding sides
classes_themeselectlight/dark text color
classes_textVariantselect (classField: true)Default/Footnote/Disclaimer/References/Legal/Boxed Warning/Glossary — each a pharma-specific presentation style
classes_columnsselectone column / two columns
analytics_idtextAnalytics tracking identifier
(common-prop include)—blockId (id: regex-validated), classes_commonCustomClass, language field — shared across many blocks via _common-properties.json

Two repeatable child models feed the filtered content area: text-container-image (image reference + alt text) and text-container-text (richtext), registered as the allowed components under the text-container filter.

HTL

Not applicable — no HTL template exists.

CSS

.text-container.text-two-columns {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  gap: var(--gap-10);
}

@media (width >= 744px) {
  .text-container.text-two-columns {
    grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr));
    gap: var(--gap-20);
  }
}

/* VARIANT: Boxed Warning */
.text-container.boxed-warning {
  border: 2px solid var(--color-black);
  padding: var(--spacing-16);
  margin: var(--spacing-16) 0;
}
.text-container.boxed-warning p:first-child {
  font-family: var(--roboto-bold-font-family);
  text-transform: uppercase;
}

/* VARIANT: Glossary — 1 col mobile, 2 col at 744px+ */
.text-container.glossary { columns: 1; }
@media (width >= 744px) {
  .text-container.glossary { columns: 2; column-gap: var(--spacing-24); }
}

The stylesheet also carries pharma-specific typographic handling not typical of a generic rich-text block: sup/sub sizing for footnote markers, inline clinical-data table styling, nested multi-level list support ("ISI multi-level"), and dedicated variant classes for regulated content types (footnote, disclaimer, references, legal, boxed warning).

Important Constraints

  • The class:/id:/lang: directive-row pattern is a authoring convention layered on top of positional row parsing, not a first-class field — it only works because isPlaceholder()/applyCommonProps() scan literal text content of the first four rows for those prefixes.
  • The placeholder-stripping logic was deliberately narrowed (per in-code comment) after a real regression where a paragraph-only content row (no nested heading/list/div) was wrongly treated as an empty placeholder and dropped — a caution for anyone touching this function again.
  • All visual variants (footnote, disclaimer, legal, etc.) are pure CSS-class toggles with no corresponding JS behavior differences — misclassifying content into the wrong variant class has no functional safeguard.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
Not applicable — no source-system component maps to this block. All fields in _text-container.json were defined directly against the target authoring model.

Text Container Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built
Fully implemented at blocks/text-container/, with brand override folders for abbvie, botox, linzess, rinvoq, and rinvoq-hcp.

File Structure

blocks/text-container/
├── text-container.js
├── text-container.css
├── _text-container.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
├── botox/
├── linzess/
├── rinvoq/
└── rinvoq-hcp/

How the JS works — decorate() first calls the shared applyCommonProps(block) utility (also used by the video block; handles lang:/id: row directives), then strips leading placeholder rows, then unwraps each remaining row's single inner <div> and tags each child element .text-container-picture or .text-container-text depending on tag type.

Tooltip Component — Technical Details

What it does

Attaches a hover/tap tooltip message to a word or phrase for additional context. In the source system this is authored as a standalone component with a dedicated tooltip ID that a separately-authored piece of text elsewhere on the page references (a decoupled attach pattern, not an inline wrapper). In the target implementation it becomes a self-contained two-row block: term + definition.

Architecture

Source system:
Author sets tooltipID, size/position/animation, customCssClasses, tooltipText
         ↓
tooltip.html renders a <span class="abbv-tooltip-message ..." id="${tooltipID}">
  containing the tooltip body — but NOT attached to any trigger element yet
         ↓
Elsewhere on the page, any element gets data-abbv-tooltip="thatTooltipID"
         ↓
tooltip.js (jQuery, on document ready + per abbvToolTip.init()):
  finds all [data-abbv-tooltip] triggers
  clones the matching #tooltipID span into each trigger element
  positions it on hover via posToolTipMsg() / isOutOfViewport() —
    flips pos-top/pos-bottom/pos-left/pos-right and adds
    toolTip-stick-left/-right when the tooltip would overflow the viewport

Target implementation:
Author authors 2 rows: term, definition (richtext)
         ↓
tooltip.js decorate(block):
  builds .tooltip-trigger (role=button, tabindex=0, aria-describedby)
  builds .tooltip-panel (role=tooltip) with the definition HTML
  wires show/hide via hover (non-touch), focus/blur, click (touch), Escape/Enter/Space
  autoFlip() repositions using getBoundingClientRect(), adding flip-bottom/flip-left/flip-right

Sling Model — ToolTipModel.java

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class ToolTipModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENT_ID_PREFIX = "abbv-tooltip";
    ...
    public String getDigitalData(){
        String id = StringUtils.isNotEmpty(getInteractionIdFromDialog())
            ? getInteractionIdFromDialog() : getId();
        Link link = DigitalDataObject.getAnalyticsLinkBean(TOOLTIP, TOOLTIP, "internal", "");
        Component component = DigitalDataObject.getAnalyticsComponentBean(
            "Content", "", "", TOOLTIP, "Content");
        Interaction interaction = DigitalDataObject.getAnalyticsInteractionBean(
            DigitalDataConstants.LINK, TOOLTIP, id, checkNullProperty(thirdPartyTags));
        ...
        return analyticsString;
    }
}

The model's only real job is assembling the analytics "digital data" JSON blob (link/component/interaction/MVA beans) consumed by the client-side analytics layer — it does not compute size/position/animation classes; those pass straight through from dialog properties into the HTL.

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Tooltip IDtooltipIDtext (validated)a-z, A-Z, 0-9 only; the id the trigger elsewhere on the page references via data-abbv-tooltip
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestextspace-separated
SizetooltipSizeselect (datasource ce-tooltip-size)small/medium/large presets
PositiontooltipPositionselect (datasource ce-tooltip-position)top/bottom/left/right
AnimationtooltipAnimationselect (datasource ce-tooltip-animation)fade/grow/none/custom
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtext (disabled, read-only display)auto-generated, printed for reference
TexttooltipTextnested text component (includewidget)the tooltip body content — authored via the full source-system Text component, not a plain textarea

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-use.tooltip="${'com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.ToolTipModel'}">
  <sly data-sly-test="${properties.tooltipID}">
    <span class="abbv-tooltip-message ${properties.tooltipSize} ${properties.tooltipPosition}
                 ${properties.tooltipAnimation} ${properties.customCssClasses}"
          id="${properties.tooltipID}"
          data-digitaldata='${tooltip.digitalData @context="unsafe"}'>
      ${properties.tooltipText @context='html'}
    </span>
  </sly>
</sly>

Note the tooltip body is rendered but never attached to a trigger in this template — attachment to a [data-abbv-tooltip] element happens entirely client-side via tooltip.js, which clones the span into whichever element declares the matching id.

CSS

[data-abbv-tooltip] {
    position: relative;
    border-bottom: 1px dotted rgb(32, 32, 32);
    cursor: help;
}
.abbv-tooltip-message {
    position: absolute;
    z-index: 99;
    visibility: hidden;
    opacity: 0;
    ...
}
[data-abbv-tooltip] .abbv-tooltip-message.size-sm { width: 100px; }
[data-abbv-tooltip] .abbv-tooltip-message.size-md { width: 200px; }
[data-abbv-tooltip] .abbv-tooltip-message.size-lg { width: 400px; }
[data-abbv-tooltip]:hover .abbv-tooltip-message.anim-prop {
    opacity: 1;
    visibility: visible;
    transition-delay: 0.2s;
}

Important Constraints

  • The source component's decoupled attach model (author places the tooltip content component somewhere, then separately hand-writes data-abbv-tooltip="id" onto an unrelated trigger element) is fragile and author-error-prone — nothing in the dialog enforces that the id used on the trigger matches a real authored tooltip.
  • Multiple triggers can share one tooltip id: tooltip.js explicitly clones the message per trigger (cloneNode(true)) and marks the source as .abbv-cloned (hidden) rather than moving it, so one authored tooltip can legitimately back many triggers.
  • No role="tooltip", no aria-describedby wiring anywhere in the source HTL/JS — it is a pure visual/mouse+jQuery hover pattern with no accessibility semantics, a confirmed a11y gap that the target implementation deliberately fixes.
  • data-digitaldata is emitted via @context="unsafe" (disables auto-escaping) for the analytics JSON payload — consistent with the analytics-bean JSON-string pattern used across most of this component family, not specific to Tooltip.

EDS Migration

// blocks/tooltip/tooltip.js (already implemented — see EDS Implementation below)
export default function decorate(block) {
  const rows = [...block.children];
  const [termRow, defRow] = rows;
  const term = termRow?.textContent.trim();
  const definition = defRow?.innerHTML || '';
  const id = `tooltip-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 6)}`;

  const trigger = document.createElement('span');
  trigger.className = 'tooltip-trigger';
  trigger.setAttribute('role', 'button');
  trigger.setAttribute('aria-describedby', id);
  trigger.textContent = term;

  const panel = createTooltipPanel(definition, id); // role="tooltip"
  block.replaceChildren(trigger, panel);
  ...
}

The target implementation collapses the source system's two-part "content component + separately-hand-wired trigger element" pattern into a single self-contained block: term and definition are authored together in one block instance, removing the id-matching authoring risk entirely. It also adds real accessibility semantics (role="tooltip", aria-describedby, aria-expanded, keyboard Escape/Enter/Space handling) that the source never had, and adds a separate wireInlineTooltips() helper that turns any <abbr title="..."> found elsewhere in the page into an equivalent tooltip — a capability with no source equivalent at all.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
tooltipIDtext (validated)—Not mappedNo longer needed — the target block is self-contained (term + definition authored together), so there is no separate id-matching trigger pattern to support
customCssClassestextclasses_commonCustomClass (via shared common-properties include)RenamedSame free-text custom-class purpose, standardized field name shared across all target blocks
tooltipSizeselect (datasource)—Not mappedNo dedicated size field in the target model; .large exists as a CSS class hook in tooltip.css but is not exposed as an authorable field
tooltipPositionselect (datasource)classes_positionRenamedSame purpose (top/bottom/left/right); target select is a fixed 4-option list rather than a source-system datasource lookup, and target JS auto-flips position at runtime regardless of the authored value, which the source never did
tooltipAnimationselect (datasource)—Not mappedTarget implementation uses a single fixed opacity/visibility transition (--transition-duration-fast) for all tooltips; no authorable animation-style choice
interactionIdtext (disabled, auto-set)—Not mappedSource-only analytics plumbing tied to the source system's digital-data/Adobe Analytics bean pipeline; the target implementation has no equivalent per-field analytics id mechanism in this block
tooltipTextnested text componentdefinitionRenamedSame purpose (tooltip body content); target field is a plain richtext field rather than a full nested Text-component include
(implicit — trigger word/phrase, authored elsewhere via hand-written data-abbv-tooltip attribute)—termRenamedThe source system never modeled the trigger text as a dialog field at all — it was free-form markup elsewhere on the page; the target implementation makes it an explicit first row of the block

Tooltip Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built
Fully implemented at blocks/tooltip/, including brand override folders for abbvie, botox, and rinvoq.

File Structure

blocks/tooltip/
├── tooltip.js
├── tooltip.css
├── _tooltip.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
│   ├── tooltip.css
│   └── _tooltip.css   (@import '../tooltip.css'; + real brand overrides)
├── botox/
└── rinvoq/

Authoring Model (_tooltip.json)

{
  "id": "tooltip",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Content", "name": "content" },
    { "component": "text", "name": "term", "label": "Term",
      "description": "The word or phrase the tooltip is attached to." },
    { "component": "richtext", "name": "definition", "label": "Definition",
      "description": "Content displayed in the tooltip panel." },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Settings", "name": "settings" },
    { "component": "select", "name": "classes_position", "label": "Panel Position",
      "options": [
        { "name": "Top (default)", "value": "" },
        { "name": "Bottom", "value": "bottom" },
        { "name": "Left", "value": "left" },
        { "name": "Right", "value": "right" }
      ]
    },
    { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
  ]
}

How the JS works

Beyond the two-row decorate flow described in Architecture, the block maintains a module-level registry (a Set) of all active tooltips on the page plus a single shared document-level click listener (attachGlobalListener, using an AbortController so it can be cleanly torn down once the registry empties) that closes any open tooltip when a click lands outside it. isTouchDevice() checks window.matchMedia('(hover: none)') to skip binding hover handlers on touch devices, falling back to tap-to-toggle via the role="button" trigger's click handler.

CSS

Token-driven (--tooltip-bg, --tooltip-offset, --tooltip-arrow-size, etc., all with fallback values) with an abbvie/ brand override that redefines the panel with hard-coded pixel values (4px radius, 6px arrow, 8px offset) rather than tokens — a simpler, non-tokenized brand skin layered on top via @import '../tooltip.css' plus overriding rules.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (tooltip)EDS (tooltip)
Authoring modelDecoupled: tooltip content authored as one component instance; trigger is hand-written markup elsewhere referencing its idSelf-contained: term + definition authored together in one block instance
Attach mechanismjQuery cloneNode into every matching [data-abbv-tooltip] element on init()Trigger element built directly by decorate(); no cross-DOM id matching
AccessibilityNone — no ARIA roles/attributes, hover-only via CSS, no keyboard pathFull pattern: role="button"/role="tooltip", aria-describedby, aria-expanded, focus/blur, Escape/Enter/Space
PositioningAuthor-selected static position class; viewport-overflow flip computed via manual getBoundingClientRect math in jQuerySame overflow-flip concept, reimplemented with autoFlip()/resetFlip() using native DOM APIs, no jQuery dependency
Touch handlingNo distinct touch path — relies on CSS :hover, which behaves inconsistently on touchExplicit isTouchDevice() branch switches to tap-to-toggle
Inline tooltip supportNot presentAdditional wireInlineTooltips() export upgrades any <abbr title> elsewhere on the page into an equivalent tooltip

UGC Detail View Component — Technical Details

What it does

Renders a user-generated-content patient story record — name, demographic/consent fields, story text, and before/after or single treatment images — pulled from properties on the resource rather than authored through a dialog. In the source system this is an internal moderation/audit view, not an author-facing content component.

Architecture

UGC submission is ingested from an external patient-story platform and stored
as resource properties (FirstName, LastName, Title, Description, PhoneNumber,
gender, EmailAddress, PreferredCommunication, Age, IsAnonymous,
DiseaseSeverity, LocationDiseaseImagery(+OtherText), HISCR_12weeks,
LiveworldModerationStatus/StatusTimestamp, TermsOptinDate, IVDescription,
BeforeTreatmentImage, AfterTreatmentImage[list])
         ↓
ugc-detail-view.html renders each present property as a label/value "row"
(each field individually gated with data-sly-test — absent fields simply
don't render a row) plus up to three image sections: a single "IVDescription"
image, a Before Images list, and an After Images list
         ↓
.abbv-ugc-detail-view
  .abbv-ugc-patient (label/value rows)
  .abbv-ugc-image-list × up to 3 (single image / before list / after list)

This is a display-only, no-dialog component (componentGroup=".hidden") — it exists purely to render already-captured moderation/patient-story data for internal review, not for content authors to place and configure on a page.

Sling Model

No dedicated Sling Model exists — confirmed by searching the source-system Java model package for any class matching "ugc"; there is none. The HTL reads directly from ${properties.*} with no model indirection at all, consistent with this being a raw property-echo view rather than a designed, dialog-driven component.

Dialog

None exists — confirmed no _cq_dialog or any dialog file anywhere under the component folder. There is nothing for an author to configure; all values are sourced from upstream property injection (the patient-story submission/moderation pipeline), not hand-authored.

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-test.firstName="${properties.FirstName}">
  <div class="row">
    <span class="label">First Name</span><span class="value">${firstName}</span>
  </div>
</sly>
...
<div class="abbv-ugc-image-list">
  <sly data-sly-list="${properties.BeforeTreatmentImage}">
    <h3>Before Images</h3>
    <div class="ugc-image">
      <img src="${item}" title="Before Image${itemList.count}" alt="Before Image${itemList.count}"/>
    </div>
  </sly>
</div>

Every field is wrapped in its own data-sly-test so absent properties render nothing rather than an empty label — a defensive pattern appropriate for a view over sparse, externally-submitted data.

CSS

.abbv-ugc-patient .row {
    border: solid 1px lightgrey;
    display: flex;
    justify-content: space-between;
    width: 300px;
}
.abbv-ugc-patient .row:nth-child(even) { background-color: lightgrey; }
.abbv-ugc-image-list {
    display: flex;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    width: 1200px;
}

Fixed-pixel-width, non-responsive layout (300px rows, 1200px image list) consistent with an internal admin/review-tool aesthetic rather than a public-facing, responsive brand page.

Important Constraints

  • This component surfaces personally-identifiable and health-related fields (name, phone, email, age, gender, disease severity, body-location imagery) with no visible access gating in the component itself — access control, if any, lives entirely outside this component (page ACLs / hidden component group), a data-privacy consideration the design analysis flags explicitly for review.
  • The double-negative IsAnonymous handling is a real, confirmed authoring quirk in the source: there are two separate, mutually-exclusive data-sly-test blocks — one for ${properties.IsAnonymous} truthy and a second, separate block for ${!properties.IsAnonymous} — both rendering a "Posted as Anonymous" row, which is redundant and slightly confusing logic to carry forward rather than simplify.
  • Images are rendered from raw property paths (${item}, ${image}) with no responsive/rendition handling — a single fixed-resolution asset reference per image.

EDS Migration

The target implementation deliberately does not attempt to port this as an internal moderation/audit view — that use case (dozens of demographic/consent/moderation-status fields rendered as label/value pairs) has no public-page authoring purpose. Instead, the target ugc-detail block models only the subset of data actually meant for public display on a brand page: portrait, name, condition/title, story quote, and an optional before/after image pair. This is a deliberate scope reduction, not a 1:1 field port, reflecting that the source component's real purpose (internal review of submitted PII) and the target block's purpose (a published patient-testimonial card) are different use cases that happen to share a name.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
IVDescription (single image)portraitRenamedRepurposed as the public-facing portrait photo; source usage was a single generic uploaded image, not specifically a portrait
FirstName / LastNamenameRenamedSource kept first/last as two separate fields for internal record-keeping; target combines into a single free-text display name field since it is presentation-only
Title (story title)titleRenamedRepurposed as the "Condition / Title" secondary line under the patient name
DescriptionstoryRenamedSource: plain text moderation-review field. Target: richtext, rendered as a styled <blockquote>
BeforeTreatmentImage (list)beforeImageRenamedSource supported an arbitrary-length image list; target models exactly one before image (single reference field, not multi)
AfterTreatmentImage (list)afterImageRenamedSame simplification — one after image, not a list
PhoneNumber—Not mappedInternal contact-record field; no public-display purpose, and publishing a patient's phone number on a live page would itself be a data-exposure risk
EmailAddress—Not mappedSame reasoning as PhoneNumber
gender—Not mappedInternal demographic field, not part of the public testimonial card content model
Age—Not mappedInternal demographic field; not modeled in the public card (could be added to the free-text title line by the author if desired, but has no dedicated field)
IsAnonymous—Not mappedInternal moderation flag; if a story is meant to display anonymously, the author simply omits name/portrait in the target model rather than toggling a boolean
PreferredCommunication—Not mappedInternal contact-preference metadata with no public-display purpose
DiseaseSeverity—Not mappedInternal clinical/moderation metadata; not part of the public testimonial presentation
LocationDiseaseImagery / LocationDiseaseImageryOtherText—Not mappedInternal moderation metadata (body location of submitted imagery), not surfaced publicly
HISCR_12weeks—Not mappedClinical eligibility/moderation flag specific to the source review workflow; no public-page equivalent
LiveworldModerationStatus / LiveworldStatusTimestamp—Not mappedThird-party moderation-platform (Liveworld) workflow metadata — belongs to the review process, not the published card; content should only reach the target implementation's authoring model after moderation is already complete
TermsOptinDate—Not mappedLegal/consent audit metadata, not public-display content
(none in source)portraitAlt, beforeImageAlt, afterImageAlt—Net-new fields; the source component hard-coded generic alt text ("Patient Image", "Before Image1", etc.) with no author control
(none in source)classes_variant (default / centered)—Net-new presentational option not present in the source view

UGC Detail Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built
Fully implemented at blocks/ugc-detail/, with brand override folders for abbvie, botox, and rinvoq.

File Structure

blocks/ugc-detail/
├── ugc-detail.js
├── ugc-detail.css
├── _ugc-detail.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
├── botox/
└── rinvoq/

Authoring Model (_ugc-detail.json, abridged)

{
  "id": "ugc-detail",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Identity", "name": "identity" },
    { "component": "reference", "name": "portrait", "label": "Portrait Photo" },
    { "component": "text", "name": "portraitAlt", "label": "Portrait Alt Text" },
    { "component": "text", "name": "name", "label": "Patient Name" },
    { "component": "text", "name": "title", "label": "Condition / Title" },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Story", "name": "story" },
    { "component": "richtext", "name": "story", "label": "Story Quote" },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Before / After", "name": "comparison" },
    { "component": "reference", "name": "beforeImage", "label": "Before Image",
      "description": "Optional. Leave blank to hide the comparison section." },
    { "component": "reference", "name": "afterImage", "label": "After Image" },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Settings", "name": "settings" },
    { "component": "select", "name": "classes_variant", "label": "Layout",
      "options": [ { "name": "Default (left-aligned)", "value": "" },
                   { "name": "Centered", "value": "centered" } ] }
  ]
}

How the JS works

decorate() reads exactly three rows (identity, story, images), locates the portrait <img> and the two non-image text cells (name, title) within the identity row, builds a <blockquote class="ugc-detail-story"> from the story row's innerHTML, and — if the images row has any <img> elements — builds a labeled Before/After comparison grid where the caption ("Before"/"After") is inferred purely from array index (first image = Before, second = After), not from any authored label.

CSS

Circular portrait (border-radius: 50%), quote styled as a left-accent-bordered blockquote using --ugc-detail-accent (falls back to --link-color), and a .centered variant that flips the identity block to a column layout and moves the story's accent border from left to top.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (ugc-detail-view)EDS (ugc-detail)
PurposeInternal moderation/audit display of a raw patient-story submission (PII-heavy)Public-facing testimonial card with only display-appropriate fields
AuthoringNone — no dialog; data comes from an external submission pipelineFully authored via Universal Editor tabs (Identity/Story/Before-After/Settings)
Data model~18 raw properties incl. phone, email, age, gender, disease severity, moderation status/timestamp, consent date7 display fields (portrait+alt, name, title, story, before/after images+alt) plus a layout variant
Before/after imagesTwo independent arbitrary-length lists (BeforeTreatmentImage/AfterTreatmentImage)Exactly one before + one after image, explicitly paired
Alt textHard-coded generic strings ("Patient Image", "Before Image1")Author-controlled alt text fields for portrait and each comparison image

Video (YouTube) Component — Technical Details

What it does

Embeds a YouTube video player via an iframe, with author-configured autoplay, full-screen permission, and player width, plus a full client-side playback-analytics layer (play/pause/resume/end + 25/50/75/90% milestone tracking) wired to the source system's Adobe-Analytics-style "digital data" pipeline. It also coordinates with a separate Brightcove video player so that starting a YouTube video pauses any playing Brightcove video, and vice versa.

Architecture

Author sets youtubeVideoId, playerWidth, autoPlay, allowFullScreen, anchorID,
customCssClasses, epassNumber (project tracking number) + analytics tab fields
         ↓
YoutubeModel.java (Sling Model over the request) builds an analytics
"digitalData" JSON string from link/component/interaction/MVA analytics beans
         ↓
youtube.html renders:
  <div class="abbv-youtube-player ${customCssClasses}" id="${anchorID}"
       data-digitaldata='${youtubeModel.analyticsString @context="unsafe"}'>
    <iframe style="width:${playerWidth||'100%'}"
            src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/${youtubeVideoId}?autoplay=...&enablejsapi=1"
            allowfullscreen="${allowFullScreen=='true'}">
  </div>
         ↓
youtube.js (jQuery + YouTube IFrame API):
  loads https://www.youtube.com/iframe_api once
  onYouTubePlayerAPIReady() (global callback) wires a YT.Player per iframe
  onPlayerStateChange() drives digitalData updates + Adobe Satellite
    (_satellite.track) calls for play/pause/resume/end + % milestones
  IntersectionObserver pauses a playing video once it scrolls >50% out of view
  cross-pauses any playing Brightcove video (typeof abbvVideo !== 'undefined')

Sling Model — YoutubeModel.java

@Model(adaptables = { SlingHttpServletRequest.class })
public class YoutubeModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENTIDPREFIX = "abbv-youtube";
    ...
    private void setAnalyticsData() {
        String id = StringUtils.isBlank(interactionId) ? getId() : interactionId;
        Link link = DigitalDataObject.getAnalyticsLinkBean(checkNullProperty(analyticsTitle),
            "", "external", "https://www.youtube.com/embed/" + youtubeVideoId);
        Component component = DigitalDataObject.getAnalyticsComponentBean("Video",
            checkNullProperty(l10Title), checkNullProperty(sectionTitle), "Youtube",
            DigitalDataConstants.CONTENT);
        ...
        analyticsString = DigitalDataObject.getDigitalDataObject(link, new Menu(), component,
            new Content(), interaction, mva);
    }
}

Entirely analytics-payload assembly — no video-ID validation, no URL construction (that happens directly in the HTL string interpolation), no thumbnail/poster logic at all in the model or template.

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
YouTube Identifier (Anchor ID)anchorIDtextAdds an anchor id above the component for in-page jump links
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestextspace-separated
Video IDyoutubeVideoIdtext (required)Bare YouTube video ID, not a full URL
WidthplayerWidthtextRaw CSS width string; defaults to 100% if empty — injected via @context='unsafe' directly into an inline style attribute
Auto play the VideoautoPlaycheckboxWhen true, appends ?autoplay=1&mute=1 to the embed URL (YouTube requires muted autoplay)
Allow Full ScreenallowFullScreencheckboxSets the iframe's allowfullscreen attribute
Project NumberepassNumbertextPer-video tracking number; description explicitly notes it must be re-entered on every individual video instance
Analytics tab: interactionId (disabled/display-only), analyticsTitle, sectionTitle, l10Title, mvaPrimary, mvaTitleForAnalytics, mvaTypeAndCategory (datasource select), analyticsEnableMVASecondary — the same standard analytics field set used across this component family

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-test="${properties.youtubeVideoId}">
  <sly data-sly-use.youtubeModel="${'com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.YoutubeModel'}"/>
  <sly data-sly-test.autoPlayParam = "${properties.autoPlay == 'true' ? '?autoplay=1&mute=1' : '?autoplay=0'}"></sly>
  <div class="abbv-youtube-player ${properties.customCssClasses}" id="${properties.anchorID}"
       data-digitaldata='${youtubeModel.analyticsString @context="unsafe"}'>
    <iframe title="YouTube Video"
            style="width:${properties.playerWidth || '100%' @ context='unsafe'}"
            src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/${properties.youtubeVideoId}${autoPlayParam}&enablejsapi=1"
            data-sly-attribute.allowfullscreen="${properties.allowFullScreen=='true'}">
    </iframe>
  </div>
</sly>

CSS

No dedicated ui/youtube.css file exists for this component — layout/sizing is driven entirely by the inline style="width:..." attribute plus whatever customCssClasses the author supplies; there is no component-specific stylesheet to quote.

Important Constraints

  • playerWidth is injected into an inline style attribute with escaping explicitly disabled (@context='unsafe') — a real, if narrow, injection surface: any author (or anything feeding this field through automated/bulk content tools) could inject arbitrary CSS or break out of the attribute.
  • Autoplay-with-sound is not supported and not requested — the code always forces mute=1 alongside autoplay=1, matching browser autoplay-policy requirements, but this is a hard-coded behavior, not an authorable choice.
  • Cross-player coordination (pausing Brightcove when YouTube plays) is implemented via a loose runtime check (typeof abbvVideo !== 'undefined') rather than an explicit dependency — works only if the Brightcove player's script happens to have loaded first.
  • onYouTubePlayerAPIReady is declared as a bare global function (required by the YouTube IFrame API contract) — a legitimate but easily-collided global if any other script defines the same name.

EDS Migration

// blocks/video/video.js (already implemented — see EDS Implementation below)
function getYouTubeId(url) {
  const usp = new URLSearchParams(url.search);
  if (usp.get('v')) return usp.get('v');
  if (url.origin.includes('youtu.be')) return url.pathname.split('/').filter(Boolean)[0] || '';
  if (url.pathname.includes('/embed/')) return url.pathname.split('/embed/')[1]?.split('/')[0] || '';
  return '';
}

function embedYoutube(url, autoplay, background) {
  const videoId = getYouTubeId(url);
  const params = new URLSearchParams();
  params.set('rel', '0');
  params.set('enablejsapi', '1');
  params.set('origin', window.location.origin);
  if (autoplay || background) params.set('autoplay', autoplay ? '1' : '0');
  ...
}

The target implementation is a generic video block, not a YouTube-specific one: it auto-detects YouTube, Vimeo, or a native file URL from a single authored link, rather than requiring a bare video-ID field tied to one provider. It adds a poster-image overlay with title/description/play-button (none of which existed in the source), a prefers-reduced-motion guard on autoplay, and IntersectionObserver-based lazy-loading of the actual embed (the source always injected the iframe immediately). Per-video analytics (milestone tracking, Adobe Satellite calls, cross-player pause coordination) are not present in the target block at all.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
youtubeVideoIdtext (required)uriRenamedSource: bare YouTube video ID only. Target: a full link (aem-content field), auto-detected as YouTube/Vimeo/native by loadVideoEmbed() — broader capability, not just a rename
anchorIDtextblockId (via common-properties include, id: prefix convention)RenamedSame in-page anchor purpose, standardized field name/pattern shared across target blocks
customCssClassestextclasses_commonCustomClassRenamedSame purpose, standardized shared field
playerWidthtext (unsafe-context inline style)—Not mappedTarget player is always full-width within its .video-shell container (responsive aspect-ratio: 16/9); no per-instance width override field, and no equivalent unsafe-context style injection
autoPlaycheckboxclasses multiselect → autoplay optionRenamedSame purpose (enable autoplay); target also always mutes autoplay video and additionally respects prefers-reduced-motion before actually autoplaying, which the source did not check
allowFullScreencheckbox—Not mappedTarget's YouTube/Vimeo iframes always set allowFullscreen = true and a full allow permissions string unconditionally; not an authorable per-instance toggle
epassNumber (Project Number)text—Not mappedSource-specific project/billing tracking number tied to the source organization's internal video-approval process; no equivalent concept in the target model
interactionId, analyticsTitle, sectionTitle, l10Title, mvaPrimary, mvaTitleForAnalytics, mvaTypeAndCategory, analyticsEnableMVASecondaryvarious—Not mappedSource-side digital-data/Adobe Analytics bean pipeline (link/component/interaction/MVA) has no equivalent field group in the target block; analytics in the target implementation, where present, is instrumented differently (event-based, not per-field authored metadata)
(none in source)—placeholderImage / placeholderAlt—Net-new poster-image capability; the source always rendered a live iframe with no poster/click-to-play stage at all
(none in source)—overlayTitle / overlayDescription / overlayBtnText / classes_overlayColor / classes_overlayBtnStyle—Net-new overlay UI (title, description, styled play button) with no source equivalent

Video Block — EDS Implementation

What's already built
Fully implemented at blocks/video/ (the standard block-collection video block, enhanced), with brand override folders for abbvie, botox, and rinvoq.

File Structure

blocks/video/
├── video.js
├── video.css
├── _video.json
├── block-config.js
├── abbvie/
├── botox/
└── rinvoq/

How the JS works

decorate() first calls the shared applyCommonProps(block) utility, then reads text rows positionally to populate poster alt text and overlay title/description/button text, explicitly calls resolveImageReference() on the placeholder-image row (mirroring the pattern used by the brightcove-podcast-player block, per an in-code comment, because EDS otherwise leaves the reference as an unresolved link rather than a real <picture>) and clones the resolved node before wiping the block's textContent. It then builds a .video-shell containing a .video-media mount point, optionally a poster <picture>, and — unless autoplay is set — a click-to-play overlay built by buildOverlay(). Actual video embedding is deferred: an IntersectionObserver triggers loadVideoEmbed() only once the block enters the viewport (or immediately queues it behind the overlay-button click), which auto-detects YouTube/Vimeo/native via embedYoutube()/embedVimeo()/getNativeVideoElement().

CSS

.video.video-stage .video-shell {
  position: relative;
  width: 100%;
  aspect-ratio: 16 / 9;
  overflow: hidden;
  background: var(--color-navy-dark);
}
.video-stage[data-embed-loaded='false']:not(.has-poster) {
  aspect-ratio: 16 / 9;
}

Layout is built around a fixed 16:9 aspect-ratio stage with absolutely-positioned poster/player/overlay layers stacked via inset: 0, so swapping from poster to live embed never causes layout shift.

Key differences from AEM implementation

AEM (youtube)EDS (video)
Provider supportYouTube only, via bare video IDYouTube, Vimeo, or native file, auto-detected from one link field
Loading strategyIframe injected immediately on page render; YouTube IFrame API script loaded globally up frontIntersectionObserver-gated lazy load; nothing embedded until viewport entry or explicit user click
Poster/overlayNone — video chrome is visible immediatelyPoster image + title/description/styled play button overlay, removed on play
Reduced motionNot checkedprefers-reduced-motion media query explicitly suppresses autoplay-on-scroll-into-view
Analytics/milestonesFull custom milestone tracking (25/50/75/90%) plus Adobe Satellite _satellite.track() calls per state changeNot present in this block
Cross-player coordinationExplicit pause-the-other-player logic against a global abbvVideo/Brightcove objectNot present in this block

Container (Flip-Card) Component — Technical Details

What it does

The source-system container component is a dual-purpose component: used plainly, it is just a nested paragraph-system (parsys) wrapper that lets authors group and class arbitrary child components together. When its "Add Flip Capability" checkbox is enabled, it becomes a front/back flip-card: a second, independent nested parsys ("back") is rendered behind the first, and the whole thing rotates in 3D on click, scroll-into-view, or hover.

Architecture

Author enables flipContainer, picks flipActivation (click/scroll/hover) and
flipDirection (ltr/rtl/ttb/btt/custom), sets flipButtonText, authors content
into two separate nested parsys drop zones: containerparsys (front) and
backcontainerparsys (back)
         ↓
container.html branches entirely on !properties.flipContainer:
  flipContainer unset  → plain .abbv-container wrapping one parsys
  flipContainer set    → .abbv-flip-card wrapping .abbv-flip-card-inner >
                          .abbv-flip-card-front (containerparsys) +
                          .abbv-flip-card-back (backcontainerparsys),
                          each with its own optional "flip" button
                          (rendered only when flipActivation == 'click')
         ↓
container.js (abbvFlipCard, jQuery):
  init() — wires click/hover handlers per data-flip-type; adds .abbv-animation
           class for the scroll-triggered variant (actual scroll-trigger
           IntersectionObserver-equivalent logic lives elsewhere / via
           .abbv-animation convention used across the codebase)
  size()/sizeAll() — measures front/back heights via jQuery .height() and
           forces both faces (and optionally a whole row of cards) to a
           shared height so the flip doesn't visibly resize the card

Sling Model — ContainerModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {SlingHttpServletRequest.class })
public class ContainerModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String COMPONENT_ID_PREFIX = "abbv-container";
    ...
    private void setContainerDigitalData() {
        String id = StringUtils.isBlank(interactionId) ? getId() : interactionId;
        Link link = DigitalDataObject.getAnalyticsLinkBean(checkNullProperty(analyticsTitle),
            checkNullProperty(flipButtonText), "internal", "");
        Component component = DigitalDataObject.getAnalyticsComponentBean("content",
            checkNullProperty(l10Title), checkNullProperty(sectionTitle), "Container", "content");
        ...
        this.analyticsData = DigitalDataObject.getDigitalDataObject(link, new Menu(), component,
            content, interaction, mva);
    }
}

Like Tooltip/Youtube, the model's only real responsibility is building the analytics "digital data" payload for the flip button's click interaction — it has no involvement in the flip mechanics or parsys resolution themselves (that is pure HTL data-sly-resource + client-side JS).

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Container Identifier (Anchor ID)anchorIDtextIn-page anchor
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestextspace-separated
Add Flip CapabilityflipContainercheckboxSwitches the whole component from plain-wrapper mode to flip-card mode
Flip Button ActivationflipActivationselectclick / scroll / hover
Flip Direction / TypeflipDirectionselectltr / rtl / ttb / btt / (blank = "use a custom CSS class")
Flip Button Title TextflipButtonTexttextPopulates the flip button's title attribute (click mode only)
Analytics tab (flip button click): interactionId (disabled/display), analyticsTitle, sectionTitle, l10Title, mvaPrimary, mvaTitle, mvaTypeAndCategory, mvaSecondary
Content (not a dialog field — authored by placing components directly into the page): front content → containerparsys; back content → backcontainerparsys

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-test.enableFlip="${!properties.flipContainer}">
  <div class="abbv-container ${properties.customCssClasses}">
    <sly data-sly-resource="${@path='containerparsys',resourceType='wcm/foundation/components/parsys'}"></sly>
  </div>
</sly>

<sly data-sly-test="${!enableFlip}">
  <div class="abbv-container abbv-flip-card ..." data-flip-type="${properties.flipActivation}"
       data-flip-direction="${properties.flipDirection}">
    <div class="abbv-flip-card-inner">
      <div class="abbv-flip-card-front">
        <sly data-sly-resource="${@path='containerparsys',resourceType='wcm/foundation/components/parsys'}"></sly>
        <button class="abbv-action-card-flip ..." data-sly-test="${properties.flipActivation == 'click'}"
                title="${properties.flipButtonText}"></button>
      </div>
      <div class="abbv-flip-card-back">
        <sly data-sly-resource="${@path='backcontainerparsys',resourceType='wcm/foundation/components/parsys'}"></sly>
        <button class="abbv-action-card-flip ..." data-sly-test="${properties.flipActivation == 'click'}"
                title="${properties.flipButtonText}"></button>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</sly>

CSS

.abbv-flip-card {
    position: relative;
    perspective: 1000;
}
.abbv-flip-card-inner {
	position: relative;
	transition: transform 0.8s;
	transform-style: preserve-3d;
}
.abbv-flip-card-front, .abbv-flip-card-back {
  position: absolute;
  backface-visibility: hidden;
  transform-style: preserve-3d;
}
.abbv-flip-card-back { transform: rotateY(180deg); }
[data-flip-direction="ttb"] .abbv-flip-card-back,
[data-flip-direction="btt"] .abbv-flip-card-back { transform: rotateX(180deg); }

.abbv-flip-card[data-flip-direction="ltr"].abbv-flip-active .abbv-flip-card-inner {
  transform: rotateY(180deg);
}

Important Constraints

  • The "plain wrapper" and "flip card" behaviors are two entirely different rendering paths inside one component, selected by a single boolean — this genuinely conflates two different concerns (a layout/grouping primitive vs. an interactive flip widget) into one component, which the design analysis explicitly calls out as worth splitting apart during migration rather than porting as-is.
  • Both parsys drop zones (containerparsys, backcontainerparsys) accept arbitrary nested components with no depth limit — authors could nest another container-with-flip inside either face, which the source system does not prevent.
  • Neither face has aria-hidden or any focus-management applied to the currently-hidden face — confirmed by reading the HTL, there is no ARIA attribute anywhere in this template. Keyboard/screen-reader users can tab into and have announced the back face's content even while it is visually hidden behind the front face (or vice versa) — a real, confirmed accessibility gap in the source.
  • size()`/`sizeAll() force both faces to a shared height via direct jQuery .height() measurement/assignment, run on init only — content that changes height after load (e.g. a lazy-loaded image) can desync the two face heights.

EDS Migration — PROPOSED, not yet implemented

No flip-card directory exists yet under blocks/ in the target implementation repository. The following is a proposed design, based directly on the "container (flip-card) → net-new flip-card block" guidance already worked out in the design-analysis reference material, and is not yet built.

The proposal splits the source component's two conflated concerns cleanly: the "plain wrapper" use case is dropped entirely — a standard section/div with default content is sufficient and does not need a dedicated block. Only the genuine flip-card interaction becomes a new block, modeled as a flat 2-row structure (no nested parsys-equivalent) rather than two separately-addressed drop zones:

Proposed authoring model — 2 rows, content authored directly in each:
  Row 0: Front Content (richtext/image/CTA authored directly)
  Row 1: Back Content  (richtext/image/CTA authored directly)
  + config fields: flipActivation (click/scroll/hover), flipDirection

Proposed _flip-card.json (illustrative):

{
  "id": "flip-card",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Content", "name": "content" },
    { "component": "container", "name": "front", "label": "Front Content" },
    { "component": "container", "name": "back", "label": "Back Content" },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Settings", "name": "settings" },
    { "component": "select", "name": "classes_flipActivation", "label": "Flip Activation",
      "options": [
        { "name": "Click", "value": "flip-click" },
        { "name": "Scroll into view", "value": "flip-scroll" },
        { "name": "Hover", "value": "flip-hover" }
      ]
    },
    { "component": "select", "name": "classes_flipDirection", "label": "Flip Direction",
      "options": [
        { "name": "Left / Right", "value": "flip-x" },
        { "name": "Top / Bottom", "value": "flip-y" }
      ]
    },
    { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
  ]
}

Proposed flip-card.js (illustrative):

// PROPOSED — not yet implemented
export default function decorate(block) {
  const [frontRow, backRow] = [...block.children];
  frontRow.className = 'flip-card-front';
  backRow.className = 'flip-card-back';

  // Fix vs AEM: hide the inactive face from assistive tech and keyboard tab order
  backRow.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
  backRow.querySelectorAll('a, button, input, [tabindex]')
    .forEach((el) => el.setAttribute('tabindex', '-1'));

  const inner = document.createElement('div');
  inner.className = 'flip-card-inner';
  inner.append(frontRow, backRow);
  block.replaceChildren(inner);

  function setFace(showFront) {
    backRow.toggleAttribute('aria-hidden', showFront);
    frontRow.toggleAttribute('aria-hidden', !showFront);
    [...frontRow.querySelectorAll('a, button, input, [tabindex]')]
      .forEach((el) => el.setAttribute('tabindex', showFront ? '0' : '-1'));
    [...backRow.querySelectorAll('a, button, input, [tabindex]')]
      .forEach((el) => el.setAttribute('tabindex', showFront ? '-1' : '0'));
    block.classList.toggle('is-flipped', !showFront);
  }

  if (block.classList.contains('flip-click')) {
    block.setAttribute('role', 'button');
    block.setAttribute('tabindex', '0');
    block.addEventListener('click', () => setFace(block.classList.contains('is-flipped')));
  } else if (block.classList.contains('flip-hover')) {
    block.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => setFace(false));
    block.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => setFace(true));
  } else if (block.classList.contains('flip-scroll')) {
    const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
      entries.forEach((entry) => setFace(!entry.isIntersecting));
    });
    observer.observe(block);
  }
}

Proposed CSS approach (illustrative): same 3D-transform mechanics as the source (perspective, transform-style: preserve-3d, backface-visibility: hidden, a rotateY(180deg)/rotateX(180deg) back face), retained because it is a sound, standards-based technique — the proposed change is not to the CSS mechanism but to explicitly pairing every visual flip with the aria-hidden/tabindex toggling shown above, which the source never did.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldMaps to EDS field (proposed)Mapping typeNotes
flipContainer (checkbox)—Not mappedNo longer needed as a toggle — in the proposed design, "plain wrapper" is not a flip-card block at all (use a default section), so the block itself is only ever the flip-card variant; there is nothing to toggle
containerparsys (front, nested parsys)front (proposed container field / row)RenamedSame purpose (front-face content), flattened from an arbitrary-depth nested parsys to one directly-authored row/slot per the general net-new-block flattening principle
backcontainerparsys (back, nested parsys)back (proposed container field / row)RenamedSame flattening as front
flipActivationclasses_flipActivation (proposed)RenamedSame purpose (click/scroll/hover); proposed field renders as a class rather than a data-flip-type attribute, to fit the target implementation's class-driven config convention
flipDirectionclasses_flipDirection (proposed)RenamedSource offers 4 explicit directions plus a "use custom CSS class" escape hatch; proposed design simplifies to axis-based (X/Y) since the visual result of ltr vs rtl (and ttb vs btt) differs only in initial rotation sign, not in final appearance
flipButtonText—Not mapped (proposed)Proposed click-mode interaction makes the whole card a role="button", rather than rendering a separate small icon-button per face with a title-attribute tooltip; no separate button-text field is needed in that design
anchorIDblockId (common-properties include, proposed)RenamedSame in-page anchor purpose, standardized field
customCssClassesclasses_commonCustomClass (proposed)RenamedSame purpose, standardized field
interactionId, analyticsTitle, sectionTitle, l10Title, mvaPrimary, mvaTitle, mvaTypeAndCategory, mvaSecondary—Not mapped (proposed)Source-side digital-data analytics bean pipeline tied to the flip button's click interaction has no proposed equivalent field group; would need separate analytics-instrumentation design if required

HTML Container (Embed Pro) Component — Technical Details

What it does

Renders whatever raw HTML an author pastes into a textarea directly into the page, completely unescaped. Internally named "Embed Pro," it exists as an escape-hatch for arbitrary custom markup/scripts that no other component supports.

Architecture

Author pastes raw HTML into a 40-row textarea (./html), plus optional
customCssClasses and anchorID
         ↓
html-container.html renders:
  <a id="${anchorID}"></a>
  <div class="abbv-html-container abbv-embed-pro ${customCssClasses}">
    ${properties.html @context="unsafe"}
  </div>
         ↓
Whatever the author typed — markup, inline <script>, <style>, iframes,
event-handler attributes — is emitted verbatim into the live page

There is no Java Sling Model backing this component at all (confirmed — no matching class found under the source-system model package), and no client-side JS clientlib beyond a shared, unrelated CSS include (link-list.css, apparently a stale/mis-scoped clientlib reference). This is the entire implementation: one four-line HTL file with a single unescaped output expression.

Sling Model

None exists. This is a deliberate architectural fact, not an oversight to fix during migration: there is no server-side processing step in the source implementation at all where a sanitization or allow-listing control could even be inserted without changing the component's fundamental nature.

Dialog

Two dialog definitions exist side by side in the source tree: a modern Granite _cq_dialog/.content.xml (the one actually wired up and used, per sling:resourceType="cq/gui/components/authoring/dialog") and an older-style dialog.xml (cq:Dialog ExtJS-era format) that appears to be dead/superseded — it references different property paths (./heroText/jcr:Heading, ./heroText/jcr:description) that do not match anything the HTL template actually reads, confirming it is stale legacy authoring config left in place rather than an active second dialog.

FieldPropertyTypeNote
HTMLhtmltextarea, 40 rows, resizableThe entire raw-HTML payload; no character/size limit, no format hint or warning text in the dialog itself
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestextspace-separated
Anchor IDanchorIDtextIn-page anchor

The component is registered with componentGroup=".hidden", meaning it does not appear in the standard component-browser sidebar for authors to drag onto a page.

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<a data-sly-test="${properties.anchorID}" id="${properties.anchorID}"></a>
<div class="abbv-html-container abbv-embed-pro ${properties.customCssClasses}">
	${properties.html @context="unsafe"}
</div>

This is the entire render logic. @context="unsafe" is HTL's explicit escaping-disabled context — it tells the template engine to output the string exactly as stored, with none of HTL's normal automatic HTML-escaping applied.

CSS

No component-specific CSS file exists; the referenced clientlib (categories="[link-list.v1,common-elements.v1]", pulling in css.txt → link-list.css) is a mis-scoped/unrelated stylesheet reference, not styling authored for this component.

Important Constraints — Security Finding

This is a confirmed, serious security finding, not a routine migration item, and it is called out here explicitly rather than folded into ordinary "gaps to fix."

  • The entire rendering path is ${properties.html @context="unsafe"} — a single expression with all HTML escaping disabled and zero sanitization, allow-listing, tag/attribute stripping, or any other compensating control anywhere in the request path (confirmed: no Sling Model, no OSGi filter/servlet, nothing between the stored property and the rendered page).
  • This is, by design-analysis assessment against the rest of the platform, the single most permissive rendering path anywhere in the reviewed source-system component set — every other component that outputs rich content either escapes it, restricts it to a specific nested component's controlled output, or passes it through a defined field type; this one passes through anything at all.
  • componentGroup=".hidden" hides the component from the authoring UI's component-browser sidebar, but this is not an access control — it does not prevent an existing page instance from being copied, does not prevent instantiation via page-JSON import/bulk content tooling, and does not prevent any author who already knows the resource type from placing it directly. Treating sidebar-hiding as a security boundary was a false sense of restriction in the source system and must not be assumed to carry any protective value going forward.
  • Governance note from the design-analysis reference material: the source-system's real, if informal, compensating control was an organizational one — approval routed through an internal publishing/governance process — not anything enforced by the component or platform itself.

EDS Migration — PROPOSED, not yet implemented, GOVERNANCE-GATED

No html-container directory exists yet under blocks/ in the target implementation repository, and — unlike the other net-new blocks in this batch — building it is explicitly not recommended as a default migration action. This is a governance decision, not a code design decision, and it should not be resolved by an engineer during ordinary migration work.

Primary recommendation: for the large majority of "embed a third-party thing" needs, use the existing, already-implemented, generic embed block instead, which injects an iframe or a provider script through a standard, reviewed pattern — not raw unescaped HTML. That block already exists in the target repository and requires no new governance decision to use.

If, and only if, there is a genuine, governance-approved need for raw-HTML embedding that the embed block cannot satisfy (mirroring the source system's own "approval-only" governance posture), a net-new html-container block could be built — but even then, several conditions must hold, none of which are things this document can satisfy on its own:

  • It must not rely on hiding the block from the component picker as its only control — that already failed as a control in the source system and provides no more protection in the target implementation.
  • Access must be gated at the Universal Editor component-definition/filter level (excluded from the default allowed-components list for most templates) and by an organizational workflow/approval step that lives outside the block's own code — because the block itself, if built, would enforce nothing, exactly as in the source.
  • No sanitization should be bolted on at the block level as a substitute for governance — sanitizing the input would defeat the block's entire purpose (rendering arbitrary raw HTML on request), so the risk acceptance has to be made explicitly at the platform/governance level, documented, and revisited — not quietly assumed during a routine code review.

Illustrative Universal Editor authoring-model entry if this block is ever approved for construction (proposed, restricted, not shipped):

// PROPOSED — not yet implemented — requires governance approval before use
{
  "id": "html-container",
  "fields": [
    {
      "component": "text",
      "name": "html",
      "label": "Raw HTML (APPROVAL REQUIRED — see governance policy before use)",
      "valueType": "string"
    }
  ]
}

No auto-blocking/heuristic-detection guidance applies regardless of the build decision — this block, if it is ever built at all, must always be the product of deliberate, explicit authoring action gated by process, never something inferred or auto-created from other content.

As a related hardening note (applies to the existing, already-built embed block, not to this proposal): the design-analysis reference material recommends adding a sandbox attribute to embed's iframe mode as an improvement over the source system, which had none — this is a good general control but is a separate, much lower-risk item from the html-container question above.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldMaps to EDS field (proposed, if ever approved)Mapping typeNotes
htmlhtml (proposed)Same nameSame raw-HTML purpose; carrying the field forward with an explicit approval-required label rather than a plain field label, since the risk profile is identical to the source and must not be understated
customCssClasses—Not mapped (proposed)Deliberately omitted from the proposed minimal field set — the fewer authorable knobs on an already-maximally-permissive block, the smaller the surface for accidental misuse; a governance-approved implementation could reinstate this if genuinely required
anchorID—Not mapped (proposed)Same reasoning — omitted from the minimal proposed field set pending an actual governance-approved design, not a technical incompatibility

Interactive Image Component — Technical Details

What it does

A single image that behaves in one of four author-selected ways: zoom-on-hover, touch-drag-to-pan/zoom, a plain link, or a trigger that opens a modal/hidden panel — plus an optional decorative icon overlay (e.g. a magnifier or external-link glyph) positioned in a corner.

Architecture

Author uploads image (+ alt/title/height/width), picks imageBehaviour
(image-hover / image-drag / external-link / modal-image), optionally an
icon + iconPosition, optionally lazy-loading, and — for external-link/
modal-image only — a target path, modal ID, link type, target type, plus
the standard analytics field set
         ↓
InteractiveImageModel.java (Sling Model):
  resolves the actual renderable image URL (imagePath) from the uploaded
  asset reference — branching on whether the asset lives in a "remotedam"
  path (uses a dedicated e-DAM rendition helper) vs. a normal DAM asset
  (uses the standard CommonUtility.getImageUrl helper)
  auto-computes imageheight/imagewidth from the asset's own metadata when
  the author left them blank AND a site-level metadata flag allows it
  builds the analytics digitalData payload (for link-type behaviors)
         ↓
interactive-image.html branches on imageBehaviour:
  hover/drag  → <div class="abbv-image {behaviour} {iconPosition}"><img/><span
                class="{icon}"/></div> — a plain non-interactive-looking DOM;
                all zoom/drag interaction is added client-side via a jQuery
                zoom plugin
  external-link/modal → delegates entirely to the shared anchor.html
                template's `interactiveImage` named template, passing image
                src/alt/title, link target/type, modal id, icon, and the full
                analytics parameter set through
         ↓
interactive-image.js (jQuery, on document ready):
  $('.image-hover').zoom()              — hover-zoom plugin init
  $('.image-drag').zoom({ on: 'grab' })  — drag-to-pan plugin init, same
                                            underlying zoom plugin, different
                                            trigger mode

Sling Model — InteractiveImageModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class}, defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class InteractiveImageModel extends AbstractComponentModel {
    private static final String ID_PREFIX = "abbv-interactiveimage";
    ...
    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        if (image != null && image.contains("/content/dam/remotedam")) {
            imagePath = CommonUtility.getImagePathBasedonEDAMRendition("large", image, resourceResolver);
        } else {
            imagePath = CommonUtility.getImageUrl(image, resourceResolver, resource);
        }
        ...
        String enableImageHeightAndWidthFromMetadata =
            CommonUtility.getEnableImageHeightAndWidthFromMetadata(resource, resourceResolver);
        if (enableImageHeightAndWidthFromMetadata.equals("true")) {
            if (Strings.isNullOrEmpty(imageheight)) {
                imageheight = CommonUtility.getImageSize(image, resourceResolver, AbbvieFormConstants.TFF_IMAGE_LENGTH);
            }
            if (Strings.isNullOrEmpty(imagewidth)) {
                imagewidth = CommonUtility.getImageSize(image, resourceResolver, AbbvieFormConstants.TFF_IMAGE_WIDTH);
            }
        }
    }
    public String getDigitalData(){
        ...
        if (checkNullProperty(getMva()).equals("true")) {
            String[] typeCategory = getMvaValue().split("\\|");
            mvaObj = DigitalDataObject.getAnalyticsMvaBean(typeCategory[0], typeCategory[1],
                getMvaName(), CommonUtility.getAnalyticsMVATier(getMvaTier()));
        }
        ...
    }
}

Two real behaviors worth noting precisely: (1) the "remotedam" branch is a distinct asset-resolution path for a separate/federated DAM source, resolved differently from normal local DAM assets; (2) automatic height/width backfill from asset metadata is itself gated by a separate site-level configuration flag (getEnableImageHeightAndWidthFromMetadata), read from page/resource metadata rather than being a fixed behavior — sites can opt out of CLS-reducing auto-sizing entirely.

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestext—
Anchor IDanchorIDtext—
Image BehaviorimageBehaviourselect (show/hide target)image-hover / image-drag / external-link / modal-image; drives which other dialog fields are visible via cq-dialog-dropdown-showhide
Iconiconselect (datasource ce-link-icon)Decorative overlay icon class
Icon PositioniconPositionselect (datasource ce-interactive-image-icon-position)top-right/top-left/bottom-right/bottom-left, per CSS rules keyed on these class names
Enable lazy loading for this imagelazyLoadingcheckbox (checked by default)Sets loading="lazy" on the <img>; dialog includes explicit author guidance text about not lazy-loading above-the-fold images
Image (upload)image/file (+ fileName/fileReference)fileupload—
Alt TextimageAltTexttext—
TitleimageTitletext—
Image Height / Widthimageheight / imagewidthtext (validated)Optional — auto-computed from asset metadata if left blank (see model)
Link/Modal tab (shown only for external-link/modal-image behaviors): pagePath (pathbrowser), modalID (text), targetType (select), linkType (select) + the standard analytics field set: interactionId (disabled/display), componentTitle, sectionTitle, l10Title, componentPosition (select), mva (checkbox), mvaName, mvaValue (select), mvaTier (checkbox)

HTL — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-test = "${(model.imageBehaviour == 'image-hover') || (model.imageBehaviour == 'image-drag')}">
  <div class="abbv-image ${model.imageBehaviour} ${model.icon ? model.iconPosition : ''} ${model.customCssClasses}">
    <div>
      <img src="${model.imagePath}" loading="${lazyLoad}" alt="${model.imageAltText || true}"
           title="${model.imageTitle}" height="${model.imageheight}" width="${model.imagewidth}"/>
    </div>
    <span data-sly-test = "${model.icon}" class="${model.icon} ${model.icon ? 'i-b' : ''}"></span>
  </div>
</sly>

<sly data-sly-test = "${(model.imageBehaviour == 'external-link')|| (model.imageBehaviour == 'modal-image')}">
  <div class="abbv-image ${model.imageBehaviour} ...">
    <sly data-sly-use.linkhelper="${'com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.LinkChecker' @link = model.pagePath}">
      <sly data-sly-call="${anchorTemplate.interactiveImage @ linkType=properties.linkType, url=linkhelper.linkFormatted,
           imgSrc=model.imagePath, imgAltText=model.imageAltText, modalID=model.modalID, ...}"/>
    </sly>
  </div>
</sly>

Note the fallback alt-text expression ${model.imageAltText || true} — if imageAltText is empty, HTL's || coalesces to the boolean true, which HTL renders as the literal string "true" as the alt value. This is a confirmed real defect in the source: an author who leaves alt text blank gets alt="true" rendered to the page rather than an empty or omitted attribute.

CSS

.abbv-image { display:inline-block; position: relative; left: 50%; transform: translate(-50%); cursor: pointer; }
.abbv-image.icon-top-right span    { top:0; right:0; }
.abbv-image.icon-top-left span     { top:0; left:0; }
.abbv-image.icon-bottom-right span { bottom:0; right:0; }
.abbv-image.icon-bottom-left span  { bottom:0; left:0; }
.abbv-image span {
  position:absolute; display:block; width:25px; height:25px;
  padding: 4px; opacity: 0.8; color: #fff; background: #72838b;
}

Important Constraints

  • The alt="true" fallback defect above is a genuine, confirmed accessibility bug — every image left without explicit alt text on this component ships a nonsensical alt value rather than an accessible empty/decorative state.
  • Hover-zoom and drag-to-pan are implemented entirely by initializing a third-party jQuery zoom plugin against a plain <img> with no visible affordance in the markup itself that the image is interactive beyond a CSS cursor: pointer — no keyboard equivalent exists for either behavior (confirmed: no keydown handlers, no tabindex, no ARIA in this component's HTL/JS).
  • The "remotedam" vs. normal-DAM branching in the model is a hard string-match on the asset path (image.contains("/content/dam/remotedam")) rather than an explicit asset-type property — fragile if that federated DAM's mount path ever changes.
  • Height/width auto-backfill from metadata depends on a separate, easy-to-miss site-level configuration flag; a site that doesn't have it enabled silently loses the CLS-reduction behavior with no visible indicator in the dialog.

EDS Migration — PROPOSED, not yet implemented

No interactive-image directory exists yet under blocks/ in the target implementation repository. This is distinct from, but related to, the already-built hotspot block (which handles click-to-open positional hotspot points on an image) — interactive-image's four behaviors (hover-zoom, drag-to-pan, plain link, modal trigger) are a different interaction set and are proposed as their own net-new block per the design-analysis reference material, rather than folded into hotspot.

Proposed authoring model (illustrative):

Row 0: Image (image reference + alt text + title)
Row 1: Behavior (select: hover-zoom / drag-pan / link / modal)
Row 2: Link target / Modal reference (used only for link/modal behaviors)
Row 3: Icon (optional reference or icon-font class) + Icon Position (select)

Proposed _interactive-image.json (illustrative):

// PROPOSED — not yet implemented
{
  "id": "interactive-image",
  "fields": [
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Image", "name": "image" },
    { "component": "reference", "name": "image", "label": "Image" },
    { "component": "text", "name": "imageAlt", "label": "Alt Text" },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Behavior", "name": "behavior" },
    { "component": "select", "name": "classes_behavior", "label": "Behavior",
      "options": [
        { "name": "Hover Zoom", "value": "interactive-image-hover" },
        { "name": "Drag to Pan", "value": "interactive-image-drag" },
        { "name": "Link", "value": "interactive-image-link" },
        { "name": "Modal", "value": "interactive-image-modal" }
      ]
    },
    { "component": "aem-content", "name": "href", "label": "Link / Modal Target",
      "description": "Used only when Behavior is Link or Modal." },
    { "component": "tab", "label": "Settings", "name": "settings" },
    { "component": "boolean", "name": "lazyLoad", "value": "true", "label": "Lazy Load" },
    { "...": "../../models/_common-properties.json#/common-prop" }
  ]
}

Proposed interactive-image.js (illustrative — addresses the two confirmed source defects: the alt="true" fallback and the lack of any keyboard equivalent):

// PROPOSED — not yet implemented
export default function decorate(block) {
  const img = block.querySelector('img');
  // Fix vs AEM: never emit the literal string "true" as alt text
  if (img && (!img.alt || img.alt === 'true')) img.alt = '';

  const behavior = [...block.classList].find((c) => c.startsWith('interactive-image-'))
    || 'interactive-image-link';

  if (behavior === 'interactive-image-hover' || behavior === 'interactive-image-drag') {
    // Fix vs AEM: give hover/drag zoom a real keyboard-operable equivalent
    block.setAttribute('role', 'button');
    block.setAttribute('tabindex', '0');
    block.setAttribute('aria-label', 'View larger image');
    block.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
      if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') {
        e.preventDefault();
        block.classList.toggle('is-zoomed');
      }
    });
  }
  // link / modal behaviors delegate to the existing modal/cta wiring patterns
  // already used by other blocks, rather than a bespoke implementation here.
}
