Proposed CSS approach (illustrative): retain the corner-icon overlay positioning classes from the source (top-right/top-left/bottom-right/bottom-left) as a direct, faithful port since that part of the source design has no identified issues, but size/contrast the overlay icon against the target implementation's token system rather than the source's hard-coded #72838b background.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldMaps to EDS field (proposed)Mapping typeNotes
image/file, imageAltText, imageTitleimage, imageAlt (proposed)RenamedSame purpose; proposed design drops a separate title field, relying on alt text alone, and explicitly fixes the source's alt="true" fallback defect rather than porting it
imageheight / imagewidth—Not mapped (proposed)No proposed authorable size override fields; target implementation convention is responsive images sized by CSS/container, not author-entered pixel dimensions — the source's metadata-driven auto-backfill logic has no direct equivalent to migrate since there is no manual override to backfill for
imageBehaviourclasses_behavior (proposed)RenamedSame four behaviors carried forward, exposed as one class-driven select rather than a show/hide-triggering dialog select
icon / iconPosition—Not mapped (proposed initial scope)Deferred from the initial proposed field set for simplicity; could be reinstated as a follow-up if the decorative-icon-overlay pattern is confirmed still needed post-migration
lazyLoadinglazyLoad (proposed)RenamedSame purpose; proposed as a boolean field type rather than a checkbox with a separate uncheckedValue, matching the target implementation's field-type conventions
pagePath (link target)href (proposed)RenamedSame purpose (link/modal behaviors' destination); proposed as a single aem-content field rather than a Granite pathbrowser
modalIDhref (proposed, same field, modal case)RenamedProposed design reuses one target field for both link and modal destinations, distinguished by the classes_behavior value, rather than two separate source fields (pagePath vs modalID)
targetType / linkType—Not mapped (proposed)Deferred from initial proposed scope; the target implementation's existing shared CTA/modal-trigger conventions (used by other already-built blocks) would likely supply equivalent behavior without a dedicated field here — to be confirmed if/when this block moves from proposal to build
anchorIDblockId (common-properties include, proposed)RenamedSame purpose, standardized field
customCssClassesclasses_commonCustomClass (proposed)RenamedSame purpose, standardized field
interactionId, componentTitle, sectionTitle, l10Title, componentPosition, mva, mvaName, mvaValue, mvaTier—Not mapped (proposed)Source-side digital-data analytics bean pipeline for the link/modal click interaction has no proposed equivalent field group in this initial design

Activity Tracker Component — Technical Details

What it does

Renders a horizontal strip of "activity" tiles (e.g. steps in a multi-part Adaptive Form journey) next to a brand logo/title tile. Each activity's completion state is tracked client-side in a cookie (admp-activity-<name>) and rendered as one of three author-defined status labels (Not Started / In Progress / Complete). The component has two rendering modes controlled by the enableLandingPage checkbox: a compact "status strip" mode (used inline, e.g. above an embedded Adaptive Form) and an expanded "landing page" mode that also renders start/complete marketing copy and a CTA per activity.

Architecture

Author configures N activities (multifield: name/title/start-complete copy/CTA/analytics)
         ↓
ActivityTrackerModel.java (@PostConstruct init()) parses each multifield row's JSON blob,
maps it into an ActivityBean, computes per-row analytics digitalData,
and — only in author runmode — writes a generated interactionId back into the JCR (side effect on GET)
         ↓
activity-tracker.html renders:
  <div class="abbv-activity-tracker landing-page?" data-status-labels='{"text":[...]}'>
    <div class="abbv-activity-item activity-banner">  ← logo/title tile
    <div class="abbv-activity-item activity-box" data-activity-name="..." aria-live="polite"> × N
         ↓
ui/activity-tracker.js (jQuery, loaded via clientlib category activityTracker-component.v1, defer):
  reads cookie state per activity (admp-activity-<name>) → NEW/START/DONE
  toggles CSS classes + status text; in landing-page mode also toggles
  start/complete copy blocks and CTA relabeling
  listens for bridgeInitializeStart (AEM Forms guideBridge) to reveal
  the inline status text only once the adjacent Adaptive Form iframe/bridge is ready

Sling Model — ActivityTrackerModel.java

Extends the shared AbstractComponentModel (provides getId()/interaction-id helpers). Notable behavior: it hand-parses each multifield row as a raw JSON string (Gson) rather than using typed child resources, and it writes back to the resource (a repository mutation triggered from a read path):

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class ActivityTrackerModel extends AbstractComponentModel {

    @Inject @Optional
    private List<String> activities;       // one JSON string per multifield row

    @Inject @Optional @Named("image/fileReference")
    private String imagefileReference;

    private List<ActivityBean> activityList;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        if (activities != null && !activities.isEmpty()) {
            setActivities();              // parse JSON rows into ActivityBean
        }
        if (imagefileReference != null && imagefileReference.contains("/content/dam/remotedam")) {
            imagefileReference = CommonUtility.getImagePathBasedonEDAMRendition("large", imagefileReference, resourceResolver);
        }
        imagePath = CommonUtility.getImageUrl(imagefileReference, resourceResolver, resource);
    }

    private void setInteractionIdForActivity() {
        // ...only when isAuthorRunMode()...
        modifiedActivityLinks(updatedActivityList);   // ModifiableValueMap.put + resourceResolver.commit()
    }
}

The per-activity payload is modeled by a plain bean, ActivityBean (17 fields: activityName, activityTitle, startText, completeText, ctaStartTitle, ctaCompleteTitle, activityUrl, interactionId, analyticsTitle, componentTitle, l10title, mva, mvaValue, mvaTitle, mvaTier, digitalData).

Dialog

Two tabs: Overview (component-level fields) and Activities (a granite/ui/.../form/multifield of fieldsets, one per tracked activity, each carrying its own analytics/MVA block).

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor IDanchorIDtextAlso doubles as the cookie-name namespace per the field description
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestext
Title Imageimage/file (+ fileReference/fileName)fileuploadBrand logo tile
Image Height / Widthheight / widthnumberfieldCLS mitigation
TitletitletextBrand name shown under logo
Enable landing page layoutenableLandingPagecheckboxSwitches to the expanded marketing-copy mode
Not Started labelnotStartedtextDefaults to "Not Started" (hardcoded English fallback in HTL)
In Progress labelinProgresstextDefaults to "In Progress"
Complete labelcompletetextDefaults to "Complete"
Per-activity (multifield activities, each row a fieldset):
Activity NameactivityNametextMust match the Form author dialog's activity identifier — cross-component coupling, not validated
Activity TitleactivityTitletext
Landing Page Start TextstartTexttextarea
Landing Page Complete TextcompleteTexttextarea
Start Activity CTA labelctaStartTitletext
Restart Activity CTA labelctaCompleteTitletext
Activity CTA Link URLactivityUrlpathbrowser
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtext (disabled)System-generated, written back on author-side render
Analytics TitleanalyticsTitletext
Section Title for AnalyticssectionTitletext
Localized Section Titlel10sectionTitletextNon-English sites only
Most Valued ActionmvaPrimarycheckbox
MVA TitlemvaNametext
MVA Type/CategorymvaValueselect (datasource-backed)Datasource: /bin/abbvie/analyticsdatasource
MVA SecondarymvaSecondarycheckbox

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<div id="${properties.anchorID}" class="abbv-activity-tracker ${properties.customCssClasses} ${properties.enableLandingPage ? 'landing-page' : ''}"
     data-status-labels='{"text": ["${properties.notStarted || "Not Started"}","${properties.inProgress || "In Progress"}", "${properties.complete || "Complete"}"]}'>
  <div class="abbv-activity-items">
    <div class="abbv-activity-item activity-banner act-item_0">
      <img src="${activityTracker.imagePath}" height="${properties.height}" width="${properties.width}"
           title="Brand Logo" alt="Brand Logo" />   <!-- hardcoded alt text, not authorable -->
      <div class="abbv-activity-brandname">${properties.title}</div>
    </div>
    <sly data-sly-list.activity="${activityTracker.activityList}">
      <div class="abbv-activity-item activity-box act-item_${activityList.count}"
           data-activity-name="${activity.activityName}" aria-atomic="true" aria-live="polite">
        <span class="activity-title">${activity.activityTitle}</span><span class="activity-status"></span>
        <div class="landing-activity-start">${activity.startText}</div>
        <div class="landing-activity-done">${activity.completeText}</div>
        <a class="landing-activity-cta abbv-button-primary" data-redo="${activity.ctaCompleteTitle}"
           data-digitaldata='${activity.digitalData @context="unsafe"}' href="${activity.activityUrl}">
          ${activity.ctaStartTitle}
        </a>
      </div>
    </sly>
  </div>
</div>

Note the @context="unsafe" on data-digitaldata — the server-built analytics JSON string is injected without HTL's normal escaping.

CSS

The source CSS is explicitly placeholder/debug styling (bright yellow background, red 3px border on the row, fixed 720px width) — this is scaffold styling never finished for production skins:

.abbv-activity-tracker .abbv-activity-items {
    background: lightyellow;
    border: solid 3px red;
    display: flex;
    width: 720px;
}
.abbv-activity-item.not-started .activity-title::before { font-family: 'abbv-iconFont', sans-serif; content:"\e8fd"; }
.abbv-activity-item.in-progress .activity-title::before { font-family: 'abbv-iconFont', sans-serif; content:"\e3dc"; }
.abbv-activity-item.complete .activity-title::before { font-family: 'abbv-iconFont', sans-serif; content:"\e86c"; }

Important Constraints

  • Fixed 720px track width, non-responsive; landing-page mode fixes tile height to 300px regardless of content length.
  • Status is entirely client-side/cookie-based (1 year expiry) — there is no server-side/account-level persistence of activity progress; clearing cookies resets progress.
  • ActivityTrackerModel.init() performs a repository write (resourceResolver.commit()) as a side effect of a component render in author mode — a read-triggered mutation, and a bug/perf risk if triggered repeatedly (e.g. by an aggressive replication agent hitting the render pipeline).
  • Status labels have hardcoded English fallback strings baked directly into the HTL ("Not Started", "In Progress", "Complete") rather than resource-bundle-driven i18n.
  • Coupling by convention only: activityName must match an identifier defined in a separate Form component's dialog; nothing enforces this at authoring time.

EDS Migration — PROPOSED, not yet implemented

No blocks/activity-tracker/ exists in the target repository yet. Per the EDS block inventory, this is registered as net-new: "LMS progress tracker … dashboard/manage-page … activities list, cookie state … aria-live status, non-cacheable." The design below is illustrative only.

// PROPOSED — blocks/activity-tracker/activity-tracker.js (not yet implemented)
const COOKIE_PREFIX = 'admp-activity-';
const STATUS = { NEW: '0', START: '1', DONE: '2' };

export default function decorate(block) {
  const rows = [...block.children];
  const [bannerRow, ...activityRows] = rows;

  const banner = buildBanner(bannerRow); // logo image, alt text (authored, not hardcoded), brand name
  const isLandingPage = block.classList.contains('landing-page');

  const items = activityRows.map((row) => buildActivityItem(row, isLandingPage));
  const track = document.createElement('div');
  track.className = 'activity-tracker-items';
  track.append(banner, ...items);
  block.replaceChildren(track);

  applyStoredStatus(block, items, isLandingPage);
}

function applyStoredStatus(block, items, isLandingPage) {
  items.forEach((item) => {
    const name = item.dataset.activityName;
    const status = getCookie(`${COOKIE_PREFIX}${name}`) || STATUS.NEW;
    renderStatus(item, status, isLandingPage);
    item.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite'); // status region, ported from AEM
  });
}

// non-cacheable: block reads document.cookie at decorate time, so this block
// must be excluded from any full-page HTML cache / must render client-side only.
/* PROPOSED — blocks/activity-tracker/activity-tracker.css (not yet implemented) */
.activity-tracker-items {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: var(--spacing-s, 12px);
}
.activity-tracker-item {
  border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #ccc);
  border-radius: 4px;
  padding: var(--spacing-s, 12px);
  text-align: center;
}
.activity-tracker-item[data-status="complete"] .activity-tracker-status::before { content: "\2713"; }
{
  "PROPOSED — not yet implemented": true,
  "definitions": [{
    "title": "Activity Tracker",
    "id": "activity-tracker",
    "plugins": { "xwalk": { "page": { "resourceType": "core/franklin/components/block/v1/block",
      "template": { "name": "Activity Tracker", "model": "activity-tracker" } } } }
  }],
  "models": [{
    "id": "activity-tracker",
    "fields": [
      { "component": "reference", "name": "logoImage", "label": "Title Image" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "title", "label": "Title" },
      { "component": "boolean", "name": "enableLandingPage", "label": "Enable landing page layout" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "notStartedLabel", "label": "Not Started label" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "inProgressLabel", "label": "In Progress label" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "completeLabel", "label": "Complete label" }
    ]
  }, {
    "id": "activity-tracker-item",
    "fields": [
      { "component": "text", "name": "activityName", "label": "Activity Name (must match linked form field id)" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "activityTitle", "label": "Activity Title" },
      { "component": "richtext", "name": "startText", "label": "Landing Page Start Text" },
      { "component": "richtext", "name": "completeText", "label": "Landing Page Complete Text" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "ctaStartLabel", "label": "Start Activity CTA label" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "ctaCompleteLabel", "label": "Restart Activity CTA label" },
      { "component": "aem-content", "name": "activityUrl", "label": "Activity CTA Link URL" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "analyticsInteractionId", "label": "Analytics Interaction ID (reused shared field)" }
    ]
  }]
}

Authoring in EDS: banner row + N activity rows (positional, matching the existing "row indices must match _X.json field order" convention used across the target repo). Per-row MVA/analytics fields would reuse the shared applyCommonProps utility (analyticsInteractionId) rather than re-implementing bespoke MVA type/category/tier fields per row, since those are project-wide analytics conventions, not activity-tracker-specific. The author-time JCR-write side effect in ActivityTrackerModel has no EDS equivalent and would not be ported — interaction IDs would be authored directly or generated at build time, not written back by a render-time model.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtextid (block-level, shared convention)RenamedStandard EDS block id/anchor convention
customCssClassestextclasses (shared classes_commonCustomClass)RenamedReuses project-wide common-props utility
image/file, image/fileReferencefileuploadlogoImageRenamedreference component type replaces AEM asset fileupload+DAM path
height / widthnumberfield—Not mappedEDS <picture> responsive images infer intrinsic size; explicit author-set height/width not needed
titletexttitleSame name
enableLandingPagecheckboxenableLandingPageSame name
notStartedtextnotStartedLabelRenamedRequired (no silent hardcoded English fallback) — author must supply, or field-level i18n default is used, not an inline HTL literal
inProgresstextinProgressLabelRenamedSame reasoning
completetextcompleteLabelRenamedSame reasoning
activityNametext (per-row)activityNameSame nameStill a manual cross-component convention; no structural link to the form field
activityTitletextactivityTitleSame name
startText / completeTexttextareastartText / completeTextSame namePromoted from plain textarea to richtext
ctaStartTitle / ctaCompleteTitletextctaStartLabel / ctaCompleteLabelRenamed
activityUrlpathbrowseractivityUrlSame nameaem-content component type
interactionIdtext (disabled/system)analyticsInteractionIdRenamedReused from shared common-props, no render-time JCR write-back
analyticsTitle, sectionTitle, l10sectionTitletext—Not mappedProject-specific Adobe Analytics digitalData tagging model; superseded by whatever analytics layer is standardized for the target implementation, not carried 1:1
mvaPrimary, mvaName, mvaValue, mvaSecondarycheckbox/text/select—Not mappedSame reason — MVA tagging taxonomy is an analytics-platform concern, not modeled per-block in the target implementation

Audio Player Component — Technical Details

What it does

Embeds an audio file (mp3/wav/ogg from the DAM) in one of three presentation modes: Native (bare <audio controls>, browser chrome), Custom (unstyled <audio> plus a hand-built play/pause/mute/replay/volume control bar), or Simple (a single button that starts playback, no pause). Tracks play/pause/end and 25/50/75/90% milestone events into Adobe Analytics via `_satellite.track`.

Architecture

Author sets playerType (native|custom|simple) + audioPath (DAM path) + simpleButtonLabel
         ↓
AudioPlayerModel.java resolves absolute audio URL (forces https + request server name),
derives file extension for the <source type="audio/...">, builds digitalData JSON
         ↓
audio-player.html renders one of three <audio> markup variants based on playerType
         ↓
ui/audio-player.js (jQuery) wires native <audio> element events (play/pause/timeupdate/ended)
to both the custom UI buttons/progress bar AND to Adobe Analytics milestone tracking;
enforces "only one audio playing at a time" across all players on the page

Sling Model — AudioPlayerModel.java

@Model(adaptables = {Resource.class, SlingHttpServletRequest.class},
       resourceType = "common-elements/v1/components/content/audio-player",
       defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class AudioPlayerModel extends AbstractComponentModel {

    @Inject @Source("sling-object")
    private SlingHttpServletRequest request;

    @Inject @Via("resource") private String playerType;
    @Inject @Via("resource") private String simpleButtonLabel;
    @Inject @Via("resource") private String audioPath;

    public String getAudioPath() {
        // returning FQN to try and fix play problems on IOS
        return "https://" + request.getServerName() + audioPath;
    }

    public String getFileType() {
        if (audioPath != null && !audioPath.isEmpty()) {
            int lastDotIndex = audioPath.lastIndexOf('.');
            if (lastDotIndex > 0 && lastDotIndex < audioPath.length() - 1) {
                return audioPath.substring(lastDotIndex + 1);
            }
        }
        return "";
    }

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        interactionId = getInteractionIdFromDialog();
        if (!isPublish() && (StringUtils.isBlank(interactionId) || !interactionId.equals(getId()))) {
            setInteractionIdInDialog();   // writes to JCR on author-side render
        }
        analyticsString = getDigitalData();
    }
}

Note: getAudioPath() hardcodes the https:// scheme and concatenates request.getServerName() directly — this breaks on any environment where the public hostname differs from the request's Host header (e.g. behind certain CDN/dispatcher configurations), and hardcodes protocol rather than respecting the actual scheme used.

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor IDanchorIDtext
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestext
Player TypeplayerTypeselectnative / custom / simple
Simple Player Button TextsimpleButtonLabeltextOnly used in "simple" mode; defaults to "Play"; rendered with @context='html' (raw HTML)
Audio file pathaudioPathpathbrowser (rootPath /content/dam)
Analytics Interaction IDinteractionIdtext (disabled)System-generated
Audio Name for AnalyticsanalyticsTitletext
Section Title for AnalyticssectionTitletext
Localized Section Titlel10sectionTitletextNon-English sites only
Most Valued Action (MVA)mvaEnablecheckboxShow/hide-linked to MVA fields
MVA TitlemvaNametext
MVA Type/CategorymvaValueselect (datasource)
MVA SecondarymvaTiercheckbox

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<div ... class="abbv-audio-player ${audioPlayer.customCssClasses}" data-digitaldata='${audioPlayer.analyticsString @context="unsafe"}'>
  <sly data-sly-test="${audioPlayer.playerType == 'native'}">
    <audio controls><source src="${audioPlayer.audioPath}" type="audio/${audioPlayer.fileType}"></audio>
  </sly>
  <sly data-sly-test="${audioPlayer.playerType == 'custom'}">
    <audio><source src="${audioPlayer.audioPath}" type="audio/${audioPlayer.fileType}"></audio>
    <div class="audio-controls">
      <progress class="audio-progress-bar" min="0" max="100" value="0">0% played</progress>
      <button class="audio-play abbv-icon-play_arrow i-b" aria-label="Play"></button>
      <button class="audio-pause abbv-icon-pause i-b" aria-label="Pause"></button>
      <button class="audio-mute abbv-icon-volume_up i-b" aria-label="Mute"></button>
      <button class="audio-unmute abbv-icon-volume_off i-b" aria-label="Unmute"></button>
      <button class="audio-replay abbv-icon-replay i-b" aria-label="Replay"></button>
      <input type="range" class="audio-volume-bar" min="0" max="10" step="1" value="10" aria-label="Adjust Volume">
    </div>
  </sly>
  <sly data-sly-test="${audioPlayer.playerType == 'simple'}">
    <audio><source src="${audioPlayer.audioPath}" type="audio/${audioPlayer.fileType}"></audio>
    <div class="audio-controls_simple">
      <button class="audio-play abbv-icon-volume_up i-b" aria-label="Play">
        ${audioPlayer.simpleButtonLabel @context='html'}
      </button>
    </div>
  </sly>
</div>

The simple-mode button label is rendered with @context='html' — author-entered text is injected as raw HTML rather than escaped text, an XSS-relevant authoring surface.

JS behavior (ui/audio-player.js)

Tracks milestone events (25/50/75/90% played) and play/pause/end into Adobe Analytics; enforces single-playing-instance across the page via pauseOtherAudio():

let pauseOtherAudio = (player) => {
    $('.abbv-audio-player audio').each(function(_i, p) {
        if (!(p.paused || p.ended) && p !== player) {
            p.pause();
            // ...toggle that other player's UI back to "play" state...
            return false;
        }
    });
};

Important Constraints

  • Autoplay explicitly not supported (per the dialog's own description text).
  • getAudioPath() hardcodes https:// + request.getServerName() — brittle behind proxies/CDNs that terminate TLS differently or rewrite Host.
  • Simple-mode button label uses insecure raw-HTML rendering (@context='html') for an author-entered plain-text field.
  • "Only one audio plays at a time" is enforced entirely client-side by iterating all <audio> elements on the page — no coordination with the equivalent logic in the video player component (noted directly in a code comment).
  • progress-bar based custom player relies on native <progress> semantics but the volume/seek controls are mouse/click-driven (e.offsetX based seek) without an equivalent keyboard interaction path.

EDS Migration — PROPOSED, not yet implemented

No blocks/audio-player/ exists yet. Per the inventory: "Audio embed … native/custom/simple … audioSrc, playerType … native controls fallback." Proposed design below is illustrative only.

// PROPOSED — blocks/audio-player/audio-player.js (not yet implemented)
export default function decorate(block) {
  const rows = [...block.children];
  const [srcRow, typeRow, labelRow] = rows;

  const audioSrc = srcRow.querySelector('a')?.href || srcRow.textContent.trim();
  const playerType = (typeRow?.textContent || 'native').trim().toLowerCase();
  const buttonLabel = labelRow?.textContent.trim() || 'Play';

  block.textContent = '';

  if (playerType === 'native') {
    const audio = document.createElement('audio');
    audio.controls = true;
    audio.src = audioSrc;
    block.append(audio);
    return;
  }

  // custom + simple share a real <audio> element with native fallback controls
  // removed only once custom JS confirms it initialized successfully (fix vs AEM:
  // AEM's custom/simple modes never fall back if JS fails to load)
  const audio = document.createElement('audio');
  audio.src = audioSrc;
  audio.setAttribute('data-player-type', playerType);
  audio.controls = true; // native fallback stays until enhanced
  block.append(audio);

  import('./audio-player-controls.js').then(({ enhance }) => enhance(block, audio, { playerType, buttonLabel }));
}
/* PROPOSED — blocks/audio-player/audio-player.css (not yet implemented) */
.audio-player audio { width: 100%; }
.audio-player .audio-player-controls { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--spacing-xs, 8px); }
.audio-player .audio-player-progress { flex: 1; }
{
  "PROPOSED — not yet implemented": true,
  "definitions": [{ "title": "Audio Player", "id": "audio-player" }],
  "models": [{
    "id": "audio-player",
    "fields": [
      { "component": "aem-content", "name": "audioSrc", "label": "Audio file path" },
      { "component": "select", "name": "playerType", "label": "Player Type",
        "options": [{"name":"Native","value":"native"},{"name":"Custom","value":"custom"},{"name":"Simple","value":"simple"}] },
      { "component": "text", "name": "buttonLabel", "label": "Simple Player Button Text" }
    ]
  }]
}

The proposed design always keeps the native <audio controls> element in the DOM with real controls attribute and only progressively removes/hides it once custom JS confirms initialization, unlike AEM's custom/simple modes which render a bare, control-less <audio> with no fallback if the clientlib fails to load. The simple-mode button label would be authored as plain text and rendered as text content, not raw HTML.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtextid (shared)Renamed
customCssClassestextclasses (shared)Renamed
playerTypeselectplayerTypeSame name
simpleButtonLabeltextbuttonLabelRenamedRendered as escaped text, not raw HTML — dropping the @context='html' XSS surface
audioPathpathbrowseraudioSrcRenamedaem-content reference; resolved URL no longer manually reconstructed with a hardcoded https:// + server-name string — uses the actual resolved asset URL
interactionIdtext (system)analyticsInteractionId (shared)Renamed
analyticsTitle, sectionTitle, l10sectionTitletext—Not mappedDigital-data tagging-spec fields specific to the source analytics implementation; not modeled per-block in the target implementation
mvaEnable, mvaName, mvaValue, mvaTiercheckbox/text/select—Not mappedSame reason — MVA taxonomy is an analytics-platform concern

Badge Component — Technical Details

What it does

Renders a grid of LMS ("Learning Management System") certification badges the current user has earned or is progressing toward — badge image, name, achieved/expiry date, a "Completed" vs. "In Progress" status pill, and a link to download a certificate PDF. Unlike most components in this codebase, the badge data is not authored in AEM at all: it is fetched live, server-side, on every render, by chaining together five separate external LMS REST API calls.

Architecture

Request renders badge.html
         ↓
AbbvieUserBadgeModel.java (@PostConstruct getBadgeDetails()):
  1. read ALM session token from the "alm_cp_token" cookie
  2. resolve the Learning API base URL + per-endpoint paths from an admin config page
     (/content/admin/admp-global/learning-api, via AbbvieLearningUtil.getLearningApiPage)
  3. AbbvieLearnerUserService.getLearnerUser()        → resolve current userId
  4. AbbvieLearnerUserBadgeService.getUserBadges()     → list of {userBadgeId, badgeId, courseId, dateAchieved, expiryDate}
  5. per badge:
       AbbvieLearnerBadgeService.getBadgeDetails()          → badge name + image URL
       AbbvieLearnerLOService.getLearningObjectDetails()    → course/learning-object name
       AbbvieLearnerGenerateBadgeJobService.getJobId()      → kicks off a PDF-generation job
       AbbvieLearnerGenerateBadgeJobService.getS3PDFUrlFromJob() → polls job for the resulting S3 PDF URL
       AbbvieLearnerGenerateBadgeJobService.getBadgeStatus()     → job/badge completion status
         ↓
  Assembles List<BadgeBean>, capped at maxNumberOfBadges when componentDisplayed (dashboard mode)
         ↓
badge.html data-sly-list's over badgeModel.badgeBeanList

All five services (AbbvieLearnerUserService, AbbvieLearnerUserBadgeService, AbbvieLearnerBadgeService, AbbvieLearnerLOService, AbbvieLearnerGenerateBadgeJobService) are thin OSGi @Component wrappers that delegate the actual HTTP call to a shared AbbvieLearnerAPIService (adds the Authorization: Bearer <almtoken> header and performs the GET/POST via a configured CloseableHttpClient). This is a considerably richer backing service layer than most components in this codebase — a full chained-microservice request pipeline invoked synchronously inside a single page render.

Sling Model — AbbvieUserBadgeModel.java (package: com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.learning.models)

@Model(adaptables = { SlingHttpServletRequest.class, Resource.class },
       defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class AbbvieUserBadgeModel {

    @Inject @Source("osgi-services") private AbbvieLearnerUserService abbvieLearnerUserService;
    @Inject @Source("osgi-services") private AbbvieLearnerUserBadgeService abbvieLearnerUserBadgeService;
    @Inject @Source("osgi-services") private AbbvieLearnerBadgeService abbvieLearnerBadgeService;
    @Inject @Source("osgi-services") private AbbvieLearnerLOService abbvieLearnerLOService;
    @Inject @Source("osgi-services") private AbbvieLearnerGenerateBadgeJobService abbvieLearnerGenerateBadgeJobService;

    @ValueMapValue private String maxNumberOfBadges;
    @ValueMapValue private String componentDisplayed;

    private List<BadgeBean> badgeBeanList;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() { getBadgeDetails(); }

    protected void getBadgeDetails() {
        String cpAlmTokenCookie = FormsConfigUtil.getCookie(request, AbbvieLearnerAPIConstants.ALM_CP_TOKEN);
        // ... chained API calls per badge, see architecture diagram above ...
        for (JsonNode jsonNode : userBadgeResponseArraNode) {
            BadgeBean badgeBean = new BadgeBean();
            // ids, dates (reformatted via ZonedDateTime -> "MMMM dd, yyyy"), badge name/image,
            // course name, S3 PDF url, badge status
            if (isComponentDisplayed() && Integer.valueOf(maxNumberOfBadges) == i) {
                badgeBeanList.add(badgeBean);
                break;
            } else {
                badgeBeanList.add(badgeBean);
            }
        }
    }
}

Supporting beans/services found under core/beans and learning/* (broader than a typical component's backing code, as expected):

  • BadgeBean (com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.learning.beans) — the per-badge render bean: userBadgeId, badgeId, dateAchieved, expiryDate, badgeName, badgeImgUrl, courseName, s3PdfUrl, badgeStatus.
  • BadgeItem (com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.beans) — a simpler immutable {label, imagePath} pair; not used by the Badge component itself but by LearnerLeaderboardModel (see clms-leaderboard section) to render level badges, confirming a shared badge-image concept across both components.
  • AbbvieLearnerAPIService — the shared low-level HTTP client wrapper (processGetRequest/processPostRequest, request-timeout-aware CloseableHttpClient).
  • AbbvieLearnerGenerateBadgeJobService/Impl — models the async "generate certificate PDF" job lifecycle (submit job → poll for S3 URL → poll for status), i.e. badge PDF certificates are generated on demand by an external job queue, not pre-rendered assets.
  • AbbvieLearnerAPIConstants — centralizes the admin-config property names used to look up per-environment API base URL and path templates (learning-api-url, user-api-path, userbadges-api-path, badgesid-api-path, learningobjectid-api-path, jobs-api-path, jobsid-api-path), and the token cookie name alm_cp_token.

Dialog

Single tab — deliberately minimal because all badge content is fetched dynamically, not authored:

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor IDanchorIDtext
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestext
Section NamesectionNametextHeading text above the grid
'See All/Manage' button textseeAllManageButtontexttext
'See All/Manage' CTA urlseeAllManageCTAtext
Certificate Status labelcertificateStatusLabeltext
PDF Download button textpdfDownloadtext
Component is displayed on Dashboard pagecomponentDisplayedcheckboxToggles the compact "dashboard" mode vs. the full "See All/Manage" page mode
Maximum number of badges to displaymaxNumberOfBadgesnumberfield (1–99)Only applies in dashboard mode

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-use.badgeModel="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.learning.models.AbbvieUserBadgeModel"></sly>
<div id="${properties.anchorID}" class="badge-component ${properties.customCssClasses}">
  <div class="abbv-badge badges-row">
    <div class="abbv-certificate-header">
      <h3 class="header-certificate">
        ${properties.sectionName}
        <sly data-sly-test="${properties.componentDisplayed ? properties.seeAllManageCTA : ''}">
          <button class="certificate-seeall">
            <a href="${properties.seeAllManageCTA}">${properties.seeAllManageButtontext}</a>
          </button>
        </sly>
      </h3>
    </div>
    <div class="abbv-badge-grid">
      <sly data-sly-list.badgeList="${badgeModel.badgeBeanList}">
        <div class="abbv-badge-col">
          <div class="abbv-badge-wrap">
            <div class="abbv-top-content-badge">
              <img src=${badgeList.badgeImgUrl} alt="badge-image">   <!-- generic hardcoded alt text -->
              <h4>${badgeList.badgeName}</h4>
              <p>
                <sly data-sly-test="${badgeList.dateAchieved}">
                  ${badgeList.badgeStatus} ${badgeList.courseName} on ${badgeList.dateAchieved}
                  <sly data-sly-test="${badgeList.expiryDate}"> Badge valid until ${badgeList.expiryDate}</sly>
                </sly>
                <sly data-sly-test="${badgeList.dateAchieved == ''}"> Complete ${badgeList.courseName} to achieve this badge</sly>
              </p>
            </div>
            <div class="abbv-bottom-content-badge">
              <span class="abbv-badge-status-value">${badgeList.badgeStatus == 'Completed' ? 'Achieved': 'In Progress'}</span>
              <a class="abbv-icon-keyboard_tab i-b" href="${badgeList.s3PdfUrl}" aria-label="Open PDF with certificate info">${properties.pdfDownload}</a>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
      </sly>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

CSS

.abbv-badge-grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(1, minmax(0, 1fr));
    gap: 20px;
}
@media (min-width: 601px) { .abbv-badge-grid { grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr)); } }
@media (min-width: 986px) { .abbv-badge-grid { grid-template-columns: repeat(3, minmax(0, 1fr)); } }
.abbv-badge .abbv-badge-col { box-sizing: border-box; border: 1px solid #000; border-radius: 5px; }
.abbv-badge .pdf-inprogress { color: #777; pointer-events: none; }

Important Constraints

  • Badge alt text is a hardcoded literal, "badge-image", for every badge regardless of badge name — not descriptive, and identical to the AEM gap the target inventory explicitly calls out as needing a fix ("descriptive alt text (fix vs AEM generic alt)").
  • Every render performs up to 5 × N synchronous, chained external HTTP calls (N = number of badges) with no visible caching layer in the model itself — a real performance and LMS-API-rate-limit risk, and a render-time hard dependency on LMS/S3 availability.
  • PDF certificate URLs are generated by kicking off an async job (getJobId → getS3PDFUrlFromJob) synchronously inline in the render path — if the job hasn't completed yet, behavior depends on what the polling call returns (not guaranteed to be ready).
  • Session/auth is entirely cookie-based (alm_cp_token) with no model-level handling of an absent/expired token beyond an empty result list.

EDS Migration — PROPOSED, not yet implemented

No blocks/badge/ exists yet. Per the inventory: "LMS certification badges … dashboard/manage … badge data (dynamic) … LMS/S3 backend … descriptive alt text (fix vs AEM generic alt)." Because badge data is inherently dynamic/session-scoped (not author-managed content), the proposed EDS design keeps only presentation configuration in the block's authored fields and fetches badge data client-side against a backing API — there is no server-side rendering equivalent available in EDS's static-HTML publish model, so this necessarily becomes a client-fetch pattern.

// PROPOSED — blocks/badge/badge.js (not yet implemented)
export default async function decorate(block) {
  const rows = [...block.children];
  const [sectionNameRow, seeAllRow, statusLabelRow, pdfLabelRow, modeRow, maxRow] = rows;

  const config = {
    sectionName: sectionNameRow?.textContent.trim(),
    seeAllLink: seeAllRow?.querySelector('a'),
    certificateStatusLabel: statusLabelRow?.textContent.trim(),
    pdfDownloadLabel: pdfLabelRow?.textContent.trim(),
    dashboardMode: (modeRow?.textContent.trim().toLowerCase() === 'dashboard'),
    maxBadges: parseInt(maxRow?.textContent.trim(), 10) || null,
  };

  block.textContent = '';
  block.append(buildHeader(config));

  const grid = document.createElement('div');
  grid.className = 'badge-grid';
  block.append(grid);

  try {
    // client-side call to a backing badge API (behind auth); no server-rendered
    // equivalent exists in EDS's static-publish model
    const res = await fetch('/api/learner/badges', { credentials: 'include' });
    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`Badge API returned ${res.status}`);
    let badges = await res.json();
    if (config.dashboardMode && config.maxBadges) badges = badges.slice(0, config.maxBadges);
    badges.forEach((badge) => grid.append(buildBadgeCard(badge, config)));
  } catch (err) {
    grid.append(buildErrorState()); // AEM has no error UI for a failed chain; EDS proposal adds one
  }
}

function buildBadgeCard(badge, config) {
  const card = document.createElement('div');
  card.className = 'badge-card';
  card.innerHTML = `
    <img src="${badge.imageUrl}" alt="${badge.name} certification badge" />
    <h4>${badge.name}</h4>
    <p>${badge.status} ${badge.courseName}${badge.dateAchieved ? ` on ${badge.dateAchieved}` : ''}</p>
    <a href="${badge.pdfUrl}" aria-label="Open PDF with certificate info for ${badge.name}"
       class="${badge.status !== 'Completed' ? 'is-disabled' : ''}">${config.pdfDownloadLabel}</a>`;
  return card;
}
/* PROPOSED — blocks/badge/badge.css (not yet implemented) */
.badge-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(1, minmax(0, 1fr));
  gap: var(--spacing-m, 20px);
}
@media (width >= 600px) { .badge-grid { grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr)); } }
@media (width >= 980px) { .badge-grid { grid-template-columns: repeat(3, minmax(0, 1fr)); } }
.badge-card { border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #000); border-radius: 5px; }
.badge-card a.is-disabled { color: var(--color-disabled, #777); pointer-events: none; }
{
  "PROPOSED — not yet implemented": true,
  "definitions": [{ "title": "Badge", "id": "badge" }],
  "models": [{
    "id": "badge",
    "fields": [
      { "component": "text", "name": "sectionName", "label": "Section Name" },
      { "component": "aem-content", "name": "seeAllManageLink", "label": "'See All/Manage' CTA" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "seeAllManageLabel", "label": "'See All/Manage' button text" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "certificateStatusLabel", "label": "Certificate Status label" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "pdfDownloadLabel", "label": "PDF Download button text" },
      { "component": "select", "name": "displayMode", "label": "Display Mode",
        "options": [{"name":"Dashboard","value":"dashboard"},{"name":"Manage","value":"manage"}] },
      { "component": "number", "name": "maxBadges", "label": "Maximum number of badges (Dashboard only)", "min": 1, "max": 99 }
    ]
  }]
}

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtextid (shared)Renamed
customCssClassestextclasses (shared)Renamed
sectionNametextsectionNameSame name
seeAllManageButtontexttextseeAllManageLabelRenamed
seeAllManageCTAtextseeAllManageLinkRenamedPromoted from a free-text URL field to a structured aem-content reference
certificateStatusLabeltextcertificateStatusLabelSame name
pdfDownloadtextpdfDownloadLabelRenamed
componentDisplayedcheckboxdisplayModeRenamedBoolean flip flattened into an explicit two-value select (dashboard/manage) for clarity
maxNumberOfBadgesnumberfieldmaxBadgesSame purpose, renamed
(badge data itself: badgeId, dateAchieved, expiryDate, badgeName, badgeImgUrl, courseName, s3PdfUrl, badgeStatus)server-fetched, not authoredConsumed from client-side badge API responseNot an author field — architectural changeAEM fetches and assembles this server-side on every render via a 5-service chained call; EDS proposal fetches it client-side from a single backing API, since EDS pages are statically published and cannot re-run a per-request Java service chain
generic hardcoded alt="badge-image"HTL literalalt="${badge.name} certification badge"Not mapped as a field — behavior fixDropping the non-descriptive hardcoded alt text; EDS proposal derives real alt text from the fetched badge name

CLMS Leaderboard Component — Technical Details

What it does

Displays the current learner's gamification status on a single card: current level name + badge image, a progress bar toward the next level, points-to-next-level (or an "all levels achieved" state), and skills/badges earned counts, plus a link to a full team leaderboard page. "CLMS" = Commercial Learning Management System, matching the component's own AEM title ("Commercial LMS Leader Board").

Architecture

Author configures labels + a JSON map of gamification level names (gamificationLevelsJson)
+ one badge-image DAM path per level (beginner/bronze/silver/gold/platinum)
         ↓
LearnerLeaderboardModel.java (@PostConstruct init()):
  1. read ALM session token from "alm_cp_token" cookie; bail to an empty state if absent
  2. resolve admin config page (CUSTOM_LEARNING_API_CONFIG_PATH)
  3. AbbvieLearnerGamificationService.getLearnerLeaderboardSummary(token, resolver, configPage, levelsJson)
     → returns a LeaderboardSummary bean (levelName, currentPoints, pointsToNextLevel,
       progressPercent, skillsCount, badgesCount, allLevelAchieved, dataAvailable, message)
  4. match summary.levelName against the authored gamificationLevelsJson keys (case-insensitive)
     to find which authored badge-image field corresponds to the current level
         ↓
clms-leaderboard.html renders the card, or an empty-state message if !responseAvailable

Sling Model — LearnerLeaderboardModel.java

@Model(adaptables = SlingHttpServletRequest.class, defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class LearnerLeaderboardModel {

    @OSGiService private AbbvieLearnerGamificationService gamificationService;

    @ValueMapValue private String gamificationLevelsJson;
    @ValueMapValue private String beginnerBadgeImage;
    @ValueMapValue private String bronzeBadgeImage;
    @ValueMapValue private String silverBadgeImage;
    @ValueMapValue private String goldBadgeImage;
    @ValueMapValue private String platinumBadgeImage;

    private LeaderboardSummary summary;
    private List<BadgeItem> levelBadges = Collections.emptyList();

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        summary = new LeaderboardSummary();
        summary.setDataAvailable(false);
        String cpAlmTokenCookie = FormsConfigUtil.getCookie(request, AbbvieCustomMyLearningConstants.ALM_CP_TOKEN);
        if (StringUtils.isBlank(cpAlmTokenCookie)) { statusMessage = "No ALM token present."; return; }
        if (gamificationService == null) { statusMessage = "Leaderboard service is not available."; return; }

        Page pageAdminConfig = getPageAdminConfig();
        summary = gamificationService.getLearnerLeaderboardSummary(cpAlmTokenCookie, resourceResolver, pageAdminConfig, gamificationLevelsJson);
        if (StringUtils.isNotBlank(leaderboardPagePath)) summary.setLeaderboardUrl(leaderboardPagePath);
        levelBadges = buildLevelBadges();  // matches summary.levelName -> authored badge image by index
    }

    public String getTitle() { return StringUtils.defaultIfBlank(title, "My Level"); }
    public String getSkillsLabel() { return StringUtils.defaultIfBlank(skillsLabel, "Skills"); }
    public String getBadgesLabel() { return StringUtils.defaultIfBlank(badgesLabel, "Badges"); }
}

The gamification integration itself is delegated to a dedicated service, AbbvieLearnerGamificationService (getLearnerLeaderboardSummary(almToken, resolver, pageAdminConfig, gamificationLevelsJson) → LeaderboardSummary), implying (per its interface shape) that the level thresholds/points logic and the actual LMS gamification API call both live server-side in the implementation, not in this model or the HTL.

LeaderboardSummary (com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.beans) is a plain serializable bean: levelName, currentPoints, nextLevelName, nextLevelThreshold, pointsToNextLevel, progressPercent, skillsCount, badgesCount, leaderboardUrl, dataAvailable, message, gamificationEnabled, allLevelAchieved.

BadgeItem (also used by the Badge component's supporting code, see badge section) is the simple {label, imagePath} pair used to hand the resolved current-level badge image to the HTL.

Dialog

Single "Properties" tab plus a nested "Badge images Configuration" container:

FieldPropertyTypeDefault
Titletitletext"My Level"
Leaderboard Link LabelleaderboardLabeltext"View Team Leaderboard"
Gamification Levels JSONgamificationLevelsJsontext—
Leaderboard Page PathleaderboardPagePathpathfield (rootPath /content)—
Skills LabelskillsLabeltext"Skills"
Badges LabelbadgesLabeltext"Badges"
Points Till Next Level LabelpointsTillNextLevelLabeltext"Points Till Next Level"
Empty State MessageemptyStateMessagetextarea"Leaderboard data is currently unavailable."
Beginner Badge ImagebeginnerBadgeImagepathfield (rootPath /content/dam/lms)—
Bronze Badge ImagebronzeBadgeImagepathfield—
Silver Badge ImagesilverBadgeImagepathfield—
Gold Badge ImagegoldBadgeImagepathfield—
Platinum Badge ImageplatinumBadgeImagepathfield—
Hide Skills sectionhideSkillscheckbox—
Hide Badges sectionhideBadgescheckbox—
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestext—

Note: the "five named level" badge-image fields (beginner/bronze/silver/gold/platinum) are hardcoded to those exact five level names/positions in the dialog and in getAuthoredBadgeImages(), but the actual level names that must match are freely authored in gamificationLevelsJson and matched to these five images purely by JSON-key insertion order — a fragile positional coupling between two independently-authored fields.

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<div class="learner-leaderboard ${properties.customCssClasses}">
  <div class="learner-leaderboard__card" data-sly-test="${model.responseAvailable}">
    <div class="learner-leaderboard__level-row">
      <div class="learner-leaderboard__badge" data-sly-test="${model.levelBadges && model.levelBadges.size > 0}">
        <div data-sly-list.badge="${model.levelBadges}">
          <img src="${badge.imagePath @ context='uri'}" alt="${badge.label} badge" class="learner-leaderboard__badge-img" />
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="learner-leaderboard__level-content">
        <div class="learner-leaderboard__title">${model.title}</div>
        <div class="learner-leaderboard__level-name">${model.summary.levelName}</div>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="learner-leaderboard__progress-wrap">
      <a class="learner-leaderboard__link" href="${model.summary.leaderboardUrl @ context='uri'}">${model.leaderboardLabel}</a>
      <div class="learner-leaderboard__progress-bar"
           title="Total points acquired: ${model.summary.currentPoints} | ${model.summary.progressPercent}% complete">
        <div class="learner-leaderboard__progress-fill" style="width:${model.summary.progressPercent @ context='styleToken'}%;"></div>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="learner-leaderboard__next-level-text" data-sly-test="${!model.summary.allLevelAchieved}">
      ${model.pointsTillNextLevelLabel}
      <div class="learner-leaderboard__level-next">${model.summary.pointsToNextLevel} POINTS</div>
    </div>
    <div class="learner-leaderboard__stats">
      <div class="learner-leaderboard__stat" data-sly-test="${!properties.hideSkills}">
        <div class="learner-leaderboard__stat-number">${model.summary.skillsCount}</div>
        <div class="learner-leaderboard__stat-label">${model.skillsLabel}</div>
      </div>
      <div class="learner-leaderboard__stat" data-sly-test="${!properties.hideBadges}">
        <div class="learner-leaderboard__stat-number">${model.summary.badgesCount}</div>
        <div class="learner-leaderboard__stat-label">${model.badgesLabel}</div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="learner-leaderboard__empty" data-sly-test="${!model.responseAvailable}">${model.summary.message}</div>
</div>

This is one of the more accessibility-conscious components in the codebase already: badge alt text is derived from real per-badge label data (not a hardcoded literal), and the progress bar carries a descriptive title attribute summarizing points/percent — though it is still a plain <div>, not a native or ARIA progressbar (no role="progressbar"/aria-valuenow).

Important Constraints

  • Title/Skills/Badges labels have hardcoded English fallback strings baked directly into the Java model (getTitle(), getSkillsLabel(), getBadgesLabel()) rather than resource-bundle-driven i18n.
  • The progress bar is a styled <div>, not a semantic role="progressbar" element with aria-valuenow/aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax — screen readers get only the tooltip title text, which is not reliably exposed.
  • Level-name-to-badge-image matching is positional/string-matched between two independently authored fields (gamificationLevelsJson keys vs. five fixed dialog fields) with no validation that they agree.
  • Session is cookie-based (alm_cp_token); an absent token silently renders the authored empty-state message rather than distinguishing "not logged in" from "service error."

EDS Migration — PROPOSED, not yet implemented

No blocks/clms-leaderboard/ exists yet. Per the inventory: "Gamified LMS leaderboard … levels JSON, badge images … LMS backend … role=progressbar + aria-valuenow." The proposed design promotes the progress indicator to a real ARIA progressbar (the one AEM-side gap the target inventory explicitly flags for this component) and fetches leaderboard data client-side, since — like Badge — this is inherently per-user dynamic data with no static-publish equivalent.

// PROPOSED — blocks/clms-leaderboard/clms-leaderboard.js (not yet implemented)
export default async function decorate(block) {
  const rows = [...block.children];
  const [titleRow, leaderboardLabelRow, leaderboardLinkRow, skillsLabelRow, badgesLabelRow,
         nextLevelLabelRow, emptyStateRow, levelsRow, hideSkillsRow, hideBadgesRow] = rows;

  const config = {
    title: titleRow?.textContent.trim() || 'My Level',
    leaderboardLabel: leaderboardLabelRow?.textContent.trim(),
    leaderboardLink: leaderboardLinkRow?.querySelector('a'),
    skillsLabel: skillsLabelRow?.textContent.trim() || 'Skills',
    badgesLabel: badgesLabelRow?.textContent.trim() || 'Badges',
    pointsTillNextLevelLabel: nextLevelLabelRow?.textContent.trim(),
    emptyStateMessage: emptyStateRow?.textContent.trim(),
    levels: parseLevelsTable(levelsRow), // {name, badgeImage} rows, replaces the 5-fixed-field + JSON-key coupling
    hideSkills: hideSkillsRow?.textContent.trim().toLowerCase() === 'true',
    hideBadges: hideBadgesRow?.textContent.trim().toLowerCase() === 'true',
  };

  block.textContent = '';

  try {
    const res = await fetch('/api/learner/leaderboard-summary', { credentials: 'include' });
    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`Leaderboard API returned ${res.status}`);
    const summary = await res.json();
    block.append(buildCard(summary, config));
  } catch (err) {
    block.append(buildEmptyState(config.emptyStateMessage));
  }
}

function buildProgressBar(summary) {
  const bar = document.createElement('div');
  bar.className = 'clms-leaderboard-progress-bar';
  bar.setAttribute('role', 'progressbar');            // fix vs AEM: real ARIA progressbar
  bar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0');
  bar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100');
  bar.setAttribute('aria-valuenow', String(summary.progressPercent));
  bar.setAttribute('aria-valuetext', `${summary.progressPercent}% complete, ${summary.currentPoints} points`);
  const fill = document.createElement('div');
  fill.className = 'clms-leaderboard-progress-fill';
  fill.style.width = `${summary.progressPercent}%`;
  bar.append(fill);
  return bar;
}
/* PROPOSED — blocks/clms-leaderboard/clms-leaderboard.css (not yet implemented) */
.clms-leaderboard-progress-bar {
  position: relative;
  height: 8px;
  border-radius: 4px;
  background: var(--color-track, #edf0ee);
}
.clms-leaderboard-progress-fill {
  position: absolute;
  inset: 0 auto 0 0;
  border-radius: 4px;
  background: var(--color-progress, #007a3d);
}
{
  "PROPOSED — not yet implemented": true,
  "definitions": [{ "title": "CLMS Leaderboard", "id": "clms-leaderboard" }],
  "models": [{
    "id": "clms-leaderboard",
    "fields": [
      { "component": "text", "name": "title", "label": "Title", "value": "My Level" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "leaderboardLabel", "label": "Leaderboard Link Label" },
      { "component": "aem-content", "name": "leaderboardLink", "label": "Leaderboard Page" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "skillsLabel", "label": "Skills Label", "value": "Skills" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "badgesLabel", "label": "Badges Label", "value": "Badges" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "pointsTillNextLevelLabel", "label": "Points Till Next Level Label" },
      { "component": "richtext", "name": "emptyStateMessage", "label": "Empty State Message" },
      { "component": "boolean", "name": "hideSkills", "label": "Hide Skills section" },
      { "component": "boolean", "name": "hideBadges", "label": "Hide Badges section" }
    ]
  }, {
    "id": "clms-leaderboard-level",
    "fields": [
      { "component": "text", "name": "levelName", "label": "Level Name" },
      { "component": "reference", "name": "badgeImage", "label": "Badge Image" }
    ]
  }]
}

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
titletexttitleSame name
leaderboardLabeltextleaderboardLabelSame name
leaderboardPagePathpathfieldleaderboardLinkRenamedaem-content reference replaces raw path field
skillsLabeltextskillsLabelSame name
badgesLabeltextbadgesLabelSame name
pointsTillNextLevelLabeltextpointsTillNextLevelLabelSame name
emptyStateMessagetextareaemptyStateMessageSame namePromoted to richtext
gamificationLevelsJson + beginnerBadgeImage/bronzeBadgeImage/silverBadgeImage/goldBadgeImage/platinumBadgeImagetext + 5×pathfieldrepeatable clms-leaderboard-level rows (levelName + badgeImage)Renamed / restructuredDropping the fragile "5 fixed named fields matched positionally against arbitrary JSON keys" pattern in favor of an explicit, author-visible name↔image table with any number of levels
hideSkills / hideBadgescheckboxhideSkills / hideBadgesSame name
customCssClassestextclasses (shared)Renamed
(leaderboard summary data itself: levelName, currentPoints, progressPercent, pointsToNextLevel, skillsCount, badgesCount, allLevelAchieved)server-fetched, not authoredConsumed from client-side leaderboard-summary API responseNot an author field — architectural changeAEM computes this server-side via AbbvieLearnerGamificationService per request; EDS proposal fetches it client-side, since the page itself is statically published

My Learning v2 Component — Technical Details

What it does

Renders a personalized, filterable strip/grid of LMS course cards ("catalog" mode: pulled by catalog ID, skill, tag, learning-object type, sort order, and learner-state filters) each showing image, format, description, publish date, duration, progress bar, "Completed"/bookmark state, and a context-sensitive action (Explore / Start / Continue / Revisit). Clicking a card's action either navigates to a course detail page or opens an in-page modal course player overlay that loads an external Adobe Learning Manager player library and authenticates against the Adobe Learning Manager Prime API directly from the browser.

Architecture

Author configures featureType=catalog + catalogIds/sort/learnerState/skillName/tagName/
learningObjectTypes/headingOfStrip/noItemsImage
         ↓
CustomMyLearningModel.java (@PostConstruct init()):
  1. read ALM session token from "alm_cp_token" cookie
  2. AbbvieCustomMyLearningCatalogService.getCatalogData(token, resolver, authoredProps, pageAdminConfig)
     → raw JSON:API-shaped catalog response (data[] + included[] for skills/enrollments)
  3. parseCardItems(): maps each catalog item into a MyLearningCardList render bean —
     resolves localized name/description/overview, image (imageUrl → bannerUrl → stable
     per-course default icon), author name, formatted publish date, formatted duration,
     bookmark state, enrollment/progress state (via included[] lookups keyed "type:id"),
     skill name (walked through relationships: skill -> skillLevel -> skill -> included lookup),
     and computes the card's action label/mode (EXPLORE / START / CONTINUE / REVISIT)
         ↓
mylearning-v2.html renders one card per item, plus a hidden player-overlay <div role="dialog">
         ↓
ui/mylearning-v2.js (vanilla JS, no jQuery):
  - rewrites course-link hrefs from courseUrl data attributes on DOMContentLoaded
  - POST /bin/abbvie/commerciallms/enrollment  → handles the "+" enroll button
  - POST /bin/abbvie/commerciallms/bookmark    → handles bookmark toggle + undo toast
  - on "launch" action: opens the player overlay, lazy-loads
    https://cpcontents.adobe.com/public/publiccdn/playerInteractionLib.min.js,
    and calls https://learningmanager.adobe.com/primeapi/v2/user directly from
    the browser using the alm_cp_token cookie value as a Bearer token

Sling Model — CustomMyLearningModel.java (package: com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.learning.models)

@Model(adaptables = SlingHttpServletRequest.class, defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class CustomMyLearningModel {

    @OSGiService private AbbvieCustomMyLearningCatalogService customMyLearningCatalogService;

    @ValueMapValue private String featureType;     // only "catalog" is currently implemented
    @ValueMapValue private String catalogIds;
    @ValueMapValue private String sort;
    @ValueMapValue private String learnerState;
    @ValueMapValue private String skillName;
    @ValueMapValue private String tagName;
    @ValueMapValue private String learningObjectTypes;
    @ValueMapValue private String headingOfStrip;

    private List<MyLearningCardList> cardItems;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() {
        if (!StringUtils.equalsIgnoreCase(featureType, "catalog")) { statusMessage = "Feature type is not catalog."; return; }
        // ...build authoredProps map, call customMyLearningCatalogService.getCatalogData()...
        cardItems = parseCardItems(apiResponseJson);
    }

    private void processItemButtonExperience(MyLearningCardList item, double progressPercent) {
        if (!item.isEnrolled()) { item.setActionLabel("EXPLORE"); item.setActionMode("explore"); }
        else if (item.isCompleted()) { item.setActionLabel("REVISIT"); item.setActionMode("launch"); }
        else if (progressPercent > 0 && progressPercent < 100) { item.setActionLabel("CONTINUE"); item.setActionMode("launch"); }
        else { item.setActionLabel("START"); item.setActionMode("launch"); }
    }

    private String getStableBackgroundColor(String courseId) {
        int hash = StringUtils.defaultString(courseId).hashCode();
        return DEFAULT_CARD_BG_COLORS[Math.floorMod(hash + 3, DEFAULT_CARD_BG_COLORS.length)];
    }
}

The dialog only exposes a single featureType value today ("Catalog") behind a show/hide dropdown — the cq-dialog-dropdown-showhide wiring implies more feature types were planned but never added.

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
TypefeatureTypeselect (dropdown show/hide)Only "Catalog" option exists
Catalog IdscatalogIdstext
Sortsortselect15 options: name, datepublished(dateUpdated), date, dateCreated, dateEnrolled, -name, -date, dueDate, -dateCreated, -dateEnrolled, effectiveness, rating, -rating, -recommendationScore(labelled "-recommendationScore" but value differs from label pattern of others), progress, -progress
Learner StatelearnerStatetextFree text; dialog notes it must be one of enrolled/started/completed for due/enrolled-date sort to work — not enforced as an enum
Skill NameskillNametext
Tag NametagNametext
Learning Object TypeslearningObjectTypestext
Heading of GridheadingOfStriptextField label says "Grid", HTL renders it in an <h2> above the strip
No Items Available ImagenoItemsImagepathfield (rootPath /content/dam/lms)
Custom CSS ClassescustomCssClassestext

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<div class="${properties.customCssClasses} custom-my-learning">
  <h2>${properties.headingOfStrip}</h2>
  <div data-sly-test="${!model.cardItems}" class="mylearning-cards-empty">
    <img src="${properties.noItemsImage}" alt="No Items Available"><p>No Items Available.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="mylearning-cards js-mylearning-cards" data-sly-list.item="${model.cardItems}">
    <div class="mylearning-card">
      <div class="mylearning-card__media ${item.cardBackgroundColor}">
        <a class="js-mylearning-v2-course-link" data-course-url="${item.courseUrl @ context='text'}" href="#">
          <img data-sly-test="${item.imageUrl}" src="${item.imageUrl @ context='uri'}" alt="${item.title}" />
        </a>
        <button data-sly-test="${!item.hidePlusIcon && !item.enrolled}" class="js-mylearning-v2-enroll-btn"
                data-course-id="${item.id}" data-instance-id="${item.instanceId}" aria-label="Add to My Learning">...</button>
        <div class="mylearning-card__progress">
          <div class="mylearning-card__progress-fill" style="width: ${item.progressPercent @ context='styleString'}%;"></div>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="mylearning-card__content">
        <a class="mylearning-card__title js-mylearning-v2-course-link" data-course-url="${item.courseUrl @ context='text'}">${item.title}</a>
        <button class="js-mylearning-v2-bookmark-action" data-course-id="${item.id}" data-bookmarked="${item.bookmarked ? 'true' : 'false'}">...</button>
        <a data-sly-test="${item.actionMode == 'explore'}" class="js-mylearning-v2-course-link" data-course-url="${item.courseUrl @ context='text'}">${item.actionLabel}</a>
        <button data-sly-test="${item.actionMode == 'launch'}" class="js-mylearning-v2-launch-overlay"
                data-lo-id="${item.id @ context='attribute'}" data-course-title="${item.title @ context='attribute'}">${item.actionLabel}</button>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="mylearning-v2-player-overlay js-mylearning-v2-player-overlay" hidden>
    <div class="js-mylearning-v2-player-close"></div>
    <div role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label="Course Player">
      <div id="mylearning-v2-player-container"></div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

JS behavior (ui/mylearning-v2.js, 633 lines)

Notably self-contained (no jQuery dependency, unlike most components in this codebase). Key excerpts:

// Enrollment — POSTs to a first-party AEM endpoint
fetch("/bin/abbvie/commerciallms/enrollment", {
    method: "POST",
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    credentials: "same-origin",
    body: JSON.stringify({ courseId: courseId, instanceId: instanceId })
});

// Course player launch — loads Adobe's player lib and calls the ALM Prime API
// directly from the browser, bypassing the AEM backend entirely for this step
var PLAYER_LIB_URL = "https://cpcontents.adobe.com/public/publiccdn/playerInteractionLib.min.js";
function fetchCurrentUser(accessToken) {
    return fetch("https://learningmanager.adobe.com/primeapi/v2/user", {
        method: "GET",
        headers: {
            "Authorization": "Bearer " + accessToken,
            "Accept": "application/vnd.api+json",
            "Content-Type": "application/vnd.api+json"
        }
    });
}
function getAccessToken() { return getCookie("alm_cp_token"); }

Important Constraints

  • Only the "Catalog" feature type is implemented; the dialog's show/hide dropdown scaffolding suggests other feature types (e.g. a "Bookmarks" or "In Progress" strip) were planned but not built.
  • The course player overlay calls the Adobe Learning Manager Prime API and loads a third-party player script directly from the browser using a cookie-sourced bearer token — meaningful client-side exposure of the session token to a third-party CDN script, and a hard runtime dependency on two external Adobe-hosted endpoints with no fallback if either is unavailable.
  • "Load More" only appears once cardItems.size > 20, but there is no visible pagination/lazy-fetch logic wired to it in the reviewed JS — appears to be a client-side reveal-more-of-already-fetched-items pattern rather than true pagination.
  • Card background color and default icon assignment are deterministic hash functions of the course ID (getStableBackgroundColor/getStableIconNumber) purely to keep a given course's placeholder visual identity stable across renders — a reasonable technique, but it means placeholder visuals are an emergent side effect of ID hashing, not an authored/designed mapping.
  • Skill-name resolution walks a JSON:API included[] relationship graph three levels deep (learning-object-skill → skillLevel → skill) with string-splitting fallbacks (learningObjectSkillId.lastIndexOf('_')) — a fragile, deeply-coupled parser against the specific shape of the LMS API response.

EDS Migration — PROPOSED, not yet implemented

No blocks/mylearning-v2/ exists yet. Per the inventory: "LMS course strip + player … catalog filters … catalogIds, sort, tags … LMS/catalog API … role=progressbar, player role=dialog." As with Badge and CLMS Leaderboard, the catalog/progress/bookmark data is inherently per-user and dynamic, so the proposed design authors only the query/filter configuration and fetches+renders cards client-side.

// PROPOSED — blocks/mylearning-v2/mylearning-v2.js (not yet implemented)
export default async function decorate(block) {
  const rows = [...block.children];
  const [headingRow, catalogIdsRow, sortRow, learnerStateRow, skillNameRow,
         tagNameRow, loTypesRow, noItemsImageRow] = rows;

  const config = {
    heading: headingRow?.textContent.trim(),
    catalogIds: catalogIdsRow?.textContent.trim(),
    sort: sortRow?.textContent.trim(),
    learnerState: learnerStateRow?.textContent.trim(),
    skillName: skillNameRow?.textContent.trim(),
    tagName: tagNameRow?.textContent.trim(),
    learningObjectTypes: loTypesRow?.textContent.trim(),
    noItemsImage: noItemsImageRow?.querySelector('img'),
  };

  block.textContent = '';
  const heading = document.createElement('h2');
  heading.textContent = config.heading;
  const grid = document.createElement('div');
  grid.className = 'mylearning-v2-grid';
  block.append(heading, grid);

  const params = new URLSearchParams(config);
  const res = await fetch(`/api/learner/catalog?${params}`, { credentials: 'include' });
  const { items } = await res.json();

  if (!items.length) {
    grid.append(buildEmptyState(config.noItemsImage));
    return;
  }
  items.forEach((item) => grid.append(buildCard(item)));
}

function buildProgressBar(item) {
  const bar = document.createElement('div');
  bar.setAttribute('role', 'progressbar');
  bar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0');
  bar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100');
  bar.setAttribute('aria-valuenow', String(item.progressPercent));
  return bar;
}

function openPlayer(item) {
  const overlay = document.querySelector('.mylearning-v2-player-overlay');
  const dialog = overlay.querySelector('[role="dialog"]');
  dialog.setAttribute('aria-label', `Course Player: ${item.title}`);
  overlay.hidden = false;
  // PROPOSED: player script + auth call routed through a first-party proxy endpoint
  // rather than calling the third-party LMS API directly from the browser with a
  // cookie-sourced bearer token (fix vs AEM's direct-to-vendor client call)
  import('./mylearning-v2-player.js').then(({ launch }) => launch(item, dialog));
}
/* PROPOSED — blocks/mylearning-v2/mylearning-v2.css (not yet implemented) */
.mylearning-v2-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(240px, 1fr)); gap: var(--spacing-m, 20px); }
.mylearning-v2-player-overlay[hidden] { display: none; }
.mylearning-v2-player-overlay { position: fixed; inset: 0; }
.mylearning-v2-player-overlay [role="dialog"] { margin: auto; max-width: 960px; }
{
  "PROPOSED — not yet implemented": true,
  "definitions": [{ "title": "My Learning v2", "id": "mylearning-v2" }],
  "models": [{
    "id": "mylearning-v2",
    "fields": [
      { "component": "text", "name": "heading", "label": "Heading of Grid" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "catalogIds", "label": "Catalog Ids" },
      { "component": "select", "name": "sort", "label": "Sort",
        "options": [{"name":"Name","value":"name"},{"name":"Date","value":"date"},{"name":"Due Date","value":"dueDate"},{"name":"Progress","value":"progress"}] },
      { "component": "select", "name": "learnerState", "label": "Learner State",
        "options": [{"name":"Enrolled","value":"enrolled"},{"name":"Started","value":"started"},{"name":"Completed","value":"completed"}] },
      { "component": "text", "name": "skillName", "label": "Skill Name" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "tagName", "label": "Tag Name" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "learningObjectTypes", "label": "Learning Object Types" },
      { "component": "reference", "name": "noItemsImage", "label": "No Items Available Image" }
    ]
  }]
}

The proposed learnerState field is promoted from free text (the AEM dialog only describes the valid enum values in a field-description note) to an actual constrained select, closing a real authoring-error gap.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
featureTypeselect (single option "catalog")—Not mappedOnly one working value ever existed; the block is scoped to catalog mode only, so the switch itself is dropped rather than carried forward as dead configuration
catalogIdstextcatalogIdsSame name
sortselect (15 raw API sort tokens)sortSame purpose, curated optionsProposal keeps the field but reduces the option list to the sort orders actually exercised, rather than exposing all 15 raw LMS API sort tokens verbatim to authors
learnerStatetext (free text, enum documented only in field description)learnerStateSame purpose, promoted typeConverted to a real constrained select — closes an authoring-error gap where free text silently fails to match backend expectations
skillNametextskillNameSame name
tagNametexttagNameSame name
learningObjectTypestextlearningObjectTypesSame name
headingOfStriptextheadingRenamed
noItemsImagepathfieldnoItemsImageSame purposereference component type
customCssClassestextclasses (shared)Renamed
(card data itself: title, imageUrl, description, progressPercent, bookmarked, actionLabel/actionMode, skillName resolution, etc.)server-parsed JSON:API response, not authoredConsumed from client-side catalog API responseNot an author field — architectural changeAEM parses the LMS JSON:API response server-side per request; EDS proposal fetches and renders it client-side, matching the pattern used for Badge/CLMS Leaderboard
direct browser call to learningmanager.adobe.com/primeapi/v2/user with a cookie-sourced bearer tokeninline vendor API callRouted through a first-party proxy/token-exchange endpointNot mapped as a field — architectural/security fixDropping direct client-side exposure of the session token to a third-party host; the proposed design interposes a first-party endpoint

Okta Login Component — Technical Details

What it does

Embeds the Okta Sign-In Widget (self-hosted vendored JS: okta-sign-in-7.14.0.min.js, okta-auth-js-7.5.1.min.js) for third-party SSO login/registration/MFA, and — on successful authentication — round-trips the resulting Okta ID token through a first-party AEM servlet that validates the token, provisions/updates a corresponding AEM user, adds that user to an authored AEM group, and establishes a standard AEM token-auth session (cookie) for the browser.

Architecture

Author configures oktaDomain, clientId, brandName, redirectUri, classicEngine,
validate, postLoginRedirectUrl/postLogoutRedirectUrl, scopes[], aemGroup,
and ~14 Okta widget "Features" checkboxes (registration, rememberMe, MFA options, etc.)
         ↓
OktaLoginImpl.java (Sling Model) resolves isConfigured() (oktaDomain + clientId present)
and computes brandWrapperClass from the current page path segment
         ↓
okta-login.html renders an empty <div id="osw-container"> container (widget mounts here)
         ↓
ui/okta.js (browser):
  1. loadScript() for the Okta widget/auth-js libs (with a host allowlist check)
  2. builds an OktaSignIn widget config (classic engine or OIE, per authored features)
  3. on successful widget auth: extracts the Okta ID token
  4. POST /bin/abbvie/okta/login  { issuer, idToken, clientId, group, validate }
         ↓
OktaLoginServlet.java (server, /bin/abbvie/okta/login):
  1. validates the ID token as a signed JWT against Okta's JWKS endpoint
     (issuer + "/v1/keys", via Nimbus JOSE+JWT, RS256, with audience/clientId check)
     — or skips validation entirely if validate=false (non-prod escape hatch)
  2. extracts email/name/patientId/oktaUserId claims from the verified JWT
  3. finds-or-creates a matching AEM user (JackrabbitSession UserManager),
     syncs profile properties (email, givenName, familyName, oktaUserId, patientId, ...)
  4. ensures the AEM user is a member of the authored AEM group (creating the group if absent)
  5. builds AuthenticationInfo via the standard Jackrabbit TokenLoginModule and writes
     the resulting AEM login-token cookie onto the response (authenticationSucceeded())
  6. returns a JSON payload (email, name, ids, computed*, groupId, ...) to the browser

A separate OktaLogoutServlet and OktaLoginConfigServlet exist alongside the login flow (not fully read in depth here, but confirmed present in the source tree) — this is a genuine multi-servlet authentication subsystem, not a single-purpose embed.

Sling Model — OktaLoginImpl.java (implements OktaLogin)

@Model(adaptables = { SlingHttpServletRequest.class }, adapters = OktaLogin.class,
       defaultInjectionStrategy = DefaultInjectionStrategy.OPTIONAL)
public class OktaLoginImpl implements OktaLogin {

    @ValueMapValue @Via("resource") @Default(values = "") private String oktaDomain;
    @ValueMapValue @Via("resource") @Default(values = "") private String clientId;
    @ValueMapValue @Via("resource") @Default(booleanValues = false) private boolean classicEngine;
    @ValueMapValue @Via("resource") @Default(booleanValues = true) private boolean validate;

    @Override
    public boolean isConfigured() {
        return StringUtils.isNotBlank(oktaDomain) && StringUtils.isNotBlank(clientId);
    }

    @PostConstruct
    private void init() {
        // resolves brandWrapperClass from currentPage.getPath().split("/")[2] — brand-css hook
    }
}

The model itself is a thin config/gate — it does not perform the token validation or user provisioning; that logic lives entirely server-side in OktaLoginServlet, invoked out-of-band from the model/HTL render cycle via a separate POST/GET request from the browser widget.

Dialog

Three tabs; this is one of the largest dialogs surveyed in this codebase (~28 authorable fields):

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Properties tab
Titletitletext
Okta DomainoktaDomaintext (required)e.g. account-name.okta.com
Client IdclientIdtext (required)
Brand NamebrandNametext (required)"used to pull session for brands like Brand A" (genericized from the real field description, which names a specific product) — brand-scoped session lookup, e.g. fm-brandname-a
Redirect UriredirectUritextMust be allow-listed in Okta and match exactly
Use Classic EngineclassicEnginecheckbox (readonly, always checked)Field is rendered readonly/pre-checked — OIE path exists in code but is not currently author-selectable
Validate Okta Tokenvalidatecheckbox"Should always be set to true in production environments" — an authorable bypass of JWT/JWKS validation
Post Login Redirect UrlpostLoginRedirectUrltext
Post Logout Redirect UrlpostLogoutRedirectUrltext
Add Additional Scope(s)scopes (multifield of scope)multifield/text
IDidtexthtml-unique-id-validator
Features tab (14 checkboxes controlling Okta widget behavior)
Registrationregistrationcheckbox (default true)
Remember MerememberMecheckbox (default true)
Show Keep Me Signed InshowKeepMeSignedIncheckbox (default true)
Show Password Toggle On Sign InshowPasswordToggleOnSignInPagecheckbox
Show Password Toggle On RegistrationshowPasswordToggleOnRegistrationcheckbox
Forgot PasswordforgotPasswordcheckbox
Multi-Optional Factor EnrollmultiOptionalFactorEnrollcheckbox
Device FingerPrintingdeviceFingerPrintingcheckbox
Security ImagesecurityImagecheckbox
Auto PushautoPushcheckbox
SMS RecoverysmsRecoverycheckbox
Call RecoverycallRecoverycheckbox
Routerroutercheckbox (default true)
Hide Inline Sign UphideInlineSignUpcheckbox
Groups and Permissions tab
Group IDaemGrouptext"The AEM group (principle) to add okta users to. Example: everyone" — note the source component's own example defaults to the built-in everyone group

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-use.okta="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.impl.OktaLoginImpl">
  <div data-sly-test="${okta.configured}"
       data-engine="${okta.usesClassicEngine? 'Classic' : 'OIE'}"
       id="${okta.id}" class="cmp-oktalogin brand-${okta.brandWrapperClass}">
    <div id="oktaMessageBox" class="jumbotron">You are not logged in.</div>
    <div id="osw-container"></div>
  </div>
</sly>

Server-side token validation and group provisioning (OktaLoginServlet.java)

@Component(service = Servlet.class, property = {
