    "sling.servlet.paths=/bin/abbvie/okta/login", "sling.servlet.methods={GET,POST}" })
public class OktaLoginServlet extends SlingAllMethodsServlet {

    public class OktaTokenValidation {
        protected void activate(String issuer, String clientId) {
            URL jwkSetUrl = (new URI(this.issuer + "/v1/keys")).toURL();
            this.jwkSource = JWKSourceBuilder.create(jwkSetUrl).retrying(true)
                .cache(TimeUnit.HOURS.toMillis(24), TimeUnit.HOURS.toMillis(1)).build();
        }
        public JWTClaimsSet validateToken(String token) {
            JWSKeySelector<SecurityContext> keySelector =
                new JWSVerificationKeySelector<>(JWSAlgorithm.RS256, jwkSource);
            jwtProcessor.setJWSKeySelector(keySelector);
            jwtProcessor.setJWTClaimsSetVerifier(new DefaultJWTClaimsVerifier<>(
                new JWTClaimsSet.Builder().issuer(issuer).build(),
                new HashSet<>(Arrays.asList("sub", "iat", "exp"))));
            JWTClaimsSet claims = jwtProcessor.process(token, null);
            if (clientId != null && !claims.getAudience().contains(clientId)) return null;
            return claims;
        }
    }

    private User findOrCreateUser(Session adminSession, UserManager userManager, JWTClaimsSet tokenClaimSet) {
        // creates or looks up an AEM User keyed by email/oktaEmail/userOktaId/oktaUserId
        // syncs profile/email, profile/givenName, profile/familyName, profile/oktaUserId,
        // profile/patientId, profile/patientMDMid, profile/oktaEmail, ... on every login
    }

    private void ensureUserIsInGroup(Session adminSession, Authorizable group, User aemUser, UserManager userManager) {
        // creates the authored AEM group if it doesn't exist, adds the user as a member
    }

    public void authenticationSucceeded(HttpServletRequest request, HttpServletResponse response, AuthenticationInfo authInfo) {
        // writes the standard AEM TokenCookie using the credentials produced by
        // triggering Jackrabbit's TokenLoginModule via repository.login()
        TokenCookie.update(request, response, repoId, token, repository.getDefaultWorkspace(), true);
    }
}

Important Constraints

  • "Use Classic Engine" is authored as a readonly, always-checked field — the OIE (Okta Identity Engine) code path exists (data-engine="OIE" attribute, widget branching in okta.js) but is not currently exposed as an authorable choice.
  • "Validate Okta Token" is an authorable checkbox that can fully disable JWT signature/issuer/audience verification server-side — explicitly documented as a lower-environment-only escape hatch, but it is a per-component authoring toggle, not an environment-gated OSGi config, so nothing prevents it from being checked off in production by an author.
  • Group provisioning is permissive by default: if the authored AEM group does not exist, the servlet creates it on the fly; any successfully-authenticated Okta user is silently added as a member, including to the special everyone/admin groups if authored (the servlet explicitly special-cases skipping anonymous/admin principal names when adding members, but does not prevent authoring aemGroup=everyone itself).
  • The load-script allowlist in okta.js only covers global.oktacdn.com and code.jquery.com plus same-origin — any future added script source needs a matching code change, not just an authoring change.
  • Token-validation and AEM-group-provisioning logic has no EDS equivalent whatsoever. This is not a gap that can be closed by writing a smarter block — EDS pages are statically published HTML with no per-request Java servlet tier, no Jackrabbit/JCR user repository, and no server-side JWT verification step. Everything in OktaLoginServlet (JWKS-based signature verification, claim extraction, AEM `User`/`Group` creation and sync, and writing the AEM token-auth cookie) is fundamentally a backend identity-provider integration problem, not a page-rendering/component problem. It requires a dedicated identity/auth workstream — e.g. an edge/serverless token-exchange function plus whatever session/identity store replaces the AEM user repository for the target platform — designed and delivered independently of, and prior to, any "okta-login block."

EDS Migration — PROPOSED, not yet implemented (scoped)

Per the inventory this is explicitly flagged "Net-New (scoped)": "Okta Sign-In Widget embed … classic/OIE … oktaDomain, clientId, scopes … Okta SDK + separate auth workstream (token validation/AEM-group provisioning have no EDS equivalent) … delegated to Okta widget." The block below is scoped deliberately narrowly: it only mounts the Okta widget and hands off the resulting token to an external auth service — it does not attempt to reimplement token validation or group provisioning inside the block.

// PROPOSED — blocks/okta-login/okta-login.js (not yet implemented)
// SCOPE NOTE: this block only mounts the Okta Sign-In Widget and forwards the
// resulting ID token to an external token-exchange endpoint. It intentionally
// does NOT perform JWT validation or user/group provisioning — see "Important
// Constraints" above. That logic must be delivered as a separate identity/auth
// workstream (e.g. an edge function backed by whatever session store replaces
// the AEM user repository), independent of this block.
export default function decorate(block) {
  const rows = [...block.children];
  const [domainRow, clientIdRow, redirectRow, scopesRow] = rows;

  const config = {
    oktaDomain: domainRow?.textContent.trim(),
    clientId: clientIdRow?.textContent.trim(),
    redirectUri: redirectRow?.querySelector('a')?.href || window.location.origin,
    scopes: (scopesRow?.textContent || 'openid,profile,email').split(',').map((s) => s.trim()),
  };

  block.textContent = '';
  const container = document.createElement('div');
  container.id = 'okta-sign-in-container';
  block.append(container);

  if (!config.oktaDomain || !config.clientId) return; // not configured

  loadOktaWidget().then(() => mountWidget(container, config));
}

async function loadOktaWidget() {
  await Promise.all([
    loadScript('https://global.oktacdn.com/okta-signin-widget/7.x/js/okta-sign-in.min.js'),
    loadStylesheet('https://global.oktacdn.com/okta-signin-widget/7.x/css/okta-sign-in.min.css'),
  ]);
}

function mountWidget(container, config) {
  const widget = new window.OktaSignIn({
    baseUrl: `https://${config.oktaDomain}`,
    clientId: config.clientId,
    redirectUri: config.redirectUri,
    authParams: { issuer: `https://${config.oktaDomain}/oauth2/default`, scopes: config.scopes },
  });

  widget.showSignInToGetTokens({ el: container }).then((tokens) => {
    // Hand off to the external identity workstream's token-exchange endpoint.
    // This block does not itself validate the token or create/provision any
    // application-side user/group record.
    return fetch('/api/auth/okta/exchange', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({ idToken: tokens.idToken.idToken }),
      credentials: 'include',
    });
  }).catch((err) => console.error('Okta widget error', err));
}
/* PROPOSED — blocks/okta-login/okta-login.css (not yet implemented) */
.okta-login #okta-sign-in-container { max-width: 400px; margin: 0 auto; }
{
  "PROPOSED — not yet implemented": true,
  "definitions": [{ "title": "Okta Login", "id": "okta-login" }],
  "models": [{
    "id": "okta-login",
    "fields": [
      { "component": "text", "name": "oktaDomain", "label": "Okta Domain", "valueType": "string" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "clientId", "label": "Client Id" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "redirectUri", "label": "Redirect Uri" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "scopes", "label": "Additional Scopes (comma-separated)" }
    ]
  }]
}

Deliberately dropped from the block's own authored fields: brandName (brand-scoped session lookup is an identity-workstream/session-store concern, not a block concern), classicEngine (OIE is the only mode the modern widget SDK targets; there is no equivalent "classic vs OIE" authoring toggle to carry forward), the 14 "Features" checkboxes (Okta widget feature flags would move to a shared, environment-level widget configuration rather than being re-authored per block instance on every page), validate (token validation is out of scope for the block entirely — it must always happen server-side in the separate auth workstream, never optionally skippable from page content), and aemGroup (there is no AEM user/group repository in the target platform to provision into; group/role assignment is an identity-workstream concern).

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
titletext—Not mappedNot rendered anywhere in the source HTL either (dead field in the AEM component itself)
oktaDomaintextoktaDomainSame name
clientIdtextclientIdSame name
brandNametext—Not mappedBrand-scoped session lookup belongs to the separate identity/auth workstream's session-store design, not to page-authored block content
redirectUritextredirectUriSame name
classicEnginecheckbox (readonly)—Not mappedField was already non-authorable in the source (readonly/always-checked); the modern widget SDK only targets OIE, so there is no meaningful "classic vs OIE" choice to carry forward
validatecheckbox—Not mapped (deliberate security fix)Dropping the ability to author-disable token validation; validation must be unconditional and live entirely in the separate server-side auth workstream, never a page-content toggle
postLoginRedirectUrl / postLogoutRedirectUrltext—Not mapped (moved)Post-auth redirect policy is proposed to live in the identity workstream's token-exchange endpoint configuration, not per-block-instance page content, so that redirect behavior is centrally auditable
scopes (multifield)multifield/textscopesSame purpose, flattenedMultifield of single-value rows flattened to one comma-separated text field, consistent with simple list authoring elsewhere in the target repo
idtextid (shared block id convention)Renamed
registration, rememberMe, showKeepMeSignedIn, showPasswordToggleOnSignInPage, showPasswordToggleOnRegistration, forgotPassword, multiOptionalFactorEnroll, deviceFingerPrinting, securityImage, autoPush, smsRecovery, callRecovery, router, hideInlineSignUp14× checkbox—Not mapped (moved)Proposed as shared, environment-level Okta widget configuration rather than 14 per-page-instance authoring toggles — reduces risk of inconsistent auth UX/security posture across pages authored by different teams
aemGrouptext—Not mappedNo EDS/target-platform equivalent exists. There is no Jackrabbit/JCR user-and-group repository in the target implementation to provision into; group/role assignment on successful login is squarely inside the separate identity/auth workstream's scope, not something a page-authored field can express
server-side JWT signature/issuer/audience validation against Okta JWKS (OktaLoginServlet.OktaTokenValidation)servlet logic, no dialog field—Not mapped — explicit architectural gapNo EDS/page-component equivalent. Must be implemented as a standalone backend service in the separate identity/auth workstream; cannot be expressed as block configuration or client-side JS, since a client cannot trust itself to validate its own token
AEM User find-or-create + profile sync + Group membership provisioning (OktaLoginServlet.findOrCreateUser/ensureUserIsInGroup)servlet logic, no dialog field—Not mapped — explicit architectural gapNo EDS/target-platform equivalent. Requires the separate identity/auth workstream to define whatever user/session/permission store replaces the AEM user repository

Search Results Coveo Component — Technical Details

What it does

Mounts a Coveo-hosted search results experience on an AEM page. Rather than rendering its own results markup, the component's job is narrow: resolve which Coveo "hosted search page" ID to use (basic vs. faceted, per an admin-configured lookup), optionally surface a page-level list of Coveo facet tags, inject that configuration into a global window.coveosearch JS object, and mount a Coveo Atomic custom element (<hosted-search-page id="hsp">) that Coveo's own client library (loaded via clientlib category commom-elements-coveo-search-results-plugin.v1 — note the genuine "commom" typo in the real category name) renders into.

Architecture

Author sets searchpagetype (basic|faceted, via a datasource-backed select)
Page author separately tags the page with one or more "coveoFacetListTags" (page property, not this dialog)
         ↓
SearchResultsCoveoModel.java (@PostConstruct init()):
  1. getGlobalCoveoConfig(): resolves /content/admin/admp-global/coveo-search-config
     (env-runmode-aware lookup) and looks up the hosted-search-page ID whose
     admin-config title matches the authored searchpagetype value
  2. initCoveoFacetList(): reads the CONTAINING PAGE's "coveoFacetListTags" property
     (a cq:Page-level multi-value tag property, set independently of this component's
     own dialog), resolves each tag via TagManager, and serializes {title: tagId} pairs
     to a JSON string
         ↓
search-results-coveo.html renders:
  <div class="coveoFacetDiv" data-coveoFacetListString="[...]"></div>
  <script>window.coveosearch = { coveoHostedSearchPageId: '...' }</script>
  <hosted-search-page id="hsp"></hosted-search-page>   ← Coveo Atomic custom element
         ↓
ui/search-result-coveo.js runs in the browser as an analytics BRIDGE ONLY —
it listens for Coveo's own search/click events and re-shapes them into this
codebase's Adobe Analytics digitalData.page.search object; it does not
implement search itself (that's entirely inside Coveo's own client library
and hosted search page configuration, which is opaque to this codebase)

Sling Model — SearchResultsCoveoModel.java

@Model(adaptables = Resource.class)
public class SearchResultsCoveoModel {

    @Inject @Named("searchpagetype") @Default(values = "basic") private String searchpagetype;
    @Inject @Named("brandName") @Optional private String brandName;   // injected but not consumed by the HTL

    private String coveoHostedSearchPageId;
    private String coveoFacetListString;

    @PostConstruct
    protected void init() { getGlobalCoveoConfig(); initCoveoFacetList(); }

    private void getGlobalCoveoConfig() {
        String coveoSearchConfigPath = MessageFormat.format(AbbvieFormConstants.ADMIN_APICONFIG_PATH, "coveo-search-config");
        Resource configRes = AdminConfigUtility.getAdminPageResource(
            AbbvieFormConstants.ADMIN_COVEOENDPOINT_PATH_RUNMODE, coveoSearchConfigPath,
            new Object[] { FormsConfigUtil.getEnvRunMode(), FormsConfigUtil.getEnvType(), "coveo-search-config" }, resourceResolver);
        if (configRes != null) {
            coveoHostedSearchPageId = FormsConfigUtil.getTitleByValue(configRes.adaptTo(Page.class), searchpagetype);
        }
    }

    private void initCoveoFacetList() {
        Page currentPage = resourceResolver.adaptTo(PageManager.class).getContainingPage(resource);
        String[] coveoTagsList = currentPage.getProperties().get("coveoFacetListTags", String[].class);
        if (ArrayUtils.isNotEmpty(coveoTagsList)) {
            JSONArray jsonCoveoObject = new JSONArray();
            for (String tag : coveoTagsList) {
                Tag tagObject = tagManager.resolve(tag);
                if (tagObject != null) {
                    JSONObject item = new JSONObject();
                    item.put(tagObject.getTitle(), tagObject.getName());
                    jsonCoveoObject.put(item);
                }
            }
            coveoFacetListString = jsonCoveoObject.toString();
        }
    }
}

Note brandName is injected via @Inject @Named("brandName") but is not referenced anywhere in search-results-coveo.html or the script-object template — either dead code or a field consumed only by some other, unreviewed integration point.

Dialog

FieldPropertyTypeNote
Anchor IDanchorIDtext
Custom CSS Class(es)customCssClassestext
Search Page Typesearchpagetypeselect (datasource: /bin/abbvie/admpGenericDataSource, category ce-coveo-search-page-type)Default "basic"; resolves to a Coveo hosted-search-page ID via the global admin config, not authored directly

Facet tags themselves are not a dialog field on this component — they are read from a separate page-level property (coveoFacetListTags), presumably authored via page properties or a template policy, making this component's actual facet behavior partly configured outside its own dialog.

HTL Template — Key DOM Structure

<sly data-sly-use.clientLib="${'/libs/granite/sightly/templates/clientlib.html'}" data-sly-unwrap>
  <meta data-sly-call="${clientLib.js @ categories='commom-elements-coveo-search-results-plugin.v1'}" data-sly-unwrap/>
</sly>
<sly data-sly-use.coveoSearchModel="com.abbvie.admp.commonelements.core.models.SearchResultsCoveoModel">
  <div class="coveoFacetDiv" data-coveoFacetListString="${coveoSearchModel.coveoFacetListString}"></div>
  <script type="text/javascript" data-sly-use.scriptObjTemp="search-results-coveo-script-object.html"
          data-sly-call="${scriptObjTemp.scriptObj @coveoSearchModel=coveoSearchModel}"></script>
  <hosted-search-page id="hsp"></hosted-search-page>
</sly>
<!-- search-results-coveo-script-object.html -->
<template data-sly-template.scriptObj="${@coveoSearchModel}">
    window.coveosearch={
        coveoHostedSearchPageId:'${coveoSearchModel.coveoHostedSearchPageId @context="html"}'
    }
</template>

Analytics bridge (ui/search-result-coveo.js)

let abbvCoveoAnalyticsBridge = (function() {
    return {
        createAnalyticsBridge: function(digitalData) {
            return (event) => {
                // maps Coveo's own event shape (selectedFacets, searchTerm, results,
                // resultPosition, resultUrl, resultTitle, ...) into this codebase's
                // digitalData.page.search object for Adobe Analytics
                const search = {
                    term: event.searchTerm || '',
                    count: event.results || '',
                    clickPosition: event.resultPosition || '',
                    clickLink: event.resultUrl || '',
                    filterCategory: filterCategory, filterValue: filterValue, filterType: filterType,
                    origin: sessionStorage.getItem('OriginTitle') ? decodeURIComponent(sessionStorage.getItem('OriginTitle')) : '',
                };
                digitalData.page.search = search;
            }
        }
    };
})();

Important Constraints

  • The actual search UI, ranking, and result rendering are entirely delegated to Coveo's hosted search page + Atomic web component (<hosted-search-page>) — this AEM component only supplies configuration (which hosted page ID, which facet tags), so accessibility of the results themselves is delegated to Coveo's own configuration, outside this codebase's control (matches the inventory's own note: "delegated a11y to Coveo config").
  • Facet behavior is split across two authoring surfaces: the dialog's searchpagetype select and a separate, non-dialog page property (coveoFacetListTags) — an author editing only this component's dialog cannot see or change which facets will show.
  • The hosted-search-page ID resolution depends on an environment-runmode-aware admin config page whose titles must exactly match the authored searchpagetype value string — a string-matching coupling between two independently maintained JCR trees, not a validated reference.
  • brandName is injected into the model but appears unused by the reviewed rendering path — either dead code, or consumed by an integration not covered in the files reviewed here.
  • The clientlib category name (commom-elements-coveo-search-results-plugin.v1) contains a genuine typo ("commom") baked into the real category identifier — noted here as a verbatim technical fact, not a documentation error.

EDS Migration — PROPOSED, not yet implemented

No blocks/search-results-coveo/ exists yet. Per the inventory this is a "Net-New enhancement": "Coveo-powered results … basic/faceted … facet config (static) … Coveo Atomic … delegated a11y to Coveo config." The proposed design keeps the same "thin configuration shell around Coveo Atomic" shape, but consolidates the two authoring surfaces (dialog + page-property) into a single block so an author editing the block sees the full configuration in one place.

// PROPOSED — blocks/search-results-coveo/search-results-coveo.js (not yet implemented)
export default function decorate(block) {
  const rows = [...block.children];
  const [pageTypeRow, hostedPageIdRow, facetTagsRow] = rows;

  const searchPageType = pageTypeRow?.textContent.trim() || 'basic';
  const hostedSearchPageId = hostedPageIdRow?.textContent.trim();
  const facetTags = (facetTagsRow?.textContent || '')
    .split(',').map((t) => t.trim()).filter(Boolean);
  // fix vs AEM: facet tags are now authored directly on this block, not on a
  // separate page property invisible to an author editing the component

  block.textContent = '';

  window.coveosearch = { coveoHostedSearchPageId: hostedSearchPageId, facetTags, searchPageType };

  import('./coveo-atomic-loader.js').then(({ loadCoveoAtomic }) => {
    loadCoveoAtomic().then(() => {
      const hostedSearchPage = document.createElement('hosted-search-page');
      hostedSearchPage.id = 'hsp';
      block.append(hostedSearchPage);
      wireAnalyticsBridge(hostedSearchPage);
    });
  });
}

function wireAnalyticsBridge(hostedSearchPage) {
  // ported analytics-bridge behavior: re-shape Coveo's event payloads into
  // whatever the target implementation's analytics layer expects
  hostedSearchPage.addEventListener('coveo:search', (event) => {
    const detail = event.detail || {};
    // ...map detail.searchTerm/resultPosition/resultUrl/selectedFacets, as in AEM's bridge...
  });
}
/* PROPOSED — blocks/search-results-coveo/search-results-coveo.css (not yet implemented) */
.search-results-coveo hosted-search-page { display: block; min-height: 400px; }
{
  "PROPOSED — not yet implemented": true,
  "definitions": [{ "title": "Search Results (Coveo)", "id": "search-results-coveo" }],
  "models": [{
    "id": "search-results-coveo",
    "fields": [
      { "component": "select", "name": "searchPageType", "label": "Search Page Type",
        "options": [{"name":"Basic","value":"basic"},{"name":"Faceted","value":"faceted"}] },
      { "component": "text", "name": "hostedSearchPageId", "label": "Coveo Hosted Search Page ID" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "facetTags", "label": "Facet Tags (comma-separated)" }
    ]
  }]
}

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtextid (shared)Renamed
customCssClassestextclasses (shared)Renamed
searchpagetypeselect (datasource-backed)searchPageTypeSame purpose, renamedProposal uses a static two-option select rather than a live datasource call to a bespoke servlet (/bin/abbvie/admpGenericDataSource), since only two values (basic/faceted) are ever offered
resolved coveoHostedSearchPageId (computed server-side from an admin config page keyed by searchpagetype)derived, not authoredhostedSearchPageIdPromoted to a direct authored fieldDropping the indirect string-matching lookup against a separate admin-config JCR tree; the author authors the resolved Coveo hosted-page ID directly (or it is set by the target implementation's own environment config, but no longer resolved by title-matching against admin page rows)
coveoFacetListTags (page property, not a dialog field on this component)page-level multi-value tag propertyfacetTagsRestructuredConsolidated onto the block itself so facet configuration is visible and editable where the component is authored, rather than split onto an invisible page property
brandNametext (injected, apparently unused by the render path)—Not mappedNo confirmed consumer found in the reviewed HTL/JS; dropped as dead/unverified configuration rather than carried forward speculatively

Forms Symptom Selector Component — Technical Details

What it does

An interactive SVG body-map widget used as a field inside an AEM Forms flow (e.g. a symptom-tracking quiz), not used directly on a page ("Unlike regular components, the Symptom Selector widget is not used directly on a page, but rather as an element of Form" — a note the source component's own build documentation states explicitly). The author defines two SVG body views (e.g. "Front"/"Back"), a set of ordered severity "Level" values, and an optional second dimension of ordered "Scale" values (e.g. mild/annoying/unbearable). End users click clickable body-part regions in the SVG, then choose a level (and optional scale) for that region; selections are visually reflected as CSS-class-driven color/shading changes on the SVG paths.

Architecture

This component has no .content.xml/_cq_dialog — unlike the other seven components in this batch, it ships purely as an AEM Forms field-type asset: an author-dialog defined by a flat ui/dialog.json (AEM Forms' own dialog schema, distinct from Granite/Coral dialogs), plus a PHP-based showcase/preview template (ui/forms-symptom-selector.php) and hand-written JS/CSS driving the interaction. There is no Java Sling Model backing this component in the reviewed source tree — its author-time configuration and runtime behavior are both implemented in the client-side JS shipped with the form field type, not in a server-rendered HTL+model pipeline like the other seven components in this batch.

Author defines (via ui/dialog.json's 4 tabs — Overview / Level Values / Scale Values / Accessibility):
  title, description, 2 view titles + 2 SVG body maps (fixed body-part paths, not authorable),
  N ordered Level values (severity), optional M ordered Scale values,
  and accessibility labels for a parallel dropdown-based interface
         ↓
forms-symptom-selector.js (jQuery, 770 lines) wires:
  - click on any SVG <path class="body-part"> → opens a per-part selection panel
    (severity radio-equivalent rows + optional scale rows)
  - selecting a level/scale applies a CSS class (levelN / scaleN) to that SVG path,
    driving color-coded shading defined in forms-symptom-selector.css
  - "Clear Selection" button resets all selected parts independently per view (regular vs accessible)
  - "Accessibility View" toggle (.js-toggle-accessibility) swaps the whole SVG-based
    interaction surface for a parallel set of native <select> dropdowns
    (body view → body part → level → scale), OFF by default

Dialog (ui/dialog.json — AEM Forms field-type dialog schema)

Four tabs. This is the exact source of the accessibility gap called out for special attention:

TabFieldTypeNote
OverviewAnchor IDtext
OverviewCustom CSS Classestext
OverviewTitletext
OverviewDescriptiontextareaOmitted entirely if blank
OverviewView 1 Title / View 2 Titletexte.g. "Front" / "Back"
OverviewInstructionstextarea
OverviewLabel for Clear Selections buttontext
Level ValuesSymptom Dimension 2 (Level) NametextDefaults to "Severity" if not specified
Level ValuesLevel Value 0 TitletextDefault value "No Symptoms"
Level ValuesAdditional Level ValuesmultifieldOrdered ascending by severity; each gets a unique levelN CSS class
Scale ValuesSymptom Dimension 3 (Scale) NametextEntire scale UI omitted if blank
Scale ValuesScale Value 0 Titletext"Entire scale content block omitted if no value entered here and any other entries in multifield are ignored" — per the dialog's own field notes
Scale ValuesAdditional Scale ValuesmultifieldEach gets a unique scaleN CSS class
Accessibility(info paragraph — quoted verbatim below)paragraph"The symptom selector is not easily accessible for keyboard or visually impaired users who can't detect the color variation for different symptom levels. The component will build an additional text-only view and a button to toggle between the views."
AccessibilityLabel for text-only interface selectiontextLabels the opt-in toggle button/switch itself
AccessibilityLabel for Front vs Back View SelectiontextDropdown label, accessible view only
AccessibilityLabel for Body Part SelectiontextDropdown label, accessible view only
AccessibilityLabel for Level SelectiontextDropdown label, accessible view only
AccessibilityLabel for Scale SelectiontextDropdown label, accessible view only; applicable only if scale dimension enabled
AccessibilityLabel for Delete symptom buttontextDefaults to "Delete"
AccessibilityLabel for Add symptom buttontextDefaults to "Add Another Body Part"

Showcase template (ui/forms-symptom-selector.php) — real self-documented accessibility gap

The component's own build/showcase notes state the gap directly, verbatim in the source:

<ul class="abbv-ui-build-notes"><span>Notes:</span>
    <li>...</li>
    <li>For accessibility purposes, an alternate view can be displayed which uses
        simple SELECT boxes instead of images to choose symptoms. This functionality
        can be ommitted by hiding the toggle button.</li>
    ...
</ul>
<div class="abbv-symptom-selector main" data-summary="false" data-show-viu="false">
  <!-- data-show-viu="false": the accessible view-independent-unit interface
       starts OFF; it is opt-in, not the default rendering -->
  <label class="abbv-switch js-toggle-accessibility" data-text-label="true">Accessibility View
    <input type="checkbox" name="show-viu" autocomplete="off">
    <span class="abbv-slider abbv-round"></span>
  </label>
  <div class="abbv-symsel-view view1" data-view="view1" data-text="Front">
    <svg ...>
      <path id="front-face" class="body-part" d="..."/>
      <!-- dozens of body-part <path> elements, each only mouse/click-interactive -->
    </svg>
  </div>
</div>

JS behavior (ui/forms-symptom-selector.js, 770 lines)

wireEvents: function () {
    me.wireViewPicker();
    $widget
        .on('click', '.js-toggle-accessibility input', function (e) {
            let isChecked = $(e.target).prop('checked');
            if (isChecked) {
                $('.abbv-symsel-viu-selector').show();
                $('.abbv-symsel-panels, .abbv-symsel-view-picker').hide();
            } else {
                $('.abbv-symsel-viu-selector').hide();
                $('.abbv-symsel-panels, .abbv-symsel-view-picker').show();
            }
            $widget[isChecked ? 'addClass': 'removeClass']('abbv-symsel-viu-active');
            me.triggerAnalytics('interaction', 'Symptom Selector - Accessible View', isChecked ? 'On' : 'Off');
        })
        // body-part click handlers only ever bind to SVG <path> mouse events —
        // there is no keyboard event binding (keydown/keyup) anywhere on .body-part
        // elements in this file; the SVG regions are not focusable/operable by keyboard
        // in the default (non-accessible) view
        ...
}
/* forms-symptom-selector.css — level/scale visual encoding, mouse-only interaction surface */
.abbv-symptom-selector svg .body-part.level1 { /* color/shading per level */ }
.abbv-symptom-selector svg .body-part.level2 { /* ... */ }
.abbv-symptom-selector svg .body-part.scale0 { /* ... */ }

Important Constraints — real, self-documented accessibility gap

  • The primary interaction surface (clicking SVG body-part paths) is not operable by keyboard and does not convey symptom-level state to screen readers — the component's own dialog explicitly states this in the Accessibility tab's info text, and the showcase build notes repeat it. Color-only encoding of severity level (via CSS classes on SVG paths) is invisible to screen-reader users and to any user who cannot perceive the color differences.
  • The mitigation that exists — a parallel text-only, native-<select>-based interface — is opt-in and off by default (data-show-viu="false", an unchecked toggle switch). A keyboard-only or screen-reader user arriving at the default rendering has no way to discover or operate the primary widget without first successfully operating the (also only mouse/click-bound) toggle switch itself.
  • The toggle switch that reveals the accessible view can itself be "hidden" per the build notes ("This functionality can be omitted by hiding the toggle button") — meaning an authoring configuration exists that removes the only accessible path through this widget entirely.
  • Body-part shapes/positions are fixed (not authorable) SVG paths; only their visual level/scale styling is authorable via CSS — so even the accessible dropdown view's body-part list is a fixed, hardcoded set of names baked into the JS/PHP template, not sourced from the SVG dynamically in an authorable way.

EDS Migration — PROPOSED, not yet implemented

No blocks/forms-symptom-selector/ exists yet. Per the inventory: "SVG body-map symptom picker … level/scale values … accessible dropdown default, not opt-in (fix vs AEM)." This fix is treated as non-negotiable for the proposed design: the accessible, native-form-control interface is the DEFAULT rendering, not an opt-in toggle. The SVG body-map becomes a progressive, visually-enhanced overlay on top of the accessible form controls, rather than the accessible controls being a hidden alternate mode behind the SVG.

// PROPOSED — blocks/forms-symptom-selector/forms-symptom-selector.js (not yet implemented)
// ACCESSIBILITY-FIRST BY DESIGN: unlike the AEM source (where the text-only/native-select
// interface is an opt-in toggle, off by default), this block always renders the native
// <select>-based body-view / body-part / level / scale controls as the primary, always-
// present interface. The SVG body-map is added ONLY as a progressively-enhanced, purely
// visual companion that mirrors the <select> state — it is never the sole way to interact,
// and its presence/absence never changes what a keyboard or screen-reader user can do.
export default function decorate(block) {
  const rows = [...block.children];
  const [titleRow, descriptionRow, view1Row, view2Row, instructionsRow,
         clearLabelRow, levelNameRow, levelValuesRow, scaleNameRow, scaleValuesRow,
         viewLabelRow, partLabelRow, levelLabelRow, scaleLabelRow] = rows;

  const config = parseConfig({
    titleRow, descriptionRow, view1Row, view2Row, instructionsRow, clearLabelRow,
    levelNameRow, levelValuesRow, scaleNameRow, scaleValuesRow,
    viewLabelRow, partLabelRow, levelLabelRow, scaleLabelRow,
  });

  block.textContent = '';
  block.append(buildAccessibleForm(config)); // ALWAYS rendered, always the primary control surface

  // Visual SVG overlay is optional, additive, and never required for full functionality.
  // If it fails to load, or JS is unavailable, the accessible form above is fully usable on its own.
  import('./forms-symptom-selector-visual.js')
    .then(({ enhanceWithSvgOverlay }) => enhanceWithSvgOverlay(block, config))
    .catch(() => { /* no-op: accessible form remains fully functional without the overlay */ });
}

function buildAccessibleForm(config) {
  const form = document.createElement('div');
  form.className = 'symptom-selector-form';
  form.innerHTML = `
    <h2>${config.title}</h2>
    ${config.description ? `<p>${config.description}</p>` : ''}
    <div class="symptom-selector-entries" role="group" aria-label="${config.title}"></div>
    <button type="button" class="symptom-selector-add">${config.addLabel || 'Add Another Body Part'}</button>
    <button type="button" class="symptom-selector-clear">${config.clearLabel}</button>`;
  form.querySelector('.symptom-selector-add')
    .addEventListener('click', () => form.querySelector('.symptom-selector-entries').append(buildEntryRow(config)));
  return form;
}

function buildEntryRow(config) {
  const row = document.createElement('fieldset');
  row.className = 'symptom-selector-entry';
  row.innerHTML = `
    <label>${config.viewLabel}
      <select class="symptom-selector-view">${config.views.map((v) => `<option value="${v.id}">${v.title}</option>`).join('')}</select>
    </label>
    <label>${config.partLabel}
      <select class="symptom-selector-part">${config.bodyParts.map((p) => `<option value="${p.id}">${p.label}</option>`).join('')}</select>
    </label>
    <label>${config.levelName}
      <select class="symptom-selector-level">${config.levels.map((l, i) => `<option value="${i}">${l}</option>`).join('')}</select>
    </label>
    ${config.scaleName ? `<label>${config.scaleName}
      <select class="symptom-selector-scale">${config.scales.map((s, i) => `<option value="${i}">${s}</option>`).join('')}</select>
    </label>` : ''}
    <button type="button" class="symptom-selector-delete" aria-label="${config.deleteLabel || 'Delete'} this body part entry">${config.deleteLabel || 'Delete'}</button>`;
  return row;
}
/* PROPOSED — blocks/forms-symptom-selector/forms-symptom-selector.css (not yet implemented) */
.symptom-selector-form { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: var(--spacing-s, 12px); }
.symptom-selector-entry { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(140px, 1fr)); gap: var(--spacing-xs, 8px); }
/* Visual SVG overlay styling lives in a separate, optional stylesheet loaded only
   by forms-symptom-selector-visual.js, keeping the accessible form's CSS free of
   any dependency on the visual layer ever loading successfully. */
{
  "PROPOSED — not yet implemented": true,
  "definitions": [{ "title": "Forms Symptom Selector", "id": "forms-symptom-selector" }],
  "models": [{
    "id": "forms-symptom-selector",
    "fields": [
      { "component": "text", "name": "title", "label": "Title" },
      { "component": "richtext", "name": "description", "label": "Description" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "view1Title", "label": "View 1 Title" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "view2Title", "label": "View 2 Title" },
      { "component": "richtext", "name": "instructions", "label": "Instructions" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "clearLabel", "label": "Label for Clear Selections button" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "levelDimensionName", "label": "Level Dimension Name", "value": "Severity" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "levelValues", "label": "Level Values (comma-separated, ascending severity)" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "scaleDimensionName", "label": "Scale Dimension Name (optional)" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "scaleValues", "label": "Scale Values (comma-separated, optional)" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "viewLabel", "label": "Label: Body View selector" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "partLabel", "label": "Label: Body Part selector" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "levelLabel", "label": "Label: Level selector" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "scaleLabel", "label": "Label: Scale selector" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "deleteLabel", "label": "Label: Delete symptom button", "value": "Delete" },
      { "component": "text", "name": "addLabel", "label": "Label: Add symptom button", "value": "Add Another Body Part" }
    ]
  }]
}

No "enable accessible view" toggle field exists in the proposed model at all — because there is no separate accessible mode to enable. The native-<select> form is simply the component.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
anchorIDtextid (shared)Renamed
customCssClassestextclasses (shared)Renamed
titletexttitleSame name
descriptiontextareadescriptionSame namePromoted to richtext
View 1 Title / View 2 Titletextview1Title / view2TitleSame purpose, renamed
instructionstextareainstructionsSame namePromoted to richtext
Label for Clear Selections buttontextclearLabelRenamed
Symptom Dimension 2 (Level) NametextlevelDimensionNameRenamed
Level Value 0 Title + Additional Level Values (multifield)text + multifieldlevelValuesFlattenedFixed "value 0 + repeatable additions" pattern flattened to one ordered comma-separated field, consistent with simple ordered-list authoring elsewhere in the target repo
Symptom Dimension 3 (Scale) NametextscaleDimensionNameRenamed
Scale Value 0 Title + Additional Scale Values (multifield)text + multifieldscaleValuesFlattenedSame flattening as Level Values; blank field still omits the whole scale dimension
Label for text-only interface selectiontext—Not mapped (deliberate)There is no separate "text-only interface" to label or toggle — the accessible native-control form is the only interface, always present
Label for Front vs Back View SelectiontextviewLabelSame purpose, renamedNo longer scoped to "the accessible view only" — this label is used by the one and only interface
Label for Body Part SelectiontextpartLabelSame purpose, renamedSame reasoning
Label for Level SelectiontextlevelLabelSame purpose, renamedSame reasoning
Label for Scale SelectiontextscaleLabelSame purpose, renamedSame reasoning
Label for Delete symptom buttontextdeleteLabelSame purpose, renamed
Label for Add symptom buttontextaddLabelSame purpose, renamed
the SVG body-map itself (fixed, non-authorable <path> regions) and the "Accessibility View" toggle switch/checkbox (show-viu) that gates the only accessible interaction pathhardcoded markup + opt-in toggle, no dialog field for the toggle's default state—Not mapped — explicit, deliberate accessibility fixThe proposed design has no equivalent toggle at all. The native-form-control interface is unconditional and always rendered; the SVG becomes an optional, purely decorative progressive-enhancement layer that can fail to load with zero loss of functionality. This directly reverses the AEM source's default (visual-first, accessibility opt-in, and even "can be hidden entirely" per its own build notes) to accessibility-first, visual-optional.

Forms File Upload — Technical Details

What it does

Lets an end user select and upload one or more image or video files, each with author-defined metadata inputs (e.g. description, score), client-side type/size validation, an upload progress dialog, and a delete-with-confirmation flow per file. Used in the source system for "before/after" photo submission flows (e.g. a patient support portal), where a page can host two independent instances of the widget side by side (a "before" instance accepting 2–5 files, and an "after" instance accepting exactly 1 file).

Architecture

This is not a registered cq:Component — there is no root .content.xml anywhere in the component folder. Instead of the standard Granite dialog framework, the folder carries a custom, non-Granite ui/dialog.json authoring-schema file (its own bespoke format: tabs → fields with type/label/tooltip/id keys — not Granite's cq:dialog XML), a plain PHP demo page, and a self-contained jQuery widget (fileupload.js/fileupload.css) that is the only place the real runtime behavior lives. There is no Java Sling Model. 

ui/dialog.json          ← custom (non-Granite) authoring-schema doc, not a real _cq_dialog
ui/forms-file-upload.php ← PHP demo page, wires 2 sample instances via inline <script>
ui/variations.php        ← style-guide "variations" panel (multi-file toggle demo only)
ui/fileupload.js         ← real widget logic (984 lines)
ui/fileupload.css        ← real widget styling
ui/data/dummy.json       ← local JSON fixture used only when devTest is set
clientlibs/.content.xml  ← real cq:ClientLibraryFolder, categories: fileupload.v1, form-components.v1
clientlibs/js.txt, css.txt ← proxy to ../ui/fileupload.js, ../ui/fileupload.css
icon.png

Runtime flow (from fileupload.js): 

Author/demo page defines window.fileUploadInit123 = { ...config... }
         ↓
$('.abbv-form-fileupload').length → abbvFileUpload.init()
         ↓
User picks file(s) via <input type="file"> → startPicking() change handler
         ↓
checkMedia() validates extension + size against cfg.imageFormats/imageMaxSize
            or cfg.videoFormats/videoMaxSize (switches on file.type.split('/')[0])
         ↓
buildStatusRow() renders a .file-status row + per-file metadata <input>s (from cfg.metaData)
         ↓
User clicks "Upload Selected Files" → sendFile() per queued file
         ↓
FormData built (file binary + metadata fields + transactionId + folderName + brand/indication/
   HCP-name context pulled from window.guideBridge or window.parent.digitalData)
         ↓
$.ajax POST to `${cfg.devTest || cfg.uploadUrl}`   ← devTest (local JSON fixture) wins if set
         ↓
success/error updates the status dialog + fires cfg.callback_success/callback_error

Sling Model

None exists. There is no Java class under core/src/main/java/.../models/ for this component — grepping the model package for FileUpload returns nothing, consistent with there being no sling:resourceType registration at all. All "configuration" is a plain JS object (window.fileUploadInit123) assigned inline by the demo PHP page, keyed by name in data-config on the widget's root div, and read back via getConfig($widget) as window[$widget.data('config')]. In a real authored page this would presumably be rendered server-side from the dialog.json-driven authoring values, but no such rendering path exists in the source tree — only the demo's inline <script> block populates it.

Dialog

Two tabs, from the custom ui/dialog.json schema (not a real Granite _cq_dialog):

TabFieldTypeNotes
OverviewAnchor IDtextAdds an id attribute to the component container
OverviewCustom CSS ClassestextSpace-separated custom class list
OverviewAllowed image file typestextComma-separated extensions, e.g. png,jpg,jpeg; blank disallows images
OverviewMaximum image size in MBvertical stepperstep 1, range 1–20, default 1
OverviewAllowed video file typestextComma-separated extensions, e.g. mov,mpg; blank disallows videos
OverviewMaximum video size in MBvertical stepperstep 5, range 1–500, default 1
OverviewMaximum number of files to uploadvertical stepperstep 1, range 1–10, default 1; feeds {maxFiles} message placeholder
OverviewUpload Success Dialog TexttextRenders to properties.uploadSuccessText, default "Success"
OverviewDelete file confirmation Dialog texttextRenders to properties.deleteConfirmationTxt, default "Delete File?"
OverviewDelete file dialog Confirm buttontextRenders to properties.confirmDeleteTitle, default "Yes"
OverviewDelete file dialog Cancel buttontextRenders to properties.cancelDeleteTitle, default "No"
OverviewFolder name for uploaded image/videotextRequired; only [a-zA-Z0-9]+; renders to config.targetFolder
MetadataDefine a metadata input fieldmultifieldRepeatable group, one per per-file metadata input
Metadata (child)Custom CSS ClassestextPer-metadata-field class list
Metadata (child)Field LabeltextRequired
Metadata (child)User input is requiredcheckboxDefault checked (true)
Metadata (child)Minimum number of charactersvertical stepper1–99, default 1
Metadata (child)Maximum number of charactersvertical stepper1–99, default 10
Metadata (child)Input ValidationselectAny characters / Letters only / Numbers only
MetadataRequired Field Missing Error MessagetextDefault "User input is required"; renders messages.errRequiredField
MetadataNot Enough Characters Error MessagetextSupports {minChars} placeholder; renders messages.errNotEnoughCharacters
MetadataIncorrect data type Error MessagetextSupports {datatype} placeholder; renders messages.errInvalidDataType

HTL / Demo Markup

No HTL template exists (no component is registered to render one). The demo PHP page (ui/forms-file-upload.php) hand-writes the widget markup directly and, critically, hardcodes the runtime configuration — including the upload endpoint — in an inline <script>: 

window.fileUploadInit123 = {
    uploadBtn: 'Select "Before" image(s)/video(s)',
    minFiles: 2,
    maxFiles: 5,
    imageFormats: 'jpeg,jpg,png',
    imageMaxSize: 10,  //size in MB
    videoFormats: 'mp4,mov,webm',
    videoMaxSize: 15,  //size in MB
    uploadUrl: '//10.253.40.20:4503/bin/abbvie/v1/beforeandafterportal',
    targetFolder: 'before',
    devTest: '/admp-ivy/common-elements-aem-parent/ui.apps/src/main/content/jcr_root/apps/
              common-elements/v1/components/content/forms-file-upload/ui/data/dummy.json',
    metaData: [
        {text: 'Description', className: 'desc', minLength: 5, maxLength: 20, datatype: 'any',
         placeholder: 'Enter Description', required: true},
        {text: 'Score', maxLength: 5, datatype: "decimal", placeholder: 'Enter Score', required: true},
        {text: 'Week', maxLength: 15, datatype: "integer", placeholder: 'Enter Week'}
    ]
};

Widget DOM structure (per instance, wired via data-config):

<div class="abbv-form-fileupload widget-before" data-config="fileUploadInit123">
    <div class="abbv-input-fileContainer">
        <input type="file" name="fileUpload1" id="fileUpload1">
        <label for="fileUpload1" class="abbv-button-tertiary abbv-icon-attach_file i-b
               abbv-fileupload-input-icon">
            <div class="abbv-fileupload-label-text"></div>
        </label>
        <input name="uploadedFiles" type="hidden" value="" />
    </div>
    <div class="abbv-fileupload-progressbar-container">...</div>
    <div class="fileupload-info"><!-- dynamically rendered rows --></div>
    <dialog class="delete-confirmation">...</dialog>
    <div class="upload-errorMsg"></div>
    <div class="upload-instruction">...</div>
    <dialog class="abbv-form-fileupload-inprogress">...</dialog>
</div>

CSS

Real excerpt from fileupload.css (uses nested CSS with custom properties scoped to the widget root):

.abbv-form-fileupload {
    --black: #000;
    --blue: #00486a;
    --green: #37cd6f;
    --off-white: #fff6f4;
    --red: #ff0c00;
    --white: #fff;
    --lightgray: #d3d3d3;

    & .abbv-input-fileContainer {
        display: none;
        position: relative;
        top: 4px;
        &.abbv-active { display: block; }
        & input[type="file"] {
            cursor: pointer;
            display: block;
            opacity: 0;
            position: absolute;
        }
    }
    & .fileupload-info {
        display: none;
        border: solid 2px var(--lightgray);
        &.abbv-active { display: block; }
        & .file-status {
            display: flex;
            flex-wrap: wrap;
            &:nth-child(even) { background-color: var(--lightgray); }
            & .file-status-message.file-invalid { color: var(--red); }
        }
    }
}

Important Constraints

  • Hardcoded internal IP endpoint (MUST-FIX security finding). The demo script hardcodes a bare internal IP address and port as the upload target (cfg.uploadUrl), reachable over plain (protocol-relative) HTTP with no host-based access control implied by the URL shape. Any authoring flow that copies this demo config forward into production ships a raw internal network address in client-side JavaScript. This must never be carried into an EDS implementation; see EDS Migration below.
  • devTest config key, when set, silently overrides uploadUrl and points at an absolute local filesystem/dev-repo path — another artifact that must not leak into a shipped config.
  • Global-variable configuration (window.fileUploadInit123, looked up by name via data-config) rather than component-scoped state — fragile with multiple instances and no encapsulation.
  • Upload payload construction branches on ambient page state ($('.guideRootPanel').hasClass('hcpSignUp') / .tempPath) to decide which brand/indication/HCP fields to attach — tightly coupled to a specific multi-step form runtime (Adaptive Forms "guide" panels), not portable as-is.
  • No component definition at all — this cannot be "migrated" as a component; it only exists as a field-type/widget pattern to reimplement.

EDS Migration (PROPOSED — not yet implemented)

No EDS block or field type for file upload exists in the target repository today (confirmed against the field model — no file-upload entry is present in the field-type inventory). The proposal is to add file-upload as a new field type recognized by the form block's field-creation dispatch table in form-fields.js (FIELD_CREATOR_FUNCTIONS), following the same shape as the existing select/toggle/checkbox entries — not a standalone block.

Proposed field schema entry (author-facing spreadsheet/JSON row consumed by createField):

{ "Type": "file-upload", "Name": "beforePhotos", "Label": "Upload before photos",
  "allowedImageTypes": "jpeg,jpg,png", "maxImageSizeMB": 10,
  "allowedVideoTypes": "mp4,mov,webm", "maxVideoSizeMB": 15,
  "minFiles": 2, "maxFiles": 5, "targetFolder": "before" }

Proposed createFileUpload field-creator function (illustrative — no such function exists yet):

// blocks/form/form-fields.js (proposed addition)
const createFileUpload = (fd) => {
  const field = document.createElement('input');
  field.type = 'file';
  field.multiple = Number(fd.maxFiles || 1) > 1;
  field.name = fd.Name;
  field.id = fd.Id;
  field.dataset.allowedImageTypes = fd.allowedImageTypes || '';
  field.dataset.maxImageSizeMb = fd.maxImageSizeMB || '';
  field.dataset.allowedVideoTypes = fd.allowedVideoTypes || '';
  field.dataset.maxVideoSizeMb = fd.maxVideoSizeMB || '';
  field.dataset.maxFiles = fd.maxFiles || '1';
  field.dataset.targetFolder = fd.targetFolder || '';

  const fieldWrapper = createFieldWrapper(fd);
  const label = createLabel(fd);
  field.setAttribute('aria-labelledby', label.id);
  fieldWrapper.append(label, field);
  return { field, fieldWrapper };
};

// registered alongside the existing entries:
// FIELD_CREATOR_FUNCTIONS = { ..., 'file-upload': createFileUpload }

Upload endpoint (MUST-FIX — the core migration requirement for this field type): The source system's demo hardcoded a bare internal IP address and port directly in client JavaScript as the upload target. The EDS implementation must never do this. Instead, uploads must POST to a proper serverless/Edge Worker endpoint — for example a Cloudflare Worker, Adobe App Builder action, or equivalent Edge-deployed function — fronted by a stable HTTPS route (e.g. https://<site>.<region>.workers.dev/api/upload or a mapped custom domain), which validates content type/size server-side before writing to durable storage (object storage bucket, not a raw AEM servlet path). No literal internal IP address, port, or unauthenticated internal hostname may appear in the shipped block JS. 

// blocks/form/form.js (proposed addition to handleSubmit-style flow for file-upload fields)
async function uploadFile(file, fieldConfig) {
  const formData = new FormData();
  formData.append('file', file);
  formData.append('targetFolder', fieldConfig.targetFolder);

  // PROPOSED: Edge Worker / serverless endpoint, never a hardcoded internal IP
  const uploadEndpoint = 'https://forms-upload.example.workers.dev/api/upload';

  return fetch(uploadEndpoint, { method: 'POST', body: formData });
}

Authoring in EDS: a file-upload row in the form's authored field sheet, with columns for allowed image/video extensions, max size per type, max file count, and target folder — mirroring the source dialog's Overview tab. The per-file metadata multifield (Metadata tab) maps to additional rows in the same sheet scoped under the file-upload field's fieldset, reusing the existing fieldset/data-fieldset grouping mechanism already in form-fields.js rather than inventing a new nested-repeater concept.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
Anchor IDtext(block-level anchor, via UE block id)Not mappedNot a per-field-type concern in the target field model; anchors are handled at the block/section level, not per field
Custom CSS Classestextfd.Style (existing createFieldWrapper mechanism)RenamedSame purpose (space-separated class list), already a generic mechanism in form-fields.js, no new field needed
Allowed image file typestextallowedImageTypesSame name (semantically)Same comma-separated extension-list format
Maximum image size in MBvertical steppermaxImageSizeMBRenamedSame purpose; EDS field model has no "vertical stepper" widget, becomes a plain numeric text field
Allowed video file typestextallowedVideoTypesSame name (semantically)Same format
Maximum video size in MBvertical steppermaxVideoSizeMBRenamedSame purpose, becomes plain numeric text field
Maximum number of files to uploadvertical steppermaxFilesSame name (semantically)Same purpose; also drives the proposed field.multiple flag
Upload Success Dialog TexttextNot mappedNot mappedStatus/success messaging is proposed to be handled by the block's shared UI copy (consistent with other form fields), not a per-field authored string; no equivalent field proposed
Delete file confirmation Dialog text / Confirm / Cancel button texttext ×3Not mappedNot mappedDelete-confirmation UX is proposed as fixed, accessible block copy rather than per-instance authored strings, to avoid re-introducing the source's untranslated hardcoded-default pattern
Folder name for uploaded image/videotexttargetFolderSame nameSame purpose — logical storage sub-path passed to the upload endpoint
Metadata multifield → Field Labeltextadditional field-sheet row LabelRenamedReuses the form block's existing per-row Label column instead of a nested dialog multifield
Metadata multifield → User input is requiredcheckboxfd.MandatoryRenamedMaps directly onto the field model's existing required-flag convention (already used by every other field type)
Metadata multifield → Min/Max charactersvertical stepper ×2Not mappedNot mappedNo native HTML min/max-length enforcement is proposed beyond standard minlength/maxlength input attributes already supported generically for text fields; no new file-upload-specific field needed
Metadata multifield → Input Validation (select)selectNot mappedNot mappedProposed to rely on the field model's existing generic pattern/type constraints on a companion text field rather than a bespoke letters/numbers/any enum
Upload endpoint (uploadUrl, hardcoded IP)inline JS stringserverless/Edge Worker upload endpoint URL (config, not authored per-instance)Not mapped (replaced)MUST-FIX: never carry a literal internal IP/hostname forward; endpoint must be a proper serverless/Edge Worker route configured outside authored content

Forms Range Slider — Technical Details

What it does

A draggable/click-to-select range slider input pattern for forms: a horizontal (or vertical) track with a draggable handle, supporting numeric or text-labeled value sets, optional "stepped" discrete positions with increment/decrement buttons, optional visible tick labels, and an optional progress "fill". Shown in the source system's style guide in four variants: plain numeric slider, numeric slider with expanded tick labels, stepped numeric slider with +/- buttons, and stepped text-labeled slider with +/- buttons (e.g. Worst/Bad/Ok/Better/Best/Special).

Architecture

This is confirmed not a registered component at all — the folder contains only icon.png, ui/forms-range-slider.php, and ui/variations.php. There is no .content.xml, no dialog of any kind (not even the custom dialog.json pattern seen on forms-file-upload), and no dedicated clientlib folder for this component. The actual interactive slider markup is duplicated verbatim from the wider forms/ui/forms.php style-guide page — this folder is a showcase fragment, not an independent widget definition.

The real runtime logic lives entirely outside this component folder, in the shared framework clientlib used by the whole style guide: 

etc/clientlibs/common-elements/clientlibs/framework/ui/forms.js   ← abbvRangeSlider IIFE (real logic)
etc/clientlibs/common-elements/clientlibs/framework/ui/forms.css  ← .abbv-range-slider rules (real CSS)
apps/.../forms-range-slider/ui/forms-range-slider.php              ← demo markup only, no logic
apps/.../forms-range-slider/ui/variations.php                      ← demo-page "Update Source View" wiring

Initialization and interaction flow, from forms.js:

$(document).ready → $('.abbv-range-slider').each(function(){ abbvRangeSlider.init(this); });
         ↓
init(rangeSlider): reads data-range-type ("number"|"text"), data-range (CSV list),
   data-stepped, data-direction, data-labelled, data-enable-fills, data-initial-value
   from the element's own data-* attributes (no server-rendered config object at all)
         ↓
   if stepped: builds a percent→value lookup table (rangeObj.range) and tick label markup
   sets initial handle/fill position via inline style percentages
         ↓
dragHandle(): mousedown/touchstart → mousemove/touchmove computes % position from pointer
   coordinates relative to the track, updates handle position + hidden .rangeInput value live
         ↓
buttonNav(): +/- increment buttons step through the ordered label list (stepped mode only)
         ↓
setSlider(program­matic set) / setFills(applies "filled" class to passed ticks)

Sling Model

None exists, and none could exist in this form — there is no dialog, no sling:resourceType, and no server-side rendering path. All slider configuration is declared directly as HTML data-* attributes on static demo markup (data-direction, data-range-type, data-range, data-stepped, data-labelled, data-enable-fills, data-initial-value, data-handle) and read client-side by the shared abbvRangeSlider jQuery module — there is nothing analogous to a Sling Model or an authoring dialog anywhere in this feature.

Dialog

No dialog exists in any form (not even a custom JSON schema, unlike forms-file-upload). Not authorable at all in the source system — every instance seen is hand-written demo markup.

HTL / Demo Markup

Real markup from ui/forms-range-slider.php (four variants; two representative examples below):

<!-- Plain numeric slider -->
<div class="abbv-range-slider" data-direction="horizontal" data-range-type="number"
     data-range="10,60" data-stepped="false" data-labelled="true" data-initial-value="20">
    <input type="text" class="rangeInput">
    <div class="abbv-slider-container">
        <div class="abbv-slider-fill"></div>
        <div class="abbv-slider-handle"><span></span></div>
        <div class="abbv-slider-labels"></div>
    </div>
</div>

<!-- Stepped text-labeled slider with +/- buttons -->
<div class="abbv-range-slider labels-top" data-direction="horizontal" data-range-type="text"
     data-range="Worst,Bad,Ok,Better,Best,Special" data-stepped="true" data-labelled="true"
     data-enable-fills="false" data-initial-value="Better">
    <input type="text" class="rangeInput">
    <button class="abbv-button-plain abbv-icon-remove i-a abbv-range-slider-increment"
            data-increment-type="minus"></button>
    <div class="abbv-slider-container">
        <div class="abbv-slider-fill"></div>
        <div class="abbv-slider-handle"><span></span></div>
        <div class="abbv-slider-labels"></div>
    </div>
    <button class="abbv-button-plain abbv-icon-add i-a abbv-range-slider-increment"
            data-increment-type="plus"></button>
</div>

CSS

Real excerpt from etc/clientlibs/.../framework/ui/forms.css:

.abbv-range-slider {
    display: flex;
    width: 100%;
    position: relative;
    justify-content: center;
    align-items: center;
}
.abbv-range-slider input { display: none; }
.abbv-range-slider .abbv-slider-container {
    height: 10px;
    background: #f1f1f1;
    width: 100%;
    margin: 30px 10px;
    position: relative;
    display: flex;
}
.abbv-slider-container .abbv-slider-handle {
    left: 0%;
    height: 20px;
    width: 20px;
    border-radius: 15px;
    background: #071d49;
    box-shadow: 0 0 0px 5px rgba(7, 28, 73,.3);
    position: absolute;
    top: 0;
    margin-top: -5px;
    margin-left: -10px;
    cursor: pointer;
    z-index: 1;
    transition: background 300ms;
}
.abbv-range-slider[data-enable-fills="true"] .abbv-slider-fill { background: #071d49; }
.abbv-slider-container .abbv-slider-label.slider-fill { background: #071d49; }

Important Constraints

  • Not a real component in any sense — no dialog, no server-side config path, no clientlib scoped to this folder. It cannot be "migrated" from anything; only the interaction pattern (drag/click a percent-mapped handle over a stepped or continuous value range) is reusable.
  • The underlying <input class="rangeInput"> element is hidden (display:none) and holds the current value only as a plain text string kept in sync by JS — there is no native form-control semantics: no role="slider", no aria-valuenow/aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax, no keyboard arrow-key support observed anywhere in dragHandle() or buttonNav(). The slider is mouse/touch-drag or button-click only.
  • A related component (an image-comparison "progressive" slider variant elsewhere in the source tree) reuses this exact same .abbv-range-slider widget as a styled-text-input slider substitute rather than a native <input type="range"> — the same missing-ARIA gap repeats wherever this pattern is reused, and should not be repeated in the target implementation.
  • Percent-to-value math (perToVal) assumes evenly-spaced discrete steps when stepped, and linear interpolation when continuous — no support for a numeric step increment on a continuous range (e.g. "step by 5 between 0–100").

EDS Migration (PROPOSED — not yet implemented)

No range-slider field type exists in the target field-type inventory today. The proposal is to add range-slider as a new field type to the form block's FIELD_CREATOR_FUNCTIONS dispatch table in form-fields.js, built on a native <input type="range"> element rather than reproducing the source's hidden-text-input-plus-styled-div pattern — this both eliminates the missing-ARIA gap identified above (native range inputs expose aria-valuenow/min/max automatically to assistive technology) and removes hundreds of lines of custom drag-math.

Proposed field schema entry:

{ "Type": "range-slider", "Name": "severityScore", "Label": "Rate your symptom severity",
  "Min": 0, "Max": 10, "Step": 1, "Value": 5 }

Proposed createRangeSlider field-creator function (illustrative — no such function exists yet):

// blocks/form/form-fields.js (proposed addition)
const createRangeSlider = (fd) => {
  const field = document.createElement('input');
  field.type = 'range';
  field.id = fd.Id;
  field.name = fd.Name;
  field.min = fd.Min ?? 0;
  field.max = fd.Max ?? 100;
  field.step = fd.Step ?? 1;
  field.value = fd.Value ?? field.min;

  const output = document.createElement('output');
  output.setAttribute('for', field.id);
  output.textContent = field.value;
  field.addEventListener('input', () => { output.textContent = field.value; });

  const fieldWrapper = createFieldWrapper(fd);
  const label = createLabel(fd);
  field.setAttribute('aria-labelledby', label.id);
  fieldWrapper.append(label, field, output);
  return { field, fieldWrapper };
};

// registered alongside the existing entries:
// FIELD_CREATOR_FUNCTIONS = { ..., 'range-slider': createRangeSlider }

Proposed CSS (illustrative, scoped under the existing .form block class per the current CSS convention seen in form.css):

/* blocks/form/form.css (proposed addition) */
.form input[type="range"] {
  width: 100%;
  accent-color: var(--link-color);
}
.form .range-slider-wrapper output {
  display: block;
  font-weight: 700;
  margin-top: 0.25em;
}

Text-labeled variants (e.g. Worst/Bad/Ok/Better/Best/Special) are proposed to be handled by mapping the range input's integer positions to a parallel authored label list (a comma-separated Options-style column, consistent with how the existing select field type already parses fd.Options in form-fields.js) and updating the <output> text via the label at the current index, rather than reintroducing a separate "text range type" code path as the source implementation does.

AEM Properties → EDS Field Mapping

AEM fieldTypeMaps to EDS fieldMapping typeNotes
data-range-type ("number"/"text")data attribute (unauthored, hardcoded in demo markup)implicit — text ranges use an Options label list against a numeric-index range inputRenamed / restructuredNot authored anywhere in the source (no dialog exists); proposed to reuse the existing select field type's comma-separated Options parsing convention instead of a separate type flag
data-range (CSV of values or labels)data attributeMin / Max (numeric) or Options (text-labeled)RenamedSame purpose (defines the value set), split into native min/max for numeric ranges per proposed design
data-stepped (true/false)data attributeStepRenamedNative <input type="range"> is always "stepped" by its step attribute; a Step value collapses the source's separate stepped/continuous code paths into one
data-initial-valuedata attributeValueSame name (semantically)Same purpose — initial slider position
data-direction (horizontal/vertical)data attributeNot mappedNot mappedNative range inputs are horizontal only cross-browser without significant custom styling/JS; no vertical variant proposed for the initial field type
data-labelled (show tick labels)data attributeNot mappedNot mappedSuperseded by the always-visible <output> current-value readout in the proposed design; discrete tick labels are not proposed for v1
data-enable-fills (highlight passed ticks)data attributeNot mappedNot mappedPurely a stepped-tick-label visual affordance from the source's custom-built slider; not applicable once discrete tick labels are dropped in favor of native range + output
data-handle (show/hide drag handle)data attributeNot mappedNot mappedNative range inputs always render a browser-supplied thumb; a handle-less variant is not meaningful for a native control and is not proposed

Technical Design Document · 96 components · Field mappings verified against the live target field configuration where an implementation exists; proposed designs are labeled explicitly where no implementation exists yet.
